PT P
PN WO2022136907-A1
TI Aqueous extract useful as a disinfectant and            antimicrobial agent is obtained from seeds of Rosa            canin by means of extraction with supercritical carbon            dioxide.
AU PIRAS A
   IBBA A
   ORRU G
   PORCEDDA S
AE UNIV CAGLIARI (UYCA-Non-standard)
GA 202285978V
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous extract obtained from seeds of Rosa                canina by means of extraction with supercritical                carbon dioxide at a pressure less-than 100 bar and                temperature less than or equal to 40 degrees C                having a dry fraction ranging between 0.1-10%                mass/mass and which includes at least one organic                compounds comprising methyl-linoleate,                methyl-oleate, methyl-palmitate, ethyl-linoleate,                ethyl-linolenate and related mixtures.
   USE - The extract is useful: as cosmetic, medical                field, phytopharmaceutical field, disinfectant,                antibacterial, antifungal, antibiofilm and/or                antimicrobial; as a disinfectant and antimicrobial                agent for instruments and surfaces in the dental                field, for domestic and health hygiene, in                commercial and industrial environments, in the                pre-operative setting, in hospital wards, against                the transmission of nosocomial cross-infections;                for the disinfection and hygiene of community                environments i.e. hotels, airports, schools, doctor                offices and dental offices, in the disinfection of                surgical instruments, as an environmental or line                disinfectant of fluids i.e. water in dental units                or air in aeration/conditioning systems or through                automatic devices, e.g. nebulizers; in the                veterinary field, in gelled and liquid                preparations, to be used as e.g. skin disinfection                in farmed or pet animals, treatment of cutaneous                mycosis, eradication treatment of pathogenic                bacteria, disinfection of milking systems; in the                agri-food sector for the treatment of diseases of                plants in cultivation or in ornamental plants, in                the disinfection of environments or equipment used                in the packaging industry of both fresh and                preserved food products; in the control and                elimination of gram positive and gram negative                bacteria, pathogenic streptococci, antibiotic                multidrug-resistant pathogens (MDR), nosocomial                clinical isolates and azole multidrug-resistant                clinical isolates or as antiviral, including                Sars-CoV-2; treating a diseases of the oral cavity,                including caries, peri-implantitis and                periodontitis (all claimed). Test details are                described but no results for the compounds of the                claims are given.
   ADVANTAGE - The extract has high antimicrobial activity,                which can be diluted in water at different                concentrations, constitutes an antimicrobial                solution that can be used in various fields, in                environmental disinfection, both in humans and                animals, as a base for antibacterial/antifungal                preparations for topical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for (I)                composition comprising the aqueous extract; and                (II) extraction process of a plant matrix                consisting of seeds of Rosa canina which includes                the following fundamental stages extract the dried                and ground seeds with carbon dioxide in the                supercritical phase as extraction solvent under the                following operating conditions: pressure less than                or equal to 100 bar, temperature less than or equal                to 60 degrees C, extraction time less than or equal                to 8 hours, relative quantity of solvent, in terms                of total mass of solvent I matrix mass subjected to                extraction Ms/Mo between 5-40, separate the extract                obtained in the previous stage from the solvent at                a pressure less than or equal to 20 bar and a                temperature less than or equal to 40 degrees C,                bring the total extract to room temperature and                pressure so that the extract spontaneously                separates into two liquid phases: an oily phase and                an aqueous phase, and recover the two separate                phases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The dry fraction                  comprises methyl-linoleate in quantity 31.4%,                  methyl-oleate in quantity 25.1%, methyl-palmitate                  in quantity 7.5%, ethyl-linoleate in quantity                  4.7%, ethyl-linolenate in quantity 3.3%,                  individually or mixed with each other. The                  extract further comprises at least one compounds                  comprising apiol, palmitic acid, linolenic acid,                  linoleic acid, oleic acid, stearic acid, benzoic                  acid, methoxybenzoic acid,                  1-hydroxy-1,1-dimethyl-cyclohexanecarboxylic                  acid, 3,4-dimethoxycyclohexanecarboxylic acid,                  trihydroxybenzene, methoxydihydroxybenzene,                  ethoxy phenyl-3-propanol, and related mixtures.                  The extract has a MIC (Minimum Inhibitory                  Concentration) with values in the range between                  6.25 and 1 .56% weight/volume and an MBC (Minimum                  Bactericidal Concentration) with values in the                  range between 12.5 and 3.12% against fungi and                  bacteria, where the extract has an MBIC (minimum                  biofilm Inhibitory concentration) with values                  between 12.5-6.25% weight/volume of inhibition of                  microbial biofilms. Preferred Process: The                  extraction is carried out at a pressure between                  80-100 bar, at a temperature between 40-60                  degrees C, for an extraction time between 2-8                  hours, and the separation is carried out at a                  pressure between 5-20 bar and at a temperature                  between 5-40 degrees C, where the recovery of the                  two separated phases is carried out by                  decantation associated with cooling or by                  centrifugation. Preferred Components: The                  pathogen comprises Escherichia coli, Escherichia                  coli ATCC 7075, Pseudomonas aeruginosa,                  Pseudomonas aeruginosa ATCC15442 (mucoid strain,                  clinical nosocomial isolate), Klebsiella                  pneumoniae, Klebsiella pneumoniae NC5 (clinical                  nosocomial isolate), Staphylococcus aureus,                  Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus ATCC                  BAA-811 (MSRA), Streptococcus intermedius,                  Streptococcus pyogenes, Streptococcus pyogenes                  NC4, Streptococcus mutans, Streptococcus mutans                  CIP103220, Candida albicans, Candida Krusei,                  Candida glabrata and the clinical isolates of                  Candida albicans BF1 , Candida krusei BF2 and                  Candida glabrata BF3 which are azoles                  multiresistant.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Antimicrobial.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of extract is via topical route                at the cutaneous and mucosal level. solid, liquid                or semi-liquid or gel form also in liposomal                formulation, such as cream, paste, lotion, soap,                suspension, milk, spray, functional base for                mouthwashes and toothpastes (claimed). No dosage                details given.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A09F; B05-B02C; B05-C04; B06-D05; B10-C04B; B10-C04C; B10-C04E3; B10-C04E4; B10-C04E5; B10-G02; B14-A01; B14-A02; B14-A04; B14-C03; B14-N06; B14-R01; B14-S12; B14-S18; C04-A08G2E; C04-A09F; C05-B02C; C05-C04; C06-D05; C10-C04B; C10-C04C; C10-C04E3; C10-C04E4; C10-C04E5; C10-G02; C14-A01; C14-A02; C14-A04; C14-C03; C14-N06; C14-R01; C14-S12; C14-S18; D03-G; D08-A; D08-B03B; D08-C; D09-A01C; D09-A03A; D09-A03B

PT P
PN CN112876433-A
TI Industrially preparing burdock seed active ingredients comprises compacting the burdock seed raw material, inactivating enzyme, extracting raw materials, separating layers, adding activated carbon, crystallizing, and drying.
AU ZHANG Y
   JI Y
   WANG X
   XIAO H
   GENG D
AE SANYUAN RUNHE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SANY-Non-standard)
GA 202164343V
AB    NOVELTY - Industrially preparing burdock seed active ingredients comprises firstly using compacting machine for compacting the burdock seed raw material, and then cooking the enzyme for inactivating the enzyme, using supercritical carbon dioxide for extracting the raw materials to obtain extract and extraction residue, using No. 6 solvent oil for dissolving the extract, collecting No. 6 solvent oil layer and insolubles respectively, adding mixed solvent to insoluble matter, stirring, separating layers after allowing to stand, collecting the ethanol layer and No. 6 solvent oil layer, adding activated carbon to concentrated solution for decolorizing, then filtering, concentrating the filtrate, refrigerating, filtering, drying to obtain burdock aglycon, then adding activated carbon for decolorizing, filtering, crushing the extraction residue, using n-butanol for extraction, adding mixed adsorbent, stirring, filtering, refrigerating, crystallizing, and drying to obtain arctiin.
   USE - The method is useful for industrially preparing burdock seed active ingredients.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has high yield; realizes comprehensive utilization of raw materials; reduces the production cost of a single product; and is more suitable for industrialized production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Industrially preparing burdock seed active ingredients comprises (a) firstly using compacting machine for compacting the burdock seed raw material, and then cooking the enzyme for inactivating the enzyme for 5-10 minutes, (b) using supercritical carbon dioxide for extracting the raw materials to obtain extract and extraction residue, where the carrier during supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is toluene or xylene, and the amount of carrier is 3-5% the mass of raw material after enzyme inactivation, (c1) using No. 6 solvent oil for dissolving the extract, where the quality of No. 6 solvent oil is 3-5 times the mass of extract, stirring, filtering, and collecting No. 6 solvent oil layer and insolubles respectively, (c2) adding mixed solvent (3-5 times) to insoluble matter, where the mixed solvent is a mixed solvent of No. 6 solvent oil and ethanol, then stirring, separating layers after allowing to stand, and collecting the ethanol layer and No. 6 solvent oil layer respectively, (c3) concentrating the ethanol layer to an alcohol degree of 60-70%, adding activated carbon to concentrated solution for decolorizing, then filtering, concentrating the filtrate to a specific gravity of 1.1-1.15, refrigerating the crystals, filtering, adding wet weight of isopropanol (1-1.5 times) to filtered crystals, stirring, filtering, and drying to obtain more than 98% burdock aglycon, and (c4) combining No. 6 solvent oil layer collected in the step (c1) and step (c2), then adding activated carbon for decolorizing, filtering, and concentrating the filtrate to obtain burdock seed oil, and (d) crushing the extraction residue to 60-80 mesh, using n-butanol (6-8 times) for extraction at room temperature while stirring, collecting the extract, concentrating the extract to alcohol degree of 5-10%, adding mixed adsorbent with a mass of 20-30% of the extraction residue, where the mixed adsorbent is a mixed adsorbent of activated carbon and neutral alumina, stirring, filtering, collecting the filter residue, then using extract residue (5-10 times) for stirring and dissolving in saturated ethyl acetate with water, filtering, combining and concentrating the filtrate to a specific gravity of 1.1-1.15, refrigerating, crystallizing, filtering, rinsing the filter residue with dichloromethane and drying to obtain more than 98% arctiin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed solvent the step (c2) is 80% ethanol, and No. 6 solvent oil in a mass ratio of 1:1-3. The volume ratio of No. 6 solvent oil to 80% ethanol is 1:2. The mass of mixed adsorbent is 25% the mass of extraction residue. The mass ratio of activated carbon to neutral alumina in the mixed adsorbent is 1:2-5, preferably 1:3. The mass of activated carbon is 10% the mass of combined solution of the No. 6 solvent oil layer collected in the step (c1) and step (c2). Preferred Method: The method comprises adding activated carbon to the concentrated solution for decolorizing for 2 hours, where the mass of activated carbon is 5% the mass of extract. The decolorizing is carried out for 1 hour. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out under the pressure of 30-35 MPa at 40-45 degrees C for 2-5 hours, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 10-12 Kg/hour.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A02; D10-A02
IP C07D-307/33; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/10

PT P
PN CN112850753-A; CN112850753-B; WO2022166116-A1
TI Producing natural alkali comprises injecting well            water into mine, removing solid particles, neutralizing            concentrated brine, crystallizing, carbonization            reaction with carbon dioxide, causticizing, calcining            and neutralizing.
AU WANG J
   LU L
   FENG Q
AE CHINA TIANCHEN ENG CORP (CNCE-C)
   CHINA TIANCHEN ENG CORP (CNCE-C)
GA 2021619212
AB    NOVELTY - Producing natural alkali comprises injecting                well water into mine for dissolving sodium                carbonate and/or sodium bicarbonate to obtain brine                1, removing the solid particles and/or total                organic compound in brine 1 to obtain brine 2,                converting the most of sodium bicarbonate in brine                2 into sodium carbonate, neutralizing the                concentrated brine 3 by concentrated sodium                hydroxide, crystallizing to obtain sodium carbonate                decahydrate crystals with low mother liquor residue                and discharge liquid 1, mixing sodium carbonate                decahydrate crystals with solutions, concentrating                brine 4, crystallizing to obtain sodium carbonate                monohydrate crystals, subjecting brine 5 to                carbonization reaction with carbon dioxide,                cooling, crystallizing to obtain sodium bicarbonate                crystals, drying to obtain sodium bicarbonate                product, causticizing discharge liquid 1 and ash                milk, separating to obtain dilute sodium hydroxide                solution and caustic mud, washing causticizing mud,                and calcining to obtain quicklime.
   USE - The method is useful for producing natural                alkali.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realize maximum utilization of                resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing natural alkali comprises (a)                injecting well water into mine for dissolving                sodium carbonate and/or sodium bicarbonate to                obtain brine 1 containing sodium carbonate and/or                sodium bicarbonate and other soluble salts, (b)                removing the solid particles and/or total organic                compound in brine 1 to obtain brine 2, (c)                converting the most of sodium bicarbonate in brine                2 into sodium carbonate by steam stripping, and                concentrating the brine to certain concentration to                obtain concentrated brine 3, (d) neutralizing the                concentrated brine 3 by concentrated sodium                hydroxide, crystallizing at low temperature in a                sodium carbonate decahydrate crystallizer, and                separating by crystal slurry to obtain sodium                carbonate decahydrate crystals with low mother                liquor residue and discharge liquid 1, (e) mixing                the sodium carbonate decahydrate crystals with                solutions for dissolving the crystals, and dividing                the obtained solution into two parts, i.e. one is                brine 4 and other is brine 5, (f) concentrating the                brine 4 by evaporation, crystallizing in sodium                carbonate monohydrate crystallizer, then separating                by crystal slurry to obtain sodium carbonate                monohydrate crystals with low mother liquor                residue, discharging liquid 2, then drying sodium                carbonate monohydrate crystals to obtain                high-quality heavy soda ash, mixing the discharged                liquid 2 with concentrated brine 3, and then                passing to sodium carbonate decahydrate                crystallizer for crystallizing and separating                sodium carbonate decahydrate, (g) subjecting the                brine 5 to carbonization reaction with carbon                dioxide in a sodium bicarbonate crystallizer, then                cooling and crystallizing to obtain sodium                bicarbonate crystals with low mother liquor                residue, discharging the liquid 3 through crystal                separation, drying the sodium bicarbonate crystals                to obtain sodium bicarbonate product, and returning                the discharged liquid 3 to the gas stripping                concentration step for reusing and/or using in the                dissolution and extraction step, (h) causticizing                the discharge liquid 1 and ash milk, clarifying and                separating to obtain dilute sodium hydroxide                solution and caustic mud, washing the causticizing                mud, and calcining to obtain quicklime, which is                used for causticizing, and (i) concentrating the                dilute sodium hydroxide solution by evaporation,                removing the impurities to obtain concentrated                sodium hydroxide solution, and neutralizing the                residual sodium bicarbonate in concentrated brine 3                by concentrated sodium hydroxide solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The condensate obtained by                  stripping concentration and/or sodium carbonate                  monohydrate crystallization and separation can be                  used in the dissolution extraction step. The                  method further comprises removing total organic                  compound contained in the system in the discharge                  liquid 1 and/or removing total organic compound                  contained in the system in the obtained dilute                  sodium hydroxide solution or concentrated sodium                  hydroxide solution. The method comprises removing                  total organic compound by activated carbon                  adsorption method or ozone oxidation method or                  resin adsorption method. The method comprises                  using carbon dioxide produced by stripping and                  concentration step for performing carbonization                  reaction in the sodium bicarbonate                  crystallization and separation steps. The method                  comprises sequentially passing the mixture of                  concentrated brine 3 and discharge liquid 2                  through two-stage flash evaporator, crystallizing                  sodium carbonate decahydrate in the crystallizer,                  and equipping the bottom of secondary flash                  evaporator and crystallizer with a stirring                  mechanism. The method comprises using ice machine                  system for cooling during crystallization and                  separation of sodium carbonate decahydrate, and                  then using ice machine system for applying heat                  to the sodium carbonate decahydrate solution,                  where the compressor outlet condenser in the ice                  machine system is a two-stage series condenser,                  and the first-stage condenser uses sodium                  carbonate decahydrate solution containing sodium                  carbonate decahydrate crystals as a cold source.                  Preferred Components: The total alkalinity in the                  concentrated brine 3 is 22-28 wt.% as sodium                  carbonate. The solution required for dissolving                  sodium carbonate decahydrate is brine 2. The at                  least one of condensate produced in the                  crystallization and separation is sodium                  carbonate monohydrate, and first-level stripping                  liquid of stripping and concentration step. The                  raw materials required for crystallization of                  sodium bicarbonate is concentrated brine 3 or a                  mixture of concentrated brine 3 and brine 5. The                  mass concentration of concentrated sodium                  hydroxide solution produced in the evaporation                  step is 20-35 wt.%. The concentration of                  concentrated sodium hydroxide is 25-30 wt.%. The                  equipment in the step of crystallization and                  separation of sodium carbonate decahydrate                  comprises two-stage flash evaporator and                  first-stage crystallizer.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC A12-W11D; E11-Q01B; E33-B; E34-D01

PN US2021130698-A1; US11225609-B2
TI Forming many synthesis gas compositions involve e.g. exposing feedstock comprising mixture of biomass and plastic waste to pyrolysis conditions at temperature to get at least low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
AU SUNDARAM N
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202145134K
AB    NOVELTY - Forming many synthesis gas compositions, comprises: exposing a feedstock comprising a mixture of biomass and plastic waste to pyrolysis conditions at a temperature of 500-1200degrees Celsius to form at least a low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; exposing at least a portion of the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream to an adsorbent at a first adsorbing temperature of 300-600degrees Celsius and a first adsorbing pressure of greater than or equal to1 MPa/g to form a hydrogen-enriched stream comprising a hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of greater than or equal to10; combining a first portion of the hydrogen-enriched stream with a first bypass portion of the low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream to form a first product (163) synthesis gas stream; and combining a second portion of the hydrogen-enriched stream with a second bypass portion of the low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream to form a second product (167) synthesis gas stream.
   USE - The method is useful for: forming many synthesis gas compositions (claimed); and co-processing of plastic waste with biomass to generate gas phase product streams with improved properties.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: exhibits high temperature swing adsorption process integrated with a pyrolysis process, gasification process or other thermal conversion process; and allows for production of multiple product streams having desirable ratios of hydrogen to carbon oxides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Forming many synthesis gas compositions, comprises: exposing a feedstock comprising a mixture of biomass and plastic waste to pyrolysis conditions at a temperature of 500-1200degrees Celsius to form at least a low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, where the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream comprises a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of 0.5-1.7; exposing at least a portion of the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream to an adsorbent at a first adsorbing temperature of 300-600degrees Celsius and a first adsorbing pressure of greater than or equal to1 MPa/g to form a hydrogen-enriched stream comprising a hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of greater than or equal to10; combining a first portion of the hydrogen-enriched stream with a first bypass portion of the low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream to form a first product (163) synthesis gas stream comprising a first hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of greater than or equal to1.8; and combining a second portion of the hydrogen-enriched stream with a second bypass portion of the low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream to form a second product (167) synthesis gas stream comprising a second hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of greater than or equal to1.8.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a process flow for performing high temperature pressure swing adsorption on the synthesis gas portion of the gas phase effluent from a gasification process.
   Biomass feed (112)
   Plastic waste feed (114)
   Gas phase effluent (125)
   First product (163)
   Second product (167)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pyrolysis conditions comprise gasification conditions. The first product synthesis gas stream comprises an S-ratio of 1.8-2.1 and/or the second product synthesis gas stream comprises an M-ratio of 1.9-2.2. The feedstock is exposed to the pyrolysis conditions in a reactor by flowing the feedstock in a first direction within the reactor, where the method further comprises regenerating the reactor by performing combustion in the reactor, and the regenerating comprises flowing the combustion products within the reactor in a second direction different from the first direction. The method further comprises: desorbing carbon dioxide from the adsorbent at a pressure of less than or equal to5 MPa/g; physically processing at least a portion of the plastic waste and/or at least a portion of the biomass, prior to exposing the feedstock to the thermal conversion conditions to form particles having a median particle size of less than or equal to3 mm; exposing a second feedstock comprising a mixture of biomass and plastic waste to pyrolysis conditions at a temperature of 500-1200degrees Celsius to form at least a second low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, where the second low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream comprises (i) a second hydrogen content that differs from a hydrogen content of the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream by greater than or equal to10 vol.% and/or (ii) a second carbon dioxide content that differs from a carbon dioxide content of the low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream by greater than or equal to10 vol.%; exposing at least a portion of the second low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream to an adsorbent at a second adsorbing temperature of 300-600degrees Celsius and a second adsorbing pressure of greater than or equal to1 MPa/g to form a second hydrogen-enriched stream comprising a hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of greater than or equal to10, the second adsorbing temperature differing from the first temperature by less than or equal to10degrees Celsius and the second adsorbing pressure differing from the first pressure by less than or equal to100 kPa; combining a first portion of the second hydrogen-enriched stream with a third bypass portion of the low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream to form a third product synthesis gas stream comprising a first hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of greater than or equal to1.8; and combining a second portion of the hydrogen-enriched stream with a fourth bypass portion of the low hydrogen content synthesis gas stream to form a fourth product synthesis gas stream comprising a second hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of greater than or equal to1.8. Desorbing carbon dioxide from the adsorbent comprises exposing the adsorbent to a purge stream of water. Exposing at least a portion of the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream to an adsorbent comprises performing a water gas shift reaction on the at least a portion of the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream. At least a portion of the water for performing the water gas shift reaction comprises water desorbed from the adsorbent. Exposing a feedstock comprising a mixture of biomass and plastic waste to pyrolysis conditions comprises forming a pyrolysis effluent comprising greater than or equal to10 vol.% hydrocarbons, where the method further comprises separating the pyrolysis effluent to form the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream. Separating the pyrolysis effluent to form the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream comprises forming a low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream with a water content of less than or equal to10 vol.%. The low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas is formed and exposed to the adsorbent without exposing the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas to a temperature of less than or equal to100degrees Celsius.Preferred Components: The feedstock further comprises at least one of a solvent and a carrier. The mixed metal oxide adsorbent comprises an alkali metal carbonate and/or an alkaline earth metal carbonate.Preferred Composition: The hydrogen-enriched stream comprises less than or equal to5 mol.% carbon monoxide and/or less than or equal to10 mol.% carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent comprises a mixed metal oxide adsorbent. The mixed metal oxide adsorbent comprises an alkaline earth oxide and/or a transition metal oxide.Preferred Composition: The feedstock comprises greater than or equal to51 wt.% biomass relative to a combined weight of the biomass and the plastic waste. The at least a portion of the low-hydrogen-content synthesis gas stream comprises greater than or equal to10 mol.% water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The greenhouse fully uses the passive solar technology, on the one hand, by solar energy, power supply mode to ensure the normal operation of the device in the shed, can automatically detect the temperature in the shed, soil humidity; illumination intensity and carbon dioxide concentration and other environment parameters, and realizing the linkage control of the related device through the control system; on the other hand, based on the photoluminescence principle, using the photoluminescence material to light and shine the characteristic to meet the requirement of the plant growth to the illumination intensity, and promoting the photosynthetic function of the plant to a certain degree; inhibiting respiratory effect of plant, finally realizing early yield of crops, yield and yield, meeting modern agricultural energy-saving and environment-friendly high-efficiency idea.

PN CN112457726-A
TI Manufacturing a discoloration-resistant spray material comprises polyvinyl alcohol, nano zinc oxide antibacterial agent, film-forming agent, defoamer, thickener, shell powder, mica powder, manganese naphthenate, and hydrophobic amino acid.
AU ZHOU L
   HUANG Y
AE DEQING PINGAO NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (DEQI-Non-standard)
GA 202126233Q
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing a discoloration-resistant spray material comprises 100-300 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, 5-15 pts. wt. nano zinc oxide antibacterial agent, 3-7 pts. wt. film-forming agent, 1-3 pts. wt. defoamer, 1-5 pts. wt. thickener, 50-150 pts. wt. shell powder, 50-150 pts. wt. mica powder, 10-20 pts. wt. manganese naphthenate, 50-70 pts. wt. hydrophobic amino acid, 20-40 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 6-10 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 100-200 pts. wt. deionized water, 10-20 pts. wt. carbon dioxide adsorbent, 5-10 pts. wt. cerium, and 5-10 pts. wt. calcium, (S2) pouring the polyvinyl alcohol, ionized water and titanium dioxide into a mixer for stirring, pouring half of the defoamer, and mixing well to obtain mixture A, (S3) pouring manganese naphthenate into the mixture A, stirring, and pouring the hydrophobic amino acid until it is evenly mixed to obtain mixture B, (S4) pouring the shell powder and mica powder into the blender, and introducing the remaining deionized water into the blender.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing a discoloration-resistant spray material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the waterproof performance of the spray material, and can expand its volume many times after montmorillonite contacts with water, and shrinks its volume after heating and dehydrating. The spray material has strong adsorption capacity and cation exchange performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing a discoloration-resistant spray material comprises 100-300 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, 5-15 pts. wt. nano zinc oxide antibacterial agent, 3-7 pts. wt. film-forming agent, 1-3 pts. wt. defoamer, 1-5 pts. wt. thickener, 50-150 pts. wt. shell powder, 50-150 pts. wt. mica powder, 10-20 pts. wt. manganese naphthenate, 50-70 pts. wt. hydrophobic amino acid, 20-40 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 6-10 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 100-200 pts. wt. deionized water, 10-20 pts. wt. carbon dioxide adsorbent, 5-10 pts. wt. cerium, and 5-10 pts. wt. calcium, (S2) pouring the polyvinyl alcohol, half of the ionized water and titanium dioxide into a mixer for stirring, pouring half of the defoamer while stirring, and mixing well to obtain mixture A, (S3) pouring manganese naphthenate into the mixture A, stirring, and pouring the hydrophobic amino acid until it is evenly mixed to obtain mixture B, (S4) pouring the shell powder and mica powder into the blender, and introducing the remaining deionized water into the blender and stirring until the mixture is uniform to obtain mixture C, (S5) filtering the mixture C to obtain the mixture D, (S6) adding the mixture B and the mixture D into a blender in S6 for stirring, pouring the nano-zinc oxide antibacterial agent into the mixer, and stirring to obtain the mixture E, (S7) pouring film-forming agent, thickener and montmorillonite into the mixture E in sequence, and evenly sprinkling the remaining defoamer many times in the process of mixing, and mixing uniformly to obtain the semi-finished coating, and (S8) adding 10-20 pts. wt. carbon dioxide adsorbent, 5-10 pts. wt. cerium, and 5-10 pts. wt. calcium to the semi-finished coating, stirring at a uniform speed, stirring evenly and filtering through a filter to obtain the finished coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The film forming agent is acrylic resin film forming agent or butadiene resin film forming agent. The shell powder, mica powder and titanium dioxide all need to be screened through a 200-mesh screen. The filters used in the filtering in the (S5) and (S8) steps are all 300 mesh filters. Preferred Method: In the steps (S1) and (S2), the stirring speed of the stirrer is 1200-1500 revolutions per minute, the stirring time is 30-40 minutes, and the heating temperature of the stirrer is 30-60 degrees C. In the steps (S4) and (S6), the stirring speed of the mixer is 500-800 revolutions per minute, and the stirring time is 20-35 minutes. In the mixer in the step S7, the stirring speed is 700-900 revolutions per minute, and the stirring time is 60-80 minutes.

PN CN112108113-A
TI Preparing carbon nanotube/graphene/nickel nanocomposite material useful for absorbing organic solvent or grease in water comprises dissolving nickel source in the carbon source solution, transferring, and purging with argon.
AU LI G
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG A & F (UZAF-C)
GA 2020D00220
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon nanotube/graphene/nickel nanocomposite material comprises adopting use of soluble divalent nickel salt as the nickel source and carbon-containing plant material as the carbon source, dissolving nickel source in the carbon source solution, stirring for 30 minutes, transferring the stirred solution to a microwave reactor, keeping at 150-180 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, collecting product by filtration, washing with deionized water and ethanol, drying in oven at 110-120 degrees C for 8-12 hours to obtain carbon-coated nickel nanoparticles, loading carbon-coated nickel nanoparticles into a tubular reactor, purging with argon with purity of 99.99% to replace the gas in the tubular reactor, setting temperature of the tubular reactor to 800-900 degrees C, maintaining for 0.5-1.5 hours, setting temperature of the tubular reactor to 350-500 degrees C to obtain graphene package coated nickel nanoparticles, and adopting use of mixed gas of methane and carbon dioxide to purge the reaction system.
   USE - The nanocomposite material is useful for absorbing organic solvent or grease in water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple: The nanocomposite material can be recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon nanotube/graphene/nickel nanocomposite material comprises (1) adopting use of soluble divalent nickel salt as the nickel source and carbon-containing plant material as the carbon source, dissolving nickel source in the carbon source solution and stirring for 30 minutes, (2) transferring the stirred solution to a microwave reactor, and keeping at 150-180 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, (3) collecting product by filtration, washing with deionized water and ethanol, and drying in oven at 110-120 degrees C for 8-12 hours to obtain carbon-coated nickel nanoparticles, (4) loading carbon-coated nickel nanoparticles into a tubular reactor, and purging with argon with purity of 99.99% to replace the gas in the tubular reactor, (5) setting temperature of the tubular reactor to 800-900 degrees C, maintaining for 0.5-1.5 hours, setting temperature of the tubular reactor to 350-500 degrees C to obtain graphene package coated nickel nanoparticles, (6) adopting use of mixed gas of methane and carbon dioxide to purge the reaction system, setting temperature to 650-750 degrees C under the mixed gas flow, keeping temperature for 0.5-1 hour, and lowering temperature of the tubular reactor until it is cooled to room temperature, and (7) exhausting residual gas to obtain carbon nanotube/graphene/nickel nanocomposite, where the mass ratio of the nickel source to the carbon source is 1:5-6, the mass ratio of the nickel source to the carbon source solution is 1:40-45, and the pH of carbon source solution is 3.0-3.2, in the step (2), the reaction pressure in the microwave reactor is 0.2-0.3 MPa, and the microwave power is controlled at 400-500 W, the tubular reactor is made of quartz, with an inner diameter of 25-30 cm, in step 6, the molar ratio of methane is greater than the molar ratio of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon source is cellulose or potato starch. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the reaction temperature is 180 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5 hours. In the step (3), adopting use of deionized water to ultrasonically clean once, and adopting use ethanol to ultrasonically clean twice, each time for 10 minutes. In the step (5), increasing temperature from room temperature to 900 degrees C at a rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute, cooling the reactor to 400 degrees C at a rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute. The flow rate of the argon gas is 500 ml/minute, and the purge time is 20-30 minutes. In the step (6), increasing temperature from room temperature to 700 degrees C at a rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute, cooling to room temperature at a rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute. The molar ratio of methane and carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 3:2, and the flow speed is 2000 ml/minute. The tubular reactor is provided with a gas distributor every 5-10 centimetres.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PT P
PN CN110862315-A; CN110862315-B
TI Extraction of rosemary leaf carnosic acid by            supercritical carbon dioxide includes crushing and            sieving dry rosemary leaves, adding into extraction            kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, and keeping            pressure for static extraction.
AU LUO W
   OUYANG H
   CAO W
AE HUNAN SHINEWAY ENTERPRISE CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
   HUNAN XIANWEI YANGGUANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202020919T
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of rosemary leaf carnosic acid by                supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) includes                crushing and sieving dry rosemary leaves, adding                into extraction kettle, introducing CO2, keeping                the pressure at 4-6 MPa for static extraction for                0.5-1 hour, adjusting the extracting kettle                pressure at 10-30 MPa and temperature at 35-60                degrees C, introducing supercritical CO2                continuously and circularly extracting for 2-4                hours, controlling the first separation kettle                pressure at 4-6 MPa and temperature at 30-50                degrees C, separating the rosemary essential oil,                taking the residual rosemary leaves in the                extraction kettle, adding 20-50 wt.% alcohol at                0.3-0.6 times by mass of dry rosemary leaves,                sealing and soaking for 1-3 hours in the extraction                kettle, introducing CO2, keeping the pressure at                8-12 MPa, conducting static extraction for 1-2                hours, and adjusting the extraction kettle pressure                at 22-50 MPa and temperature at 40-70 degrees                C.
   USE - The method is for extraction of rosemary leaf                carnosic acid by supercritical CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of rosemary leaf carnosic acid by                supercritical CO2 comprises crushing and sieving                dry rosemary leaves, adding into extraction kettle,                introducing CO2, keeping the pressure at 4-6 MPa                for static extraction for 0.5-1 hour, adjusting the                extracting kettle pressure at 10-30 MPa and                temperature at 35-60 degrees C, introducing                supercritical CO2 continuously and circularly                extracting for 2-4 hours, controlling the first                separation kettle pressure at 4-6 MPa and                temperature at 30-50 degrees C, separating the                rosemary essential oil, taking the residual                rosemary leaves in the extraction kettle, adding                20-50 wt.% alcohol at 0.3-0.6 times by mass of dry                rosemary leaves, sealing and soaking for 1-3 hours                in the extraction kettle, introducing CO2, keeping                the pressure at 8-12 MPa, conducting static                extraction for 1-2 hours, adjusting the extraction                kettle pressure at 22-50 MPa and temperature at                40-70 degrees C, introducing supercritical CO2                continuously and circularly extracting for 2-4                hours, where entrainer is at 50-80 wt.% of alcohol                and added at 0.2-0.5 times the amount of rosemary                leaves in each addition, and the adding frequency                is once per hour, controlling the first separation                kettle pressure at 8-15 MPa and temperature at                35-55 degrees C, second separation kettle pressure                at 4-6 MPa and temperature at 30-40 degrees C, and                rectifying column pressure at 4-15 MPa and                temperature at 30-50 degrees C, respectively                collecting the first separation kettle, second                separation kettle, and rectifying column extraction                liquid, decompressing and concentrating the                extraction liquid to obtain carnosic acid powder                with low color and odor and purity of more than                90%, combining the extraction liquid in the first                separation kettle and second separation kettle,                membrane separation and filtering, washing,                concentrating to obtain the concentrated solution,                adjusting the pH of the concentrated solution at                3.5-5, adding n-hexane for reverse phase                extraction, and vacuum drying to obtain pale yellow                carnosic acid powder having purity of more than                80%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Hydrochloric acid,                  phosphoric acid, acetic acid or citric acid is                  used for adjusting the pH of the concentrated                  solution at 3.5-5, and n-hexane, which is 5-8                  times the concentrated solution is added for                  reverse phase extraction.

PN CN110664782-A
TI Preparing medicine-loaded vesicle solution useful for treating pleural effusion comprises adding heparin to pleural effusion, centrifuging, adherent culturing, irradiating, gradient centrifugation, and adding physiological saline solution.
AU ZHAO K
AE ZHAO K (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 202006282N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medicine-loaded vesicle solution comprises adding heparin to pleural effusion, centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, then adding the pellet to a mixed solution containing Roswell Park memorial institute (RPMI) 1640 and ficoll, mixing uniformly, culturing, collecting the suspended cells, inoculating in another group of RPMI 1640 medium, performing adherent culturing to obtain adherent tumor cells, selecting adherent tumor cells under aseptic conditions, then placing in RPMI 1640 medium, combining the obtained tumor cells with culture solution, irradiating the tumor cell solution with ultraviolet rays, ultrasonically crushing the tumor cell solution, and performing gradient centrifugation to obtain tumor vesicle solution, incubating the chemotherapeutic agent solution and tumor vesicle solution to obtain a mixed solution, centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, and adding physiological saline solution to the vesicles containing chemotherapeutic agent.
   USE - The medicine-loaded vesicle solution is useful for treating pleural effusion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine-loaded vesicle solution: has homogeneity with the tumor cells so that the tumor cells can take up more medicine-loaded vesicle which has the targeted localization of homologous tumor cells, and reduces the side effects of drug-loaded vesicles; and does not cause a inhibiting effect on normal cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing medicine-loaded vesicle solution comprises (a) adding 8-10 IU/ml heparin to 50-100 ml pleural effusion, centrifuging at a rate of 800-1000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, then adding the pellet to a mixed solution containing 20-30 ml Roswell Park memorial institute (RPMI) 1640 and ficoll in a ratio of 1:1, V/V, mixing uniformly, centrifuging at a rate of 1500-2000 revolutions/minute for 10-20 minutes, adding RPMI 1640 medium, culturing in a 4-6% carbon dioxide incubator at 36-38 degrees C for 2-4 hours, collecting the suspended cells, inoculating in another group of RPMI 1640 medium, culturing in 4-6% carbon dioxide incubator at 36-38 degrees C for 18-20 hours, and then performing adherent culturing to obtain adherent tumor cells, (b) selecting adherent tumor cells under aseptic conditions, then placing in RPMI 1640 medium, expanding the culture in 4-6% carbon dioxide incubator at 36-38 degrees C, combining the obtained tumor cells with the culture solution to obtain tumor cell solution, (c) irradiating the tumor cell solution with ultraviolet rays under aseptic conditions for 36-42 hours, then ultrasonically crushing the tumor cell solution for 20-30 minutes, and performing gradient centrifugation to obtain tumor vesicle solution, (d) incubating the chemotherapeutic agent solution and tumor vesicle solution under aseptic conditions in a volume ratio of 1-2:5 at 20-25 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain a mixed solution, (e) centrifuging the mixture at a rate of 15000-20000 revolutions/minute at 2-6 degrees C for 50-70 minutes under aseptic conditions, discarding the supernatant, and the resulting pellet is a vesicle containing chemotherapeutic agent, and (f) adding physiological saline solution in a volume ratio of 1:10-12 to the vesicles containing chemotherapeutic agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The ultrasonically crushing in the step (c) is carried out at 2-6 degrees C. The gradient centrifugation in the step (c) comprises (c1) centrifuging the tumor cell solution at a rate of 1000-2000 revolutions/minute at 2-6 degrees C for 5-10 minutes after performing ultraviolet irradiation, discarding the pellet, centrifuging the supernatant at a rate of 5000-8000 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes, discarding the precipitate, and taking the supernatant as the initial vesicle solution, (c2) centrifuging the initial vesicle solution at 2-6 degrees C at a rate of 15000-20000 revolutions/minute for 50-70 minutes, discarding the supernatant, and taking the pellet as the tumor vesicle, and (c3) resuspending the tumor vesicle solution in 10-20 ml phosphate buffered saline solution, oscillating and mixing to obtain tumor vesicle solution. Preferred Components: The RPMI 1640 is RPMI 1640 containing 8-12% fetal bovine serum. The amount of cells after expanding is 1.5-2x 107. The pH of the phosphate buffered saline solution is 7.2-7.4. The chemotherapeutic agent is etoposide, 5-fluorouracil or carboplatin (CBP). The concentration of physiological saline is 0.8-1%.
   ACTIVITY - Respiratory-Gen.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-C02E1; B04-F02A; B05-A01B; B05-A03B3; B05-B02A3; B05-C01; B05-C07; B05-Z; B06-A02; B06-A03; B07-A02; B07-D11; B07-D12; B10-B02B; B10-C02; B12-M07; B14-H01; B14-K01; B14-S18; D05-H08B

PT P
PN US2019367455-A1; WO2019232010-A1; US10640467-B2; CN112204007-A; EP3784655-A1; JP2021526142-W
TI Preparing carboxy-phenyl-piperidine-carboxylic acid tert-butyl ester, comprises e.g. reacting bromo-benzaldehyde with alkyl oxobutanoate in piperidine and reacting resultant with alkali metal hydroxide to form bromo-phenyl-pentanedioic acid.
AU RAPTA M
   FILIPOV D
AE THERAVANCE BIOPHARMA R & D IP LLC (THRX-C)
   THERAVANCE BIOPHARMA R & D IP LLC (THRX-C)
   THERAVANCE BIOPHARMA R & D IP LLC (THRX-C)
   SCHWANN BIOPHARMACEUTICAL RES & DEV IP (SCHW-Non-standard)
   THERAVANCE BIOPHARMA R & D IP LLC (THRX-C)
   THERAVANCE BIOPHARMA R & D IP LLC (THRX-C)
GA 2019A16529
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (P1) 4-(2-carboxy-phenyl)-piperidine-1-carboxylic acid tert-butyl ester (I), comprises: (a) reacting 2-bromo-benzaldehyde (1) with a 1-6C alkyl 3-oxobutanoate in the presence of piperidine to form substituted 2,4-diacetyl-3-(2-bromo-phenyl)-pentanedioic acid compounds (2); (b) reacting (2) with an alkali metal hydroxide, after acidification of the reaction product with an acid, to obtain 3-(2-bromo-phenyl)-pentanedioic acid (3); (c) reacting (3) with an ammonia reagent to form 4-(2-bromo-phenyl)-piperidine-2,6-dione (4); (d) reacting (4) with a reducing agent to form 4-(2-bromo-phenyl)-piperidine (5); (e) reacting (5) with di-tert-butyl dicarbonate to form 4-(2-bromo-phenyl)-piperidine-1-carboxylic acid tert-butyl ester (6); (f) reacting (6) with an alkyl lithium reagent to form (2-(1-(tert-butoxycarbonyl)piperidin-4-yl)phenyl)lithium (7); and (g) reacting (7) with carbon dioxide to form (I).
   USE - The processes are useful for preparing (I) which is useful as an intermediate for preparing ampreloxetine or its salts for treating neurogenic orthostatic hypotension. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes utilize the steps (d), (d1) (e) and (e1) that are conducted in the same reaction mixture without isolation of the product from step (d), thus generating less waste and improving the overall efficiency and yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (P1) 4-(2-carboxy-phenyl)-piperidine-1-carboxylic acid tert-butyl ester of formula (I), comprises: (a) reacting 2-bromo-benzaldehyde of formula (1) with a 1-6C alkyl 3-oxobutanoate in the presence of piperidine to form substituted 2,4-diacetyl-3-(2-bromo-phenyl)-pentanedioic acid compounds of formula (2); (b) reacting (2) with an alkali metal hydroxide, after acidification of the reaction product with an acid, to obtain 3-(2-bromo-phenyl)-pentanedioic acid of formula (3); (c) reacting (3) with an ammonia reagent to form 4-(2-bromo-phenyl)-piperidine-2,6-dione of formula (4); (d) reacting (4) with a reducing agent to form 4-(2-bromo-phenyl)-piperidine of formula (5) or its salts; (e) reacting (5) or its salts with di-tert-butyl dicarbonate to form 4-(2-bromo-phenyl)-piperidine-1-carboxylic acid tert-butyl ester of formula (6); (f) reacting (6) with an alkyl lithium reagent to form (2-(1-(tert-butoxycarbonyl)piperidin-4-yl)phenyl)lithium of formula (7); and (g) reacting (7) with carbon dioxide to form (I).
   R = 1-6C alkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing (P2) (I), comprising (a1) reacting (1) with ethyl 3-oxobutanoate in the presence of piperidine to form 2,4-diacetyl-3-(2-bromo-phenyl)-pentanedioic acid diethyl ester (2a), (b1) reacting (2a) with potassium hydroxide, after acidification of the reaction product with hydrochloric acid, to obtain (3), (c1) reacting (3) with urea to form (4), (d1) reacting (4) with sodium borohydride/boron trifluoride tetrahydrofuan complex to form (5), (e1) reacting (5) with di-tert-butyl dicarbonate to form (6), (f1) reacting (6) with n-butyl reagent lithium to form (7), and (g1) reacting (7) with carbon dioxide to form (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal hydroxide comprises potassium hydroxide (preferred), lithium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide. The acid comprises hydrochloric acid (preferred), hydrobromic acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid or acetic acid. The ammonia reagent comprises urea (preferred), ammonia, ammonium hydroxide, ammonium chloride or magnesium nitride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - In the process (P2), step (d1) and step (e1) are conducted in the same reaction mixture without isolation of the product of step (d1). Preferred Components: The reducing agent comprises sodium borohydride/boron trifluoride tetrahydrofuran complex (preferred) or lithium aluminum hydride. 13. The alkyl lithium reagent comprises n-butyl lithium (preferred) or tert-butyl lithium.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R = methyl or ethyl.
   ACTIVITY - Hypertensive.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor.
   EXAMPLE - To a solution of 13.5 kg tert-butyl 4-(2-bromophenyl)piperidine-1-carboxylate in 80 kg anhydrous tetrahydrofuran (THF) was added dropwise 13.75 kg n-butyl lithium (2.5 M in n-hexane) at -85 degrees C to -72 degrees C over a period of 1 hour under dry nitrogen. Resulting mixture was stirred at -75 degrees C to -70 degrees C for 2 hours. 10 kg Dry ice was added portion-wise at -80 degrees C to -69 degrees Cover a period of 2 hours under dry nitrogen. Mixture was warmed to -58 degrees C over a period of 12 hours. 50 kg Water was added dropwise while keeping the reaction temperature below 10 degrees C. Mixture was then concentrated under vacuum at 35-45 degrees C to remove THF (-60 kg). Residue was adjusted to pH 10-11 with 18.5 kg of 10% aqueous sodium hydroxide while keeping the mixture temperature below 20 degrees C. Mixture was extracted with 2x 50 kg of MTBE. Aqueous layer was acidified to pH of 2-3 with aqueous 40 kg 3 N hydrochloric acid, while keeping the mixture temperature below 10 degrees C. 50 kg Ethyl acetate was added and the mixture was stirred for 30 minutes. Aqueous layer was separated and extracted with 2x 30 kg ethyl acetate. Combined organic layers were washed with an aqueous solution of 1x 5 kg sodium chloride in 1x 15 kg water, dried with 5 kg sodium sulfate and filtered. Filtrate was concentrated to a volume of 40 l during which time, a precipitate formed. 80 l Petroleum ether was added drop-wise at 60 degrees C and the resulting mixture was allowed to cool to room temperature. Mixture was filtered obtain 4-(2-carboxy-phenyl)-piperidine-1-carboxylic acid tert-butyl ester with a yield of 3.95 Kg.
DC B03 (Other heterocyclics.)

PT P
PN JP6607584-B1; JP2020070222-A
TI Lime cake baking system used for producing lithium storage battery comprises kiln which bakes high water content lime cake supplied from lime cake drying machine, heat exchanger, and dust collector of powder form calcium oxide.
AU SETO H
AE SETECH KK (SETE-Non-standard)
GA 2019974421
AB    NOVELTY - A lime cake baking system comprises kiln (3) which bakes high water content lime cake supplied from lime cake drying machine which dries high water content lime cake, and lime cake drying machine (2), and thermally decomposes to calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2); heat exchanger (6) which is provided in latter stage of waste gas discharge unit in the kiln where the air supplied from low temperature side of heat exchanger is heated, and is set as combustion air for kilns with mixed gas introduced into high temperature side of heat exchanger while introducing combustion air into kiln; and dust collector of powder form calcium oxide (7) (CaO) which is installed in latter stage of heat exchanger and separates collection of calcium oxide from mixed gas. The mixed gas after separation collection is introduced into lime cake drying machine. The improvement of dry heat transfer efficiency is aimed at by direct-contact with mixed gas and lime cake.
   USE - A lime cake baking system used for production of lithium storage battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has high removal performance, such as smoke-emitting dry-type desulfurization and desalination, and reduced usage amount of slaked lime. The lime cake discharged in purification process can be used for farmland soil improvement material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A lime cake baking system comprises kiln (3) which bakes high water content lime cake supplied from lime cake drying machine (2) which dries high water content lime cake, and lime cake drying machine, and thermally decomposes to calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2); heat exchanger (6) which is provided in latter stage of waste gas discharge unit in the kiln where the air supplied from low temperature side of heat exchanger is heated, and is set as combustion air for kilns with mixed gas introduced into high temperature side of heat exchanger while introducing combustion air into kiln; and dust collector of powder form calcium oxide (7) (CaO) which is installed in latter stage of heat exchanger and separates collection of calcium oxide from mixed gas. The mixed gas after separation collection is introduced into lime cake drying machine. The improvement of dry heat transfer efficiency is aimed at by direct-contact with mixed gas and lime cake. In order to prevent the re-carbonation by calcium oxide (CaO) and carbondioxide (CO2) which are contained in baking waste gas discharged from the kiln, air is mixed and quickly cooled to the baking waste gas in waste gas discharge portion of kiln while the temperature of mixed gas is less than or equal to 550 degrees C, partial pressure of carbon dioxide (CO2) in mixed is less than or equal to 0.012 MPa.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the baking system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Lime cake drying machine (2)
   Kiln (3)
   Fuel supply unit (4)
   Heat exchanger (6)
   Dust collector of powder form calcium oxide (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The mixed gas of baking waste gas and air is used as high temperature side thermofluid of heat exchanger while aiming at re-carbonation adhering prevention of calcium oxide (CaO) by making into a flow path the thin-tube group which comprises flow path by a straight tube portion; to-be-heated side air divides a heat-exchanger-tube case portion into a multiple cell; letting the flow path which carries out heat recovery of outer surface orthogonal to multiple stages orthogonal to heat exchanger tube be a low temperature side flow path; and the heat exchanger is provided with flow path and check portion for checking and removing re-carbonation deposit of straight tube.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN CN110150424-A
TI Health food having anti-oxidation effect comprises green tea extract which is prepared by super critical carbon-dioxide extraction of green tea raw material, adding distilled water as entraining agent, extracting and separating.
AU HE M
   XU G
AE JIANGSU VOCATIONAL COLLEGE MEDICINE (JSVM-C)
GA 201977412R
AB    NOVELTY - Health food having anti-oxidation effect comprises green tea extract which is prepared by supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction of green tea raw material, adding 20 wt.% distilled water as an entraining agent, extracting at 45 degrees C, separation temperature at 40 degrees C at extraction pressure of 28 mega Pascal for 4 hours. The decaffeinated tea obtained after supercritical extraction is added to distilled water in a ratio of 1:6, microwaved for 2 minutes, treated three times, filtered, and the filtrate is combined, and the filtrate is spray-dried to control the inlet air temperature of 175 degrees C and the outlet temperature of 80 degrees C, the air volume of 37 cubic meter per hour to obtain powdery substance. The preparation of the grape seed extract involves taking fresh grape fruit and separating the grape juice and the fruit residue, recycling the pomace in a polyol solution to obtain a grape extract. The obtained grape juice is added and polyol is butanediol.
   USE - Health food having anti-oxidation effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The health food having anti-oxidation effect has good synergistic effect, good anti-oxidation effect, and has exert multiple effects of two kinds of substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Health food having anti-oxidation effect comprises green tea extract which is prepared by supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction of green tea raw material, adding 20 wt.% distilled water as an entraining agent, extracting at 45 degrees C, separation temperature at 40 degrees C at extraction pressure of 28 mega Pascal for 4 hours. The decaffeinated tea obtained after supercritical extraction is added to distilled water in a ratio of 1:6, microwaved for 2 minutes, treated three times, filtered, and the filtrate is combined, and the filtrate is spray-dried to control the inlet air temperature of 175 degrees C and the outlet temperature of 80 degrees C, the air volume of 37 cubic meter per hour to obtain powdery substance. The preparation of the grape seed extract involves taking fresh grape fruit and separating the grape juice and the fruit residue, recycling the pomace in a polyol solution to obtain a grape extract. The obtained grape juice is added and polyol is butanediol. The pomace is subjected to preliminary milling under the condition of a polyol solution, and the mixed solution after the initial milling is extracted, and the extracted mixture is further milled and circulated. The mass ratio of the pomace and the polyol solution during the initial milling process is 1:2. The amount of the polyol solution added during the extraction is 8-12 times the amount of pomace. The cycle extraction time is 1.2-2.8 hours, the extraction temperature is 45-55 degrees C, and the cycle speed is 5-15 times per hour. The green tea extract prepared above and the grape seed extract are uniformly mixed, and then the mixture is subjected to electrode treatment. The electrode treatment was a lower electrode medium of a plasma apparatus in which a mixed reaction liquid was placed at a voltage of 16 kilovolt, a current of 1.2 milliampere, and a gap of 3 mm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The health food having antioxidant effect comprises an active ingredient and an excipient which is acceptable for any of the commonly used oral dosage forms of the preparation of soft capsules, hard capsules, tablets, granules or oral liquids. Preferred Conditions: The excipient is an auxiliary material used in preparing tablets by using microcrystalline cellulose and magnesium stearate.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-D02; D03-D03B; D03-H01G1; D03-H01T2B

PT P
PN CA2993683-A1; US2019240647-A1; WO2019150259-A1; US10730036-B2; AU2019213752-A1; CN111629825-A; IN202037032644-A; KR2020118424-A; SG11202007289-A1; EP3746220-A1; BR112020014951-A2; JP2021523820-W
TI Preparation of mixed oxide catalyst used in            oxidative dehydrogenation of alkane or alkene, involves            mixing aqueous solution of ammonium heptamolybdate            tetrahydrate and telluric acid and aqueous solution of            vanadyl sulfate.
AU GAO X
   BARNES M
   SIMANZHENKOV V
   SULLIVAN D
   KIM Y
   DRAG H
   DE WIT P
   WIT P D
AE NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 201968198L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst comprising mixed                oxides of molybdenum, vanadium, niobium and                tellurium involves forming (s1) aqueous solution of                ammonium heptamolybdate tetrahydrate and telluric                acid, at 30-85 degrees C and adjusting pH of the                solution to 6.5-8.5 using nitrogen-containing base                to form soluble salts of metals, stirring (s2)                pH-adjusted solution for 15 minutes or more,                adjusting (s3) the pH of the resulting solution to                4.5-5.5 using an acid and stirring the resulting                solution at 75-85 degrees C, preparing (s4) an                aqueous solution of vanadyl sulfate, mixing (s5)                the solutions of steps (s1) and (s4) to obtain a                solution, preparing (s6) a solution of oxalic acid                and niobium pentoxide hydrate (I), adding (s7) the                solution of step (s6) to solution of step (s5) to                obtain slurry of molybdenum and niobium, and                heating (s8) resulting slurry in an autoclave under                an inert gas, air, carbon dioxide and/or carbon                monoxide under pressure of more than 1 psig at                140-190 degrees C for more than 6 hours.
   USE - Preparation of catalyst comprising mixed                oxides of molybdenum, vanadium, niobium and                tellurium used in oxidative dehydrogenation of 3-6C                alkane or alkene (claimed) e.g. ethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides catalyst having high                activity, and conversion rate to ethylene of                25%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of catalyst comprising mixed                oxides of molybdenum, vanadium, niobium and                tellurium involves forming (s1) an aqueous solution                of ammonium heptamolybdate(tetrahydrate) and                telluric acid in a molar ratio of molybdenum and                tellurium of 1:0.14-0.20, at 30-85 degrees C and                adjusting pH of the solution to 6.5-8.5 using                nitrogen-containing base to form soluble salts of                the metals, stirring (s2) pH-adjusted solution for                15 minutes or more, adjusting (s3) the pH of the                resulting solution to 4.5-5.5 using an acid and                stirring the resulting solution at 75-85 degrees C                until the solution is homogeneous, preparing (s4)                an aqueous solution of vanadyl sulfate at molar                ratio of 0.30-0.50 at room temperature to 80                degrees C, mixing (s5) the solutions of steps (s1)                and (s4) to obtain a solution in the molar ratio of                vanadium and molybdenum of 1-1.67:1, preparing (s6)                a solution of oxalic acid and niobium pentoxide                hydrate of formula: Nb2O5.xH2O (I), in molar ratio                of 3:1:1-6.5:1, slowly adding (s7) the solution of                step (s6) to the solution of step (s5) to obtain                slurry of molybdenum and niobium in the molar ratio                of 5.56-7.14:1, and heating (s8) resulting slurry                in an autoclave under an inert gas, air, carbon                dioxide and/or carbon monoxide under pressure of                more than 1 psig at 140-190 degrees C for more than                6 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                method for oxidative dehydrogenation of a mixed                feed containing ethane and oxygen in volume ratio                of 70:30-95:5 and optionally one or more 3-6C                alkane or alkene and oxygenated species containing                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, which involves                passing the mixture over the catalyst at 320-385                degrees C under 0.8-7 atmospheric pressure and gas                hourly space velocity of more than 100/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The gaseous product                  species are vented from the reactor in condenser                  upstream of the autoclave outlet. The condenser                  is operated more than 0 degrees C and less than                  reaction temperature. The pressure in the                  autoclave is maintained using a liquid filled                  column, bubbler or pressure regulating                  device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aqueous slurry                  introduced into the autoclave comprises                  molybdenum, vanadium, niobium and tellurium salts                  in the molar ratio of                  1:0.40-0.70:0.14-0.18:0.14-0.20. Preferred                  Process: The hydrothermal treatment in autoclave                  is carried out at 140-180 degrees C under                  pressure of 1-200 psig for 6-60 hours. The                  heat-treated slurry from step (s8) is treated                  with 0.3-2.5 mL of 30 wt.% solution of aqueous                  hydrogen peroxide per gram of catalyst precursor.                  The precatalyst from step (s8) is separated from                  the aqueous phase and washed with distilled water                  and/or aqueous oxalic acid solution and dried in                  an oven at 70-120 degrees C for more than 6                  hours. The dried precatalyst is grounded to                  particle size of less than 125 mu m and calcined                  at 200-650 degrees C for 1-20 hours. The dried                  catalyst is pre-dried in oven at 90 degrees C for                  more than 6 hours before calcination. The                  calcined material is mixed with 0.1-10 wt.%                  niobium pentoxide hydrate (I) at 90 degrees C in                  water and dried at 300 degrees C in air. The                  catalyst in an amount of 10-95 wt.% is bounded or                  agglomerated with 5-90 wt.% binder chosen from                  acidic, basic or neutral binder slurries of                  titania, zirconia, aluminum oxide, aluminum                  oxyhydroxide and niobium pentoxide provided that                  zirconia is not used in combination with                  aluminum-containing binder. The oxidative                  dehydrogenation is carried out at less than 375                  degrees C and gas hourly space velocity of                  preferably more than 500/hour. The catalyst in                  the oxidative dehydrogenation process is capable                  of forming fixed bed. The oxidative                  dehydrogenation process has selectivity to                  ethylene of more than 90% at target ethane                  conversion of more than 25-35%, preferably                  35%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
