AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises e.g. (i) calcining limestone to obtain active lime; (ii) adding activated lime into water to obtain active calcium hydroxide emulsion and digesting with hot water; (iii) aging digested crude slurry, separating and sieving; (iv) adjusting the concentration and temperature of the refined calcium hydroxide emulsion; (v) adding a polycarboxylate dispersing agent into the lime milk; (vi) adding a crystal form controlling agent into the lime milk; (vii) introducing kiln gas containing carbon dioxide into the lime milk and carbonizing; (viii) passing nano-calcium carbonate slurry obtained through a vibrating screen, sieving, adding fatty acid treatment agent and stirring; (ix) dehydrating, drying and depolymerizing; and (x) adding active nano calcium carbonate into a high-speed mixer, adding coupling agent, stirring for 3-6 minutes when the temperature reaches 110-130 degrees C and discharging and depolymerizing material again.
   USE - The nano calcium carbonate is useful for rubber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces nano calcium carbonate with good dispersibility, compatibility with the rubber substrate and mechanical properties; and used as an excellent reinforcing filler for rubber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (i) calcining limestone to obtain active lime; (ii) adding activated lime into water to obtain active calcium hydroxide emulsion, digesting with hot water and controlling temperature of hot water at 40-60 degrees C, and ratio of gray water and water is controlled at 1:4-6 and concentration is 10-18%; (iii) aging digested crude slurry, separating by a spinning solution and sieving to obtain a refined emulsion; (iv) adjusting the concentration and temperature of the refined calcium hydroxide emulsion by adding water and heat exchange and controlling concentration at 6-11% and temperature at 18-30 degrees C; (v) adding a polycarboxylate dispersing agent into the lime milk prepared in the step (iv); (vi) adding a crystal form controlling agent into the lime milk prepared in the step (v) to control the nucleation stage of the calcium carbonate, where the dosage is 0.5-2%; (vii) introducing kiln gas containing carbon dioxide into the lime milk prepared in the step (vi), adding silicon source for inorganic coating after the end of carbonization, carbonizing carbon dioxide continuosly when detecting Ph less than or equal to 7, carbonizing continuosly to for 20-30 minutes to ensure complete reaction of calcium hydroxide particles and coating of silicon source onto the nanometer calcium carbonate particles; (viii) passing nano-calcium carbonate slurry obtained through a vibrating screen, sieving part of the coarse particles, pumping into the surface treatment tank, heating calcium carbonate slurry to 60-70 degrees C before the surface treatment, adding fatty acid treatment agent and stirring; (ix) dehydrating and drying the nano-calcium carbonate slurry treated in the step (viii) until the water content is less than 0.5%, depolymerizing and classifying to obtain active nano calcium carbonate; and (x) adding active nano calcium carbonate obtained in the step (ix) into a high-speed mixer when the temperature reaches to 100 degrees C or higher, adding coupling agent, stirring for 3-6 minutes when the temperature reaches 110-130 degrees C and discharging and depolymerizing material again.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The limestone particle size in the step (i) ranges from 4-8 cm or 8-12 cm and calcining at 950-1150 degrees C for 12-36 hours. The concentration of the crude milk obtained by the digestion in the step (ii) is 13-15%. The amount of adding treatment agent in the step (viii) accounts for 2-5 wt.% calcium carbonate and stirring for 30-120 minutes. Preferred Components: The polycarboxylate dispersing agent in the step (v) is sodium polyacrylate, the molecular weight is 4000-5000, and the amount is 0.1-0.4 wt.% of the milk of the lime. The crystal form controlling agent in the step (vi) comprises mixture of at least one citrate, maleic acid, aluminum chloride, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid -dosodium, sulfuric acid and water-soluble sulfate, and the amount is 0.8-1.5 wt.%. The silicon source in the step (vii) comprises a soluble alkali metal silicate and a silicate silicon source, the soluble alkali metal silicate comprising at least one sodium metasilicate, sodium silicate, sodium silicate, potassium silicate and ammonium silicate and silicate-based silicon source comprises at least one methyl orthosilicate, tetraethyl orthosilicate or propyl orthosilicate. Preferred Method: The drying in step (ix) is carried out at 100-130 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic pyrolysis reaction system for organic solid waste to prepare gas fuel, comprises primary drying chamber (2), second-stage drying room (28), pyrolyzing furnace (7), crusher (26), combustion chamber (24), catalytic cracking chamber (22), condenser (21), carbon dioxide tower (17) and gas (16), first level drying chamber, second-stage drying room and pyrolyzing furnace connecting in turn, pyrolyzing furnace is provided with gas outlet and solid residue outlet. The crusher is connected with combustion chamber.
   USE - Catalytic pyrolysis reaction system for organic solid waste to prepare gas fuel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic pyrolysis reaction system has wide raw material source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing gaseous fuel by catalytically pyrolyzing organic solid waste using reaction system, which involves:
   (A) adding residual organic solid waste into primary drying chamber for bio-drying process, collecting waste to remove inorganic components, bio-drying of water outlet to enter sewage through sewage processing device to carry out purification treatment, bio-gas generated by drying process by first fan into combustion chamber to burn;
   (B) subjecting biological drying organic solid waste enters secondary drying chamber for drying through conveying device, secondary drying chamber for drying in process of draining water through sewage outlet to enter sewage treatment device for purification treatment, secondary drying chamber in drying process generating gas by second blower into combustion chamber for burning;
   (C) drying by organic solid waste spiral feeding machine enters pyrolysis furnace in pyrolysis;
   (D) using vacuum pump to control vacuum degree of pyrolyzing furnace, pyrolysis reaction in oxygen-free or very low oxygen condition, organic solid waste pyrolytic carbon generated by pyrolysis of pyrolyzing furnace, stirring action of conveying device reaches solid residue outlet of pyrolysis furnace, pyrolytic carbon fed into crusher by spiral discharging machine, crushing pyrolytic carbon in crusher, then is sent to burning chamber and auxiliary fuel for burning, inorganic solid waste generated by pyrolysis in pyrolysis furnace and gas-liquid mixture enters catalytic cracking chamber by fan to secondary cracking and refining, secondary cracking refining gas into condenser by vacuum pump, saturated temperature is lower than temperature of liquid product after condensing water under pressure to prevent water vapor mixed gas, non-condensed gas discharged from condenser passes through carbon dioxide absorption tower and second bag-type dust remover to purify, gas fuel into storage bottle, liquid condensed in condenser enters oil tank, returns to catalytic cracking chamber catalytic cracking again through oil pump, combustion chamber for burning gas generated to pyrolyzing furnace and smoke pipeline of catalytic cracking chamber, pyrolysis furnace and catalytic pyrolysis chamber for heating, flue gas heating in pyrolysis furnace and catalytic pyrolysis chamber into two-stage drying chamber of bottom smoke inlet of biological drying organic solid waste after drying; and
   (E) drying flue gas after organic solid waste into first bag-type dust remover to remove dust, reaches discharge standard after entering chimney for discharging by draft fan.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic views of catalytic pyrolysis reaction system.

TI Seawater battery for secondary battery complex system, comprises cathode portion in which electrons generated upon charging are charged, anode portion in which electrons generated in cathode portion are discharged, and solid electrolyte.
AU KIM G T
   KIM J W
   KIM C M
   SHIN J Y
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
   UNIV SOOKMYUNG WOMENS IND ACAD COOP (UYSM-C)
GA 201923294E
AB    NOVELTY - A seawater battery comprises a cathode portion comprising a cathode current collector comprising a sodium metal, in which electrons generated upon charging are charged, sodium ions are stored as sodium metal, sodium metal stored during discharge is oxidized and electrons are generated, and sodium ions move from seawater to the cathode portion, and electrons move to the cathode portion, an anode portion in which electrons generated in the cathode portion are discharged, and sodium ions oxidized in the cathode portion is moved during discharge, a solid electrolyte arranged between the cathode portion and the anode portion, which allows the sodium ions to selectively pass, a seawater inlet port through which seawater flows, a gas inlet port through which gas generated during discharge is discharged, and a seawater outlet port through which the sodium carbonate is dissolved when seawater supplied to the anode portion is discharged below seawater inlet.
   USE - Seawater battery e.g. sodium seawater battery is used for secondary battery complex system (all claimed) for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The seawater battery has improved economical efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A seawater battery comprises a cathode portion comprising a cathode current collector comprising a sodium metal, in which electrons generated upon charging are charged, sodium ions are stored as sodium metal, sodium metal stored during discharge is oxidized and electrons are generated, and sodium ions move from seawater to the cathode portion, and electrons move to the cathode portion, an anode portion in which electrons generated in the cathode portion are discharged, and sodium ions oxidized in the cathode portion is moved during discharge, a solid electrolyte arranged between the cathode portion and the anode portion, which allows the sodium ions to selectively pass, a seawater inlet port through which seawater flows, a gas inlet port through which gas generated during discharge is discharged, and a seawater outlet port through which the sodium carbonate is dissolved when seawater supplied to the anode portion is discharged below seawater inlet. In the anode portion, carbon dioxide transferred from the outside during discharge is collected by sodium carbonate by reaction with the sodium ions, and is dissolved in seawater. The anode portion comprises an anode current collector impregnated in seawater. The seawater outlet port and the anode portion is directly or indirectly connected to seawater, so as to generate hydroxide ions capable of preventing acidification of seawater by chemical reaction between the dissolved carbonate ions of the sodium carbonate and seawater. In the system, twice the amount of sodium chloride is electrolyzed to sodium ions, chlorine and two electrons, sodium hydroxide is electrolyzed to sodium ions, water, oxygen and four electrons, sodium, carbonate ions and electrons combined to form sodium carbonate and carbon, sodium carbonate is electrolyzed to sodium ions and carbonate ions, carbon dioxide and water combined to form hydrogen and bicarbonate ions which give hydrogen and carbonate ions, and carbonate ions and water combined to form bicarbonate ions and hydroxide ions. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for secondary battery complex system.

GA 201913324M
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering ethane from natural gas comprises                e.g. performing decarbonization of the feed gas to                obtain carbon dioxide (CO2) regeneration gas and                purified gas, pressurizing and blending obtained                CO2 regeneration gas into the external product gas,                dehydrating the obtained purified to obtain dry                gas, cooling the obtained drying gas by the natural                gas sub-cooling box and then enters the                low-temperature separator and A part of the gas                phase separated by the low-temperature separator                enters the natural gas expander and expands into                the middle of the demethanizer tower, A part of the                gas phase enters the upper part of the demethanizer                by heat exchange and supercooling of the natural                gas main cold box and the demethanizer top gas,                performing rectification, where A part of                condensate separated by the cryogenic separator is                heat-cooled and supercooling by the natural gas                main cold box and the demethanizer top gas, and                supercooling.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering ethane                from natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realizes high CO2 (CO2 to less                than or equal to 1.4% (v/v)) raw material gas                without deCO2 treatment, or only preliminary CO2                removal treatment, which reduces engineering wages                and equipment operating energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering ethane from natural gas comprises                (i) performing decarbonization of the feed gas to                obtain carbon dioxide (CO2) regeneration gas and                purified gas; (ii) pressurizing and blending                obtained CO2 regeneration gas into the external                product gas; (iii) dehydrating the obtained                purified to obtain dry gas; (iv) cooling the                obtained drying gas by the natural gas sub-cooling                box and then enters the low-temperature separator                and A part of the gas phase separated by the                low-temperature separator enters the natural gas                expander and expands into the middle of the                demethanizer tower, A part of the gas phase enters                the upper part of the demethanizer by heat exchange                and supercooling of the natural gas main cold box                and the demethanizer top gas, performing                rectification, where A part of condensate separated                by the cryogenic separator is heat-cooled and                supercooling by the natural gas main cold box and                the demethanizer top gas, supercooling, returning                to the upper part of demethanizer, where the heavy                component in the low temperature condensate absorbs                CO2 in natural gas, and the remaining condensate                directly enters the middle of the demethanizer and                recovering low-temperature natural gas at the top                of demethanizer by natural cold box of natural gas                and then pressurizing to external pressure, A part                of the pressurized natural gas is exported as a                product natural gas, and another part of the reflux                is used as a circulating dry gas to rectify the gas                phase of the demethanizer; and (v) performing                fractionation of CO2 containing condensate to                obtain CO2 containing ethane, liquefied gas and                stable light hydrocarbons, separately transporting                the liquefied gas and stabilized light hydrocarbon                to the storage tank and storing; and (vi) obtaining                the CO2-containing ethane by fractional                distillation, which needs to be decarburized by the                alcohol amine method and resulting ethane is used                in subsequent units.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) further                  comprises pressurizing the natural gas. In the                  step (i), the operation pressure of the                  demethanizer is 0.9-3 MPa, and the raw material                  gas is decarburized by an alcohol amine method                  and purified gas CO2 content is more than 1.2%                  (v/v). In the step (i), the CO2 regeneration gas                  obtained is pressurized by the CO2 compressor,                  the blending process controls the blended                  external product gas to meet the national                  second-class gas index i.e. the CO2 content is                  greater than or equal to 3% (v/v). In the step                  (iii), the obtained purified gas is dehydrated by                  a three-column isobaric regenerated molecular                  sieve dehydration process and the moisture                  content of dry gas is more than 1 ppm. In the                  step (iv), the obtained ethane recovery comprises                  cooling dry gas to a temperature of -50 to 60                  degrees C after passing through a natural gas                  sub-cooling box, entering the cryogenic separator                  to 80-90% (v/v) of the gas phase separated by the                  cryogenic separator enters the middle of                  demethanizer after entering natural gas expander                  and expanding to below -85 degrees C and the                  remaining 10-20% (v/v) gas phase is heat-cooled                  to the top of demethanizer by the heat exchange                  between the main cold box of the natural gas and                  the top gas of the demethanizer to -90 degrees C,                  and the rectification is carried out and the                  liquid content is 10-100% (v/v) is supercooled by                  heat exchange between main cold box of natural                  gas and the top gas of demethanizer, and after                  being subcooled to below -95 degrees C, returning                  to the upper part of demethanizer, and the heavy                  component in low temperature condensate to absorb                  CO2 from natural gas and the remaining 0-90%                  (v/v) condensate directly enters the middle of                  the demethanizer, the degassing tower top gas is                  recovered by the natural gas main cold box, and                  then the natural gas external compressor is                  pressurized to the external pressure is 80-90%                  (v/v) of natural gas is exported as product                  natural gas, and the remaining 10-20% (v/v) is                  refluxed as a circulating dry gas to rectify the                  gas phase of the demethanizer. In the step (iv),                  the demethanizer top gas is a low temperature                  natural gas at the top of demethanizer and the                  temperature is greater than or equal to -102                  degrees C, and its CO2 content is greater than or                  equal to 0.5% (v / v), the temperature of the                  demethanizer overhead gas after recovering the                  cold through the cold box is less than or equal                  to 30 degrees C. In the step (iv), the liquid                  phase outlet of the cryogenic separator is                  provided with a flow distribution adjusting                  device to control the condensate ratio of 10-100%                  (v/v), the cryogenic separator has a gas phase                  supercooling ratio of 10-20% (v/v), the                  condensate outlet line of the cryogenic separator                  is provided with a temperature control device.                  The overcondensed liquid enters the position of                  demethanizer, The gas is subcooled into the tower                  position of the cryogenic separator by 2-6 trays,                  and the circulation of the natural gas returned                  to the demethanizer at the outlet of the external                  compressor is 10-20%. In the steps (iv) and (v),                  the condensate for absorbing CO2 is fractionated                  by a rectification process, the ethane fine                  decarburization and natural gas crude                  decarburization share an alcohol amine solution                  regeneration and a solution circulation                  system.

TI Inducing amplification of high killing activity            alpha-delta T cells in vitro, by preparing peripheral            blood mononuclear cell by lymphocyte separation            solution, performing inoculation and preparing            amplification culture medium.

   LI W

GA 2018A0487F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inducing amplification of high                killing activity gamma delta T cells in vitro                involves taking peripheral blood, anticoagulating                heparin, isolating the peripheral blood mononuclear                cell (PBMCs) by lymphocyte separation solution and                resuspending 2 times with PBS buffer, adding 5-10%                autologous plasma to serum-free medium, adding                gamma delta T cell expansion factor in accordance                with the measurement of 1 mu l gamma delta T cell                expansion factor per ml of medium to the serum-free                medium to obtain gamma delta T cell expansion                medium and removing the stimulating medium by                centrifugation, adjusting the cells density to 2-3x                106 cells/ml, replenishing for every 2-3 days in                accordance with the cell density of 7-9 days,                adding 2.5-7.5 mM nicotinamide in the medium,                adding gamma delta T cell expansion medium to the                cells for every 2-3 days, maintaining the density                at 2-3x 106 cells/ml, adding 2.5-7.5 mM                nicotinamide in the medium and culturing in 5%                carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for `10-14                days.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing                amplification of high killing activity gamma delta                T cells in vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to process, exhibits                strong operability and increases the purity to more                than 90% in 14 days of culture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for inducing amplification of high                killing activity gamma delta T cells in vitro                involves (a) taking peripheral blood,                anticoagulating heparin, isolating the peripheral                blood mononuclear cell (PBMCs) by lymphocyte                separation solution and resuspending 2 times with                PBS buffer, (b) adding 5-10% autologous plasma to                serum-free medium, adding gamma delta T cell                stimulating factor in accordance with the                measurement of gamma delta T cell stimulating                factor per ml of serum-free medium and 1 mu l gamma                delta T cell stimulating factor to obtain gamma                delta T cell stimulating medium, suspending PbMCs                using gamma delta T cell stimulation medium,                adjusting the density to 1-3x 106 cells/ml,                inoculating into a culture flask and culturing at                37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide, (c) adding gamma                delta T cell expansion factor in accordance with                the measurement of 1 mu l gamma delta T cell                expansion factor per ml of medium to the serum-free                medium to obtain gamma delta T cell expansion                medium and (d) removing the stimulating medium by                centrifugation, adjusting the cells density to 2-3x                106 cells/ml using the gamma delta T cell expansion                medium when culture to the 3rd day in the step (b),                replenishing for every 2-3 days in accordance with                the cell density of 7-9 days, adding 2.5-7.5 mM                nicotinamide in the medium, adding gamma delta T                cell expansion medium to the cells for every 2-3                days, maintaining the density at 2-3x 106 cells/ml,                adding 2.5-7.5 mM nicotinamide in the medium and                culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37                degrees C for `10-14 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The gamma delta T cell                  stimulating factor comprises zoledronic acid                  containing 1-10 mM, preferably 5 mM zoledronic                  acid, 2x 105-1x 106 IU/ml, preferably 6x 105                  IU/ml interleukin-2 (IL-2), 5-50 mu g/ml,                  preferably 10 mu g/ml IL-7 and 5-50 mu g/ml,                  preferably 10 mu g/ml IL-15. The gamma delta T                  cell expansion factor comprises 2x 105-1x 106                  IU/ml, preferably 6x 105 IU/ml IL-2, 5-50 mu                  g/ml, preferably 10 mu g/ml IL-7 and 5-50 mu                  g/ml, preferably 10 mu g/ml IL-15. Preferred                  Method: The step (c) involves adding 5-10                  volume/volume% autologous plasma to the                  serum-free medium. Preferred Composition: The                  amount of the nicotinamide in the medium is                  2.5-7.5 mM.

PN WO2018058190-A1
TI Bio-electrochemical system for removing organic contaminants and recovering caustic soda from alkaline liquid contains electrochemically active microorganism which catalyzes oxidation of organic contaminant to carbon dioxide and bioreactor.
AU CHENG K Y
   KAKSONEN A
   GINIGE M
   WEERASINGHE MOHOTTIGE T
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 2018261291
AB    NOVELTY - A bio-electrochemical system (10) for removing organic contaminants and recovering caustic soda from alkaline liquid comprises anodic chamber (14) which has anode (16) and receives alkaline liquid; greater than or equal to 1 electrochemically active microorganism which is disposed in proximity to anode and catalyzes oxidation of organic contaminants to carbon dioxide to remove organic contaminants from alkaline liquid and produce partially treated alkaline liquid; cathodic chamber (18) which has cathode arranged to produce hydroxide ions; and bioreactor (36) which is connected with anodic and cathodic chambers.
   USE - A bio-electrochemical system for removing organic contaminants and recovering caustic soda from alkaline liquid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Integration of bioreactor with dual chamber electrolytic cell facilitates return of stream of treated alkaline liquid and recovered caustic soda to alkaline liquor process circuit, thus, need to source alternate liquor and associated operating costs are reduced by lowering consumption of reagents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A bio-electrochemical system (10) for removing organic contaminants and recovering caustic soda from alkaline liquid comprises anodic chamber (14) which has anode (16) and receives alkaline liquid; greater than or equal to 1 electrochemically active microorganism which is disposed in proximity to anode and catalyzes oxidation of organic contaminants to carbon dioxide to remove organic contaminants from alkaline liquid and produce partially treated alkaline liquid; cathodic chamber (18) which has cathode arranged to produce hydroxide ions; cation selective membrane which separates anodic and cathodic chambers and allow cations to migrate from anodic chamber to cathodic chamber where hydroxide ions and cations in cathodic chamber produce caustic soda; bioreactor (36) which is connected with anodic chamber in arrangement where bioreactor receives partially treated alkaline liquid and facilitates biodegradation of remaining organic contaminants in partially treated alkaline liquid to produce treated alkaline liquid, and connected with cathodic chamber in arrangement where bioreactor discharges portion of treated alkaline liquid to cathodic chamber; and greater than or equal to 1 power source which applies voltage between anode and cathode. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for removing organic contaminants and recovering caustic soda from alkaline liquid which involves providing electrolytic cell (12) having anodic chamber and cathodic chamber separated by cation selective membrane; treating alkaline liquid by contacting alkaline liquid with anode and greater than or equal to 1 electrochemically active microorganism in anodic chamber; and applying voltage to electrolytic cell sufficient to (i) oxidize organic contaminants in anodic chamber to remove organic contaminants from alkaline solution to produce partially treated alkaline liquid, (ii) produce hydroxide ions in cathodic chamber and (iii) migrates cations from anodic chamber to cathodic chamber to produce caustic soda solution in cathodic chamber; passing partially treated alkaline liquid to bioreactor to facilitate biodegradation of remaining organic contaminants in partially treated alkaline liquid to produce treated alkaline liquid, and, optionally, mixing portion of treated alkaline liquid with caustic soda solution produced in cathodic chamber to produce alkaline liquid having recovered caustic soda.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for removing organic contaminants and recovering caustic soda from highly alkaline solutions.
   Bio-electrochemical system (10)
   Electrolytic cell (12)
   Anodic chamber (14)
   Anode (16)
   Cathodic chamber (18)
   Bioreactor (36)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: Electrochemically active microorganism is capable of catalyzing greater than or equal to 1 organic compounds selected from acetate, oxalate, succinate, malonate, formate compound or salts. Electrochemically active microorganism contains electrochemically active microorganism originating from culture collection, activated sludge, bioreactor or natural or engineered environment, preferably Proteobacteria, Firmicutes, Bacterioidetes, Tenencutes, and Actinobacteria.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: Cathodic chamber discharges stream containing treated alkaline liquid and recovers caustic soda into alkaline liquid circuit, pond or another location, or discharges recovered caustic soda into an alkaline liquid circuit, pond or another location. Bioreactor discharges treated alkaline liquid to alkaline liquid circuit, pond or another location. Anodic chamber and/or bioreactor receive diluent and/or greater than or equal to 1 nutrients. Preferred Parameters: Voltage applied to electrolytic cell is 0-5000 mV. Potential applied to anode is - 600 to 600 mV vs silver/silver chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Partially treated alkaline liquid is mixed with diluent and/or greater than or equal to 1 nutrients in bioreactor before passing partially treated alkaline liquid to bioreactor. Alkaline liquid is mixed with diluent and/or greater than or equal to 1 nutrients in anodic chamber before treating alkaline liquid in anodic chamber. Preferred Components: Anode and cathode are graphite granules. Cation selective membrane is sodium selective membrane.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN CN107774083-A
TI Full-temperature absorptive extracting, dehydrating, removing impurity and purifying olefin comprises e.g. gas phase adsorption, extracting and carrying out desorption, introducing water vapor and discharging condensed water.
AU CHEN Y
   LIU K
   CAI Y
   ZHONG Y
AE SICHUAN TIANCAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018210369
AB    NOVELTY - Full-temperature absorptive extracting, dehydrating, removing impurity and purifying olefin comprises e.g. gas phase adsorption, extracting and carrying out desorption using near-critical or supercritical carbon dioxide as an extractant, introducing the water vapor obtained in the extraction/desorption step into separation kettle of the extractant regeneration step, and discharging the condensed water containing trace impurities from the bottom of the tank.
   USE - The method is useful for full-temperature absorptive extracting, dehydrating, removing impurity and purifying olefin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safe and stable; consumes low energy; ensures high product purity; realizes low cost device investment and greater than or equal to 99% yield. The olefin has greater than or equal to 99.9-99.99% product purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Full-temperature absorptive extracting, dehydrating, removing impurity and purifying olefin comprises (1) through pre-processing unit removing acetylene, oxygen, carbon monoxide, arsenic-mercury and acid gases after feeding crude olefin gas, where the temperature is 30-120 degrees C, normal pressure is 3 MPa, introducing into absorbing tower, absorbing under 30-120 degrees C absorbing temperature and normal pressure to 3 MPa adsorption pressure, filling water and micro impurity as adsorbate, adsorbent in the adsorption tower is adsorbed, and entering into next step after the adsorption is saturated, olefin not adsorbed but flows out from the top of the adsorption tower to obtain olefin products with an olefin content of more than 99.9-99.99% (vol./vol.), when the adsorption of one adsorption tower is completed and the process of extraction and desorption is completed, the other adsorption tower undergoes extraction and desorption and then enters the liquid-phase adsorption process to realize the continuous cycle adsorption operation, (2) extracting and carrying out desorption using near-critical or supercritical carbon dioxide as an extractant, the extraction agent temperature is 30-120 degrees C, the pressure is 6-8 MPa, and the adsorption desorption from the top of the column leads to the saturated adsorption tower, the extraction and desorption temperature is the same as the adsorption operation temperature, and the extraction and desorption operation pressure is 6-8 MPa, the agent extracts and dissolves the adsorbed water and trace impurities from the surface and channels of the adsorbent to form desorption gas enriched in water, referred to as water vapor, and the regeneration agent regeneration process, after extraction desorption step is completed, feeding olefin feed gas, and introducing gas phase adsorption process, carrying out cycle operation, and (3) introducing the water vapor obtained in the extraction/desorption step into separation kettle of the extractant regeneration step, reducing the pressure to less than or equal to 6 MPa, the temperature to 30-120 degrees C, discharging the condensed water containing trace impurities from the bottom of the tank, the carbon dioxide escapes from the top of the kettle and is pressurized or directly used as an extractant before entering the extraction and desorption process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the flow chart for full-temperature absorptive extracting, dehydrating, removing impurity and purifying olefin (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent loaded in the adsorption tower of the gas phase adsorption process is activated alumina, activated carbon and molecular sieves, the gas phase adsorption process may be composed of more than two adsorption towers in parallel, and is suitable for the treatment of olefin feedstock gas, and scale is greater than 100,000 standard/hour operating conditions. The extractant in the extraction/desorption process may use other substances other than critical carbon dioxide, e.g. critical carbon (III) component, methanol, ethanol, low carbon mixed alcohol ether ester and critical water. The water vapor and condensed water obtained in the extraction and desorption steps and the extractant regeneration step contain trace amounts of impurities, including trace amount of small molecular weight oxygen compounds produced in the olefinic gas, which can be adsorbed by the adsorbent together with water, and the impurity components dissolved by the critical carbon dioxide extractant, e.g. methanol, dimethyl ether, ethanol, acetaldehyde, ketone, acetone, formic acid, formaldehyde-acetaldehyde, methyl formate, methyl acetate and ethyl acetate. Preferred Method: In the method, the gas phase adsorption process crude olefin feed gas is treated by a pretreatment unit, including palladium catalysts for catalytic hydrogenation of acetylenic hydrocarbons, copper or manganese catalysts for catalytic deoxidation, copper catalysts for catalytic carbon monoxide, solid bases/organic amines remove acid gases, and catalytically removing arsenic/mercury. In the method, the gas phase adsorption process crude olefin feed gas can be derived from the steam cracking of petroleum (light oil, heavy oil, light hydrocarbon gas, shale gas), light hydrocarbon dehydrogenation, coal gasification, and methanol synthesis, and olefin produced by ethanol condensation dehydration, mainly based on ethylene propylene. The operating temperature and pressure of the extraction and desorption process depend on the type of extractant selected, where selection of critical propylene as an extractant, no need to go through the extractant regeneration process, and recycle directly after treatment. In the method, the water vapor obtained in the extraction and desorption process enters the separation kettle of the extractant regeneration process, when critical carbon dioxide is selected as the extractant, the pressure is reduced to 3-6 MPa, separating the bottom of the kettle results in a water mixture containing a small amount of olefins, can be returned to the gas phase adsorption process to further recover olefins, and further removing dehydration impurity, further reducing the operating pressure of the separation vessel to atmospheric pressure, discharging all the water and trace impurities from the bottom of the vessel, the carbon dioxide escapes from the top of the column and used as an extractant after being pressurized to produce critical carbon dioxide, and returning the process to the extraction/desorption process and recycling. In the method, the gas phase adsorption step can be changed to the liquid phase adsorption step, and the feed material is changed from crude olefin gas to crude olefin liquid. In the method, the olefin product flowing out in the gas-phase or liquid-phase adsorption process enters a solid alkali or one-time precise carbon dioxide removal unit, removes the trace extractant by the olefin product, and further ensures the purity of the olefin product.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

The controller saves utilization history associated with person in the vehicle and amount of recovery as data. PN CN111847911-A; CN111847911-B
TI Control method of dual-chamber lime kiln system involves controlling carbon dioxide concentration test equipment, obtaining carbon dioxide concentration data, calculating carbon dioxide concentration derivative, and reversing.
AU LIU Q
   ZHOU H
AE ZHONGYE CHANGTIAN INT ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 2020A9614C
AB    NOVELTY - Control method of dual-chamber lime kiln system involves controlling carbon dioxide concentration test equipment, obtaining carbon dioxide concentration data in a flue gas connection channel, calculating carbon dioxide concentration derivative based on the carbon dioxide concentration data, judging whether the carbon dioxide concentration derivative satisfies a preset reversing condition, and initiating reversing operation when the carbon dioxide concentration derivative satisfies the preset reversing condition. The reversing operation includes changing the open state or closed state of an interface (I2), an interface (I3), an interface (I4) and an interface (I5).
   USE - Control method of dual-chamber lime kiln system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts the dual-chamber lime kiln system working state of a calcination zone of lime kiln main components at suitable time, and provides high-quality quicklime while saving energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the dual-chamber lime kiln system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The reversing operation further includes reversing the charging of limestone and reversing fuel injection. The preset reversing condition include carbon dioxide concentration derivative of 0. The carbon dioxide concentration derivative before reversing, is less than 0.

PT P
PN CN110015940-A; CN110015940-B
TI Dehydrogenation of methylcyclohexane to produce toluene and methane under carbon dioxide atmosphere comprises introducing methylcyclohexane and carbon dioxide in reactor and reacting.
AU WANG Y

GA 201963771K
AB    NOVELTY - Dehydrogenation of methylcyclohexane to produce toluene and methane under carbon dioxide atmosphere comprises introducing methylcyclohexane and carbon dioxide in reactor, reacting at 100-500 degrees C under 0.1-2 MPa and reacting catalyst bed of isothermal device to form toluene and methane. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide and methylcyclohexane is 1:1-1:10. The carbon dioxide mixed injection volume airspeed is 100-1000 per hour. The catalyst is gamma -aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide, silicon dioxide, mordenite, Zeolite Socony Mobil (ZSM)-5 or activated carbon. The active component of catalyst is nickel. The loading range is 0.5-50 wt.%. The auxiliary agent is platinum and cerium. The loading amount range is 0-40 wt.%.
   USE - The method of dehydrogenation of methylcyclohexane to produce toluene and methane under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes recovery and resource utilization of carbon dioxide, increases conversion rate of carbon dioxide and reduces risk of hydrogen transportation and has high promotion value.

AU KOSOI A A

GA 202070227G
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to apparatus for condensing carbon dioxide in power plants. Carbon dioxide condensation unit includes a carbon dioxide supply line (2) from the carbon dioxide source (1), cold source (7), a heat and mass exchange apparatus and refrigerating unit (19). Plant contains residual heat recovery unit (3). Heat and mass exchange apparatus is heat-mass-exchange apparatus (4) of low pressure. Plant additionally comprises heat-mass exchange device (6) of high pressure and heat and mass exchange device (5) of intermediate pressure. Each heat-mass exchange device (4, 5, 6) includes heat exchanger (8) of cold supply line connected by cold source (7) and residual heat recovery unit (3). Each heat-mass exchange device (4, 5, 6) includes bubbler (9), at that bubbler (9) of heat-mass-exchange apparatus (4) of low pressure is connected to line (2) of carbon dioxide supply from source (1) of carbon dioxide. Inner volumes of heat-mass-exchange apparatus (4, 5, 6) are connected to each other through throttles (14) configured to control the level of condensed carbon dioxide in each heat-mass exchange apparatus (4, 5, 6). Heat-mass-exchange apparatus (4) of low pressure and at least one heat and mass exchange apparatus (5) of intermediate pressure are connected to lines (10) for discharging gaseous carbon dioxide, wherein each line (10) for discharging gaseous carbon dioxide gas includes compressor (11) configured to supply gaseous carbon dioxide gas through a residual heat recovery unit (3) to bubbler (9) of the heat-mass-exchange apparatus of the next pressure stage, which reduces consumption of energy spent on condensation of carbon dioxide, as well as the return of at least part of the spent energy in the cycle. Heat-mass-exchange apparatus (4) of low pressure and heat and mass exchange apparatus (5) of intermediate pressure are connected to line (12) for discharge of liquefied carbon dioxide by pumps (13), configured to discharge condensed carbon dioxide from said heat-mass-exchange apparatus (4, 5) into heat-mass exchange apparatus (6) of high pressure. Discharge line (15) from the high-pressure heat-mass exchange apparatus (6) of liquefied carbon dioxide gas is configured to discharge at least a portion of the liquid carbon dioxide gas to the burial place, and the other part to high-pressure pump (16) through the residual heat recovery unit (3) to the consumers.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result consists in reduction of energy consumption, spent for condensation of carbon dioxide, and also return of at least part of spent energy in cycle in form of heat of carbonic gas in supercritical condition.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide hair washing method involves setting carbon dioxide inlet and ultrasonic transducers at the bottom of hair washing kettle (k1), sequentially arranging water separation membrane and porous bottom plate from bottom to top in the kettle (k1), forming water-enriched area between the water separation membrane and the bottom of the kettle (k1), forming impurity-enriched area between the porous bottom plate and water separation membrane, adding wool fibers into wool washing kettle (k2), arranging kettle (k2) above the porous bottom plate, covering porous cover of the kettle (k2), providing perforated cover plate with a slide rail in which one end of the opening rod slides on the slide rail and other end of the opening rod extends into the kettle (k2), covering porous cover of the kettle (k2), closing the kettle (k2) with a lid, forming mixing passage between the distributor and the porous cover plate, setting carbon dioxide outlet between the porous cover and distributor of kettle (k2), providing top of the lid with a co-solvent inlet, forming co-solvent path between co-solvent inlet and the distributor, providing kettle cover with an ultrasonic vibration rod extending into the mixing passage, and controlling magnetic actuator to control sliding of the opening rod on the slide rail, pressurizing liquid carbon dioxide to 1-15 MPa and heating at -50 degrees C, mixing biological enzyme solution with 1-5 vol.% supercritical carbon dioxide and sending to kettle (k2) through the carbon dioxide inlet or sending biological enzyme solution to wool scrubber directly from the co-solvent inlet or carbon dioxide inlet, starting magnetic actuator, ultrasonic transducer, ultrasonic vibrating rod and carbon dioxide circulation pump, and circulating for 10-30 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The biological enzyme solution is cellulase, lipase, alkylamine polyoxyethylene ether and water, in the mass ratio of 1:2:2:4-6. The usage amount of the biological enzyme solution is 0.1-4 wt.% of wool fibers. The wool fibers are wool, cashmere, yak hair, yak cashmere, alpaca hair or mohair.

TI Method for capturing and sequestering carbon dioxide from gas mixture using membrane involves providing a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture, compressing the gas mixture, and transferring the compressed gas mixture to heat exchanger.
AU KIM J K
   LEE S H
   YOUN S W
AE UNIV HANYANG IUCF-HYU (UHYG-C)
GA 2018593044
AB    NOVELTY - The method for capturing and sequestering carbon dioxide from gas mixture using membrane involves providing a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture, compressing the gas mixture to 1.2:5, transferring the compressed gas mixture to a heat exchanger for cooling, transferring the cooled gas mixture to a separation membrane module having a selective permeability to carbon dioxide to form a first carbon dioxide-depleted retentate stream and a carbon dioxide-enriched first permeate stream, transferring the first retentate stream to the heat exchanger in a reduced pressure state by the inflator, raising the temperature by heat exchange, compressing the first permeate stream and transferring it to the heat exchanger in a pressurized state, transferring the cooled first permeate stream to a distillation column, separating the high purity carbon dioxide liquid stream into a bottom stream by vapor/liquid separation and the low purity carbon dioxide-containing gaseous mixture into a top stream.
   USE - Method for capturing and sequestering carbon dioxide from gas mixture using membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to effectively capture and separate the carbon dioxide contained in a gas mixture, such as exhaust gas, generated from a variety of combustion processes such as power plants, boilers and furnaces, at a low temperature and recovering it with high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for capturing and sequestering carbon dioxide from gas mixture using membrane involves providing a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture, compressing the gas mixture to 1.2:5, transferring the compressed gas mixture to a heat exchanger for cooling, transferring the cooled gas mixture to a separation membrane module having a selective permeability to carbon dioxide to form a first carbon dioxide-depleted retentate stream and a carbon dioxide-enriched first permeate stream, transferring the first retentate stream to the heat exchanger in a reduced pressure state by the inflator, raising the temperature by heat exchange, compressing the first permeate stream and transferring it to the heat exchanger in a pressurized state, transferring the cooled first permeate stream to a distillation column, separating the high purity carbon dioxide liquid stream into a bottom stream by vapor/liquid separation and the low purity carbon dioxide-containing gaseous mixture into a top stream, recovering high purity carbon dioxide liquid phase stream, where the method also involves separate closed external cooling cycle using a single or mixed refrigerant and where the closed external cooling cycle provides the necessary cooling for the process by heat exchange within the heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of heat exchanger.
   Compressor (101)
   Dryer (102)
   Heat exchanger (103)
   Separating film module (104)
   Divider (105)
   Expander (106)

PN CN112316960-A; CN112316960-B
TI Catalyzing carbon dioxide to form formaldehyde comprises adding hydrotalcite-supported ruthenium catalyst and water into closed reaction vessel, adding carbon dioxide and hydrogen into reaction vessel, and reacting.
AU DENG L
   LIU X
   XU M
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202117903B
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyzing carbon dioxide to form formaldehyde comprises (a) adding hydrotalcite-supported ruthenium catalyst and water into a closed reaction vessel, and (b) adding carbon dioxide and hydrogen into the reaction vessel, maintaining at 10-100 degrees C, and reacting for 1-12 hours to obtain formaldehyde.
   USE - The method is useful for catalyzing carbon dioxide to form formaldehyde.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses a hydrotalcite-supported ruthenium catalyst to catalyze carbon dioxide and hydrogen to form formaldehyde; can generate carbon dioxide and hydrogen into formaldehyde; has high preparation efficiency; and is simple.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hydrotalcite-supported ruthenium catalyst comprises 0.1-0.6 wt.% ruthenium. Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises reacting at 25-30 degrees C under the carbon dioxide and hydrogen pressure range of 10-20 bar. The hydrotalcite-supported ruthenium catalyst is prepared by (i) dissolving salt containing divalent metal ion and the salt containing trivalent metal ion in a mixed solution of deionized water and ethanol, and then adding propylene oxide to obtain hydrotalcite precursor colloid, (ii) allowing to stand the hydrotalcite precursor colloid and then washing to obtain hydrotalcite, and (iii) immersing the hydrotalcite in a ruthenium-containing salt solution, then stirring and drying. The step (iii) further comprises reducing the dried hydrotalcite-supported ruthenium catalyst under the protection of hydrogen atmosphere. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of hydrotalcite-supported ruthenium catalyst to water is 0.03-0.08:10, preferably 0.05:10. The divalent metal ion is magnesium ion or calcium ion. The trivalent metal ion is aluminum ion. The salt containing divalent metal ions comprises calcium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium nitrate or magnesium nitrate. The salt containing trivalent metal ions is aluminum chloride or aluminum nitrate. The molar ratio of divalent metal ion to trivalent metal ion is 2-5:1. The total molar ratio of propylene oxide to divalent metal ion and trivalent metal ion is 2-4:1.

PN CN110270231-A; CN110270231-B
TI Method for preparing metal organic framework-derived gas separation membrane for use in separating carbon dioxide/methane gas, involves dissolving benzenetricarboxylic acid and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate in deionized water.
AU ZHANG G
   YE L
   FAN Z
   MENG Q
   QIN L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2019825986
AB    NOVELTY - A metal organic framework-derived gas separation membrane preparing method involves dissolving 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate in deionized water, adding an auxiliary agent to the mixture to obtain a reaction mixture A and drying to obtain a MIL-100Al. The MIL-100Al and triblock copolymer are dissolved in an alcohol and hydrochloric acid solution is added to the mixture. The copolymerization reaction of the mixture is carried out to obtain the reaction mixture B and post-treatment is carried out to obtain a solid mixture. The solid mixture obtained is carbonized, the carbonized product is washed with dilute hydrochloric acid and water and dried to obtain MIL-100Al porous carbon material. The carbon material is dissolved in N-methylpyrrolidone to obtain a clear and uniform viscous solution. The viscous solution is cast on a clean glass plate with a pouring rod and vacuum dried. The cast glass plate is immersed in a water bath to obtain the membrane.
   USE - Method for preparing metal organic framework-derived gas separation membrane for use in separating carbon dioxide/methane gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables achieving excellent carbon dioxide capture ability under normal pressure, better separation selectivity for carbon dioxide and methane and better recycling ability that the obtained membrane structure is rich in micropores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal organic framework-derived gas separation membrane preparing method involves dissolving 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate in deionized water, adjusting the pH of the mixture to 1.9-2.0, adding an auxiliary agent to the mixture to obtain a reaction mixture A, heating the reaction mixture A at 200 degrees C for 3-6 hours and cooling at room temperature, washing the obtained solid with dilute nitric acid and deionized water and drying to obtain a MIL-100Al, where the auxiliary agent is an agent containing a methyl or ethyl group, the auxiliary agent is added in an amount of the number of methyl or ethyl group and the number of the methyl or ethyl group groups is equal to the number of acid groups in the 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid and the aluminum nitrate nonahydrate is added in an amount to completely react the 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid. The MIL-100Al and triblock copolymer are dissolved in an alcohol at room temperature under ultrasonic conditions, 37 wt.% hydrochloric acid solution is added to the mixture and ultrasonically mixed at room temperature for 24 hours. The copolymerization reaction of the mixture is carried out at 100-120 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain the reaction mixture B and post-treatment is carried out to obtain a solid mixture, where the triblock copolymer is a compound containing oxygen or a nitrogen group in each block, the mass ratio of the MIL-100Al and triblock copolymer is 0.4-0.5:1 and the hydrochloric acid solution is added in an amount of 0.2-0.25 ml/g based on the mass of the MIL-100Al. The solid mixture obtained is carbonized at a temperature of 600-800 degrees C under the protection of nitrogen for 2.5-3.5 hours, the carbonized product is washed with dilute hydrochloric acid and water to neutrality and dried to obtain MIL-100Al porous carbon material. The MIL-100Al porous carbon material is dissolved in N-methylpyrrolidone and stirred for 12 hours to obtain a clear and uniform viscous solution. The viscous solution is cast on a clean glass plate with a pouring rod and placed at 60-80 degrees C and vacuum dried under nitrogen of 50 kPa until the solvent is completely evaporated. The cast glass plate is immersed in a water bath for separation to obtain a polymer which is dried under vacuum for 24 hours to obtain a metal organic framework-derived gas separation membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is anhydrous methanol or absolute ethanol. Preferred Composition: The N-methylpyrrolidone solution is added in an amount of 8.5-9.0 ml/g based on the mass of the MIL-100Al porous carbon material. The aluminium nitrate nonahydrate and 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid are present in the ratio of 1.2:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The triblock copolymer is poly(ethylene oxide)-poly(propylene oxide)-poly(ethylene oxide).

GA 201876192P
AB    (CN207891081-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a water processing device, especially a rotary filler for recycling CO2 carbon-eliminating device, at least comprising a de-activated and blower. Wherein, it further comprises a water tank, an air purifying device and a CO2 recovery device; removing device connected with a water inlet pipe; water outlet, an air inlet and an air outlet pipe; the water inlet pipe is provided with a valve, the water outlet connected to the water tank; the air inlet and air is at least provided with a blower and an air purifying device, an air inlet, a fan and an air purifying device are connected through a pipe valve; the air outlet pipe connected with a CO2 recovery device, CO2 exhaust recycling device through a pipe valve is connected with the atmosphere.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for recycling carbon dioxide by rotating filler type carbon removal, which involves:
   (A) removing external air from the air by the air purifying device, and then entering the air blower to pressurize, heating purified air, after pressurized by the air heating device, and entering water to decarbonized through the inlet pipe of the decarburizer;
   (B) entering air in decarburizer and subjecting to countercurrent heat transfer on the rotating packing inside the decarburizer quality, so that free carbon dioxide in the water is released into the air;
   (C) passing air after the gas-liquid exchange through the gas outlet of the decarburizer into the gas-liquid separator to separate the carried liquid, and the air separated by the gas-liquid separation enters the carbon di-oxide recovery device to collect the carbon di-oxide and is discharged to the atmosphere;
   (D) separating liquid by gas-liquid separator, again entering decarburizer to remove carbon; and
   (E) completing the gas-liquid exchange to remove the free carbon di-oxide is introduced into the water tank through the outlet pipe of the decarburizer, and buffering and then entering subsequent water treatment process.

PN CN108048393-A
TI Rapidly culturing epidermal basal stem cells by preparing coating solution coated flask, processing dermal tissue, centrifuging cell suspension, adding basal stem cell proliferation composition and culturing cells.
AU XIE J
   SHU B
   ZHOU Z
AE XIE J (XIEJ-Individual)
   SHU B (SHUB-Individual)
   ZHOU Z (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 201840936F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for rapidly culturing epidermal basal stem cells involves (a) preparing coating solution coated flask by dissolving human type IV collagen in 0.1 g/l phosphate buffered saline (PBS) to obtain coating solution, adding 5 ml coating solution into culture flask, laying flat, refrigerating the flask overnight, discarding supernatant, placing the flask on clean bench to dry, packing coated flask and storing the coated flask using refrigerator, (b) processing dermal tissue, and (c) adding 0.25% trypsin, and 0.01% EDTA, incubating the flask at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide for 2-3 minutes, observing cell retraction under inverted microscope for enlarged cell gap, adding 10 ml stem cell suspension preparation to the culture flask to terminate digestion, repeatedly blowing the culture at bottom of the flask using straw to cause the cells to fall off, centrifuging the cell suspension, adding composition for basal stem cell proliferation, and culturing the cells to 2-5 passages.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing epidermal basal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method rapidly cultures epidermal basal stem cells and increases viability of cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for rapidly culturing epidermal basal stem cells involves (a) preparing coating solution coated flask by dissolving human type IV collagen in 0.1 g/l phosphate buffered saline (PBS) to obtain coating solution, adding 5 ml coating solution into culture flask, laying flat, refrigerating the flask overnight, discarding supernatant, placing the flask on clean bench to dry, packing coated flask and storing the coated flask using refrigerator, (b) processing dermal tissue by collecting skin, disinfecting the skin, rinsing the skin, removing the subcutaneous fat layer, retaining the dermis and epidermal layer, cutting into small pieces, digesting the small pieces using 0.25% trypsin, refrigerating at 4 degrees C for overnight, terminating digestion process by adding an equal volume of Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium containing high glucose and 10% fetal bovine serum, rinsing the pieces, detaching epidermis using forceps, and washing the processed piece to obtain dermal tissue, cutting the processed material into small pieces, rinsing thrice using PBS, placing the processed material in sterilized container, adding digestive solution containing 1 U/ml neutral protease II, incubating at 4 degrees C for overnight, separating epidermis using forceps, cutting dermis layer into small pieces of 1 mm3, adding digestive solution containing 0.5% collagenase type I, shaking the mixture at 37 degrees C for 1 hours, adding equal amount of DMEM/nutrient mixture (F12) medium, terminating the digestion process, centrifuging the cell suspension at 180 g for 10 minutes, filtering the cell suspension using filter to remove residual tissue blocks, adjusting cell density to 1x 105 cells/ml, inoculating the cell suspension into a T25 culture flask coated with type IV collagen, incubating at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide for 10-20 minutes, aspirating the supernatant and retaining adhered cells, collecting 10 ml cell culture solution, adding cell culture to T25 flask, culturing at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide, where the solution is changed for every 2-3 days, aspirating the culture solution, after the cell fusion degree reaches 80% or more, and gently washing the cells twice using PBS solution, and (c) adding 0.25% trypsin, and 0.01% EDTA, incubating the flask at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide for 2-3 minutes, observing cell retraction under inverted microscope for enlarged cell gap, adding 10 ml stem cell suspension preparation to the culture flask to terminate digestion, repeatedly blowing the culture at bottom of the flask using straw to cause the cells to fall off, centrifuging the cell suspension at 180 g for 5 minutes, adding composition for basal stem cell proliferation, and culturing the cells to 2-5 passages.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The stem cell suspension preparation is prepared by collecting human epidermal tissue, digesting using trypsin and EDTA, quenching the serum, gently blowing using a straw, filtering the mixture using 200 mesh sieve, centrifuging the mixture, discarding the supernatant to obtain the cell solution, inoculating the cell solution into culture bottle modified with type IV collagen, collecting adherent cells after 10-20 minutes of rapid adhesion, adding serum-free DMEM, culturing the cells at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide, changing the solution once in every 3 days, and adding silk fibroin, chitosan and vitamin C, when the cells reaches 70%-80% fusion state, and mixing uniformly, where the silk fibroin, chitosan and vitamin C are sterilized at high temperature and pressure before addition. The step (b) involves cutting the processed skin to pieces measuring 0.5x 0.5 mm. The step (a) involves adjusting the temperature of the refrigerator to 4 degrees C. Preferred Component: The composition for basal stem cell proliferation is prepared by mixing 4',7,8-trihydroxyisoflavone, 4',6,7-trihydroxyisoflavone, 3',4',7-trihydroxyisoflavone, and o-dihydroxyisoflavone derivative uniformly.

GA 2020C06632
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating and recovering carbon                dioxide gas, nitrogen gas and oxygen gas, involves                bringing exhaust gas into contact with an aqueous                solution containing alkali carbonate, reacting                carbon dioxide gas in the exhaust gas to form an                aqueous solution containing hydrogen carbonate                alkali, recovering a gas containing nitrogen gas                and oxygen gas discharged from the gas absorption                step, decomposing at least a portion of the                hydrogen carbonate alkali obtained in the gas                absorption step into alkali carbonate and carbon                dioxide, circulating at least a portion of the                alkali carbonate obtained in the decomposition step                to the gas absorption step, bringing the carbon                dioxide gas containing the carbon dioxide gas                discharged in the decomposition step into contact                with the aqueous solution when the carbon dioxide                gas obtained in the decomposition step is                recovered, and recovering the carbon dioxide                gas.
   USE - Method for separating and recovering carbon                dioxide gas, nitrogen gas and oxygen gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers a highly purified carbon                dioxide gas and nitrogen gas with high purity                efficiently using cheap inorganic compound.

Preferred Method: The method further involves                  confirming the composition of exhaust gas before                  supplying it to the gas absorption step. Preferred Composition:                  The volume ratio of nitrogen gas and oxygen gas                  in the exhaust gas is 10/1-100/1.

PT P
PN CN109932276-A
TI Method for measuring solubility of solid sample in supercritical carbon dioxide, involves injecting carbon dioxide gas into autoclave to discharge air in autoclave and pumping autoclave and using negative pressure in autoclave.
AU SHI Q
   XU X
   ZHANG Q
   CHAI G
   MAO J
   ZHANG W
   FAN W
   LIU J
   XI H
   LIU S
   FU Y
AE ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
GA 201958982A
AB    NOVELTY - A solid sample solubility measuring method involves injecting a carbon dioxide gas into the autoclave to discharge air in the autoclave and pumping the autoclave to form a negative pressure in the autoclave. The negative pressure is used in the autoclave to draw a quantitative solid sample into the autoclave and the carbon dioxide is injected into the autoclave. The pressure and/or temperature are adjusted in the autoclave, so that the carbon dioxide is in a supercritical state and so that all solid samples are dissolved in carbon dioxide. The pressure and/or temperature are adjusted in the autoclave to precipitate the solid sample from the carbon dioxide, the pressure and temperature are recorded in the autoclave and the solubility of the solid sample is calculated in the supercritical carbon dioxide at the pressure and temperature.
   USE - Method for measuring solubility of solid sample in supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables measuring solubility of solid sample with enhanced accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for measuring solubility of solid sample in supercritical carbon dioxide.

   USE - Processing of ionic liquid catalyst used for                synthesis of 5-membered ring carbonate                (claimed).

   a continuous method for producing the                5-membered ring carbonate, which involves                continuously injecting carbon dioxide and epoxy                compound into the reaction unit, reacting the                injected carbon dioxide and epoxy compound in                presence of the processed ionic liquid catalyst,                and discharging the 5-membered ring                carbonate.

TI Process for converting mixture of hydrogen gas and gaseous carbon oxide that is carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide to product composition comprising alcohols and liquid hydrocarbons via Fischer-Tropsch synthesis by contacting mixture of hydrogen gas and gaseous carbon oxide and olefin co-feed.
AU PATERSON A J
   SUNLEY J G
AE BP PLC (BRPE-C)
   BP PLC (BRPE-C)
GA 202281813U
AB    NOVELTY - Process for converting a mixture of hydrogen gas and a gaseous carbon oxide that is carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, or their combination to a product composition comprising alcohols and liquid hydrocarbons via a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction, involves contacting a mixture of hydrogen gas and the gaseous carbon oxide (e.g., in the form of a synthesis gas mixture) and an olefin co-feed with a supported cobalt-manganese Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst to provide the product composition, where the olefin co-feed comprises at least one 2-14C olefin and is present in an amount of 0.001-40 wt.% relative to the total amount of hydrogen, the gaseous carbon oxide and olefin, a weight ratio of manganese to cobalt in the catalyst is at least 0.0.05 on an elemental basis and the molar ratio of hydrogen to the gaseous carbon oxide in the mixture of hydrogen and the gaseous carbon oxide is at least 0.5.
   USE - As process for converting a mixture of hydrogen gas and a gaseous carbon oxide that is carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, or their combination to a product composition comprising alcohols and liquid hydrocarbons via a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The process can produce products having increased amounts of alcohols from a mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of manganese to cobalt present in the synthesis catalyst is in the range of 0.05-3 on an elemental basis. The synthesis catalyst comprises at least 0.5 wt% manganese on an elemental basis. The synthesis catalyst comprises in the range of 2.5-25 wt.% manganese on an elemental basis. The synthesis catalyst comprises cobalt in an amount of 2-35 wt.%, on an elemental basis. The synthesis catalyst comprises cobalt in an amount of 5-20 wt.%, on an elemental basis. The total amount of cobalt and manganese in the synthesis catalyst is no more than 40 wt.% on an elemental basis, based on the total weight of the synthesis catalyst. The olefin co-feed is present in an amount of 0.001-30 wt.%, relative to the total amount of hydrogen, the gaseous carbon oxide and olefin. The olefin co-feed is present in an amount of 0.1-20 wt.%, relative to the total amount of hydrogen, the gaseous carbon oxide and olefin. The olefin co-feed is at least 90% 2-14C olefins. The olefin co-feed in at least 90% 2-5C olefins. The product composition comprises at least 10 wt.% alcohols. The product composition comprises at least 20 wt.% alcohols. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises a support material that comprises at least one oxide chosen from alumina, zirconia, zinc oxide, ceria, silica and titania. The gaseous carbon oxide is carbon monoxide. The gaseous carbon oxide is carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The olefin co-feed comprises at least one linear olefin. The olefin co-feed comprises at least one cyclic olefin or at least one branched olefin. Preferred Method: In the process, the contacting is conducted at a pressure of 1-10 MPa absolute. In the process, the contacting is conducted at a temperature of not greater than 350 degrees C.

Preferred Method: The                  preparation of cucurbituril-amine compound-porous                  body complex further comprises pre-activating the                  organic-inorganic porous body at 150-220 degrees                  C for 20-30 hours before step (b).
   EXAMPLE - 3.794 g tetraethylenepentamine and 5 g                cucurbit(6)uril were added into a beaker containing                50 ml anhydrous methanol and dispersed by stirring                to obtain 8.7 g                cucurbit(6)uril-tetraethylenepentamine.

AU WANG J

   LI C
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202283385Y
AB    NOVELTY - Zinc zirconium oxide surface solid solution                catalyst comprises zirconium oxide body phase and                the solid solution formed on the surface of                described zirconium oxide body phase. The solid                solution is formed by dissolving zinc on the                surface of zirconium oxide.
   USE - Zinc zirconium oxide surface solid solution                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The zinc zirconium oxide surface solid                solution catalyst has high methanol selection,                large specific surface area, good stability, strong                heat resistance, and improves the carbon dioxide                conversion and methanol space-time yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. method for preparing a zinc zirconium oxide                surface solid solution catalyst, involving:
   a. synthesizing the zinc zirconium oxide                surface solid solution catalyst by an impregnation                method to obtain a suspension I containing a zinc                precursor and a zirconium oxide precursor;
   b. removing the solvent in the suspension I,                and calcining the remaining solid phase to obtain                product or
   ynthesizing zinc zirconium oxide surface                solid solution catalyst by deposition precipitation                method to obtain suspension II containing zinc                precursor and zirconium oxide precursor; obtaining                solution III containing precipitant, mixing the                suspension II and the solution III, and filtering                to obtain a precipitate, roasting the precipitation                to obtain zinc zirconium oxide surface solid                solution catalyst; and
   2. method for synthesizing methanol by                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, involving: taking                carbon dioxide and hydrogen as raw materials,                reacting under the condition that a catalyst exists                to obtain methanol, where the reaction temperature                is 280-400&#8451;, reaction pressure is 1-10                megaPascal, space velocity is 3000-80000mL/(gh),                n(hydrogen):n(carbon dioxide) molar ratio is                1-8.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The content of zinc in                  the solid solution is 0.1-30%. The roasting                  temperature is 300-800&#8451;. The content of                  the zinc precursor is 0.01-10 mole/L. The content                  of the zinc precursor is 0.01-7. The content of                  the precipitant is 0.01-14 mole/L. The zirconium                  oxide precursor is heated in a reaction furnace                  at 25-300&#8451;, the gas-phase zinc                  precursor is introduced, and the operation causes                  the zinc in the zinc precursor to be deposited on                  the surface of the zirconium oxide precursor to                  obtain an intermediate. The vapor-phase zinc                  precursor is decomposed or reacted by controlling                  the temperature or introducing a substance                  capable of reacting with the vapor-phase zinc                  precursor, so that zinc is deposited on the                  surface of the zirconium oxide precursor. The                  decomposition control temperature is                  50-500&#8451;. The conditions of the reaction                  includes reaction temperature is                  100-200&#8451;, the substance capable of                  reacting with the gas-phase zinc precursor is                  oxygen, water, and ozone. Preferred Components:                  The zirconium oxide precursor is measured by the                  mass of zirconium, and the mass ratio of the zinc                  precursor to the zirconium oxide precursor is                  1:999-3:7. The zinc precursor is zinc nitrate,                  zinc acetate, zinc halide, diethyl zinc, and                  zincocene. The zirconium oxide precursor is                  zirconium carbonate, zirconium hydroxide, basic                  zirconium carbonate and oxygen-deficient                  zirconia. The precipitating agent is ammonia                  water, ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate,                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium                  hydroxide and potassium hydroxide.
