TI New catalyst compound comprising a silver layer and a copper layer, useful for electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide to small molecules e.g. gaseous hydrocarbons or liquid molecules.
AU VOIRY D
   WU H
   QI K
AE CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV MONTPELLIER (UMON-C)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIM MONTPELLIER (ECOL-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV MONTPELLIER (UMON-C)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIM MONTPELLIER (ECOL-Non-standard)
GA 202268912K
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst compound comprising a silver layer and a copper layer, the copper layer is functionalized with at least one of (I) or (II), is new.
   USE - The compound is useful as a catalyst to convert carbon dioxide into small molecules, preferably methane, ethyl alcohol, CO, formic acid, or small amount of hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst compound: has increased current density and improved selectivity of the reaction towards the production of ethylene and ethanol up to -70 mA. and greater than or equal to 60% at -1.2 V compared to -32 for pristine non-functionalized copper-based electrocatalyst when measured in a 3-electrode configuration; can be easily obtained by electrodepositing copper and silver metals in the form of a porous structure for larger active surface, where functionalization is easy and cheap as the molecules needed are very common and easily obtainable; is obtained in economical manner; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst compound comprising a silver layer and a copper layer, the copper layer is functionalized with at least one of (S-R1) (I) or (R2) (II), is new.
   R1 = diazole, thiadiazole, triazole or triazine (all optionally substituted by amine, thiol, amide, a carboxylic acid or a short linear or branched 1-3C alkyl chain); and
   R2 = diazole, thiadiazole, triazole or triazine (all optionally substituted by amine, thiol, amide, a carboxylic acid or a short linear or branched 1-3C alkyl chain).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for:
   (1) manufacturing (P1) the catalyst compound; and
   (2) converting (P2) carbon dioxide into small molecules comprising contacting carbon dioxide with the catalyst compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The method comprises (a) electrodeposition of silver on a conductive support, (b) electrodeposition of copper on the support obtained in step (a), the copper being deposited on the silver, (c) functionalizing the support obtained in step (b) by contacting with (H-S-R1) (Ia) or (II). The step (c) is performed in an organic solvent. The concentration of Ia or II in the organic solvent is 1-100 mM. The step (c) lasts from 5 seconds to 12 hours, and is preferably done at room temperature. Preferred Components: The compound: having a fern-like dendritic morphology; comprises 70-90 wt.%, preferably 85 wt.% copper and 10-30 wt.%, preferably 15 wt.% silver. The sulfur/copper atomic surface ratio is 0.2-0.6, in number of atoms, being estimated from the top 5 nm of the surface of the compound and measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy. The nitrogen/copper atomic surface ratio is 0.2-4, preferably 0.4 and 2. It is meant by atomic surface ratio, the nitrogen/copper atomic surface ratio, in number of atoms, being estimated from the top 5 nm of the surface of the compound and measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy. The compound further comprises a conducting support, preferably a commercial carbon-based gas diffusion electrode. Preferred Process: The process according further comprises step (d) of washing the catalyst compound with an organic solvent, the solvent preferably being ethanol. In the process (P2) the conversion reaction of carbon dioxide is done under atmospheric pressure and at room temperature.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of methods that do not support the claims.

TI Device for preparing hydrogen by supercritical water, has supercritical mixture that comprises supercritical carbon dioxide and supercritical hydrogen, and generated supercritical mixture discharged from air floating bed reaction kettle.
AU YANG C
   YANG J
AE YANG J (YANG-Individual)
GA 202232074D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a supercritical boiler (100), an air floating bed reaction kettle (101), and a reaction material feeding device (102). The supercritical boiler provides supercritical water. The reaction material feeding device receives the reaction material, and discharging the reaction material. The reaction material discharged by the reaction material feeding device is mixed with the supercritical water into the air floating bed reaction kettle in the air floating bed reaction kettle. The reaction material reacts with supercritical water to generate supercritical mixture. The supercritical mixture comprises supercritical carbon dioxide, supercritical hydrogen and unreacted supercritical water. The generated supercritical mixture is discharged from the air floating bed reaction kettle.
   USE - Device for preparing hydrogen by supercritical water. Uses include but are not limited to coal, petroleum, biomass, and carbon rubbish based fuel such as carbon-based fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The device does not need to prepare pure oxygen or external electric heating, the system control is simple, the hydrogen production cost is low, the whole process only uses supercritical water as heat source, without adding air or oxygen, no explosion danger, and higher safety. The produced hydrogen and carbon dioxide are supercritical state, only need to expand and reduce the temperature to obtain the liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen, completely solving the hydrogen transportation problem.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic diagram of the hydrogen production device of a device for preparing hydrogen by supercritical water. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Supercritical boiler (100)
   Air floating bed reaction kettle (101)

PT P
PN EP3954668-A1; WO2022033877-A1; WO2022033877-A8
TI Manufacturing concrete parts by mixing cement and            aggregate with water, introducing carbon dioxide into            fresh concrete, curing until calcium aluminates are            hydrated, subjecting to carbon dioxide, and storing for            further hydration.
AU ZAJAC M
   SKOCEK J
   BEN HAHA M
AE HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
GA 202222978S
AB    NOVELTY - Concrete parts are manufactured by providing                hydraulic cement and aggregate, mixing the cement                and aggregate with water to provide a fresh                concrete, introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) into the                fresh concrete in an amount resulting in a                carbonation degree of less than or equal to 5 wt.%                of the total carbonatable calcium (Ca) and                magnesium (Mg) phases, which are calculated as                0.785x (CaO-0.56 CaCO3-0.7 SO3)+1.091x (MgO-0.479                MgCO3) for a first carbonation step, curing the                fresh concrete until greater than or equal to 15                wt.% of the calcium aluminates are hydrated to                provide a green concrete material, subjecting the                green concrete material to CO2 in an amount                resulting in a carbonation degree of greater than                or equal to 10 wt.% for a final carbonation step,                and storing the material for 0.5 hour to 28 days                for further hydration of not-yet carbonated,                not-yet hydrated cement to provide the concrete                parts.
   USE - Manufacture of concrete parts (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to manufacture concrete                component, which has excellent strength, and                reduced carbon dioxide emissions, and provides                green and early strength and consume water, and                carbonation hardening to provide final strength                followed by hydration in post-curing which provides                an easy to apply and economic process without the                need for complicated devices. The obtained concrete                parts have the required properties comparable to                parts from hydraulic hardening of ordinary Portland                Cement (OPC) and allow using conventional steel                reinforcement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for concrete                parts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Compositions: Hydraulic cement is                  selected from Portland cements, Portland                  composite cements, calcium aluminate cements,                  calcium sulfoaluminate cements, and dicalcium                  silicate cements, preferably Portland cements and                  Portland composite cements. Water:cement weight                  ratio in the fresh concrete is 0.1-1, preferably                  0.2-0.5. Fresh concrete additionally contains one                  or more of admixture(s) and additive(s). CO2                  introduced in the first carbonation step has a                  concentration of 5-99 vol.% and/or is provided in                  the form of gas, solution, or as solid material.                  Amount of CO2 introduced in the first carbonation                  step results in a carbonation degree of 0.5-4%,                  preferably 1-3%. CO2 is a gas from modified                  clinker production lines, e.g. oxyfuel kiln lines                  or indirect calcination of the calcium carbonate,                  or from carbon capture technologies, such as                  amine-based CO2 scrubber and membrane-based gas                  separation. Amount of carbonate in the concrete                  parts is 5-100 wt.%, preferably 40-60 wt.% of the                  clinker content of dry cement. Preferred                  Processes: CO2 is introduced into the fresh                  concrete by one or more of (i) mixing of cement,                  aggregate, and water in the presence of CO2, (ii)                  adding solid CO2 during mixing, (iii) dissolving                  CO2 in an aqueous component added to form the                  fresh concrete, (iv) pre-carbonating the dry                  cement before mixing, and/or (v) soaking a                  portion or all aggregate with a CO2 containing                  solution before addition to the fresh concrete.                  CO2 is introduced into the fresh concrete by                  dissolving the carbon dioxide in the mixing water                  or a portion of it, in an admixture                  solution/suspension, in an additive                  solution/suspension or in more than one aqueous                  component. CO2 is introduced into the fresh                  concrete by a combination of two or more of                  variants (i)-(v), preferably by using CO2                  solution depleted in CO2 by soaking the aggregate                  according to variant (v) as mixing water                  according to variant (iii) or by using gaseous                  carbon dioxide with a CO2 concentration decreased                  by having been bubbled through the fresh concrete                  during mixing in variant (i) for precarbonating                  the dry cement according to variant (iv). Fresh                  concrete is filled into a mold after mixing or                  after the first carbonation step and demolded                  after curing. Preferred Conditions: Fresh                  concrete is cured for 60-300 minutes, preferably                  10-120 minutes, and/or until a strength of 0.5                  MPa, preferably of 2 MPa, is reached and/or until                  greater than or equal to 5 wt.%, preferably                  greater than or equal to 15 wt.% of the calcium                  aluminates are hydrated. CO2 used for subjecting                  the green concrete parts to CO2 is provided in                  the form of gas, preferably with a concentration                  of 5-99 vol.%.

TI Method for estimating likelihood of pulmonary arterial hypertension, involves determining score based upon end-tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide and minute ventilation/carbon dioxide production relationship at ventilatory threshold.
AU MACCARTER D J
   ANDERSON S T
   ANDERSON D M
AE SHAPE MEDICAL SYSTEMS INC (SHAP-Non-standard)
GA 202222937L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining ratios of minute ventilation to rate of oxygen uptake based on breath-by-breath data for a patient, and determining a ventilatory threshold based upon the determined ratios. A score is determined based upon an end-tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide value and a minute ventilation/carbon dioxide production relationship (VE/VCO2) value. The score is used to estimate a likelihood that the patient has pulmonary arterial hypertension. The estimated likelihood is caused to be displayed on a user interface e.g. graphical user interface.
   USE - Method for estimating likelihood of pulmonary arterial hypertension of a patient. Uses include but are not limited to a blood test, immunology, HIV test, abdominal ultrasound scan, 6-minute walk test, peak VO 2, right heart catheterization and vaso-reactivity testing.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas exchange system comprises a flow sensor configured to sense a respiratory flow of a patient, where an analyzer is configured to determine a composition of a portion of the respiratory flow and a computing device receives gas exchange measurements based on breath-by-breath data captured by the flow sensor and the analyzer during a gas exchange test and plot minute ventilation against rate of oxygen uptake, and thus enables to improve the accuracy of the ventilatory threshold estimation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of estimating a ventilatory threshold for a patient.

PT P
PN WO2021239831-A1
TI Circular carbon process, comprises reacting hydrogen and carbon monoxide to get methane and water, decomposing methane into carbon and hydrogen, and using e.g. carbon in chemical process to get carbon monoxide and reduced substance.
AU BODE A
   FLICK D
   SPIEGEL A
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2021D92573
AB    NOVELTY - Circular carbon process, comprises: (i) reacting hydrogen and carbon monoxide to produce methane and water; (ii) decomposing methane into carbon and hydrogen; and (iii) using carbon as reducing agent and/or in a carbon-containing material as reducing agent in a chemical process to produce carbon monoxide and a reduced substance, where the methane produced in the first step is used in the second step, the carbon produced in the second step is used in the third step and carbon monoxide produced in the third step is used in the first step.
   USE - Used as circular carbon process.
   ADVANTAGE - The circular carbon process: avoids carbon dioxide emissions to enable carbon neutral production while still using carbon containing material as a reduction agent; reduces hydrogen and electricity demand by using carbon monoxide methanation instead of carbon dioxide methanation; generates homogeneous carbon material without significant changes in purity of other material properties; replaces carbon purchases by own production; and invests alternative for carbon dioxide emission reduction versus carbon capture and storage (CCS) requiring carbon dioxide capture with energy demand fulfilled by reaction heat from the exothermic methanation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a joint plant for circular carbon process, comprising a plant using carbon as reduction agent in a chemical process including a carbon monoxide separation and conditioning, a methanation plant downstream producing methane and water, a pyrolysis plant downstream of the methanation plant decomposing methane to solid carbon and hydrogen, and a carbon solids transport device between the pyrolysis plant and the plant using carbon as reducing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The chemical process in the third step is a reduction of calcium oxide to calcium carbide via oxidizing of carbon to carbon monoxide, a reduction of silicon oxide to silicon or silicon carbide via oxidizing of carbon to carbon monoxide, a reduction of tin oxide to tin via oxidizing of carbon to carbon monoxide, a reduction of chromium oxide to chromium via oxidizing of carbon to carbon monoxide, a reduction of manganese oxide to manganese via oxidizing of carbon to carbon monoxide and/or a reduction of calcium phosphate to phosphorus via oxidizing of carbon to carbon monoxide. The reaction heat from the exothermic methanation reaction in the first step is used in the third step for separation or purification of carbon monoxide. The hydrogen produced in the second step is used in the first step. The hydrogen is produced: in an additional fourth step and used in the first step; and via water electrolysis or steam methane reforming with or without carbon capture and storage in the fourth step. The water produced in the first step is used for the water electrolysis in the fourth step. The streams from outside the circular process e.g. hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and/or carbon are introduced into the circular process or streams e.g. hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and/or carbon are extracted from the circular process to supply external demand and/or for storage of carbon. The biogas is used as additional methane source. The processes of the first and the second steps are both conducted in a pressure of 1-30 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The plant comprises in addition: an electrolysis plant downstream of the methanation reactor separating water in oxygen and hydrogen; a gas pipeline feeding methane-rich mixture from the methanation plant to the pyrolysis plant and a gas pipeline for carbon oxide transport from the plant using carbon as reducing agent to the methanation plant; and a gas pipeline for hydrogen transport from the pyrolysis plant and/or the electrolysis plant to the methanation plant and a pipeline for liquid water transport from the methanation plant to the electrolysis plant a transport pipeline or solids transport device for carbon supply from external sources to the plant using carbon as reducing agent.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).);

PT P
PN CN113582201-A
TI Method for recycling cyanamide process tail gas,            involves obtaining high-purity liquid ammonia from            unabsorbed gas in acid absorption working section, and            releasing high-purity carbon dioxide gas from            absorption liquid in acid absorption working section            and obtaining alkaline liquid.
AU QIN Y
   SONG G
   KONG D
   TANG Y
AE SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D4438S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing acid absorption                working section, where an alkaline or neutral                liquid is used for absorbing acidic components in                the process tail gas to obtain an absorption liquid                and unabsorbed gas. The high-purity liquid ammonia                is obtained from unabsorbed gas in an acid                absorption working section. The high-purity carbon                dioxide gas is released from the absorption liquid                in the acid absorption working section and alkaline                liquid is obtained. The absorption pressure in the                acid absorption section is 0.2-0.4 MPa, and the                temperature of unabsorbed gas is 20-45 degrees                Celsius. An absorption tower with a heat exchange                function is used in the acid absorption section,                and countercurrent absorption and heat transfer are                simultaneously completed.
   USE - Method for recycling cyanamide process tail                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyanamide process tail gas recycling                technique can obtain high quality ammonia product                and carbon dioxide product with good economical                efficiency. The high pressure makes ammonia more                easily left in the liquid phase, and only carbon                dioxide leaves from the top of the tower in the                form of gas phase. The liquid phase operation                temperature can reach 120-190 degree C, absorption                liquid is still liquid state in the pressure range,                liquid can absorb acidic substance, and can absorb                alkaline substance, can be used for absorbing acid                section, carbon desorption section and ammonia                purifying liquefying section.

PN KR2021110951-A
TI Exhaust system for combustion engine, has pressure control valve that is installed on connection exhaust pipe to control pressure between first exhaust pipe and flue.
AU SUNG Y
   KIM J
   JONG P S
   LEE D H
AE SAMSUNG HEAVY IND CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 2021A4027A
AB    NOVELTY - The exhaust system (1) has a first gas receiver (10) provided to receive the exhaust gas discharged from some of the combustion cylinders (EG) of a combustion engine. A second gas receiver (20) is provided to receive the exhaust gas discharged from the rest of the combustion cylinders. A first exhaust pipe (30) connects the first gas receiver and a carbon dioxide collecting device (90) to supply the exhaust gas from the first gas receiver to the carbon dioxide collecting device. A second exhaust pipe (40) connects the second gas receiver and a flue (F) to discharge the exhaust gas from the second gas receiver to the flue. A connection exhaust pipe (50) is provided to connect the two exhaust pipes and the flue. A pressure control valve (60) is installed on the connection exhaust pipe to control the pressure between the first exhaust pipe and flue.
   USE - Exhaust system for a combustion engine. Can be used for supplying exhaust gas accommodated in a gas receiver to a chimney and a carbon dioxide collecting device, respectively.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaust system has pressure control valve configured to adjust the pressure between two exhaust pipes so as to match the pressure of the exhaust pipes and the flue.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the configuration of the exhaust system of a combustion engine.
   Exhaust system (1)
   First gas receiver (10)
   Second gas receiver (20)
   First exhaust pipe (30)
   Second exhaust pipe (40)
   Connection exhaust pipe (50)
   Pressure control valve (60)
   Carbon dioxide collecting device (90)
   Combustion cylinders (EG)
   Flue (F)

TI Generating reflex tear secretion involves chemical            stimulation of cornea of eye of subject by applying            controlled jet of gas mixture with high carbon dioxide            content to produce transient decrease in pH of tear            film that covers cornea.
AU BELMONTE MARTINEZ C
   GALLAR MARTINEZ J
   ACOSTA BOJ M C
   MERINO SUAREZ M L
   MERINO SUAREZ M
   BELMONTE M C
   GALLAR M J
   ACOSTA B M C
   MERINO S M L
   CARLOS B M
   GALLARMARTINEZ J
   CARMEN A M
   LUISA M S M
AE UNIV HERNANDEZ ELCHE UMH MIGUEL (UYHE-Non-standard)
   CONSEJO SUPERIOR INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNSJ-C)
   HIGH-SCI RES BOARD DIRECTORS (HIGH-Non-standard)
   UNIV HERNANDEZ ELCHE UMH MIGUEL (UYHE-Non-standard)
   CONSEJO SUPERIOR INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNSJ-C)
GA 2021795224
AB    NOVELTY - Generation of a reflex tear secretion involves                carrying out chemical stimulation of cornea of the                eye by applying a controlled jet of a gas mixture                with a high carbon dioxide, of at least 80% carbon                dioxide to produce a transient pH decrease of the                tear film that covers the cornea and due to the                immediate formation of carbonic acid resulting of                the interaction in the tear of the carbon dioxide                with water to release protons that stimulate the                nerve endings of the polymodal nociceptors of the                corneal surface. The chemical stimulation is                combined with the mechanical and cold stimulation                evoked by the gas jet for a maximal reflex tear                secretion, and the magnitude is measured by                existing techniques for tear flow measurement used                in ophthalmology and optometry for the diagnosis of                tear secretion disturbances for dry eye                disease.
   USE - Generation of reflex tear secretion for dry                eye disease or diagnosing keratoconjunctivitis                sicca.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of easily and                effectively diagnosing the dry eye disease with                high sensitivity and sensitivity and without any                adverse side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) compact device for the generation of                reflex tear secretion; and
   (2) Kit for carrying out a measurement                analysis of the tear flow magnitude.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of                portable hand-held compact device.
   Compact device (1)
   Housing (2)
   Gas outlet nozzle (3)
   Gas puff trigger (4)
   Spacer bracket (5)
   Protective material (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of the gas                  used contains 99% carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The peripheral edge of                  the spacer bracket (5) in the device, intended to                  come into contact with the eye socket is covered                  by a protective material (6) containing                  biocompatible material, such as silicone, with                  antibacterial and hypoallergenic properties for                  greater hygiene and comfort.

The pigs enter the upper pig cage above the ground, the main driving device sinks them to the bottom of the suffocating machine contain full of carbon dioxide, and suffocates the pigs and circulates them above the ground and opens the pig cage to take out the pigs.

GA 202152382E
AB    NOVELTY - System (100) comprises a primary supply unit (120) for supplying a source gas including carbon dioxide and hydrogen, a secondary supply unit (130) supplying consumable nutrients for the methanation reaction, a reaction unit (110) having a reactor in which a methanation reaction is performed by receiving the source gas and the consumable nutrients from the primary supply unit and the secondary supply unit, a tertiary supply unit (140) for supplying a basic substance to the reaction unit, a discharge unit for discharging the methane produced from the reaction unit and a control unit for controlling at least one of the primary to third supply units.
   USE - The system is useful in biological methanation for producing methane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system solves problem of pH fluctuations due to the difference in solubility of carbon dioxide and hydrogen according to change in concentration of the medium and the pressure change according to the biological reaction, and reduces greenhouse gases through the use of carbon dioxide due to the expansion of the supply of renewable energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for controlling system, comprising (a) injecting a medium containing the strain into the reactor, (b) culturing the strain to produce methane, (c) discharging the methane produced through the culture, and (d) adjusting the concentration and pH of consumable nutrients in the culture medium in which the strain is cultivated
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system.
   System (100)
   Reaction unit (110)
   Primary supply unit (120)
   Tertiary supply unit (130)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The consumable nutrients includes primary consumable nutrients and secondary consumable nutrients, where the primary consumable nutrients and the secondary consumable nutrient comprises sodium sulfide and ammonium chloride. The reaction unit includes a medium for culturing the strain, where the strain is cultured by receiving carbon dioxide, hydrogen and consumable nutrients, and the cultured strain undergoes a methanation reaction to produce methane. The strain is an anaerobic strain, and the anaerobic strain is based on Methanothermobacter sp.KEPCO1 (KCTC13648BP). The medium is treated anaerobicly to cultivate anaerobic strains. Preferred Method: The methane preparation is carried out by supplying carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and consumable nutrients independently, and measuring respectively; and injecting independently according to the measured concentration by maintaining pH of 7.1-7.5 in order to cultivate the strain by measuring the pH of the medium and controlling the supply amount of injected hydrogen according to the measured pH value, where basic substance is injected to control the pH when the supply amount of the injected hydrogen exceeds a threshold value,
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The control unit comprises a pH measuring sensor that measures the pH of the reaction unit, a recirculation pump for extracting the culture solution from the reaction unit to measure the pH and a concentration measuring sensor for measuring concentration of consumable nutrients. The control unit controls the supply flow rate of the primary supply unit and the tertiary supply unit according to the measured pH, controls the supply flow rate of the secondary supply unit according to the measured concentration, and controls the primary supply unit, secondary supply unit, and the tertiary supply unit by controlling the supply flow rate of the supply unit and maintains the pH of 7.1-7.5.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - Catalyst used for catalytic conversion of gasoline to produce more ethylene and propylene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst enables to realize catalytic conversion of gasoline and effective utilization of carbon dioxide (CO2) under mild conditions, promotes production of ethylene and propylene, and has high selectivity of ethylene and propylene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing catalyst, which involves mixing noble metal salt, or supported noble metal, FER structure molecular sieve, natural minerals, inorganic oxide, or inorganic oxide precursor; crushing, then spray dried; and roasting; and
   (2) a method for catalytic conversion of gasoline, which involves contacting gasoline, and carbon dioxide with catalyst, and diluent gas to react, where catalyst, which comprises catalyst for catalytic conversion of gasoline to produce more ethylene, and propylene, where conditions of contact reaction, which includes temperature of 350-850 degrees C with pressure of 0.1-2 MegaPascal, weight ratio of carbon dioxide, and gasoline of 0.22-3:1, and gasoline mass space velocity of 0.3-10 per hour; more preferably conditions of contact reaction includes temperature of 500-650 degrees C, pressure of 0.1-0.3 MegaPascal, weight ratio of carbon dioxide, and gasoline of 0.25-2:1, and gasoline mass space velocity of 0.5-5 per hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst includes 20-60 wt.% natural minerals, 10-50 wt.% inorganic oxides, 10-55 wt.% FER structure molecular sieves and 0.1-5 wt.% noble metals in terms of elements, preferably 30-55 wt.% natural minerals, 10-40 wt.% inorganic oxides, 25-50 wt.% FER structure molecular sieves and 0.5-2.5 wt.% noble metals in terms of elements, 0.5-10 wt.% auxiliary agent. Preferred Components: The noble metal is selected from one or more of gold (Au), silver (Ag), ruthenium (Ru), rhodium (Rh), palladinum (Pd), platinum (Pt), iridium (Ir) and osmium (Os), preferably selected from one or more of Au, Ru, Rh and Ir, more preferably Au, Pd and Rh. The FER structure molecular sieve is at least one of ZSM-35 zeolite, Ferrierite zeolite, fluorouracil (FU)-9 zeolite, ISI-6 zeolite, NU-23 zeolite and Sr-D zeolite, preferably FER structure molecular sieve includes ZSM-35 zeolite and Ferrierite zeolite. The natural minerals are selected from one or more of kaolin, montmorillonite, diatomaceous earth, attapulgite, sepiolite, halloysite, hydrotalcite, bentonite and rectorite. The inorganic oxide is selected from silicon oxide, aluminum oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide, zirconium oxide, titanium oxide, boron oxide, amorphous silicon aluminum, aluminum phosphate, tungsten oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide. The auxiliary agent is selected from at least one of IIA, IIIA, IVA, VA, lanthanide, yttrium (Y), chromium (Cr), manganese (Mn), rhenium (Re), iron (Fe), and cadmium (Cd), preferably selected from calcium (Ca), Fe, gallium (Ga), indium (In), bismuth (Bi), lanthanum (La) and Mn. The supported noble metal includes carrier and noble metal supported on carrier; carrier is selected from aluminum oxide, silicon oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide, zirconium oxide, tungsten oxide, tungsten oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide, molybdenum oxide-zirconia, titanium oxide, molybdenum oxide-titanium oxide, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, tin oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide and niobium oxide, preferably aluminum oxide, aluminum oxide-silicon oxide and tungsten oxide-zirconia. Preferred Method: The method for preparing catalyst involves introducing auxiliary agent into catalyst followed by mixing and crushing auxiliary agent precursor, noble metal salt and supported noble metal, FER structure molecular sieve, natural mineral, inorganic oxide or inorganic oxide precursor; mixing and crushing supported noble metals containing additives, FER structure molecular sieve, natural minerals, inorganic oxides or inorganic oxide precursors. Preferred Conditions: The firing conditions includes firing atmosphere is air atmosphere, inert atmosphere or water vapor atmosphere, firing temperature is 400-800 degrees C, and firing time is 0.5-8 hours.

By storing greenhouse gases in the form of carbonates that exist in their natural state, the loss of ammonia is reduced by removing side reactions caused by sulfur oxides remaining during ammonia regeneration and preventing impurities from being included when recovering ammonia.

PT P
PN CN112390246-A
TI Synthesizing nitrogen-doped porous carbon by selectively growing ultra-small copper template method in carbon nano-sheet by anion-induced carbon nano-sheet involves carbonizing poly-4-vinyl pyridine and copper chloride dihydrate and washing.
AU YANG Z
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2021210687
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing nitrogen-doped porous carbon by selectively growing ultra-small copper template method in carbon nano-sheet by anion- induced carbon nano-sheet comprises taking poly-4-vinyl pyridine and copper chloride dihydrate as raw materials, taking cupric ion and poly-4-vinyl pyridine to form coordination compound, then carbonizing and acid-washing, obtaining the nitrogen- doped porous carbon Cu-NDPC, adjusting the size of in-situ copper nano-cluster generated in the carbonization process by anion chloride-and carbonization temperature, where the hole diameter of Cu-NDPC obtained by in-situ Cu nano-cluster as template is 2-15 angstrom.
   USE - The nitrogen-doped porous carbon is useful as an adsorbent for the adsorption of acetylene, ethane, propane and carbon dioxide, and has x/methane and carbon dioxide/N2IAST selectivity, where x includes acetylene, ethane, propane or carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon: has unique carbon nano-sheet network structure, super-high specific surface area, ultra-large micropore volume, proper surface N doping and centralized hole diameter distribution, exhibits super-high acetylene, ethane, propane and carbon dioxide adsorption capacity and ultra-high x/CH4 and CO2/N2IAST selectivity; and has good cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing nitrogen-doped porous carbon by selectively growing ultra-small copper template method in carbon nano-sheet by anion- induced carbon nano-sheet, by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) mixing 4-ethylene pyridine and copper chloride dihydrate in solvent, stirring, then vacuum extracting and distilling to obtain coordination compound Cu-P4VP, (ii) carbonizing Cu-P4VP under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain copper/nitrogen-doped carbon composite material (Cu-NDC), and generating in-situ Cu nano-cluster in the carbonization process, (iii) removing in-situ copper nano-cluster remained in the Cu-NDC by acid washing, and (iv) washing the Cu-NDC after acid washing with de-ionized water to neutral, filtering to obtain filter cake, drying the filter cake to obtain the Cu-NDPC. The step (ii) comprises carbonizing at 600-1000 degrees C for 1-10 times, and heating at a rate of 2.5-7.5 degrees C/minute. The step (iii) comprises using hydrochloric acid for acid washing for 5-24 hours, where the concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 0.5-2.5 M. The step (iv) comprises drying at 40-80 degrees C for 12-36 hours. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of poly-4-ethylene pyridine and copper chloride dihydrate in the step (i) is 0.1-1.0:1.0-10.0, the mol ratio of cupric ion/N is 5 in coordination compound Cu-P4VP-5, and the solvent is absolute ethyl alcohol. The nitrogen-doped porous carbon has a carbon nano-sheet network structure, and the specific surface area of 1100- 2800 m2, preferably 1300-2200 m2/g, micropore volume of 0.4-1.5 cm3/g, preferably 0.6-1 cm3/g, surface N doping of 1-10 at.%, preferably 2-6 at.% and aperture of 2-15 Angstrom, preferably 4.1-4.8 Angstrom.

Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Controlling automobile air quality comprises obtaining carbon dioxide concentration in target vehicle, calculating concentration ratio, judging concentration ratio value greater than ratio threshold value, and controlling target vehicle.
AU XIAO J
   ZHU X
   YANG J
   ZHAO S
AE UNIV JIUJIANG (UNJJ-C)
GA 202077263V
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling automobile air quality comprises obtaining carbon dioxide concentration in target vehicle and carbon monoxide environmental concentration of environment where target vehicle is currently located, calculating concentration ratio between carbon dioxide concentration and carbon monoxide environmental concentration, judging whether concentration ratio value is greater than ratio threshold value, controlling target vehicle to switch to external circulation when judging concentration ratio value is greater than ratio threshold value, controlling target vehicle to switch to internal circulation when judging concentration ratio value is less than or equal to the ratio threshold value, obtaining carbon monoxide vehicle concentration in target vehicle, sending reminders and alarms to driver when carbon monoxide concentration in vehicle is within preset concentration range and controlling target vehicle flameout when carbon monoxide concentration in vehicle is greater than first preset concentration.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling automobile air quality.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: automatically judges whether the internal circulation or the external circulation needs to be switched by obtaining the carbon dioxide concentration in the target vehicle and the carbon monoxide concentration in the current environment; improves the efficiency of automobile air quality control; prevents the phenomenon of low control efficiency caused by manual switching.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) automobile air quality control system;
   (2) mobile terminal; and
   (3) storage medium comprising a set of instructions for controlling automobile air quality.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for controlling automobile air quality (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   USE - A greenhouse gas (GHG) neutral and GHG plus built environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The GHG Neutral and GHG Plus Built Environments, not only scientifically supersedes rating methodologies, but also quantifies the actual and real building footprint throughout conception through its usable duration. It specifically tunnels down into one of the foremost contributors to global warming potential at the facility level, while incorporating all quantifiable facets of such contributive factors. It has the capacity within the defined scope to precisely omit discrepancies within legislative policy and programs geared at addressing the building industries (both public and private) climate affects through its solution. AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen peroxide absorption, decomposition and discharge mechanism for cell tissue workstations has a cell tissue workstation unit (1), a carbon dioxide incubator (4), a transmission window (3), a through hole opened in the unit of the cell tissue workstation and a hydrogen peroxide shunt discharge device (7) connected to the through hole. A partition doors (6) are installed between the cell tissue workstation units and the carbon dioxide incubators and transmission windows installed on both sides. The transmission window is connected to a hydrogen peroxide generator (2). The cell tissue workstation unit internally has a cell operation area, a catalyst (10) for absorbing used hydrogen peroxide inside and a centrifuge (5) connected with the cell operation area. The hydrogen peroxide shunt discharge device is provided with a number of accelerated adsorption components for absorbing used hydrogen peroxide.
   USE - Hydrogen peroxide absorption, decomposition and discharge mechanism for cell tissue workstations.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanism reduces decomposition time, where the hydrogen peroxide atomized gas is directly discharged to the atmosphere without decomposition of the catalyst, and the atomized hydrogen peroxide is further adsorbed by the accelerated adsorption component.

PN WO2020052979-A1; AU2019337955-A1; CA3112445-A1; IN202147016502-A; CN112673124-A; KR2021059703-A; BR112021004742-A2; EP3850124-A1; VN79290-A; US2021363079-A1; JP2022500555-W; US11247955-B2
TI Preparing methanol product involves e.g. mixing first process stream including carbon dioxide and second process stream including hydrogen by electrolyzing water and catalytically converting obtained methanol synthesis gas into raw methanol.
AU TJAERNEHOV E A
   PEDERSEN L S
   HULTQVIST M
   ESKESEN S G
   JENSEN L W
   TJARNEHOV E A
   LOUISE J W
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2020218682
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a methanol product, comprises: (a) providing a first process stream consisting essentially of carbon dioxide; (b) providing a second process stream consisting of hydrogen by electrolyzing water in an electrolysis unit; (c) mixing the first and second process stream to obtain a methanol synthesis gas with a mole ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide of 2.5-3.5; (d) catalytically converting the methanol synthesis gas into raw methanol in at least one methanol reactor; (e) purifying the raw methanol in a distillation unit; and (f) recovering waste heat generated in the electrolysis unit in step (b) by transferring the waste heat to a circulating heat transfer medium by indirect heat exchange with the waste heat and by indirect heat exchange of the heated heat transfer medium with steam used for the distillation of the raw methanol, where the heated transfer medium is compressed upstream the indirect heat exchange with steam.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing a methanol product.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes electrolysis of water for the preparation of hydrogen and carbon dioxide recovery from e.g. off or flue gas, in the catalytic preparation of methanol; utilizes the waste heat from water electrolysis in methanol distillation where steam is used, this will lower the requirement to steam produced; and reduces the overall energy consumption as the power required to operate the compressor is less than the power from heat utilized.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The heat transfer medium has a boiling point lower than the waste heat from the electrolysis at a pressure prevailing in the indirect heat exchange with the waste heat and a boiling point higher than the temperature in the indirect heat exchange with the steam at a pressure prevailing in the indirect heat exchange with the steam. The distillation unit comprises at least two distillation towers operated in series. At least a part of the water electrolyzed in the electrolysis unit is distillation water withdrawn from the methanol distillation unit.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202019369X
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus comprises an acquisition unit                that is configured to acquire hydrogen supplyamount                in a supply base (2) and the hydrogen demanding                amount at each of several demand base (4). A route                generation unit is configured to generate several                delivery routes for connecting supply bases and the                demand bases. A delivery plan unit is configured to                generate the delivery plan of the hydrogen                generated at supply base based on evaluation value.                An evaluation value calculation unit is configured                to calculate evaluation value based on carbon                dioxide equivalent amount for every demand bases                according to usage-amount of alternative energy                with respect to insufficient hydrogen quantity when                lack of hydrogen arises when hydrogen supplyamount                is supplied to demand bases.
   USE - Hydrogen delivery plan apparatus mounted in                delivery vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The generation of the carbon dioxide which                arises when delivering hydrogen to several demand                base is prevented.

The middle of nozzle is O2 channel. The outside of O2 channel is the CO2 annular slot channel. The water inlet ring seam is set outside the CO2 ring seam channel. PN US2019343756-A1
TI Sorghum distillery residue extract using Kinmen Sorghum distillery residue by first statically extracting with ethanol and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid and continuously dynamically extracted used as additive for cosmetic preparation.
AU KO W
   WU Y
AE HAIR ORIGHT INT CORP (HAIR-Non-standard)
GA 201995316Y
AB    NOVELTY - Sorghum distillery residue extract using Kinmen Sorghum distillery residue as raw material and carbon dioxide as solvent where the distillery residue raw material is first statically extracted by ethanol and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid for 2 hours and then continuously dynamically extracted by the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid for 2 hours, a moisture content of the used Sorghum distillery residue is lower than 30% after drying, an operating pressure is 150-350 bar, and a temperature is 40-60 degrees C is claimed.
   USE - The distillery residue extract is useful as antioxidative, and inhibit tyrosinase and reduce the content of melanin produced, and as an additive for preparation of cosmetic or cosmeceutical compositions for skin aging prevention, whitening, tender whitening, fading spots, or bright skin (claimed). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The extract is obtained by method: that is performed and avoids the use of organic solvents, which cause harm to the environment and human body thus fully green and nontoxic extraction process without harmful chemical solvents; with high purity and low toxicity; makes good use of the waste, generated after the Sorghum distillation and brewing process of the sorghum liquor, and hopes to transform the Sorghum distillery residue waste into a usable matter thus reducing environmental pollution and increasing economic value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Sorghum distillery residue extract using Kinmen Sorghum distillery residue as raw material where the extract is conducted with pure water at 50-99 degrees C for 2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The optimal conditions for supercritical fluid extraction are: an operating pressure of 170 bar and a temperature of 40 degrees C, an operating pressure of 170 bar and a temperature of 50 degrees C, an operating pressure of 170 bar and a temperature of 65 degrees C, an operating pressure of 250 bar and a temperature of 40 degrees C, and an operating pressure of 300 bar and a temperature of 40 degrees C. The compositions comprises 0.001-0.02 wt.% extract based on total weight of polyphenols, from the distillery residue. The extracted with a supercritical fluid, has optimal cytotoxicity, antioxidative capacity, and whitening ability when being in an amount of 0.001-0.003 wt.%, based on a total weight of total polyphenols.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Sorghum distillery residue was treated at 100-120 degrees C by a continuous fluidized bed dryer and then are grinded by a pulverizer. 20 g of the Sorghum distillery residue was weighed and kept into conical flask, and then a one-hour of stirring extraction was conducted with 200 ml pure water (75 degrees C) and 200 ml pure water (90 degrees C), respectively. At the end of the extraction, the volumes of filtrates were quantified after filtration with filter paper. The supercritical fluid extraction was conducted by using a 250 ml extraction tank. The extraction takes 93.51 g of Sorghum distillery residue powder. Carbon dioxide was turned on to enter the pipeline. A back pressure regulator was adjusted to control system pressures of 170 bar, and the temperature in the extraction tank was adjusted (time using a temperature of 40 degrees C.). The solvents used are supercritical carbon dioxide and ethanol. Timing was started after the pressure and temperature reach the experimental set values. The sample was first soaked in the extraction tank for 2 hours for static extraction using ethanol and a carbon dioxide supercritical fluid. After that, dynamic extraction was conducted with carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 0.2 l/hour and ethanol at a flow rate of 0.4 l/hour for 2 hours. The sample was collected in two separation tank was mixed homogeneously and stored for use.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

PN CN110200865-A
TI Preparing Rosa roxbunghii extract used in e.g. anti-allergic composition, by pretreating Rosa roxbunghii, juicing, performing supercritical fluid extraction of solid residue, combining juice and extract, dialyzing and concentrating extract.
AU HE J
AE GUANGZHOU QIANZHOU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201978602D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Rosa roxbunghii extract, involves (i) washing the R.roxbunghii, removing core and cutting into slices, (ii) crushing the slices using a press to obtain a pressed juice, performing re-infiltration, re-pressing the juice to obtain a pressed juice, combining the juices, and retaining the solid residue, (iii) adding the solid residue to a supercritical fluid extraction tank, adding solvent, preheating, uniformly stirring, performing pressure reduction and temperature adjustment step, reducing the solvent below the critical pressure of carbon dioxide, separating the extract from carbon dioxide, since the solubility of carbon dioxide is extremely reduced, extracting components by a separator, and introducing into an extraction tank to obtain an extract, and recycling the carbon dioxide by a circulation pump, and (iv) combining the juice and the extract, dialyzing to remove small molecular sugars and impurities, and concentrating to obtain the extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing R.roxbunghii extract, which is used in anti-aging composition, anti-allergic composition, anti-oxidant composition and anti-inflammatory composition (all claimed) for providing anti-oxidation, anti-allergic, anti-inflammatory and anti-aging other activities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the combination of pressing juice and supercritical fluid extraction to extract the R.roxbunghii extract, is simple and efficient, has mild extraction condition, does not destroys the components of R.roxbunghii, and adopts dialysis purification to remove inactive impurities and highly viscous small molecular sugars. The R.roxbunghii extract contains various active ingredients, has long shelf life, ensures convenient storage and transportation, and has broad application prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the pressure is reduced to less than 7 MPa in the pressure reduction step, and the temperature is adjusted to 30 degrees C in the temperature adjustment step. In step (iii), the solvent is carbon dioxide, and the temperature of the preheating step is 20 degrees C. In step (iv), the dialysis membrane pore size is 10-50 nm. In step (iv), the concentration step is carried out under a low pressure of -0.01 MPa at 30-50 degrees C. Preferred Device: In step (ii), the press is a twin screw press. Preferred Properties: In step (iv), the density of the R.roxbunghii extract is 1.5-2 g/cm3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The material of the dialysis membrane is chosen from regenerated cellulose, cellulose acetate, polyacrylonitrile, ethylene-vinyl alcohol copolymer, poly(methyl methacrylate), polysulfone and/or polyacrylamide.
   ACTIVITY - Antiallergic; Nootropic; Dermatological; Antiinflammatory.

PN JP2019098357-A
TI Laser processing apparatus for irradiating carbon            dioxide laser beam to workpiece and performing fine            hole-punching process, injects laser beam at right            angles to workpiece and completes laser beam emitted            from galvanometer mirror.
AU WADA S
   INAGAWA O
   MATSUBARA Y
AE IBIDEN CO LTD (IBIG-C)
GA 201952003F
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a laser resonator (1) which                emits laser beam (B) of carbon dioxide laser. Two                acousto-optical deflectors (AOD) (2, 3) are                mutually located in series on optical path of laser                beam emitted from laser resonator. Two AODBs make                the angle according to frequency of ultrasonic wave                input that respectively deflects laser beam within                mutually right-angled plane. A galvanometer mirror                (4, 5) of two sheets deflects laser beam that is                combined together so that laser beam rotates to                surroundings of axis line at right angles to each                other, and emitted from AOD. An optical system (8)                is located between AOD and galvanometer mirror,                expands beam diameter of laser beam emitted from                AOD, and injects laser beam into galvanometer                mirror. A lens (7) injects laser beam at right                angles to workpiece, and completes laser beam                emitted from galvanometer mirror in position of                workpiece. The AOD uses germanium crystal.
   USE - Laser processing apparatus for irradiating                carbon dioxide laser beam to workpiece and                performing fine hole-punching process.
   ADVANTAGE - The through holes formed in object to be                processed than in past is reduced and density                increase of an electronic circuit is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a laser                processing method for irradiating carbon dioxide                laser beam to workpiece and performing fine                hole-punching process.

PN US2019128101-A1; US10459897-B2
TI Method for development of residual oil zone (ROZ) resource, involves quantitatively and qualitatively evaluating how in situ microbial self limitation (MSL) conditions in ROZ govern microbial processing of petroleum.
AU VANCE D B
AE ARCADIS CORP SERVICES INC (ARCA-Non-standard)
GA 201937401L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves quantitatively and qualitatively evaluating how in situ MSL conditions in a ROZ govern microbial processing of petroleum. Operations associated with the ROZ development are determined through processes, including identifying and obtaining media samples of gas, water, petroleum, and mineral matrix from the ROZ. Specific microbial consortia and metabolic pathways within the samples are identified. The effects of the microbial processes of the consortia on mineral species are determined. Using the consortia, biogenic mineral production of amorphous, nano-crystalline and chaotic mineral forms with enhanced reactivity towards carbon dioxide are effected by determining activity of the consortia in presence of petroleum hydrocarbons, whether modification of native hydrocarbon species generates charged or polar species, effect of biosurfactants on the samples, capacity for stimulation of iron reduction, and effect of sulfate reduction.
   USE - Method for development of ROZ resource, particularly for petroleum recovery and carbon dioxide sequestration.
   ADVANTAGE - Efficient identification and assessment of residual oil zones (ROZs) significantly increases the geographic footprint and target locations into which carbon dioxide can be injected and ultimately utilized and permanently sequestered in commercial operations generating value to partially offset the parasitic costs associated with the capture of anthropogenic carbon dioxide. Enables use of multimedia sampling and analyzes to generate data defining MSL conditions to quantitatively and qualitatively evaluate the condition in the ROZ to allow for decision making regarding the economics and proper operations that will be associated with development of that ROZ resource.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic of a petroleum production zone.

PN CN109612011-A; CN109612011-B
TI Method for controlling fresh air system, involves calculating opening degree of control valve according to cooling load and temperature of cooling water, and controlling control valve according to opening degree.
AU CAO L
AE GD MIDEA AIR-CONDITIONING EQUIP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
   GUANGDONG MIDEA AIR-CONDITIONING EQUIP (MEDE-C)
GA 2019357267
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining concentration of indoor carbon dioxide. Judgment is made to determine whether the concentration of the carbon dioxide is less than preset concentration threshold. A control valve is controlled to close when first humidity is equal to or greater than sum of second humidity and preset difference humidity. A fan is turned on to set gear position. The gear position is determined on according to the concentration of the carbon dioxide. Cold load required for fresh air cooling of a fresh air system is obtained according to the gear position when the fan is turned on. Temperature of cooling water is obtained. Opening degree of the control valve is calculated according to the cooling load and the temperature of the cooling water. The control valve is controlled according to the opening degree.
   USE - Method for controlling a fresh air system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables turning on the fan to prevent mildew in a ventilating pipeline when indoor carbon dioxide concentration is low and humidity in the ventilating pipeline is high, and accurately reducing fresh air supply temperature, and realizing simple implementation and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a device for controlling a fresh air system
   (2) an electronic device comprising a processor and a memory for executing a method for controlling a fresh air system
   (3) a computer-readable storage medium for storing set of instructions for a method controlling a fresh air system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for controlling a fresh air system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

TI Carbon abatement system for naturally abating carbon emissions adjacent to paved environments, has carbon abatement panel which is rotatable about axis to maximize exposure of carbon abatement material to sunlight.
AU LARSON A H
AE LARSON A H (LARS-Individual)
GA 201919651X
AB    NOVELTY - The system (20) has a carbon abatement panel (22) including a carbon abatement material (40). The carbon abatement panel is rotatable about an axis to maximize exposure of the carbon abatement material to sunlight. The carbon abatement panel is mounted adjacent an area where carbon dioxide is emitted such that the carbon dioxide naturally contacts the carbon abatement material. The area where carbon dioxide is emitted includes a paved environment. The axis of the first carbon abatement is parallel and offset from the second carbon abatement panel. The carbon abatement panel includes carbon abatement assembly. The carbon abatement material is a part of the carbon abatement assembly.
   USE - Carbon abatement system for naturally abating carbon emissions adjacent to paved environments.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon abatement panel is rotated about an axis to maximize exposure of the carbon abatement material to sunlight. By forming the carbon abatement panel from multiple of carbon abatement modules, each individual modules is easily be replaced e.g. in the event of a failure of the carbon abatement material. The heating elements are selectively operable to eliminate snow or ice accumulation on when the panel is geographically located in a region that experiences a winter climate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for abating carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN US2018179071-A1; KR2018087222-A; KR1978938-B1; US10759664-B2
TI Preparing silicon carbide used as a highly            heat-resistant porous catalyst support, a filter, or an            engineering material, comprises reacting a            silicon-containing compound with carbon dioxide, where            a reducing agent is used.
AU CHO W C
   SEO M W
   LEE H I
   LEE J G
   BAE K K
   KIM C H
   KIM J W
   PARK C S
   KANG K
   JEONG S U
   CHO H S
   WON C C
   MYUNG W S
   HAE I L
   JAE G L
   CHANG H K
   HYUN S C
   CHUL C W
   WON S M
   IN L H
   GOO L J
   HEE K C
   SEOK C H
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
   KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2018506248
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon carbide, comprises reacting                a silicon-containing compound with carbon dioxide,                where a reducing agent is used.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon                carbide which is useful as a highly heat-resistant                porous catalyst support, a filter, or an                engineering material (all claimed), where the                filter is e.g. a diesel engine dust filter, dust                filters for heat exchangers and power plants, and                filters for casting, and special applications e.g.                high-temperature lightweight structural materials,                kiln furniture for high temperature furnaces,                bulletproof materials, shock absorbers and preforms                for composite materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the silicon carbide: with                a yield of greater than or equal to 90%; and with a                high Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area of greater                than or equal to 50 m2/g (claimed), hence has                excellent processability; having 2 phases i.e.                alpha -silicon carbide phase and the beta -silicon                carbide phase that can be easily adjusted through a                heat scavenger; and that has an excellent physical                strength and high resistance to chemical attacks,                and thus may be used in a variety of fields. The                method utilizes the carbon dioxide generated as a                byproduct in conventional iron or cement                manufacturing processes or from solid dry ice at                room temperature conditions, hence reducing the                amount of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and                thus is eco-friendly, safe and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the                silicon carbide prepared by the method as above per                se, has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area of                greater than or equal to 50 m2/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon-containing                  compound is amorphous silica, where the amorphous                  silica is chaff-derived silica. The silicon                  carbide comprises alpha -silicon carbide and beta                  -silicon carbide, where a content of the beta                  -silicon carbide is greater than or equal to 40                  wt.% with respect to a total weight of the                  silicon carbide. The silicon carbide: is porous                  silicon carbide; has a pore volume of greater                  than or equal to 0.26 cm3/g; and has a pore size                  of greater than or equal to 2 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises:                  reacting 4 moles of magnesium with silica and                  carbon dioxide gas to give 4 moles of magnesium                  oxide and silicon carbide; or a two-stage                  reaction comprising reacting 4 moles of magnesium                  with silica to give magnesium silicide and 2                  moles of magnesium oxide in the first stage and                  reacting the obtained magnesium silicide with 2                  moles of magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide gas                  to give 4 moles of magnesium oxide and silicon                  carbide in the second stage; or reacting                  magnesium silicide with 2 moles of magnesium                  oxide and carbon dioxide gas to give 4 moles of                  magnesium oxide and silicon carbide. The reaction                  is performed at 1400-1700 degrees C. The method                  further comprises: using a heat scavenger; and                  post-treating silicon carbide prepared using the                  method.
   EXAMPLE - 8 g of Chaff-derived silica, 12.94 g of                magnesium and 5.86 g of carbon dioxide (dry ice)                were placed into a 500 ml container and milled                using a milling machine in an argon gas atmosphere                up to ignition time, thus completing the                preparation of silicon carbide. At this time, the                RPM was 350, and a ball mass to powder mass ratio                was 30:1. Then 1600 ml of distilled water, 200 ml                of ethanol, and 200 ml of hydrochloric acid were                mixed to prepare an acid treatment solution, and                then the prepared silicon carbide was added to the                acid treatment solution, followed by stirring at                room temperature and 250 rpm for 1 hour, thus                completing primary acid treatment. The primary acid                treatment-completed silicon carbide was filtered                using a filter, and then dried in an oven at 80                degrees C, followed by secondary acid treatment                using an acid treatment solution prepared by mixing                1600 ml of distilled water, 200 ml of ethanol, and                200 ml of hydrochloric acid. At this time, the                secondary acid treatment was performed using a                sonicator at room temperature for 1 hour. The                secondary acid treatment-completed silicon carbide                was filtered using a filter and then dried in an                oven at 80 degrees C. and the dried silicon carbide                was mixed with sodium hydroxide in 2000 ml of                distilled water to satisfy a pH of 14, followed by                stirring at 80 degrees C and 250 rpm for 1 hour                using a magnetic stirrer, filtration using a                filter, and drying in an oven at 80 degrees C to                give the silicon carbide with high                Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area and high pore                size.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN108187628-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorption material,            involves mixing gangue, calcium carbonate and zinc            chloride, carrying out microwave pyrolysis, adding            alkali solution, heating and reacting with calcium            nitrate aqueous solution.
AU CHEN H
   GAO J
   GAO W
   HAN J
   LI C
   LIU Y
AE CHEN H (CHEN-Individual)
   GAO J (GAOJ-Individual)
   GAO W (GAOW-Individual)
   HAN J (HANJ-Individual)
   LI C (LICC-Individual)
   LIU Y (LIUY-Individual)
GA 201850868P
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of carbon dioxide adsorption                material, involves (1) mixing gangue, calcium                carbonate and zinc chloride at mass ratio of                (80-90) g, (1-5) g and (1-5) g, carrying out                microwave pyrolysis with power of 300-400 W at                700-850 degrees C for 10-25 minutes and cooling to                obtain sample (A), (2) adding sample (A) to 1.2-2.5                mol/L alkali solution, stirring at 60-70 degrees C                for 2.5-3 hours, filtering and drying to obtain                sample (B), (3) immersing sample (B) in mixed                aqueous solution of calcium nitrate and nickel                nitrate, ultrasonically reacting for 40 minutes                with power of 700 W, filtering, drying and                calcining to obtain sample (C), (4) heating sample                (C) at 374.3 degrees C, introducing water and                nitrogen at mass ratio of 3:1, controlling pressure                at 22.05 MPa and reacting for 20-25 minutes to                obtain sample (D), (5) mixing organic amine with                alcohol solution, adding sample (D), ultrasonically                reacting for 30 minutes with power of 700 W,                heating to remove alcohol solvent and drying at                100-115 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorption                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon                dioxide adsorption material with large collection                capacity and fast absorbing speed by simple and                economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In step (1), mixing gangue, calcium carbonate                and zinc chloride at mass ratio of (80-90) g, (1-5)                g and (1-5) g. The organic amine is                triethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine,                hexamethylenetetramine, hexamethyleneimine,                triethylenediamine, polyethyleneimine,                diethanolamine or triethanolamine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The step (2) involves                  using calcium acetate aqueous solution as alkali                  solution. The concentration of calcium nitrate                  solution is 1.02-1.35 mol/L, and the                  concentration of nickel nitrate solution is                  0.1-0.3 mol/L. The volume ratio of two solutions                  is 1:2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol is                  methanol, ethanol or propyl alcohol.

PN CN108047457-A; CN108047457-B
TI Preparation of manganese metal organic frame material used for conversion of carbon dioxide to epoxycarbonate involves reacting 2,4,6-tris((p-carboxyphenyl)amino)-1,3,5-triazine and manganese chloride, and cooling slowly to room temperature.
AU MA J
   CHEN Y
   CHENG P
   CHEN J
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
   UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 201840957B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of manganese metal organic frame material comprises dissolving 2,4,6-tris((p-carboxyphenyl)amino)-1,3,5-triazine and manganese chloride in N,N-dimethylacetamide, adding distilled water, reacting mixture in PTFE -lined reactor at 120 degrees C for 72 hours, and cooling slowly to room temperature at 5 degrees C/hour rate to obtain product yield of 83%. The mass-volume ratio of 2,4,6-tris((p-carboxyphenyl)amino)-1,3,5-triazine, manganese chloride, N,N-dimethylacetamide and distilled water is 73 mg:12.6 mg:3 mL:0.75 mL:25 mL.
   USE - Method for preparing manganese metal organic frame material used for conversion of carbon dioxide to epoxycarbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good structure stability and high catalytic activity, and can be recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of manganese metal organic frame material for catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to epoxycarbonate comprising mixing 100 mg manganese metal organic frame material and 3.6 mmol tetrabutylammonium bromide Schlenk flask, connecting flask to vacuum line to degas, filling with carbon dioxide gas, injecting flask with 5 mmol substrate oxidized styrene, 2-(chloromethyl)oxirane, 3-phenoxy-1,2-epoxypropane, 3-(1-naphthyloxy)-1,2-epoxypropane and benzyl glycidyl ether, placing in 60 degrees C oil bath, reacting for 24 hours to obtain crude product, and passing through silica gel column to obtain product yield of 90%.
