PN CN107858197-A
TI Preparing low-acid-price high-quality edible tea            seed oil involves crushing dehulled tea seeds, adding            to multi-stage separation supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction device to adjusting extraction temperature,            and extraction pressure.
AU LIN S
   XIE J
   LIN X
   YU J
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018299193
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-acid-price high-quality edible                tea seed oil involves crushing dehulled tea seeds,                adding to a multi-stage separation supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction device to adjusting                extraction temperature, extraction pressure and                extraction time separating the kettle I                temperature, separating the kettle I pressure,                separating kettle II temperature, separating the                kettle II pressure.
   USE - Method for preparing low-acid-price                high-quality edible tea seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare low-acid-price                high-quality edible tea seed oil does not produces                any residue of solvent and heavy metal, in                cost-effective manner, which high oil yield, short                life cycle, avoids oxidation and spoilage of                product, keeps nutrient component in the oil, no                inflammable and explosive danger, and keeps natural                quality of the tea oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low-acid-price high-quality edible                tea seed oil involves crushing dehulled tea seeds,                adding to a multi-stage separation supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction device to adjusting                extraction temperature, extraction pressure and                extraction time separating the kettle I                temperature, separating the kettle I pressure,                separating kettle II temperature, separating the                kettle II pressure, separating the kettle III                temperature and separating the kettle III pressure                to carrying out the camellia oil extraction to                obtain camellia oleifera oil from the separation                kettle I. The multi-stage separation supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction oil camellia oil is added                to the molecular distillation refining process,                adjusted the vacuum degree, distillation                temperature, scraped film speed, feed rate and                condensation temperature for refining, and                resulting oil is low-acid high quality edible tea                seed oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The supercritical                  carbon dioxide extraction temperature is 40-50                  degrees ., and the supercritical CO2 extraction                  pressure is 30-45MPa for 1-2 hours. The                  temperature of the separation kettle I is 45-60                  degrees C and pressure of the separation kettle I                  is 10-20MPa. The temperature of the separating                  kettle II in the separating kettle is 30-50                  degrees C, and the pressure of the separating                  kettle II is 4-8MPa. The temperature of the                  separation kettle III in the separation kettle is                  30-50 degrees C, and the pressure of the                  separation kettle III is 4-8MPa. The degree of                  vacuum in the molecular distillation is 2-9                  Pascal and the distillation temperature is                  120-180 degrees C. The speed of scraping the film                  in the molecular distillation is 100-300                  revolutions per minute. The feeding speed in the                  molecular distillation is 10-50 mL/minute. The                  condensation temperature in the molecular                  distillation is 20-50 degrees C.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN111762784-A
TI Production of high purity carbon dioxide by controlling to finish conveying raw material gas, washing raw material gas with water, removing mechanical impurity and water soluble gas, compressing and cooling raw material gas, and purifying.
AU HUANG J
   LIU C
   CAI Y
AE CHONGQING TONGHUI GAS CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2020A20531
AB    NOVELTY - Production of high purity carbon dioxide comprises controlling to finish conveying the raw material gas; washing the raw material gas with water, and removing the mechanical impurity and water soluble gas; compressing and cooling the raw material gas; purifying the raw material gas, and removing the water, impurity and abnormal smell in the raw material gas; refrigerating and liquefying, converting the gas carbon dioxide into liquid by a large amount of cold energy generated by converting liquid ammonia from liquid state to gaseous state; purifying, removing the impurity in the liquid carbon dioxide by the rectifying principle; gasifying the liquid carbon dioxide to obtain gaseous carbon dioxide; adsorbing impurities using adsorption column; refrigerating and rectifying, converting gaseous carbon dioxide into liquid carbon dioxide, rectifying and purifying; and storing and filling.
   USE - The method is for production of high purity carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The finally obtained liquid carbon dioxide does not have other impurities, and has purity of more than 99.999%. The method converts gas ammonia into liquid ammonia to realize circulation use of liquid ammonia, reduces the cost, only needs to ensure the raw material gas supply is enough to realize continuous production of liquid carbon dioxide, and does not need to add or supplement the material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The raw material gas water washing uses desalted water to clean the impurity in the raw material gas. It comprises using raw material gas water washing tower, circulating water pump and cooling water tower, so as to circularly use the desalted water. The raw material gas compression uses oil-free lubrication compressor to pressurize the gas. It is divided into three sections of compression, where the gas pressure is gradually increased to 2.0-2.5 MPa by stages, and the raw material gas cooling uses evaporation cooler to cool the gas after three sections of compression to room temperature.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).)

PN CN111720948-A
TI Control method for fresh air air conditioner, involves calculating the difference between the first carbon dioxide concentration and the second carbon dioxide concentration to obtain the change value of the carbon dioxide concentration.
AU CHEN C
   YANG J
   JI M
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC ZHUHAI (GREZ-C)
GA 202099211K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating the difference between the first carbon dioxide concentration and the second carbon dioxide concentration to obtain the change value of the carbon dioxide concentration. The windshield of the fresh air air conditioner is controlled according to the comparison result of the carbon dioxide concentration change value and a preset threshold value. If the comparison result is that the carbon dioxide concentration change value is not less than the preset threshold, it is determined that the carbon dioxide detection device has failed, and the air quality in the area before the carbon dioxide detection device fails is determined, and the windshield of the fresh air conditioner is controlled according to the air quality.
   USE - Control method for fresh air air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The fresh air control method can perform fresh air control even when the carbon dioxide detection device fails.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a control device for a fresh air conditioner; and
   (2) a non-volatile storage medium with a stored program, where the program is operated to control the device of the non-volatile storage medium to perform the method for controlling fresh air air conditioner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a control method for fresh air air conditioner. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AU BROWN S M
AE SAGETECH MEDICAL EQUIP LTD (SAGE-Non-standard)
GA 202150467M
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of desflurane involves reacting an                isoflurane-dissolved supercritical carbon dioxide                (201) with a fluorine donor at a temperature at or                above the critical temperature of carbon dioxide                and pressure at or above the critical pressure of                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Manufacture of desflurane used as anaesthetic                agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces desflurane with improved                yield using an environmentally-friendly                solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                (100) for manufacturing and purification of                desflurane, which has a reaction chamber (210) in                which the supercritical carbon dioxide passes,                isoflurane is introduced and dissolved into the                supercritical carbon dioxide, and the fluorine                donor is introduced to cause a fluoro-substitution                reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - Carbon dioxide 201 contained in a pressurised                cylinder 202 is transferred above critical pressure                (72.9 bar) by a pump 206 and passed into an                accumulator 208 in a temperature controlled                environment (not shown) above the critical                temperature of carbon dioxide (31.1 degrees                Centigrade). Supercritical CO2 passes into a first                reaction chamber 2I0a made of a pressure and                temperature tolerant material, preferably although                not exclusively stainless steel or aluminium, and                coated in an inert material, preferably but not                limited to Teflon or another material that does not                react with fluoroethers or supercritical carbon                dioxide.
   System (100)
   Carbon dioxide (201)
   Cylinder (202)
   Pump (206)
   Reaction chamber (210)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluorine donor                  comprises potassium fluoride, sodium fluoride,                  anhydrous fluorine, or gaseous fluorine. The                  reaction mixture is passed through the reaction                  chamber containing a catalyst comprising antimony                  pentahalide, a transition metal trifluoride, a                  transition metal oxide and/or a phase-transfer                  catalyst. The transition metal trifluoride is                  cobalt trifluoride. The transition metal oxide is                  chromium oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phase-transfer                  catalyst is tetramethylammonium chloride.Preferred Process: Manufacture of desflurane                  further involves removing unwanted products using                  chromatography and/or fractional separation,                  selectively capturing wanted products and/or                  reactants using chromatography and/or fractional                  separation, and collecting the purified product.                  The method is being under the control of feedback                  from a detector such as UV, infrared, mass                  spectrometry, photoacoustic spectroscopy or                  advanced resistivity scanner. The                  fluoro-substitution reaction is performed                  optionally without catalysis and further involves                  transferring the reactants into another reaction                  chamber containing the catalyst, passing the                  products including desflurane through a detection                  device so as to allow control the flow of                  reactants and solvent into the reaction                  chamber.Preferred Apparatus: The system further has                  a unit for separation of anaesthetic agent                  product, desirable reactants and unwanted waste                  using supercritical chromatography.
   ACTIVITY - Anesthetic.
   No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

   ADVANTAGE - The process: greatly simplifies industrial                operation of ethylene epoxidation processes for                plant operator as maximum selectivity performance                at constant value of ethylene oxide production                parameter can be advantageously achieved by                maintaining concentration of organic chloride                reaction modifier in feed gas either constant or                within narrow concentration range for given feed                gas; utilizes plant operator will find operation                greatly simplified as the plant operator no longer                needs to conduct optimization of chloride                concentrations and may simply ensure overall                catalyst chloriding effectiveness value is                maintained within narrow range throughout catalyst                operation; provides improved effect on catalyst                performance, with the operating temperature                increasing as the concentration of carbon dioxide                present in the inlet feed gas increases; is                beneficial, if the fluoride-mineralized alpha                -alumina carrier contains water leachable potassium                in a quantity of less than 40 ppmw; and is                commercial plant operator has beneficial in                operating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises reacting at least a portion of the                  ethylene oxide produced with at least one reagent                  having water, an alcohol, carbon dioxide and an                  amine to form ethylene glycol, an ethylene glycol                  ether, ethylene carbonate or ethanolamine,                  respectively. Preferred Components: The reaction                  temperature (T1) is at 180-260 degrees C. The                  reaction temperature (Tx) is at 200-300 degrees                  C. The inlet feed gas further comprises 0.10-10                  mole.% carbon dioxide. The reaction modifiers                  comprise methyl chloride, ethyl chloride,                  ethylene dichloride, and/or vinyl chloride. The                  alkali metal promoter comprises lithium, sodium,                  potassium, rubidium, and/or cesium. The                  epoxidation catalyst further comprises co                  promoter having sulfur, phosphorus, boron,                  tungsten, molybdenum and/or chromium. The                  epoxidation catalyst comprises a first co                  promoter having sulfur, phosphorus, and/or boron                  and a second co promoter having tungsten,                  molybdenum, and/or chromium. The epoxidation                  catalyst further comprises a further metal having                  beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium,                  titanium, hafnium, zirconium, vanadium, thallium,                  thorium, tantalum, niobium, gallium, germanium,                  and/or manganese. The fluoride-mineralized alpha                  -alumina carrier has a surface area of 0.1-10                  m2/g, preferably 0.7-3 m2/g, relative to the                  weight of the fluoride-mineralized alpha -alumina                  carrier, as measured in accordance with the                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller method. The                  fluoride-mineralized alpha -alumina carrier has a                  particulate matrix having lamellar or                  platelet-type morphology. The lamellar or                  platelet-type morphology is that particles having                  in at least one direction a size greater than 0.1                  mm have at least one substantially flat major                  surface.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of catalyst                preparation and characteristic entities that do not                support the claims.

PN US2022213397-A1
TI Production of syngas involves charging oxidant and            feedstock composition to gasification zone within            gasifier, gasifying feedstock composition together with            oxidant in gasification zone, discharging at least            portion syngas composition, and producing organic            compound from the syngas composition.
AU WEST N M
   MURPHY J W
   TRAPP W L
AE EASTMAN CHEM CO (EACH-C)
GA 202287775V
AB    NOVELTY - Production of syngas involves charging an                oxidant and a feedstock composition to a                gasification zone within a gasifier, the feedstock                composition comprising a solid fossil fuel and                comprising plastics present in an amount up to 25                wt.% based on the weights of solids in the                feedstock composition, gasifying the feedstock                composition together with the oxidant in a                gasification zone to produce a syngas composition,                discharging at least a portion of the syngas                composition from the gasifier, and producing an                organic compound from the syngas composition, where                the gasifier is an entrained flow gasifier, and the                gasifier does not employ a plasma process.
   USE - Method for production of syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has minimized an increase of carbon                dioxide content over that generated by the solid                fossil fuels alone, has allowed sufficient motion                relative to the gaseous reactants, to assure                substantially complete gasification, and provides                pump-able slurries of high solids content with a                minimum of grinding, has reduced the heat load on                the gasifier and improves the efficiency of                utilization of both fuel and oxygen, and the                oxidant stream is introduced into the reaction zone                of the gasifier as high velocity to both exceed the                rate of flame propagation and to improve mixing                with the feedstock stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The organic compound comprises acetic acid,                  methanol, methyl acetate, acetate, acetic                  anhydride, 2-5 C oxygenated compounds,                  formaldehyde, dimethyl ether, MTBE, oxo products,                  aldehydes, or isobutene, where the feedstock is a                  slurry. The ratio of carbon monoxide/hydrogen                  generated by gasifying the feedstock composition                  and the oxidant is within 10 wt.% of the ratio of                  carbon monoxide/hydrogen generated by gasifying a                  solid fossil fuel only feedstock and said                  oxidant. The average content of elements other                  than carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, and                  sulfur, in plastics, is at least 0.5 wt.% and                  does not exceed 10 wt.%.

TI CVAV intelligent group control method for livestock and poultry farm in e.g. building, involves determining group of executed fan and fan frequency, and starting fan corresponding to group of fan executed according to determined fan frequency.
AU GAO X
   NIU Z
   WU J
AE SHANGHAI EURUS AGRITECH CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202271201F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a reference index. Multiple fans are grouped in a level group. Total air discharge amount is determined according to the reference index, where each group corresponds to a specific number and a specific size of a fan. A group of an executable fan and a corresponding fan frequency is calculated according to total air exhaust. Energy consumption of the group of different executable fans is calculated. The group of the executed fan and the fan frequency according to lowest energy consumption is determined. A fan is started corresponding to the set of the fan executed according to a determined fan frequency.
   USE - CVAV intelligent group control method for livestock and poultry farm. Can also be used in building, experiment environment, feeding environment, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide content of environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the reference index sudden change and can not be adjusted to the suitable air quantity, keeps the environment stable. The software in the environment controller can realize the flexible grouping of the fan group, improving the flexibility of the system configuration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a CVAV intelligent group control device.

PN US2022119576-A1
TI Making phenolic compound-formaldehyde gel is used in catalyst supports, adsorbents, fuel cells, involves preparing reaction mixture comprising phenolic compound and formaldehyde, and irradiating reaction mixture with ionizing radiation, thus producing phenolic compound-formaldehyde gel.
AU AL-MUHTASEB S
   AWADALLAH-F A
AE UNIV QATAR (UYQA-Non-standard)
GA 2022530002
AB    NOVELTY - Making a phenolic compound-formaldehyde gel involves preparing a reaction mixture comprising a phenolic compound and formaldehyde. The reaction mixture is irradiated with ionizing radiation, thus producing the phenolic compound-formaldehyde gel.
   USE - Method for making a phenolic compound-formaldehyde gel is used in catalyst supports, adsorbents (e.g., for carbon dioxide capture, gas separation and purification, hydrogen storage, soil remediation, and water purification), fuel cells and electrochemical processes, selective membranes, insulations, and the like.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low cost, ease of production, environmental friendliness, and sterilization that may allow such products to be used directly in biomaterials, cosmetics, or drug fields. The method has features of cleanness, easy production and mass scale of product, environmental friendliness, lack of additives in the final products, and short time required to produce the gel product. This method focuses on the synthesis of Radio frequency (RF) gels with different compositions of the main reactants using an irradiation of gamma rays (e.g., gamma-rays of 60Co) in a wide range. The activated carbons of the outcome gels are prepared, and the resulting RF gels and their derived activated carbons are identified by different techniques.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a proposed radiolytic reaction mechanism of water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phenolic compound is selected from the group consisting of a dihydroxybenzene, a trihydroxybenzen, and mixtures. The phenolic compound is selected from the group consisting of resorcinol, catechol, hydroquinone, and mixtures. The ionizing radiation is gamma irradiation. The ionizing radiation is produced by a radiation source comprising radioactive cobalt (60Co). The reaction mixture does not comprise a polymerization initiator, a polymerization catalyst, an acid, a base, a buffer, a solvent. The phenolic compound-formaldehyde gel is a xerogel, an aerogel, or a cryogel.Preferred Parameters: The gamma irradiation is a dose from about 10-70 Kilogray (kGy).

TI Manganese composite valence state crosslinked material adsorption catalytic material used for removing nitrogen-containing inorganic substance comprises manganese-doped carbon point, active carbon, alumina, manganese cross-linked montmorillonite powder, and sodium carbonate.
AU WANG C
   LU X
   CHENG X
AE RUIRAN SHANGHAI ENVIRONMENTAL ENG TECHNO (RUIR-Non-standard)
GA 202257156T
AB    NOVELTY - Manganese composite valence state crosslinked material adsorption catalytic material comprises 1-5 pts. wt. manganese-doped carbon point, 20-40 pts. wt. active carbon, 10-20 pts. wt. alumina, 15-25 pts. wt. manganese cross-linked montmorillonite powder, and 5-10 pts. wt. sodium carbonate.
   USE - The material is useful for removing nitrogen-containing inorganic substance, sulfur-containing inorganic substance, halogen-containing inorganic substance, phosphorus-containing inorganic substance, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide in the filtered gas, sulfur containing organic matter, nitrogen-containing organic matter, phosphorus-containing organic matter, halogen-containing organic matter, fatty acid, and aromatic compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: uses easily available raw materials and has wide application prospect. The method: is simple, is convenient; and has strong practicability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing manganese composite valence state crosslinked material adsorption catalytic material comprising mixing uniformly manganese doped carbon point, active carbon, alumina, manganese cross-linked montmorillonite powder in a powder stirring kettle, then slowly spraying 30-35 wt.% sodium carbonate solution in the mixed material until the weight of sodium carbonate is 5-10 pts. wt., continuously stirring uniformly, using 3-8 MPa of high pressure granulating to form the regular 3-5 mm particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The manganese doped carbon point is prepared by reacting gallic acid and manganese dioxide, preferably mixing gallic acid and manganese dioxide in a mol ratio of 8:1-20:1, performing hydrothermal reaction, sealing, reacting for 2-12 hours at 200-800degreesC, centrifuging and retaining the precipitate, which is the manganese-doped carbon point. The manganese crosslinked montmorillonite powder is prepared by crosslinking of manganese (III) acetylacetonate and montmorillonite, preferably mixing manganese acetylacetonate (III) and montmorillonite in a weight ratio of 1: 3 to 1: 15, and grinding to obtain the product.

TI Manufacturing cathode active material e.g. for lithium secondary battery comprises e.g. mixing transition metal precursor of nickel and lithium-containing raw material, applying pressure and calcining to form lithium transition metal oxide.
AU MOON J Y
   WOO K
   SUNG H
   KIM G
   OH M
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202248545W
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing cathode active material, comprises (A) preparing a mixture by mixing a transition metal precursor containing nickel in an amount of 60 mol% or more based on the total number of moles of the transition metal and a lithium-containing raw material; and (B) applying a pressure of 10-750 psi to the mixture to obtain pellets, and then calcining the pellets to obtain lithium transition metal oxide.
   USE - The cathode active material is useful in cathode for lithium secondary battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cathode active material utilized in lithium secondary battery has high energy density and voltage, long cycle life, and low self-discharge rate; minimizes the amount of residual lithium present in the cathode active material and the amount of lithium carbonate that directly plays a role in the generation of carbon dioxide; and improves the capacity and efficiency of the lithium secondary battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cathode active material comprising lithium transition metal oxide containing 60 mol% or more nickel based on the total number of moles of transition metals other than lithium, when the ratio of the amount of c carbonate to the total amount of remaining lithium carbonate and lithium hydroxide is 0.52 or less.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal precursor has a composition of formulae ((NiaCobM1cM2d)(OH)2) (I) and ((NiaCobM1cM2d)O.OH) (II). The transition metal precursor and the lithium-containing raw material are mixed in a molar ratio of 1:0.9-1:1.1. The cathode active material has a complex of formula (Lix(Nia'Cob'M1c'M2d')O2) (III).M1 = Mn and/or Al;M2 = B, Mg, Ca, Ti, V, Cr, Fe, Zn, Ga, Y, Zr, Nb, Mo, Ta and/or W;a = 0.7-1;b = 0-0.3;c = 0-0.3;d = 0-0.1;a+b+c+d = 1;x = 0.9-1.1;a' = 0.7-1;b' = 0-0.3;c' = 0-0.3;d' = 0-0.1; anda'+b'+c'+d' = 1.Preferred Method: The pressure applied to the mixture in the step (B) is 10-300 psi . The calcining is performed at 750-850 degrees C under an oxygen atmosphere.
   EXAMPLE - A mixture was prepared by mixing a transition metal precursor having a complex (Ni0.88Co0.05Mn0.07(OH)2) and lithium hydroxide as a lithium-containing raw material in a molar ratio of 1:1.07. A pressure of 10 psi was applied to 5 g of the mixture to obtain pellets having a diameter of 15 mm using an extruder (Carver's Model No. 3912). The calcined product was pulverized using a grinder, 100 pts. wt. deionized water was added based on 100 pts. wt. calcined product, washed with water at 10 degrees C, and moisture was removed using a vacuum pump and dried at 130 degrees C for 10 hours to obtain cathode active material.

   ZHANG C
AE LI WEI (LIWE-Non-standard)
GA 202244661T
AB    NOVELTY - Direct reduction method of hydrocarbon-rich gas-based vertical furnace involves feeding iron oxides with preset particle size into the gas-based vertical furnace through the furnace top charging device lined with refractory material, heating hydrocarbon-rich gas as reducing gas and oxidant to 1100-1350degrees Celsius from outside the vertical furnace, injecting unheated hydrocarbon-rich gas to form mixed gas, injecting the mixed gas into furnace, so that iron oxide reacts with reducing gas, directly reducing to obtain reduced iron, discharging reduced iron out of the furnace through the lower portion of the gas-based vertical furnace, and discharging the crude gas produced by the reaction from the top of the vertical furnace. The oxidant is one or more of oxygen, water and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Direct reduction method of hydrocarbon-rich gas-based vertical furnace in steel production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and simple, and improves the utilization rate of the raw material gas, and solves the problem of efficient utilization of gas with different property in iron and steel complexes.

PN IN202241006297-A
TI Determining performance of alkali-activated slag concrete (AASC) and conventional concrete (CC) against sulfuric acid attack, by subjecting CC and AASC specimens to sulfuric acid solution, magnesium sulfate and sodium sulfate solutions.
AU GONTHINA M
   RAO S K
AE GONTHINA M (GONT-Individual)
   RAO S K (RAOS-Individual)
GA 202245882P
AB    NOVELTY - Determining performance of alkali-activated slag concrete (AASC) and conventional concrete (CC) against sulfuric acid attack, comprises: (a) preparing concrete specimens of size 100 mm for CC and AASC; (b) setting separate curing conditions for ambient curing and heat curing where the specimens are kept at a temperature of 60 degrees C for 24 hours; and (c) subjecting CC and AASC specimens to sulfuric acid solution of 2.5, 5 and 10% concentrations, magnesium sulfate of 5% concentration and sodium sulfate solutions of 5% concentration for different immersion periods of 56, 91 and 180 days; and (d) determination of weight change, depth of penetration and relative compressive strength.
   USE - The method is useful for determining performance of AASC and CC against sulfuric acid attack.
   ADVANTAGE - The deterioration of AASC specimens is less when compared to CC specimens against acid attack. The AASC specimens immersed in sodium sulfate solution have higher relative compressive strength compared to AASC specimens immersed in magnesium sulfate solution. The performance of AASC is better than CC. The AASC is a suitable alternative to CC in reducing the emission of carbon dioxide gases into the atmosphere.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a flow chart for durability aspects of CC and AASC.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The specimens prepared from AASC are S-0.4-2.33-16M, S-0.5-2.33-12M, S-0.5-1-12M and S-0.5-2.33-16M. The S-0.5-2.33-12M specimens immersed in 10% sulfuric acid solution show minimum depth of penetration of 12 and 14.23 mm for an immersion period of 180 days under ambient curing and heat curing respectively. Preferred Components: The four mixes of AASC e.g. S-0.4-2.33-16M, S-0.5-2.33-12M, S-0.5-1-12M and S-0.5-2.33-16M) have attained compressive strength of greater than or equal to 40 MPa at 28 days under ambient curing and heat curing, where for an immersion period of 180 days in 5% sulfuric acid solution, the maximum relative compressive strength observed is 61.96% for S-0.4-2.33-16M specimens under ambient curing, and it is 58.97% for S-0.5-1-12M specimens under heat curing. The visual examination of AASC and CC specimens under ambient curing and heat curing exposed to 5% concentration of sodium sulfate and 5% concentration of magnesium sulfate solutions exhibit a color change, a slight surface deterioration and a white precipitate. The AASC specimens show less weight loss than CC specimens under ambient curing and heat curing. The relative compressive strength of AASC specimens exposed to magnesium sulfate solution is less compared to sodium sulfate solution.

GA 202240221X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing the workpiece made of carbon steel in a gas nitriding furnace with a temperature of 560-580 degrees C for nitrocarburizing treatment. The cracked NH3 is introduced to replace the first introduced to replace the air in the furnace in the furnace in the replacement stage. The ammonia (NH3), dinitrogen (N2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) is introduced in the nitrocarburizing stage to obtain the required nitrogen potential. The NH3 and cracked NH3 are first introduced in the cooling stage, and N2 is introduced until the workpiece made of carbon steel is released after the temperature drops to specific range. The gases used in the nitrocarburizing stage are NH3, N2 and CO2, the temperature is 560-580degreesC, the nitrogen potential is controlled at 1.6-2.0, and the nitriding time is 2-5h.
   USE - Method for improving surface porosity of carbon steel nitrocarburizing.

PN IN202021028500-A
TI Integrated process for rapid cultivation, and harvesting microalgal cells from cultivation media in pilot-scale photobioreactors used for producing biofuels, involves culturing microalgae cells in cultivation media, and harvesting biomass.
AU PATEL M J
AE PATEL M J (PATE-Individual)
GA 202226429J
AB    NOVELTY - Rapid cultivation and harvesting microalgal cells comprises providing cultivation media using tris-acetatephosphate (TAP) or modified TAP media comprising one or multiple microalgal cells in the reactor vessel, adding initially or adding initially followed by intermittent addition of a volatile fatty acid during cultivation that drastically decreases the settling time of microalgal biomass. One or multiple microalgal cells are precipitated in the cultivation media in presence of a flocculant, where the one or multiple microalgal cells are associated with the precipitated solids. The precipitated solids are allowed to settle creating a harvested broth over the settled solid, and then separated the harvested broth from the settled solid precipitate (harvested biomass). The flocculent is separated from the harvested broth to obtain clearer solution which is recycled for media preparation.
   USE - Method for rapid cultivation and harvesting one or multiple microalgal cells from a cultivation media in photobioreactors for producing biofuels and high-value co-products such as proteins, omega fatty acids and beta carotenes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide rapid cultivation and harvesting of microalgae in pilot-scale photobioreactors with high photosynthesis efficiency and unique potential to sequester carbon dioxide from atmosphere or industrial flue gases, and high yield of microalgal biomass coupled with rapid harvesting of same.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volatile fatty acid is selected from acetic acid glacial (AAG), propionic acid or combination. The microalgae can be selected from Chlorella sorokiniana, Chlorella vulgaris, Chlorella lewinii, Chlorella pyrenoidosa, Chlorella protothecoides, Chlamydomonas reinhardtii, Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain number 211-7a, and Scenedesmus obliquus. The flocculent is separated from the harvested broth by adding 0.025-0.15% phosphate stock solution, where phosphate stock solution is a mixture of dipotassium phosphate and monopotassium phosphate. The flocculent are selected from potassium aluminum sulfate, aluminum sulfate octadecahydrate, ferric sulfate and combinations. The cultivation media is made of Tris buffer or ammonium chloride or urea or combination.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The extraction pressure variable for supercritical CO2 is 5 megapascal, 10 megapascal, 15 megapascal, 20 megapascal or 25 megapascal. The extraction temperature variable for supercritical CO2 is 25degrees Celsius, 30degrees Celsius, 35degrees Celsius, 40degrees Celsius or 45degrees Celsius. The CO2 flow variable for supercritical CO2 is 2 L/hour, 4 L/hour, 6 L/hour, 8 L/hour or 10 L/hour. The residence time variable for supercritical CO2 is one of 45 minutes, 65 minutes, 85 minutes, 105 minutes, and 125 minutes. The formula for calculating the amount of squalene extracted is N=XxY'-1, where N is the output of squalene, in mg/g, X is the quality of squalene obtained according to the standard curve, in mg and Y' is the dry weight of the raw material entering the extraction device, in g.

TI METHOD FOR DISSOLVING IRRADIATED MATERIALS CONTAINING TRANSURANIUM ELEMENTS, INCLUDING IRRADIATED NUCLEAR FUEL FOR DESTRUCTIVE RADIOCHEMICAL TESTING.
AU MOMOTOV V N
   ERIN E A
   VOLKOV A YU
AE NAUKA INNOVATSII STOCK CO (NAUK-Non-standard)
GA 202210881Q
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to nuclear technology, predominantly to analytical support of the process of recycling irradiated nuclear fuel and isolating minor actinides for subsequent burning. A sample of irradiated nuclear material containing transuranium elements, including irradiated nuclear fuel, is dissolved in a conical Erlenmeyer flask with a volume of 500 ml, with a reflux column with a height of at least 150 mm, connected in series with three traps constituting Petri absorbers. The first Petri absorber is intended for capturing ruthenium flying off on dissolution of the tested sample and comprises a solution of 0.05 mol/l nitric acid, the second and third absorbers are intended for capturing 14C released in the form of carbon dioxide, containing a solution of 1 mol/l sodium hydroxide. The volumes of the solution in all Petri absorbers are at least 30 ml. The sample is dissolved in 250 ml nitric acid with a concentration of 8 mol/l while heating to a temperature of 95 to 100C for 16 hours and constantly purging the solution volume by an ozone-air mixture containing 5 wt.% O3 at a rate of 1.2 lxh-1, followed by cooling the resulting solution to ambient temperature.
   USE - Nuclear technology.
   ADVANTAGE - Method allows for quantitative determination of volatile fission products, including carbon-14, tritium, ruthenium isotopes, in the analysed sample.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the super high strength benzoxazine                aerogel comprising (i) dissolving a bisphenol F                type benzoxazine monomer in a strong polar solvent,                stirring to completely dissolve to obtain clear and                transparent benzoxazine monomer solution, (ii)                adding acid as catalyst into the resultant                benzoxazine monomer solution, where the volume                ratio of the acid and bisphenol F type benzoxazine                monomer solution is 1-2:22 ml and the reaction                temperature is 10-50degrees Celsius, stirring and                reacting for 10-30 minutes to obtain                polybenzoxazine, (iii) pouring the polybenzoxazine                sol prepared in the step two into the container,                sealing and standing 3-16 hours obtain                polybenzoxazine gel, the obtained polybenzoxazine                gel for multiple times of solvent cleaning, each                cleaning time is 10-14 hours to obtain the                polybenzoxazine gel, and (iv) performing carbon                dioxide supercritical fluid drying treatment to the                solvent cleaned polybenzoxazine gel obtained in the                resultant step.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The strong polar                  solvent comprises dimethylformamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide or                  xylene. The concentration of the benzoxazine                  monomer solution is 10-40%.The acid catalyst                  comprises hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, oxalic                  acid or p-toluenesulfonic acid. The acid catalyst                  is concentrated hydrochloric acid. The solvent                  used in the solvent cleaning is                  dimethylformamide, acetonitrile, n-pentane,                  acetone, dimethylformamide, acetonitrile and                  n-pentane and acetone.Preferred Method: The sealed and allowing to                  stand time is 6-10 hours. The solvent cleaning                  time for each solvent is 12 hours for 2 times.                  The drying treatment temperature is at                  100-200degrees Celsius.

GA 2021B5199B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves causing an atmospheric gas                containing oxygen and selected from the group                consisting of nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and water                vapor to flow in a heat treatment furnace in which                battery waste is arranged. The battery waste is                heated while adjusting the partial pressure of                oxygen in the furnace. The partial pressure of                oxygen in the furnace during heating is maintained                within the specified range. The oxygen                concentration of the atmospheric gas when                introduced into the heat treatment furnace is 0.05%                by volume to 4.00% by volume.
   USE - Heat treatment method for battery-waste                containing lithium e.g. waste of in-vehicle battery                pack (claimed) mounted on vehicle such as hybrid                vehicle, fuel cell vehicle, or electric vehicle or                consumer-use battery waste.

The generation of lithium carbonate is promoted, by                containing a relatively small amount of oxygen in                the atmospheric gas and adjusting the oxygen                partial pressure in the furnace.

AU SHIMIZU T
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
GA 2021A66895
AB    NOVELTY - Semiconductor device comprises silicon carbide layer, metal layer and conductive layer positioned between the silicon carbide layer and metal layer, conductive layer contains silicide of one metal element (M) selected from nickel (Ni), palladium (Pd), and platinum (Pt). The conductive layer has carbon concentration of 1\asterisk10^1^7 cm^-^3 or less.
   USE - Semiconductor device.
   ADVANTAGE - The semiconductor device improves characteristics of the inverter circuit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: a inverter circuit, which comprises semiconductor device;#a driving device, which comprises semiconductor device;#a vehicle, which comprises semiconductor device;#a elevator, which comprises semiconductor device; and#a method for manufacturing semiconductor device, which involves forming first metal film on silicon carbide layer, first metal film containing one metal element (M) selected from nickel (Ni), palladium (Pd), and platinum (Pt), performing heat treatment in atmosphere containing carbon dioxide and atomic hydrogen, and heat treatment causing the silicon carbide layer to react with the first metal film to form metal silicide layer containing the metal element and forming second metal film having chemical composition different from chemical composition of the first metal film on the metal silicide layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of semiconductor device.
   Silicon carbide layer (10)
   Contact electrode (12)
   Metal silicide layer (14)
   Insulating layer (16)
   Contact structure (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon carbide layer contains impurity, impurity is p-type impuriy or n-type impurity, and concentration distribution of the impurity in the silicon carbide layer and conductive layer has peak at ch involvessbetween the silicon carbide layer and conductive layer. Preferred Conditions: The atomic ratio (M/Si) of the metal element (M) to silicon (Si) in the conductive layer is 1.2 or more. The semiconductor device further comprises insulating layer in contact with silicon carbide layer on side of the silicon carbide layer. The metal layer is positioned, depth of interface between the silicon carbide layer and the conductive layer, when interface between the silicon carbide layer and insulating layer is set as a reference is 50 nanometer or more. The atomic ratio (M/Si) of the metal element (M) to silicon (Si) in the conductive layer is 1.2 or more. The fourth silicon carbide region contains aluminum, and conductive layer has aluminum concentration of 1\asterisk10^1^7 cm^-^3 or less. The depth of the conductive layer is deeper than a depth of the third silicon carbide region. The thickness of the conductive layer in a direction from the first plane to the second plane is larger than 100 nanometer. Preferred Process: The method involves removing unreacted first metal film after forming the metal silicide layer and before forming the second metal film.

AB    NOVELTY - Tire code comprises a fiber base material                woven from a warp thread comprising nylon, rayon,                aramid or polyethylene terephthalate, in which the                warp thread has a density of 65% or higher per warp                or the number of warps per unit length (n/inch), an                adhesive layer formed on a fiber substrate, and a                rubber coating layer formed on the adhesive layer,                where the rubber coating layer comprises synthetic                rubber or natural rubber, and the application                amount per unit area of the rubber coating layer to                the adhesive layer is 75-300 g/m2.
   USE - The tire code is useful in a tire (claimed) as                a cap ply, a belt, or a carcass.
   ADVANTAGE - The tire code has excellent durability,                stability, thin thickness, adhesion to rubber,                adhesiveness, rubber reinforcing performance,                rolling resistance, improved fuel efficiency,                stable adhesive layer, fatigue resistance                performance, bonding strength, focusing property                and coating properties, is easy to adhered to                rubber even without existing rolling process,                reduces energy consumption, prevents density and                weight of tire from being increased, reduces air                pockets during manufacturing of green tire, reduces                tire defect rate and carbon dioxide emissions and                reduces overall thickness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing the tire cord.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The fiber base is                  woven with a density between warps of 65% or more                  and a number of warps per unit length (n/inch) of                  35 or more. The fiber substrate has a thickness                  of 0.1-0.6 mm. The rubber coating layer has a                  thickness of 0.01-0.2 mm. The tire cord has a                  breaking strength of 2-15 g/d and an elongation                  at break of 10-30%, according to the standard                  test method of ASTM D885, and adhesive strength                  of 10 kgf or more, when evaluating the adhesive                  strength according to the ASTM D4393 standard                  test method. Preferred Components: The rubber                  coating layer comprises 10-30 wt.% elastic                  polymer composition comprising synthetic rubber                  or natural rubber, and is formed by applying a                  rubber coating solution comprising 70-90 wt.%                  solvent on the adhesive layer. The adhesive layer                  comprises resorciol-formaldehyde-latex.

The sample formed by the mechanism is sealed in a high-pressure reaction vessel, and carbon dioxide is injected through a high-pressure pump to make the pressure in the vessel reach 25 MPa. When the temperature and pressure of the entire system are stabilized, the constant temperature and pressure are maintained for a period of time. The foamed sample coated with the metal oxide nanomaterial and the nano-TiO2 photocatalyst is processed in a muffle furnace to obtain a supercritical foaming material. The metal oxide nanomaterial comprises nano-aluminum oxide, nano-boron trioxide, nano-potassium oxide, nano-magnesium oxide, nano-calcium oxide, nano-zinc oxide, nano-silicon dioxide, and/or nano-titanium dioxide photocatalyst. The test is performed in accordance with and the degradation rate of the supercritical foam material.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing a heterogeneous catalyst, comprising supporting the active ingredient on a support, and reducing and activating the active ingredient; and
   (2) producing hydrogen, comprising (i) forming carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H2) from a liquid reactant through a reaction of a heterogeneous catalyst, and (ii) reacting carbon monoxide (CO) with water and converting it into hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) by a water gas shift, where heterogeneous catalyst comprises a support and a metal catalyst supported on support and metal catalyst contains at least one of platinum (Pt) and cobalt (Co).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a liquid phase reforming reaction apparatus for hydrogen production using a heterogeneous alloy catalyst (Pt-Co) synthesized.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active ingredient comprises an alloy of platinum (Pt) and cobalt (Co). The active component is platinum-cobalt based compound of formula (PtxCoy) (I).x and y = 1-4.The molar ratio of platinum: cobalt is 1:4-4:1. The active ingredient is a content of 1-20 wt.% based on the total mass of the heterogeneous catalyst. The platinum is located on the outermost surface of the catalyst. The heterogeneous catalyst comprises a first region in which platinum (Pt) exists and a second region in which cobalt oxide (CoO) exists. The surface composition (at%) of the heterogeneous catalyst is 20-80% Pt and 20-80% Co when the composition is analyzed by an energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) elemental analyzer. That the support contains aluminum oxide or silicon dioxide. The heterogeneous catalyst selectively decomposes C-C and C-OH bonds contained in the liquid reactant to form carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H2). The liquid reactant is a liquid mixture in which xylitol or xylose is uniformly dissolved. Preferred Composition: The heterogeneous catalyst further comprises an oxide film located on the surface of the active ingredient. Preferred Method: In the supporting step, mixing dispersion solution and active ingredient precursor and wet impregnating. The activation step comprises changing chemical properties of the surface of the heterogeneous catalyst by adjusting the reduction conditions including the activation temperature or the atmospheric gas composition. The activation step is performed at 300-500 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The method for preparing the heterogeneous catalyst comprises passivating the heterogeneous catalyst. The passivation step is performed in a gas atmosphere containing oxygen and argon. The method further comprises (i') forming a synthesis gas by reacting methane (CH4) and carbon dioxide (CO2) in the biogas with a heterogeneous catalyst; and (ii') converting carbon monoxide (CO) contained in the synthesis gas into hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) by reacting with water vapor. The steps (i) and (i') are performed at a temperature of 200-300 degrees C and a pressure of 20-50 bar. The steps (ii) and (ii') are converted through a heterogeneous catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The cathode structure comprises a cathode substrate and a cathode catalyst according to the direct-type multi-ether compound fuel cell can make the multi-ether compound under the action of the anode catalyst for oxidation reaction to generate water and carbon dioxide, and releasing the electron and proton, and the oxygen and released proton under the action of the cathode catalyst for reduction reaction.
   USE - Direct-type multi-ether compound fuel cell for direct-type multi-ether compound fuel cell system.
   ADVANTAGE - The battery has high safety coefficient and high chemical energy-electric energy conversion efficiency, and uses ether compound as fuel without reforming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a direct type multi-ether compound fuel cell system, which comprises a fuel supply unit, which is used for providing a polyethers compound in the direct-type polyethers compound fuel cell anodeoxidation reaction. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The platinum compound comprises platinum (acetylacetonato). The ruthenium compound comprises ruthenium acetylacetonate. The bismuth compound comprises bismuth nitratepentahydrate. An anode catalyst comprises a composite of Pt-Ru-Bi catalyst and acidic polymer catalyst and/or a composite of platinum-carbon catalyst and acidic polymer catalyst, preferably a composite of Pt-Ru-Bi catalyst and acidic polymer catalyst. The preparation of anode catalyst includes Pt-Ru-Bi catalyst and/or platinum carbon catalyst, acidic polymer dispersed in the mixed solution of ethanol and water, then adding perfluorosulfonic acid type polymer solution, and then ultrasonically processing to obtain the anode catalyst. The preparation of the Pt-Ru-Bi catalyst includes adding platinum compound, a ruthenium compound, a bismuth compound under the protective atmosphere. The first mixed reaction system of polyethylene glycol and ethyleneglycol at 100-160 degrees C for 30-180 minutes to obtain Pt-Ru-Bi catalyst. The polyethers compound comprises polymethoxy dimethyl ether DMMn and/orpolymethoxy diethyl ether DEMn with a length of the polymerized chain segment as the n2-12 n2-12 the polymethoxy dimethyl ether DMMn and/or the polymerized chain segment. An anode substrate comprises carbon fiber cloth and/or carbon fiber paper. The cathode substrate comprises carbon fiber cloth and/or carbon fiber paper. The proton exchangemembrane comprises a sulfonated polymer film. The proton exchange membrane comprises perfluorinated sulfonic ion membrane, Nafion-117 proton exchange membrane, inorganic acid doped membrane, inorganic acid composite membrane in any one or combination of more than two of them. An anode structure of the direct type polyethers compound fuel cell and the outer side of the cathode structure are further provided with a diffusion assembly, a flow channel assembly, a current collecting assembly, an insulating assembly, a supporting assembly. The direct polyethers compound fuel cell comprises multiple stacked anode structure and cathode structure and/or, the direct polyethers compound fuel cell further comprises a functional component. The functional component comprises a temperature control component. Preferred Process: The preparation of acidic polymer catalyst includes making the second mixed reaction system containing vinylimidazole, 1, 3-propane sultone and toluene react for 1-24 hours at 30-110 degrees centigrade to obtain vinyl sulfopropyl imidazole inner salt. The initiator is selected fromazodiisobutyronitrile initiator and mixed acidic polymer precursor with the H2SO4 solution andreacting for 1-72 hours at 25-90 degrees centigrade to obtain the acidic polymer catalyst. The fuel cell is a fuel cell. The cathode catalyst is selected from transition metal-based catalyst. The transition metal-based catalyst is selected from the group consisting of Fe-based catalyst, Co-based catalyst or Ni-based catalyst. The preparation of the cathode catalyst includes mixing pyromellitic dianhydride, cobalt chloride, urea, ammonium chloride and ammonium molybdate uniformly, and performing in-situ solid polycondensation for 2-48 hours at 210-280 degrees centigrade, obtaining polymerized cobaltphthalocyanine, then in a protective atmosphere, the obtained polymerized phthalocyanine cobalt at 600-1000 degrees centigrade heat treatment for 1-6 hours to obtain the cathode catalyst.

PT P
PN CN112331865-A; CN112331865-B
TI Composite cathode electrode for solid oxide battery, is a core-shell structure cathode composed of a cathode framework and a shell layer supported on the surface of the cathode framework.
AU LI W
   ZHAO H
   WANG H
   WANG S
   DONG B
   ZHAO L
   WU C
AE UNIV HUBEI (UYUB-C)
   UNIV HUBEI (UYUB-C)
GA 202115079Y
AB    NOVELTY - Composite cathode electrode is a core-shell structure cathode composed of a cathode framework and a shell layer supported on the surface of the cathode framework. The cathode framework in the core-shell structure cathode is lanthanum-strontium-cobalt-iron-oxide (La0.6Sr0.4Co0.2Fe0.8O3). The shell layer is lanthanum-nickel-iron-oxide (La1-xNi0.6Fe0.4O3) with A site vacancy, where x is 0-0.1.
   USE - Composite cathode electrode for a solid oxide fuel cell (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode has excellent oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) catalytic activity and good stability under the carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a composite cathode electrode of a solid oxide battery, which involves:
   (A) calcining after supporting the shell precursor liquid on the surface of the cathode framework;
   (B) dissolving the stoichiometric ratio of metal nitrate containing lanthanum, nickel and iron in isopropanol or ethanol to obtain the first solution;
   (C) dissolving the surfactant and the complexing agent in water to obtain a second solution, and mixing the second solution with the first solution as a shell precursor; and
   (D) injecting the shell precursor solution onto the surface of the cathode framework, and then calcining to obtain a core-shell structure cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The overall thickness of the cathode skeleton in the core-shell structure cathode is 10-20 micrometer, and the thickness of the shell layer is 50-150 nanometer. The volume ratio of isopropanol or ethanol to water in the shell precursor liquid is 2.5-3.5:1. Preferred Components: The surfactant is one or more of polyvinylpyrrolidone, methylpyrrolidone and polyethylene glycol octylphenyl ether. The complexing agent includes one or more of glycine and citric acid. The binder includes one or more of ethyl cellulose, terpineol and fish oil. The electrolyte is an oxygen ion conductor. Preferred Process: The preparation method of the cathode framework involves dissolving metal nitrates containing lanthanum, strontium, cobalt and iron in a stoichiometric ratio in water, adding citric acid, and adjusting the pH to 8-9, then stirring to the gel, drying the gel, grinding, and calcinating to obtain lanthanum strontium cobalt ferrite (LSCF) cathode powder. The LSCF cathode powder and the binder uniformly ground to obtain a cathode slurry, screen printing the cathode slurry on the electrolyte, and calcining to obtain the cathode skeleton. The metal nitrate containing lanthanum, strontium, cobalt, and iron, citric acid are stirred under 80-90 degrees C oil bath conditions.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of single-atom catalyst involves                providing nitrogen and carbon-rich organic matter                as a precursor, high-temperature polymerizing                nitrogen-and-carbon-rich organic material to obtain                a bulk carbon nitride material, stripping material                to obtain ultra-thin carbon nitride nanosheets,                performing hydrothermal reaction of the ultra-thin                carbon nitride nanosheets containing carbon                nitride, transition metal source and the solvent to                obtain transition metal oxide/carbon nitride                composite material, calcining and etching the                composite material.
   USE - Preparation of single-atom catalyst used for                photocatalytic reduction-type reaction e.g.                photocatalytic nitrogen fixation reaction,                photocatalytic hydrogen production reaction, and                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reaction (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of single-atom                catalyst having excellent nitrogen fixation effect,                and ammonia production efficiency of 675 mu                mol.g-1.hour-1, and loads high transition metal                single atoms on nanosheets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the single-atom catalyst, which comprises                ultra-thin carbon nitride nanosheets, and 2-5 wt.%                transition metal single atom chosen from tungsten,                vanadium, chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt,                nickel, copper, zinc, and molybdenum supported on                the nanosheets; and
   (2) photocatalytic nitrogen fixation reaction,                which involves providing the single atom catalyst,                introducing nitrogen into the mixed reaction system                (r2) containing the single-atom catalyst and water,                and reacting under light conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The ultra-thin carbon                  nitride nanosheets has thickness of 2.1-10 nm.                  Preferred Process: The catalyst preparation                  method involves polymerizing                  nitrogen-and-carbon-rich organic material at                  450-600 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain bulk                  carbon nitride material, and/or peeling,                  preferably liquid phase ultrasonically-peeling.                  The catalyst preparation method involves                  dissolving transition metal source in a solvent                  to form a transition metal solution, mixing with                  dispersion comprising carbon nitride ultra-thin                  nanosheets, adding acid to form hydrothermal                  reaction system with pH of less than 1, at                  170-190 degrees C for 5-10 hours, in which the                  acid is chosen from hydrochloric acid and/or                  sulfuric acid, calcining the composite material                  at 400-600 degrees C for 2 hours, in a protective                  atmosphere, etching the calcined composite                  material in an alkaline and/or acidic environment                  for 2-12 hours, in which the protective                  atmosphere is an inert gas atmosphere and/or                  nitrogen atmosphere, the alkaline and/or acidic                  environment is potassium hydroxide and/or sodium                  hydroxide, calcined transition metal oxide/carbon                  nitride composite material comprises                  tungsten-based oxide/carbon nitride composite                  material, chromium-based oxide/carbon nitride                  composite material, iron-based oxide/carbon                  nitride composite material, cobalt-based                  oxide/carbon nitride composite material,                  nickel-based oxide/carbon nitride composite                  material, copper-based oxide/carbon nitride                  composite material, zinc-based oxide/carbon                  nitride composite material, and/or                  molybdenum-based oxide/carbon nitride composite                  material, further involves under an ammonia                  atmosphere, performing ammoniating material                  obtained after the etching treatment at 300-500                  degrees C for 1-5 hours. The photocatalytic                  nitrogen fixation reaction after completing                  reaction in the system (r2), subjecting obtained                  solution to Nessler's reagent color development                  process, and obtaining the ammonia concentration                  of the obtained solution through the UV-spectrum                  test. Preferred Components: The                  nitrogen-and-carbon-rich organic material is                  chosen from melamine, urea, and thiourea. The                  transition metal source is chosen from tungsten                  source, vanadium source, chromium source,                  manganese source, iron source, cobalt source,                  nickel source, copper source, zinc source, and                  molybdenum source, preferably tungsten source,                  and/or the transition metal oxide in the                  transition metal oxide/carbon nitride composite                  material chosen from tungsten-based oxide,                  vanadium-based oxide, chromium-based oxide,                  manganese-based oxide, iron-based oxide,                  cobalt-based oxide, nickel-based oxide,                  copper-based oxide, zinc-based oxide, and                  molybdenum-based oxide. The solvent is chosen                  from water and ethanol. Preferred Composition:                  The molar ratio of the carbon nitride ultra-thin                  nanosheets to the transition metal source is 2:1.                  Preferred Conditions: The second mixed reaction                  system (r2) further comprises ethanol, and/or, in                  which the light source used in the illumination                  condition is an xenon lamp, and the illumination                  intensity is 26 mW.cm-2, and the light source is                  full-spectrum light.

AE HEILONGJIANG LIMIAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 202107331L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compound heat exchanger (1), an intelligent controller (2), an indoor sensor (3), an outdoor sensor (4) and an exhaust fan (9) in which the compound heat exchanger integrates a sensible heat exchange unit and a total heat exchange unit. The sensible heat exchange unit discharges humid air in a greenhouse to maintain a temperature in the greenhouse and the total heat exchange unit maintains the temperature and humidity in the greenhouse unchanged. The outdoor sensor includes an outdoor temperature and humidity sensor and a solar illuminance sensor. The indoor sensor includes an indoor temperature and humidity sensor and a carbon dioxide concentration sensor. The intelligent controller, indoor sensor and exhaust fan are placed in a vegetable shed. The compound heat exchanger and the outdoor sensor are placed outside the vegetable shed.
   USE - System for ventilating vegetable greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The system guarantees the stable control of temperature and humidity in the vegetable greenhouse, and provides obvious effects on increasing the yield and efficiency of greenhouse crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an intelligent control method.

TI Composition used in food product for preparing            microalgal flour, protein concentrate, isolate,            comprises Euglena biomass selected from microalgal            flour, protein concentrate, and protein isolate.
AU NOBLE A J
   SABOURI S
   CLARKE C J
   SWAIN A
   CAVERLY M R R
   MAHESHWARI P
   KIRKWOOD J
   ZHANG C
   CAMERON L E
   SABRI S
AE NOBLEGEN INC (NOBL-Non-standard)
   NOBLEGEN INC (NOBL-Non-standard)
   NOBLEGEN INC (NOBL-Non-standard)
GA 202100958G
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 5-100% Euglena                biomass.
   USE - Composition used in food product for preparing                microalgal flour, protein concentrate, and protein                isolate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition helps in improving                functionality, high nutritional value, minimal                waste stream, reduces water usages and reduces                carbon dioxide emissions; improves stability,                shelf-life, digestion, and taste; improves                appearance, texture and mouth-feel; improves                hydration and increases yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a food product, which comprises 0.1-100%                Euglena biomass, and edible ingredient;
   (2) a method for preparing microalgal flour,                which involves culturing Euglena; concentrating                Euglena into microalgal biomass; washing; adjusting                pH; and drying microalgal biomass to produce                microalgal flour;
   (3) a method for preparing protein                concentrate, which involves culturing Euglena;                bringing culture to about 1-30% solids to form                biomass; adjusting biomass to pH of about 3-11;                homogenizing biomass; centrifuging homogenate, and                separating homogenate into three, or more layers,                where layers are pellet, aqueous middle layer, and                top lipid enriched layer, aqueous middle layer,                which comprises soluble protein; and
   (4) a method for preparing protein isolate,                which involves culturing Euglena; bringing culture                to about 1-30% solids to form biomass; adjusting                biomass to pH of about 6-11; homogenizing;                centrifuging; and separating homogenate into three,                or more layers, where layers are pellet, aqueous                middle layer, and top lipid enriched layer, aqueous                middle layer, which contains soluble protein.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 10-75 wt.% Euglena biomass which is                  derived from Euglena cultured heterotrophically.                  The microalgal biomass comprises 25-99% intact                  cells, 25-55 wt.% microalgal flour. Preferred                  Components: The Euglena biomass is predominantly                  intact Euglena cells, microalgal flour. The                  protein concentrate is isolated from Euglena                  biomass. The protein isolate is isolated from                  Euglena biomass or from protein concentrate. The                  oil is extracted from Euglena biomass which is                  added to food products. The Euglena biomass is                  selected from microalgal flour, protein                  concentrate, and protein isolate. The protein                  concentrate or protein isolate is at least 40%                  protein. The food product is selected from sauce,                  tea, candy, cookies, cereals, breads, fruit                  mixes, fruit salads, salads, snack bars, protein                  bars, fruit leather, yogurt, health bars,                  granola, smoothies, soups, juices, cakes, pies,                  shakes, ice cream, protein beverages, nutritional                  beverages, animal analogues, and health drinks;                  or non-dairy product selected from cheese,                  yogurt, milk, casein replacement, whip cream,                  protein drinks, non-dairy creamer, and                  combinations; animal analog which is selected                  from meat analogue, sausage analogue,                  pepperoni/cured meat analogue, chicken analogue,                  turkey analogue, pork analogue, bacon analogue,                  beef analogue, tofu replacement, ground beef                  analogue, jerky analogue, egg analogue, egg                  replacement, hard-boiled egg replacement,                  powdered egg replacement, liquid egg replacement,                  frozen egg replacement, salad dressing,                  mayonnaise, and combinations. The food product is                  extruded product selected from protein crisp,                  cracker, bar, pretzels, seaweed-like snack,                  cereal, pasta, crisps, puffs, oatmeal, cookies,                  and combinations; nutritional beverage; protein                  bar. The pellet contains discrete layer of                  protein concentrate sludge which is physically                  separated from aqueous and insoluble components.                  The aqueous middle layer is further centrifuged                  to produce protein concentrate sludge which is                  diluted in equal weight of water to create                  protein concentrate slurry which is further                  centrifuged and resuspended in water, which are                  repeated one or more times. The Euglena is                  selected from Euglena gracilis, Euglena                  sanguinea, Euglena deses, Euglena mutabilis,                  Euglena acus, Euglena viridis, Euglena anabaena,                  Euglena geniculata, Euglena oxyuris, Euglena                  proxima, Euglena tripteris, Euglena                  chlamydophora, Euglena splendens, Euglena texta,                  Euglena intermedia, Euglena polymorpha, Euglena                  ehrenbergii, Euglena adhaerens, Euglena clara,                  Euglena elongata, Euglena elastica, Euglena                  oblonga, Euglena pisciformis, Euglena cantabrica,                  Euglena granulata, Euglena obtusa, Euglena                  limnophila, Euglena hemichromata, Euglena                  variabilis, Euglena caudata, Euglena minima,                  Euglena communis, Euglena magnifica, Euglena                  terricola, Euglena velata, Euglena repulsans,                  Euglena clavata, Euglena lata, Euglena                  tuberculata, Euglena contabrica, Euglena                  ascusformis, Euglena ostendensis, or                  combinations. The Euglena biomass is wet Euglena                  biomass. The composition or food product further                  comprises flavoring, masking agent and additional                  ingredients. Preferred Conditions: The food                  product is in liquid form, dry or powdered form.                  The pH of aqueous middle layer is adjusted to                  about 3.5-5.5. The aqueous middle layer is                  incubated at 22 degrees C for at least 1 hour.                  The protein concentrate sludge or protein                  concentrate slurry is adjusted to pH of about                  5.5-8.5. The protein concentrate slurry is spray                  dried by producing protein concentrate which has                  protein concentration of at least 40% protein.                  The protein concentrate is defatted by organic                  solvents, and increasing protein content to                  greater than 80%. The pellet is greater than 95%                  beta -glucan. The flavoring, masking agent and                  additional ingredients is applied to culturing                  Euglena and biomass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is                  selected from acetone, benzyl alcohol,                  1,3-butylene glycol, carbon dioxide, castor oil,                  citric acid esters of mono- and di-glycerides,                  ethyl acetate, ethyl alcohol, ethyl alcohol                  denatured with methanol, glycerol, glyceryl                  diacetate, glyceryl triacetate (triacetin),                  glyceryl tributyrate (tributyrin), hexane,                  isopropyl alcohol, methanol, 2-butanone,                  methylene chloride, monoglycerides and                  diglycerides, monoglyceride citrate,                  1,2-propylene glycol, propylene glycol                  mono-esters and diesters of fat-forming fatty                  acids, triethyl citrate, and combinations.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition is administered by oral                route.
