TI Producing water purification composition,            comprises providing substrate having functional group,            depositing solution of metal salt on substrate,            depositing solution of carboxylic acid compound on            substrate to mixture and heating the mixture.
AU VISWANATHAN T
AE UNIV ARKANSAS (UYAR-C)
GA 2020B09480
AB    NOVELTY - Producing water purification composition,                comprises: providing a substrate having at least                one functional group that has hydroxyl, thiol,                carboxyl, and/or amino group; depositing a solution                of a metal salt on the substrate; depositing a                solution of carboxylic acid compound on the                substrate; forming a mixture where the metal                crosslinks the hydroxyl, thiol, carboxyl and/or                amino groups on the surface of the substrate, and                the carboxylic acid compound; and heating the                mixture till the product is dry.
   USE - The method is useful for producing water                purification composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides composition that to                reduce: level of mercury or arsenic in fluid                streams by contacting fluid stream with used                alumina, i.e. used Claus catalyst; and phosphorus                and other contaminants from contaminated                waters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing a water purification composition,                comprising providing starting substrate capable of                reacting with ammonium zirconium carbonate,                ammonium zinc carbonate, or an activated zirconium                species generated by thermal decomposition,                depositing a carboxylic acid compound solution on                the surface of the substrate, treating the                carboxylic acid compound deposited on the surface                of the substrate with a solution of ammonium                zirconium carbonate, ammonium zinc carbonate, or an                activated zirconium species to form a mixture and                heating the mixture till the product is ammonia and                carbon dioxide free.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: treating the mixture with an alkali so                  that the carboxylic acid compound becomes                  covalently attached to the metal; heating the                  mixture so that the metal cross-links the                  hydroxyl, thiol, carboxyl and/or amino groups on                  the surface of the substrate. The mixture is                  heated to a temperature up to 80 degrees C. The                  mixture is heated at 70-80 degrees C for a period                  of time effective to form a composite that is                  free of ammonia and carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Components: The substrate comprises wood,                  cellulose, alginate, carboxymethylcellulose,                  hydroxypropyl cellulose, chitin, lignin, tannin,                  silk, wool, chitosan, peanut hulls, wood pulp,                  grass, bamboo, polyvinyl alcohol, soybean waste,                  okara, cork, phenol-formaldehyde resins, maize                  cob, old newspaper, coconut husk, graphene oxide,                  activated carbon, amino resin, open celled foams                  containing hydroxyl, amino or carboxyl groups on                  surface, coffee grounds, and/or protein. The                  carboxylic acid compound comprises betaine or a                  betaine derivative, aromatic carboxylic acid,                  arginine, lysine, and/or histidine. The                  carboxylic acid compound comprises e.g.                  isoleucine betaine hydrochloride salt, or                  trimethylamine moiety of formula                  (N(CH3)3-CH2-C(=O)-OH) (I). The metal salt                  contains a cation comprising aluminum, titanium,                  manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc,                  zirconium, molybdenum, tin, bismuth, lanthanum,                  cerium, and/or yttrium. The metal in the                  composition is in a reduced oxidation state                  compared with the metal in the metal salt. The                  metal salt contains an anion comprising chlorine,                  nitrate, acetic acid, and/or sulfate. The metal                  salt comprises e.g. ammonium zirconium carbonate,                  ammonium zinc carbonate, zirconium nitrate, or                  freshly prepared aqueous soluble zirconium(IV)                  hydroxide. The produced water purification                  composition comprises the substrate with a                  surface containing hydroxyl, thiol, carboxyl                  and/or amino groups, carboxylic acid compound,                  and metal, where the metal cross-links the                  hydroxyl, thiol, carboxyl and/or amino groups on                  the surface of the substrate, and the carboxylic                  acid compound.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU XU F
   YANG Z
AE NANJING CHUQING ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B1416S
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a multi-stage purifying automobile tail gas purifying pipe, comprising a catalytic layer, a filter layer, a baffle plate and a radiating ring; one side of the pipe body is provided with an air inlet pipe; the other side is provided with an exhaust pipe; one side of the air inlet pipe is provided with a catalytic layer; the inner part of the catalytic layer is provided with a catalyst; and one side of the catalytic layer is provided with a filter layer; one side of the filter layer is provided with a baffle plate; the outer side of the exhaust pipe is provided with a radiating ring. The utility model has scientific and reasonable structure, convenient operation, through the catalytic layer can make the gas and catalyst generate catalytic reaction, the harmful gas in the waste gas by oxidation, reduction reaction, converting into water harmless to human body, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, so as to reduce the discharge of the pollutant, reduce the pollution to the environment; through the filter layer, it can effectively absorb the harmful gas contained in the purified waste gas, improve the waste gas purifying quality, improve the environment pollution phenomenon.

PT P
PN CN111826343-A
TI Cell culture solution used for enhancing induction            of cartilage differentiation for preparing mesenchymal            stem cells, comprises glucosamine sulfate and sodium            glycerophosphate.
AU XIU B
   LI S
   SONG Y
   SHI X
   LIU S
AE BEIJING ZHONGWEI YIZHENG TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B08954
AB    NOVELTY - Cell culture solution comprises glucosamine                sulfate and sodium glycerophosphate, where the                concentration of glucosamine sulfate is 0.5-1.5                %mass, and the concentration of sodium                glycerophosphate is 0.5-1 mu M.
   USE - The cell culture solution is useful for                enhancing induction of cartilage differentiation                for preparing mesenchymal stem cells (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method discards placental blood for the                extraction and prepares platelet lysates with wide                range of sources and low cost, stably grows and                adheres the mesenchymal stem cells to the wall,                obtains similar characteristics to the cells                obtained by conventional culture methods in which                the cells are in a typical spindle-shaped spiral                shape under the microscope, activates the                expression of cartilage differentiation marker                genes SOX9 and COL2A1 in cells, increases the                number and intensity of chondrogenesis after                induction, and shows stronger chondrogenic                differentiation ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for enhancing mesenchymal stem cells to induce                differentiation of chondrocytes, involving adding                chondrogenic induction medium, and culturing in an                incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide and                saturated humidity at 37 degrees C, where the                chondrogenic differentiation medium comprises                DMEM/F12 (Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium/Nutrient                Mixture F-12) medium supplemented with 100 mu M                dexamethasone, 10 vol.%/vol.% fetal bovine serum, 1                mu M ascorbic acid and 1.5 mu M sodium                glycerophosphate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The cell culture fluid                  further comprises penicillin/streptomycin double                  antibody, and peripheral blood-derived platelet                  lysate (PBPL). The basic medium in the cell                  culture solution comprises Dulbecco's Modified                  Eagle's medium (DMEM) or Iscove's Modified                  Dulbecco's medium (IMDM). Preferred Composition:                  The concentration of the penicillin/streptomycin                  double antibody in the cell culture fluid is 100                  U/ml. The concentration of the PBPL is 5-10                  vol.%. Preferred Method: The PBPL is prepared by                  (a) taking 100-200 ml placental blood into blood                  collection bag, using heparin sodium of 2000-5000                  UI as anticoagulant, aspirating the placental                  blood into a 50 ml sterile centrifuge tube,                  centrifuging at a speed of 1500-2500 rpm at 4-30                  degrees C for 10-15 minutes, and collecting the                  supernatant, (b) transferring the collected                  supernatant to a 50 ml centrifuge tube,                  centrifuging at 1200-2000 rpm for 10-15 minutes,                  discarding the upper 3/4 of the supernatant and                  bottom red blood cells, gently oscillating the                  remaining middle layer on a vortex oscillator to                  mix, which is the placental blood platelet-rich                  plasma, (c) preparing the platelet-rich plasma by                  three freeze-thaw methods to prepare platelet                  lysates, and (d) centrifuging the platelet lysate                  at a speed of 8000-12000 rpm at 4-30 degrees C                  for 20-30 minutes, and discarding the precipitate                  to obtain supernatant which is the product.

The central processing unit is electrically connected to a 485 communication module and a human-computer interaction module. A signal end of the 485 communication module is electrically connected with a sensor. An end of a power module is connected in series with the central processing unit, the 485 communication module, the control module and the human-computer interaction module through a wire. Preferred Method: The extraction temperature is 150-180 degrees C. The supercritical CO2 extraction is performed at 20-30 MPa and 40-60 degrees C.

AE CHEN X (CHEN-Individual)

The gasification agent generation and gas distribution system is composed of a gasification agent generation device. The main components are carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O) and nitrogen (N2). The medium and high temperature oxygen depletion agent enters the boiler through the pipeline and the gas distribution device. The closed material crushing, drying and conveying and distribution system is composed of a closed automatic feeding device and a material conveying device, which is combined with the gasification agent generation and gas distribution system.
   USE - Garbage and biomass gasification-gas boiler system.

PT P
PN CN111719143-A; CN111719143-B
TI Preparation of silver-plated terahertz hollow core optical fiber used in preparation of e.g. silver iodide infrared optical fiber, involves treating acrylonitrile butadiene styrene plastic capillary, and forming silver coating layer.
AU JING C
   YU S
   XIE G
   ZHONG Y
   LU X
   LIU S
   HU M
   XU J
   CHENG Y
   CHU J
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 2020983904
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of acrylonitrile butadiene styrene (ABS) plastic capillary silver-plated terahertz hollow core optical fiber involves treating the inner surface of ABS plastic capillary as a base tube by physical and chemical methods, forming a dense metallic silver coating layer on the inner surface of the ABS plastic capillary using a peristaltic pump, by a liquid-phase chemical deposition method.
   USE - Preparation of acrylonitrile butadiene styrene plastic capillary silver-plated terahertz hollow core optical fiber used in preparation of silver/silver iodide infrared hollow core optical fiber and silver/cyclic olefin copolymer terahertz/infrared hollow core optical fiber (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of acrylonitrile butadiene styrene plastic capillary silver-plated terahertz hollow core optical fiber with high toughness, elasticity, and bonding strength between the metal coating layer and the ABS plastic, and low loss transmission of terahertz wave and carbon dioxide infrared laser, by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of an ABS plastic capillary silver/silver iodide infrared hollow core optical fiber, which involves forming a silver halide reflective film on the surface of the dense metallic silver coating layer through a halogenation reaction; and
   (2) preparation of an ABS plastic capillary silver/cyclic olefin copolymer terahertz or infrared hollow core optical fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of iodine and cyclohexane in the cyclohexane solution of iodine, is 60-100 mL/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The halogenation reaction is carried out using cyclohexane solution of iodine. The cyclic olefin copolymer layer is deposited on the surface of the dense metallic silver coating layer using a chloroform solution of cyclic olefin copolymer. The liquid-phase chemical deposition is performed using 2-100 mol/L tin chloride sensitizing solution, deionized water, 2-100 mmol/L silver nitrate alkaline solution and 2-100 mmol/L glucose solution. The physical and chemical methods are performed by washing the inner surface of the ABS plastic capillary using a cleaning agent which is a mixed solution of detergent and decontamination powder in a mass ratio of 1:0.5-2, and injecting ethanol and deionized water. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of cyclic olefin copolymer and chloroform in the chloroform solution of cyclic olefin copolymer is 1:5-15 or 1:5-8.

TI Cryopreserved human PBMC resuscitation fluid useful for neutralizing toxic effect of dimethyl sulfoxide on cells and breaking down cell metabolites comprises e.g. recombinant human albumin, transferrin, glutamine, insulin and vitamin B.
AU KONG W
   LAN X
   HE J
   CHEN Z
AE ZHUHAI BASO CELL SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 202093651C
AB    NOVELTY - Cryopreserved human PBMC resuscitation fluid comprises a recombinant human albumin, transferrin, glutamine, insulin, vitamin B, dextran, sodium chloride, and calcium chloride.
   USE - The cryopreserved human PBMC resuscitation fluid is useful for neutralizing toxic effect of dimethyl sulfoxide on cells, breaking down cell metabolites, improving cell viability, and increasing cell membrane stability.
   ADVANTAGE - The cryopreserved human PBMC resuscitation fluid: can increase the cell viability after resuscitation; restores to the maximum the biological activity before cryopreservation, so that immune cells can be effectively induced and activated into target cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for resuscitating cryopreserved human PBMC using the resuscitation fluid comprising (1) removing the cells to be resuscitated from the liquid nitrogen, and immediately transferring them to a 37 degrees C water bath for rapid dissolution within 2 minutes, (2) transferring the dissolved cell suspension to a pre-prepared centrifuge tube containing 5 times the volume of resuscitation solution, centrifuging at 500x g for 10 minutes at room temperature, and discarding the supernatant and (3) resuspending the cells, adjusting the cell density to 2-2.5x 106 cells/ml with the resuscitation solution, incubating in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator for 60 minutes, centrifuging at 500x g for 10 minutes at room temperature, and collecting the cells.

   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
GA 2020765911
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing solid carbon and hydrogen gas by molten metal pyrolysis of hydrocarbons, involves (i) feeding a stream of hydrocarbon into a pyrolysis reactor through a catalytic layer of molten metal to pyrolyze the hydrocarbon into solid carbon and hydrogen gas, (ii) feeding a stream of molten salt into the pyrolysis reactor to separate the solid carbon from the molten metal, (iii) collecting a product gas containing hydrogen gas that evolves from the reactor, (iv) collecting a mixture comprising solid carbon and molten salt, and (v) separating the mixture obtained in step (iv) into a product comprising solid carbon and separated salt.
   USE - The method is used for producing solid carbon and hydrogen gas by molten metal pyrolysis of hydrocarbons. The molten salt is used for separating solid carbon from a molten metal (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves the use of only a single layer of catalyst, preferably having reduced carbon dioxide gas emission, preferably resulting in less waste emission. The method improves the output of valuable products from pyrolysis processes, or improves the quality of the products. The method separates solid carbon from molten metal, preferably at high temperatures and/or in a continuous process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a reactor for performing molten metal pyrolysis of hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The reactor has an inlet for receiving the hydrocarbon at or near the bottom end of the reactor, an outlet for discharging a mixture of carbon and molten salts in a side wall, and an outlet for discharging a product gas comprising hydrogen at or near the top end.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves (vi) separating the product gas obtained in step (iii) into unconverted hydrocarbon gas and hydrogen gas, preferably by using an adsorbent material to obtain purified hydrogen gas and recovered hydrocarbon. The recovered hydrocarbon is recycled back into the pyrolysis reactor as part of step (i). The layer of molten salt is present in the pyrolysis reactor, and the step (iv) involves skimming to collect the solid carbon and a part of the layer of molten salt, such that substantially all of the solid carbon is removed from the reactor. The step (v) involves separating solid carbon from the separated salt by filtering and/or washing the mixture with an aqueous liquid, preferably by using a metal filter or a ceramic filter to obtain a product comprising pure solid carbon and a separated salt. The separated salt is recycled into the reactor as part of step (ii). Preferred Conditions: The reactor is kept at a temperature of 250-1500 degrees C. Preferred Components: The metal in the molten metal is chosen from indium, bismuth, stannum, gallium, lead, silver, copper, tin, platinum, nickel, and gold. The salt has a heat capacity of at most 2 J/K, preferably 1.6 J/K, and/or the salt comprises at least one of potassium chloride, sodium chloride, lithium chloride, magnesium chloride, zinc chloride, zinc bromide and sodium bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon comprises a 1-4C hydrocarbon, preferably methane.

The regeneration processing device comprises a lime-iron salt processing device and a calcium ion removing device; the lime-iron salt processing device comprises orderly connected lime reaction tank; a first thickener, an iron salt reaction tank, a second thickener and a liquid storage tank; the calcium ion removing device comprises a venturi tube, a calcium removing reactor and a circulating pump; the venturi tube is orderly connected with the calcium removing reactor and the circulating pump; the circulating pump is connected with the venturi tube; the venturi tube is connected with the lime reaction tank through the air suction pipe; the calcium removing reactor is further connected with the liquid storage tank. The regeneration processing device is added with calcium ion removing device on the basis of the original " lime-iron salt method " processing device, fully uses the carbon dioxide generated in the lime neutralizing stage, at the same time, effectively removing the content of calcium ion in the regenerated water, The invention reduces the processing cost and reduces the discharge amount of the waste gas in the processing process.

PN DE102019134104-A1; US2020220234-A1; CN111430644-A; US11289745-B2
TI Lithium ion battery for use in e.g. battery powered vehicle, has separator, positive electrode, and negative electrode that are composed of transition metal compound that catalyzes any gaseous reactants formed in battery to form liquid.
AU DADHEECH G V
   YANG L
   XIAO X
   QI G
   SAHARAN V P
AE GM GLOBAL TECHNOLOGY OPERATIONS INC (GENK-C)
   GM GLOBAL TECHNOLOGY OPERATIONS INC (GENK-C)
GA 202061845C
AB    NOVELTY - The lithium ion battery (20) has a positive electrode (24) and a negative electrode (22) between which a separator (26) is arranged. The separator, positive electrode, and negative electrode are composed of transition metal compound that catalyzes any gaseous reactants formed in the lithium ion battery to form a liquid. The gaseous reactants are selected from methane, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, ethylene, and ethane. The transition metal compound is selected from ruthenium, titanium, molybdenum, nickel, cobalt, palladium, and iridium. The ruthenium compound is selected from bis cyclopentadienyl ruthenium, bis ethylcyclopentadienyl ruthenium II, ruthenium III acetylacetonate, ruthenium carbonyl, ruthenium IV oxide, ruthenium III 2,4-pentanedionate, dichlor mesitylene ruthenium II dimer, and bis 2,4-dimethylpentadienyl ruthenium II.
   USE - Lithium ion battery for use in battery powered vehicle and portable consumer electronic device such as laptop computer, tablet computer, cellular phone, and wireless power tool.
   ADVANTAGE - The performance of lithium-ion batteries is improved by avoiding or suppressing gas formation while using desirable active materials that provide long-lasting batteries with sustained high capacity, high discharge rates, and long life.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the electrochemical cell.
   Lithium ion battery (20)
   Negative electrode (22)
   Positive electrode (24)
   Separator (26)
   Positive electrode current collector (34)

PN RU2725031-C1
TI NANOPOROUS MATERIAL FOR SENSITIVE ELEMENTS OF GAS SENSORS AND METHOD OF PRODUCTION THEREOF.
AU MENSHUTINA N V
   TSYGANKOV P YU
   KHUDEEV I I
   LEBEDEV A E
   IVANOV S I
AE MENSHUTINA N V (MENS-Individual)
   TSYGANKOV P YU (TSYG-Individual)
   KHUDEEV I I (KHUD-Individual)
   LEBEDEV A E (LEBE-Individual)
   IVANOV S I (IVAN-Individual)
GA 202063627H
AB    NOVELTY - Invention can be used to create gas sensors intended for detecting a wide range of gases and vapours. Essence of the invention is that in the method of producing nanoporous material for sensitive elements of gas sensors at a preliminary step, a sol is obtained, wherein the sol is obtained by mixing silanes with an organic solvent and an aqueous acid solution with further stirring for 1015 minutes and subsequent holding for 24 hours at room temperature, after which carbon nanomaterial is added to obtained sol at preliminary stage at weight ratio of carbon nanomaterial:sol = 1:(462300) and sol is mixed with carbon nanomaterial in ultrasonic bath for two hours, after which gel agent NH3 is added to the mixture of sol and nanomaterial in the form of ammonia water with mixing for gelation for 23 minutes and then the obtained gel is held for 24 hours at room temperature for final formation of structure of composite material which is subjected to aging, for which it is placed in solvent for 24 hours with replacement of solvent every 8 hours and then performing its supercritical drying, for which is loaded into a sealed unit with liquefied carbon dioxide in the supply mode, inside which pressure 120180 atm is set and maintained and temperature 40100C for 612 hours.
   USE - Machine building.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is enabling creation of high-sensitivity gas sensors designed to detect a wide range of gases and vapours.

PN US2020203738-A1; WO2020128800-A1; US11201337-B2
TI Fuel cell system for production of electricity, has semi-permeable membrane that separates main chamber and sub chamber, and is configured for allowing water to diffuse, and removing water from anode exhaust stream.
AU JOLLY S
   CHIMBOLE F J
   JAHNKE F C
   MALWITZ J
   GHEZEL-AYAGH H
AE FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 202057111Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an anode that is configured to output an anode exhaust stream comprising hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and water. A membrane dryer (230) is configured to receive the anode exhaust stream, remove water from the anode exhaust stream, and output a membrane dryer outlet stream. The membrane dryer comprises a first chamber (231) that is configured to receive the anode exhaust stream. A second chamber (232) is configured to receive a purge gas. A semi-permeable membrane separates the first chamber and the second chamber. The semi-permeable membrane is configured to allow water to diffuse, thus, removing water from the anode exhaust stream.
   USE - Fuel cell system for production of electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The size of the dryer needed is minimized. The water removed by the membrane dryer or the separation system is used to provide water steam needed for internal reforming of methane in fuel cell fuel, thus, eliminating the need for external process water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for removing water from anode exhaust stream.

PN CN111268994-A
TI Gas-driven 3D printing material comprises carbonized cementitious materials, carbonized reinforced phase, binder, and water.
AU WANG F
   ZHONG K
   LIU Z
   HE Y
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202054988Q
AB    NOVELTY - Gas-driven 3D printing material comprises 100 pts. wt. carbonized cementitious materials, 10-15 pts. wt. carbonized reinforced phase, 1-2 pts. wt. binder, and 28-38 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Used as a gas-driven 3D printing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas-driven 3D printing material: realizes the simultaneous printing process and curing process; shortens the molding time, thus improves printing efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing gas-driven 3D printing material comprising mixing the carbonized cementitious material with the carbonized reinforced phase ball mill, obtaining a mixture A, dissolving the binder in the water to obtain a solution B, adding the mixture A to the stir in the solution B to obtain a gas-driven 3D printing material; and
   (2) use method of gas-driven 3D printing material comprising building the target printing model through 3D data modelling, slicing the target print model and plan the scanning route, referring to the print size and print precision requirements of the target printing model, and matching the appropriate printing parameters, after the air bubbles in the 3D printing material driven by gas are discharged, e loading into the barrel of the 3D printer, turning the infrared radiant heater on to preheat the printing area, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the sealed printing area to discharge the air in the sealed printing area, and starting the printing, completing the printing, carbonizing and hardening to obtain a printed product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonized cementitious material is gamma -type dicalcium silicate, monocalcium silicate, tricalcium disilicate, steel slag powder and portland cement mixture, and the particle size of the carbonized gelling material is 2-10 microns. The carbonized cementitious material is a mixture of steel slag powder and Portland cement, The gas-driven 3D printing material also includes: 1-2 pts. wt. water reducing agent. The carbonization enhanced phase is chitosan or amorphous siliceous material. The binder is polyethylene glycol, polyvinyl alcohol, aqueous polyurethane, ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, or sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. Preferred Method: The water-reducing agent is a composite water-reducing agent formed by mixing a polycarboxylic acid-based high-efficiency water-reducing agent and a sodium citrate retarder. The treatment temperature of the carbonization hardening treatment is 0-100 degrees C, the relative humidity is 5-100%, and the carbon dioxide concentration is 10-100%.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); P64 (Working cement, clay, stone (B28).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN US2020181070-A1; US10793517-B2
TI Producing taurine involves subjecting ammonia solution of ammonium isethionate and hydrogenation catalyst to ammonolysis reaction in presence of hydrogen, removing ammonia and decomposing ammonium taurinate to taurine and separating taurine.
AU HU S
AE VITAWORKS IP LLC (VITA-Non-standard)
GA 202050389M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing taurine from ammonium isethionate, comprises: (a) preparing an ammonia solution comprising ammonium isethionate and at least one hydrogenation catalyst; (b) subjecting the ammonia solution to an ammonolysis reaction in the presence of hydrogen or at least one hydrogen donor to yield a solution comprising ammonium taurinate; (c) removing ammonia and decomposing the ammonium taurinate to taurine by heating to obtain a crystalline suspension of taurine; and (d) separating the taurine by solid-liquid separation to obtain a mother liquor.
   USE - The process is useful for producing taurine from ammonium isethionate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes solid ammonium isethionate that provides taurine with improved quality and high overall yield of greater than 90% to nearly quantitative without generating any inorganic salt as byproduct, and without requiring the purge of mother liquor; greatly reduces or eliminates waste discharge; does not require acids comprising sulfuric acid, carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide or sulfurous acid, hence it is greatly simplified and no salt as byproduct is formed; does not require alkali recycling, thus greatly increasing reaction mass efficiency; and can be carried out discontinuously, semi-continuously, and continuously. The taurine produced from the process does not contain any inorganic contaminants comprising alkali sulfide or alkali halide as compared to the taurine produced from the conventional processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogenation catalyst comprises catalysts based on nickel, cobalt, copper, platinum, palladium, ruthenium, rhodium, osmium, iridium and/or rhenium, preferably Raney nickel, Raney copper, palladium on carbon or ruthenium on carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The mother liquor after the separation of the taurine is mixed the ammonia solution prepared in the step (a). Preferred Components: The hydrogen donor is formic acid or a secondary alcohol.
   EXAMPLE - 1200 ml 24% Ammonia solution, 296 g sodium isethionate and 2 g sodium hydroxide were added into a 2 l autoclave. The solution was heated to 260 degrees C for 2 hours under autogenous pressure. After cooling, 286.2 g ammonium isethionate was added and ammonia was removed by boiling to bring the pH of the solution to pH 6.5. After heating to remove excess ammonia, the solution was concentrated and cooled to room temperature, to obtain a suspension of crystalline taurine. Taurine was recovered by filtration and dried to 189.3 g. Taurine was recovered in a yield of 75.7%.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-A09B8; E11-F07A; J04-E01; J04-E04B; N01-C03; N02; N02-F01; N02-F02; N03-E; N06-C01; N06-F; N07-D08A

   ADVANTAGE - The spray is non-toxic to the human body, which can prevent viral upper respiratory tract infection, COVID-19, and hand-foot-and-mouth diseases, simple filling, economical, easy to use and blocks the absorption of harmful substances in the air through the respiratory tract mucosa and lungs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the spray, comprising (i) preparing composite nano-additives with particles less than 1 mu m; (ii) mixing the quantities of Forsythia phenol, Eucalyptus globulus and sodium alginate separately; (iii) mixing by air classification ACM mill, cold crushing, class C sieving, performing rapid expansion, air sieve separating, and deposition method into the composite nano-additive to make the suspended particle spray; (iv) filtering the suspended particle spray through a special bag; (v) pouring into the spray bottle quantitatively through the canning equipment to form the suspended particle spray; and (vi) packing the canned floating particle spray in the spray bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The spray comprises 6% Forsythia phenol, 3% Eucalyptus globulus, 0.3% sodium alginate and composite nano-additive fluidization agent (remaining amount). The suspended particle has particle size of 10-15 mu m, particle size distribution of uniform, and geometric structure of uniform. The particle diameter of the composite nano-additive fluidization agent is less than 1 mu m. The composite nano-additive fluidization agent is inorganic montmorillonite material. Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises activating the biological material system-slow release using supercritical carbon dioxide and bismuth particles, heating, drying by accelerated centrifugal atomization and attaching the nano-materials to the surface of the montmorillonite inorganic particles.

PT P
PN CN111118099-A
TI Screening high-throughput drugs for leukemia comprises collecting bone marrow samples, separating mononuclear cells using bone marrow mononuclear cell separation liquid kit, and isolating leukemia stem cell population.
AU ZHOU B
   LU Y
   LI J
   DONG Y
   YU J
   SHEN L
AE TUS-HEALCARE SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TUSH-Non-standard)
GA 202043505P
AB    NOVELTY - Screening high-throughput drugs for leukemia comprises (i) (ia) collecting bone marrow samples, (ib) separating mononuclear cells using bone marrow mononuclear cell separation liquid kit, (ic) isolating leukemia CD34+/CD38- stem cell population by immunomagnetic bead method, and (id) separating and purifying the magnetic beads, inoculating the cells on DMEM medium containing 20 ng/ml epidermal cell growth factor, 100 mu /ml penicillin and 100 mu /ml streptomycin, and 10 wt.% fetal bovine serum, incubating at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator, determining the adherence of cells after 12 hours, changing the medium, and sub-culturing, and (ii) selecting the drug concentration according to the drug instructions and pharmacokinetics, and mixing with blood tumor stem cells to analyze the inhibitory effect of drugs on blood tumor stem cells.
   USE - The method is useful for screening high-throughput drugs for leukemia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: personally and accurately gives specific treatment plan opinions to patients, and strive for valuable treatment opportunities for relapsed and refractory patients; reduces the risk of test drugs; increases the curative effect of the drug to the greatest extent; reduces the side effects of the drug; uses drug combination; and provides reference for the patient to develop clinical combination medication.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The specific steps of using the bone marrow mononuclear cell separation liquid kit in the step (i) to separate mononuclear cells are: (a) adding specific amount of LF solution to the collected bone marrow cells, uniformly mixing, centrifuging at 500 g for 20min, discarding the supernatant, washing the pellet twice using solution F, and then suspending it with whole blood and tissue dilution containing 20 wt.% fetal bovine serum, (b) adding the same amount of separation liquid as the bone marrow single-cell suspension to the 15 ml centrifuge tube, and separating liquid greater than or equal to 7 ml, (c) carefully taking the bone marrow single cell suspension on the liquid surface of the separation liquid by centrifugation, and centrifuging at a speed of 400-500 g for 20-30 minutes, (d) after centrifugation, dividing the cells in the centrifuge tube into four layers from top to bottom, where the first layer is the diluent layer, the second layer is the ring-shaped milky white mononuclear cell layer, and the third layer is the transparent separation liquid layer, and the fourth layer is the red blood cell layer, using pipette to carefully suck the second layer of milky white mononuclear cells into another 15 ml centrifuge tube, and adding 10 ml washing solution to the centrifuge tube and mixing the cells, (e) centrifuging at 250 g for 10 minutes and discarding the supernatant, (f) resuspending the cells with 5-10 ml of washing solution with a pipette, (g) centrifuging at 250 g for 10 minutes, and discarding the supernatant, and (h) taking 0.5 ml of the corresponding liquid required for subsequent experiments, and resuspending the cells. The specific steps of the immunomagnetic bead separation of CD34+/CD38- stem cell population in the step (i) are: (1) magnetically labeling, adding 800 mu l buffer for every 107 total cells to resuspend, adding 200 mu l biotin antibody, uniformly mixing, incubating at 4 degrees C for 15 minutes, washing the cells with buffer, centrifuging at 1200 revolutions per minute/minute for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding micro-strain buffer containing 200 mu l biotin antibody, uniformly mixing, incubating at 4 degrees C for 15 minutes, washing the cells using buffer, centrifuging at 1200 revolutions per minute/minute for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding 800 mu l buffer to 107 total cells to resuspend, placing into the MACS sorter, taking 2 ml buffer solution slowly into the LD-type sorting column, then adding the resuspended cell suspension to the LD-type analyzing column for negative selection, and collecting all negative components and counting, and (2) magnetically labeling, resuspending cells in 200 mu l buffer, adding 100 mu l blocker, 200 mu l CD34 magnetic beads, mixing, incubating at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes, washing the cells using buffer, centrifuging at 1200 revolutions per minute/minute for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding 800 mu l buffer for every 107 total cells to resuspend, magnetic bead sorting, placing into MACS sorting instrument, taking 2 ml buffer solution slowly into LD type sorting column, negative cell components flow out first, adding 500 mu l buffer solution to the empty original sample test tube and then adding to the LD-type sorting column, after repeating for 3 times, removing the LD-type sorting column from the sorter, adding 1000 mu l buffer to the LD-type sorting column, and using the piston equipped with the sorting column to quickly push out the labeled cells. In the step (ii), calculating the screening doses of 1.1 mu M and 1 mu M according to the drug instructions and pharmacokinetic calculations, calculating the inhibition rate of the drugs on tumor stem cells, i.e., in the high-throughput drug in vitro test, the drug screening dose has a killing effect on the patient's tumor cells, and the calculation formula of the inhibition rate is: inhibition rate is (1-number of tumor cell survival in drug screening group/number of tumor cell survival in blank control group)x 100%, sorting according to the level of inhibition rate, the higher the inhibition rate, the better the effect of drugs on tumor stem cells, and using drugs with an inhibition rate greater than 65% as clinical drugs. Preferred Components: The selection of the basic drug spectrum of leukemia treatment in the drug library is: vincristine, vinblastine, etoposide VP16, teniposide, irinotecan, gemcitabine, Methotrexate, arabinosporine, 5-fluorouracil, cyclophosphamide, ifosfamide, redpas, busulfan, melphalan, chlorambucil, cisplatin, carboplatin, bleomycin, lomustine, carmustine, dacarbazine, hydroxyurea doxorubicin, epirubicin, daunorubicin, mitoxantrone, pirarubicin, imatinib, nilotinib, dasatinib, bortezomib, decitabine, thalidomide, lenalidomide, dexamethasone, prednisone, methylprednisone, hydrocortisone, cinobufagin, bexarotene, ouabain formate, homoharringtonine and hydroxycamptothecin.

PT P
PN US2020079699-A1; US11104617-B2
TI Treating manure to isolate target precipitate            involves acidifying manure slurry to instigate nutrient            release, separating nutrient-rich solution from            nutrient-deficient solids, precipitating target            precipitate by adding base and collecting.
AU BLANEY L
   SHASHVATT U
AE UNIV MARYLAND BALTIMORE COUNTY (UMBA-C)
GA 202020397F
AB    NOVELTY - Treating manure in order to isolate a target                precipitate, comprises: (a) providing a manure                slurry; (b) acidifying the manure slurry to                instigate nutrient release; (c) separating                nutrient-rich solution from nutrient-deficient                solids; (d) precipitating the target precipitate by                introducing at least one base to the nutrient-rich                solution; and (e) collecting the target                precipitate.
   USE - The process is useful for: treating manure in                order to isolate a target precipitate, where the                target precipitate comprises struvite and/or                potassium struvite (claimed) and/or ammonia; and                phosphorus extraction. The precipitated                nutrient-rich minerals are useful as a slow release                fertilizer, specialty fertilizer in plant nurseries                or main agricultural fertilizers.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: does not require the addition of                external chemicals e.g. calcium, magnesium and                ammonium ions, other than the acid and the base;                converts high concentrations of animal wastes into                nutrients and other useful products; provides                bubbling of the manure slurry with carbon                dioxide-enriched gas, hence reduces operating costs                and agitates system, resulting in smaller particle                sizes and faster nutrient release kinetics;                provides slow release fertilizer even when applied                at relatively high application rates, without                damaging plant roots and with low leaching                potential; and reduces particle size, and/or                removes unwanted large material to ensure easy flow                through system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: A pH of the acidified                  manure slurry is 4.5-5.5. The pH of the acidified                  manure slurry is achieved by adding at least one                  acid to the manure slurry and optionally by                  bubbling the manure slurry with carbon                  dioxide-enriched gas. A pH of the nutrient-rich                  solution is raised to 8-10 to initiate                  precipitation of the target precipitate. The                  process further comprises: sparging the                  nutrient-rich solution with air during                  precipitation; and collecting and reusing                  nitrogen- and potassium-rich effluent remaining                  after precipitation of the target precipitate.                  The steps (a)-(e) can be repeated more than once.                  Preferred Components: The acid comprises                  hydrochloric acid and/or sulfuric acid. The base                  comprises sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide                  and/or magnesium hydroxide. The manure slurry can                  have a concentration of 10-1000 g/l.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110655901-A
TI Polyurethane hot melt adhesive useful for rapid assembly, comprises isocyanate, cyclic polydimethylsiloxane, hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer, castor oil, antifoaming agent, wetting agent, plasticizer and catalyst.
AU DAI S
   MIAO J
   LIU F
AE CHANGZHOU WANQUAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202007552V
AB    NOVELTY - Polyurethane hot melt adhesive comprises 15-35 pts. wt. isocyanate, 15-30 pts. wt. cyclic polydimethylsiloxane, 20-40 pts. wt. hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer, 15-30 pts. wt. castor oil, 3-5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, 3-5 pts. wt. wetting agent, 1-3 pts. wt. plasticizer and 1-3 pts. wt. catalyst.
   USE - The adhesive is useful for rapid assembly (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesive: has short curing time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polyurethane hot melt adhesive comprising (i) placing the isocyanate and hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer in a planetary agitator at 100 degrees C, stirring at a speed of 100-200 revolutions/minute, introducing protective gas into the planetary agitator and stirring for 30-45 minutes to obtain hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer/isocyanate, (ii) casting castor oil into carbon dioxide supercritical fluid for extraction, removing excess water from castor oil, pouring into the planetary agitator at 80 degrees C, introducing protective gas continuously for 1-3 hours, pressurizing to 2 standard atmospheric pressure for 15-30 minutes, stirring and discharging to obtain polyurethane hot melt adhesive prepolymer, and (iii) hot melting the obtained hot melt adhesive prepolymer, adding antifoaming agent, wetting agent, plasticizer and catalyst, heating uniformly at 130 degrees C in hot melt state, evacuating, removing bubbles for 30 minutes, stirring and discharging the material to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The isocyanate includes difunctional isocyanate, toluene diisocyanate and/or 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate. The antifoaming agent includes polydimethylsiloxane or polyether-modified silicone. The wetting agent includes propylene glycol or glycerol. The plasticizer includes diisobutyl phthalate or diisononyl phthalate. The catalyst includes february acid dibutyl tin, stannous octoate, lead octoate, triethylamine, triethylenediamine, cobalt octoate, octyl octanoate or tetraisobutyl titanate.

PN CN110559982-A; CN110559982-B
TI Method for improving performance of single layer of hydrotalcite to adsorb carbon-dioxide (CO2) gas, involves determining strong adsorption capacity for CO2 under optimal configuration, and designing single-layer hydrotalcite.
AU XIA S
   FANG L
   NI Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2019A67479
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing supercell models of single-layer hydrotalcite nanoplatelets doped with different metals with a lattice constant of vacuum layer to obtain a single-layer hydrotalcite model with several different components using material studio 5.5 quantum simulation software. The quantum chemical package Dmol3 is used to optimize the structure of the constructed single-layer hydrotalcite model with different components to obtain its most stable structural model with the lowest energy. The charge density difference and Mulliken charge are analyzed in the optimal configuration by using the Dmol3 package, the electron transfer conditions are clarified and the bonding strength is analyzed. The strong adsorption capacity for CO2 under the optimal configuration is determined, and a single-layer hydrotalcite is designed with improved CO2 gas adsorption performance.
   USE - Method for improving performance of single layer of hydrotalcite to adsorb CO2 gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrotalcite single layer performance improving method uses density functional theory simulation method to successfully construct a single-layer hydrotalcite structure, and the doped metal atom quickly predicts the stability of the single-layer hydrotalcite structure and shortens the experimental synthesis cycle. The quantum chemical software simulates the adsorption capacity of monolayer hydrotalcite with different composition on CO2, and the single-layer hydrotalcite structure with strong adsorption capacity for CO2 is screened by characterization data.

AU JI S
   GAO M
   MU J
   LIU J
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201980563D
AB    NOVELTY - An open metal site metal-organic framework                (MIL-101) chromium (Cr) assembled bimetallic                catalyst comprises a nano bicomponent gold-metal                catalytically active component supported on open                metal site MIL-101 Cr material.
   USE - The open metal site metal-organic framework                (MIL-101) chromium (Cr) assembled bimetallic                catalyst is used for catalytic conversion of carbon                dioxide and amine/hydrogen to N-methylamine                compound and N,N-dimethylamine compound                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The open metal site MIL-101 Cr assembled                bimetallic catalyst is easy to prepare.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the open metal site MIL-101 chromium                assembled bimetallic catalyst, comprising:
   (A) dispersing MIL-101 Cr material in ethanol                to obtain MIL-101 Cr dispersion as liquid A;
   (B) dissolving chloroauric acid is dissolved                in an ethanol-water mixed solution to prepare a                metal precursor solution as liquid B;
   (C) dissolving a metal salt including cesium                chloride, palladium chloride, or chloroplatinic                acid in a mixed solution of ethanol and water to                prepare a metal precursor solution as liquid C;                and
   (D) stirring while adding liquid B and liquid                C to the liquid A, at 50-100 W for 50-60 minutes,                filtering, washing with deionized water and                ethanol, and vacuum-drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal is ruthenium,                  palladium, or platinum. The catalyst comprises                  1-10 wt.% gold, and 1-5 wt.% metal. It comprises                  1.58-5.12 wt.% gold, 1.01-3.03 wt.% ruthenium,                  1.06-4.26 wt.% palladium, and 1.56-4.68 wt.%                  platinum. The concentration of MIL-101 (Cr) in                  liquid A is 18.7-19 mass%. The concentration of                  chloroauric acid in liquid B is 1-6 mass%. The                  concentration of the metal salt in liquid C is                  1-5 mass%.

AU ZHANG T

   UNIV SHANGHAI MEDICINE & HEALTH SCI (USMH-C)
GA 201962037N
AB    NOVELTY - Imidazo(1,2-a)pyridine ratio-type pH                fluorescent probe i.e.                (E)-4-(2-(2-(4-methoxyphenyl)imidazo(1,2-a)pyridin-7-yl)vinyl)aniline                compound (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for detecting pH changes in                intracellular cell, extremely acidic pH change in                Escherichia coli (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) exhibits a ratiometric emission                fluorescence characteristic under extremely acidic                conditions; and has high sensitivity to hydrogen                ion, excellent selectivity, and large Stokes                shift.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Imidazo(1,2-a)pyridine ratio-type pH                fluorescent probe i.e.                (E)-4-(2-(2-(4-methoxyphenyl)imidazo(1,2-a)pyridin-7-yl)vinyl)aniline                compound of formula (I) is new.
   R = methyl group or phenyl group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: 1.                preparation of (I); 2. use of (I) for detecting pH                changes in intracellular cell, comprising mixing                cells with (I) at 37 degrees C, incubating for 15                minutes in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator, then                washing with phosphate buffer separately, adding                nigericin, continuously incubating the cells for 10                minutes, and detecting by laser confocal                microscopy; and 3. use of (I) for detecting an                extremely acidic pH change in Escherichia coli,                comprising incubating Escherichia coli and (I) in a                shaker for 2 hours and detecting by laser confocal                microscopy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) dissolving                  4-methoxy-N,N-dimethylaniline and 4-chloroaniline                  in anhydrous methanol under an inert gas                  atmosphere, and heating to reflux for 4-5 hours,                  then cooling the reaction solution to room                  temperature and pouring a large amount of                  distilled water, and extracting with a solvent of                  dichloromethane, and distilled under reduced                  pressure to obtain                  (E)-4-chloro-N-((1-isopropyl-1,2-dihydropyridin-4-yl)methylene)aniline,                  (ii) dissolving                  (E)-4-chloro-N-((1-isopropyl-1,2-dihydropyridin-4-yl)methylene)aniline                  obtained in the step (i) and                  2-(4-methoxyphenyl)-7-methylimidazo(1,2-a)pyridine                  in N,N-dimethylformamide and then reacting using                  potassium t-butoxide as an alkaline reagent and                  heating under reflux condition at 90 degrees C                  for 14-15 hours, cooling the reaction solution to                  room temperature, pouring into a large amount of                  distilled water, extracting with dichloromethane                  solvent, and distilling under reduced pressure,                  and (iii) separating product obtained in the step                  (iii) and purifying through silica gel column                  using eluent is composed of solution of ethyl                  acetate and n-hexane in a volume ratio of 1:1 to                  obtain                  (E)-4-(2-(2-(4-methoxyphenyl)imidazo(1,2-a)pyridin-7-yl)vinyl)-N,N-dimethylaniline;                  or (i) dissolving 4-methoxy-N,N-diphenylaniline                  and 4-chloroaniline in anhydrous methanol under                  an inert gas atmosphere, and heating to reflux                  for 4-5 hours, then cooling the reaction solution                  to room temperature and pouring a large amount of                  distilled water, extracting by dichloromethane                  solvent, and distilling off under reduced                  pressure to obtain                  (E)-N-((1-benzhydryl-1,2-dihydropyridin-4-yl)methylene)-4-chloroaniline,                  (ii) dissolving                  (E)-N-((1-benzhydryl-1,2-dihydropyridin-4-yl)methylene)-4-chloroaniline                  obtained in the step (1) and                  2-(4-methoxyphenyl)-7-methylimidazo(1,2-a)pyridine                  in N,N-dimethylformamide, then reacting using                  potassium tert-butoxide as an alkaline reagent                  and heating under reflux at 90 degrees C for                  14-15 hours, cooling the reaction solution to                  room temperature, pouring into a large amount of                  distilled water, extracting with dichloromethane                  solvent, and distilling off the solvent under                  reduced pressure, and (iii) separating product                  obtained in the step (iii) and purifying through                  silica gel column using eluent is a solution of                  ethyl acetate and n-hexane in a volume ratio of                  1:1 to obtain                  (E)-4-(2-(2-(4-methoxyphenyl)imidazo(1,2-a)pyridin-7-yl)vinyl)-N,N-diphenylaniline.                  Preferred Components: The pH of the Escherichia                  coli and (I) are less than 4.

TI Liquid sodium silicate for the production of steel molds, comprises solid sodium silicate having certain modulus, water, sodium oxide, industrial sodium carbonate and sodium sulfate.
AU DANG S
AE DANG S (DANG-Individual)
GA 201956354M
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid sodium silicate comprises 20-25 wt.% solid sodium silicate having a modulus of 3.2-3.3, 40-50 wt.% water, 5-15 wt.% sodium oxide, 5-15 wt.% industrial sodium carbonate and 5-15 wt.% sodium sulfate.
   USE - Liquid sodium silicate for the production of steel molds.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid sodium silicate is cost-effective as it does not use carbon dioxide in the production process. The steel piece is not bonded to the mold shape, and the demolding effect is good during the demolding process, and the surface of the steel casting portion is smoother.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing liquid sodium silicate for the production of steel molds, which involves:
   (A) pulverizing sodium oxide, industrial sodium carbonate and sodium sulfate into particles of 1.5-2.5 mm;
   (B) screening the pulverized sodium oxide, industrial sodium carbonate and sodium sulfate, and re-crushing if it is unqualified;
   (C) adding solid sodium silicate, water, sodium oxide, industrial sodium carbonate and sodium sulfate to the steel reaction tank in proportion;
   (D) heating he steel reaction tank to 180-200 degrees C for 4-5 hours to obtain liquid sodium silicate; and
   (E) filtering out the solid impurities in the liquid sodium silicate collected in the steel reaction tank and storing them in a sealed form.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The liquid sodium silicate comprises 23 wt.% solid sodium silicate having a modulus of 3.2-3.3, 45 wt.% water, 12 wt.% sodium oxide, 12 wt.% industrial sodium carbonate and 8 wt.% sodium sulfate. Preferred Composition: Sodium oxide, industrial sodium carbonate and sodium sulfate powder are pulverized to a particle size of 2.0 mm. The reaction temperature is 190 degrees C and the reaction time was 4.5 hours. The pressure of the steel reaction tank is 7-8 Newton, and the pressure of the steel reaction tank is 0.8-1 mega Pascals (MPa). The steel reaction tank has a rolling speed of 25 revolutions per minute (rpm). The prepared liquid sodium silicate has a modulus of 2.6-2.7 and a concentration of 42-45.

PN DE102018008081-A1
TI Clausius Rankine method for driving e.g. vehicle, involves providing gas compressor that is propelled by fuel cell engine, where method is able to performed by heat energy of mobile fuel cell so as to propel vehicle.
AU SEUFFERHELD J E
AE SEUFFERHELD J E (SEUF-Individual)
GA 201942028B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a gas compressor (42) that is propelled by a fuel cell engine, where the gas compressor is able to be alternately propelled by compression energy of the gas compressor. Energy provided by the fuel cell engine and the compression energy are together utilized for heating up a working medium during the Clausius Rankine process. A turbine with multiple turbine stages is able to be operated with a temperature of the working medium that is less than 200 degrees Celsius during the Clausius Rankine process, where the method is able to be performed by the heat energy of a mobile fuel cell so as to propel a vehicle.
   USE - Clausius Rankine method for driving a vehicle and turbine. Uses include but are not limited to car, lorry, ship and airplane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing heat energy transformation of the mobile fuel cell so as to propel the vehicle, thus allowing to increase the pressure of carbon dioxide so as to allow the turbine to be propelled in an economic manner, while significantly increasing the efficiency of the Clausius Rankine process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a cooling circuit.

AU FINDER Z
   SICK S
   HALLY S
   LOHR I
AE POLYTEX SPORTBELAEGE PRODN GMBH (POLY-Non-standard)
   POLYTEX SPORTBELAEGE PRODN GMBH (POLY-Non-standard)
GA 201938775L
AB    NOVELTY - Production of one component polyurethane system involves preparing an initial mixture and reacting the initial mixture with a prepolymer mixture. The initial mixture comprises mixture of methylene diphenyl diisocyanate monomers comprising 5.9 wt.% 2,2'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, 2,4'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate and 4,4'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate and polyol component. The prepolymer mixture comprises prepolymers. The polyol component comprises carbon dioxide based polyethercarbonate polyol.
   USE - Production of one component polyurethane system for manufacturing elastic flooring such as elastic sub construction layer, base layer, in-situ foamed elastic flooring for athletic tracks, play grounds and outdoor surfaces such as tiles, matt and lane (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to soccer, tennis, handball, sprint races, upholstered furniture and artificial turf backings.
   ADVANTAGE - The one component polyurethane system enables manufacture of elastic flooring having excellent physicochemical properties, UV resistance, heat resistance, water resistance, fungal resistance and storage stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of elastic flooring, which involves applying the manufactured prepolymer mixture on a ground (103) and hardening the applied prepolymer mixture to form the elastic flooring.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the three fresh polyurethane foam lanes.
   Ground (103)
   Sports field (137)
   Width of flooring (140,142)
   Lanes (144,146,148)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide based polyethercarbonate polyol is atmospheric carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide of industrial facilities and/or renewable carbon source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The initial mixture preferably comprises 5.9-11.5 wt.% 2,2'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate monomer, 16.6-23.5 wt.% 2,4'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate monomer and 16.1-17.0 wt.% 4,4'-methylene diphenyl diisocyanate monomer and 10-20 wt.% polyethercarbonate polyol and further comprises 10-15 wt.% plant-based oil or naphthenic oil and phosphoric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polyols further comprise 30-40 wt.% polypropylene glycol. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide based polyethercarbonate polyol is generated by catalytic copolymerization of carbon dioxide with alkylene oxides in the presence of one or more hydrogen-functional starter substances. The method involves providing 60-98 wt.% preferably 70-95 wt.% prepolymer. The elastic flooring is prepared by mixing the prepolymer mixture with rubber granules before the prepolymer mixture is applied to the ground. Preferred Properties: The polyethercarbonate polyol has viscosity of 900-1100 mg potassium hydroxide/g at 25 degrees C, average molecular weight of 900-1100 g/mol, hydroxyl number of 100-120 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The polypropylene glycol has viscosity of 200-500 mPa.second at 25 degrees C and average molecular weight of 1800-2200.

TI Liver-protecting oil useful for protecting liver and protecting stomach, comprises kudzu root water extract, camellia oil, soybean lecithin, sesamin, glucose and honey.
AU LIN S
   LONG T
   YU J
   XIE J
   LIN X
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201940823H
AB    NOVELTY - Liver-protecting oil comprises 50-55% kudzu root water extract, 35-43.5% camellia oil, 2-4% soybean lecithin, 1.5-3% sesamin, 2-4% glucose and 1-2% honey.
   USE - The liver-protecting oil is useful for protecting liver and protecting stomach, preventing and treating drunkenness, discharging toxins, minimizing liver damage, accelerating liver repair, and improving liver regeneration function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the liver-protecting oil, comprising mixing glucose and honey into kudzu root water extract and stirring evenly to prepare a kudzu mixture, adding sesamin and soybean phospholipid to the oil tea seed oil, stirring uniformly to prepare the oil tea seed blending oil, mixing kudzu mixture and the camellia seed blend oil, stirring, high pressure homogenizing and autoclaving; and
   (2) preparing the hangover oil emulsion for protecting the liver and protecting the stomach, comprising utilizing camellia seed oil and sesame oil are prepared by camellia oleifera seeds and sesame seeds as raw materials through supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, respectively, preparing sesame seed by molecular distillation of sesame seeds, mixing glucose, honey and kudzu root water extract to prepare a kudzu mixture, preparing oil tea seed blending oil by mixing sesamin, soybean phospholipid and camellia seed oil, mixing kudzu mixture and camellia oleifera blend oil, stirring, high pressure homogenizing and sterilizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The preparation of camellia oil comprises adding camellia oleifera seeds to the extraction vessel of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment after drying, de-sparing, shelling and crushing, adjusting extraction conditions to 40-60 degrees C, pressurizing to 33-45 MPa for 1-3 hours, entering the separation kettle to carry out the vacuum separation of carbon dioxide and oil, adjusting the process conditions of the separation kettle, maintaining temperature of the separation kettle to 30-50 degrees C, pressurizing the separation kettle to 6-15 MPa and obtaining camellia seed oil from the separation kettle, adding extracted camellia seed oil to a molecular distillation sample tank to adjust the molecular distillation process parameters, where the degree of vacuum is -0.5 to 5 Pa, controlling first distillation temperature to 100-120 degrees C, controlling second distillation temperature to120-150 degrees C, controlling third-stage distillation temperature to 150-180 degrees C, controlling scraping speed to 100-300 revolutions/minute, controlling feeding speed to 20-60 l/h, controlling condensation temperature to 30-50 degrees C, refining processing and collecting refining process. The preparation of sesamin comprises crushing sesame, adding to the extraction vessel of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment to adjust the extraction conditions, extracting at 45-55 degrees C, pressurizing to 30-40 MPa for 1.0-3.0 hours, entering the separation vessel for carbon dioxide, separating from the pressure of the oil and fat, adjusting the process conditions of the separation vessel, controlling temperature of the separation vessel to 30-45 degrees C, pressurizing separation vessel to 8-14 MPa to obtain sesame oil is from separation vessel, separating and purifying sesamin by supercritical carbon dioxide fractionation equipment, filtering sesame oil obtained by the above extraction, pumping into a separation tower, mixing carbon dioxide into the fractionation column from the bottom of the column, mixing with sesame oil, adjusting temperature in the column to 20-40 degrees C, pressurizing to 14-28 MPa, where the ratio of sesame oil to carbon dioxide is 1:14-1:22, extracting sesamin from the top of the tower, and separating into sesamin by a primary separation tank and a secondary separation tank. The preparation of kudzu root water extract comprises heating, warm immersion, ultrasonic or microwave-assisted extracting, preferably cutting the kudzu root, adding 5-10 times of water and decocting for 2-3 hours and filtering.
   ACTIVITY - Hepatotropic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PT P
PN US2019071381-A1; US10392336-B2
TI Forming alpha, beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid, comprises contacting metalalactone comprising specified group metal and ligand, diluent and polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations and applying conditions to reaction mixture.
AU IACONO P
   HLAVINKA M L
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 201921778L
AB    NOVELTY - Forming alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt, comprises (1) contacting (a) a metalalactone comprising a group 8-10 metal and at least one ligand, (b) a diluent and (c) polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations to provide a reaction mixture; and (2) applying conditions to the reaction mixture suitable to induce a metalalactone elimination reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for forming alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The molar yield of the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, or its salt based on the transition metal precursor compound is at least 100%. The contacting step is carried out in the absence of a non-nucleophilic base. The process: utilizes anionic polyelectrolytes that result in surprisingly high yields of the desired alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid; provides for various modifications of the polymeric anionic stationary phase (anionic polyelectrolytes), for example in a column or other suitable solid state configuration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt comprising contacting in any order group 8-11 transition metal precursor, olefin, carbon dioxide, diluent and a polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations to provide a reaction mixture and applying conditions to the reaction mixture suitable
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The contacting step comprises contacting the metalalactone, the diluent, and the polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations in any order. The conditions suitable to induce a metalalactone elimination reaction comprise contacting the reaction mixture with a metal-containing base. The step of contacting the group 8-11 transition metal precursor with the olefin and carbon dioxide is conducted using 10-1000 psig of ethylene partial pressure and using a constant addition of the olefin and carbon dioxide to provide the reaction mixture. The ethylene and carbon dioxide are added in a constant or a variable ethylene: carbon dioxide molar ratio of 10:1-1:10, to provide the reaction mixture. The conditions suitable to produce the acid or its salt comprise contacting the reaction mixture with a ketone, an ester, an amide, an alcohol, or water, heating the reaction mixture to a temperature 50- 1000 degrees C, conducting the contacting step at a total pressure of 5-10000 psig, conducting the contacting step at 0-250 degrees C and/or conducting the contacting step at a weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) of 0.05-50 hour-1, based on the amount of the polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations. The process comprises contacting the reaction mixture with a metal-containing base and, further comprises acidifying the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid salt to form an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid. Preferred Components: The polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations comprises a metalated phenol-formaldehyde resin, a metallated polyhydroxyarene-formaldehyde resin, or a metallated polyhydroxyarene- and fluorophenol-formaldehyde resin. The polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations comprises a sodium phenol-formaldehyde resin, a potassium phenol-formaldehyde resin, a sodium resorcinol- and 2-fluorophenol-formaldehyde resin, or a potassium resorcinol- and 2-fluorophenol-formaldehyde resin. The polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations is insoluble in the diluent or the reaction mixture. The reaction mixture comprises an adduct of the metalalactone and the polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations. The associated metal cations are group 1, 2, 12 or 13 metal. The associated metal cations are lithium, sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, zinc or aluminum. The metal-containing base is alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal oxide, hydroxide, alkoxide, aryloxide, amide, alkyl amide, arylamide, or carbonate. The metalalactone comprises nickel. The olefin comprises ethylene (preferred), propylene, butene, pentene, hexene, heptene, octene, or styrene. The diluent comprises a non-protic solvent, a weakly coordinating solvent, or a non-coordinating solvent. The diluent comprises an aromatic hydrocarbon solvent, an ether solvent, a carbonyl-containing solvent and/or halogenated aromatic hydrocarbon solvent. The anionic polyelectrolyte is polyphenoxide or a polyaromatic resin, each with their respective associated metal cations.
   EXAMPLE - 1 l Reactor was charged with solvent followed by a combined mixture of 0.10 mmol bis(cyclooctadiene)nickel(0), 0.11 mmol bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)ethane and 1 g poly(4-vinylphenoxide in 10 ml solvent. The reactor was set to 50 degrees C, pressurized with ethylene at the desired level, and equilibrated for 5-10 minutes prior to being pressurized and equilibrated with carbon dioxide. The reactor was then set to 100 degrees C and stirred for 6 hours. After this reaction time, and after cooling to ambient temperature, the reactor was slowly vented and the mixture was collected. The solvent was removed and the residue was stirred in 10-20 ml of deuterium oxide for 30 minutes prior to the addition of a sorbic acid/ acetone-d5 solution.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The surface area of                  vanadium oxide catalyst is 5-10 m2 per gram. The vanadium precursor is                  ammonium metavanadate.

PN KR2019015857-A
TI Extracting coffee beans, comprises e.g. removing various foreign substances attached to coffee beans surface, sterilizing various bacteria remain on coffee beans surface, heating water, cooling, and heating by injecting coffee beans.
AU JUN K M
AE JUN K M (JUNK-Individual)
GA 2019188549
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting coffee beans, comprises (a) removing various foreign substances attached to coffee beans surface, (b) sterilizing various bacteria remain on cleaned coffee beans surface using carbon dioxide, UV rays and/or ozone water, (c) heating water at 100oC for greater than or equal to 10 minutes to kill bacteria, and cooling to less than or equal to 50 degrees C, (d) maintaining pressure in a pressure vessel to 5-10 bar while heating at 30 to less than or equal to 60 degrees C for 3 hours by injecting 10-50 wt.% sterilized coffee beans and 50-90 wt.% sterilized water into pressure vessel of predetermined size, (e) primarily filtering seedlings eluted with natural physiologically active substances containing polyphenol with mesh net of less than or equal to 100 mesh, and collecting seeds, and (f) squeezing remaining soybean beans by a mesh screen to extract natural decoction materials containing natural bioactive substances including polyphenol, secondarily filtering through a mesh of less than or equal to 100 mesh to collect green beans water, and mixing with primary filtered green beans water.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting coffee beans.
   ADVANTAGE - The coffee beans having maximized content of polyphenol-containing natural physiologically active substances.

TI Water injection series reactor comprises e.g. a vacuum pump, first visible high pressure reactor, second visible high pressure reactor, seventh connecting valve, and a foam incubator for ensuring high temperature anti-blocking requirements.
AU GUO K
   ZHANG F
   LIN W
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201917198V
AB    NOVELTY - Water injection series reactor comprises a vacuum pump (1), a first visible high pressure reactor (13), a second visible high pressure reactor (15), a seventh connecting valve V7, a foam incubator (8) for ensuring high temperature anti-blocking requirements of the upper visible high pressure reaction vessel and the intermediate connecting valve, a regulator (10) for regulating the heating power. The first visible high pressure reaction vessel and the second visible high pressure reaction vessel can be filled with deionized water, are connected to the seventh connecting valve V7 through a connecting pipe to form a series connection. The vacuum pump is connected to the seventh connecting valve V7. The tandem reaction kettle comprises a monitoring device which facilitates experimenter to determine state change in kettle.
   USE - Used as water injection series reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor provides a process feature for the experimenter to observe the replacement of underwater natural gas hydrate with carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for experimental method for an experimental system for replacing underwater methane hydrate using the water injection series reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the water injection series reactor.
   Vacuum pump (1)
   Laser controller (8)
   Laser (10)
   Focusing lens (13)
   Second visible high pressure reactor (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), V8 or V9 is turned on to pass the gas of the methane pipeline to the second reaction vessel, and then the valve is closed, and then the second visible high pressure reactor methane gas pipeline is depressurized by opening the exhaust valve V10 and pressure in the original methane gas pipeline is 9-10 MPa, after conveying to second reactor, the pressure in the reactor was 5.5-6.5 MPa, and the pressure was reduced to 3.5-4 MPa after the exhaust. In step (b), the high-temperature low-temperature static methane gas hydrate for about 72 hours causes the hydrate to become dense and translucent. In step (d), a stable aqueous layer is formed on the hydrate layer. In step (f), the replacement is completed, adjusting the ambient climate chamber to reduce the hydrate temperature to a specified temperature of 268 K.

The electromagnetic oscillation from the oscillator is controlled by a pulse mode by a control apparatus (4).
