PT P
PN CN109170423-A
TI Preparing watermelon vinegar drink involves selecting mature fresh watermelon as raw material, cleaning it, removing peel and extracting watermelon pulp juice from juicer, and filtering the obtained juice.
AU ZOU X
   GUO P
AE UNIV DALIAN MINZU (UYND-C)
GA 201908463V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing watermelon vinegar drink involves selecting mature fresh watermelon as raw material, cleaning it, removing peel and extracting watermelon pulp juice from juicer, and filtering the obtained juice. The watermelon juice is mixed with rice vinegar in a ratio of 1:3. The contents are stirred uniformly and subjected to fermentation at 25-28 degrees C for 15 days. 10 wt.% White sugar is added to the fermented product, and after dissolving it, the obtained mixture is poured into a pot and boiled for 30 minutes. Caffeine and cola essence are further added to the obtained mixture. The obtained solution is subjected to fermentation for 2 months at 25 degrees C to obtain fermented liquid. The fermented liquid is mixed with water in a mass ratio of 2-5:1. The obtained mixture is stirred with a speed of 3500 rotation per minute for 30 minutes and then sterilized. The sterilized product is charged with carbon dioxide to obtain carbonated drink and the obtained drink is further packaged.
   USE - Method for preparing watermelon vinegar drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbonated drink in a simple and easy way, and is suitable for expansion of production. The obtained drink has hangover effect and cola flavor that makes it extremely suitable for drinking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The carbonated drink contains 1 wt.% caffeine and 0.5 wt.% cola essence. Preferred Condition: The fermented liquid and water are taken in a mass ratio of 3:1.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Device for injecting water to internal combustion engine of motor vehicle, has conveyor element arranged such that pressure is generated between shut-off element and injector to allow evaporation of water between tank and conveying element.
AU GLADEL P
   BURAK I
   SCHENK P
AE BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
   BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
GA 2018A4190Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a water tank (5) for storing water. A water injector (6) injects the water into an internal combustion engine (2). A conveying element (3) conveys the water from the water tank into the water injector. A shut-off element (11) is connected between the water tank and the water injector. The conveyor element is arranged such that system pressure is generated between the shut-off element and the water injector to maintain water level and allow evaporation of the water between the water tank and the conveying element. The shut-off element is designed as a stop valve.
   USE - Device for injecting water to an internal combustion engine (claimed), of a motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The conveyor element is arranged such that the pressure is generated between the shut-off element and the injector to allow the evaporation of the water between the tank and the conveying element, so that carbon dioxide emissions of the engine can be reduced, thus reducing fuel consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic side view of a device for injecting water to an internal combustion engine.
   Internal combustion engine (2)
   Conveying element (3)
   Water tank (5)
   Water injector (6)
   Shut-off element (11)

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of electrode from banana stem involves reducing water content of banana stems up to 10% or more, pre-carbonizing at a temperature of 200-250 degrees C for 1-2 hours, grinding the pre-carbonized product, passing through 53 mu m and 38 mu m sieves, activating the sieved particles using potassium hydroxide having a concentration of 0.1 to less than 1 M, washing the activated particles using hydrochloric acid, neutral washing with water, drying the particles, such that the water content is less than 5%, molding the particles to form a green electrode, carbonizing at a temperature of 40-800 degrees C in a nitrogen gas atmosphere, activating the carbonized electrode at a temperature of 800-950 degrees C in a carbon dioxide gas environment, polishing the electrode to a thickness of 0.2-0.9 mm, and soaking the polished electrodes in sulfuric acid electrolyte for 0.5-2 M.
   USE - Manufacture of electrode used for energy storage device. Uses include but are not limited to capacitor, supercapacitor, electrochemical capacitor, battery, fire material cell, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical manufacture of electrode. The electrode provides energy storage device having improved energy density and power density.

PN CN108724402-A
TI Wood anti-corrosion treatment process comprises peeling the wood, using sand paper, polishing, washing wood surface y de-ionized water and acetone, treating, reducing pressure, heating, and using the anti-corrosion paint.
AU SONG Y
AE ANHUI HONGRUN CRAFTS CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201887302J
AB    NOVELTY - Wood anti-corrosion treatment process comprises (i) peeling the wood, using 400-600 meshes sand paper for 20-30 minutes, using 800-1200 sand paper, polishing for 20-40 minutes, using sand paper of 1500-2000 meshes for 30-40 minutes, the wood surface is smooth and crack-free, washing wood surface for 2-3 times by de-ionized water and acetone, cold air drying, (ii) placing wood into the supercritical equipment, the pressure is 25-28 MPa, the temperature is 55-65 degrees C, the medium is carbon dioxide, treating for 5-10 minutes, reducing pressure to 12-15 MPa, heating to 55-65 degrees C, supercritical processing 5-10 minutes, releasing, and taking out, (iii) adding silane coupling agent diluted by 5-10% absolute ethyl alcohol solution by coating the wood, placing in the steam drying chamber, drying the wood 30-40 minutes taken out after coating and drying again, repeating for 2-3 times, and (iv) using the anti-corrosion paint for wood for coating, and curing can be in the ventilated place.
   USE - The method is useful for wood anti-corrosion treatment process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can increase the wear resistance of the surface of the wood and prolongs its service life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the anticorrosive paint is composed of 10-20 pts. wt. modified talc powder, 80-120 pts. wt. polyvinyl chloride resin, 3-10 pts. wt. castor oil, 3-5 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose, and 1-2 pts. wt. stearic acid. The modified talc powder preparation method is (i) adding the tal at a stirring speed of 300-500 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes, rising to 500-1000 revolutions/minute, stirring for 3-5 minutes, placing into ball mill for ball milling for 1-2 hours, placing in 100-110 degrees C vacuum drying box to bake for 1-3 hours, (ii) adding 10-20 pts. wt. chitosan after drying for 6-8 hours at 45-60 degrees C, adding 100-150 pts. wt. mass fraction is 2-5 % of acetic acid solution, 300-500 revolutions/minute stirring at normal temperature to dissolve completely, adding 3-4 pts. wt. tea polyphenol, increasing to 600-800 revolutions/minute, continuously stirring to uniform solution, (iii) adding tal, adding the solution, ultrasonically processing for 50-80 Hz at 30-40 minutes, filtering the obtained product into a vacuum drying box to dry 12-24 hours, taking out ball milling until the particle diameter is 100-500 nm. Preferred Method: The silane coupling agent is gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The polyvinyl chloride resin whose solid content is 20-40%.

   ACTIVITY - Insect Repellent.

TI Corrosion-resistant magnesium alloy comprises samarium, tin, calcium, aluminum, strontium, magnesium and unavoidable impurities containing iron, copper, nickel and cobalt.
AU CHEN J

AE UNIV HENAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTG-C)
GA 2018786306
AB    NOVELTY - Corrosion-resistant magnesium alloy comprises 0.8-1 wt.% samarium, 1-1.5 wt.% tin, 0.5-0.6 wt.% calcium, 0.3-0.7 wt.% aluminum, 0.6-0.9 wt.% strontium and remaining amount of magnesium and unavoidable impurities. The impurity comprises less than or equal to 0.004 wt.% iron, less than or equal to 0.001 wt.% copper, less than or equal to 0.002 wt.% nickel and less than or equal to 0.001 wt.% cobalt. The total content of other impurity is less than or equal to 0.02%.
   USE - Used as corrosion-resistant magnesium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The alloy has improved stability, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing corrosion-resistant magnesium alloy, comprising (a) weighing pure magnesium, pure tin, pure aluminum, pure strontium, magnesium-samarium intermediate alloy and magnesium-calcium intermediate alloy, (b) melting pure magnesium, pure aluminum and pure tin under the protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas, adding pure strontium until the melt temperature reaches to 660-670 degrees C, adding magnesium-samarium intermediate alloy and magnesium-calcium intermediate alloy, melting at 690-700 degrees C, allowing to stand for 8-10 minutes, casting when the temperature drops to 670-680 degrees C, and stress-relieving heat treating the cast alloy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The impurity comprises less than or equal to 0.0005 wt.% iron, less than or equal to 0.0001 wt.% copper, less than or equal to 0.0002 wt.% nickel and less than or equal to 0.0005 wt.% cobalt. Preferred Method: The pure magnesium, pure tin, pure aluminum, pure strontium, magnesium-samarium intermediate alloy and magnesium-calcium intermediate alloy are preheated at 200-220 degrees C for 1-2 h before use. The casting is carried out using steel mold. The stress-relieving heat treatment temperature is carried out at 150-175 degrees C for 1-2 hours.

GA 201861517L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a fuel cell                system with a first reactor (R1) for converting                fuel (B) e.g. paraffin, natural gas and alcohol,                into gaseous fuel (BG1), and a second reactor (R2)                arranged downstream from the first reactor. A fuel                cell is surrounded by a protective housing (5) to                generate electrical energy. Gas i.e. nitrogen or                carbon dioxide, or partial gas mixture is separated                from the gaseous fuel by a gas separation device                (1). The separated gas or separated partial gas                mixture is supplied into the protective housing as                inert gas. Residual gas enriched with hydrogen is                supplied as second gaseous fuel (BG2) for operating                the fuel cell, where fuel gas contains gaseous                water.

PT P
PN CN108327232-A; CN108327232-B
TI Method for forming liner of refrigerator, involves extracting organic matter in plastic sheet by fluid, vacuuming plastic sheet to isotropic mixture, moving upper convex mold upward, and molding cooling process to refrigerator liner.
AU ZHANG J
   JIANG B
   ZHU X
AE QINGDAO HAIER CO LTD (HIER-C)
GA 2018632268
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating a plastic sheet. The plastic sheet is placed between an upper convex mold and a lower concave mold for blowing treatment. The upper convex mold is moved downward. A supercritical fluid is blows the lower concave mold from a gas channel. A volatile organic matter is extracted in the plastic sheet by the supercritical fluid to form an isotropic mixture. The plastic sheet is vacuumed to discharge the isotropic mixture. The upper convex mold is moved upward to reset. A cooling process is molded to form a refrigerator liner.
   USE - Method for forming liner of refrigerator (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing supercritical carbon dioxide to extract the plastic sheet for refrigerator inner, containing volatile organic matter and forming process of the refrigerator inner, reducing content of the volatile organic matter, and removing the volatile organic matter to rate reaches 65 to 75%. The method utilizing new refrigerator, reducing content of volatile organic compounds, and generating less or even no influence to a health of a human body in long-term use process.

TI Preparation of masonry cement for construction materials, involves pulverizing hydrated lime, mixing hydrated lime with fly ash derived from exhaust gas passing through chimney by electrostatic precipitator, and adding sand to mixture.
AU CHAUDHARY S R
AE CHAUDHARY S R (CHAU-Individual)
   CHAUDHARY S R (CHAU-Individual)
GA 2018555915
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of masonry cement involves adding quick lime in water in a tank to convert quick lime to 30% hydrated lime, pulverizing hydrated lime, mixing hydrated lime with fly ash in a mechanical mixer for 15-20 minutes, and adding sand to the mixture. The weight ratio of hydrated lime and fly ash is 45:55 for LP20 grade mortar and 20:80 for LP7 grade mortar. The source of the fly ash is an exhaust gas passing through a chimney by electro static precipitators.
   USE - Preparation of masonry cement for use as construction materials e.g. brick, cinder block, and concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides masonry cement without efflorescence, good board life, excellent workability, acceptable color, durability, high performance, improved smoothness, water resistance and less cracks (due to negligible shrinkage). The process adopts energy efficient process, maximizes the use of local resources and skills, generating opportunity for local employment, produces construction material of desired quality and minimizes the carbon dioxide impact on the environment. The cement absorbs micro movement of building elements, and gains strength with increase in age by carbonation process. The process does not require heavy or sophisticated machineries. The process uses industrial waste as raw materials, and reduces environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lime is selected from Class A or Class B or Class C lime of BIS code IS:712-1984. The fly ash includes silica.

   LI Y
   ZHANG S
   GAO S

AE SUNSTONE DEV CO LTD (SUNS-Non-standard)
GA 201851406P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pre-baked anode comprises (i)                sieving and classifying the calcined petroleum coke                raw material to obtain granular material and a                powder material, where powder material is a fine                powder having a particle diameter of less than or                equal to 0.15 mm; and the coal pitch is weighed,                taking 0.2-0.7 wt.% of additive based on the total                weight of the particulate material and the powder                and the coal pitch, taking 2-6 wt.% powder (a)                based on the weight of the coal tar pitch; (ii)                mixing the coal tar pitch, the additive and the                powder a, preheating, adding pellet and the                remaining powder, mixing, kneading evenly, heating                for specific time to obtain a mixed paste and                loading mixed paste into the mold and hot pressing                to obtain a prebaked anode green block; and (iv)                placing prebaked anode green block in a roasting                furnace for calcining and cooling naturally.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pre-baked                anode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves energy, improves the                wettability, fluidity , permeability of the coal                tar pitch through specific additives, carbon                dioxide and air reactivity and reduces carbon                consumption and produces prebaked anode with lower                resistivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                pre-baked anode, prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The calcined petroleum                  coke granules and powders comprises a micro                  particulate material at size less than 0.15 mm                  and particle size is less than or equal to 1 mm,                  a fine granule of less than 1 mm and particle                  size is less than or equal to 3 mm, medium                  particle material less than 3 mm with particle                  size less than or equal to 6mm and coarse                  particle material less than 6mm and particle size                  is less than or equal to 14 mm. The additive                  comprises 12C-22C long-chain fatty acids,                  preferably palmitic acid. Preferred Composition:                  The weight ratio of the particulate material and                  powder is (65-95) wt.% and (5-35) wt.%                  respectively. The weight ratio of the particulate                  material and powder and coal pitch is                  (78-90):(10-22). The amount of additive is (0.2-                  0.7) wt.% of particulate material and the powder                  and the coal pitch. Preferred Method: The                  calcining is carried out form room temperature to                  250 degrees C for 2-3 hours, heating at 250-650                  degrees C for 6-7 h, 650-1000 degrees C for 5-6                  hours and 1000 degrees C for 3-4 hours.

PN CN108172799-A
TI Preparing ternary anode material of lithium ion battery with core-shell structure comprises adding mixture of nickel-, cobalt-, manganese- and aluminum-salt into ammonia and sodium hydroxide, precipitating and forming alumina coating.
AU TANG Y
   WU L
   SHANG G
   GUO Q
AE QINGYUAN JIAZHI NEW MATERIALS RES INST (QING-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG JIANA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018491823
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ternary anode material of lithium ion battery with core-shell structure comprises (a) preparing a multi-component mixed solution of nickel salt, cobalt salt, manganese salt and polybasic aluminum salt, (b) adding the multi-component mixed solution to the reaction device containing a bottom liquid along with aqueous ammonia and sodium hydroxide to adjust the flow rate, pH, ammonium concentration, rotation speed and temperature for coprecipitation reaction to obtain nickel cobalt manganese (NCM) hydroxide precursor, (c) stopping the addition of sodium hydroxide, aqueous ammonia water and polyol solution, infusing carbon dioxide gas so that the NCM precursor surface form a layer of aluminum hydroxide coating, and (d) filtering the precursor, washing and drying, adding lithium source to carry out heat treatment to obtain NCM material having a surface coated with alumina.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ternary anode material of lithium ion battery with core-shell structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures that the prepared precursor has uniform pore distribution in the core-shell structure with proper spacing and large specific surface area; and produces anode material, which exhibits high charge and discharge specific capacity, long circulation stability performance and good multiplying power performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel salt is nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, nickel acetate or nickel sulfate. The manganese salt is manganese nitrate, manganese chloride, manganese acetate, manganese sulfate. The cobalt salt is cobalt nitrate, cobalt chloride, cobalt acetate or cobalt sulfate. The aluminum salt is aluminum sulfate, aluminum chloride, aluminum nitrate or aluminum acetate. The mol ratio of nickel, cobalt and manganese element in the multi-component mixed solution is 8:1:1. In the step (a): the mol ratio of aluminum to cobalt or manganese is 1:20-3:10. In the step (b): the feeding rate of the multi-component mixed solution is 90-150 ml/minute, the sodium hydroxide feed rate is 30-70 ml/minute and aqueous ammonia water feed rate is 5-40 ml/minute. In the step (a): the total ion concentration of multi-component mixed solution is 1-1.5 mol/l; the concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 7.5-10 mol/l; and the concentration of aqueous ammonia is 6-8 mol/l. The material is a core-shell structure with NCM ternary material as core and alumina as shell layer, where ternary precursors and anode materials are used in lithium ion batteries. The D50 particle size of the core is 8-12 mu m, and the thickness of the alumina coating layer is 50-500 nm. Preferred Method: The co-precipitation reaction of the step (b) is carried out at a pH of 10-12, where the concentration of ammonium ion is 5-9 g/l, the reaction temperature is 50-60 degrees C and the stirring speed is 200-400 revolutions/minute. The step (d) comprises adding lithium source for heat treatment, where the ratio of lithium salt and ternary precursor is 1.05:1, mixing under oxygen atmosphere at 300-500 degrees C, sintering for 2-4 hours, heating to 700-1000 degrees C for 10-16 hours.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded.    VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
GA 201846793L

TI Preparing filter material for water treatment, involves using pond sludge, dehydrating and drying pond sludge to reduce water content to obtain dehydration, after dewatered pond sludge is crushed, sieved, and used.
AU GAO Y
   LIU R
   YIN K
AE ZHOU R (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 201848263K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing filter material involves using pond sludge, followed by dehydrating and drying pond sludge to reduce water content to 30-40 wt.%, to obtain dehydrated pond sludge, crushing and sieving to obtain the filter material for further use, mixing water, holly leaves, and rhododendron leaf in mass ratio of 5-6:1-2:1-2, mixing, and soaking for 10-12 hours to obtain mixture, extracting the mixture by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to obtain an extract, crushing the slag to obtain the powder, mixing powder and surfactant in mass ratio of 2-3:0.8-1, balling mixture to obtain ball-milled product, soaking ball-milled product in mass ratio of 1:5-6 for 10-12 hours and filtering to obtain filter residue.
   USE - Method for preparing filter material for water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare filter material which has regeneration efficiency, ability of removing heavy metal is high, safe and high-efficient water treatment filter, and it is worth to popularize and use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The filter material and filter residue are mixed with stirring for 15-20 minutes. The stirred mixture is placed in a rotary granulator for granulation for 20-30 minutes to obtain granules which are dried at 105-110 degrees C and then sintered to obtain desired filter material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction is performed at a pressure of 15-20 mega Pascal and a temperature of 40-60 degrees C. Preferred Component: The surfactant is sodium lauryl sulfate or polyethylene glycol.

GA 201845260E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves directing an acoustically agitated fluid stream (120) at a first surface of a substrate to cause the substrate to vibrate mechanically thereby dislodging contaminant particles on the substrate. The first surface of the substrate is opposite a second surface of the substrate. A second surface of the substrate includes a pattern. Amplitude of the acoustically agitated fluid stream is configured to produce an acoustic response along an entirety of the second surface. The acoustically agitated fluid stream includes of ozone water, tetramethylammonium hydroxide (TMAH), carbon dioxide (CO2 ) dissolved in water, hydrogen (H2 ) dissolved in water and standard clean 1 (SC1).
   USE - Method of extreme (EUV) mask cleaning.
   ADVANTAGE - The lack of photomask flipping prevents and limits previously removed contaminants from reattaching the photomask during the cleaning process. The low thermal expansion material (LTEM) substrate serves to minimize image distortion due to mask heating.

   ADVANTAGE - The concrete: has water resistant, leakage resistance and excellent quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing crack-resistant high-strength concrete comprising (i) mixing calcium oxide and water into mixing tank in a mass ratio of 1:3-4 for 15-20 minutes to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, (ii) adding modified treatment liquid with a total mass of 4-6% the calcium hydroxide slurry obtained in step (i) and stirring for 30-35 minutes to obtain mixed slurry, and (iii) passing excess carbon dioxide gas into mixed slurry obtained in step (ii), filtering and then drying filtered slag to obtain modified calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The crack-resistant high-strength concrete comprises 95-100 pts. wt. cement, 43-45 pts. wt. sand, 18-20 pts. wt. stone, 10-12 pts. wt. coal gangue, 5-6 pts. wt. coconut shell cellulose, 5-6 pts. wt. rubber particle, 23-25 pts. wt. modified calcium carbonate, 2-3 pts. wt. urea, 3-4 pts. wt. calcium sulfate, 1.5-2 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 1.2-1.5 pts. wt. zinc chloride, 3-4 pts. wt. potassium nitrate, 0.8-1 pts. wt. sodium lignosulfonate and 23-25 pts. wt. water. Preferred Components: The particle size of sand is 0.2-0.5 cm. The particle size of stone is 1-2.5 cm. The particle size of coal gangue is 1-2 cm. Preferred Method: In step (ii), modified treatment liquid is prepared by mixing methyl myristate and glycerol into the reaction kettle in a molar ratio of 2.5-2.7:1, adding 2-2.4% sodium hydroxide, stirring for 20-25 minutes, adding isomeric tridecyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether having a total mass of 16-20% the methyl myristate into the reactor, adding 6-9 wt.% polyvinyl alcohol, 2-4 wt.% sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, 0.3-0.6 wt.% dimethyl sulfate, 3-6 wt.% nano titanium dioxide, 4-7 wt.% carbon nanotubes into the reactor at 65-70 degrees C, 0.3-0.4 MPa and then stirring for 15-20 minutes. In step (iii), drying temperature is 80-85 degrees C.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) dissolving trivalent indium,                  metal M salt solution and terephthalic acid in                  N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring by ultrasonic                  vibration, adding sodium acetate into the                  mixture, performing hydrothermal reaction at                  90-150 degrees C (constant temperature) for 0.5-8                  hours, washing and drying to obtain indium-based                  heteronuclear MIL-68, and (ii) heating the above                  obtained product at 500-800 degrees C at a                  heating rate of 2-10 degrees C/minute for 0.5-2                  hours to obtain final product. Preferred Method:                  In step (i), hydrothermal reaction temperature is                  100 degrees C and hydrothermal reaction time is 1                  hour. In step (i): the sodium acetate is added in                  the form of an aqueous solution, and the                  concentration of the aqueous sodium acetate                  solution is preferably 0.01-10 mol/l. In step                  (ii), the calcination temperature is 500-800                  degrees C, preferably 500 degrees C, the                  calcination time is 0.5-2 hours, preferably 1                  hour, and the temperature increase rate is                  preferably 5 degrees C/minute. Preferred                  Components: In step (i), the concentration of                  sodium acetate in the reaction system is 0.5-25                  mmol/l, preferably 20 mmol/l. In step (i): the                  molar ratio between trivalent indium and metal M                  salt is 20:1-5:1, preferably 10:1; the total                  molar ratio between terephthalic acid and metal M                  salt is preferably 1:1, and the terephthalic acid                  concentration is 0.1-0.01 mol/l, preferably 0.1                  mol/l.

PN CN108905654-A; CN108905654-B
TI Polyimide film useful as separation membrane in fields of biogas purification and decarburization.
AU WANG Q
   DING B
   ZHANG P
   DENG G
   ZANG L
AE UNIV LIAONING SHIHUA (ULNS-C)
GA 201898568H
AB    NOVELTY - Polyimide film is claimed. The polyimide film is obtained by hybridizing polyimide film with different side chains and different organometallic skeleton compounds, where the polyimide film has a microporous structure, the separation pressure is 0.1 Mpa, the temperature is room temperature, the polyimide film has a carbon dioxide and methane permeability coefficient 80-85 times that of a conventional polyimide film, and the separation factor for carbon dioxide/methane is 23-30.
   USE - The polyimide film is useful as separation membrane in fields of biogas purification and decarburization (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding 80% concentrated sulfuric acid into the pyromellitic dianhydride at 78-82 degrees C, stirring while adding dropwise, and stirring the reaction for 20-22 hours to obtain sulfonated product of pyromellitic dianhydride (PMDA), where the molar ratio between concentrated sulfuric acid and pyromellitic dianhydride is 3:2; and introducing a nitro group into the pyromellitic dianhydride at 30-50 degrees C in the presence of concentrated sulfuric acid and concentrated nitric acid to obtain nitro product of pyromellitic dianhydride, the molar ratio between concentrated sulfuric acid, concentrated nitric acid and pyromellitic anhydride is 3:3:2, (ii) drying sulfonated product of pyromellitic dianhydride and nitro product of pyromellitic dianhydride respectively under vacuum at 130 degrees C for 6 hours, adding 0.02 mol sulfo/nitro pyromellitic dianhydride and 4,4-diaminodiphenyl ether (ODA) into a 250 ml round bottom flask containing 80 ml N,N-dimethylacetamide (DMAC) at room temperature and sealing under the condition of nitrogen atmosphere, subjecting the mixture to magnetic stirring for 6 hours to obtain side chain sulfo/nitro polyamic acid, dissolving zirconium(IV) chloride and terephthalic acid in N,N-dimethylformamide, respectively, reacting the solution at a constant temperature of 120 degrees C for 24 hours, filtering to room temperature and cooling, washing the filter cake with methanol and drying at 80 degrees C for 12-14 hours to obtain uio-66 (Zr) powder i.e. uio-66 (Zr) organometallic skeleton compound, dissolving 2.4 g 2-methylimidazole and 1 g zinc nitrate (hexahydrate) in 20 ml ethanol, respectively, mixing the two solutions and stirring at room temperature for 2 hours to obtain ZIF-8 (Zn) particles, removing ethanol, and drying at 60 degrees C to obtain ZIF-8 (Zn) powder i.e. ZIF-8 (Zn) organometallic skeleton compound, and (iv) ultrasonic dissolving uio-66(Zr)/ZIF-8(Zn) powder in N,N-dimethylacetamide (DMAC), then adding uio-66 (Zr) or ZIF-8 (Zn) N,N-dimethylacetamide (DMAC) into the product side chain sulfo/nitro polyamic acid obtained in step (ii), and stirring the solution on a magnetic stirrer for 48 hours to uniformly mixing to obtain casting solution, filtering through a 200 mesh copper wire filter, then using a SZQ type four-sided wet film preparation machine, spring different ratios of the casting solution on a clean glass plate to form 200 mu m film, and placing in an electric furnace for thermal imidization reaction, carrying out heat treatment at 50 degrees C for 4 hours, and then carrying out heat treatment at 100-300 degrees C for 1 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, taking out and carefully peeling off in water at 80 degrees C, and naturally drying to obtain side chain type polyimide-uio-66 (Zr)/ZIF-8 (Zn) hybrid film material i.e. polyimide film.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbamic acid ester involves reacting an amine, carbon dioxide, and an alkoxysilane compound in presence of the catalyst containing a zinc compound.
   USE - Manufacture of carbamic acid ester for forming isocyanate used in preparation of polyurethane for manufacturing lifestyle/construction materials, motor vehicle component, coating material, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical production of carbamic acid ester with high yield and selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The zinc compound is zinc oxide, zinc halide, zinc trifluoromethanesulfonate, and zinc acetate, preferably zinc acetate. The alkali metal compound is a potassium compound, a rubidium compound, and/or a cesium compound, preferably rubidium acetate, cesium acetate, rubidium carbonate, cesium carbonate, potassium acetate, potassium carbonate, potassium hydrogen carbonate, potassium hydroxide, and potassium t-butoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The process is carried out at a pressure of 3-10 MPa in presence of an ionic liquid chosen from acetate ion, trifluoroacetic acid ion, and 2,2,2-trifluoroethanol ion, preferably a compound chosen from amine-based compounds of formulae (1)-(5), or at a pressure of 3-5 MPa using an alkali metal compound-containing catalyst. The temperature maintained during reaction between the compounds is 150-180 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The catalyst further contains a ligand chosen from 1,10-phenanthroline, 2,2'-bipyridine, N,N'-bis(2-pyridylmethyl)ethylenediamine, and 1,4,8,11-tetraazacyclotetradecane. The ionic liquid contains a cation chosen from compounds (1) and (2), and anion such as an acetate ion. The alkoxysilane compound is tetramethoxysilane.

PT P
PN CN113533241-A
TI Atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration high precision inversion method based on satellite infrared hyper-spectroscopy, involves pre-processing spectrum data to obtain correction spectrum data, and using carbon dioxide and oxygen to dry air mixing ratio of carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG C
   HONG X
   LIU C
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2021C7256X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pre-processing spectrum data to obtain correction spectrum data applied to actual inversion. A prior gas reference spectrum database is obtained based on real-time updating simulation of a vertical profile library as a prior gas information. Data of carbon dioxide and oxygen is selected from a spectrum wave band from the corrected spectrum data. A non-linear iterative fitting mode is used to obtain inclined column concentration SCDCO2 and SCDO2 corresponding to the carbon dioxide and oxygen. The the inclined column concentrations SCDCO2 and SCDO2 corresponding to carbon dioxide and oxygen is utilized to dry air mixing ratio of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration high precision inversion method based on satellite infrared hyper-spectral for use in greenhouse gas remote sensing monitoring field for global warming.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the prior gas reference spectrum as the constraint; enables performing nonlinear iterative fitting on the corrected high-quality satellite infrared hyper-spectral and finally obtaining the inversion result of the atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration of the high precision, thus realizing the target of the carbon dioxide monitoring service.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) high-precision retrieval system of atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration based on satellite infrared hyperspectroscopy;
   (2) processing device; and
   (3) readable storage medium comprising a set of instructions for atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration high precision inversion based on satellite infrared hyper-spectroscopy

AE FROSTIME LLC (FROS-Non-standard)

GA 201870892T
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressed liquid and/or gas                carbon dioxide container in an upright position                including a carbon dioxide refrigerant retained                within an interior chamber surrounded by a                circumferential sidewall and a top of each of the                carbon dioxide containers. A siphon tube reaches a                bottom of the carbon dioxide container in the                upright position. The siphon tube allows liquid                carbon dioxide to flow from the bottom to the top                of each carbon dioxide container and exit through a                control or release valve, where the container is                selected from an insulated vessel and a non                insulated vessel. The carbon dioxide container                comprises a mechanism to transmit the liquid and or                carbon dioxide gas at an inside of the vessel and                an outside of the vessel, where a connection                delivers carbon dioxide as coolant to the vessel                when a power supply outage occurs through a                capillary.
   USE - Standalone and self-contained cooling system                for providing steady and constant flow of liquid                and/or carbon dioxide gas to insulated or                non-insulated portable units e.g. ice chests,                coolers, and lunch boxes, stationary units e.g.                refrigerators and freezers, and compartments of                vehicles e.g. trunks, car or autonomous vehicles,                aircrafts, small unmanned aerial vehicles,                motorcycles, scooters and bicycles.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is envisioned to be specifically                designed to be connected and attached to a                refrigerator system to minimize or eliminate an                amount of heat transfer from a refrigerator to an                external environment.

AB    NOVELTY - Integrated-type carbon dioxide generator (100) comprises: an upper container (100a) accommodating an acid or aqueous solution and including an outlet pipe through which carbon dioxide gas is discharged; a lower container (100b) located under the upper container and accommodating a base containing a salt of carbonic acid or a aqueous solution; a dropping hole area formed in a bottom surface of the upper container so that the acid or aqueous solution is supplied from the upper container to the lower container by gravity; a speed control valve that is externally manipulable and configured to open and close the dropping hole area; and a pressure equilibrium and gas passage pipe configured to form a path through which carbon dioxide generated in the lower container is transferred to the upper container.
   USE - The integrated-type carbon dioxide generator is useful for producing a carbon dioxide using neutralization reaction between an acid and a base containing a salt of carbonic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The integrated-type carbon dioxide generator is capable of controlling production rates of carbon dioxide or stopping generation of carbon dioxide, prevents an excessive increase in pressure, is easy to use and repeatedly used by refilling raw materials.

A sealing material preventing leakage of a gas is interposed between the body and the upper portion of the upper container. PN CN113011642-A
TI Method for predicting total amount of carbon dioxide in city, involves adding up total carbon dioxide emissions of seven types of cities predicted in certain year in future to obtain carbon dioxide emissions of cities in region in future.
AU CAO L
   CAI B
   ZHANG Z
AE MIN ECOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENT ENVIRONMENTAL (ECOL-Non-standard)
GA 202174348L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves classifying the cities in the region according to the geographical location of a certain region and the industrial structure of a certain year, and set their own benchmark values for each department in a certain type of city, and combined it with the predicted total population in a certain year in the future or the actual unit industrial added value in a certain year. The total carbon dioxide emissions of a certain type of city predicted in a certain year in the future are obtained. The set own benchmark values are repeated to obtain the total carbon dioxide emissions of each type of city predicted in a certain year in the future. The total carbon dioxide emissions of the seven types of cities predicted in a certain year in the future are added up to obtain the carbon dioxide emissions of all cities in the region in the future. The forecast of carbon dioxide emissions is completed for all cities in a certain year in the future.
   USE - Method for predicting total amount of carbon dioxide in city.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can accurately predict the city's carbon dioxide emissions in the certain year in the future, and modify the prediction results, and at the same time can better control and allocate the city's carbon dioxide emissions, thus, greatly improving work efficiency and data accuracy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart illustrating the method for predicting total amount of carbon dioxide in city. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

AB    NOVELTY - Generating pressure inside an aerosol package                involves using an adsorbent material and an inert                permanent gas. The adsorbent matrix generates a final                pressure ratio pressure, and the final pressure                ratio is greater than about 2.
   USE - Method for generating pressure inside an                aerosol package used in applications such as home                care, personal care, medical, food, and vehicle                care applications (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method employs an adsorbent material that                can adsorb inert permanent gas to form an adsorbent                matrix having an uptake constant K greater than                about 0.015 g/g.psig at about 100 psig. The                adsorbent matrix generates a pressure inside the                aerosol package, and the pressure remains constant                throughout the useful life of the aerosol                package.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                aerosol package, which comprises a bag, a can, and                a valve, where the volume between the bag filled                with product and the can is a propellant volume,                and the propellant volume comprises metal organic                framework (MOF) and carbon dioxide, where the                carbon dioxide is partly adsorbed in the MOF,                forming an adsorption matrix with uptake constant K                greater than about 0.015 g/g.psig at about 100                psig, and the remainder of the carbon dioxide is in                equilibrium with the adsorbed carbon dioxide at a                pressure, where the pressure remains constant                throughout the useful life of the aerosol                package.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent material                  is selected from the group comprising metal                  organic framework (MOF), covalent organic                  framework (COF), zeolitic imidazolate framework                  (ZIF), or mixtures. The MOF is MIL-101 or                  MOF-177. The inert permanent gas is selected from                  the group comprising nitrogen, carbon dioxide,                  helium, argon, and mixtures, preferably, carbon                  dioxide. The adsorbent material comprises an MOF.                  The inert permanent gas comprises carbon dioxide.                  The aerosol package is selected from the group                  comprising a bag-in-bottle package and a                  bag-on-valve package. Preferred Conditions: The                  aerosol package is reused by refilling it with                  product after the end of its useful current                  life.

PT P
PN US2021388764-A1
TI Method for power generation, involves combusting a fuel in a combustor with an oxidant in the presence of a compressed stream of carbon dioxide that forming a compressed combustion product stream.
AU FORREST B A
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 2021E34671
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves combusting a fuel in a combustor (110) with an oxidant (102) in the presence of a compressed stream of carbon dioxide to form a compressed combustion product stream. The compressed stream is expanded across a turbine (120) to generate power and to provide an expanded product stream that is passed through a primary heat exchanger (130) to recuperate an available quantity of heat and form a cooled turbine exhaust stream. Water is removed from the exhaust stream to provide a stream of the carbon dioxide. The stream is compressed to form the compressed stream, which is recycled back to the combustor.
   USE - Method for power generation with combustion of carbonaceous fuel as well as input of heat from low-grade heat source, such as solar heat source.
   ADVANTAGE - System allows a variety of low-grade heat sources, such as solar energy, to be effectively integrated with a supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) power cycle by interfacing the low- grade heating directly with a recuperative heat exchanger train instead of utilizing terminal heat transfer e.g., heating to the highest desired temperature in the cycle. The low-pressure fluid in the CO2 power cycle is directly heated while still providing high temperature heat recovery in the recycle CO2 but without direct communication with the low grade heat source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for power generation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of a method for power generation.
   Oxidant (102)
   Fuel source (104)
   Combustor (110)
   Turbine (120)
   Primary heat exchanger (130)

PT P
PN WO2021133744-A1; CA3151950-A1; EP4004138-A1
TI Pre-filled injector used for treating e.g., migraine comprises formulation comprising dihydroergotamine mesylate and carbon dioxide at concentration sufficient to retard oxidative degradation of dihydroergotamine mesylate.
AU HARIHARAN S
   KOLLA B T
   SANGHVI S
   SURANA R
AE SCIENTURE INC (SCIE-Non-standard)
   SCIENTURE INC (SCIE-Non-standard)
GA 202174183G
AB    NOVELTY - A pre-filled injector comprises a pharmaceutically acceptable formulation comprising dihydroergotamine mesylate at a concentration of 3-6 mg/mL; and carbon dioxide at a concentration sufficient to retard oxidative degradation of the dihydroergotamine mesylate.
   USE - A pre-filled injector used for treating a migraine or a cluster headache attack in a patient (all claimed). The pre-filled injector is fixed dose pen injector or needle-less pen injector.
   ADVANTAGE - The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation in the pre-filled injector is stable and ready-to-use. The treatment reduces the severity, duration, or occurrence of headaches or migraines experienced by the patient. The injectors provide an indication to the user to confirm that a particular dose has been delivered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) pharmaceutically acceptable formulation comprising 3-6 mg/mL dihydroergotamine mesylate; 3-10 mg/mL pharmaceutically acceptable alcohol; 5-15 mg/mL caffeine; 1-3 mg/mL isomer of cresol selected from o-cresol, m-cresol, and/or p-cresol; carbon dioxide at a concentration sufficient to retard oxidative degradation of the dihydroergotamine mesylate; and an osmotic agent selected from dextrose, glycerin, mannitol, and/or sucrose;
   (2) treating a migraine or a cluster headache attack in a patient, which involves parenterally administering to the patient a therapeutically effective amount of pharmaceutically acceptable formulation; and
   (3) manufacturing a pre-filled injector which involves preparing a pharmaceutically acceptable formulation; loading a sterile cartridge with pharmaceutically acceptable formulation under a headspace of carbon dioxide at a concentration sufficient to retard oxidation of dihydroergotamine mesylate; and attaching the sterile cartridge operably to an injector.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation further comprises caffeine. Concentration of caffeine is 5-15 mg/mL. The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation further comprises an osmotic agent selected from dextrose, glycerin, mannitol, and/or sucrose. The osmotic agent is dextrose at a concentration of 1-20 mg/mL. The osmotic agent is glycerin at a concentration of 1-20 mg/mL. The osmotic agent is mannitol at a concentration of 20-50 mg/mL. The osmotic agent is sucrose at a concentration of 2-20 mg/mL. The volume of pharmaceutically acceptable formulation to be dispensed from the pre-filled injector is adjustable to 0.1-0.3 mL per injection. The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation further comprises an antioxidant. The antioxidant is selected from methionine, monothioglycerol, sodium bisulfite, sodium metabi sulfite, sodium thiosulfate, sodium citrate, and/or thiourea. The antioxidant is methionine at a concentration of 1.5-5 mg/mL. The antioxidant is methionine at a concentration of 1.5 mg/mL. The antioxidant is monothioglycerol at a concentration of 2-3 mg/mL, preferably 2 mg/mL. The antioxidant is sodium metabisulfite at a concentration of 0.2-4 mg/mL. The antioxidant is sodium citrate at a concentration of 0.1-4 mg/mL. The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation is contained within 3 mL sterilized cartridge. The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation further comprises a preservative which is an isomer of cresol selected from o-cresol, m-cresol, and/or p-cresol. The preservative is o-cresol, or m-cresol in concentration of 1-5 mg/mL, preferably 1.5 mg/mL. The preservative is p-cresol. The preservative is benzyl alcohol at a concentration of 5-100 mg/mL, preferably 10 mg/mL. The preservative is a composition of greater than or equal to 1 parabens comprising methylparaben and/or propylparaben. The pharmaceutically acceptable alcohol is selected from propylene glycol, ethanol, and/or pharmaceutically acceptable polyethylene glycol, preferably ethanol. The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation further comprises a cyclodextrin selected from 2-hydroxypropyl- beta -cyclodextrin, O-methyl- beta -cyclodextrin, and/or gamma -cyclodextrin. Preferred Parameters: The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation has a pH of 2.5-4.5. Preferred Process: The pharmaceutically acceptable formulation is administered to the patient from an injector pre-loaded with the pharmaceutically acceptable formulation.
   ACTIVITY - Antimigraine; Analgesic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Dosage of pharmaceutically acceptable formulation is 0.05-0.5 ml, preferably 0.15-0.25 ml by parenteral, subcutaneous, intramuscular or intravenous route.

PN US2021189745-A1; WO2021133587-A1; US11236517-B2
TI Print head for extruding cementitious composition, has feed barrel oriented and shaped to receive cementitious composition, and print head controller coupled to valve assembly and programmed to execute carbon dioxide and injection protocol.
AU FADHEL B A
   AL-HUNAIDY A S
   BAMAGAIN R
   AMR I T
   JAMAL A
   AL-KHOWAITER A O
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202170138G
AB    NOVELTY - The head (200) has a feed barrel (202) oriented and shaped to receive a cementitious composition (300) and to direct the cementitious composition to a print head nozzle (204). A print head controller is operatively coupled to the selective valve assembly and is programmed to execute a carbon dioxide and steam injection protocol where steam is exclusively selected for injection by an extrusion head injector (212) into a cementitious composition extruded from the print head nozzle to enhance a hydration reaction and formation of hydroxide in the cementitious composition before carbon dioxide is exclusively selected for injection by the extrusion head injectors into the cementitious composition extruded from the print head nozzle to enhance a carbonation reaction in the cementitious composition.
   USE - Print head for extruding cementitious composition of 3D printing architecture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The extrusion head injector cover is placed around the dual use extrusion head injector helps to minimize the loss of injected steam and carbon dioxide to the atmosphere. The print head enhances a hydration reaction and the formation of hydroxide in the cementitious composition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1) a three-dimensional (3D) printing architecture; and
   2) a method for extruding cementitious composition from print head.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the print head.
   Print head (200)
   Feed barrel (202)
   Print head nozzle (204)
   Extrusion head injector (212)
   Cementitious composition (300)

PT P
PN CN112979984-A
TI Ion type metal-organic frameworks material prepared by e.g. taking water as solvent in container, adding chromium nitrate nonahydrate, 2-amino-terephthalic acid, sodium hydroxide, stirring, sealing container, reacting and slowly cooling.
AU HAN Z
   LIU S
   GAO M
   LIU L
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 2021718875
AB    NOVELTY - Ion type metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) material prepared by (i) taking water as solvent in a container, adding chromium nitrate nonahydrate, 2-amino-terephthalic acid, sodium hydroxide, stirring at room temperature, sealing the container and adding into the oven, reacting at high temperature, slowly cooling to room temperature, the obtained product (NH2-MIL-101) orderly using water, N, N-dimethyl formamide, ethanol washing, vacuum drying, activating at 120 degrees C, and (ii) adding NH2-MIL-101 obtained in the step (i) in a container, using acetonitrile as solvent, adding ethanolamine, formaldehyde, hydrobromic acid, stirring for 30 minutes at 0 degrees C, adding glyoxal, stirring at room temperature, sealing the container and adding into the oven, reacting at high temperature, slowly cooling to room temperature to obtain the green crystal, orderly using acetonitrile, ethanol to wash, and drying to obtain the target product (IM-101), is claimed.
   USE - The ionic MOFs material is useful as a catalyst in the high-efficiency catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonates under mild conditions without solvents and promoters.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic MOFs efficiently catalyzes and convert carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonate without solvent and auxiliary catalyst under mild condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of catalyst in the high-efficiency catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonates under mild conditions without solvents and promoters comprising adding the ionic MOFs materials the container containing the epoxy compound, passing in carbon dioxide, stirring and heating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), mole ratio, chromium nitrate nonahydrate, 2-aminoterephthalic acid and sodium hydroxide is 2:2:5. The temperature of the high temperature reaction is 423K and kept for 12 hours. The container is sealed and placed in an oven at a heating rate of 2-8 degrees C/minutes. The cooling rate of slowly cooling to room temperature is 2-8 degrees C/minutes, and it is allowed to stand at room temperature for at least 24 hours. In the step (ii), mole ratio, NH2-MIL-101, ethanolamine, formaldehyde, hydrobromic acid and glyoxal is 2:2:2:3:2. The high temperature reaction is maintained at 373K for 24 hours. Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is 1,2-propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, cyclohexene oxide, and/or oxide styrene.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN KR2019004493-A; KR2003469-B1
TI Fungi monitoring system has measuring unit for measuring relative humidity and carbon dioxide concentration of reference region and control unit which is arranged for determining possibility of fungi in target area based on detected result.
AU CHUN K D
   KIM D C
AE FIELDSOLUTION CO LTD (FIEL-Non-standard)
GA 201907407R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first measuring unit (10) for measuring a relative humidity and a carbon dioxide concentration of the reference region. A second measuring unit (20) is arranged for measuring the relative humidity and the carbon dioxide concentration of the target area. An appropriate value of a predetermined environmental variable is given according to the difference between the relative humidity and the carbon dioxide concentration measured by the first measuring unit and the second measuring unit. A control unit (30) is arranged for determining the possibility of fungi in the target area based on the detected result.
   USE - Fungi monitoring system.
   ADVANTAGE - The possibility of fungi in the target area is determined, if the appropriate value of the constant variable is given according to the relative humidity of the reference region and the target region, the CO2 concentration, and the temperature, and the sum value of the respective optimum values exceeds the threshold value. The occurrence of fungi in the target area is prevented and avoided by informing the user of the possibility of occurrence of fungi in the target area without directly detecting the fungi through sample collection and cultivation of the target area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fungi monitoring method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the structure of fungi monitoring system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   First measuring unit (10)
   Second measuring unit (20)
   Control unit (30)
   Alarm unit (40)

TI Perovskite oxide-based composition useful for            two-step thermochemical cycle decomposition of water            and carbon dioxide and in solar photothermal chemical            conversion applications, comprises gallium-based            perovskite oxide.
AU LI C
   CHEN Z
   JIANG Z
   JIANG Q
   YANG M
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2018804316
AB    NOVELTY - A perovskite oxide-based composition comprises                gallium-based perovskite oxide.
   USE - Perovskite oxide-based composition is useful                for two-step thermochemical cycle decomposition of                water and carbon dioxide (claimed) and in solar                photothermal chemical conversion                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The perovskite oxide-based composition is                environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A perovskite oxide-based composition comprises                gallium-based perovskite oxide of formula:                AGa1-xB1xO3-R.
   R, x = 0-1;
   A = rare-earth ion or alkaline earth ion such                as La, Nd, Sm, Gd, Dy, Y, Ce, Sr, Ca, Ba or                Bi;and
   B1 = transition metal ion or alkali metal ion                such as Fe, Mn, Cu, Co, Ni, Al, Cr, Sc or Mg.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of perovskite oxide-based                composition, which involves weighing combustion                improver including glycine, citric acid and urea,                metal ion and deionized water and then baking,                dissolving in deionized water, adding 1-3 mol/l                sodium hydroxide solution, potassium hydroxide                solution or aqueous ammonia additive including                polyvinyl alcohol, polypropylene, polyethylene,                ethanolamine and ethylenediamine and then heating,                adding citric acid and vinyl alcohol and stirring;                and
   (2) use of perovskite oxide-based composition                for two-step thermochemical cycle decomposition of                water and carbon dioxide, where the application                method involves weighing perovskite oxide-based                composition and then performing reduction reaction                at high-temperature under inert gas atmosphere,                carrying out deoxygenation reaction at 800-1400                degrees C and reacting with water and carbon                dioxide at 500-1400 degrees C.

The exhaust fan, the solar panel, the temperature and humidity sensor, the carbon dioxide sensor, and the control circuit board are electrically connected to the battery. The control circuit board controls the opening and closing of the exhaust fan based on the data detected by the temperature and humidity sensor and the carbon dioxide sensor.
   USE - Solar self-powered greenhouse air exhausting device for cultivating plantings of fruit e.g. strawberry and vegetable in rural areas.
   ADVANTAGE - The air exhausting device can automatically exhaust the air according to the detected environmental parameters in the greenhouse, thus effectively reducing the manpower requirement, providing a better growth environment for fruits and vegetables and improving the harvest quality of fruits and vegetables. The air exhausting device can provide a better temperature and humidity environment for planters and can alleviate the diseases caused by the environment to some extent.

GA 2022A4015Q
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing biodegradable plastics comprises                (1) pre-pulverizing, mixing and plasticizing                biomass composite raw material including                hemicellulose, biomass and general-purpose resin,                and (2) mixing, kneading and extruding an oxidative                biodegradant to the material prepared in the mixing                step (1).
   USE - The biodegradable plastic is useful as food                packaging material.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (1)                  hemicellulose is generated in the paper or pulp                  manufacturing process. In the mixing and                  plasticization step, stirring pre-pulverized                  biomass composite raw material and pulverizing,                  and increasing temperature of biomass composite                  raw material through the stirring and                  pulverization to generate water vapor and                  kneading. The temperature of the composite raw                  material rises to 180-220 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: In the step (1), biomass is rice                  husk, wood flour, wheat bran and/or starch. The                  biomass composite raw material is 5-15 wt.%                  hemicellulose, 20-40 wt.% biomass, 20-40 wt.%                  polypropylene, 10-30 wt.% HDPE, 4-8 wt.% maleated                  polyethylene (MAPP), 1-3 wt.% polyethylene wax                  (PE-WAX), and 1-3 wt.% zinc stearate. The                  oxidative biodegradation agent comprises 10-20                  wt.% inorganic filler, 0.1-1 wt.% biodegradation                  catalyst, 60-95 wt.% binder resin, 1-2 wt.%                  automatic oxidizing agent, 1-3 wt.% lubricant and                  0.1-1 wt.% metal ion activator. The mixing weight                  ratio of biomass composite raw material and                  oxidative biodegradant is 98:2-99.5:0.5. The                  biodegradable plastics exhibits more than 70%                  biodegradability compared to cellulose                  standards.
   EXAMPLE - The example provide details of production of                oxidative biodegradant that do not support the                claims.

PN CN108863774-A
TI Synthesis of 2,4-dichlorophenylacetyl chloride by dissolving 2,4-dichlorophenylacetic acid in dichloroethane, slowly adding triphosgene, controlling temperature, reacting for an hour, and removing solvent under reduced pressure.
AU DING Y
   DING G
   DING T
   LIU X
   LIU Y
AE SHIJIAZHUANG LVFENG CHEM CO LTD (SHIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018976713
AB    NOVELTY - 2,4-Dichlorophenylacetyl chloride is synthesized by dissolving 2,4-dichlorophenylacetic acid in dichloroethane, slowly adding triphosgene, controlling temperature to less than or equal to 40 degrees C, reacting for 1 hour, and removing solvent under reduced pressure.
   USE - Synthesis of 2,4-dichlorophenylacetyl chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves product quality and yield, and avoids large amount of waste, as opposed to the use of thionyl chloride, making the method environment-friendly, and and byproduct is carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Triphosgene is used in place of thionyl chloride. Triphosgene molar ratio used is 1:1.05-1.3, preferably 1.1. Solvent is dichloromethane, dichloroethane, or toluene, preferably dichloroethane in an amount of 2.5 times the mass. Preferred Conditions: Reaction to form 2,4-dichlorophenylacetyl chloride is carried out without further purification, obtaining a next generation product. Reaction temperature is 30-60 degrees C, preferably 40 degrees C. Off-gas produced requires an exhaust gas absorption system. Preferred Processes: Solvent is required to be removed under reduced pressure, and temperature is controlled to be less than or equal to 50 degrees C. The product produced can be used directly in the next reaction without further purification.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

PN CN113955954-A
TI Fly ash carbon dioxide extracting and decalciming method, involves continuously introducing carbon dioxide, separating liquid in high pressure reaction kettle, and calcining calcium carbonate to obtain quicklime and carbon dioxide gas.
AU XU L
   XU G
   CHAI X
AE JIANGXI WANNIAN CSU EP IND COLLABORATIVE (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI GAIYA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202219481T
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide high-pressure extraction and decalcimulation of fly ash comprises (i) washing fly ash water to prepare a slurry, filtering the prepared slurry through a suction filter to obtain a water washing solution and solid slag, (ii) washing the solid residue to prepare slurry and (iii) placing the slurry in a high pressure container, introducing excessive carbon dioxide gas into slurry at the same time, using carbon dioxide high pressure leaching to convert the calcium contained in solid residue into calcium bicarbonate and dissolving in water, (iv) continuing to introduce carbon dioxide, separating the liquid in the high pressure reactor, where the liquid is a solution of calcium carbonate aqueous solution under normal pressure decomposition, (v) calcining the calcium carbonate obtained in step (vi) to obtain calcium oxide and carbon dioxide gases, and recovering the carbon dioxide in the stage of high pressure carbon dioxide leaching.
   USE - Fly ash carbon dioxide high pressure extracting and decalciming process for flue gas purification system and heat recovery system such as heat-saving device and boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The fly ash water washing process uses carbon dioxide gas to adjust the pH value of the fly ash slurry, increasing heavy metal and calcium ion precipitation, which can improve the water washing efficiency, low cost. The process separates the calcium carbonate by means of carbon dioxide high pressure leaching, generating quicklime after calcining, more convenient than cement kiln synergistic process, recycling carbon dioxide. The obtained quicklime capable of adding water to generate calcium hydroxide can be used for the smoke gas purification stage of the rubbish, so as to generate the closed loop of the resource utilization in the whole rubbish.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a process for fly ash decalciming. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN111848363-A
TI Preparing high-purity cannabidiol, involves            extracting by using industrial hemp, performing            supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain            primary extract, and separating by dynamic axial            compression industrial chromatography.
AU LIU X
   CAO L
AE HEILONGJIANG YANGGUANG IND HEMP RES INST (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9649R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-purity cannabidiol,                involves (a) extracting by using industrial hemp as                raw material, and performing supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid extraction to obtain the primary                extract, and (b) separating the primary extract by                dynamic axial compression industrial chromatography                to obtain cannabidiol.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity                cannabidiol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for the industrial                production of high-purity cannabidiol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction in step (a)                  includes drying the raw materials, pulverizing,                  passing through a 10-100 mesh sieve, putting in                  an extraction kettle, and collecting the                  extraction fractions after extraction to obtain                  the initial cannabidiol extract, where the                  extraction is performed at 30-60 degrees C and at                  an extraction pressure of 10-35 MPa for 60-360                  minutes, the carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-180                  l/hour, the temperature of the separation vessel                  (I) of 20-55 degrees C, the pressure of the                  separation vessel (I) of 5-30 MPa, the                  temperature of the separation vessel (II) of                  15-35 degrees C, and the pressure of the                  separation vessel (II) is 2-18 MPa. The                  separation in step (b) includes: adding the                  obtained primary extract to column chromatography                  silica gel filler, mixing with methanol, drying,                  and passing through a dynamic axial compression                  industrial preparative chromatographic column for                  gradient elution, where the mobile phase for                  gradient elution consists of mobile phase (A) and                  mobile phase (B) composition, where the mobile                  phase (A) is n-hexane, petroleum ether,                  dichloromethane or chloroform, and the mobile                  phase (B) is acetone, ethyl acetate, methanol or                  ethanol; and collecting the main-stage eluates                  containing cannabidiol components respectively,                  combining and filtering through a concentrated                  80-120 mesh filter or a plate and frame filter                  press, and drying the filtrate after recovering                  the solvent to obtain cannabidiol crystals. In                  step (b), the drying is freeze drying, spray                  drying or vacuum drying. Preferred Conditions:                  The drying in step (a) is performed at 40-90                  degrees C for 6-12 hours. After drying in step                  (a), the moisture content of the raw material is                  controlled within 3-5%. In step (b), the particle                  size of the column chromatography silica gel                  filler is 300-1400 mesh, and the concentration is                  concentration under reduced pressure. In step                  (b), the freeze-drying is performed under                  pre-freezing temperature of -35 degrees C to -45                  degrees C, pre-freezing time of 7-9 hours,                  partition temperature of 50-60 degrees C, and                  drying time of 28-32 hours. The spray drying is                  performed under inlet air temperature of 70-80                  degrees C, atomization pressure of 28-36 psi,                  material temperature of 50-60 degrees C, and                  outlet temperature of 35-50 degrees C. The vacuum                  drying is performed under vacuum degree of -0.09                  MPa to -0.1 MPa, at 45-60 degrees C, and drying                  to moisture below 3%. Preferred Components: The                  raw material is one or a mixture of industrial                  hemp flakes and leaves. Preferred Composition: In                  step (b), the gradient elution refers to the                  gradient elution of mobile phase (A) and mobile                  phase (B) in a volume ratio of (99:1)-(5:95). The                  mobile phase is one of the situations: (1) a                  mixture of n-hexane and acetone in the volume                  ratio of (12:1)-(3:1); (2) a mixture of n-hexane                  and ethyl acetate in the volume ratio of                  (10:1)-(2:1); (3) a mixture of n-hexane and                  methanol in the volume ratio of (15:1)-(5:1); (4)                  a mixture of n-hexane and ethanol in the volume                  ratio of (12:1)-(4:1); (5) a mixture of                  dichloromethane and methanol in the volume ratio                  of (90:1)-(10:1); (6) a mixture of                  dichloromethane and ethanol in the volume ratio                  of (95:1)-(15:1); (7) a mixture of                  dichloromethane and ethyl acetate in the volume                  ratio of (60:1)-(5:1); (8) a mixture of                  dichloromethane and acetone in the volume ratio                  of (80:1)-(20:1); (9) a mixture of petroleum                  ether and acetone in the volume ratio of                  (15:1)-(3:1); (10) a mixture of petroleum ether                  and ethyl acetate in the volume ratio of                  (12:1)-(3:1); a mixture of petroleum ether and                  methanol in the volume ratio of (20:1)-(8:1); a                  mixture of petroleum ether and ethanol in the                  volume ratio of (18:1)-(6:1); a mixture of                  chloroform and acetone in the volume ratio of                  (80:1)-(15:1); a mixture of chloroform and ethyl                  acetate in the volume ratio of (75:1)-(10:1); a                  mixture of chloroform and methanol in the volume                  ratio of (95:1)-(20:1); and a mixture of                  chloroform and ethanol in the volume ratio of                  (96:1)-(30:1).
