TI Preparing polyether carbonate polyol, comprises            using low molecular weight polyether polyol as            initiator and highly active amorphous double metal            cyanide complex as catalyst and performing            copolymerization of olefin oxide and carbon            dioxide.
AU ZHANG H
   ZAI Y
   LI P
   TU J
   CHEN F
   GU R
AE CHANGHUA CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020258990
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyether carbonate polyol,                comprises (i) using low molecular weight polyether                polyol as an initiator; (ii) using highly active                amorphous double metal cyanide complex as a                catalyst; (iii) performing copolymerization of                olefin oxide and carbon dioxide under lower                pressure to obtain polyether carbonate                polyol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyether                carbonate polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  low-molecular-weight polyether polyol is                  polyether polyol with a molecular weight of                  100-1500. The soluble cyanide complex salt is one                  of potassium hexacyanocobaltate, sodium                  hexacyanocobaltate or potassium hexacyanoferrate                  (III); the soluble metal salt is one of zinc                  chloride, zinc bromide or ferric chloride; the                  nonionic surfactant is one of nonylphenol                  polyoxyethylene ether, polyethylene polyamine                  polyoxyethylene ether or stearyl alcohol                  polyoxyethylene ether; and the specific surface                  area of the highly active amorphous double metal                  cyanide complex catalyst is 1-7 m2/g. In the step                  (i), the low-molecular-weight polyether polyol is                  polyether diols or polyether triols with a                  molecular weight of 100-1500. Preferred Method:                  The high activity amorphous double metal cyanide                  complex catalyst is obtained by reacting soluble                  cyanide complex salt and soluble metal salt under                  the contact of tert-butanol and nonionic                  surfactant to obtain the highly active amorphous                  double metal cyanide complex having specific                  surface area of metal cyanide complex catalyst is                  1-10 m2/g. The soluble cyanide complex salt                  comprises at least one of potassium                  hexacyanocobaltate, sodium hexacyanocobaltate,                  potassium hexacyanoferrate (III) acid, cobalt                  hexacyanocobaltate, tin hexacyanocobaltate and                  zinc hexacyanoiridium (III) acid; the soluble                  metal salt is one of zinc chloride, zinc bromide,                  ferric chloride, nickel chloride, tin chloride,                  lead chloride or cobalt chloride; the nonionic                  surfactant is one of nonylphenol polyoxyethylene                  ether, polyethylene polyamine polyoxyethylene                  ether, stearyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether or                  methallyl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether; high                  activity specific surface area of the stereotyped                  double metal cyanide complex catalyst is 1-8                  m2/g. The lower pressure range is -0.1-0.43 MPa;                  the alkylene oxide is at least one of ethylene                  oxide, propylene oxide or butylene oxide. The                  mass ratio of carbon dioxide to olefin oxide is                  1:1-20; the reaction temperature is 80-140                  degrees C, high activity amorphous double metal                  cyanide complex catalyst dosage is 30-100                  partspermillion (ppm); and the mass ratio of low                  molecular weight polyether polyol to alkylene                  oxide is 1:0.3-4. The alkylene oxide is at least                  one of ethylene oxide and propylene oxide; the                  mass ratio of carbon dioxide to alkylene oxide is                  1:4-15; the reaction temperature is 90-130                  degrees C; high activity amorphous double metal                  cyanide complex catalyst dosage is 50-100 ppm;                  and the mass ratio of low molecular weight                  polyether polyol to alkylene oxide is                  1:1-3.5.

PN KR2019111287-A; KR2046244-B1
TI New aminomethylphenol-based organic catalyst useful for producing alkylene carbonate.
AU KIM Y J
   KIM J
AE UNIV CHUNGBUK NAT IND-ACADEMIC COOP FOUN (UYCK-C)
GA 201984596P
AB    NOVELTY - Aminomethylphenol-based organic catalyst (I), is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for producing alkylene carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) produces cyclic alkylene carbonate at higher conversion rate than conventional catalyst using carbon dioxide as raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Aminomethylphenol-based organic catalyst of formula (I), is new.
   R = H, 1-10C alkyl, I, Br, Cl, F, OMe, NO2 or NMe2, preferably 4C alkyl or NMe2; and
   X = I, Br, Cl or NO3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing alkylene carbonate comprising reacting epoxide with carbon dioxide in presence of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: The epoxide includes ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, hexylene oxide, styrene oxide, methyl glycidyl ether, tert-butyl glycidyl ether, epichlorohydrin and/or glycidol. The alkylene carbonate is of formula (II). Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out at 10-200 degrees C under atmospheric pressure of 0.1-100 bar by using 0.01-10 mol.% (I). The reaction is carried out without the use of solvent or using solvent including dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, methylene chloride, diethyl ether, hexane and/or water.R = H, C, Cl, 1-4C alkyl, alkoxy or aryloxy group.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 6 Compounds (I) are specially claimed, e.g. 2-(bis(2-hydroxybenzyl)amino)-N,N,N-trimethylethanaminium iodide of formula (Ia), 2-(bis(5-tert-butyl-2-hydroxybenzyl)amino)-N,N,N-trimethylethanaminium iodide, 2-(bis(5-tert-butyl-2-hydroxybenzyl)amino)-N,N,N-trimethylethanaminium iodide, 2-(bis(5-tert-butyl-2-hydroxybenzyl)amino)-N,N,N-trimethylethanaminium iodide and 2-(bis(2-hydroxy-5-methoxybenzyl)amino)-N,N,N-trimethylethanaminium iodide.
   EXAMPLE - 0.9 g 2,2'-(2-(Dimethylamino)ethylazanediyl)bis(methylene)diphenol was dissolved in 25 ml acetonitrile solution, 1.7 g methyl iodide was added, mixture was refluxed at 50 degrees C for 12 hours, reaction mixture was cooled to room temperature, all volatiles were removed under reduced pressure, solid obtained was washed three times with 40 ml diethyl ether, and vacuum dried to obtain 1.19 g 2-(bis(2-hydroxybenzyl)amino)-N,N,N-trimethylethanaminium iodide pale yellow solid compound with 90% yield.

PN CN110105248-A; CN110105248-B
TI Preparing toluene diisocyanate comprises carrying out coupling reaction using 2,6-diaminotoluene and gaseous carbon dioxide as raw material and polyoxometallate as catalyst.
AU YU H
   DAN D
   WU Z
   WEI Y
   HAN S
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 201970683L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing toluene diisocyanate comprises carrying out coupling reaction using 2,6-diaminotoluene and gaseous carbon dioxide as raw material and polyoxometallate as catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing toluene diisocyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts Anderson type heteropoly acid as catalyst; has mild reaction conditions and high specific selectivity; is environmentally friendly and recyclable; improves cleanliness of industrial reaction and economical efficiency of the process; is economical; reduces environmental pressure; and facilitates industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises adding a mixture of 2,6-diaminotoluene, polyoxometallate and solvent to the reaction vessel, adding phenyl silane, acid binding agent and dehydrating agent, uniformly mixing the mixture, introducing gaseous carbon dioxide, where the reaction temperature is set to 0-40 degrees C, stirring and reacting for 6-24 hours, and separating and purifying to obtain the product. The reaction temperature is set to 0-30 degrees C, preferably 30 degrees C for 6-12 hours, preferably 12 hours. The method comprises recycling the polyoxometallate catalyst after the reaction is recovered by using organic solvent. Preferred Components: The catalyst is an Anderson-type polyoxometallate centered on iron, copper, nickel or chromium, or an Anderson-type polyoxometallate having iron, copper, nickel or chromium modified by a trialkoxy derivative as a central metal. The acid binding agent is pyridine or triethylamine. The dehydrating agent is dimethyl sulfoxide or phosphorus oxychloride. The solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, toluene or anhydrous acetonitrile. The molar ratio of the polyoxometallate, 2,6-diaminotoluene, phenyl silane, acid binding agent and dehydrating agent is 0.02:2:4:1:2. The ratio of the amount of the solvent to the 2,6-diaminotoluene is 3 ml/mmol.

TI Method for improving production efficiency of water electrolysis cell while sequestering carbon dioxide gas, involves introducing carbon dioxide gas into catholyte containing hydroxyl ion, and reacting carbon dioxide gas with hydroxyl ion to form one or more of bicarbonate and carbonate.
AU RAU G H
   TAHMASEBI S
AE PLANETARY HYDROGEN INC (PLAN-Non-standard)
   PLANETARY HYDROGEN INC (PLAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022739853
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving production efficiency of water electrolysis cell (20a) while sequestering carbon dioxide gas, involves introducing carbon dioxide gas into a catholyte containing hydroxyl ion, by introducing the carbon dioxide gas at a rate, resulting in a reduced pH level of the catholyte, but not exceeding a rate leading to total consumption of hydroxyl ions, and reacting carbon dioxide gas with hydroxyl ion to form one or more of bicarbonate and carbonate, and conducting the electrolysis with the catholyte having reduced pH level, where the production efficiency is measured by quantity of product produced by the electrolysis cell per volt of electrical potential applied between cathode (24) and anode of the cell or per watt of power used by the cell, the product is selected from a gas formed at the cathode, a gas formed at the anode, acid, a carbonate, and a bicarbonate.
   USE - Method for improving production efficiency of water electrolysis cell while sequestering carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The reaction of the carbon dioxide with hydroxide ions present in the catholyte reduces pH of the catholyte, for increasing production efficiency of the electrolysis cell. The apparatus has improved production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) an apparatus for improving production efficiency of water electrolysis cell while sequestering carbon dioxide gas, which includes a unit for introducing carbon dioxide gas into catholyte containing hydroxyl ions, and a unit for conducting the electrolysis with the catholyte having reduced pH level; and (2) an electrochemical system with improved production efficiency and sequestration of the carbon dioxide gas, which includes a unit for introducing carbon dioxide gas into catholyte containing hydroxyl ions, and a unit for controlling the rate of the introduction of carbon dioxide gas to achieve predetermined value of a control variable, for improving the production efficiency of the electrolysis cell while sequestering the carbon dioxide gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining operation of the electrolysis cell of the electrochemical system.
   Electrolysis cell (20a)
   Cathode (24)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves controlling the rate of introduction of carbon dioxide gas to achieve predetermined pH level of the catholyte, for controlling an increase of the production efficiency of the electrolysis cell, controlling the rate of the introduction of carbon dioxide gas to achieve a predetermined value of a control variable, where the control variable is selected from one or more of pH level of the catholyte in the cathode area inside the electrolysis cell, pH level of the catholyte removed from the cathode area of the electrolysis cell, pH level of an electrolyte of the electrolysis cell, current of the electrolysis cell, voltage of the electrolysis cell, concentration of the carbon dioxide in a gas stream, concentration of carbon dioxide in the catholyte, concentration of carbon dioxide in the catholyte removed from the cell, concentration of carbon dioxide in the electrolyte and quantity of the product produced per unit time. The introducing step is carried out by introducing the carbon dioxide gas into the catholyte in a cathode area of the electrolysis cell, and introducing the carbon dioxide gas into an electrolyte prior to introduction of the electrolyte into the electrolysis cell. The conducting step is carried out by conducting saline water electrolysis, with saline water containing a salt dissolved in water, the dissolved salt being selected from salts whose ions consist of ammonium, calcium, iron, magnesium, potassium, sodium or copper cations, and carbonate, chloride, nitrate, phosphate or sulfate anions, preferably one or more of sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate, sodium phosphate, sodium carbonate, potassium sulfate, potassium nitrate, potassium phosphate and potassium carbonate. The introducing step is carried out by contacting the catholyte with a gas stream containing carbon dioxide. The conducting step is carried out by conducting the electrolysis in the electrolysis cell having at least one ion-exchange membrane arranged between cathode and anode and defining cathode area and anode area, and the introducing step is carried out by introducing carbon dioxide gas into the cathode area in close proximity to the cathode. The conducting step is carried out by conducting electrolysis in the electrolysis cell having a cation exchange membrane and an anion exchange membrane arranged in the electrolysis cell between cathode and anode and defining cathode area, anode area and central area, and the introducing step is carried out by introducing the carbon dioxide gas into one or more of the cathode area and the central area. Preferred Components: The product is selected from hydrogen, oxygen, chlorine, and acid. The carbon dioxide gas is derived from one or more of atmosphere, a waste stream, biomass, soil, ocean and fossil source. Preferred Conditions: The reduced pH level is from 14 to 7, preferably 11 to 9. The production efficiency of the electrolysis cell is increased up to 30%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The apparatus further includes a unit for controlling the rate of the introduction of carbon dioxide gas to achieve predetermined value of control variable, for controlling increase of the production efficiency of the electrolysis cell.

PT P
PN GB2565863-A
TI Carbon dioxide turbine system for electricity generation, in which outlets of chambers feed carbon dioxide into turbine, and inlets of chambers receive carbon dioxide from turbine.
AU BUDHDEO S P
AE ENGEN HOUSE-FZC (ENGE-Non-standard)
GA 201918873S
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide turbine system (10) comprises a turbine (14) and multiple chambers (12), each having an inlet (16) and an outlet (18), a heating element and a cooling element. The outlets of the chambers are configured to feed carbon dioxide into the turbine. The inlets of the chambers are configured to receive the carbon dioxide from the turbine.
   USE - Carbon dioxide turbine system for electricity generation in rankine cycle plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is closed loop and self-contained, requiring no water cooling or external fossil fuel. All energy, except mechanical losses, is utilized or re-absorbed, such that the latent heat of vaporization is not lost as in steam or organic Rankine cycle (ORC) Rankine Turbines. The mechanical efficiency of the Rankine cycle is enhanced by the rapid condensation of the carbon dioxide to solid. The turbine stores energy as a phase change battery with minimal losses, as energy is recovered via the cryocooler and heat recovery box and pushed back to re-usable energy via a heap pump loop and back into the turbine. The differential valve system removes the need for a pump or mechanical energy loss in the system, since the differential valves ensure that the carbon dioxide inlet pressure is delivered without using any pumps. Capillary tubes offer the greatest surface area for heat transfer into the carbon dioxide and for liquid nitrogen or other cryocooled medium to absorb the latent heat of condensation. The chambers act as both expansion chambers and condensation chambers in sequence. The only moving parts are the pumps in the cryocooler, the valves, the turbine blades and the electrical generator apparatus. The mechanical efficiency is higher than any carbon dioxide turbine and overall efficiency when including input energy is the highest of any Rankine turbine, by using the expansion ratio of carbon dioxide in the phase change from solid to gas to produce the pressures, which is far greater than using super-critical carbon dioxide turbines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method of generating electricity using the carbon dioxide turbine system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the turbine system.
   Carbon dioxide turbine system (10)
   Chambers (12)
   Turbine (14)
   Inlet (16)
   Outlet (18)

   USE - The method is useful for ultrasonic                supercritical combined extraction of tea                polyphenols.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the extraction rate and                purity of tea polyphenol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ultrasonic supercritical combined extraction                of tea polyphenols comprises carrying out raw                material processing, ultrasonic treatment,                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, separation                and purification steps, preferably supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction comprises carrying out                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of treated                slurry, adding sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate,                ethyl acetate and polysorbate into the carbon                dioxide fluid, controlling the extraction pressure                and temperature, the carbon dioxide flow rate is                20-25 l/h, and the extraction time is 1.5-3.5 hours                to obtain the crude tea polyphenol a, separating                the crude tea polyphenol from the slurry, and the                separation pressure is 3-6 Mpa, the separation                temperature is 40-50 degrees C, again extracting                the remaining slurry by supercritical carbon                dioxide, adding acetone and sodium dodecylbenzene                sulfonate into the carbon dioxide fluid to control                the extraction pressure and temperature, the carbon                dioxide flow rate is 20-25 l/hours, the extraction                time is 1-2 hours to obtain crude tea polyphenol b,                separating the crude tea polyphenol from the                slurry, the separation pressure is 3-6 MPa, the                separation temperature is 40-50 degrees C, and                mixing obtained crude tea polyphenol a and the                crude tea polyphenol b, and adding hydrochloric                acid, and adjusting the pH to 3-5.2, centrifuging                or filtering to obtain tea soup.

The method comprises adding acetone                  and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate into the                  carbon dioxide fluid to control the extraction                  pressure to 22-27 MPa. The weight ratio between acetone and                  sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate is 1.6:1. AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating a fuel cell (31)                to generate electricity and an exhaust stream                comprising steam and carbon dioxide, in which the                fuel cell being connected to a power distributor.                The exhaust stream is conditioned and separated                using gas recovery unit (GRU) (2) to produce                streams of steam and carbon dioxide. A reformer                input stream is produced by combining a stream of                methane, the stream of carbon dioxide, and the                stream of steam according to a stoichiometric ratio                required to produce a stream of synthesis gas. The                reformer input stream is reacted in the electric                catalytic reformer to produce the stream of                synthesis gas, in which an electric catalytic                reformer (8) is powered by the power distributor.                The heat is recovered from the stream of synthesis                gas using a power generator, to produce                electricity, in which the power generator being                connected to the power distributor.
   USE - Method of producing synthesis gas using fuel                cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The overall plant energy requirements are                reduced and the overall efficiency is improved. The                efficiencies in the production of methanol are                improved. The emission of GHG to the atmosphere is                prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The stream of synthesis gas is reacted in a                synthesis reactor (19) to produce a petrochemical.                The heat is recovered from the synthesis reactor                using a Rankine Cycle or an Organic Rankine Cycle                power generator that is connected to the power                distributor. The heat is recovered from the methanol                synthesis reactor using a power generator to                produce electricity. The fuel cell is operated to                produce a desired amount of steam and carbon                dioxide, and any excess electricity is sold to a                third-party consumer. The power distributor receives electricity from the                fuel cell. The GRU is connected to the fuel cell. An                electric catalytic reformer receives the second                methane stream, and the stream of carbon dioxide.                The electric catalytic reformer is being powered by                electricity from the power distributor to produce a                stream of synthesis gas. The power generator                recovers heat from the stream of synthesis gas to                produce electricity. The power generator is                connected to the power distributor. The synthesis                reactor reacts the stream of synthesis gas to                produce a petrochemical in an exothermic reaction.                The power generator comprises a Rankine cycle or an                organic Rankine Cycle that generates electricity                from heat. The synthesis reactor is adapted to                produce methanol. The fuel cell is                adapted to operate the power distributor to supply                excess electricity to a third party consumer, the                excess electricity being generated fuel cell is                operated to produce a desired amount of steam and                carbon dioxide, and any excess electricity is sold                to a third-party consumer.

TI Combined heat and power supply device for coupling carbon dioxide generation and geothermal energy, comprises heat absorber, carbon dioxide turbine, high temperature carbon dioxide regenerator, condenser, evaporator, and low temperature regenerator.
AU WANG H
   AN H
   BAI Y
   HUANG Y
   ZHOU X
   PENG S
   CAI H
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2022273426
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide turbine (3) in which high temperature and high-pressure carbon dioxide in heat absorber (2) enters to perform work after absorbing heat, and enters a high temperature carbon dioxide regenerator (4) for heat exchange after doing the work. The water evaporates into vapor and enters a condenser (7) for heat exchange after the water in the lithium bromide concentrated solution in the solution circulation unit absorbs heat. The water condenses itself into liquid water and enters an evaporator (8) to continue heat exchange after heat exchange. The water evaporates again into vapor and enters the solution circulation unit after heat exchange. The geothermal water enters the evaporator to release heat and then recharges into a geothermal well (16). The heat network return water enters a carbon dioxide cooler (15) for heat exchange and then enters the solution circulation unit to continue heat exchange.
   USE - Combined heat and power supply device for coupling carbon dioxide generation and geothermal energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves grade of middle and low temperature geothermal energy, realizes comprehensive application of power generation and heat supply, and flexibly adjusts matching between electric and heat, thus realizing solar energy, nuclear energy and new energy. The phase change heat characteristic of the absorption type heat pump controls the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide at the compressor inlet, thus improving stability of a supercritical CO2 power generation system. The device utilizes the hot net return water to absorb carbon dioxide waste heat absorber heat, condenser heat, and realizes solar energy and nuclear energy for generating heat and comprehensive utilization, so that combined scheduling of multiple new energy in a future energy structure can be improved, and flexibility of the energy structure is improved.

AB    NOVELTY - Cyclic method of capturing and utilizing                carbon dioxide (CO2) contained in a gas stream,                involves first step of introducing a first gas                stream (2) to contact a first solid material,                second solid material and CO2 sorbent material,                where first gas stream comprises CO2 and greater                than or equal to 1 reductant, with the process                conditions of temperature of 573-1473 K and                pressure of 0.1-100 bar; and second step of                introducing a second gas stream (3) to contact CO2                sorbent material, second solid material and first                solid material, where the second gas stream                comprises greater than or equal to 1 oxidant with                the process conditions of temperature of 573-1473 K                and pressure of 0.01-10 bar. The first solid                material has a first thermodynamic equilibrium                oxygen partial pressure p1O2 and second solid                material has a second thermodynamic equilibrium                oxygen partial pressure p2O2, with p2O2 that is                larger than p1O2 at the process conditions of first                step and at the process conditions of second                step.
   USE - Cyclic method of capturing and utilizing                carbon dioxide (CO2) contained in a gas                stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat that is required can be generated                in-situ. The method does not require external heat                supply. The method method that incorporates CO2                capturing, CO2 utilization and synthesis gas                production in an integrated chemical looping                process. The method allows a lower operating                temperature and does not impose high requirements                on the materials used in the method.

The first solid material oxidizes greater than or                equal to 1 reductant partially in first step and                under the process conditions of first step and the                first solid material is oxidized by CO2 in second                step and under the process conditions of second                step. The second solid material oxidizes greater                than or equal to 1 reductant in first step and                under the process conditions of first step and                second solid material is not oxidized by CO2 and                not oxidized by water in second step and under the                process conditions of the second step. The CO2                sorbent material is capturing CO2 in first step                under the process conditions of first step and CO2                sorbent material is releasing CO2, preferably the                CO2 captured in first step, in second step and                under the process conditions of second step. PN WO2022080088-A1; JP2022064671-A
TI Producing hydrocarbon used as valuable resource, involves introducing carbon dioxide into underground storage site in which moisture and catalyst metal are present, and reacting water in moisture with carbon dioxide in subcritical state or supercritical state at storage site.
AU TAKASHI F
   MASATERU N
   FUJII T
   NISHIOKA M
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 202253367R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a hydrocarbon, involves introducing carbon dioxide to underground storage site in which moisture and a catalyst metal are present and having pressure of greater than or equal to 5 MPa and temperature of greater than or equal to 40 degrees C to obtain carbon dioxide to subcritical state or supercritical state, and reacting water in moisture with carbon dioxide in subcritical state or supercritical state at storage site to synthesize hydrocarbon.
   USE - The method is used for producing hydrocarbon, which is used as valuable resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the carbon dioxide on ground, forms valuable resources by converting carbon dioxide stored in ground to solid or liquid hydrocarbons, and suppresses the leakage of carbon dioxide stored in the ground.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves introducing carbon dioxide and catalyst particles having a catalytic metal to storage site in ground, which comprises water and having pressure of greater than or equal to 5 MPa and temperature of greater than or equal to 40 degrees C, and obtaining carbon dioxide to subcritical state or supercritical state, and reacting the water in water with carbon dioxide in subcritical state or supercritical state at the storage site to synthesize hydrocarbon. Preferred Components: The catalyst particles comprise core as nanoparticle carrier and shell as catalyst metal. The catalyst metal comprises metal that promotes Fischer-Tropsch reaction. The catalyst metal comprises nickel, iron, cobalt and palladium. Preferred Properties: The storage point is 800-1200 m underground. The pressure at storage point is greater than or equal to 8 MPa.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the general hydrocarbon synthesis that does not support the claims.

PN CN113683765-A; CN113683765-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide-epoxide copolymer            involves mixing solvent and catalyst, introducing            carbon dioxide and adding epoxy monomer continuously or            in batches under supercritical conditions for            reaction.
AU WANG X
   ZHOU Q
   JIA F
   CAI Y
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2021E1326C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-epoxide copolymer                involves mixing solvent and the catalyst,                introducing carbon dioxide and adding epoxy monomer                continuously or in batches under supercritical                conditions for reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide-epoxide                copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures strong solubility, low                viscosity, and high diffusion coefficient of carbon                dioxide-epoxide copolymer by increasing the                polymerization pressure to make the carbon                dioxide-epoxide mixture in an ultra-critical state,                maintains the high molecular weight and high                carbonate content of the product through continuous                or batch feeding technology, also maintains the                epoxy monomer at a low concentration and eliminates                the risk of reaction heat runaway, reduces the                complexity and investment of industrial equipment,                and also avoids epoxy monomer self-polymerization,                increases ether segment, and improves the                efficiency of carbon dioxide fixation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  dioxolane, dioxane, dimethyl carbonate, diethyl                  carbonate, methylene chloride, chloroform and                  2-8C alkane. The catalyst is a heterogeneous                  catalyst or a homogeneous catalyst. The                  heterogeneous catalyst is ternary rare earth                  catalyst, a dicarboxylic acid zinc catalyst and a                  double metal cyanide catalyst. The homogeneous                  catalyst is metalloporphyrin catalyst, salen                  metal catalyst and modified catalytic system. The                  epoxide monomer is ethylene oxide, 1, 2-propylene                  oxide, 1, 2-butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin and                  cyclohexene oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: The mass of the                  catalyst is 0.01-4 wt.% the solvent and the epoxy                  compound monomer. The mass ratio of the epoxide                  monomer to the solvent is 1:99-50:50. The epoxide                  monomer and solvent in volume ratio of                  45:55-55:45. Preferred Conditions: The heating                  temperature is 40-110degrees Celsius and pressure                  is 7.6-12 mega Pascal by introducing carbon                  dioxide during heating and the reaction time is                  3-24 hours. The time for continuously adding the                  epoxide monomer is controlled to be 3-20 hours.                  The epoxide monomers are added in batches for                  0.5-2 hours and the number of batches is 5-10.                  The content of carbonate units in the carbon                  dioxide-epoxide copolymer is 88.5-99.7%.
DC A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

PN CN112180049-A; CN213482169-U
TI Air quality sensor circuit, has carbon dioxide sensor for detecting carbon dioxide concentration information in air, and electronic switch connected with carbon dioxide sensor and communication path.
AU ZHOU Z
   HUANG L
   WANG Y
   WANG D
AE SHENZHEN HOTO INTELLIGENT CONTROL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN HOTO INTELLIGENT CONTROL TECHNO (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021076559
AB    NOVELTY - Circuit has a carbon dioxide sensor for detecting carbon dioxide concentration information in air. A particle sensor detects PM2.5 concentration information in the air. A TVOC sensor detects TVOC concentration information in the air. An electronic switch is connected with the carbon dioxide sensor and the particle sensor. A controller is connected with the electronic switch. The TVOC sensor obtains the carbon dioxide concentration information, the PM2.5 concentration information and the TVOC concentration information. The electronic switch controls a communication path between the carbon dioxide sensor and the controller.
   USE - Air quality sensor circuit for measuring concentration information of gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The air quality sensor circuit can independently detect and obtain the concentration information of each gas index, which improves the measuring accuracy of the data.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure of the air quality sensor circuit.

PT P
PN CN110903843-A; CN110903843-B
TI Preparing isoparaffins by catalytic hydrogenation            of carbon dioxide involves using mixed gas containing            carbon dioxide and hydrogen as raw material gas, which            is directly converted into isoparaffins under the            catalytic action.
AU GE Q
   WEI J
   YAO R
   SUN J
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202024129C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing isoparaffins by catalytic                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide involves using                mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen as                raw material gas, which is directly converted into                isoparaffins under the catalytic action of a                multifunctional catalyst. The multifunctional                catalyst is a first component consisting of                iron-based carbon dioxide hydrogenation to produce                low-carbon olefin catalyst, mixed with one or two                or more metal-modified or unmodified molecular                sieves which play a role in olefin polymerization                and hydroisomerization as the second component. The                mass ratio of the first component to the second                component is 1:10-10:1, preferably 1:3-3:1.
   USE - Method for preparing isoparaffins by catalytic                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare isoparaffins by                catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide develops                anew route from the carbon dioxide to produce                isoparaffin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The auxiliary agent is                  one kind or two or more kinds of potassium (K)                  oxide, sodium (Na) oxide, copper (Cu) oxide,                  manganese (Mn) oxide, zirconium (Zr) oxide, zinc                  (Zn) oxide, and cerium (Ce) oxide. Preferred                  Composition: The optimal content of the auxiliary                  agent in the catalyst accounts for 0.5-10% of the                  total mass of the catalyst component for the                  hydrogenation of iron-based carbon dioxide to                  low-carbon olefins. The carbon dioxide is a gas                  containing carbon dioxide, and the gas refers to                  any one or more than two kinds of carbon dioxide                  absorbed in industrial exhaust gas containing                  carbon dioxide, automobile exhaust gas,                  coal-fired exhaust gas, atmosphere, and seawater.                  Preferred Conditions: The main active component                  of the iron-based carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                  low-carbon olefin catalyst is optimized as an                  iron-based component with good reverse water gas                  shift function and CO hydrogenation to olefin                  function, optimized for ferrous-ferric oxide                  (Fe3O4), with or without additives it is an                  oxide, and the content of additives accounts for                  0-20% of the total mass of the catalyst. The                  molecular sieve refers to one or more of MCM-22                  molecular sieve, Beta molecular sieve, ZSM-5                  molecular sieve, SAPO-11 molecular sieve, Y-type                  molecular sieve, and MOR molecular sieve,                  preferably one or two of MCM-22 and Beta                  molecular sieve, and the silicon-aluminum ratio                  of the molecular sieve used is 10-250. The metal                  used for the modified molecular sieve includes                  one or two or more of Ga, La, Cu, and Zn,                  preferably one or two of Ga and Cu, and the metal                  element accounts for 0.1-20% of the mass of the                  modified molecular sieve, preferably 0.5-10%. The                  catalyst component using iron-based CO2                  hydrogenation to produce low-carbon olefin has                  the following characteristics, the catalyst                  component using iron-based CO2 hydrogenation to                  produce low-carbon olefin can be prepared by one                  of the following processes. The catalyst adopts                  one-step synthesis method includes mixing soluble                  iron (Fe) (II) salt and soluble Fe (III) salt to                  form a salt solution, or the soluble Fe (II)                  salt, soluble Fe (III) salt and auxiliary salt                  are mixed to form a salt solution. The Fe (III)                  concentration in the salt solution is 0.02-0.8                  mol/L, and the hydrochloride (HCl) solution is                  added. The HCl concentration is 3-10.1 mol/L, and                  the pH of the solution is adjusted to 1-6. The                  molar ratio of Fe (III) to Fe (II) in the salt                  solution 2:(0.8-1.2). The soluble Fe (II) salt                  and soluble Fe (III) salt refer to salt compounds                  that can dissolve in water, preferably divalent                  Fe salt and trivalent Fe salt are one of chloride                  salt, nitrate salt and acetate salt Or two or                  more. The auxiliary salts are salt compounds that                  are soluble in water, preferably one or more of                  chloride, nitrate and acetate, add alkaline                  solution to the solution, gradually adjust the pH                  value of the solution is 1-6 to alkaline pH value                  is 9-12. The aging is 1-10 hours after the                  dropwise addition. The alkaline solution refers                  to an alkaline solution capable of adjusting the                  pH value of the solution, preferably one or more                  of sodium hydroxide (NaOH), potassium hydroxide                  (KOH), sodium carbonate (Na2CO3), sodium                  bicarbonate (NaHCO3), potassium carbonate                  (K2CO3), potassium hydrogen carbonate (KHCO3),                  sodium oxalate (Na2C2O4), potassium oxalate                  (K2C2O4), Sodium formate (RCOONa), RCOOK and                  ammonia water. The concentration of alkaline                  solution is 0.1-8 mol/L. The R refers to organic                  functional groups, including 1-12C alkyl groups,                  2-12C alkenyl groups or 6-15C aryl groups,                  optimized for methyl, ethyl or phenyl. the                  deposited product is separated from magnetic                  field adsorption, centrifugation or suction                  filtration After the reaction is completed. The                  deposited product is fully washed with deionized                  water, dried, roasted or not roasted, the                  roasting temperature is 300-800?OC, and the                  roasting time is 2-10 hours, that is, an                  iron-based catalyst without additives or                  additives is prepared. The catalyst is prepared                  by a one-step synthesis method,which includes                  mixing the soluble Fe (II) salt and the soluble                  Fe (III) salt to form a salt solution according                  to the required ratio of the catalyst                  composition. The Fe (III) concentration in salt                  solution is 0.02-0.8 mol/L, and adding HCl                  solution, HCl concentration is 3-10.1 mol/L. The                  molar ratio of Fe (III) to Fe (II) in the salt                  solution 2: (0.8-1.2). The alkaline solution                  containing Na or K added to (1), gradually adjust                  the pH value of the solution is 1-6 to alkaline                  pH value is 9-12, The aging is 1-10 hours after                  the dropwise addition, after the reaction is                  completed, the deposited product is separated                  from (2) by magnetic field adsorption,                  centrifugation or suction filtration. The                  deposited product is washed with deionized water,                  (by controlling the number of washings and the                  amount of water used for each washing to control                  the content of residual Na or K in the catalyst),                  and drying. The roasting or not roasting,                  roasting temperature is 300-800 degrees C,                  roasting time is 2-10 hours, that is to prepare                  iron-based catalyst containing sodium (Na) and/or                  potassium (K) additives. The catalyst first                  synthesizes Fe3O4 by co-precipitation method, and                  then adds the auxiliary agent by impregnation                  method includes mixing soluble Fe (II) salt,                  soluble Fe (III) salt according to the required                  ratio of the catalyst composition. A salt                  solution is formed, the Fe (III) concentration in                  the salt solution is 0.02-0.8 mol/L, and add HCl                  solution, the HCl concentration is 3-10.1 mol/L.                  The molar ratio of Fe (III) to Fe (II) in the                  salt solution 2: (0.8-1.2). The alkaline solution                  is add to (1), gradually adjust the pH value of                  the solution is 1-6 to alkaline pH value is 9-12,                  after dropping, aging for 1-10 hours. The                  deposited product is separated from (2) by                  magnetic field adsorption, centrifugation or                  suction filtration, and the deposited product is                  fully washed with deionized water and dried to                  obtain the active ingredient Fe3O4 With or                  without the addition of iron-based catalysts. The                  immersion method is used to combine the auxiliary                  salt with the active ingredients to form a                  catalyst. The specific process is according to                  the required auxiliary agent content, calculate                  the theoretical amount of auxiliary agent salt                  required. An aqueous solution of an auxiliary                  salt is formulate, immerse the Fe3O4 (equal                  volume) prepared in (3) in the solution, after                  stirring, standing, drying and roasting                  procedures, the roasting temperature is 300-800                  degrees C. The calcination time is 2-10 hours,                  that is, the iron-based catalyst containing the                  auxiliary agent is prepared. The method includes                  when the molecular sieve is modified, the metal                  component can be loaded onto the molecular sieve                  by one of the following two methods first is                  prepared by the equal volume dipping method. The                  specific process is calculating the amount of                  modified metal salt required in theory according                  to the required modified metal content,                  formulated into an aqueous solution of modified                  metal salt. The modified metal salt is selected                  from any one or more of nitrate, chloride,                  bromide, acetate, acetylacetonate, citrate,                  oxalate, and benzoate. The molecular sieve (equal                  volume) to be modified is immersed in the                  solution, and the metal-modified molecular sieve                  is prepared by stirring, standing, drying, and                  roasting procedures at a roasting temperature of                  300-800 degrees C and a roasting time of 2-10                  hours. The second method is prepared by ion                  exchange method, the specific process includes                  calculating the amount of theoretically required                  modified metal salt according to the required                  modified metal content, formulated into an                  aqueous solution of modified metal salt. The                  modified metal salt is selected from any one or                  more of nitrate, chloride, bromide, acetate,                  acetylacetonate, citrate, oxalate, and benzoate.                  The molecular sieves that need to be modified                  mixing with a solid liquid mass ratio of 1:                  (10-200), and ion exchange for 2-4 hours, after                  washing, drying and roasting procedures, the                  roasting temperature is 300-800 degrees C and the                  roasting time is 2-10 hours, then the                  metal-modified molecular sieve is prepared. The                  two components of the multi functional composite                  catalyst can be mixed in one of the following two                  ways first is particle mixing method includes                  weighing the first component and the second                  component powder separately, separately tableting                  and sieving, mixing particles according to the                  required mass ratio to form a composite catalyst.                  The second is layered filling method, which                  includes filling the catalyst bed with the first                  component and the second component in order from                  the top to the bottom of the reactor, with or                  without an inert material separation layer                  between the two catalyst bed components, the mass                  ratio of the inert material separation layer to                  the composite catalyst active component is                  0.01-10. The raw material gas passes through the                  first component and the second component                  respectively to complete the reaction process.                  The reaction temperature is 260-470 degrees C                  (preferably 280-380), the reaction pressure is                  0.01-10.0 megapascal (MPa) (preferably 0.5-6.0),                  and the space velocity of the raw material gas is                  500-50000 mL/(h gcat) (preferably 1000-15000),                  and the H2/CO2 molar ratio in the raw material                  gas is 0.5-8.0 (preferably 1-5). The method                  includes before using the catalyst, the reduction                  conditions are adopted under normal pressure, in                  H2 (600-3000 mL/h gCat), and the reduction time                  at 300-400 degrees C is 2-12 hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

The pressure control enables containers to be filled with carbon dioxide in a particularly rapid and controlled manner. PN CN113278996-A
TI Preparation of 2,4,5-trifluorophenylacetic acid used as pharmaceutical intermediate, involves stirring electrolyte solution and 2,4,5-trifluorobenzyl chloride in electrolytic cell, passing carbon dioxide gas, electrolyzing to obtain 2,4,5-trifluorophenylacetate solution, acidifying and purifying.
AU DONG K
   ZHANG X
AE ANHUI HAIKANG PHARM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202199499B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 2,4,5-trifluorophenylacetic acid (I) involves adding chlorinating agent and paraformaldehyde to container, adding 1,2,4-trifluorobenzene (II), reacting at 30-50 degrees C, pouring the reaction mixture into ice water, separating the organic layer, washing with water, drying, purifying to obtain 2,4,5-trifluorobenzyl chloride (III), filling sealed electrolytic cell with carbon dioxide, adding electrolyte solution and 2,4,5-trifluorobenzyl chloride, stirring, continuously passing carbon dioxide gas for 1 hour, using one of inert metal or platinum mesh electrode, carbon electrode, graphite electrode, and glass carbon electrode as cathode and active metal or metallic platinum as anode, passing current of 1-5000 mA/cm2 on cathode surface area, electrolyzing at -50 degrees C to 100 degrees C to obtain 2,4,5-trifluorophenylacetate solution, acidifying and purifying.
   USE - Preparation of compound (I) used as pharmaceutical intermediate.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is safe and carried out using inexpensive and easily available raw materials under mild reaction condition to produce product with high purity, low generation of three wastes and stable quality, which meets requirements of pharmaceutical intermediate, and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of 2,4,5-trifluorophenylacetic acid of formula (I) involves adding chlorinating agent and paraformaldehyde to container, adding 1,2,4-trifluorobenzene of formula (II), reacting at 30-50 degrees C, pouring the reaction mixture into ice water, separating the organic layer, washing with water, drying, purifying to obtain 2,4,5-trifluorobenzyl chloride of formula (III), filling sealed electrolytic cell with carbon dioxide, adding electrolyte solution and 2,4,5-trifluorobenzyl chloride, stirring, continuously passing carbon dioxide gas for 1 hour, using one of inert metal or platinum mesh electrode, carbon electrode, graphite electrode, and glass carbon electrode as cathode and active metal or metallic platinum as anode, passing current of 1-5000 mA/cm2 on cathode surface area, electrolyzing at -50 degrees C to 100 degrees C to obtain 2,4,5-trifluorophenylacetate solution, acidifying and purifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert metal is chosen from metallic copper, metallic nickel, metallic platinum, metallic tungsten, metallic silver, and metallic titanium. The active metal is chosen from metallic zinc, metallic aluminum, metallic magnesium, metallic manganese and metallic iron.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte solution is chosen from N,N-dimethylformamide, acetonitrile or N,N-dimethylacetamide solution of tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium iodide, tetrabutylammonium perchlorate, tetrabutyltetrafluoroborate, tetrabutylammonium hexafluorophosphate, tetraethylammonium chloride, tetraethylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium iodide, tetraethylammonium perchlorate and tetraethyl tetrafluoroborate. The acid used for acidification is one of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, and acetic acid. Preferred Composition: The concentration of electrolyte solution is 0.01-5 M, and 2,4,5-trifluorobenzyl chloride in the electrolyte solution is 0.01-5 M. Preferred Condition: The purification process is carried out by performing distillation and rectification.

PN EP3865202-A1; WO2021160424-A1; CN115038508-A
TI Using gaseous effluent containing carbon dioxide            gas fraction and optional non-carbon dioxide gas            fraction by contacting gaseous effluent with liquid            nitrogen to capture carbon dioxide as mixture and            contacting with wet concrete ingredients.
AU JARRY L
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202195664F
AB    NOVELTY - A gaseous effluent containing carbon dioxide                (CO2) gas fraction and optionally a non-CO2 gas                fraction, is used by (a) providing liquid nitrogen                at not greater than - 196 degrees C at a 1st                location and causing gaseous effluent to contact                liquid nitrogen to capture a portion of CO2 present                in the CO2 gas fraction as a mixture of CO2                particles and liquid nitrogen, (b) conveying a                portion of the mixture to a 2nd location, and (c)                bringing the mixture into contact with greater than                or equal to 1 ingredients of a wet concrete at the                2nd location, before and/or during and/or after the                wet concrete is prepared by blending the                ingredients of the wet concrete in a blender, so                that the mixture extracts heat from the ingredients                of the wet concrete, and CO2 from the mixture                partially carbonates calcium-compounds present in                the wet concrete.
   USE - Method of use of gaseous effluent containing                carbon dioxide (CO2) gas fraction and optionally a                non-CO2 gas fraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method makes it possible with a single                intervention to achieve effective heat extraction                together with beneficial partial carbonation. At                the same time, detrimental excessive carbonation is                avoided. The method is entirely compatible with                known technologies where combustion of fuel with an                oxidant is used to heat an installation, where hot                CO2-containing combustion gasses (gaseous effluent)                are evacuated from the installation and where the                energy efficiency of the installation is improved                in that thermal energy is recovered from the                evacuated combustion gasses and reintroduced into                the installation, in particular in that the                recovered thermal energy is used to heat the fuel,                the oxidant, and/or a charge before it is                introduced into the installation. The method                improves energy efficiency by heat recovery and                improved efficiency CO2 capture during step (a) and                can provide additional cooling of the liquid                nitrogen and/or of the mixture in order to prevent                a temperature increase and/or partial evaporation                of the liquid nitrogen. When partial evaporation of                the liquid nitrogen occurs, it is possible to                recondense the evaporated liquid nitrogen, and                possibly recycle the same.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: At the 2nd location,                  the mixture is brought into contact with the wet                  concrete after the blending step, preferably                  during agitation of the wet concrete. The mixture                  is brought into contact with the wet concrete                  during agitation of the wet concrete in the                  blender or in a mixer to which the wet concrete                  prepared in the blender is transferred. Gaseous                  effluent is subjected to a cooling step before it                  contacts the liquid nitrogen. Thermal energy is                  recovered from the gaseous effluent during                  cooling step, where a portion of the recovered                  thermal energy is used as a heat source in a                  heating process, as an energy source for the                  production of mechanical energy or as an energy                  source for the production or electrical energy.                  Gaseous effluent is caused to contact the liquid                  nitrogen until the mixture presents a                  CO2-particle content which is equal to or greater                  than a predetermined minimum CO2-particle                  content. CO2-particle content of a portion of the                  mixture is adjusted before the portion of the                  mixture is brought into contact with the wet                  concrete. Conveying of the mixture includes                  transport of the mixture in a mobile cryogenic                  reservoir. The mixture is brought into contact                  with the ingredients of the wet concrete in a                  mixer, preferably in the blender in which the wet                  concrete is prepared and/or in a mixer to which                  the wet concrete prepared in the blender is                  transferred. A portion of the liquid nitrogen                  evaporates during step (a), where the evaporated                  nitrogen is utilized at the 1st location. Step                  (a) is performed at multiple 1st locations;                  mixtures obtained at multiple 1st locations are                  conveyed to a 3rd location, where mixtures are                  combined to form a final mixture of                  solid-CO2-particles and liquid nitrogen; the                  final mixture is transported to a mixer in which                  wet concrete is mixed; and the final mixture is                  introduced into the mixer and brought into                  contact with the wet concrete in the mixer,                  causing the wet concrete to be cooled and                  partially carbonated. Preferred Compositions: CO2                  gas fraction constitutes 7-100 vol.%, preferably                  25-60 vol.% of the gaseous effluent. Gaseous                  effluent comprises or consists of combustion                  gases. Preferred Device: Mixer is a rotating drum                  mixer of a concrete mixer truck. Preferred                  Location: The 2nd location is situated at a site                  where the wet concrete is prepared in the                  blender, at a site where the wet concrete is                  cast, preferably a construction site, a site                  where the prepared wet concrete is introduced                  into a mixer, preferably the rotating drum mixer                  of a truck, and/or at an intermediate site                  between a site where the wet concrete is prepared                  and a site where the wet concrete is cast.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

PT P
PN CN111789283-A
TI Multi-stage utilization of tobacco stems by            subjecting tobacco stem raw material to supercritical            carbon dioxide extraction and formulating primary            extract into tobacco pigment and secondary extract into            tobacco aroma substances.
AU YU S
   WEN S
   LI P
   YANG X
   TONG Y
AE CHINA TOBACCO HUBEI IND LLC (CNTA-C)
   HUBEI XINYE RECONSTITUTED TOBACCO DEV CO (CNTA-C)
GA 2020A6454U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for multi-stage utilization of tobacco                stems involves cutting tobacco stem raw material,                subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction, collecting the extracted tobacco stem                raw materials from the extraction kettle, primary                extract from the primary separation kettle and                secondary extract from the secondary separation                kettle, extracting, beating and papermaking tobacco                stem raw material to prepare a substrate,                formulating primary extract to obtain tobacco                pigment, adding glycerol to the secondary extract,                evenly stirring the mixture and performing                molecular distillation separation to obtain tobacco                aroma substances.
   USE - The method is useful for multi-stage                utilization of tobacco stems.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares treated tobacco stems                which have a loose structure, are more conducive to                the later fiber splitting, reduces energy                consumption and improves fiber utilization. The                method prepares primary extract which is rich in                tobacco pigments and improves coloring effect and                appearance quality of reconstituted tobacco                product. The method prepares aroma component which                retains aroma characteristics as tobacco and                improves sensory quality of the reconstituted                tobacco product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for multi-stage utilization of tobacco                stems based on supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction technology involves (a) cutting the                tobacco stem raw material into long sections and                removing the debris by winnowing and using impurity                removal device to obtain the tobacco stalk section,                (b) adding the tobacco stem section into an                extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon                dioxide extractor, (c) adding entrainer into the                entrainer tank, (d) starting the carbon dioxide                refrigeration and circulation system, when the                system reaches the extraction parameter setting                value, turning on the entrainer peristaltic pump,                injecting the entrainer and continually extracting,                (e) collecting the extracted tobacco stem raw                materials from the extraction kettle, primary                extract from the primary separation kettle and                secondary extract from the secondary separation                kettle, (f) extracting, beating and papermaking the                tobacco stem raw material to prepare a substrate,                (g) formulating the primary extract to obtain                tobacco pigment, (h) adding glycerol to the                secondary extract and evenly stirring the mixture                to obtain a pretreatment liquid and (i) performing                molecular distillation separation on the                pretreatment liquid and collecting the light                components of molecular distillation to obtain                tobacco aroma substances.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  cutting the tobacco stem raw material into 2-6 cm                  long sections. The step (c) involves adding 3-18                  %mass entrainer, which is 2-8% ethyl acetate. The                  step (d) involves controlling extraction kettle                  pressure at 20-35 MPa and temperature at 40-60                  degrees C, primary separation vessel analytical                  pressure at 4-8 MPa and temperature at 25-50                  degrees C and secondary separation vessel                  analytical pressure at 1.5-4 MPa and temperature                  at 10-30 degrees C and extracting for 60-240                  minutes. The step (g) involves adding 75% ethanol                  solution to the primary extract, evenly stirring                  the mixture, allowing to stand for 24 hours at -4                  degrees C to -2 degrees C, filtering the                  supernatant with 0.8 mu m filter membrane,                  concentrating the filtrate under reduced pressure                  until there was no obvious taste of ethanol and                  obtaining the pigment, where the mass ratio of                  the primary extract to 75% ethanol is 1:3-6. In                  step (h), the mass ratio of the secondary extract                  and glycerol is 1:2-4. The step (i) involves                  performing molecular distillation at vacuum                  degree of less than or equal to 10 tor, scraper                  speed of 30-60 revolutions per minute (rpm),                  distillation temperature of 35-50 degrees C,                  condensation temperature of -10 degrees C to -5                  degrees C and distillation speed of 0.5-1                  kg/hour.
DC D18 (Skins, hides, pelts, leather and chemical treatment of tobacco.); P15 (Tobacco (A24).)
MC D07-C; D07-D; P15-L05
IP A24B-015/24; A24B-003/14

TI Refrigerant used to cool device and test chamber for conditioning air comprises cooling circuit comprising heat exchanger, refrigerant mixture, mass fraction of carbon dioxide and fraction of pentafluoroethane and mass fraction of component.
AU AYDIN M
   ZAHRT Y
   BLAUFELDER D
   HAACK C
AE WEISS UMWELTTECHNIK GMBH (WEIS-Non-standard)
   WEISS UMWELTTECHNIK GMBH (WEIS-Non-standard)
   WEISS UMWELTTECHNIK GMBH (WEIS-Non-standard)
   VICE UMBERTOTECHNIC GMBH (VICE-Non-standard)
GA 2020691614
AB    NOVELTY - Refrigerant comprises a cooling circuit (11) comprising at least one heat exchanger (12) in which the refrigerant undergoes a phase transition, the refrigerant being a refrigerant mixture composed of a mass fraction of carbon dioxide, a mass fraction of pentafluoroethane, and a mass fraction of at least one other component, where the mass fraction of carbon dioxide in the refrigerant mixture is 20-74 wt.%, the mass fraction of pentafluoroethane is 8-65 wt.%, the other component being fluoromethane.
   USE - The refrigerant is useful in cooling device and test chamber for conditioning air (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerant: has improved flame-retardant effect; improved temperature stability, thus improving zeotropic refrigerant; and have relatively low carbon dioxide equivalent i.e. relative global warming potential as low as possible in order to avoid indirect damage to the environment in case refrigerant is released.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) test chamber, comprising a test space which serves to receive test material and which can be sealed against an environment and is temperature-insulated, and a temperature control device for controlling the temperature of the test space, a temperature in a temperature of -70-180 degrees C is establishable within the test space by means of the temperature control device, the temperature control device having a cooling device (10) comprising a cooling circuit with the refrigerant, a heat exchanger, a compressor (13), a condenser (14) and an expansion element; and
   (2) use of a refrigerant consisting the refrigerant mixture composed of a mass fraction of 20-74 wt.% carbon dioxide, 8-65 wt.% a mass fraction of pentafluoroethane and a mass fraction of at least one other component comprising fluoromethane for conditioning air in a test space of a test chamber, the test space serving to receive test material and being sealed against an environment and temperature-insulated, a cooling device of a temperature control device of the test chamber being used to establish a temperature in a temperature of 60-180 degrees C, within the test space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic cross-sectional view of the cooling device.
   Cooling device (10)
   Cooling circuit (11)
   Heat exchanger (12)
   Compressor (13)
   Condenser (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass fraction of carbon dioxide is 28-51wt.%. The mass fraction of pentafluoroethane is 49-57 wt.%. The refrigerant mixture comprises 7-25 wt.% mass fraction of fluoromethane, 13-21 wt.%, preferably15-19 wt.% mass fraction of fluoromethane, 26-34 wt.% mass fraction of carbon dioxide. The mass fraction of pentafluoroethane is 51-55 wt.%. The refrigerant has a temperature glide of 4-16 K. The refrigerant has a relative carbon dioxide equivalent of less-than 2300 over 100 years. The refrigerant is nonflammable. The cooling circuit has an internal heat exchanger, the internal heat exchanger being connected to a high-pressure side of the cooling circuit upstream of the expansion element and downstream of the condenser and to a low-pressure side of the cooling circuit upstream of the compressor and downstream of the heat exchanger. The heat exchanger is dimensioned in a manner that only part of the refrigerant can evaporate in the heat exchanger. The condenser is realized as a cascade heat exchanger of another cooling circuit of the cooling device. The temperature control device has a heating device comprising a heater and a heating heat exchanger in the test space. The first bypass having at least one controllable second expansion element is realized in the cooling circuit, the first bypass being connected to the cooling circuit upstream of the internal heat exchanger and downstream of the condenser, the first bypass being realized as a controllable additional internal cooling system. The internal heat exchanger of the cooling circuit connected to a high-pressure side of the cooling circuit upstream of the expansion element and downstream of the condenser and to a low-pressure side of the cooling circuit upstream of the compressor and downstream of the heat exchanger is used to cool the refrigerant of the high-pressure side, the cooling of the refrigerant of the high-pressure side by means of the internal heat exchanger being exploited to lower an evaporation temperature at the expansion element. The refrigerant of the high-pressure side is cooled by the refrigerant of the low-pressure side at constant suction pressure on the low-pressure side by means of the internal heat exchanger.

PN WO2020148378-A1; AU2020208917-A1; CN113677653-A
TI Preparing methanol synthesis gas comprises e.g. providing first feed stream having hydrocarbon and second feed stream, introducing first and second stream into two-step steam reforming stage having steam reforming and autothermal reforming.
AU DAHL P J
   ROSTRUP-NIELSEN T
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202068987Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methanol synthesis gas comprises: (a) providing a first feed stream comprising hydrocarbon and steam; (b) providing a second feed stream comprising carbon dioxide in amount to result in a carbon dioxide/hydrocarbon ratio is 0.1-0.8, preferably 0.15-0.7 if the second feed stream is mixed with the first stream; (d) introducing the first and second stream into a two-step steam reforming stage comprising a steam reforming step and an autothermal reforming step; (e) withdrawing a first synthesis gas stream; (f) providing a third feed stream comprising hydrogen; and (g) applying the first synthesis gas stream together with the third feed stream as methanol synthesis gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methanol synthesis gas and revamping existing the methanol synthesis gas plant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide introduced together with hydrocarbon feed stream into a two-step reforming stage; reduces energy consumption and reduced carbon dioxide footprint; allows to generate a more reactive synthesis gas for methanol production than the typical gas gendered by two step reforming having a module close to 2; has higher reactivity makes the synthesis reaction proceed faster and thus less reactor volume and catalysts used; and increases production capacity of existing methanol plants, thus improving possibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for revamping an existing the methanol synthesis gas plant, comprising adding a steam reformer section and/or an electrolysis unit and/or a pipeline connected to an external hydrogen source in parallel to an existing two-step reforming section; connecting the existing two step steam reforming section and optionally the added steam reformer section and/or electrolysis unit and/or pipeline connected to an external hydrogen source, to a pipeline connected to a carbon dioxide source and; and connecting an outlet line of the existing two-step reforming section and an outlet line of the added steam reformer and/or electrolysis unit and/or pipeline hydrogen source to the methanol synthesis plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first and a part or all of second feed stream are mixed upstream the steam reforming step in step (d). The part or the entire second stream is added to the reformed first stream upstream the autothermal reforming step. The third feed stream comprising hydrogen is provided by (a1) steam reforming of a stream comprising hydrocarbon and steam arranged in parallel with the two step reforming process; (b1) electrolysis of steam or water; and (c1) an external hydrogen source. In the method, further stream comprises: carbon dioxide is added in (a1) to the stream comprising hydrocarbon upstream the steam reforming; carbon dioxide is added to the third stream comprising hydrogen. The first synthesis gas has a module (M1) is less-than 2, preferably greater-than 1.4 to less-than 1.9, where M1 is (dihydrogen-carbon dioxide )/(carbon monoxide + carbon dioxide) . The module M1 in the third stream comprises hydrogen is larger than the module M1 in the first synthesis gas. The module M1 of the third stream comprising hydrogen and optional added carbon dioxide is greater-than 2. The mixture of the first synthesis gas stream and the third stream with optional carbon dioxide added has a module M1 is 1.8-2.3. The carbon dioxide in the second feed stream or the further stream comprising carbon dioxide added to the third feed stream is obtained from flue or off-gas. The steam reforming process is performed in a tubular re former, a bayonet tube reformer or a convective reformer. The first synthesis gas stream and the third stream with optional added carbon dioxide is in a further step converted to a methanol product.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
