PN US2020023149-A1
TI Portable rebreathing system for enriched oxygen breathing, has rebreathe control valve that is provided for closing alternative breathing passage connected to specific ambient air port when oxygen pressure is applied in oxygen supply port.
AU TELBY O
   STOEOED J O
AE GLOBAL RESCUE INNOVATION AB (GLOB-Non-standard)
GA 202009026W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a common valve housing (X) that is connected with a mask connector (4c) to the breathing mask (4). A carbon dioxide scrubber (3,31) is connected with a scrubber connector (3a) to the common valve housing. A counter lung (2) is connected with a counter lung connector (2a) to the carbon dioxide scrubber. An oxygen supply port (5) and an ambient air port are arranged in the common valve housing. The mask connector is connected to a primary chamber in the common valve housing with a total dead volume of less than 10 centiliters. The primary chamber in turn is connected to a first exhale passage containing carbon dioxide scrubber via a first one way check valve (6u) and a second inhale passage containing counter lung via a second one way check valve (6i). A rebreathe control valve is provided for closing the alternative breathing passage connected to the second ambient air port (7b) when oxygen pressure is applied in the oxygen supply port.
   USE - Portable rebreathing system for enriched oxygen breathing for miners or people caught in toxic fumes.
   ADVANTAGE - The development with improved functionality that minimizes the dead volume of exhaled carbon dioxide (CO2) rich air that is inhaled and minimizes the necessary addition of oxygen from the oxygen source and could establish a higher concentration of oxygen in the inhalation flow at need. The effective operational time is increased for the portable rebreathing equipment and reduces weight and volume of the pressurized oxygen source. The overall size of the conventional oxygen treatment system is to a large extent also dependent of the large pressurized oxygen tank, that needs a large portion of the limited storage space in the rescue vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of the oxygen enrichment mode.
   Counter lung (2)
   Counter lung connector (2a)
   Carbon dioxide scrubber (3,31)
   Scrubber connector (3a)
   Breathing mask (4)
   Mask connector (4c)
   Oxygen supply port (5)
   One way check valves (6u,6i)
   Air port (7b)
   Common valve housing (X)

PN CN108754523-A; CN108754523-B
TI Electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide based on synergistic action of a covalent organic framework-metal interface comprises e.g. reducing activated covalent organic framework-300 in a sodium borohydride-methanol system and filtering.
AU DENG H
   ZHUANG L
   LIU H
   CAI X
   YIN Z
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 201888517W
AB    NOVELTY - Electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide based on synergistic action of a covalent organic framework-metal interface comprises e.g. reducing activated covalent organic framework-300 in a sodium borohydride-methanol system, filtering, washing solid with methanol and hot water, activating with ethanol in a Soxhlet extractor, degassing at room temperature to obtain a covalent organic framework material covalent organic framework-300-AR based on secondary amine linkage, mixing covalent organic framework-300-AR powder, water, ethanol and nafion solution, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain covalent organic framework-300-AR dispersion, adding covalent organic framework-300-AR dispersion onto the metal foil, drying under infrared light to obtain covalent organic framework-metal electrode, using covalent organic framework-metal electrode as the working electrode, carbon cloth electrode as the counter electrode and saturated calomel electrode as a reference electrode.
   USE - The method is useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide based on synergistic action of a covalent organic framework-metal interface.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improve the selective diffusion to the surface of the electrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide based on synergistic action of a covalent organic framework-metal interface comprises (i) reducing activated covalent organic framework-300 in a sodium borohydride-methanol system, filtering, washing solid with methanol and hot water, activating with ethanol in a Soxhlet extractor, degassing at room temperature to obtain a covalent organic framework material covalent organic framework-300-AR based on secondary amine linkage, (ii) mixing covalent organic framework-300-AR powder, water, ethanol and nafion solution, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain covalent organic framework-300-AR dispersion, adding covalent organic framework-300-AR dispersion onto the metal foil, drying under infrared light to obtain covalent organic framework-metal electrode, (iii) using covalent organic framework-metal electrode as the working electrode, carbon cloth electrode as the counter electrode, saturated calomel electrode as a reference electrode, nafion membrane as a cation exchange membrane in the separation tank, adding freshly prepared potassium bicarbonate solution as electrolyte, opening the carbon dioxide gas path to ventilate to saturation in the electrolyte and then carrying out constant voltage electrolysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), adding 1 equivalent of terephthalic acid, activated covalent organic framework-300 powder containing 1 equivalent of imine bond into methanol, mixing, adding not less than 38 equivalents of sodium borohydride powder, stirring at -25 to -5 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, stirring at room temperature, suction filtering, washing solid crude product with methanol and hot water, removing by-products and catalyst to obtain pale yellow powder, activating light yellow powder with ethanol in a Soxhlet extractor and then degassing at room temperature to obtain final product. Preferred Components: In step (ii), volume ratio between water and ethanol is 3:1. In step (ii), concentration of Nafion in covalent organic framework-300-AR dispersion is 0.2%, and concentration of covalent organic framework-300-AR in covalent organic framework-300-AR dispersion is 5 g/l. The metal foil is a silver foil. The thickness of the covalent organic framework-300-AR layer on the covalent organic framework-metal electrode is 10-20 mu m, preferably 15 mu m. In step (iii), concentration of the potassium hydrogencarbonate solution is 0.1 M.

PT P
PN CN107597119-A; CN107597119-B
TI Anti-deposition carbon-type cobalt-based            low-temperature methane carbon dioxide reforming            catalyst is prepared by taking cobalt nanoparticles as            active phase, magnesium aluminum oxide as carrier and            zirconium dioxide as promoter.
AU LI D
   XU S
   SONG K
   WAN C
   SHI Z
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
   UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 201807453S
AB    NOVELTY - An anti-deposition carbon-type cobalt-based                low-temperature methane carbon dioxide reforming                catalyst is prepared by taking cobalt nanoparticles                as active phase, magnesium aluminum oxide (Mg(Al)O)                as carrier, and zirconium dioxide as promoter. The                mass percentage of cobalt in catalyst is 12%. The                molar ratio of (cobalt and magnesium)/(aluminum and                zirconium) is 3. The molar ratio of                zirconium/(aluminum and zirconium) is                0.025-0.1.
   USE - Anti-deposition carbon-type cobalt-based                low-temperature methane carbon dioxide reforming                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-deposition carbon-type cobalt-based                low-temperature methane carbon dioxide reforming                catalyst has excellent catalytic activity and                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of anti-deposition carbon-type                cobalt-based low-temperature methane carbon dioxide                reforming catalyst, which involves initially                synthesizing cobalt-magnesium-aluminum zirconium                hydrotalcite-like compounds as precursors by                coprecipitation, then calcining to obtain                magnesium(cobalt, aluminum)oxide-zirconium dioxide,                and then reducing to obtain a catalyst by                hydrogenation, where the specific steps involves:                (1) uniformly mixing cobalt nitrate hexahydrate,                magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate                nonahydrate and zirconium(IV) nitrate pentahydrate                under stirring at a speed of 800 rpm to obtain                mixed solution, adding the mixed solution to sodium                carbonate solution at a speed of 30 drops per                minute by dropping funnel, simultaneously dripping                sodium hydroxide solution into sodium carbonate                solution at a speed of 35 drops per minute,                carrying out the whole precipitation process at                room temperature, maintaining pH-value at 10 plus                minus 0.5, continuing stirring for 1 hour after                dripping is completed, leaving still for 24 hours,                filtering and washing with deionized water until                the pH-value is 7 plus minus 0.2, drying at 100                degrees C for 12 hours to obtain                cobalt-magnesium-aluminum zirconium                hydrotalcite-like compounds; and (2) putting                cobalt-magnesium-aluminum zirconium                hydrotalcite-like compounds as precursors obtained                in step (1) into a muffle furnace, roasting to                obtain mixed oxide of magnesium(cobalt,                aluminum)oxide and zirconium dioxide, recording                mixed oxide as magnesium(cobalt,                aluminum)oxide-zirconium dioxide, and then reducing                with hydrogen to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The roasting step in                  step (2) is carried out at a heating rate of 3                  degrees C/minute to 800 degrees C for 5 hours                  under air atmosphere. Preferred Components: The                  concentration of sodium hydroxide solution in                  step (1) is 2 mole/l.

PT P
PN JP2021066646-A
TI Carbon dioxide from exhaust gas is effectively used in agriculture by injecting mixed aqueous solution of water and aqueous solution of slaked lime into liquid injection chamber and reacting with carbon dioxide accompanying combustion.
AU HIRAMATSU K
AE HIRAMATSU K (HIRA-Individual)
GA 202151300N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide taken in from exhaust gas is effectively used in agriculture by producing mixed aqueous solution of water and aqueous solution by placing aqueous solution of slaked lime in water tank containing water; in the state where exhaust gas is discharged from chimney upper portion during boiler operation, injecting mixed aqueous solution into a mist through injection nozzle into liquid injection chamber interior provided at intermediate point between chimney and boiler housing; and reacting carbon dioxide accompanying combustion with injected mixed aqueous solution so that carbon dioxide is taken in, and calcium bicarbonate increases. These actions indicate that carbon dioxide from exhaust gas is taken into the liquid without being taken into the aqueous solution of slaked lime, but rather, the carbon dioxide is taken into the mixed aqueous solution of the aqueous solution and water. Ratio of water with respect to aqueous solution of slaked lime is 8 to 2.
   USE - Effective use of carbon dioxide taken in from exhaust gas in agriculture for improving productivity within a house by active photosynthetic activities (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas accompanying combustion is taken in into a liquid; and the plant currently grown by carrying in into a greenhouse the liquid containing the carbon dioxide, is taken in by a photosynthetic activity, and oxygen is discharged. The method solves the problem of global warming by taking in carbon dioxide emitted from factories by using photosynthesis of plants and releasing oxygen at the same time to obtain the effect of suppressing carbon dioxide released into the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) use of carbon dioxide taken in from exhaust gas in agriculture, in the greenhouse cultivation which produces tomato, eggplant, cucumber, etc., which involves discharging carbon dioxide in a house for the purpose of the improvement of productivity, taking out mixed aqueous solution from the water tank, and spraying into a greenhouse, where carbon dioxide is discharged first by heating within a greenhouse the mixed aqueous solution containing calcium bicarbonate using electricity and utilized for the photosynthesis by a plant by diffusing the generated carbon dioxide in a house using an air blower; and
   (2) improving productivity within a house which involves suppressing amount of carbon dioxide discharged to the atmosphere by implementing effective use method of carbon dioxide taken in from exhaust gas into a mixed aqueous solution, where photosynthesis of the generation plant becomes active by carrying out superheat discharge of the mixed aqueous solution containing calcium bicarbonate into a greenhouse, and consequentially, more oxygen is discharged.

PN CN112029576-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of sea buckthorn seed oil comprises e.g. preparing raw materials, selecting large, thick and oily sea buckthorn seeds as raw materials for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, drying, and cooling.
AU SONG D
AE WUHU MARTIN BAUER TEA & BOTANICALS CO (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C5351C
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of sea buckthorn seed oil comprises e.g. preparing raw materials, selecting large, thick and oily sea buckthorn seeds as raw materials for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and drying and cooling, crushing raw materials, feeding the selected sea buckthorn seeds into the crusher for crushing, and the crushing particle size is 40 mesh cleaning sieve, performing raw material wall breaking treatment, adding additional field to perform cell wall breaking treatment on broken sea buckthorn seeds during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and feeding process, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology extraction, layering sea buckthorn seeds of broken wall treatment and packing into the extraction kettle for extraction, carbon dioxide flow rate of 25 kg/hour, extraction time is 2 hours, circulating supercritical fluid after the extraction of carbon dioxide into the separator through the expansion valve.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of sea buckthorn seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem of rapid, full and efficient contact extraction between carbon dioxide and active ingredients during supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of sea buckthorn seed oil comprises (1) preparing raw materials, selecting large, thick and oily sea buckthorn seeds as raw materials for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and drying and cooling, (2) crushing raw materials, feeding the selected sea buckthorn seeds into the crusher for crushing, and the crushing particle size is 40 mesh cleaning sieve, (3) performing raw material wall breaking treatment, adding additional field to perform cell wall breaking treatment on broken sea buckthorn seeds during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and feeding process, (4) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology extraction, layering sea buckthorn seeds of broken wall treatment and packing into the extraction kettle for extraction, extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa, extraction temperature of 40-50 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate of 25 kg/hour, extraction time is 2 hours, circulating supercritical fluid after the extraction of carbon dioxide into the separator through the expansion valve, separating in the separator by the separation method of isothermal and depressurization, the separation temperature is 40-50 degrees C, the separation pressure is 8 MPa, and the carbon dioxide changes again after decompression, recycling into gas to obtain crude oil extracted from sea buckthorn seeds, (5) refining and purifying the extract, sending the crude sea buckthorn seed oil into the supercritical fluid chromatography system through the sampling pump for further separation and purification, and (6) collecting the fine products of sea buckthorn seeds, detecting different components of the mixture by the ultraviolet detector in the supercritical fluid chromatography system, the control system automatically collects the components in the collector, depressurizing in the separator, and collecting sea buckthorn seeds in the separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), drying raw materials of sea buckthorn seeds in a vacuum drying oven, and the moisture content is 3-5% after drying, and cooling to room temperature through a cooling chamber. In the step (3), the additional external field is an ultrasonic field, the ultrasonic power is 300 W, the ultrasonic is 10 minutes, and the ultrasonic is a sound wave with a frequency range of 20-10 MHz. In the step (3), the additional external field is an electric field, and the electric field is an electrostatic field, the electric field intensity is 500 kv/m, and the treatment is 5 minutes. In the step (4), the thickness of each layer of the layered material is about 2 mm, and keeping a gap between two adjacent layers of materials. In the step (5), the supercritical fluid chromatography system is a supercritical fluid chromatographic separation and purification device, and refining different supercritical extracts by using different chromatographic stationary phases and regulators.

PN CN114106897-A
TI Coupling system for producing methane from carbon dioxide and producing liquefied natural gas from natural gas, includes natural gas drying and liquefaction device outputs flash steam in one way, and liquefied natural gas in other way.
AU TANG Y
   JIAN X
   ZHANG W
AE CHENGDU ZEJIA HUAFEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU BAOAILUO ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202243218H
AB    NOVELTY - Coupling system includes a raw material natural gas compressor (1), a natural gas decarbonization absorption tower (2), a natural gas drying and liquefaction device (3), a natural gas decarbonization regeneration tower (4), a carbon dioxide compressor (5), a carbon dioxide purification device, and a methanation device, natural gas drying and liquefaction devices are connected in sequence. The natural gas drying and liquefaction device outputs flash steam in one way, and liquefied natural gas (LNG) in the other way. The natural gas decarbonization regeneration tower and the natural gas decarbonization absorption tower are used to absorb carbon dioxide.The outlet end of the methanation device is connected between the raw natural gas compressor and the natural gas decarbonization absorption tower for providing methane produced from the recovered carbon dioxide.
   USE - Coupling system for producing methane from carbon dioxide and producing LNG from natural gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The coupling system uses green electricity to produce hydrogen by electrolysis or externally supplied green hydrogen and reacts with carbon dioxide to generate methane, which is coupled with natural gas liquefaction, and the heat generated by methanation is used for natural gas liquefaction, and saves natural gas consumption of natural gas liquefaction fuel and increases LNG output.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a coupling process for producing methane from carbon dioxide and producing LNG from natural gas, which involves compressing raw natural gas by the raw natural gas compressor, mixing with the methane gas from methanation, entering the natural gas decarbonization absorption tower, absorbing carbon dioxide in the natural gas by the regenerated methyl diethanolamine (MEDA) solution, and the MEDA solution that absorbs the carbon dioxide enters the natural gas decarbonization regeneration tower, removing regenerated carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide compressor, and recycling regenerated MEDA solution to the absorption tower, natural gas after the carbon dioxide is removed enters the natural gas drying and liquefaction unit to produce LNG products, mixing natural gas decarburization regeneration tower with the flash steam and then entering the carbon dioxide compressor for compression, and the compressed gas enters the carbon dioxide purification device to remove the sulfide in the carbon dioxide to below 0.05 parts per million, passing through the methanation unit, generating methane gas and sending to the natural gas decarbonization absorption tower.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a connection diagram provides a carbon dioxide to prepare methane and natural gas LNG coupling. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Raw material natural gas compressor (1)
   Natural gas decarbonization absorption tower (2)
   Natural gas drying and liquefaction device (3)
   Natural gas decarbonization regeneration tower (4)
   Carbon dioxide compressor (5)

PN FR3064260-A1; WO2018172698-A1; CN108622898-A; CN208361896-U; FR3064260-B1
TI Method for separating gaseous mixture of carbon            dioxide and less volatile component, involves feeding            portion of condensed gas or portion of carbon dioxide            enriched liquid at point upstream of phase            separator.
AU GAERTNER A
   GRANADOS L
   PRASAD B
   TOVAR R J E
   TRANIER J
   TOVAR RAMOS J E
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201875196W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling and expanding the                gaseous mixture (3) to be separated, to form an                expanded flow which is sent into a phase separator                (9), to form a first liquid (11) enriched with a                less volatile component and a first gas (13)                enriched with carbon dioxide. A portion of the                enriched liquid of less volatile component is sent                to a distillation column (15). A second gas (27)                enriched with carbon dioxide is withdrawn from the                top of the column and a second liquid (17) is                enriched with the less volatile component of the                column vessel. A portion of the first gas and/or                the second gas enriched in carbon dioxide and/or                carbon dioxide-enriched liquid is condensed from                the top of the column. A portion of condensed gas                (43) and/or a portion of the carbon dioxide                enriched liquid is transmitted at a point upstream                of the phase separator.
   USE - Method for separating gaseous mixture of                carbon dioxide and less volatile component less                volatile than carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The gaseous mixture of carbon dioxide and less                volatile component is separated efficiently by the                separating apparatus. The recovery of carbon                dioxide is maximized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The portion of the condensed gas and/or                portion of the liquid is recycled to a reactor from                which the gas mixture originates. A portion of the                second enriched liquid is recycled to a less                volatile component to the reactor from which the                gas mixture originates. The second liquid enriched                with a less volatile component is separated into a                gas depleted of less volatile component and a                liquid product containing a less volatile                component. The condensed depleted gas is returned                to the phase separator. The gas is not withdrawn                from the phase separator. The liquid or condensed                gas is pressurized in a pump. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for an apparatus for separating gaseous                mixture of carbon dioxide and component less                volatile than carbon dioxide, has a cooling device                for cooling the gaseous mixture. A valve for the                cooled gaseous mixture is expanded to form a                relaxed two-phase flow. A distillation column is                connected to a conduit provided for withdrawing                carbon dioxide-enriched gas from the phase                separator. The gaseous mixture is produced in the                reactor fed with carbon dioxide. The portion of the                condensed gas is fed into the condenser and the                portion of the carbon dioxide enriched liquid is                fed at point upstream of the phase separator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating the process for separating gaseous                mixture of carbon dioxide and component less                volatile than carbon dioxide.

AU LIU X

   YUAN H
   CUI Y
   MU L
   YUAN W
   DONG B
   LIU P
   LIU Y
AE CHINA NAT CHEM ENG HEAVY-MECHANIZED CORP (CHNA-Non-standard)
   UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 2021A2671L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drilling a blast hole with predetermined number and predetermined depth at a site to be blasted. A certain amount of liquid carbon dioxide is filled to a carbon dioxide cracking pipe. A prepared carbon dioxide cracking pipe is placed in the blast hole. A certain amount of explosive is filled in a blast hole blocking cylinder according to a rock physical construction condition of a current-to-be-blasting ground. The carbon dioxide cracking pipe is initiated after interval of 50-100 millisecond. The blocking cylinder and the carbon dioxide cracking pipe are recycled after blasting.
   USE - Liquid carbon dioxide induced cracking tube differential delay control blasting method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recycling and recycling the blocking cylinder without waiting for drying the concrete when using the blocking cylinder so as to improve flexibility of construction and working efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the liquid carbon dioxide induced cracking tube differential delay control blasting method (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   USE - Current collector in the form of a plate used                in a carbon dioxide reduction device, which is used                for reducing carbon dioxide (all claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The opening for                  accommodating the electrode layer for carbon                  dioxide reduction reaction is formed in the flat                  surface. The material of the current collector                  includes copper, nickel, aluminum, platinum,                  palladium, silver, gold, and alloys. The                  reduction electrode is a gas diffusion electrode                  comprising a conductive substrate and a catalyst                  layer formed on the conductive substrate. The                  reduction electrode is a gas diffusion electrode                  comprising a conductive substrate, a metal thin                  film layer coated on the conductive substrate,                  and tin catalyst fine particles formed on the                  metal thin film layer. The catalyst layer or the                  metal thin film layer includes tin, palladium,                  mercury, gold, silver, nickel, copper, cadmium,                  lead and indium. The gas diffusion electrode has                  a catalyst formed on the conductive substrate                  without the use of a binder. The metal thin film                  layer is formed by electron beam irradiation                  treatment. The catholyte having a buffer salt                  concentration of 0.05-5 moles is used for                  reducing carbon dioxide. Preferred Conditions:                  The tin catalyst fine particles are formed by                  electrodeposition at a temperature of 0-55                  degrees C.

PN CN112553002-A
TI Preparation of rose essence embedded spice involves mixing rose essential oil, N-alkylimidazole and water to prepare mixed solution, and mixing mixed solution and carbon dioxide to form aggregate solution.
AU SHI Q
   ZHANG Q
   HUA C
   XU X
   HU J
   MAO J
   CHAI G
   FAN W
   SONG Y
   LIU J
   QU Z
   ZONG Y
AE ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
GA 2021346262
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of rose essence embedded spice involves mixing rose essential oil, N-alkylimidazole and water to prepare a mixed solution, and mixing the mixed solution and carbon dioxide to form an aggregate solution. The alkyl in the N-alkylimidazole is a straight-chain 10-12C alkyl.
   USE - Preparation of rose essence embedded spice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides stable aggregate solution by utilizing sensitivity of N-alkylimidazole bicarbonate to carbon dioxide in the water system and reverse conversion of N-alkylimidazole bicarbonate into N-alkylimidazole. N-alkylimidazole has no surface activity so as to control release of the embedding system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for controllable release of rose essence by carbon dioxide, which involves using carbon dioxide lean gas to destroying the formed aggregate solution, releasing the rose essential oil. The carbon dioxide lean gas does not contain carbon dioxide or is a low concentration carbon dioxide having a carbon dioxide concentration of not more than 20%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide lean gas is selected from one or more of nitrogen, air, and argon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In each 50 ml of water, the content of rose essential oil is 0.1-0.2 g and the content of N-alkylimidazole is 0.1-0.4 g. Preferred Process: N-alkyl imidazole is prepared by reacting beta -cyano ethylimidazole and bromoalkane in a solvent at 100-120 degrees C, removing the solvent after the reaction, adding an alkali liquid and chloroform, separating out organic phase, and post-processing. The mixing of the mixed solution and carbon dioxide involves passing carbon dioxide gas or a highly concentrated carbon dioxide mixed gas with a carbon dioxide concentration of at least 80% into the mixed solution, and/or adding dry ice to the mixed solution. Preferred Conditions: The speed of inletting carbon dioxide gas or high concentration carbon dioxide mixed gas is 0.05-0.5 L/minute. The passage time of carbon dioxide gas or high concentration carbon dioxide mixed gas is 10-60 minutes. The destroying step involves introducing carbon dioxide lean gas into the aggregate solution at a speed of 0.05-0.5 L/minute. Preferred Property: The average grain diameter of the aggregate solution is 100-300 nm.

AU KOJI T

AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 2021B2659H
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing agent is produced by reducing the                carbon dioxide by contacting with a raw material                gas containing carbon dioxide, and a composite                metal oxide (I).
   USE - The reducing agent is useful when producing                produced gas containing carbon monoxide, and used                in chemical looping method.
   ADVANTAGE - The reducing agent efficiently produces                production gas containing carbon monoxide from raw                material gas containing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing agent is produced by reducing the                carbon dioxide by contacting with a raw material                gas containing carbon dioxide, and a composite                metal oxide of formula: Ce1-x(M)xOy (I)
   M = metal element whose ionic radius is                smaller than the ionic radius of Ce having the same                valence and coordination number;
   x = positive real number;and
   y = 1-4 real number.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing a gas, involving contacting the                above-mentioned reducing agent with the raw                material gas containing carbon dioxide, where the                carbon dioxide is reduced to produce the produced                gas containing carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The difference between                  the ionic radius of cerium and ionic radius of                  the metal element (M) is 0-47 pm, when the                  valence is 3 and the coordination number is 6,                  and 0-24 pm or less, when the valence is 4 and                  the coordination number is 8. The reducing agent                  is used for reducing the carbon dioxide by                  contacting with the raw material gas containing                  carbon dioxide to produce the produced gas                  containing carbon monoxide, where the oxidized                  reducing agent is reduced by contacting with a                  reducing gas containing hydrogen, and is used in                  separate reaction steps for the reduction                  reaction of carbon dioxide and the reduction                  reaction of the oxidized reducing agent.                  Preferred Components: The composite metal oxide                  comprises oxygen ion conductivity and functions                  as an oxygen carrier. The metal element (M)                  comprises samarium, hafnium, yttrium, gadolinium,                  niobium, praseodymium, titanium, neodynium,                  europium, silver, gold, scandium and/or                  zirconium. The reducing agent comprises a carrier                  that carries the composite metal oxide, and has                  oxygen ion conductivity, and a function as an                  oxygen carrier. The carriers comprise vanadium,                  manganese, iron, titanium, molybdenum, yttrium,                  chromium, lanthanum, cobalt, nickel, copper,                  tungsten, niobium and/or cerium. Preferred                  Composition: The amount of the composite metal                  oxide is 60 parts mass or more or 50 parts mass                  or less.
   Preferred Definitions:
   x = 0.05-0.4.

PN CN108129310-A
TI Preparing ethylene glycol in parallel from ethylene oxide comprises e.g. reacting ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol and then vaporizing liquid carbon dioxide and ethylene oxide mixture.
AU SHI L
   YAO J
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 201846440P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethylene glycol in parallel from ethylene oxide comprises e.g. reacting ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol to obtain product ethylene glycol with carbonate in a molar ratio of 1:1; vaporizing liquid carbon dioxide and ethylene oxide mixture of the raw material liquid by metering pump P-1 via the vaporizer V-1, entering the bottom of the first reactor T1, preheating another mixture of the raw materials methanol and ethanol is by preheater V-2 and then adding from the top of the first reactor, reacting two raw materials in contact with each other in catalyst portion in the middle of the first reactor to obtain the first mixture, where first mixture includes carbon dioxide, ethylene oxide, methanol, ethanol, methyl ethyl carbonate, dimethyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate and/or ethylene glycol; and light components from the top of the first reactor is carbon dioxide, ethylene oxide, methanol and ethanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ethylene glycol in parallel from ethylene oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The ethylene glycol: has high efficiency; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ethylene glycol in parallel from ethylene oxide comprises reacting ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol to obtain product ethylene glycol with carbonate in a molar ratio of 1:1; vaporizing liquid carbon dioxide and ethylene oxide mixture of the raw material liquid by metering pump P-1 via the vaporizer V-1, entering the bottom of the first reactor T1, preheating another mixture of the raw materials methanol and ethanol is by preheater V-2 and then adding from the top of the first reactor, reacting two raw materials in contact with each other in catalyst portion in the middle of the first reactor to obtain the first mixture, where first mixture includes carbon dioxide, ethylene oxide, methanol, ethanol, methyl ethyl carbonate, dimethyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate and/or ethylene glycol; light components from the top of the first reactor is carbon dioxide, ethylene oxide, methanol and ethanol; and another part of the higher boiling component is dimethyl carbonate and methanol azeotrope, ethyl methyl carbonate and ethanol azeotrope and a small amount of diethyl carbonate and condensing via condenser E-1, preheating in the V-3 preheating tank, adding into the pressurized distillation column T2 by the P-2 metering pump; high boiling components from the bottom of the first reactor is glycol or diethyl carbonate; condensing by the E-2 condenser, enters the top of the pressurized rectification column T2, another part of the crude ethylene glycol directly enters the ethylene glycol batch distillation column T4 and carrying out water treatment to obtain ethylene glycol, entering the components of the pressurized rectification column T2, separating original atmospheric azeotropic composition under high pressure, where light components are methanol and dimethyl carbonate azeotrope and ethanol, returning to the T1 first reactor to participate in the reaction; and high boiling point composition is ethyl methyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate or ethylene glycol, entering the T3 atmospheric distillation column to continue separation, cooling ethyl methyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate in E-3 condenser, condensing ethylene glycol, entering the high boiling component of the preheating tank V-4 the T3 atmospheric distillation column, distilling ethyl methyl carbonate from the top of T3 and condensing by E-6 to obtain pure ethyl methyl carbonate to obtain diethyl carbonate at the bottom of the T3, condensing ethylene glycol mixture through an E-7 condenser into a T4 ethylene glycol batch distillation column, treating crude ethylene glycol with T4 ethylene glycol batch distillation column to obtain ethylene glycol, collecting diethyl carbonate from the top of the column or returned to the T1 first reactor to participate in the reaction, where catalyst is multifunctional composite alkaline material; catalyst carrier is molecular sieve having a microporous composite pore or metal elements as active components; amount of catalyst is 0.1-3% the total mass of raw material; molar ratio between raw material ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide is 0.1:1-10:1; vaporizer V1 temperature is 40-100 degrees C; pumping the liquid raw material methanol and ethanol using metering pump in a molar ratio of 1:5-5:1, preheating tank V2 temperature is 25-60 degrees C; a device for producing ethyl methyl carbonate, reacting T1 with the first reactor, the T1 first reactor has a catalyst inlet arranged at intervals from top to bottom, the top of the first reactor of the T1 reaction has a first discharge port and the bottom of the T1 reaction first reactor has a second discharge port, T2 pressurized distillation column, the pressurized rectification column has two second feed ports connected to the first discharge port, the lower portion of the tower of the pressurized rectification column has a second discharge port and the bottom of the pressurized rectification column has a third discharge port, a T3 atmospheric distillation column having the second and the atmospheric distillation column, a third feed port connected to the three discharge ports with the fourth discharge port at the top of the tower, fifth discharge port connected with the T4 ethylene glycol batch rectification column, a T4 ethylene glycol batch rectification column having a fourth feed port connected to the fifth discharge port, the top of the ethylene glycol batch distillation column has a sixth discharge port, and the lower portion has a fifth feed port and the bottom portion has a seventh discharge port, where reaction method sequence is as given in the specification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises in the first reactor T1, reacting raw material under the action of the catalyst to obtain a first mixture; the gaseous light component of the top of the first mixture is CO2 or ethylene oxide, condensing through condenser E-1 into the T1 cycle; other lower boiling liquids are methanol, ethanol and its azeotrope, ethyl methyl carbonate, etc. enters the T2 pressurized distillation column for pressure separation; light components are methanol at the top of the T2 tower ethanol and a small amount of azeotrope, combining and returning to the T1 reactor. The pressure in the column of the first reactor is 0.01-5 MPa, temperature at the top of the tower is 64-90 degrees C, bottom temperature is 130-160 degrees C, pressure in the column of the pressurized rectification column T2 is 1-15 MPa, temperature at the top of the tower is 50-70 degrees C, bottom temperature is 130-150 degrees C, pressure in the column of the atmospheric distillation column T3 is 0.1-0.5 MPa, temperature at the top of the tower is 105-125 degrees C, bottom temperature is 110-130 degrees C, pressure in the column of the ethylene glycol batch rectification column T4 is 0.1-0.5 MPa, temperature at the top of the tower is 130-160 degrees C, bottom temperature is 140-180 degrees C; condensing lower liquid in the kettle into the T2 pressurized distillation column through the E-2 condenser when the ethylene glycol content of the first reactor column of T1 reaches 20% or more and then adding bottom of the T1 reactor to the T4 ethylene glycol batch rectification column when the ethylene glycol content reaches 90% or more. The ratio between cross-sectional area of the preliminary distillation section and the cross-sectional area of the side line section is 1:1-3:1. The number of trays in the initial distillation section of the reactor is 1-30 pieces, number of trays in the side line section is 1-30 pieces, number of plates in the common rectification section is 1-30 pieces, and number of plates in the common stripping section is 1-30 pieces. The first reactor of the reactor T1, T2 pressurized distillation column, T3 atmospheric distillation column, T4 ethylene glycol batch distillation column is a plate tower, packed column or plate-filler mixing tower.

PT P
PN CN107720800-A; WO2019091259-A1; US2020361781-A1
TI Recycling carbon dioxide by carrying out            chlorination on calcium-containing silicate and/or            magnesium-containing silicate, mixing calcium chloride            and/or magnesium chloride with aqueous ammonia and            carbon dioxide and carbonizing.
AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
   YUANCHU TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (YUAN-Non-standard)
   YUANCHU TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (YUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201817192K
AB    NOVELTY - The method for recycling carbon dioxide                involves (a) carrying out chlorination on                calcium-containing silicate and/or                magnesium-containing silicate to obtain calcium                chloride and/or magnesium chloride, (b) mixing                calcium chloride and/or magnesium chloride with                aqueous ammonia and carbon dioxide and carrying out                carbonation reaction to convert carbon dioxide into                calcium carbonate and/or magnesium carbonate and                produce an ammonium chloride solution, and (c)                recovering ammonium chloride solution and carrying                out decomposition to produce hydrogen chloride                directly used for chlorination.
   USE - Method is useful for recycling carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables energy-efficient recycling                carbon dioxide with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                system for recycling carbon dioxide, which                comprises a chlorination reactor arranged for                chlorination of calcium-containing silicates and/or                magnesium-containing silicates to obtain calcium                chloride and/or magnesium chloride, a carbonation                reactor connected to the chlorination reactor for                mixing calcium chloride and/or magnesium chloride                with aqueous ammonia and carbon dioxide and                carrying out carbonation reaction to convert carbon                dioxide into calcium carbonate and/or magnesium                carbonate and simultaneously produce an ammonium                chloride solution, and a recovery device connected                between the carbonation reactor and the                chlorination reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Silica is also produced in                  the chlorination step, before the carbonation                  step. The method further involves discharging the                  silica after separation, and discharging calcium                  carbonate and/or magnesium carbonate after                  separation. The chlorination step (a) involves                  reacting calcium-containing silicate and/or                  magnesium-containing silicate with ammonium                  chloride to produce ammonia gas, and reacting                  calcium-containing silicate and/or                  magnesium-containing silicate with hydrogen                  chloride. The recovering step (c) involves                  recovering ammonium chloride solution, removing a                  portion of water for chlorination of                  calcium-containing silicate and/or                  magnesium-containing silicate, evaporating                  ammonium chloride solution to obtain water vapor                  and concentrated ammonium chloride solution used                  for chlorination.

PN CN110308261-A; CN110308261-B
TI Coal seam heating and carbon dioxide co-intensified gas drainage simulation test method, involves optimizing coal seam heating temperature and coal seam carbon dioxide pressure parameters to guide gas intensification of coal seams.

   WANG E
   LIU Z
   ZHAO E
   FENG X
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 201994459U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves monitoring concentration of each component in a gas-carbon dioxide mixed gas. Coal seam heating temperature and carbon dioxide injection pressure are changed. Analysis of conventional gas extraction efficiency is performed based on gas filling and extraction in conventional gas extraction tests. Relationship between enhanced gas drainage efficiency and coal seam heating temperature are analyzed. The carbon dioxide injection pressure is injected into coal seam based on carbon dioxide enhanced gas drainage test of coal seam injection. Relationship between enhanced gas drainage efficiency and coal seam heating temperature, and coal seam carbon dioxide pressure are analyzed. TI Ultra-critical carbon dioxide based heat cascade utilizing method, involves converging two ultra-critical carbon dioxides by middle-low temperature heat source, and returning ultra-critical carbon dioxide to temperature heat source.
AU WANG J
   HUANG Y
AE CHINA NUCLEAR POWER DESIGN INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2019030629
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves flowing ultra-critical carbon dioxide through a high temperature heat source (1) of a turbine (2), a main heat regenerator (4), a cooler (5) and a gas compressor (6). The ultra-critical carbon dioxide in a low-temperature side of the main heat regenerator is returned to the high temperature heat source to form a cycle. Two ultra-critical carbon dioxides are converged by a middle-low temperature heat source (8) after passing through a low-temperature side of the main heat regenerator. The converged ultra-critical carbon dioxide is returned to the high temperature heat source.
   USE - Ultra-critical carbon dioxide based heat cascade utilizing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing complexity of ultra-critical carbon dioxide circulation system by introducing an external heat source, improving working efficiency greater than 10%, and reducing investment cost upto 30%. The method enables avoiding various complex ultra-critical carbon circulation by an internal parameter adjusting mode, realizing easy adjusting operation so as to easily adopt operation condition for rapidly changing a load.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an ultra-critical carbon dioxide based heat cascade utilizing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of an ultra-critical carbon dioxide based heat cascade utilizing system.
   High temperature heat source (1)
   Turbine (2)
   Main heat regenerator (4)
   Cooler (5)
   Gas compressor (6)
   Middle-low temperature heat source (8)

AB    NOVELTY - The integrated method involves using a liquid cooler to control the pre-cooling temperature to cool the sand-carrying liquid and then pump it to the sand mixing tank. At the same time, the proppant is also added through the sand. The device is used to enter the sand mixing tank, and the sand liquid and proppant are mixed in the sand mixing tank. Then, the sand liquid mixture is pumped to the carbon dioxide and sand liquid mixing tank through the solid piston pump, and at the same time, the liquid carbon dioxide is pumped into the carbon dioxide and sand liquid mixing tank. The liquid carbon dioxide and the sand liquid are fully mixed by the circulation ejector and the rotary valve, and finally, the mixed liquid is pumped into the wellbore by the booster pump truck.

GA 2019A73874
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises fuel reactor having a fuel inlet configured to receive fuel from a fuel source and a carrier inlet configured to receive oxygen carriers, air reactor in fluid communication with fuel reactor, a compressor having a carbon dioxide inlet configured to receive a carbon dioxide stream from a carbon dioxide source, where the compressor is configured to increase pressure of the carbon dioxide stream and creating a high pressure supercritical carbon dioxide stream, a first heat exchanger in fluid communication with the compressor and fuel reactor, where first heat exchanger is configured to receive and heat the supercritical carbon dioxide stream, a second heat exchanger in fluid communication with the first heat exchanger and the air reactor, a second portion of the re-oxidized oxygen carrier from the air reactor, the energy from the oxygen-depleted air, where turbine is configured to receive the supercritical carbon dioxide stream from the second heat exchanger.
   USE - System for coupling a chemical looping arrangement and a supercritical carbon dioxide cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is cost-effective and energy-efficient system, and ensures combustion of sour gas with high efficiency and reduced amount of water especially in water scarce environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system comprises fuel reactor having a fuel inlet configured to receive fuel from a fuel source and a carrier inlet configured to receive oxygen carriers, where the fuel reactor is configured to react the fuel with the oxygen carriers resulting in reformed or combusted fuel and reduced oxygen carriers, an air reactor in fluid communication with the fuel reactor, where air reactor has an air stream inlet configured to receive an air stream from an air source, the air reactor is configured to receive the reduced oxygen carriers from the fuel reactor and to re-oxidize the reduced oxygen carriers via the air stream resulting in oxygen-depleted air, the air reactor is configured to transport a first portion of the re-oxidized oxygen carriers back to the fuel reactor, a compressor having a carbon dioxide inlet configured to receive a carbon dioxide stream from a carbon dioxide source, where the compressor is configured to increase the pressure of the carbon dioxide stream and creating a high pressure supercritical carbon dioxide stream, a first heat exchanger in fluid communication with the compressor and the fuel reactor, where first heat exchanger is configured to receive and heat the supercritical carbon dioxide stream, and being configured to receive a portion of the reformed or combusted fuel from the fuel reactor, the energy from the reformed or combusted fuel is used to heat the supercritical carbon dioxide stream, a second heat exchanger in fluid communication with the first heat exchanger and the air reactor, where second heat exchanger is configured to receive and further heat the supercritical carbon dioxide stream received from the first heat exchanger, and being configured to receive the oxygen-depleted air and a second portion of the re-oxidized oxygen carrier from the air reactor, the energy from the oxygen-depleted air and the second portion of the re-oxidized oxygen carrier is used to heat the supercritical carbon dioxide stream, and a turbine in fluid communication with the second heat exchanger, where turbine is configured to receive the supercritical carbon dioxide stream from the second heat exchanger and expand the supercritical carbon dioxide, the expansion of the supercritical carbon dioxide generates power, and the turbine comprises an outlet for the expanded supercritical carbon dioxide, and the expanded supercritical carbon dioxide is used to heat the fuel from the fuel source and the air stream from the air source prior to their respective deliveries to the fuel reactor and the air reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for power generation using a coupled chemical looping arrangement and a supercritical carbon dioxide cycle.

TI Apparatus used for manufacturing carbon dioxide, consists of gas circulation line for circulating mixed gas, heat exchanger, reactor, splitter, raw material powder supply line, carbon dioxide recovery line, and air supply line.
AU SHIMA H
   KOMATSU T
   TAKADA Y
   TAKAYAMA Y
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
   MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 201986439H
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus (10) consists of a gas circulation line (11) for circulating a mixed gas (G) containing carbon dioxide and air (E), a heat exchanger (12,15), a reactor (13), and a splitter (14) arranged on the gas circulation line in this order, a raw material powder supply line (21) connected to the gas circulation line, a carbon dioxide recovery line (22), and an air supply line (23). The heat exchanger performs heat exchange between the gas circulation line and a high-temperature atmosphere region (H) maintained at a temperature higher than the gas circulation line. The raw material powder supply line is connected between the reactor and the heat exchanger or connected to the reactor, and introduces a raw material powder (M) for generating carbon dioxide into the gas circulation line. The reactor generates carbon dioxide by a decarboxylation reaction carried out by contacting the generated raw material powder with the mixed gas.
   USE - Apparatus is used for manufacturing carbon dioxide (claimed) used as food material and industrial material, and for growing plant and algae.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus enables efficient manufacture of low-concentration carbon dioxide with reduced content of toxic substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus consists of a gas circulation line for circulating a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and air, a heat exchanger, a reactor, and a splitter arranged on the gas circulation line in this order, a raw material powder supply line connected to the gas circulation line, a carbon dioxide recovery line, and an air supply line. The heat exchanger performs heat exchange between the gas circulation line and a high-temperature atmosphere region maintained at a temperature higher than the gas circulation line. The raw material powder supply line is connected between the reactor and the heat exchanger or connected to the reactor, and introduces a raw material powder for generating carbon dioxide into the gas circulation line. The reactor generates carbon dioxide by a decarboxylation reaction carried out by contacting the generated raw material powder with the mixed gas heated by the heat exchanger. The splitter separates the generated raw material powder and the mixed gas. The carbon dioxide recovery line recovers the mixed gas from the system of the gas circulation line. The air supply line supplies air to the gas circulation line. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of carbon dioxide, which involves supplying air to the gas circulation line through the air supply line, heating the mixed gas using the heat exchanger in the high-temperature atmosphere region, carrying out decarboxylation reaction for generating carbon dioxide by contacting the mixed gas directly with the generated raw material powder, separating the generated raw material powder and the mixed gas, recovering the mixed gas in an amount corresponding to the amount of carbon dioxide produced during the reaction, and refluxing the remaining mixed gas to the gas circulation line; and
   (2) method for designing carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus, which involves determining the supply amount of generated raw material powder to the gas circulation line based on the temperature of the mixed gas and the supply amount of air to the gas circulation line, after heating, such that the generation amount of carbon dioxide becomes plus minus 10% with respect to the maximum value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus (10)
   Gas circulation line (11)
   Heat exchangers (12,15)
   Reactor (13)
   Splitter (14)
   Raw material powder supply line (21)
   Carbon dioxide recovery line (22)
   Air supply line (23)
   Preheater (31)
   Cement kiln (32)
   Clinker cooler (33)
   Calcination furnace (34)
   Air (E)
   Mixed gas (G)
   High-temperature atmosphere region (H)
   Raw material powder (M)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The high-temperature atmosphere region has a cement kiln (32), a clinker cooler (33), a preheater (31), a calcining furnace (34), and an extraction duct connecting the clinker cooler and the calcining furnace in a cement manufacturing apparatus. The carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus further consists of a return line for returning the generated raw material powder separated by the splitter towards the cement kiln, the preheater, the calciner, or the extraction duct.

PN CN114909113-A
TI Movable water-gas alternating gas injection device            for extracting shale oil from petroleum resources, has            nitrogen gas storage tank body and carbon dioxide            storage tank body that are fixedly connected to top            part of support plate and fixed on top part of adding            assembly.
AU SONG W
   MENG X
   BAI Z
   QIN H
   WANG L
   WANG T
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2022A8228D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a gas injection pipe whose end                is fixedly connected with an outlet end of a gas                injection pump. A water injection pump and the gas                injection pump are fixedly connected to a top part                of a supporting plate (1). An inlet end of the gas                injection pump is fixedly connected with a gas                mixing shell (10). An inner cavity of the gas                mixing shell is provided with a stirring assembly.                A side of the gas mixing shell is provided with a                nitrogen gas storage tank body (11) and a carbon                dioxide storage tank body (12). The gas mixing                shell, the nitrogen gas storage tank body and the                carbon dioxide storage tank body are fixedly                connected to the top part of the support plate. The                nitrogen storage tank body and the carbon dioxide                storage tank body are fixed on a top part of an                adding assembly. An end of a fixing pipe (5) is                fixedly connected with with a bent pipe (6).
   USE - Movable water-gas alternating gas injection                device for extracting shale oil from petroleum                resources.
   ADVANTAGE - The device injects mixed gas into a shale oil                reservoir to exert carbon dioxide competitive                adsorption capacity, and nitriding effect of                nitrogen, so that mixed gas purifying concentration                requirement is low or not need to be purified, thus                effectively reducing total cost of gas source and                mixed gas displacing coal bed gas and effectively                improving recovery rate of shale oil. The device                utilizes a moving structure to adjust transverse                and longitudinal position of the alternating air                pipe, thus ensuring wide range of applications of                the gas injection device, and improving                practicability of the gas injection device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                movable water-gas alternating gas injection                device.

PN WO2022128164-A1
TI Supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract used to increases pigmentation of human hair and/or skin and hydration, reduces oxidative stress and improves cutaneous barrier properties, comprises carotenoids, chlorophylls and free fatty acids.
AU HERRMANN M
   STUHLMANN D
   GAEBLER S
   TITZE N
   WESELOH A
   KRAELING R
AE SYMRISE AG (SYMR-C)
GA 202281594L
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract of Isochrysis sp and/or Tisochrysis sp comprises: (a) not greater than 1.5% body weight (BW) of carotenoids; (b) not greater than 2%BW of chlorophylls; and (c) at least 15%BW free fatty acids, where the extract has a gardner color value of not greater than 9 at an extract concentration of 0.018% in oil.
   USE - The extract is useful for increasing pigmentation of human hair and/or pigmentation of human skin, increasing skin hydration, reducing oxidative stress induced changes when applied to the skin, and improving the cutaneous barrier properties (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The extract: has gardner color value of not greater than 9 at an extract concentration of 0.018% in oil; has many non-polar to moderately polar compounds, while more polar compounds can only be extracted, SFE can be considered a sound cleantech strategy to extract natural compounds with an undisputed environmental friendliness, this is due to the non-toxic nature of extract and extract technology is available in industrial scale; provides safe and effective agents for stimulating and boosting skin and/or hair (meaning scalp hair, eye lashes, eye brows, beard or other hair) pigmentation; and utilizes natural or producible in sustainable way, stable, without intensive color and/or odor as well as easy to handle and formulate in common applications and they give natural tan, when applied to the skin and/or good hair pigmentation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1) obtaining the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract, comprising extracting cell material of Isochrysis sp. and/or Tisochrysis sp. with supercritical carbon dioxide, where the extraction comprises (a1) exposition of the cell material to the supercritical carbon dioxide for up to 24 hours, preferably for up to 8 hours at a temperature of 30-70 degrees C and a pressure of 150-290 bar; and (2) cosmetic composition comprising the supercritical fluid extract and at least one further ingredient comprising tanner, skin moisturizing agent, stabilizer, and/or antioxidant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The extract comprises not greater than 1 wt. % b.w., preferably not greater than 0.5%BW of carotenoids, not greater than 1%BW, preferably not greater than 0.5%BW chlorophylls, at least 20%BW, preferably at least 25%BW free fatty acids, and not greater than 27%BW long chain alkenones. The cell material of Isochrysis sp. and/or Tisochrysis sp. is obtained by a method comprising (a2) cultivating Isochrysis sp. cells, (b2) harvesting the cells to obtain completely or substantially intact cell material, (c2) optionally washing the cell material of step (b2) at least once time to obtain washed, substantially or completely intact cell material, washed, substantially or completely intact cell material, (d2) optionally freeze-drying the cell material of step (b2) and/or step (c2), or (e2) optionally spray-drying the cell material of step (b2) and/or (c2), or (f2) optionally thermal drying the cell material of step (b2) and/or (c2), or (g) optionally vacuum drying the cell material of step (b2) and/or (c2), (h2) optionally milling the cell material of step (b2) and/or step (c2) and/or step (d2) and/or step (e2) and/or step (f2) and/or step (g2).
   EXAMPLE - Freeze-dried 223 g Tahitian and Isochrysis (T-SO) biomass extraction for 1 hour at 150 bar and 60 degrees C followed by extraction for another 2 hours at 290 bar and 60 degrees C (cultivated in photo bioreactors under natural light, characterized by a loss on drying as determined by use of an infrared/halogen dryer of 4.2%), was pre-treated using a mechanical three rower mill and then extracted with supercritical carbon dioxide in a supercritical fluid extraction unit equipped with a 1 l extraction vessel to obtain supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extract.

TI Device for sealing shaft end of electromagnetic restraint type carbon dioxide turbine, has rotor tooth electrode and stator tooth electrode connected with anode and cathode of high voltage direct current power supply.

AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021C6303H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a rotor tooth electrode (3) directly processed on a rotor shaft head (1). A stator shaft sleeve (2) is fixed on a shell through a bolt. A stator tooth electrode (4) is directly processed on the stator shaft sleeve. A stator permanent magnet (7) is embedded in the stator shaft sleeve. A rotor permanent magnet (5) is embedded in the rotor shaft head. An outer wall of a rotor permanent magnet carbon fibre protective ring (6) is flush with an outer wall of the rotor shaft head. The rotor tooth electrode, an electric brush (9), the stator tooth electrode are connected with a conductor. The rotor tooth electrode and the stator tooth electrode are respectively connected with an anode and a cathode of a high voltage direct current power supply.
   USE - Device for sealing a shaft end of an electromagnetic restraint type carbon dioxide turbine of a supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation system. Can also be used for a direct current (DC)-DC converter.
   ADVANTAGE - The device captures a plasma by a convergent magnetic field and reflected back to an interior of a sealing chamber under effect of magnetic mirror effect when a plasma is driven by high pressure in a turbine to flow to an outlet of the sealing chamber, so as to reduce or completely eliminate leakage of a working substance, thus avoiding defect of dry gas sealing of a mechanical seal, and hence providing theoretical guidance for next generation sealing device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for sealing a shaft end of an electromagnetic restraint type carbon dioxide turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a device for sealing a shaft end of an electromagnetic restraint type carbon dioxide turbine.

   Stator permanent magnet (7)

PN PL430623-A1
TI Manufacture of household chemical products containing plant extracts involves introducing extract of dried lavender, mixing with nonionic surfactant mixture, pH modifier and preservative, and reacting with probiotic microorganisms.
AU PEKSA M
   WASILEWSKI T
   ORNOCH D
   JASINSKA J
   BYSTRZYCKI R
AE GRUPA INCO SA (INCO-Non-standard)
GA 202118059F
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of household chemical products containing plant extracts involves introducing plant extract of dried lavender obtained in supercritical carbon dioxide conditions into the encapsulation segment and heating at the temperature and time necessary to homogenize the extract components, mixing the carbon dioxide extract in an auxiliary mixer, under anaerobic conditions, with nonionic surfactants from the group of solubilizers having hydrophilic-lipophilic balance of 12-18, heating the mixture to 45 degrees C for 2-5 hours, introducing up to 3 %mass water, mixing at 45 degrees C, mixing with mixture containing 82-93 wt.% water, 2.9-4.4 wt.% surfactant, 1.8-2.9 wt.% cocamidopropyl betaine, 4.1-6 wt.% 8-10C fatty alcohol alkyl polyglucoside under inert gas atmosphere, introducing pH modifier and preservative, stirring the components at up to 35 degrees C, and introducing probiotic microorganisms from Bacillus spp., mixing at 35 degrees C, with the flow of inert gas.
   USE - Manufacture of household chemical products containing plant extracts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of household chemical products containing plant extracts involves introducing plant extract of dried lavender obtained in supercritical carbon dioxide conditions into the encapsulation segment and heating at the temperature and time necessary to homogenize the extract components, mixing the carbon dioxide extract in an auxiliary mixer, under anaerobic conditions, with nonionic surfactants from the group of solubilizers having hydrophilic-lipophilic balance of 12-18, heating the mixture to 45 degrees C for 2-5 hours, introducing up to 3 %mass water, mixing at 45 degrees C, mixing with mixture containing 82-93 wt.% water, 2.9-4.4 wt.% surfactant, 1.8-2.9 wt.% cocamidopropyl betaine, 4.1-6 wt.% 8-10C fatty alcohol alkyl polyglucoside under inert gas atmosphere, introducing pH modifier and preservative, stirring the components at up to 35 degrees C, introducing at least 0.01 wt.% probiotic microorganisms from Bacillus spp., mixing at 35 degrees C, with the flow of inert gas optionally shutting off after adding the premix to the mixture in the main mixer and before or after adding the modifiers. The weight ratio of the solubilizer to carbon dioxide is at least 4:1.

PT P
PN CN112246284-A; CN112246284-B
TI Single-molecule supported metal-organic framework material for catalyzing the conversion of carbon dioxide into 2,4(1H,3H)-quinazolindione, is obtained by combining organic metal single-molecule dichlorobis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(II).
AU MA J
   ZHOU Z
   CHENG P
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 202111084A
AB    NOVELTY - Single-molecule supported metal-organic framework material is obtained by combining organic metal single-molecule dichlorobis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(II) (Pd(PPh3)2Cl2) with the metal-organic framework (MIL-101), and obtaining the single-molecule supported metal-organic framework material (s-Pd(PPh3)2Cl2-MIL-101). The material not only maintains the activity of the organometallic single molecule, but also relies on the advantages of the metal-organic framework itself to achieve superior performance.
   USE - Single-molecule supported metal-organic framework material used in catalyzing the conversion of carbon dioxide into 2,4(1H,3H)-quinazolindione (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is simple to prepare, has good structural stability and high catalytic activity, can be recycled, and achieves the purpose of sustainable production, and the preparation of 2,4(1H,3H)-quinazolindiones in a heterogeneous system has been realized, which requires low equipment and has a wide range of raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using the single molecule supported metal-organic framework material (s-Pd(PPh3)2Cl2-MIL-101) in catalyzing the conversion of carbon dioxide into 2,4(1H,3H)-quinazolindione, which involves:
   (A) weighing the single-molecule supported metal-organic framework material (s-Pd(PPh3)2Cl2-MIL-101) as a catalyst and 1.0 equivalence of 2-iodoaniline into the Schlenk tube, and amount of the catalyst is 1 mol.% of the reactant 2-iodoaniline;
   (B) taking the Schlenk bottle out of the glove box, connecting the branch pipe of the Schlenk bottle to the vacuum line and degas, and then connecting the carbon dioxide balloon to the branch pipe to fill the Schlenk bottle with carbon dioxide gas;
   (C) using a syringe to inject 1.5 equivalance of isocyanate, 2.0 equivalence of 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene (DBU) and ultra-dry acetonitrile into the Schlenk bottle;
   (D) placing the Schlenk bottle in an oil bath at 50-80 degrees C and reacting overnight; and
   (E) obtaining the crude product, which is then passing through a silica gel column to obtain the pure product 2,4(1H,3H)-quinazolindione, and recovery yield reached 91.9%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for preparing the single-molecule-loaded metal-organic framework, which involves mixing chromium nitrate nona-hydrate (Cr(NO3)3.9H2O), 1,4 benzenedicarboxylic acid (H2BDC), hydrogen fluoride (HF) and water (H2O) with a molar ratio of 1.0:1.0:1.0:267 in a reactor lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, and reacting at 200-220 degrees C for 8-10 hours. The obtained solid is refluxed overnight with dimethylformamide (DMF), hot ethanol, distilled water, and ammonium fluoride aqueous solution to obtain MIL-101. The above-mentioned MIL-101 and triphenylphosphine (PPh3) are weighed according to a mass ratio of 1:0.6, added them to a Schlenk bottle, added ethanol under the protection of inert gas, reacted overnight at room temperature, and centrifuged to obtain PPh3-MIL-101, and drained the solvent. The PPh3-MIL-101 added to ethanol and heat, added palladium(II) chloride (PdCl2) to the hot solution and reflux for one day, where the molar ratio of PPh3 to PdCl2 is 2:1, and after Soxhlet extraction, vacuum is applied to finally obtain the single-molecule supported metal-organic framework material (s-Pd(PPh3)2Cl2-MIL-101).

PN US2020148963-A1; US11193073-B2
TI Carbonaceous fuel gasification system for            supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) power cycle system,            has input coupled to output of syngas cooler and            performs supercritical CO2 power cycle for generating            power.
AU WORMSER A
   PARKES J
   TODD D M
AE WORMSER ENERGY SOLUTIONS INC (WORM-Non-standard)
GA 202039889A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a micronized char preparation                system (104) with a devolatilizer. The micronized                char preparation system is configured to receive                solid carbonaceous fuel, hydrogen, oxygen, and                fluidizing steam and produces micronized char,                steam, hydrogen, and volatiles at outlets. An                indirect gasifier (106) is provided with a vessel.                A combustion chamber is configured to receive                syngas and an oxidant and to burn the mixture of                syngas with the oxidant to provide heat for                gasification and for heating incoming flows. A                syngas cooler (114) is provided with an inlet                coupled to the outlets of the gasification chamber                of the indirect gasifier. The syngas cooler is                configured to cool the syngas and to generate steam                at an output. A supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2)                power cycle system is provided with an input that                is coupled to an output of the syngas cooler. The                supercritical CO2 power cycle system performs                supercritical CO2 power cycle for generating                power.
   USE - Carbonaceous fuel gasification system for                supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle system in                large commercial power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The system increases the power generation                efficiency, reduces size and cost, and generally                result in a lower environmental footprint while                using coal or other carbonaceous fuels. The                supercritical carbon dioxide is non-toxic and                non-flammable, so using it reduces the burden on                power plant operations, which results in a lower                cost of operation. The supercritical carbon dioxide                is also less corrosive than steam and has favorable                thermal stability as compared to steam, which helps                to reduce the power system footprint. The air blown                combustor eliminate the large expensive air                separation plant for producing oxygen, normally                used for such systems, and significantly improves                the efficiency and cost. Integrated high                temperature heat recovery systems using specialized                high temperature heat exchangers support the                overall system with very high efficiency. The need                for an oxygen plant is eliminated which reduces the                costs and will also reduce the footprint of the                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a system block diagram of                an all-steam gasification system with air-based                combustion for a generic supercritical CO2 power                cycle system with carbon capture system.
   Carbon capture system (100)
   Char preparation system (104)
   Indirect gasifier (106)
   Syngas cooler (114)
   Compressor (122)
