PN CN111039467-A
TI Sludge backflow and reduction in electroplating wastewater treatment comprises e.g. introducing raw water of electroplating wastewater into first pH adjusting tank, and converting the carbonate into carbon dioxide and water.
AU CHEN X
   YU H
   REN K
   HE X
   XU W
   JIN S
   LOU H
AE ZHEJIANG HTE EVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202034638D
AB    NOVELTY - Sludge backflow and reduction in electroplating wastewater treatment comprises (i) introducing raw water of electroplating wastewater into the first pH adjusting tank, adjusting the pH by the return sludge of the secondary sedimentation tank controlling the pH of the wastewater in the first pH adjustment tank, converting the carbonate into carbon dioxide and water, and blowing out carbon dioxide, (ii) entering waste water into second pH adjusting tank, adding lime , taking out copper, chromium, zinc, iron and other metals form hydroxide precipitates, entering the subsequent reaction precipitation system, heavy metal sludge enters the sludge storage tank, pressure filtering, and outsourcing disposal and (iii) entering first-stage sedimentation effluent into third pH adjustment tank, adding lime, taking out nickel form nickel hydroxide precipitation. In the subsequent treatment system.
   USE - The method is useful for sludge backflow and reduction in electroplating wastewater treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sludge backflow and reduction in electroplating wastewater treatment comprises (i) introducing raw water of electroplating wastewater into the first pH adjusting tank, adjusting the pH by the return sludge of the secondary sedimentation tank controlling the pH of the wastewater in the first pH adjustment tank to less than 4 to ensure that the calcium carbonate in the secondary return sludge reacts with the acid, converting the carbonate into carbon dioxide and water, where the reaction time is 30-45 minutes, and blowing out carbon dioxide, where the gas-water ratio is controlled at 10-20: 1, (ii) entering waste water into second pH adjusting tank, adding lime to adjust the pH to 7-9, taking out copper, chromium, zinc, iron and other metals form hydroxide precipitates, entering the subsequent reaction precipitation system, heavy metal sludge enters the sludge storage tank through sludge discharge, pressure filtering, and outsourcing disposal and (iii) entering first-stage sedimentation effluent into third pH adjustment tank, adding lime to adjust the pH to 10-11, taking out nickel form nickel hydroxide precipitation. In the subsequent treatment system, and returning the sludge from the secondary sedimentation tank into first pH adjustment tank
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The method for adjusting the pH value using the return sludge of the secondary sedimentation tank in step (i) comprises instating an online pH device in the first pH adjustment tank, linking the online pH device with the return pump, forming the first linkage control system to control the start and stop of the return pump, starting the reflux pump when the pH value of the wastewater in the first pH adjusting tank is less than 3.5, flowing sludge in the secondary sedimentation tank back into the first pH adjustment tank, where the pH of the wastewater in the first pH adjusting tank gradually rises when the pH value rises to 4, and stopping the reflux pump. In the step (v), the secondary sedimentation tank is equipped with an ultrasonic mud level gauge, and a second linkage system is formed by the linkage of the ultrasonic mud level gauge with the first linkage system, control the start and stop of the first linkage system, when the sludge level is more than 2 m, and the first linkage system starts, and when the sludge level is lower than 1 m, and the first linkage system stops.

PN CN109609220-A
TI Preparing liquefied natural gas comprises e.g. (i) mining coalbed methane, passing into deoxidation reactor in presence of catalyst bed, atomizing decarburization solution and performing low temperature freezing process.
AU ZHANG X
   FENG H
   LAN H
   CAO M
   HE D
   GUO H
   YUAN L
   ZHANG Y
   QU B
   FENG X
AE SHANXI YIGAO COALBED METHANE CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019357953
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing liquefied natural gas comprises e.g. (i) mining coalbed methane, filtering and separating particulate impurities, (ii) adjusting pressure of filtered coalbed methane (iii) passing pretreated coalbed methane into deoxidation reactor in the presence of catalyst bed and cooling, (iv) separating moisture in coalbed methane and passing dehydrated coalbed methane into decarbonization reactor, (v) atomizing decarburization solution, making decarburization solution sufficiently contact with coalbed methane in decarbonization reactor (vi) subjecting decarburization solution to decompression analysis, resolving absorbed carbon dioxide, drying, passing hydrogen into resolved carbon dioxide and reacting to obtain methane gas and water, (vii) passing obtained methane gas and decarburized coalbed methane gas into dry reactor and performing dehydration, (viii) passing dehydrated gas into adsorber containing adsorbent, and (ix) performing low temperature freezing process.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing liquefied natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The product: has reduced oxygen content. The method has reduced loss of methane gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing liquefied natural gas comprises (i) mining coalbed methane, filtering and separating particulate impurities, (ii) adjusting pressure of filtered coalbed methane to 0.2-0.4 MPa and oxygen content to 12-15%, (iii) passing pretreated coalbed methane into deoxidation reactor in the presence of catalyst bed, simultaneously cooling to 500-620 degrees C, (iv) separating moisture in coalbed methane and passing dehydrated coalbed methane into decarbonization reactor, (v) transferring coalbed methane from bottom to top in decarbonization reactor, atomizing decarburization solution, making decarburization solution sufficiently contact with coalbed methane in decarbonization reactor and absorbing carbon dioxide gas, (vi) subjecting decarburization solution to decompression analysis, resolving absorbed carbon dioxide, drying, passing hydrogen into resolved carbon dioxide and reacting to obtain methane gas and water, (vii) passing obtained methane gas and decarburized coalbed methane gas into dry reactor and performing dehydration treatment by absorbing moisture from the molecular sieve, (viii) passing the dehydrated gas into adsorber containing adsorbent and removing benzene and mercury, and (ix) performing low temperature freezing process to obtain final product at -162 degrees C and removing nitrogen from liquefied natural gas by flash evaporation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (iii), deoxidation catalyst bed is sulfur tolerant catalyst bed. In step (v), decarburization solution is solution of methyldiethanolamine doped with active agent, where active agent is ethylenediamine phosphate, propylenediamine phosphate, ammonium sulfate or ammonium hydroxide. The adsorbent is coal-based activated carbon and copper(II) chloride modified magnetic beads in a mass ratio of 1:1. Preferred Method: The coalbed methane pressure before liquefying and compressing is 3-5 MPa and temperature is 20 plus minus 10 degrees C.

AU DANG Y

AE ANHUI CHINA HERB FLAVORS & FRAGRANCES CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201881309T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of WS-series cooling agent intermediate menthyl carbonate involves adding magnesium chips to a Grignard reactor, pumping tetrahydrofuran, stirring, adding ethyl bromide dropwise at 60-70 degrees C, adding chloromenthol to the reactor at 45 degrees C and drip acceleration of 1 kg/minute, stirring for 30 minutes, aging for 1 hour to precipitate, discharging lower layer of viscous liquid into a drying container until the discharged liquid is thin, and dilute liquid into another drying container, pumping the dilute liquid into addition reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas when the internal temperature of the reactor is 35 degrees C, controlling the flow of carbon dioxide gas to 2 L/minute, stirring for 1 hour, inhaling, pumping obtained viscous liquid into a hydrolysis reactor, controlling the temperature at less than 10 degrees C, adding 20% hydrochloric acid, and hydrolyzing.
   USE - Preparation of WS-series cooling agent intermediate menthyl carbonate by Grignard process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of WS-series cooling agent intermediate menthyl carbonate with high utilization rate of magnesium chips and yield, and reduced raw material loss rate, without generating byproduct and discharging waste liquid, in a short period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of WS-series cooling agent intermediate menthyl carbonate involves adding magnesium chips to a Grignard reactor, pumping tetrahydrofuran, stirring, adding ethyl bromide dropwise at 60-70 degrees C, adding chloromenthol to the reactor at 45 degrees C and drip acceleration of 1 kg/minute, stirring for 30 minutes, aging for 1 hour to precipitate, discharging lower layer of viscous liquid into a drying container until the discharged liquid is thin, and dilute liquid into another drying container, pumping the dilute liquid into addition reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas when the internal temperature of the reactor is 35 degrees C, controlling the flow of carbon dioxide gas to 2 L/minute, stirring for 1 hour, inhaling, pumping obtained viscous liquid into a hydrolysis reactor, controlling the temperature at less than 10 degrees C, adding 20% hydrochloric acid, hydrolyzing, pumping the lower layer viscous liquid into the Grignard reactor containing magnesium chips, and performing above process.

TI Device for producing high-strength carbon dioxide pellets from carbon dioxide snow, of cleaning device for blasting surfaces, has fluidic another transfer device configured to convey pellets from compression device to transfer device, and arranged between compression device and transfer device.
AU PETZOLD R
   ECKSTEIN L
AE KAERCHER SE & CO KG ALFRED (KARC-C)
GA 202263790P
AB    NOVELTY - The production device has a compression device (44) for compressing carbon dioxide snow (36) to form carbon dioxide pellets (16). A transfer device (82) transfers the carbon dioxide pellets in comprises a compressed gas flow. A fluidic another transfer device conveys carbon dioxide pellets from the compression device to the transfer device. Another transfer device is arranged or formed between the compression device and the transfer device. Another transfer device is provided with an inlet. The compression device is provided with an outlet. The compression device outlet and the transfer device inlet are fluidly connected to one another.
   USE - Device for producing high-strength carbon dioxide pellets from carbon dioxide snow, for cleaning device (claimed) for blasting surfaces to be treated with mixed flow of compressed gas and carbon dioxide pellets.
   ADVANTAGE - The reliable operation of the cleaning device is achieved. The continuous movement of the carbon dioxide pellets in the transfer device is ensured. The risk of carbon dioxide pellets, on side or boundary walls of the transfer device is avoided. The force of gravity acting on the carbon dioxide pellets is optimally utilized in order to transfer the carbon dioxide pellets from the compression device to the transfer device. The gas flowing through to be accelerated is enabled in a simple manner and the carbon dioxide pellets to be conveyed is enabled more simply and easily from the compression device outlet to the transfer device. The carbon dioxide pellets adhering to the collection funnel is easily detached. The number of moving portion on a cleaning device or a device for generating CO2 pellets, is minimized if the CO2 pellets are transferred with excess carbon dioxide gas using fluid mechanics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The production device has the transfer device that is provided with a collecting hopper. The collecting hopper is tapered in cross section in the direction of the transfer device. The collecting hopper is oriented parallel or essentially parallel to the direction of gravity and tapers in the direction of gravity. The transfer device is provided with a transfer space arranged between the compression device outlet and the introduction opening. The transfer space is tapered at least in sections in the direction of gravity or provided with an upper portion of the collecting funnel in relation to the direction of gravity. The transfer space is divided by a separating element into at least one first transfer space area and at least one second transfer space area. The separating element defines a first separating element side surface which points in the direction of the compression device outlet and laterally delimits the first transfer space region, with the first separating element side surface parallel to the direction of gravity. The separating element reaches at least as far as the collecting funnel. The separating element is provided with lower portion in relation to the direction of gravity to dip into the collecting funnel. The collecting hopper defines a hopper height which extends from the insertion opening which defines the largest cross-section of the collecting hopper to a collecting hopper outlet which defines the smallest cross section of the collecting funnel. The relative immersion depth of the separating element into the collecting funnel starting from the insertion opening in relation to the funnel height is in a range of 10-50%, preferably in a range of 20-40%. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a cleaning device (10) for irradiating surfaces to be treated with a mixed flow of a compressed gas and carbon dioxide pellets, has carbon dioxide connection line (20) for connection to a carbon dioxide storage device (24) containing liquid carbon dioxide, a compressed gas connection line (52) for connecting to a compressed gas generating device (54) for generating a compressed gas stream from a compressed gas and a carbon dioxide pellet acceleration device (58) for accelerating carbon dioxide pellets. The compressed gas line (50) is fluidly connected to the compressed gas connection portion or the compressed gas generating device. A jet connection line (62) is arranged or designed for connection to a jet line (64) or that the transfer device is in fluid communication with a jet line downstream, a jet nozzle (66) being arranged or formed in particular at a free end of the jet line; and
   (2) a method for producing high-strength carbon dioxide pellets from carbon dioxide snow.

PT P
PN CN114410733-A; US2022195379-A1; ZA202202972-A
TI Testing carbon dioxide inhibiting performance of            Shewanella putrefaciens by culturing Shewanella            putrefaciens, processing aquatic product, cutting,            immersing in ethanol, inoculating bacterial suspension            and testing inhibitory effect.
AU CHEN Y
   WANG J
   LI P
   XIE J
AE UNIV SHANGHAI OCEAN (USHO-C)
   UNIV SHANGHAI OCEAN (USHO-C)
GA 202264635D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for testing carbon dioxide inhibiting                performance of Shewanella putrefaciens involves (a)                taking out the frozen S.putrefaciens, thawing, then                adding to the trypticase soy broth (TSB) medium,                shaking and culturing on a shaker to obtain primary                culture solution, inoculating the primary culture                solution into the liquid medium, again shaking and                culturing to prepare a bacterial suspension,                preparing the aquatic products for processing,                cutting into pieces, then washing, immersing in                ethanol solution, then taking out, washing sterile                distilled water, sterilizing with UV light,                inoculating bacterial suspension into the                sterilized sample, adding into modified atmosphere                packing bags, starting the vacuum pump, storing the                packed samples in a refrigerator, randomly                sampling, testing inhibitory effect on the                S.putrefaciens performance.
   USE - The method is useful for testing carbon                dioxide inhibiting performance of                S.putrefaciens.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces growth and                reproduction rate of S.putrefaciens and spoilage                degradation ability of the treated sample, delays                destruction rate of S.putrefaciens to the quality                of aquatic products and is safe,                environmentally-friendly and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for testing carbon dioxide inhibiting                performance of Shewanella putrefaciens involves (a)                taking out the frozen S.putrefaciens, thawing, then                adding to the trypticase soy broth (TSB) medium and                shaking and culturing on a shaker for 10-14 hours                to obtain primary culture solution, (b) inoculating                the primary culture solution into the liquid                medium, again shaking and culturing for 4-8 hours                to prepare a bacterial suspension with a bacterial                concentration of 106-108 colony forming unit                (CFU)/ml, (c) preparing the aquatic products for                processing, cutting into pieces of 80-120 g mass,                then washing with 0-4degrees Celsius water and                draining to obtain samples, (d) immersing several                samples in ethanol solution for 20-40 seconds, then                taking out, washing 2-4 times with sterile                distilled water, then sterilizing with UV light for                15-25 minutes to obtain several sterilization                samples, diluting the bacterial suspension to a                bacterial concentration of 103-105 CFU/ml, and then                inoculating the diluted bacterial suspension into                the sterilized sample to obtain several inoculated                samples, (e) adding several inoculated samples into                modified atmosphere packing bags respectively, (f)                starting the bag-type modified atmosphere packing                machine, starting the vacuum pump, using the                modified atmosphere packaging mode for packing,                where the fresh-keeping gas during packing contains                0-100 vol.% carbon dioxide and remainder of                nitrogen, (g) storing the packed inoculated samples                in a refrigerator at 4plus minus0.5degrees Celsius                and (h) randomly selecting 3 samples as parallel                groups after 0-18 days of storage, testing the                parallel groups to test the inhibitory effect on                the S.putrefaciens performance under the condition                of fresh-keeping gas with different carbon dioxide                concentrations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  adding 5-10 ml TSB medium. The step (a) involves                  inoculating 0.5-1.5 %mass primary culture                  solution into the liquid medium. In step (d), the                  ethanol solution is ethanol solution and sample                  in a mass ratio of (2-4):1 and the ethanol                  solution has concentration of 70-80 %mass. In                  step (d), the sample is 5-15 %mass sterilization                  sample, so that the bacterial content of the                  sterilization sample is less than or equal to 105                  CFU/ml. The step (f) involves setting the mass                  ratio of the fresh-keeping gas volume in each                  modified atmosphere packaging bag to the                  inoculated sample to be 2-4 ml/g, setting the                  vacuuming time to 5-15 seconds, the inflation                  time to 3-5 seconds, and the heat sealing                  temperature to be 125-150degrees Celsius, setting                  the fresh-keeping gas source pressure to 4-6                  kg/cm2, and the power gas source pressure to 7-8                  kg/cm2, when the bag-type modified atmosphere                  packaging machine runs stably, placing the bag                  opening of the modified atmosphere packaging bag                  containing the inoculated samples in the place of                  gas replacement and heat sealing, and performing                  modified atmosphere packing. In step (h), the                  detection contents are the total number of                  colonies, biofilm, adenosine triphosphate                  content, sulfhydryl content, myofibrillar protein                  tertiary structure, myofibrillar protein                  ultrastructure and hardness value.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D12 (Butchering, meat treatment, processing poultry or fish (A22).)

PN CN114309891-A
TI Improving welding efficiency of thick plate vertical butt welding useful in e.g. ship construction technology field comprises e.g. installing welding auxiliary piece, starting carbon dioxide gas protection welding machine, backing and filling welding of weld, and performing cover welding.
AU ZHENG Y
   ZHU W
   QIAN H
   CHEN Z
   MU J
   LIU L
AE SHANGHAI JIANGNAN CHANGXING SHIPBUILDING (CHSB-C)
GA 2022559934
AB    NOVELTY - Improving welding efficiency of thick plate vertical butt welding comprises (i) installing a welding auxiliary piece before welding, (ii) starting carbon dioxide gas protection welding machine, setting welding current, welding voltage and welding speed for backing and filling welding of weld between two structural panels to be welded, when filling layer welding seam surface 1-15 mm away from front face of structure panel to be welded, ending welding work of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, (iii) installing and adjusting gas-electricity vertical welding machine so that welding wire is located at center of width direction and depth direction of non-welding groove, and (iv) starting gas-electric vertical welding machine, and performing cover welding on non-welding groove between two structural panels to be welded with set welding current, welding voltage and welding speed, oscillates welding wire back and forth along width of non-welding groove, and automatically walks up.
   USE - The method is useful for improving welding efficiency of thick plate vertical butt welding, which is useful in ship construction technology field, in gas-electric vertical welding, multiple multi-layer multi-pass welding, welding slag cleaning, interlayer temperature control and other technical requirements.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical; improves welding efficiency of 35-50 mm thick plate vertical butt joint in ship construction and steel structure manufacturing field; combines the common carbon dioxide gas protection welding with the gas-electric vertical welding technology, efficiently performs the welding seam performance; and improves the thick plate vertical butt welding efficiency. The welding seam can meet the related technical index requirement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving welding efficiency of thick plate vertical butt welding comprises (i) installing a welding auxiliary piece before welding, (ii) starting carbon dioxide gas protection welding machine, setting welding current, welding voltage and welding speed for backing and filling welding of weld between two structural panels to be welded, when filling layer welding seam surface 1-15 mm away from front face of structure panel to be welded, ending welding work of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, (iii) installing and adjusting gas-electricity vertical welding machine so that welding wire is located at center of width direction and depth direction of non-welding groove, and (iv) starting gas-electric vertical welding machine, and performing cover welding on non-welding groove between two structural panels to be welded with set welding current, welding voltage and welding speed, oscillates welding wire back and forth along width of non-welding groove during welding process, and automatically walks up according to molten pool forming until welding is completed.
DC X24 (Electric Welding)
MC X24-C01; X24-B06; X24-B04
IP B23K-009/16; B23K-009/28; B23K-009/32

PN KR2021112759-A
TI Lithium hydroxide cartridge unit for submarines, has door that is formed to be opened and closed through surface of main case to insert or remove lithium hydroxide cartridge toward interior of main case and cartridge stopper is in close contact with lithium hydroxide cartridge.
AU KIM K
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 2021B12659
AB    NOVELTY - The cartridge unit (100) has a main case (110) having an air inlet (120) through which air for removing carbon dioxide is introduced, an air outlet (130) through which air from which carbon dioxide is removed is discharged into the box, and a cartridge inserting unit having a set size through the inside. A lithium hydroxide cartridge is inserted through the cartridge insert and removes carbon dioxide from the air through a chemical reaction. A door is formed to be opened and closed through at least one surface of the main case to insert or remove the lithium hydroxide cartridge in a transverse direction toward the interior of the main case. A cartridge stopper is installed inside the main case and is in close contact with the lithium hydroxide cartridge into the main case.
   USE - Lithium hydroxide cartridge unit for submarines.
   ADVANTAGE - The size of the equipment and the quantity of cartridges can be optimized, and the air flow through structural improvements such as air inlet/outlet and cartridge arrangement can be improved. The air pressure loss can be minimized, and carbon dioxide can be efficiently removed even with a small air volume and wind pressure. By applying a cartridge stopper to improve the cartridge unit passage rate of the inlet air to improve the carbon dioxide removal rate in the air, thus achieving effect of miniaturization and efficiency improvement. The cartridge unit can be applied in the required quantity according to the required capacity of the current ship. The required air volume can be reduced according to the decrease in the cartridge quantity, and by applying the cartridge from the longitudinal direction to the transverse direction and locating the duct INLET/OUTLET position to the upper/lower portion of the unit, the air flow inside the case is optimized to optimize the pressure loss can be minimized. It is possible to improve the passage rate of the cartridge by minimizing vibration during the retracting movement and increasing the adhesion between the case opening and the cartridge. The carbon dioxide removal ability is improved. By a butterfly bolt fastening method to the door portion, it is possible to improve sealing force and minimize air leakage. The support for the duct connected to the unit is installed so that the duct support structure is reinforced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a lithium hydroxide cartridge unit.
   Lithium hydroxide cartridge unit (100)
   Main case (110)
   Air inlet (120)
   Flange-shaped air inlet duct fastening unit (121)
   Air outlet (130)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q24 (Ships; waterborne vessels; related equipment (B63))

PN CN211866838-U; CN113385780-A
TI Wireless remote control wire feeder for carbon            dioxide welder, has arc-receiving current controller            for controlling arc-receiving current and arc-receiving            voltage, and lithium battery for supplying power to            central control board.
AU JIANG Y
AE JIANG Y (JIAN-Individual)
GA 2020B2552V
AB    (CN211866838-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide                welder wireless remote control wire feeding                machine, belonging to the technical field of                welding machine, aiming at solving the problem that                the welding machine main machine is connected with                the wire feeding machine through the six-core wire;                in the field operation process, the six-core wire                is easy to damage the operation; It comprises a                wire feeding machine main body and a wireless                remote control box; the wireless remote control box                is fixed on the wire feeding machine main body; the                wireless remote control box comprises a central                control plate and an emitting controller                electrically connected with the central control                plate; a motor controller, a gas electromagnetic                valve controller, an arc collecting current                controller and a lithium battery; it further                comprises a receiving controller in wireless                communication connection with the emitting                controller; The wireless remote control wire                feeding machine of the utility model realizes the                wireless control of the wire feeding machine, saves                wire cost and greatly reduces the fault rate of the                device; the arc-receiving voltage and the                arc-receiving current do not need to be adjusted on                the host; the field operation is very convenient;                and the working efficiency is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                wireless remote control wire feeding machine of                carbon dioxide welder.
EA    NOVELTY - Wireless remote control wire feeding machine                of carbon dioxide welder comprises wire feeding                machine main body and wireless remote control box.                The wireless control box is fixed on wire feeder                main body. The wireless control box comprises                central control board and emitting controller                electrically connected with central control board.                A motor controller, gas electromagnetic valve                controller, arc collecting current controller and                lithium battery are electrically connected with                emission controller. The motor controller is                electrically connected with wire feeding motor. The                motor controller is used for controlling starting                and stopping of wire feeding motor in main body.                The gas electromagnetic valve controller is used                for controlling working state of gas                electromagnetic valve. The gas electromagnetic                valve is used for controlling pipeline on-off of                the control protection gas. The gas electromagnetic                valve is fixedly set on wire feeding machine                protection gas pipeline.
   USE - Used as wireless remote control wire feeding                machine of carbon dioxide welder.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine: saves wire cost; and greatly                reduces the fault rate of the device; does not need                to be adjusted on the host, so that the field                operation is very convenient; and improves the                working efficiency.

PT P
PN US2018016159-A1; WO2018013350-A1; CN109071270-A; KR2019029687-A; EP3484821-A1; US10519045-B2
TI Deaerating feed seawater stream containing oxygen, air, nitrogen and carbon dioxide by providing rotating packed bed device, infusing feed seawater stream into interior region, and removing deaerated seawater stream and gas stream.
AU MELLO M V D E
   HUANG H
   DUTRA E MELLO M V
   DUTRA E M M V
   HUANG H M
AE CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
   CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
   CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
GA 201805278V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for deaerating feed seawater stream containing oxygen, air, nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide, involves (a) providing rotating packed bed device, (b) infusing the feed seawater stream by way of exit port into the interior region, where the feed seawater stream passes radially outwardly through each rotatable packing rings, (c) removing the deaerated seawater stream from the housing through the liquid outlet, and (d) removing the gas stream having at least partially passed through one or more rotatable packing rings from interior region through the gas outlet.
   USE - The method is useful for deaerating feed seawater stream containing oxygen, air, nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to deaerate feed seawater stream in a cost-effective manner using system, which is compact for deployment on offshore platforms and vessels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for deaerating feed seawater stream containing oxygen, air, nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide, involves (a) providing rotating packed bed device comprising (i) one or more rotatable packing rings arranged within a housing, where one or more rotatable packing rings have an interior region, (ii) liquid inlet in the housing for feeding liquid into the interior region, the liquid inlet is in communication with an exit port arranged in an interior region for infusing the liquid into interior region, (iii) gas outlet in the housing for accepting a gas, which has at least partially passed radially inwardly through one or more rotatable packing rings and removing gas from the interior region, (iv) liquid outlet in housing for removing deaerated seawater stream from the housing, (v) rotatable shaft coupled to one or more rotatable packing rings for causing one or more rotatable packing rings to rotate, and (vi) motor coupled to rotatable shaft for driving the rotatable shaft, (b) infusing the feed seawater stream by way of exit port into the interior region, where the feed seawater stream passes radially outwardly through each rotatable packing rings thus forming deaerated seawater stream having lower concentration of the oxygen, air, nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide than the feed seawater stream and gas stream comprising the oxygen, air, nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide having been removed from the feed seawater stream, (c) removing the deaerated seawater stream from the housing through the liquid outlet, and (d) removing the gas stream having at least partially passed through one or more rotatable packing rings from interior region through the gas outlet. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system, which comprises (a) rotating packed bed device, (b) one or more level control vessels connected to the liquid outlet, and (c) water injection connected to one or more level control vessels for injecting the deaerated seawater stream into a hydrocarbon-producing-reservoir to enhance oil recovery from the hydrocarbon-producing-reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method further involves (e) mixing feed seawater stream with at least one of oxygen scavenger, biocide agent, antiscalant agent and their combinations before or concurrently with infusing feed seawater stream by way of exit port into the interior region in step (b), (f) sending gas stream from gas outlet of the rotating packed bed device to at least one of vent, flare, vacuum pump or their combinations, where rotating packed bed device further comprises gas inlet in the housing in fluid communication with one or more rotatable packing rings, and (g) injecting gas stream free of oxygen through the gas inlet, where ratio of gas stream to feed seawater stream is 1-10 standard cubic feet of gas stream/standard cubic feet of the feed seawater stream, and feed seawater stream and gas stream pass through each rotatable packing ring. The ratio of gas stream to feed seawater stream is 0.1-2 standard cubic feet of gas steam per barrel of seawater and feed seawater stream and gas stream pass through each of one or more rotatable packing rings. The oxygen scavenger comprises compound chosen from bisulfite salt, sodium sulfite, sodium erythorbate, hydrazine and their combinations. The vacuum is applied to the gas outlet of rotating packed bed device by connecting the gas outlet to a source of vacuum. The rotating packed bed device further comprises gas inlet in the housing. The rotating packed bed device further comprises vacuum port in housing and/or in one or more level control vessel connected to liquid outlet. The method further involves applying the vacuum to vacuum port. The source of vacuum comprises one or more liquid ring vacuum pumps, one or more ejectors or their combinations. The source of vacuum maintains pressure in the rotating packed bed device at 10-250 mm Hg absolute, preferably 15-30 mm Hg absolute. The method further involves injecting the deaerated seawater stream into a hydrocarbon-producing-reservoir using a water injection. The water injection comprises one or more injection pumps. The liquid outlet of rotating packed bed device is connected to one or more vessels for receiving the deaerated seawater stream and providing the deaerated seawater stream to one or more injection pumps. The method further involves filtering feed seawater stream prior to infusing the feed seawater stream into interior region in step (b). The method further involves passing the feed seawater stream through membrane unit for removing sulfates from the feed seawater stream before infusing the feed seawater stream into the interior region in step (b) or after passing the feed seawater stream through one or more level control vessels connected to the liquid outlet. The method further involves providing (a) separator in fluid communication with a hydrocarbon-producing-reservoir, where the separator comprises at least an inlet and separator gas outlet, (b) separating produced fluids from the hydrocarbon-producing-reservoir in the separator into an oil phase, gas phase and aqueous phase, and (c) passing gas phase from the separator gas outlet of separator to gas inlet of rotating packed bed device such that the gas phase is received as gas stream free of oxygen. The method further involves removing salts from the aqueous phase and passing the aqueous phase to a water injection component. The gas stream free of oxygen is chosen from fuel gas, methane, nitrogen, carbon dioxide and their combinations, where one or more rotatable packing rings rotate at a tip velocity of 4-80 m/second, and one or more rotatable packing rings rotate at a speed of 500-2500 revolutions per minute (rpm).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred System: The system further comprises (a) lift pump system connected to liquid inlet of rotating packed bed device for drawing seawater from an ocean environment and filter in fluid communication with lift pump system for filtering the seawater, (b) membrane unit connected to liquid inlet of the rotating packed bed device or outlet of one or more level control vessels for removing sulfates from feed seawater stream, where the rotating packed bed device further comprises gas inlet in fluid communication with one or more rotatable packing rings for receiving a gas stream free of oxygen. The system further comprises filter in fluid communication with produced water outlet on a separator for removing salts from an aqueous phase and line connecting the filter to the water injection component. The rotating packed bed device further comprises second liquid inlet. The rotating packed bed device has a height of 12-20 feet. The liquid inlet of rotating packed bed device further comprises liquid distributor comprising pipe having central conduit and annulus about the conduit, where the annulus is in fluid communication with several protruding tabs that extend into the interior region, the protruding tabs has several exit ports for infusing the liquid into the interior region, and central conduit in fluid communication with the interior region serves as gas outlet. The exit port arranged for infusing the liquid into interior region and gas outlet in the housing for accepting the gas, and removing gas from the interior region are orifices in a generally hollow cylindrical liquid distributor located in the interior region, the generally hollow cylindrical liquid distributor comprises center conduit, gas outlet orifices extending radially through center conduit and liquid-orifices extending radially through the center conduit, where two rotatable sets of packing rings rotate in a co-current direction, or countercurrent direction. The rotation of each two rotatable sets of packing rings is driven by shaft coupled to the motor. The rotatable packing rings are comprised of porous material chosen from metal foam, plastic, composite, stainless steel, titanium, super duplex stainless steel alloy, metal wire mesh, non-metal wire mesh, and glass. The water injection comprises one or more injection pumps. The system comprises multiple rotating packed bed devices arranged in parallel such that a shaft coupled to motor, which is coupled to one or more rotatable packing rings of each multiple rotating packed bed device. The system comprises multiple rotating packed bed devices arranged in series such that the liquid outlet of first rotating packed bed device, which is connected to liquid inlet of a second rotating packed bed device.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. PT P
PN CN114573799-A
TI New biodegradable carbon dioxide-based            copolyester-carbonate diol, obtained by            copolymerization of phthalic acid dihydroxy fatty diol            ester, carbon dioxide and aliphatic diol under the            action of catalyst.
AU QUAN Y
   ZHONG W
AE NINGBO MEISHAN BONDED PORT AREA HONGLUE (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202277815G
AB    NOVELTY - Biodegradable carbon dioxide-based                copolyester-carbonate diol (I), is new.
   USE - The biodegradable carbon dioxide-based                copolyester-carbonate dihydric alcohol is useful as                carbon dioxide as a green resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes easy separation of                catalyst and product, and recycling of carbon                dioxide. (I) has good antioxidant and degradable                performances, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biodegradable carbon dioxide-based                copolyester-carbonate diol of formula (I), is                new.
   m, n = greater than or equal to 1;
   R = 2-6C aliphatic hydrocarbon group;                and
   Ar = benzene ring.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing (I).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a nuclear magnetic 1 HNMR                diagram of the polymer 1 of the present                preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of (I)                  comprises subjecting first monomer phthalic acid                  dihydroxy fatty diol ester, the second monomer                  carbon dioxide and third monomer aliphatic diol                  to copolymerization reaction under the action of                  the catalyst, where the first monomer phthalic                  acid dihydroxy fatty diol ester is of formula                  (II) and is obtained by the esterification                  reaction of phthalic acid or phthalic anhydride                  and aliphatic diol.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  adding phthalic acid or phthalic anhydride and                  aliphatic diol and catalyst into a reactor,                  introducing nitrogen and heating to obtain first                  monomer, switching nitrogen to carbon dioxide as                  second monomer, providing excess aliphatic                  dihydric alcohol in the system as third monomer,                  subjecting three monomers to copolymerization                  reaction, and filtering when it is hot to remove                  the catalyst to obtain purified carbon dioxide                  based copolyester-carbonate diol. The feeding                  molar ratio of the catalyst and the aliphatic                  diol is 1:20-3000, the feeding molar ratio of                  phthalic acid or phthalic anhydride and aliphatic                  diol is 1:2-10, the nitrogen flow rate is more                  than 100 ml/minute, the carbon dioxide flow rate                  is more than 200 ml/minute, the reaction pressure                  is 0.01-0.1 MPa, the reaction temperature is                  200-200degrees Celsius and the reaction time is                  4-96 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aliphatic diol is                  2-6C aliphatic diol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is                  loaded-type copper-nickel bimetallic catalyst or                  metal oxide. The metal oxide is cerium oxide, tin                  oxide or zirconium oxide.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A05-E01A1; A10-D05

AU DU Y
   LI S

   HAN L

GA 2022775100
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 1H-benzothiazole derivative (I)                using carbon dioxide involves contacting o-amino                disulfide (II) and a borohydride compound in a                solvent under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of derivative (I) using carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, quick, easy,                efficient, environmentally-friendly and carried out                using stable and easily available raw materials to                produce product with high purity and high                yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of 1H-benzothiazole derivative of                formula (I) using carbon dioxide involves                contacting o-amino disulfide of formula (II) and a                borohydride in a solvent under carbon dioxide                atmosphere.
   R = H, halo, -CX3, 1-8C alkoxy, 1-8C alkyl,                sulfone substituted by 1-4C alkyl or optionally                substituted phenyl; and X = halo.
                                                                                                                                                                                 
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the compound (II) and the borohydride compound is                  1:1-7, preferably 1:2-5.Preferred Condition: The reaction is carried                  out at 80-140&#8451;, preferably                  110-130&#8451; under carbon dioxide pressure                  of 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 1-4 MPa for 6 hours or                  more, preferably 15-36 hours.Preferred Process: The method further                  involves concentrating the reaction mixture by                  atmospheric distillation or vacuum distillation,                  and purifying the obtained product by column                  chromatography and/or recrystallization.Preferred Component: The solvent is at least                  one of N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, dimethylformamide,                  tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl sulfoxide, 1,4-dioxane                  and acetonitrile, preferably at least one of                  dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and                  dimethylformamide. The borohydride compound is at                  least one of borohydride salt, ammonia borane and                  tertiary amine borane complex, preferably at                  least one of ammonia borane, sodium borohydride,                  potassium borohydride, lithium borohydride,                  borane triethylamine complex,                  tris(trimethylamino)borane (BH3N(CH3)3) and                  tris(isopropylamino)borane (BH3N(C3H7)3). The                  eluent used for column chromatography is mixture                  of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate, or mixture                  of dichloromethane and ethyl acetate. The solvent                  used for recrystallization is ethyl acetate                  and/or n-hexane.

PT P
PN WO2020005482-A1; US2021115572-A1
TI Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide involves introducing the carbon dioxide to a catalyst comprising transition metal carbide, nitride, or carbonitride in electrochemical cell, and then applying a potential to the electrochemical cell.
AU ASADI M
   KONDORI A
   ESMAEILIRAD M
   RUIZ BELMONTE A
   RUIZ B A
AE ILLINOIS INST TECHNOLOGY (ILLV-C)
   ASADI M (ASAD-Individual)
   KONDORI A (KOND-Individual)
   ESMAEILIRAD M (ESMA-Individual)
   RUIZ BELMONTE A (BELM-Individual)
   RUIZ B A (RUIZ-Individual)
   ESMAEILIRAD M (ESMA-Individual)
   KONDORI A (KOND-Individual)
   ASADI M (ASAD-Individual)
   ILLINOIS INST TECHNOLOGY (ILLV-C)
GA 2020022254
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide involves introducing the carbon dioxide to a catalyst comprising a transition metal carbide, nitride, or carbonitride in an electrochemical cell, applying a potential to the electrochemical cell and converting the carbon dioxide to a hydrocarbon, preferably methane.
   USE - Method for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electrochemical cell having a cathode with at least one MXene catalyst, and in contact with an electrolyte;
   (2) a catalyst composition for carbon dioxide reduction, comprising at least one transition metal MXene; and
   (3) a composition, where the transition metal MXene comprises a nanostructured carbide, nitride, and/or carbonitride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrochemical cell comprises a cathode, where the cathode is coated with the catalyst. The lectrolyte, such as a solution of 1M KHC03, is saturated with the carbon dioxide. The catalyst comprises a nanostructured MXene. The catalyst comprises MyXz, where M is a transition metal, X is carbon and/or nitrogen, and y and z are stoichiometric ratio integers. The transition metal comprises molybdenum, tungsten, titanium, or cobalt. The catalyst comprises a nanoparticle form. The catalyst comprises a nanoflake, nanosheet, or nanoribbon form. The MXene catalyst comprises a nanostructured transition metal carbide, nitride and/or carbonitride. The MXene catalyst comprises molybdenum, tungsten, titanium, or cobalt. The MXene catalyst comprises a nanoparticle form. The transition metal MXene comprises molybdenum, tungsten, titanium, or cobalt. The transition metal MXene comprises a nanoflake, nanosheet, or nanoribbon form. Preferred Process: The method further involves providing the electrochemical cell including a cathode coated with the catalyst, and an electrolyte in contact with the cathode and the catalyst, providing carbon dioxide to the electrochemical cell and applying the potential to the electrochemical cell in the presence of the carbon dioxide to reduce the carbon dioxide to the hydrocarbon. Preferred Conditions: The catalyst nanoparticles have an average size between about 1 nm and 400 nm.
DC J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN WO2018079103-A1; JP2018070957-A; JP6631467-B2
TI Device used for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises oxidation electrode, and reduction electrode comprising nitrogen-doped graphene, immersed in electrolyte solution to generate carbon compound by reduction reaction of carbon dioxide.
AU YAMAGUCHI HITOSHI
   YAMAGUCHI H
AE DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
   DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
GA 201834174X
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction device comprises an oxidation electrode (13) immersed in an electrolyte solution (11) to generate oxygen and hydrogen ions by oxidation reaction of water, and a reduction electrode (14) immersed in the electrolyte solution to generate a carbon compound by reduction reaction of carbon dioxide. The reduction electrode comprises nitrogen-doped graphene.
   USE - Device is used for reducing carbon dioxide in production of carbon compound e.g. methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The device enables simple, efficient and stable reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide reduction device.
   Container (10)
   Electrolyte solution (11)
   Photovoltaic element (12)
   Oxidation electrode (13)
   Reduction electrode (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The carbon dioxide reduction device further comprises a photovoltaic element (12) which generates an internal photoelectric effect by irradiation of light, electrically connected to the oxidation electrode and the reduction electrode. The reduction electrode further comprises a metal electrode. The reduction electrode is integrated to a cathode side of the photovoltaic element. The oxidation electrode is integrated to an anode side of the photovoltaic element.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nitrogen-doped graphene is bonded to portion(s) of the metal electrode surface. The metal electrode comprises nickel, gold, copper, tin, indium, platinum and/or their oxide. In nitrogen-doped graphene, the number of nitrogen atoms doped with respect to the number of carbon atoms constituting graphene is 1/6 or less.

PT P
PN CN112553269-A
TI Pretreatment and enzymolysis of corn straw, by cutting corn straw, adding particles into dilute sulfuric acid solution, after pretreatment under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions, and subjecting to enzymolysis to obtain xylose.
AU XU J
   GU S
   WU H
   ZUO Z
   WEI W
   HU S
   ZHANG K
   XU X
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 2021343498
AB    NOVELTY - Method for pretreatment and enzymolysis of corn straw involves (a) cutting corn straw, crushing, washing and drying to obtain particulate matter, (b) adding the particles obtained in step (a) into dilute sulfuric acid solution according to a certain solid-liquid ratio, after pretreatment under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions for a period of time, collecting filtrate and filter residue after suction filtration, and placing the filter residue in an oven to dry for a period of time to remove moisture to obtain solids, and (c) using the solid obtained in step (b) as substrate for enzymolysis, adding xylanase and trisodium citrate buffer at a certain solid-liquid ratio to obtain xylose.
   USE - The method is useful for pretreatment and enzymolysis of corn stover to obtain xylose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is scientific and reasonable, uses dilute acid to enhance pretreatment effect of supercritical carbon dioxide at low temperature, improves efficiency of enzymolysis, better breaks complex cross-linked structure of lignocellulose itself and breaks chemical bond between lignin and semi-fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the corn straw is cut and crushed to 30-50 meshes. The particles are added to the dilute sulfuric acid solution according to solid-liquid ratio of 1:8-12, where the dilute sulfuric acid solution has sulfuric acid concentration of 1-2%. The pretreatment under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions is performed at 35-70 degrees C for 30-240 minutes. The drying is performed in an oven at 30-60 degrees C for 10-14 hours. The xylanase is an acid xylanase with an enzyme activity of 200,000 U/g. Amount of xylanase used is 28000-33000 U/g substrate, preferably 31250 U/g substrate. In step (c), the trisodium citrate buffer has pH of 5, and xylanase and trisodium citrate buffer are added at a solid-liquid ratio of 1:40-60, preferably 1:50 and enzymolysis is performed for 20-28 hours, preferably 24 hours.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)

PN CN112354381-A
TI Preparing full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane, comprises taking hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide as dispersed phase, and polyimide as continuous phase to prepare mixed matrix gas separation membrane.
AU ZHANG Y
   LI M
   JIANG X
   LIU S
   CHI Z
   XU J
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 202118224C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane, comprises taking hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide as dispersed phase, and polyimide as continuous phase to prepare mixed matrix gas separation membrane.
   USE - The full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane is useful in separation of carbon dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/nitrogen, hydrogen/carbon dioxide, hydrogen/ methane, and oxygen/nitrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane: utilizes hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide, which has high specific surface area, acid resistance and heat resistance, and is suitable for natural gas separation in high temperature and acidic environment; and has improved compatibility of the two phases, which reduces non-selective defects between the interface caused by the separation of the continuous phase and the dispersed phase compared with the existing mixed matrix membrane and improve selectivity of the mixed matrix gas separation membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide dispersed phase is prepared by one-step method by dissolving equal molar ratio of amine and anhydride, triamine or tetraamine monomer and dianhydride monomer in high boiling point solvent, then adding catalyst, and reacting at 30-60 degrees C for 80-150 minutes, 70-100 degrees C reaction for 80-150 minutes, 110-150 degrees C for 10-15 hours, 150-180 degrees C for 80-150 minutes, and finally reacting at 190-210 degrees C for 5-15 hours, precipitating, filtering and performing Soxhlet extraction to obtain hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide dispersed phase powder, where the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface area of hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide is 400-1200 m2/g. The polyimide continuous phase is prepared by a two-step method and dividing into chemical imidization or thermal imidization. The chemical imidization includes dissolving equal amount of diamine monomers and dianhydride monomers in a solvent, and reacting at -5 to -10 degrees C for 8-24 hours to prepare polyamic acid precursors, then cooling to room temperature, and adding catalyst and dehydrating agent and reacting for 4-12 hours to form polyimide, and precipitating, filtering and performing Soxhlet extraction to obtain to obtain continuous phase of polyimide. The thermal imidization includes dissolving equal amount of diamine monomer and dianhydride monomer in a solvent, then reacting at -5 to -10 degrees C for 8-24 hours to prepare polyamic acid precursor, then cooling to room temperature, and adding catalyst and toluene, reacting at 180-200 degrees C for 4-12 hours to form polyimide, and precipitating, filtering and performing Soxhlet extraction to obtain to obtain continuous phase of polyimide. The preparation of full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane comprises dissolving continuous phase of polyimide in a solvent to prepare a casting solution having solid content of 10-20 wt.%, then adding dispersed phase of hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide, and ultrasonically dispersing uniformly and performing defoaming treatment, then pouring on a dust-free glass plate, and molding by knife coating, casting or spin coating, drying and waiting for the solvent to volatilize to form full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane, where the loading amount of the dispersed phase of the hyperbranched micro-porous polyimide is 1-30 wt.% in full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane. The molding membrane thickness by casting method or spin coating is 400-600 mu m, and placing in a vacuum oven at 80-160 degrees C to dry until the solvent evaporates to form full polyimide mixed matrix gas separation membrane, automatically peeling off the membrane in deionized water, drying in a blast drying oven at 60-120 degrees C, and obtaining membrane having thickness of 10-80 mu m. Preferred Components: The high boiling point solvent is m-cresol, N-methylpyrrolidone, 1,3,5-trimethylbenzene and/or imidazole; the catalyst is pyridine, isoquinoline and/or triethylamine; and the triamine or tetraamine monomer is of formula (I) and of formula (II); and dianhydride monomer is of formula (III). The polyimide is 5-(1,3-dioxo-2-(4-(1,3,3-trimethyl-2,3-dihydro-1H-inden-1-yl)phenyl)isoindoline-5-carbonyl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (IV), 5-(1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoro-2-(2-mesityl-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-5-yl)propan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide of formula (V), 5-(1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoro-2-(2-(5-(1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoro-2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)propan-2-yl)-2-hydroxyphenyl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-5-yl)propan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (VI), 5-(2-(1,3-dioxo-2-(4-phenoxyphenyl)isoindolin-5-yl)-1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoropropan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (VII), 5-(2-(1,3-dioxo-2-(2,3,5,6-tetramethylphenyl)isoindolin-5-yl)-1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoropropan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (VIII), 5-(2-(2-(4-(3,5-dimethylbenzyl)-2,6-dimethylphenyl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-5-yl)-1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoropropan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (IX), 5-(2-(1,3-dioxo-2-(4-(9-phenyl-9H-fluoren-9-yl)phenyl)isoindolin-5-yl)-1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoropropan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (X), 5-(2-(2-(3,3'-dimethyl-1,1'-binaphthyl-4-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-5-yl)-1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoropropan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (XI) and/or 5-(2-(1,3-dioxo-2-o-tolylisoindolin-5-yl)-1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoropropan-2-yl)-1,3-dioxoisoindolin-2-ide polymer of formula (XII). The solvent is chloroform, N,N'-dimethylformamide, m-cresol and/or N-methylpyrrolidone. The catalyst is pyridine, isoquinoline and/or triethylamine. The dehydrating agent is acetic anhydride.n = 100-3000;R = trip-tolylamine-tri-yl, 1,3,5-tris(4-methylphenyl)benzene-tri-yl, 2,4,6-trip-tolyl-1,3,5-triazine-tri-yl, 2,4,6-trimethyl-1,3,5-triazine-tri-yl, 1,3,5-tris(4-(4-methylphenyl)phenyl)benzene-tri-yl, tetrap-tolylmethane-tri-yl or 1,3,5-trimethyladamantane-tri-yl; andR1 = 1,2,4,5-tetramethylbenzene-tetra-yl, 1,4,5,8-tetramethylnaphthalene-tetra-yl, 3,4,9,10-tetramethyl-3,10-dihydroperylene-tetra-yl, 1,2,7,8-tetramethyl-1,3,5,10-tetrahydrocoronene-tetra-yl or 1,2,3,4-tetramethylcyclobutane-tetra-yl.

TI Operating a blast furnace involves collecting a            blast furnace gas from the blast furnace, where the            blast furnace gas being carbon dioxide containing gas,            combining blast furnace gas with a fuel gas to obtain            gas mixture.
AU KINZEL K P
   AGRAWAL A K
   KASS G
AE WURTH SA PAUL (WURP-C)
   WURTH SA PAUL (WURP-C)
   WURTH SA PAUL (WURP-C)
GA 2020B71261
AB    NOVELTY - Operating a blast furnace involves collecting                a blast furnace gas from the blast furnace, where                the blast furnace gas being carbon dioxide                containing gas, combining the blast furnace gas                with a fuel gas to obtain a gas mixture, where the                fuel gas being a hydrocarbon containing gas,                subjecting the gas mixture to a reforming process,                producing a synthesis gas containing carbon                monoxide and hydrogen and feeding at least a                portion of the synthesis gas and an oxygen-rich gas                into the blast furnace, where the blast furnace gas                is combined with the fuel gas while containing                substantially the same amount of carbon dioxide as                when exiting the blast furnace and the blast                furnace gas is combined with the fuel gas in an                over-stoichiometric ratio, so that the synthesis                gas contains a surplus portion of the blast furnace                gas.
   USE - Method for operating blast furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to operate blast furnace                increases the amount of auxiliary fuel fed into the                blast furnace.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The blast furnace gas                  is combined with the fuel gas in an                  over-stoichiometric ratio before the reforming                  process. The blast furnace gas is combined with                  the fuel gas in a stoichiometric ratio before the                  reforming process, while additional blast furnace                  gas is combined with the synthesis gas after the                  reforming process. The oxygen-rich gas has                  temperature of 100 degrees C. The synthesis gas                  is fed into the blast furnace at a tuyere level                  and/or lower shaft level having a temperature of                  800 degrees C. The over-stoichiometric ratio is                  adjusted to control a top gas temperature of the                  blast furnace. The auxiliary fuel is fed into the                  blast furnace. The portion of the blast furnace                  gas is burned with oxygen to create a waste gas.                  The waste gas is condensated and cooled. The                  portion of the blast furnace gas is burned in a                  heating device. The portion of the waste gas is                  used for carbon capture and storage and/or carbon                  capture and utilisation. The portion of the waste                  gas is used for synthesis gas production.                  Preferred Components: The oxygen-rich gas (26)                  contains 60 wt.% oxygen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111480816-A
TI Processing freshwater fish involves taking sea            crystal solution to soak freshwater fish, putting into            water, introducing carbon dioxide, bloodletting,            rinsing, brining, kneading with deodorant marinade,            washing, air-drying and tempering.
AU QIANG J
   XU P
   ZHU H
   BAO J
   HE J
AE CAFS FRESHWATER FISHERIES RES CENT (CAFS-C)
GA 202076569G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing freshwater fish involves                (i) taking sea crystal solution to soak freshwater                fish to obtain primary cleaned freshwater fish,                (ii) putting the primary cleaned freshwater fish                into water, introducing carbon dioxide, and                performing bloodletting and killing in a saturated                carbon dioxide aqueous solution to obtain                pretreated freshwater fish, (iii) rinsing the                pretreated freshwater fish with clean water and                performing brine operation in sequence to obtain                cleaned freshwater fish, mixing and kneading the                cleaned freshwater fish with a deodorant marinade                to obtain a pickled freshwater fish, and (iv)                washing the pickled freshwater fish, and air-drying                at low temperature and tempering in order.
   USE - The method is useful for processing freshwater                fish.
   ADVANTAGE - The method locks the nutrient components of                the fish body and retains the original flavor of                the freshwater fish products. The freshwater fish                products are suitable for low-temperature storage,                is transportable to northern cities by refrigerated                trucks, and convenient to eat without adding other                seasoning.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The deodorizing                  marinade comprises 15-40 parts mass salt, 2-6                  parts mass pepper powder, 5-10 parts mass ginger                  powder, 3-8 parts mass white pepper and 20-50                  parts mass white granluated sugar. In step (iii),                  the mass ratio of the deodorizing marinade and                  the pretreated freshwater fish is (45-104):1000.                  The concentration of the sea crystal solution is                  5-10 %mass. The sea crystal solution also                  includes ginger juice with a concentration of                  0.5-1 vol.%. preferred Conditions: In step (iii)                  further involves mixing the deodorant marinade                  with water before mixing, where the volume ratio                  of the fishy marinade and water in the tumbling                  is 1:(1.8-2.5). In step (iii), the mixing and                  kneading is interval kneading, specifically,                  stopping for 1-2 minutes every 6-8 minutes, the                  time of the mixing and kneading is 50-90 minutes                  and the speed of the tumbling is 20-40 rpm. In                  step (ii), the bloodletting and killing involves                  cutting gills, bloodletting, descaling, opening                  fish belly, and removing visceral and gill. In                  step (ii), the bloodletting is carried out at                  room temperature for 15-20 minutes. In step (i),                  the temperature of the sea crystal solution is                  25-28 degrees C, and the soaking time is 24-48                  hours. In step (iii), the time for rinsing with                  clean water is 5-10 minutes, the time for rinsing                  with brine is 5-8 minutes, and the concentration                  of the brine is 5-10 %mass. In step (iv), the                  washing is carried out with water for 5-10                  minutes. In step (iv), the low-temperature                  air-drying is carried out at 5-35 degrees C for                  6-8 hours, where the humidity is 20-80%.

PN CN111446447-A
TI Method for preparing sulfur tin compound/carbon composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, involves annealing samples in protective atmosphere furnace, and obtaining annealed samples after cooling to obtain material samples.
AU WU H
AE ZHEJIANG CHANGXING GREEN BATTERY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202073806L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves uniformly mixing a sulfur source, a tin source, a solvent and a carbon source in certain proportion to obtain mixture. The mixture is put into a high-pressure ball milling tank. Carbon-di-oxide gas is quickly released in the high-pressure ball milling tank. Reaction liquid is collected. Obtained samples are rinsed with absolute ethanol and deionized water for 2-4 second and freeze-drying. Obtained samples are annealed in a protective atmosphere furnace at temperature of about 300-600 degree Celsius for 0.5-12 hour. Annealed samples are obtained after cooling to obtain sulfur tin compound/carbon composite material samples.
   USE - Method for preparing sulfur tin compound/carbon composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring low reaction temperature, environmental protection and easy industrialization.

PN CN111196950-A
TI Extracting citronella essential oil comprises washing fresh citronella, immersing in liquid nitrogen, mixing citronella powder, passing supercritical carbon dioxide into extraction kettle and vacuum separation.
AU YE G
AE NANJINH AIMENG LITE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202048654J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting citronella essential oil comprises (a) washing the fresh citronella, soaking in water, placing into the sieve basket, immersing in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, lifting and placing at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, immersing the sieve basket in whole 30-60 minutes in liquid nitrogen, repeating 2-3 times, (b) drying, crushing, passing the citronella at low temperature to obtain a citronella powder, (c) mixing the citronella powder with the solvent evenly, placing in the extraction kettle, passing supercritical carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle to control the temperature of the extraction kettle to 40-50 degrees C and the extraction pressure to 30-45Mpa and (d) flowing of carbon dioxide containing extract from the extraction tank and entering the separation tank for vacuum separation to obtain citronella essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting citronella essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids considering the loss of components in the process of citronella extraction and improves the quality and yield of citronella essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) comprises mixing citronella powder with the biological enzyme (0.01-0.08 times) before being placed in the extraction kettle and allowing to stand for 30-60 minutes. The step (b) comprises vacuum-drying citronella at low temperature, the process is: stacking citronella in a vacuum drying box, controlling the vacuum degree to 0.009-0.36Mpa and the vacuum drying temperature to 40-52 degrees C. In the step (d), the pressure of the separation kettle is 7-9 MPa and the temperature is 25-30 degrees C. Preferred Components: The biological enzyme is one of beta -glucosidase, pectinase and cellulase. The citronella is dried to a moisture content of less than 5%. The solvent is a low boiling point solvent, preferably at least one of acetone, ethanol, ether, methanol and methylene chloride.

AU SIDOROVA N A
   SAVUSHKIN A I
AE MIKROBIOM LLC (MIKR-Non-standard)
GA 2020262940
AB    NOVELTY - Nutrient medium for culturing bacteria comprising Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans used for leaching rare-earth metals from mixed ores at ore boundary, comprises a leach solution containing nitrogen and carbon dioxide sources, potassium chloride ions and calcium nitrate, cofactors (potassium dihydrogenphosphate heptahydrate (KH2PO4x 7H2O)) and water. Sodium bicarbonate is used as a source of carbon dioxide, and potassium chloride and calcium nitrate are used as compounds regulating the osmotic conditions in the following ratio and component: 0.4 g/L ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4); 0.25 g/L sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3); 0.25 g/L KH2PO4; 0.45 g/L magnesium sulfate heptahydrate (MgSO4x 7H2O); 0.001 g/L calcium nitrate tetrahydrate (Ca(NO3)2x 4H2O); 0.1 g/L potassium chloride (KCl); 44.0 g/L iron sulfate heptahydrate (FeSO4x 7H2O); and 1000 ml distilled water.
   USE - The mutrient medium is useful for culturing bacteria comprising Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans used for leaching rare-earth metals from mixed ores at ore boundary.

PN CN110725142-A
TI Dyeing method for improving use characteristics of ramie fiber for textile products, involves pretreating ramie fiber, irradiating, performing supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing, treating with plasma, and further dyeing.
AU LUAN J
AE JIESHOU SUAO TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 202011006W
AB    NOVELTY - Dyeing method for improving use characteristics of ramie fiber involves placing ramie fiber on a wooden sieve, performing steam fumigation treatment for 2-3 hours, removing the ramie fiber, placing the fiber in a low-energy proton irradiation box, irradiating for 2-3 minutes, adding dye to bottom portion of a dyeing container, winding the ramie fiber on a spiral tube, placing around the agitator, performing supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing for 30-40 minutes, removing the fiber, placing the fiber in a vacuum chamber for plasma treatment using argon as a gas source, processing for 4-6 minutes, adding dye to bottom portion of the dyeing container, winding the ramie fiber on a spiral tube, placing around the agitator, performing supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing for 14-18 minutes, and removing the ramie fiber.
   USE - Dyeing method for improving use characteristics of ramie fiber for textile products.
   ADVANTAGE - The ramie fiber having excellent flexibility, dyeability and softness can be produced stably and efficiently.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Steam fumigation is carried out by boiling mixture of 1-2 pts. wt. N-methylpyrrolidone, 0.9-1.1 pts. wt. N-methylmorpholine-N-oxide, 0.6-0.8 pt. wt. liquid ammonia, 0.2-0.4 pt. wt. fatty acid glyceride, 0.9-1.3 pts. wt. fatty acid sorbitan, 13-15 pts. wt. purified water, adding anhydrous ethanol, and boiling. Dyeing is carried out using a mixture comprising 0.2-0.3 pt. wt. 3-chloro-2-hydroxypropyltrimethylammonium chloride, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. sodium hydroxide, 2-3 pts. wt. phthalic anhydride, 14-18 pts. wt. disperse Red S-R, 0.4-0.6 pt. wt. cetylpyridine chloride, 0.2-0.3 pt. wt. cetylpyridine bromide, 1-1.6 pts. wt. lanthanum nitrate, and 400-600 pts. wt. sterile water.
