   ADVANTAGE - The zinc catalyst has efficiency up to 217 g polymer/g catalyst, and the molar content of polycarbonate in the copolymer exceeds 93%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Zinc catalyst is 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalaldehyde zinc complex, and is represented by formula (I). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing polycyclohexylene carbonate by using zinc-based catalyst, which involves:
   (A) carbon dioxide and epoxy cyclohexane;
   (B) heating autoclave to 85 degrees C and sealing;
   (C) adding nitrogen and removing water 3 times;
   (D) adding cyclohexene oxide and 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalate zinc complex, raising temperature to 80~160 degrees C and charging carbon dioxide at 3-6MPa;
   (E) reacting for 10-40 hours and lowering temperature to room temperature and releasing pressure;
   (F) dissolving resulting product in methylene chloride; and
   (G) precipitating with anhydrous methanol, and drying the precipitated product under vacuum overnight to obtain polycyclohexylene carbonate.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The ligand of the complex catalyst is 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalate, and the center of metal is diethyl zinc. 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalate ligand is prepared dissolving 2-amino-6-methylpyridine and phthalaldehyde in methanol, and adding 2-amino-6-methylpyridine solution dropwise to the phthalaldehyde solution while stirring at room temperature. After the addition is complete, the mixture is heated under reflux and stirring is continued for 3 hours. The precipitate is filtered off, and the precipitate is washed with ether and dried to obtain 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalate ligand. The 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalate ligand is added into a dry flask and cooled in an ice bath. A solution of diethyl zinc in toluene is added under nitrogen protection at a concentration of 10% and stirred for 1 hour to remove the ice salt bath. The mixture is heated to 60 degrees C and stirred for 1 hour to obtain a 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalaldehyde zinc complex. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 2-amino-6-methylpyridine to phthalaldehyde is 2:0.8-1.2. The molar ratio of 2-amino-6-methylpyridine phthalaldehyde to diethyl zinc is from 1:1 to 1.2. The mass of the catalyst is 1:100-500 for the mass of epoxy cyclohexane.

PT P
PN WO2020165479-A1; ES2779058-A1; ES2779058-B2
TI Preparing refined sugar syrup from raw sugar used for bottling plants for non-alcoholic sugary beverages, involves dissolving solid raw sugar by hot recirculation until obtaining a syrup, adding reagents for the formation of primary clots.
AU HOLGER F
   FUNKE H
AE SEPPELEC SL (SEPP-Non-standard)
GA 2020797728
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing refined sugar syrup from raw sugar involves dissolving solid raw sugar by hot recirculation until obtaining a syrup with a concentration of 62-64 Brix (62-64 g dissolved sugar per 100 g of solution), and adding reagents for the formation of primary clots and that improve the physicochemical characteristics of the syrup during the dissolution stage. A flocculating polymer and carbon dioxide is added, which positively modify the nature of the gas-liquid and solid-liquid interface, and agglomerations of flocs is formed that rise with the help of carbon dioxide (CO2) to the surface. The obtained syrup mixture is stirred and clarified by flotation, in order to obtain a clean syrup at the bottom, where mud and impurities rise to the surface and are compacted together thanks to the help of carbon dioxide. The clean syrup is filtered and elimined non-floating sludge, and the syrup obtained by the counterflow ion exchange technique is discolored.
   USE - Method for preparing refined sugar syrup from raw sugar used for bottling plants for non-alcoholic sugary beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare refined sugar syrup from raw sugar improves the physicochemical characteristics of the syrup, ensures the elimination of the microbial load, does not produce caramelization, and no chemical bleaches are used in the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing refined sugar syrup from raw sugar involves dissolving solid raw sugar by hot recirculation until obtaining a syrup with a concentration of 62-64 Brix (62-64 g dissolved sugar per 100 g of solution), and adding reagents for the formation of primary clots and that improve the physicochemical characteristics of the syrup during the dissolution stage. A flocculating polymer and carbon dioxide is added, which positively modify the nature of the gas-liquid and solid-liquid interface, and agglomerations of flocs is formed that rise with the help of carbon dioxide (CO2) to the surface. The obtained syrup mixture is stirred and clarified by flotation, in order to obtain a clean syrup at the bottom, where mud and impurities rise to the surface and are compacted together thanks to the help of carbon dioxide. The clean syrup is filtered and elimined non-floating sludge, and the syrup obtained by the counterflow ion exchange technique is discolored. The syrup of the reverse ionic exchange technique is demineralized to eliminate conductometric ash and metals at a temperature of 60-40 degrees C. The syrup using counter flow adsorbent resins is deodorizes, polished the syrup to provide a final finish with a degree of turbidity between 1-3 NTU, and cooled to room temperature for storage and later use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The process is carried out at a temperature of 75-85 degrees C, with the solid sugar being supplied, preferably through a Venturi feeding system. The primary clot-forming reagents are selected from among calcium oxide, phosphoric acid and metal sulfate salts, or polymerized coagulants. The high molecular weight anionic polyacrylamide is used as flocculant. A serpentine diffuser is used with nozzles whose opening is 0.2-0.6 mm arranged on the coil so that fine CO2 bubbles form over the entire surface. The process is carried out with diatomaceous earth filters with a particle size distribution between 10-20 microns. The process of the syrup is performed at a temperature between 75 degrees C and 70 degrees C, passed from the tank. The syrup is passed through an anionic resin with a strong base and subsequently through a column that contains a high pK weak acid cationic resin. The macroporous adsorbent resins are used of the process. The process uses bag or cartridge filters. The recovered fresh water is reused for the hot dissolution of the raw sugar after its pasteurization at 70-80 degrees C. The sugar syrup obtained has the following characteristics 99.7-99.99 wt.% purity, 1-3 NTU turbidity, 4.0-4.5 pH, color less than or equal to 35 IU, ash content 0.04 - 0.01%, sediment content less than 5 mg/kg and mold and yeast content less than 10 colony-forming unit (CFU)/10 g.

PT P
PN CN110980792-A
TI Processing high-purity copper oxide powder used in            integrated circuit, by adding ammonia-copper carbonate            stock solution to centrifuge, heating solid mixture,            and adding high-purity basic copper carbonate to            circulating electric furnace.
AU XU Y
AE HANYA SEMICONDUCTOR MATERIALS GUIXI CO (HANY-Non-standard)
GA 2020303564
AB    NOVELTY - Processing high-purity copper oxide powder                used in integrated circuit, by (i) passing liquid                nitrogen into water, passing carbon dioxide into                concentrated aqueous ammonia, (ii) adding                carbonized aqueous ammonia to ammonia bicarbonate                and copper material, bubbling oxygen, (iii) adding                ammonia-copper carbonate stock solution to                ultra-high-speed centrifuge, adding deionized water                to rinse for solid-liquid separation to obtain                solid mixture containing basic copper carbonate and                ammonium carbonate, collecting aqueous ammonia and                drying solid mixture, and (iv) heating solid                mixture in reaction kettle, separating and                collecting high-purity basic copper, heating and                separating ammonium carbonate into ammonia gas,                carbon dioxide and water, and collecting ammonia,                and (v) adding high-purity basic copper carbonate                to heat-sealed circulating electric furnace,                cooling and sieving to obtain high-purity copper                oxide powder, and collecting carbon dioxide and                water vapor.
   USE - The process is useful for processing                high-purity copper oxide powder used in integrated                circuit.
   ADVANTAGE - The process avoids introduction of heavy metal                ions and chloride ions, ensures purity of copper                oxide products, and improves economical                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for processing high-purity copper                oxide powder used in integrated circuit, involves                (i) passing liquid nitrogen into water to prepare                140-165 g/l concentrated aqueous ammonia, passing                carbon dioxide into concentrated aqueous ammonia to                prepare carbonized aqueous ammonia with a                carbonization degree of 60-75%, (ii) adding                carbonized aqueous ammonia to ammonia bicarbonate                and copper material, bubbling oxygen during the                reaction to prepare ammonia-copper carbonate stock                solution, where the ratio of copper material,                carbonized aqueous ammonia, ammonia bicarbonate and                oxygen is 2:6:2:1, where the reaction formula is:                2Cu+6NH3+2NH4HCO3+O2 gives 2Cu(NH3)4CO3+2H2O, (iii)                adding the ammonia-copper carbonate stock solution                to the ultra-high-speed centrifuge, adding                deionized water to rinse for solid-liquid                separation to obtain a solid mixture containing                basic copper carbonate and ammonium carbonate,                collecting aqueous ammonia and drying solid mixture                by a dryer, where the reaction formula is:                2Cu(NH3)4CO3+2H2O gives                CuCO3-Cu(OH)2+(NH4)2CO3+6NH3, and (iv) adding the                solid mixture to a reaction kettle, heating at                58-75 degrees C, separating and collecting                high-purity basic copper, heating and separating                ammonium carbonate into ammonia gas, carbon dioxide                and water, and collecting ammonia, where the                reaction formula is: (NH4)2CO3 gives 2NH3+CO2+H2O,                and (v) adding the high-purity basic copper                carbonate to a heat-sealed circulating electric                furnace to obtain copper oxide powder, cooling and                sieving to obtain high-purity copper oxide powder,                and collecting carbon dioxide and water vapor,                where the reaction formula for calcination and                decomposition is: CuCO3-Cu(OH)2 gives                2CuO+H2O+CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves using                  the aqueous ammonia collected in step (iii), the                  ammonia gas collected in step (iv) and the carbon                  dioxide and water vapor collected in step (v) to                  prepare ammonium bicarbonate, where the reaction                  formula is: CO2+NH3+H2O gives NH4HCO3. The step                  (i) involves maintaining the pressure of passing                  carbon dioxide into the concentrated aqueous                  ammonia at 0.05-0.2 MPa, feeding the carbon                  dioxide into the concentrated aqueous ammonia                  feeding cooling coil located in the concentrated                  aqueous ammonia with cooling water having a                  temperature of less than 15 degrees C for                  cooling. The step (iv) involves transferring the                  ammonia gas in the reaction kettle from the lower                  portion of the condenser, performing second-stage                  condensation, entering the fifth-stage ammonia                  absorption tower, automatically controlling the                  water pump of the fifth-stage ammonia absorption                  tower by programmable logic controller,                  determining whether to replenish water, where the                  automatic system drives the ammonia storage tank                  in time according to the amount of ammonia in the                  absorption tower. Preferred Condition: The drying                  process step (iii) is carried out at 60-100                  degrees C.

TI Carbon dioxide laser three-tube structure, has first discharge pipe whose end portion is connected with end of second discharge pipe through refractor, and third discharge pipe whose end is provided with output mirror.
AU ZHAN W
AE NANTONG ZHUORUI LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)

PN CN114558543-A
TI Preparing absorbent used for absorbing carbon            dioxide in flue gas comprises calcining calcium-based            material, and pulverizing calcium-based calcined            product, adding calcium-based calcined powder into            water, mixing, adding ethylene bis-stearic acid amide            and potassium carbonate and mixing.
AU CHEN G
   ZHANG M
   CUI G
   WANG Z
AE SICHUAN YINENG KANGSHENG ENVIRONMENTAL (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202277445C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing an absorbent comprises (1) calcining                the calcium-based material to obtain a                calcium-based calcined product, and pulverizing the                calcium-based calcined product after cooling to                obtain a calcium-based calcined powder, (2) adding                calcium-based calcined powder into water, and                mixing uniformly, (3) adding calcium-based                bentonite and mixing evenly, slowly adding                calcium-based bentonite while stirring, (4) adding                ethylene bis-stearic acid amide and potassium                carbonate and mixing evenly, allowing to stand for                precipitation, drying and calcining to obtain an                efficient absorbent for carbon dioxide capture in                flue gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing an                absorbent, which is used for absorbing carbon                dioxide in flue gas in a circulating absorption                method in a carbonation furnace (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent: has high absorption capacity.                The method: adopts recycling process, repeatedly                calcining, the microscopic structure is small, and                the cycle performance is good.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium-based                  material is limestone. The content of the                  calcium-based bentonite montmorillonite is                  greater than 92% and 325 meshes. In the step (2),                  the mass ratio of the calcium-based calcined                  powder to the water is 10-15: 80-100. The mass                  ratio of the added calcium-based bentonite to the                  calcium-based calcined powder is 4-5: 1. In the                  step (4), the addition of ethylene bis-stearic                  acid amide is 1-1.5% of the weight of the                  calcium-based calcined powder, and the addition                  of the potassium carbonate is 2-2.3% of the                  weight of the calcium-based calcined                  powder.Preferred Method: In the process, (1), fully                  calcining the calcium-based material for 2 hours                  under the condition of 800&#8451; to obtain                  calcium-based calcined product, after cooling,                  crushing the calcium-based calcined product to                  obtain calcium-based calcined powder with a                  particle diameter of 300 meshes, (2) adding the                  calcium based calcined powder into water, the                  mass ratio of calcium-based calcined powder to                  water is 10: 90, mixing uniformly, (3) adding                  calcium-based bentonite to mix uniformly, the                  mass ratio of calcium based bentonite and                  calcium-based calcined powder is 4: 1, under the                  stirring speed of 500-600 revolutions per minute,                  slowly adding calcium-based bentonite, (4) adding                  ethylene bis stearamide and potassium carbonate                  mixed uniformly, adding amount of ethylene bis                  stearamide is calcium base calcined powder weight                  of 1%, the adding amount of the potassium                  carbonate is calcium-based calcined powder weight                  of 2%, allowing to stand and precipitating,                  drying the condition of 800&#8451;, fully                  calcining for 2 hours to obtain high-efficient                  absorbent for carbon dioxide capture the flue                  gas.

TI Manufacture of electrochemical catalyst structure            used for converting carbon dioxide, involves thermally            treating metal nanoparticles and carbon nanofiber            composite to selectively oxidize carbon and forming            graphite carbon shell on metal nanoparticles.
AU JOO Y C
   KIM J Y
AE UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 202240623G
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of electrochemical catalyst                structure involves preparing metal nanoparticles                and carbon nanofiber composite, thermally treating                the composite to selectively oxidize carbon and                forming a graphite carbon shell on the surface of                the metal nanoparticles.
   USE - Manufacture of electrochemical catalyst                structure (claimed) used for converting carbon                dioxide for forming 2C or more hydrocarbon                compounds such as ethylene used as raw material for                various chemical processes such as polyethylene and                ethylene glycol synthesis, or synthesis gas such as                hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of                electrochemical catalyst structure with increased                stability even at high current density reduction                potential when carbon dioxide is converted into 2C                or more hydrocarbon compounds or synthesis gas, by                introducing the graphite carbon shell into the                metal nanoparticles through selective oxidation                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                electrochemical catalyst structure, which comprises                the metal nanoparticles distributed on carbon                nanofibers and graphite carbon shell coated on the                surface of the metal nanoparticles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of preparing the                  composite is performed by electrospinning a                  spinning solution containing a metal precursor                  and a carbon nanofiber precursor. The graphite                  carbon shell is produced by decomposing carbon                  monoxide produced by oxidation of carbon on the                  surface of metal nanoparticles. The graphite                  carbon shell is coated to a nano thickness. The                  heat treatment is performed in an atmosphere in                  which only carbon is selectively oxidized without                  metal being oxidized. The heat treatment is                  performed by adjusting the oxygen partial                  pressure. The method involves doping a p-block                  element other than oxygen (O) to the metal                  nanoparticles in which the graphite carbon shell                  is formed. The p-block element is doped on the                  surface of the metal nanoparticles by penetrating                  the graphite carbon shell. Preferred Components:                  The metal nanoparticles include a transition                  metal or a transition metal compound. The                  transition metal is chosen copper, nickel, iron                  and/or cobalt. The metal precursor includes at                  least one chosen from metal acetate, metal                  nitride, and metal chloride. The p-block element                  includes at least one of boron, nitrogen,                  phosphorus, chlorine and fluorine. The metal                  nanoparticles include a transition metal or a                  transition metal compound. The electrochemical                  catalyst structure further comprises a p-block                  element of groups 13 to 17, doped on the surface                  of the metal nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon nanofiber                  precursor is polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyaniline,                  polypyrrole, cyanamide or polybenzimidazole. The                  carbon nanofiber is a porous structure.

TI Preparing nickel-zirconium co-doped mesoporous silica material used as catalyst in methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction, involves dissolving nickel nitrate in water, and adding ammonia water to obtain nickel-containing solution.
AU WANG X
   PAN W
   YUAN X
   CAO X

AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2021A7987N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-zirconium co-doped mesoporous silica material involves dissolving nickel nitrate in water, adding ammonia water to obtain a nickel-containing solution, and then dissolving zirconyl nitrate in water to obtain zirconium-containing solution. The nickel nitrate is nickel nitrate containing crystal water or nickel nitrate without crystal water. The zirconyl nitrate contains or does not contain crystal water. The zirconium-containing solution, the nickel-containing solution and the silica sol are mixed, heated and stirred, and dried to obtain a solid product. The solid product is roasted at 600-800 degrees C for 4-7 hours to obtain a nickel-zirconium co-doped mesoporous silica material.
   USE - Method for preparing nickel-zirconium co-doped mesoporous silica material used as catalyst in methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the nickel-zirconium co-doped mesoporous silica material in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with high yield, and good catalytic activity in the catalytic methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction.

The inlet stream or inlet streams to last                  reactor of two or more reactors is or are not                  cooled.

PN KR2214668-B1
TI Drying of ecamsule powder used for drugs and cosmetics, involves injecting ecamsule solution into reactor, continuously supplying liquefied carbon dioxide at flow rate, and reducing pressure inside reactor to atmospheric pressure.
AU KIM J H
   MYINT A A
   HARIYANTO P
AE UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
GA 202117048Q
AB    NOVELTY - Drying of ecamsule powder involves (i) injecting ecamsule solution into the reactor and raising the temperature to a temperature (T1), (ii) continuously supplying liquefied carbon dioxide to the reactor at a flow rate (a1),(iii) raising the pressure of the reactor to a predetermined pressure (P1) so that the carbon dioxide becomes a supercritical state and maintaining the pressure for a time (t1), (iv) increasing the pressure inside the reactor from the pressure (P1) to a pressure (P2) and applying a flow pressure cycle for lowering the pressure to the pressure (P1) for a predetermined number of times, and (v) reducing the pressure inside the reactor to atmospheric pressure.
   USE - Drying of ecamsule powder used in drugs and cosmetics useful for protecting skin from sunlight and preventing diseases associated with UV exposure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and enables drying of ecamsule powder by completely removing ethanol with reduced energy consumption in a short period of time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The temperature (T1) is carried out at 40-60 degrees C. The step (iii) is carried out at a pressure (P1) of 10-12 MPa for 2-5 minutes. In the step (iv), the flow pressure cycle is maintained for 100 times, and the pressure (P2) is maintained for 1.3-1.6 times of the pressure (P1). The step (iv) is carried out for 35-60 minutes. Preferred Property: The reactor has a flow rate (r1) of 20-40 mL/minute based on 250 mL of the ecamsule solution. Preferred Process: The method further involves separating ethanol and carbon dioxide from the mixture discharged from the reactor.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing hydrogen from ultra-thick coal                layer transwell injection-sampling involves                arranging gas injection wells and production wells                containing deep and ultra-thick coal seams,                carrying out blasting pressure operation at bottom                of injection well, so that there are gaps in coal                seam within bottom of well, and coal seam gas is                produced, injecting air, oxygen and steam to gas                injection wells, keeping formation pressure above                22.17 MPa, igniting coal seam at the bottom of                well, so that coal seam temperature exceeds 578                degrees C, producing carbon dioxide and heat by                supercritical combustion gasification of oxygen                with coal, producing carbon monoxide and hydrogen                under the action of heat by carbon dioxide and                coal, applying heat on coal seams in dry zone to                release coal bed methane gas, reacting with carbon                monoxide with carbon dioxide and hydrogen to                produce methane and discharging carbon monoxide,                hydrogen, and methane from gas well.

PT P
PN CN110343134-A; CN110343134-B
TI Process for the preparation of bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide, involves phenylphosphine metallization and carbon dioxide to directly construct phenylphosphinodicarboxylate, and then in situ activation of carboxyl group.
AU ZHANG Z
   ZHOU Y
AE ZHANG Z (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2019891443
AB    NOVELTY - Process for the preparation of bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide, involves phenylphosphine metallization and carbon dioxide (CO2) to directly construct phenylphosphinodicarboxylate, and then in situ activation of the carboxyl group into weinreb amide derivative (I) with N,O-dimethylhydroxylamine hydrochloride (DMHA), then using 2,4,6- trimethylphenyl grignard reagent or organolithium reagent attack, addition to obtain the main skeleton (II), and finally oxidized to obtain bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide.
   USE - Process for the preparation of bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for the preparation of bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide, involves phenylphosphine metallization and carbon dioxide (CO2) to directly construct phenylphosphinodicarboxylate, and then in situ activation of the carboxyl group into weinreb amide derivative of formula (I) with N,O-dimethylhydroxylamine hydrochloride (DMHA), then using 2,4,6- trimethylphenyl grignard reagent or organolithium reagent attack, addition to obtain the main skeleton of formula (II), and finally oxidized to obtain bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Using phenylphosphine or phenylphosphine dichloride as a starting material, and preparing a phenylphosphine metalate intermediate with an active metal or an alkyl metal reagent, further introducing CO2 gas to prepare a phenylphosphinodicarboxylate. Condensation of diformate with N,O-dimethylhydroxylamine hydrochloride (DMHA) acid amine under basic conditions, condensation product weinreb amide derivative of with 2,4,6-trimethylphenyl grignard reagent or Organic lithium reagent addition. Finally, an oxidizing agent is added to carry out an oxidation reaction to obtain bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide. The active metal includes sodium metal, potassium, and lithium and the metal alkyl reagent includes alkyl lithium or sodium alkyl. Under the protection of inert gas, 1 equivalent of phenylphosphine or phenylphosphine dichloride and 2-2.5 equivalents of active metal or alkyl metal reagent in a dry organic solvent, stirred to phenylphosphine at a certain temperature metallization is formed, and then dry CO2 is slowly introduced to obtain a phenylphosphonium dicarboxylate. 1 equivalent of phenylphosphonic acid diformate, 2-3 equivalents of DMHA, 4-10 equivalents of organic base are placed in a dry organic solvent, and reacted at room temperature to 60 degrees C to obtain Weinreb amide. Under the protection of inert gas, 1 equivalent of Weinreb amide is dissolved in a dry organic solvent, and 2-3 equivalents of 2,4,6-trimethylbenzene Grignard reagent or organolithium reagent is added dropwise to obtain product. An oxidizing agent is added to carry out an oxidation reaction to obtain bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phenylphosphine oxide. 1 equivalent of phenylphosphine dicarboxylate, 2-3 equivalents of DMHA, 3-4.5 equivalents of a condensation activator, 4-10 equivalents, organic base is placed in a dry organic solvent and reacted at room temperature to 60 degrees C to obtain a weinreb amide, where the condensation activator comprises Hexafluorophosphate Azabenzotriazole Tetramethyl Uronium (HATU), hydroxybenzotriazole and triazole/1-Ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide (HOBT/EDCI), phophorud tetrachloride (PCl3), preferably PCl3, where the reaction temperature is preferably 40 degrees C. Preferred Components: The organic solvent described includes one or more of an alkane, an aromatic hydrocarbon, and an ether. The temperature is selected from --70-30 degrees C. The alkane comprises pentane, hexane, heptane, aromatic hydrocarbon comprises benzene, toluene and the ether comprises diethyl ether and methyl ether, butyl ether, tetrahydrofuran, the temperature is preferably -30-20 degrees C. The organic base described in the second step includes triethylamine, diisopropylethylamine (DBU) the organic solvent includes one or more of an alkane, an aromatic hydrocarbon, and an ether, preferably, the alkane comprises pentane, hexane, heptane. The aromatic hydrocarbons include benzene and toluene and the ethers include diethyl ether, methyl tert-butyl ether, and tetrahydrofuran. The organic solvent is selected from one or more of diethyl ether, methyl tert-butyl ether and tetrahydrofuran, the grignard reagent of 2,4,6-trimethylbenzene includes 2,4, 6-trimethylphenylmagnesium bromide, 2,4,6-trimethylphenylmagnesium chloride. The organolithium reagent includes 2,4,6-trimethylphenyllithium. The oxidizing agent described in the fourth step is selected from the peroxyacetic acid, peroxybenzoic acid, peroxybutanol, hydrogen peroxide, ozone, nitric acid, and nitrate. Preferred Conditions: After the dry CO2 is introduced in the first step, 2-3 equivalents of DMHA are directly added to obtain Weinreb amide.
DC E11 (Containing P and/or Si.)

PN CN109824470-A; CN109824470-B
TI Conversion of benzene into toluene and xylene in            benzene-rich gasoline by syngas comprises in presence            of metal oxide/solid acid catalyst, catalytically            reacting syngas with catalytic reforming benzene-rich            gasoline and cooling.
AU DU J
   LIU J
   ZHANG X
   LIN C
   NIE H
   ZHANG L
   XU L
   HE C
   XU Y
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201951029R
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of benzene into toluene and xylene                in benzene-rich gasoline by syngas comprises in the                presence of a metal oxide/solid acid catalyst,                catalytically reacting the syngas with the                catalytic reforming benzene-rich gasoline, cooling                the reaction product to separate water, separating                the 60-90 degrees C cut fraction, which is a high                octane number gasoline component, where the syngas                has carbon dioxide (CO2) fraction of 2-20 vol.%,                and the catalytic reforming benzene-rich gasoline                has benzene fraction of 10-90 vol.%, and subjecting                the high octane number gasoline component to                solvent extraction and rectification to obtain                toluene and xylene.
   USE - The method is for conversion of benzene into                toluene and xylene in benzene-rich gasoline by                syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method directly uses the syngas as raw                material to prepare toluene and xylene at a high                conversion rate, simplifies the reaction process,                reduces the production cost, and utilizes carbon                dioxide to reduce carbon dioxide emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The syngas has CO of 0-28                  vol.%. The ratio of the total volume of CO2 to CO                  to the volume of hydrogen is 1:2.3-4. The metal                  oxide is copper oxide (CuO)-zinc oxide                  (ZnO)-alumina (Al2O3). The syngas has a gas space                  velocity of 1500-6000/hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The solid acid catalyst is                  an imidazole-modified HZSM-5 catalyst. In                  CuO-ZnO-Al2O3/imidazole-modified HZSM-5 catalyst,                  the mass ratio of CuO-ZnO-Al2O3 to                  imidazole-modified HZSM-5 is 1-3:1. In the                  catalytic reaction, the catalytic reforming                  benzene-rich gasoline has a liquid space velocity                  of 0.5-3/hour. The catalytic reaction is                  conducted at 3-8 MPa and 320-420 degrees C using                  a fixed bed reactor.

TI Extracting ionone in sweet Osmanthus by crushing dried sweet Osmanthus medicinal material into powder, extracting, introducing carbon dioxide into kettle, extracting and performing resin adsorption and analysis on upper layer material.
AU REN L
AE REN L (RENL-Individual)
GA 201929482C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting ionone in sweet Osmanthus involves (i) cleaning the sweet Osmanthus, (ii) drying the cleaned sweet Osmanthus medicinal material to a surface moisture content of less than or equal to 0.6%, (iii) crushing the dried sweet Osmanthus medicinal material into a powder having a particle size of 40-120 mu m, (iv) placing the crushed sweet Osmanthus medicinal material powder into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, (v) extracting under an environment with an analytical temperature of 15-50 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 4-8 MPa, introducing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 20-35 l/hour and extracting for 2-4 hours, (vi) leaving still at room temperature for 3-10 hours and taking the upper layer material, and (vii) adsorbing and analyzing the upper layer material through a resin and drying the upper layer material.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting ionone in sweet Osmanthus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the extraction efficiency of ionone.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The dried sweet Osmanthus medicinal material is crushed into powder having a particle size of 80 mu m. The step (v) involves extracting under an environment with an analytical temperature of 25 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 6 MPa, introducing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 25 l/hour and extracting for 2.5 hours. The step (vii) specifically involves adsorbing and analyzing the upper layer material through a resin having a pore diameter of 350 nm.

PT P
PN WO2018206237-A1; CA3059649-A1; AU2018264670-A1; CN110603338-A; KR2020004380-A; EP3622094-A1; US2020190625-A1; JP2020519759-W; EP3622094-B1; ES2886195-T3
TI Reducing content of oxygen, present as copper            oxide, in molten metallic copper by adding carbon            monoxide produced from carbon dioxide in solid oxide            electrolysis cell stack during copper refining process            to remove oxygen.
AU TARASENKO A
   JAKOBSSON N B
   BLENNOW B P G
   HADSBJERG C
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2018897310
AB    NOVELTY - Content of oxygen, present as copper oxide, in                molten metallic copper obtained by conventional                method and subjected to copper refining process, is                reduced by adding carbon monoxide produced from                carbon dioxide in solid oxide electrolysis cell                (SOEC) stack during refining process to remove                oxygen according to reaction of copper(II) oxide                (CuO) and carbon monoxide (CO) to afford copper                (Cu) and carbon dioxide (CO2) to increase purity of                metallic copper.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing content of                oxygen, present as copper oxide, in molten metallic                copper obtained by conventional method and                subjected to copper refining process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a good match for providing CO to                reduce oxygen content in copper, where the mixture                of CO and CO2 obtained by electrolysis of CO2 can                be fed directly into copper refining process, which                significantly reduces cost and greatly reduces the                plant complexity of the CO generating plant. In the                method, hydrogen is avoided, which will otherwise                increase the brittleness of the produced                copper.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Processes: The gas from the SOEC                  stack is compressed and added to copper melt                  through injection system that disperses reducing                  gas in small bubbles throughout copper melt to                  maximize gas/solid contact surface. The gas from                  SOEC stack is purified to greater than or equal                  to 99% pure carbon monoxide and mixed with                  nitrogen prior to injection into the copper melt.                  Preferred Composition: The gas from SOEC stack                  comprises greater than or equal to 10%,                  preferably greater than or equal to 40% CO, and                  balance CO2 or inert gas.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN108654628-A
TI Preparation of nickel-cerium-zirconium composite oxide/gamma-alumina catalyst for methanation catalysis of carbon dioxide, involves mixing nickel salt, cerium salt, zirconium salt and citric acid mixed aqueous solution with gamma-alumina.
AU NI W
   WANG X
   SHENG Y
   ZOU X
   SHANG X
AE NANTONG LONGXIANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 201883722T
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of nickel-cerium-zirconium composite oxide/( gamma )-alumina catalyst involves (1) providing a mixed aqueous solution of nickel salt, cerium salt, zirconium salt and citric acid, mixing the mixed aqueous solution with ( gamma )-alumina, and heating to obtain gel, where the( gamma )-alumina has specific surface area of 250-550 m2/g, pore volume of 0.2-0.4 cm3/g, and an aperture of 2-5 nm, and (2) drying the obtained gel in order, and calcining.
   USE - Preparation method of nickel-cerium-zirconium composite oxide/( gamma )-alumina catalyst (claimed) used for methanation catalysis of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nickel-cerium-zirconium composite oxide/( gamma )-alumina catalyst with carbon dioxide conversion rate of 92% and methane conversion selectivity of 100%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for nickel-cerium-zirconium composite oxide/( gamma )-alumina catalyst, which comprises ( gamma )-alumina substrate and a nickel-cerium-zirconium composite oxide that is dispersed on a surface of the ( gamma )-alumina.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The sum of the amounts of the substances of nickel, cerium and zirconium in the mixed aqueous solution is 1-2 times the amount of the substance of citric acid. The amount of nickel element in the mixed aqueous solution is 5-50 %mass by mass of ( gamma )-alumina. The amount of cerium element and zirconium element in the mixed aqueous solution is 10-30 %mass by mass of ( gamma )-alumina. Preferred Conditions: In the step (1), the heating temperature is 70-90 degrees C. In the step (2), the drying temperature is 90-110 degrees C and the drying time is 10-14 hours. In the step (2), the calcining temperature is 450-550 degrees C and the calcining time is 8-12 hours.

PT P
PN US9919241-B1; CA3036013-A1
TI Extracting terpenes, cannabinoids, and tetrahydrocannabinol from cannabis involves placing dried cannabis in extraction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide, moving carbon dioxide with extracted components to separator, and removing products.
AU AUERBACH M I
   REILLY W L
AE ENTHEO GARDENS INC (ENTH-Non-standard)
   ENTHEO GARDENS INC (ENTH-Non-standard)
GA 201821821C
AB    NOVELTY - Terpenes, cannabinoids, and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) are extracted from cannabis by (a) placing dried cannabis in an extraction vessel; (b) introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) into extraction vessel at an extraction pressure of 500-2000 psi and at - 50-80 degrees F; (c) extracting components from cannabis; (d) moving CO2 with extracted components from the extraction vessel to 1st separator held at a 1st separator pressure of 500-900 psi and at 60-600 degrees F to drop out extracted terpenes, cannabinoids, or THC; and (e) removing extracted terpenes, cannabinoids, or THC from the 1st separator.
   USE - Extraction of terpenes, cannabinoids, and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) from cannabis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a high-cannabinoid full spectrum (HCFSE) extraction that is ready to use and that does not require post processing. Lower pressure and colder temperatures results in higher terpene values in the output. The device can be operated to provide a high terpene output with low cannabinoid, or a high terpene high cannabinoid output, as desired, with no or less wax content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further includes moving CO2 with any remaining extracted components from 1st separator to 2nd separator at a pressure of 500-900 psi and at 120-130 degrees F; and removing any additional extracted terpenes, cannabinoids, or THC from the 2nd separator. The method further includes (f) cooling CO2 after 2nd separator to return it to a liquid state. The method further includes (g) reintroducing the cooled CO2 to the extraction vessel at an extraction pressure of 500-2000 psi and at temperatures of - 50-80 degrees F, and cycling through steps (c) through (g) for 1-24 hours. Preferred Conditions: Extraction pressure is 500-700 psi to extract high terpene low cannabinoid components. The 1st separator pressure is 500-600 psi to extract high terpene low cannabinoid components. Extraction pressure is 500-700 psi, and 1st separator pressure is 500-600 psi to extract high terpene low cannabinoid components. Extraction pressure is 900-2000 psi to extract high terpene high cannabinoid components. The 1st separator pressure is 600-900 psi to extract high terpene high cannabinoid components. The 1st separator pressure is 500-600 psi, and the 2nd separator pressure is 500 psi to extract high terpene low cannabinoid components. Extraction pressure is 900-2000 psi, and 2nd separator pressure is 600-1200 psi to extract high terpene high cannabinoid components. The 1st separator is held at a temperature of 120-130 degrees F. Repeating steps (c)-(f) is continued for 1-24 hours, preferably 1-2 hours.
DC B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2022225974-A1
TI Bioreactor used to culture immune cells, has            housing which defines internal cavity having            frustoconical shape formed so that horizontal            cross-sections are coaxial about line which is angled            with respect to vertical axis and multiple ports.
AU FAINSHTEIN M
   GUBAEV N
   CAPLAN V
AE ORGENESIS INC (ORGE-Non-standard)
GA 2022D3053R
AB    NOVELTY - Bioreactor comprises a housing (14) extending                along a vertical axis and defining an internal                cavity within the housing, and top and bottom                covers at opposite ends of the housing, and                multiple ports, each configured to facilitate fluid                communication between the internal cavity and the                exterior of the bioreactor, where the internal                cavity has a frustoconical shape. The frustoconical                shape is formed such that horizontal cross-sections                are coaxial about a line which is angled with                respect to the vertical axis.
   USE - The bioreactor is useful for culturing immune                cells, preferably CAR T cells configured for                adoptive cell therapy (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bioreactor: has easy maintenance and has                low operating costs; optimizes cell culture, with                many factors influencing this optimization,                including dissolved oxygen (DO) and carbon dioxide                (CO2) levels; controls the level of oxygen and                carbon dioxide which can impact the culture media                pH and the product quality; and carries out                different mechanical and/or chemical processes                within the bioreactor to ensure that DO levels                remain within a predetermined range without causing                excessive shear stress on the cells being                cultured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   an apparatus;
   culturing immune cells comprising (a) seeding                a population of immune cells in media in the                internal cavity of a bioreactor, (b) activating the                immune cells, (c) transducing the activated immune                cells with a viral vector, (d) expanding the                transduced immune cells, and (e) harvesting the                transduced immune cells when the desired                concentration of cells is obtained;
   population of T cells expressing chimeric                antigen receptor;
   connection panel configured to facilitate                functionally connecting a bioreactor to a control                unit; and
   a kit for use with a connection panel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                asymmetric bioreactor.

TI Industrial system used for integration of carbon            monoxide-consuming process selected from carbohydrate            fermentation, autothermal reforming of biogas,            comprises gaseous substrate output conduit in fluid            communication with removal module comprising carbon            dioxide-treated gas stream output conduit.
AU SIMPSON S
   LI X
   BOURDAKOS N
   CONRADO R
   MIHALCEA C
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 2022C1533G
AB    NOVELTY - Industrial system comprises gaseous substrate                output conduit in fluid communication with a                removal module 120 comprising a carbon dioxide                (CO2)-treated gas stream output conduit 112,                CO2concentration module in fluid communication with                the CO2-treated gas stream output conduit and                comprises a CO2-concentrated gas stream output                conduit. A CO2electrolysis module 130 is set in                fluid communication with the CO2-concentrated gas                stream output conduit and comprises a carbon                monoxide (CO)-enriched stream output conduit and a                first oxygen (O2)-enriched stream output conduit.                An O2separation module is set in fluid                communication with the first O2-enriched stream                output conduit and comprises a second O2-enriched                stream output conduit in fluid communication with                the industrial system and an O2-lean stream output                conduit. A bioreactor comprises a culture of                C1-fixing microorganism in fluid communication with                the CO-enriched stream output conduit and O2-lean                stream output conduit.
   USE - Industrial system used for integration of                CO-consuming process selected from carbohydrate                fermentation, cement making, pulp and paper making,                steel making, oil refining and associated                processes, petrochemical production, coke                production, anaerobic or aerobic digestion,                gasification, natural gas extraction, oil                extraction, metallurgical processes, production                and/or refinement of aluminum, copper, and/or                ferroalloys, geological reservoirs, Fischer-Tropsch                processes, methanol production, pyrolysis, steam                methane reforming, dry methane reforming, partial                oxidation of biogas or natural gas, autothermal                reforming of biogas or natural gas, or any mixture                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The industrial system improves carbon                conversion efficiency by recycling CO2produced by a                CO-consuming process to the CO2electrolysis                process, and it is capable of providing substantial                benefits to the CO2-generating industrial process                and CO-consuming process, which is a C1-fixing                fermentation process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows integration of an                industrial process carried out by industrial                system.

PN US2022275752-A1
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide system for use in            power system for generating power for aircraft,            comprises turbine, which is operably coupled to            compressor, where generator is operably coupled to            turbine, and configured to generate power.
AU GREENE C B
   HOLLEY B M
AE RAYTHEON TECHNOLOGIES CORP (RAYT-C)
GA 2022B0672C
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical fluid system comprises a                turbine (404) operably coupled to the compressor                (402), where a generator (406) is operably coupled                to the turbine and configured to generate power. A                primary working fluid and a secondary working fluid                are compressed and mixed within the compressor to                form a mixture of the two fluids. The separator                (416) separates the primary working fluid from the                secondary working fluid from the mixture of the two                fluids and direct the primary working fluid back to                the turbine along the primary working fluid flow                path (408) and the secondary working fluid along                the secondary working fluid flow path (428) to the                generator. The secondary working fluid is hydrogen.                The secondary working fluid is helium. The                separator is a cyclone separator, a gravity                separator, or a membrane separator. The turbine is                a shrouded blade turbine. The compressor, the                generator, and the turbine are part of an                industrial generator.
   USE - Supercritical fluid system or supercritical                carbon dioxide system, for use in aircraft power                system (claimed) for generating power for aircraft.                Can also be used as a waste heat recovery system or                power generation system for a gas turbine engine or                high-bypass geared aircraft engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical fluid system improves                efficiency through the reduction of operating                temperatures while reducing parasitic losses                associated with pumping in such systems, where the                viscous and heat transfer properties of a secondary                working fluid as compared to a primary working                fluid can reduce windage and provide cooling to a                generator rotor. The additional cooling and                buffering can be provided through application of                the secondary working fluid to front-faces of the                turbomachinery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                aircraft power system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a closed                loop supercritical system.
   402Compressor
   404Turbine
   406Generator
   408Primary Working Fluid Flow Path
   416Separator
   428Secondary Working Fluid Flow Path

PN CN114874482-A
TI Preparing temperature-resistant polylactic acid            (PLA) knife and fork spoon used in catering tool,            comprises injection molding PLA knife, fork and spoon            raw material, and soaking obtained blank in carbon            dioxide at constant temperature and pressure to            complete crystallization of non-crystallized PLA.
AU ZHU Y
   HUANG G
   JIN Y
   HU Q
   TAO Y
   CHEN Q
   LIU Y
   LI W
AE TAIZHOU YUMI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (TAIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022B00236
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing temperature-resistant polylactic                acid (PLA) knife and fork spoon, comprises: (i)                injection molding PLA knife, fork and spoon raw                material through an injection molding machine to                prepare knife, fork and spoon-shaped blanks                containing PLA not capable of completing                crystallization; and (ii) soaking knife, fork and                spoon-shaped blank in carbon dioxide at constant                temperature and constant pressure to complete the                crystallization of non-crystallized PLA in knife,                fork and spoon-shaped blanks to obtain PLA knife,                fork and spoon products with temperature resistance                &#8805; 65C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                temperature-resistant polylactic acid knife, fork                and spoon, used in catering tool.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare                temperature-resistant PLA knife, fork and spoon,                which has excellent temperature resistance, bending                and cracking resistance, and is biodegradable, and                has better appearance, white or silver white. The                knife fork spoon is made into micro-nano-bubble                structure to reduce the raw material dosage and                reduce the production cost of the product. The                method uses PLA as main material, and hence the                produced knife-fork and spoon product can be used                as nucleating agent and can be made into                crystalline PLA knife fork. The cost of raw                material can be reduced by 10%. The PLA with common                trademark is used, which is good for reducing the                production costs of product, and the used waste                will not cause environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                temperature-resistant PLA knife, fork and spoon,                where the PLA knife, fork and spoon is a                micro-foaming product, the density of the knife                fork spoon is reduced by 5-30% relative to the                knife fork spoon-shaped blank, and the knife fork                spoon is provided with temperature resistance of                more than 65&#8451;.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the method                  for soaking in carbon dioxide, comprises placing                  PLA knife and fork spoon-shaped blank in                  temperature-adjustable sealed container that can                  resist high temperature and high pressure,                  injecting carbon dioxide into the sealed                  container, and soaking at constant temperature                  and pressure. In step (ii), the pressure                  maintaining temperature is 45-110&#8451;, the                  pressure maintaining pressure is 4-12 MPa, the                  pressure maintaining time is 6-60 minutes, and                  the pressure releasing speed is 5-45 MPa/second.                  The knife fork and spoon products of different                  temperature resistance is obtained by adjusting                  the formulation and soaking parameter, where the                  temperature-resistance of knife, fork and spoon                  product is &#8805; 65&#8451; and less                  than 100&#8451;, and the high                  temperature-resistance of knife, fork and spoon                  product is more than 100&#8451;. The knife,                  fork and spoon product with low density can be                  obtained by adjusting the soaking parameter, the                  pressure maintaining pressure is &#8805; 7                  MPa and less than 12 MPa, preferably &#8805;                  9MPa and less than 10.5MPa, the pressure                  releasing speed is &#8805; 24 MPa/second,                  preferably &#8805; 31 MPa/second. The density                  of the obtained knife, fork and spoon product is                  reduced when compared with the density of knife,                  fork and spoon-shaped blank, where the reduction                  percentage is 0-30% and does not contain 0.Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid or                  sub-critical carbon dioxide fluid. In step (i),                  the raw material of PLA knife, fork and spoon                  product comprises 50-80 wt.% main material, 0-20                  wt.% auxiliary material, 10-30 wt.% filler and                  0-5 wt.% modifier. The main material is PLA. The                  auxiliary material is polybutylene adipate                  terephthalate (PBAT), polybutylene succinate                  (PBS) or a blend of PBAT and PBS. The filler is                  talc, calcium carbonate, silicon dioxide,                  bentonite, coffee ground powder and/or bamboo                  powder. The modifier is nucleating agent,                  antioxidant, antistatic agent, antibacterial                  agent, color masterbatch, compatibilizer,                  toughening agent, lubricant, release agent, chain                  extender and/or crosslinking agent. The PLA is                  obtained from a single trademark or a blend of                  multiple trademarks. The raw material of PLA                  knife, fork and spoon is prepared into granules                  by extruding, blending and granulating.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.)

PN WO2022114690-A1; KR2022075836-A; TW202235245-A
TI Foam molding mold used for manufacturing shoe            insoles, has upper mold in which gas injection unit is            installed, lower mold having cavity for accommodating            foaming compounding resin, and channels for guiding gas            supplied from gas injection unit to cavity.
AU BAE S S
   KANG C E
   LEE J H
   KANG W J
   SONG H S
   HANSOO S
AE HANWHA SOLUTIONS CORP (HANW-C)
GA 202274709D
AB    NOVELTY - The mold (1) has an upper mold (10) having a                gas injection unit (15) installed in it. A lower                mold (20) has a cavity (30) accommodating the                foaming compounding resin. The lower mold has                channels (40, 50) for guiding the gas supplied from                the gas injection unit to the cavity. The upper                mold and the lower mold is a foam molding mold that                is detachable from each other. The cavity is formed                in the center of the lower mold. The gas is                selected from the group consisting of nitrogen,                carbon dioxide, supercritical carbon dioxide,                argon, and helium. The foaming compounding resin is                in the form of sheet or pellet.
   USE - Foam molding mold used for manufacturing shoe                insoles, midsoles, outsoles, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The channel for guiding gas is formed in the                cavity for accommodating the compounding resin for                foaming, so that it is possible to prevent clogging                of the gas injection portion, and is easy to clean                the mold with smooth gas injection, thus, the                processing time is shortened. The physical foaming                process using the foam molding mold does not use a                chemical foaming agent, so that the process is                eco-friendly. By shortening the processing time, a                foam with improved physical properties is                manufactured at low cost. The mold is able to                control the cell structure of the foam as it is                easy to change the processing conditions, and is                able to control mechanical properties including                split tear strength (STS) and rebound                elasticity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                physical foaming process using the foam molding                mold.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                foaming molding mold.
   Foam molding mold (1)
   Upper mold (10)
   Gas injection unit (15)
   Lower mold (20)
   Cavity (30)
   Channels (40,50)

PT P
PN WO2022066092-A1
TI Method for performing procedure such as gas-based stunning procedure and gas-based killing procedure on animal, involves covering animal with foam, and performing procedure using gas having gas composition suitable for performing procedure.
AU KIEZEBRINK H
AE KIEZEBRINK H (KIEZ-Individual)
GA 202247684T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves covering the animal with a foam. The procedure is performed using a gas having a gas composition suitable for performing the procedure. The animal is covered with a first medium. The first medium is a foam containing air, and subsequently a second medium is introduced below the foam. The second medium has the gas having composition suitable for performing the procedure. The second medium contains less than 2 percent by vol. oxygen, preferably less than 1 percent by vol. and more preferably less than 0.5 percent by vol. The second medium contains gas with a carbon dioxide concentration of less than 2 percent by vol. The second medium contains 98 percent by volume of nitrogen. The foam is a first foam, and the second medium is a second foam containing gas having the gas composition suitable for performing the procedure.
   USE - Method for performing procedure such as gas-based stunning procedure and gas-based killing procedure on animal such as poultry such as chicken and turkeys and mammals such as pigs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the problem of the breaking up of foam and is suitable for a space that may be as desired a container or a room (stable). The events of being covered with (first) foam and being exposed to a gas composition for the procedure are not concomitant, so the peak of stress may be reduced. The second medium is for example gas comprising a concentration of carbon dioxide that is at least 40 percent by vol. which may be used to render the animal unconscious and is heavier than air which helps it to remain trapped below the foam. The foam will serve to both hinder the access of oxygen to the fire and to shield the animal from the noxious gas, and the animals may be culled using the second medium more humanely.
DC D12 (Butchering, meat treatment, processing poultry or fish (A22).)

PN CN114214636-A
TI Preparation of cobalt-based nanosheet self-supporting electrode, comprises dissolving selenium-containing ligand-bis(3,5-dimethyl-1H-pyrazol-4-yl) selenium and cobalt salt in dimethylformamide solution, and soaking self-supporting electrode substrate into mixed solution.
AU DU Z
   CAO L
   WEN X
   ZHANG J
   HE C
AE UNIV JIANGXI NORMAL (UYJX-C)
GA 202246830K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cobalt (Co)-based nanosheet self-supporting electrode, comprises dissolving selenium (Se)-containing ligand-bis(3,5-dimethyl-1H-pyrazol-4-yl) selenium H2Sebmpz and Co salt in dimethylformamide (DMF) solution to form a clear solution, then quickly soaking the clean self-supporting electrode substrate into the mixed solution, after reacting for 6-24 hours at 150-170degrees Celsius under stirring, taking out the self-supporting electrode and using DMF solution to wash for two times, drying in the vacuum oven at 55-65degrees Celsius, placing the dried Se-Co-PCP/CC ceramic boat in the center of ttubular furnace, and placing the porcelain boat with Sodium hypophosphite (NaH2PO2) upstream, heating the sample at 2degrees Celsius/minute from room temperature to 350-450degrees Celsius, naturally cooling to room temperature after constant temperature for 3 hours, and obtaining two-dimensional ultra-thin nanosheet (Se-CoP) self-supporting electrode of selenium-doped cobalt phosphide after pyrolyzing and annealing.
   USE - Preparation of cobalt-based nanosheet self-supporting electrode used for electrocatalysis water decomposition, oxygen reduction reaction (ORR), carbon dioxide reduction reaction (CO2RR) and multiple organic catalytic reactions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides cobalt-based nanosheet self-supporting electrode with high current output, high specific surface area and uniform distribution of metal nanoparticles. The method is simple, inexpensive and easy to prepare, and provides raw material with excellent catalytic performance from the supporting electrode, in the electrolytic water decomposition and oxygen reduction reaction (ORR), carbon dioxide reduction reaction, and multiple organic catalytic reactions. The preparation cost of the electrode is low, has potential industrial application value in the energy catalytic field, and can be used for electro-catalytic water- decomposition. The electrode shows excellent alkaline hydrothermal hydrogen/oxygen reduction reaction/oxide reduction reaction catalytic activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt salt comprises cobalt nitrate salt, cobalt acetate salt and/or cobalt chloride salt. The self-supporting electrode comprises carbon cloth, foam nickel, foam copper or carbon paper.

PN IN202041020460-A
TI Air quality management for in vehicles comprises first sensor to determine first particulate matter (PM) value corresponds to amount of particulate matter inside vehicle cabin, second sensor to determine second PM value to amount.
AU RAUT N
   LAKSHMANAN R
   REDDY R
AE NISSAN MOTOR CO LTD (NSMO-C)
GA 2021E7916X
AB    NOVELTY - Air quality management system comprises first sensor to determine a first particulate matter (PM) value corresponding to amount of particulate matter inside a vehicle cabin, a second sensor to determine a second PM value corresponding to amount of the particulate matter outside the vehicle, a sensing component to determine a CO2 value corresponding to amount of a carbon dioxide inside the vehicle cabin, and a controller to receive signals corresponding to the first PM value, second PM value, and the CO2value from the first sensor, the second sensor, and the sensing component, respectively and switch a mode of operation of an air conditioning unit between a recirculation mode and a fresh air mode based on the meeting of a multiple conditions based on the first PM value, the second PM value, and the CO2value.
   USE - Method for air quality management in vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The Air quality management system has provided to the passengers to switch the modes of operation, effectiveness of the system in improving air quality depends on the passenger s decision to switch modes, improved with negligible impact on cooling or heating efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for air quality management, which involves receiving signals indicative of a first particulate matter (PM) value, the first PM value corresponding to an amount of particulate matter inside a vehicle cabin, receiving signals indicative of a second PM value corresponding to an amount of the particulate matter outside the vehicle, receiving signals indicative of a CO2 value corresponding to an amount of carbon dioxide inside the vehicle cabin, and controlling a mode of operation of an air conditioning unit of the vehicle based on the meeting of a condition of a multiple of conditions based on the first PM value, the second PM value, and the CO2value, where the mode of operation is one of, a recirculation mode and a fresh air mode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Condition: The sensing component is one of a seat belt latch sensor, seat load cell, cabin camera, Human Machine Interface (HMI), and combinations. The controller is to receive signals from the sensing component and compute the CO2 value, switch the mode of operation from the recirculation mode to the fresh air mode when a first condition of the multiple of conditions is met. Preferred Condition: one of the first PM value and the CO2 value is greater than a particulate matter cabin threshold and a carbon dioxide cabin threshold respectively, and the second PM value is lesser than a particulate matter outside threshold, the controller is to retain the mode of operation in the recirculation mode when one of: a third condition, a fourth condition, and a fifth condition of the multiple of conditions is met. Preferred Condition: The third condition: the first PM value and the CO2value are lesser than a particulate matter cabin threshold and a carbon dioxide cabin threshold, respectively, in the fourth condition the first PM value is greater than the particulate matter cabin threshold, the second PM value is greater air than a particulate matter outside threshold, and the CO2 value is lesser than the carbon dioxide cabin threshold and in the fifth condition. the first PM value is greater than the particulate matter cabin threshold, the second PM value is greater than the particulate matter outside threshold and the CO2 value is greater than the carbon dioxide cabin threshold.

A monitoring collector (4) monitors whether a fire is present near to a cabinet body (1). A controller opens the electromagnetic valve when the monitoring collector monitors the fire in vicinity of the cabinet. A sealing plug is sleeved with a sealing ring. A side of the powder storage tank is connected with a feeding cylinder.
   USE - High safety fire controlling power distribution cabinet for use in a power distribution system and a balance motor control center. Can also be used for a processing electric appliance.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry powder extinguishing agent extinguishes fire of the power device, so that the fire potential near to the cabinet is inhibited while protecting the cabinet body for reducing the casualties, thus reducing the possibility of the powder containing box filled with excessive carbon dioxide and maintaining high air pressure in the sealing ring.    USE - The copper-based bipolar mixed conductor oxygen permeable membrane material is used as oxygen source for high temperature complex atmosphere oxygen industry, such as oxygen-enriched combustion, water decomposition and methane coupling field.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed conductor oxygen-permeable membrane material with high oxygen permeability can be obtained by regulating the proportion of the praseodymium element with multiple valence states and the proportion of the + 2 valence copper element which can effectively reduce the sintering temperature.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Copper-based double-phase mixed conductor oxygen-permeable membrane material comprises cerium-containing compound of formula 60 wt.% Ce0.85Pr0.1Cu0.05O2-delta-40 wt.%PrxSr1-xFe1-yCuyO3-delta (I).
   x=0.6 or 0.4; and
   y=0.05, 0.1 or 0.2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of copper-based double-phase mixed conductor oxygen-permeable membrane material comprising weighing nitrate according to stoichiometric ratio, dissolving in water, stirring, adding citric acid monohydrate complexing agent and dispersant ethylene glycol to beaker, adding ammonia to adjust pH value to 7-10, where ratio of amount of metal ions in solution and amount of citric acid is 1:2, stirring solution until it becomes clear and transparent, placing beaker on heated magnetic stirrer, evaporating until the solution turns into gel, putting gel in oven at 140 degrees C, drying for 24 hours to obtain fluffy dry gel, placing into crucible, calcining to remove organic matter at 600 degrees C for 8 hours, crushing, placing in crucible, calcining at 950 degrees C for 10 hours, pressing powder at 10 MPa to obtain cake-like flake, slowly heating at 1150 degrees C for 5 hours, and sintering.

PN WO2021131390-A1; JP2021566905-X
TI Elemental analysis device for analyzing element contained in sample, has flow rate controller which controls control valve so as to minimize deviation between set flow rate which is preset and measured flow rate measured by flow rate sensor.
AU INOUE T
   UCHIHARA H
AE HORIBA LTD (HORB-C)
GA 202173588U
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a removal mechanism (9) which is provided on an outlet flow path (L2) and removes carbon dioxide or water from a mixed gas. A thermal conductivity analysis unit (11) is provided downstream of the removal mechanism on the outlet flow path and detects nitrogen contained in the mixed gas that has passed through the removal mechanism. A flow rate sensor measures the flow rate of the mixed gas flowing between the removal mechanism and the thermal conductivity analysis unit in the outlet flow path. A control valve is provided between the removal mechanism and the thermal conductivity analysis unit in the outlet flow path. A flow rate controller controls the control valve so as to minimize the deviation between a set flow rate which is preset and the measured flow rate measured by the flow rate sensor. The removal mechanism is made of silica gel, activated carbon, or zeolite.
   USE - Elemental analysis device for analyzing element such as nitrogen, hydrogen, and oxygen contained in sample e.g. metal sample or ceramic sample contained in graphite crucible on basis of sample gas generated by heating sample.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the control valve is sequentially controlled so that the flow rate of the mixed gas is maintained at a set flow rate by feeding back the measured flow rate of the flow rate sensor provided between the removal mechanism and the thermal conductivity analysis unit, the pressure of the mixed gas in the thermal conductivity analysis unit can be stabilized even if the amount of carbon dioxide or water removed by the removal mechanism varies, thus maintaining high measurement accuracy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the elemental analysis device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
