PN CN112452344-A
TI Synthesis of monoatomic bismuth oxide bridged titanium dioxide-bismuth oxychloride composite photocatalytic material, by adding hydrogen titanate nanotube to solution, annealing, and light inducing bismuth oxychloride.
AU DONG W
   LI R
   LUAN Q
   DONG C
   TANG W
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202128164F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for in-situ synthesis of monoatomic bismuth oxide bridged titanium dioxide-bismuth oxychloride composite photocatalytic material, involves (a) adding a hydrogen titanate nanotube into an acidic alcohol solution of bismuth chloride, and enabling the hydrogen titanate nanotube to be rich in defects, (b) performing annealing treatment, converting the defect-rich hydrogen titanate nanotube to a defect-rich titanium dioxide nanotube, while the bismuth oxychloride nanosheet and the titanium dioxide nanotube form a closely bonded heterostructure, and (c) light inducing bismuth oxychloride to produce bismuth oxide single atoms at the heterogeneous interface in the airtight light-transmitting vessel of carbon dioxide and water vapor, which accelerates the electron transport at the heterogeneous interface and constructing titanium-bismuth bimetallic sites.
   USE - The method is used for in-situ synthesis of monoatomic bismuth oxide bridged titanium dioxide-bismuth oxychloride composite photocatalytic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cheap and cost-effective, and is carried out in a simple manner with excellent repeatability, and adopts easily obtainable raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for in-situ synthesis of monoatomic bismuth oxide bridged titanium dioxide-bismuth oxychloride composite photocatalytic material, involves (a) adding a hydrogen titanate nanotube into an acidic alcohol solution of bismuth chloride, and enabling the hydrogen titanate nanotube to be rich in defects by the corrosion action of an acidic bismuth chloride alcohol solution so as to load the bismuth oxychloride nanosheet on the outer surface of the hydrogen titanate nanotube, (b) performing annealing treatment, converting the defect-rich hydrogen titanate nanotube to a defect-rich titanium dioxide nanotube, while the bismuth oxychloride nanosheet and the titanium dioxide nanotube form a closely bonded heterostructure, and (c) light inducing bismuth oxychloride to produce bismuth oxide single atoms at the heterogeneous interface in the airtight light-transmitting vessel of carbon dioxide and water vapor, which accelerates the electron transport at the heterogeneous interface and constructing titanium-bismuth bimetallic sites.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrogen titanate nanotube is prepared by adding 20-70 mg photocatalytic of titanium dioxide powders (P25) to 50 ml 8-12 M sodium hydroxide solution, magnetically stirring for 0.5 hour, transferring to the reactor, heat treating at 150 degrees C for 30-50 hours, after being naturally cooled to room temperature, centrifuging the white precipitate, washing with 0.1 M hydrochloride solution and deionized water to pH 7, and drying at 80 degrees C for 12 hours. The step (a) comprises adding 50-300 mg bismuth chloride and 50-300 mu l concentrated hydrochloric acid to 42 ml absolute ethanol to obtain a clear bismuth chloride alcohol solution, adding 50 mg hydrogen titanate nanotubes to the above solution, after ultrasonic dispersion, stirring for 1 hour, and centrifuging to collect the precipitate, drying the precipitate at 80 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain a defect-rich hydrogen titanate/bismuth oxychloride composite material. The defect-rich hydrogen titanate/bismuth oxychloride composite material obtained in step (a) is placed in a tube furnace and annealed in air to obtain a defect-rich titanium dioxide-bismuth oxychloride heterojunction, where the heating rate of the tube furnace is 5-10 degrees C/minute, the heat treatment temperature is 300-450 degrees C, and the heat treatment time is 1-5 hours. The step (c) comprises placing the defect-rich titanium dioxide-bismuth oxychloride composite material obtained in step (b) in a 50-2000 ml airtight and light-transmitting container, injecting 0.5-2 ml water and 5 mu l acetonitrile, filling with high-purity carbon dioxide to a pressure of 50-150 kPa in the container, and using a xenon lamp to the sample, which is illuminated for 0.1-12 hours to obtain a single-atom bismuth oxide bridged titanium dioxide-bismuth oxychloride composite photocatalytic material.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN112420136-A
TI Tracing latent fault of sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device involves detecting trace gas concentration in a cavity of sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device with latent fault.
AU YANG R
   LIU J
   YUE X
   TIAN Z
   WANG J
   SU B
   XUE Q
   WANG H
AE STATE GRID SHAANXI ELECTRIC POWER CO ELE (SGCC-C)
   ELECTRIC POWER RES INST STATE GRID SHAAN (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
GA 202128796A
AB    NOVELTY - Tracing latent fault of sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device involves detecting trace gas concentration in a cavity of a sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device with latent fault. The trace gas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, tetrafluoromethane, carbon dioxide, carbon hexafluoride, sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide, sulfur dioxide and carbon disulfide. The trace gas concentration content is classified by cluster analysis to obtain a variable approximation value matrix. A latent fault type of the sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device is determined. The correlation between the classified trace gasses and the variables before cluster analysis is analyzed. The statistical inspection is performed on standardized index data to obtain threshold concentration of each gas, and then combined the classification results to obtain the following conclusions to complete traceability of latent failures of sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage equipment.
   USE - Method for tracing latent fault of sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device.
   ADVANTAGE - The latent fault of the sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device can be traced effectively.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Tracing latent fault of sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device involves detecting trace gas concentration in a cavity of a sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device with latent fault. The trace gas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, tetrafluoromethane, carbon dioxide, carbon hexafluoride, sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide, sulfur dioxide and carbon disulfide. The trace gas concentration content is classified by cluster analysis to obtain a variable approximation value matrix. A latent fault type of the sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage device is determined. The correlation between the classified trace gases and the variables before cluster analysis is analyzed. The statistical inspection is performed on standardized index data to obtain threshold concentration of each gas, and then combined the classification results to obtain the following conclusions to complete traceability of latent failures of sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage equipment. The concentration of hydrogen or methane in the standardized index data exceeds their respective threshold concentration, where the latent failure type of the sulfur hexafluoride high-voltage equipment is intrusive. The standardized index data includes carbon tetrafluoride, hexafluoroethane, carbon disulfide or octafluoropropane.

TI Method for separating or recovering basic gases,            involves adding first retentate to larger volume of            ammonia-acid gas solution to form first combined            solution, concentrating first combined solution using            membrane to form second retentate.
AU NOVEK E J

GA 202119636N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves absorbing ammonia from an                ammonia containing gas stream into an ammonia-lean,                an ammonia-ultra-lean, or ammonia free solution, to                form an ammonia-rich solution. The ammonia-rich                solution is enriched with acid gases to form an                ammonia-rich, acid gas rich solution. The                ammonia-rich is concentrated, acid gas rich                solution using membranes to form a first retentate                and a permeate where permeate is suitable for use                as portion of the ammonia-ultra-lean or                ammonia-free solution. The first retentate is added                to a larger volume of ammonia-acid gas solution to                form a first combined solution. The first combined                solution is concentrated using a membrane to form a                second retentate and a second permeate where the                second permeate comprises an amount of water that                is the same or greater than an amount of water in                the first retentate.
   USE - Method for separating or recovering basic                gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The rich presence of carbon dioxide or other                acid gas or gases in solution may reduce or                suppress the equilibrium partial pressure of                ammonia relative to a solution without or with                lesser relatively presence or concentration of                carbon dioxide or acid gas (G-7). The method                significantly improve ammonia absorption                efficiency, while enabling greater ammonia                absorption capacity per a unit mass or volume of                solution. The concentration of basic gas relative                to acid gas and the total concentration of both                reagents in solution may be tailored to maximize                absorption capacity of ammonia, minimize or prevent                acid gas volatility or losses, minimize acid gas                equilibrium partial pressure, minimize ammonia                equilibrium partial pressure, and minimize energy                consumption.

TI New replicating oncolytic adenovirus that            activates immune costimulatory signal pathway useful in            preparation of drug, preferably anti-tumor drugs that            stimulates expression of interferon (IFN)-gamma.
AU WEI J
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG H
   WU J
   DONG J
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
   NANJING WEIYADE BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020872240
AB    NOVELTY - A replicating oncolytic adenovirus that                activates an immune costimulatory signal pathway,                is new. The virus replicates in tumor cells, and                expresses and secretes soluble proteins, and the                soluble protein is CD137L that binds CD137.
   USE - The replicating oncolytic adenovirus is useful                in preparation of drug, preferably anti-tumor drugs                that stimulates expression of interferon (IFN)-                gamma , where tumor is liver cancer, ascites                cancer, melanoma or breast cancer (all                claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) use of the replicating oncolytic                adenovirus that activates the immune costimulatory                signal pathway, in preparation of activating                anti-tumor immune drugs; and
   (2) method for constructing replicating                oncolytic adenovirus AD5 sCD137L that activates the                immune costimulation signal pathway, involves                linearizing the constructed shuttle vector                AD5-pShuttlesCD137L with PmeI after transformation,                transferring to competent pAdEasy-BJ5183, using 50                mu g/ml kanamycin lysogeny broth (LB) plate for                screening, selecting positive clones for culture                identification, identifying correct clone plasmid                and retransforming DH5a competent for secondary                screening identification, identifying after                correct, performing plasmid extraction to obtain                AD5 sCD137L plasmid, linearizing AD5 sCD137L                plasmid with PacI, after purification, transforming                293T cells into 1 mu g/well in a 6-well plate,                culturing in 5% carbon dioxide and 37 degrees C                after 2 days, digesting the cells, transferring to                10 cm plate, changing the medium for 3 days until                80% of the cells show disease, using 10 ml medium                to blow down the cells, collecting into 15 ml                centrifuge tube, repeating the freeze-thawing cycle                twice, centrifuging at 3000 revolutions per minute                (rpm) for 15 minutes, collecting the virus                supernatant, storing at -80 degrees C as virus                species, adding 50 mu l virus seed solution and 60%                293T cells to 10 cm plate, culturing at 5% carbon                dioxide at 37 degrees C, and cell density of above                90%, passaging for 1-3, until 80% of cells,                collecting the virus according to the above method,                purifying the virus by cesium chloride density                gradient centrifugation, and measuring titer by the                TCID50 method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The soluble protein is                  sCD137L, and the amino acid sequence and                  nucleotide sequence of sCD137L are 216 or 648                  base pair sequence (SEQ ID NOs: 1 and 4) all                  fully defined in the specification. The                  replicating oncolytic adenovirus is capable of                  oncolysis. The method involves constructing AD5                  sCD137L virus with gene clone of CD137L                  extracellular domain (sCD137L) and constructing                  adenovirus shuttle plasmid carrying sCD137L gene,                  where the mouse CD137L belongs to membrane                  protein, and its structure is: N-terminal signal                  peptide- extracellular region-transmembrane                  region-intracellular region C-terminal, the                  functional unit of CD137L and CD137 is the                  extracellular region, using the N-terminal signal                  peptide sequence of CD33 and the CD137L                  extracellular region sequence, designing and                  synthesizing primers CD137L-F, CD137L-R, CD137LF                  and CD137L-R primers, and using mouse spleen cDNA                  as a template to amplify the fragment EXO-CD137L.                  The method involves using infusion technology to                  connect sCD137L fragment with AD5-pShuttle                  (pZD55), using restriction endocytosis Enzyme                  BglII linearized AD5-pShuttle (pZD55), ligating                  purified fragments according to the 2:1 ratio of                  sCD137L, obtaining carrier sCD137L by                  transformation and amplification verification,                  and obtaining gene-based adenovirus shuttle                  plasmid AD5-pShuttle-sCD137L.

A predetermined portion of the pressurized CO2 fluid through the restricted flow pathway and into the vapor CO2 container is introduced. The system (10) is filled with the pressurized CO2 fluid such that a total weight of the pressurized CO2 fluid occupying the system is no more than 68 wt percentage by water weight.
   USE - Method for filling on-site carbon dioxide (CO2) delivery system (claimed) such as carbonated beverage dispenser with CO2 to avoid over pressurization.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressure differential device is activated to close below the predetermined pressure difference, thus preventing the transfer of the CO2 fluid from the liquid CO2 container to the vapor CO2 container so as to preferentially dispense CO2 vapor from the vapor CO2 container.

   (3) a method for dispensing CO2 product to end-user from on-site CO2 multiple container system.

      frameworks-8 ultrasonically in 40 ml of methanol                solution to obtain zeolitic imidazolate                frameworks-8 methanol solution, dissolving in 10-20                mg copper(II) chloride dihydrate in 10 ml of                methanol solution to obtain cupric chloride                dihydrate. methanol solution, adding cupric                chloride dihydrate methanol solution to zeolitic                imidazolate frameworks-8 methanol solution, mixing                two at room temperature for 12 hours to obtain                copper(II) chloride- zeolitic imidazolate                frameworks methanol solution, washing copper(II)                chloride- zeolitic imidazolate frameworks methanol                solution once by centrifugation and drying under                vacuum, catalyst precursor copper(II) chloride-                zeolitic imidazolate frameworks composite material                can be obtained, ultrasonically dispersing                copper(II) chloride zeolitic imidazolate                framework-8 composite material is further in a 40                ml methanol solution to obtain a copper(II)                chloride zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 composite                material methanol solution, dissolving 10-20 mg                potassium hydroxide in 10 ml methanol solution to precursor copper(II)                hydroxide-zeolitic imidazolate framework-8                composite under inert nitrogen at 1000 degrees                C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper-nitrogen                  co-doped nano-carbon material comprises ZIF-8,                  cupric chloride dihydrate, and potassium                  hydroxide, and mass ratio is 100-300 mg: 10-20                  mg: 10-20 mg. The particle size of the                  copper-nitrogen co-doped nano-carbon material                  catalyst is 40-70 nm. The size of the working                  electrode is 0.5 x 0.5 cm and mass of supported                  carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst                  is 10-20 mg. The solvent is deionized water or                  ethanol. The loading amount of the                  copper-nitrogen co-doped nano-carbon material                  catalyst on working electrode is 0.1-0.5 mg/cm2.                  Preferred Method: The temperature at room                  temperature at 25-30 degrees C, and the methanol                  solution of analytical grade. The copper in the                  copper-nitrogen co-doped nano-carbon material                  catalyst accounts for 5-10% of total molar amount                  of the mixture. The specific method comprises                  dispersing the catalyst carbon-Copper(II)                  hydroxide/zeolitic imidazolate framework -1000                  into 0.95 ml ethanol solution to obtain a                  carbon-catalyst Copper(II) hydroxide /zeolitic                  imidazolate framework -1000 ethanol solution,                  adding 50 mu l of 5 wt.% nafion solution to                  carbon-catalyst copper(II) hydroxide /zeolitic                  imidazolate framework-zeolitic imidazolate                  framework-1000 ethanol solution, ultrasonically                  dispersing for 30 minutes, applying mixed                  solution to working electrode, coating working                  electrode with the mixed solution allowing to dry                  naturally to obtain working electrode carrying a                  carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                  catalyst.

TI Dust exposure amount measuring apparatus used in office, has control unit that calculates dust exposure amount of user for predetermined time based on respiratory volume calculated based on heart rate measured by heart rate sensor unit.
AU WOOCHANGGYU
   HAN B W
   KIM H J
   KIMYONGJIN
AE KOREA INST MACHINERY & MATERIALS (KIMA-C)
GA 201997079P
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a main case (100) that is worn by the user. A dust sensor unit (110) is placed on the main case and adapted for measuring a dust concentration of a place where the user is located. A time measuring unit (120) is placed on the main case and adapted for measuring a time when the user is exposed to dust. A heart rate sensor unit (130) is placed in the main case and adapted for measuring a heart rate of the user. A control unit (140) is placed in main case. The control unit is adapted for measuring dust concentration through a dust sensor unit and for measuring exposure time through the time measuring unit. The control unit is adapted to calculate a dust exposure amount of the user for a predetermined time based on the respiratory volume calculated based on the heart rate measured by the heart rate sensor unit.
   USE - Dust exposure amount measuring apparatus used in general home or office.
   ADVANTAGE - The dust exposure amount of the user is accurately measured using the dust concentration and the respiratory rate calculated by the user's heart rate. The respiratory volume in consideration of the user's factor is accurately calculated by setting the user's factor in the heart rate sensor unit. The air quality in the place where the user is mainly located can be effectively managed, by distinguishing the total amount of dust exposure when the user is outdoors and indoors. The warning alarm provides an audible alert for dust exposure and carbon dioxide concentrations, thus providing immediate response to dust exposure and carbon dioxide concentrations.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the dust exposure amount measuring apparatus.
   Main case (100)
   Dust sensor unit (110)
   Time measuring unit (120)
   Heart rate sensor unit (130)
   Control unit (140)

AU LI X
   YAN Y
   SUN Y
   JING X
   CHEN J
   CHANG Y
   ZHENG H
   PU Y
   TANG L
   LI W
AE GUO Y (GUOY-Individual)
GA 201978000S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing resin-based hydrophobic silica anticorrosive coating comprises (i) preparing hydrophobic silica by taking base polymer: alpha , ~o-dihydroxy polydimethylsiloxane, adding silica powder mixed with dimethylsilane, adding proper amount of ethanol to ignite and when burning small amount of gray smoke is generated as nano-hydrophobic silica dust, which is recovered; (ii) preparing polycarbonate by non-phosgene method, preparing ethylene carbonate by carbon dioxide, epoxy acetylene, and transesterification of ethylene carbonate with carbonic acid by carbon monooxide, methanol and oxygen to obtain dimethyl carbonate and dimethyl carbonate, adding phenol into ester for transesterification to obtain diphenyl carbonate by transesterification of diphenyl carbonate and bisphenol-A and carrying out melt polycondensation; and (iv) dissolving dichloromethane as solvent and acrylnitrile-butadiene-styrene polycarbonate blend substrate, and adding hydrophobic silica powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing resin-based hydrophobic silica anticorrosive coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating is hard to crack, has strong weather resistance, high strength, low comprehensive cost and can protect for long time. The method is environmentally friendly, solves corrosion problem of metal from fundamental source and uses less much plastic improving additive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing resin-based hydrophobic silica anticorrosive coating comprises (i) preparing hydrophobic silica by taking base polymer: alpha , ~o-dihydroxy polydimethylsiloxane, adding silica powder mixed with dimethylsilane, adding proper amount of ethanol to ignite and when burning small amount of gray smoke is generated as nano-hydrophobic silica dust, which is recovered; (ii) preparing polycarbonate by non-phosgene method, preparing ethylene carbonate by carbon dioxide, epoxy acetylene, and transesterification of ethylene carbonate with carbonic acid by carbon monooxide, methanol and oxygen to obtain dimethyl carbonate and dimethyl carbonate, adding phenol into ester for transesterification to obtain diphenyl carbonate by transesterification of diphenyl carbonate and bisphenol-A and carrying out melt polycondensation to obtain polycarbonate; (iii) preparing matrix acrylnitrile-butadiene-styrene polycarbonate blend; and (iv) dissolving dichloromethane as solvent and acrylnitrile-butadiene-styrene polycarbonate blend substrate, and adding hydrophobic silica powder and adding small amount of polydimethylsiloxane oil anti-foaming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of silica powder and dimethylsilane is 1:1. In the step (i), product obtained is grey-white or white powder. The mass ratio of polycarbonate and acrylnitrile-butadiene-styrene polycarbonate is 3:2. The molar ratio of dichloromethane solvent and acrylnitrile-butadiene-styrene polycarbonate blend substrate is 1:2 and mass ratio of solution and hydrophobic silica is 3:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), there is small amount of carbon impurities due to insufficient combustion and oxygenation combustion is performed to remove carbon impurities to obtain high-purity hydrophobic silica powder. In the method, certain proportion of polycarbonate and acrylnitrile-butadiene-styrene are blended to obtain acrylnitrile-butadiene-styrene polycarbonate blend substrate.

PN CN109704510-A; CN109704510-B
TI Deep processing municipal sewage comprises e.g.            adding acidic coagulant to the biochemical effluent of            landfill leachate, entering coagulation sedimentation            tank, acidifying the leachate, and discharging the            effluent.
AU SHI R
   ZHAO H
AE WEISHUI XINGKONG BEIJING ENVIRONMENTAL (WEIS-Non-standard)
GA 201943472T
AB    NOVELTY - Deep processing municipal sewage comprises (i)                adding acidic coagulant to the biochemical effluent                of landfill leachate, entering the coagulation                sedimentation tank to mix and stand still, (ii)                providing the precipitated landfill leachate enters                the heterogeneous fluidized bed for heterogeneous                catalytic pretreatment, which causes the partially                refractory macromolecular long-chain organic matter                to be broken, and generates small-molecular                short-chain organic matter which is easy to                biodegrade, (iii) providing the landfill leachate                after heterogeneous catalytic pretreatment enters                the BAF treatment system to remove the small                molecule short-chain organic matter produced during                heterogeneous catalytic pretreatment, and (iv)                acidifying the leachate, and enters the                heterogeneous fluidized bed for the heterogeneous                oxidation deep treatment, the organic matter is                deeply removed, and the organic matter is                completely mineralized to form carbon dioxide,                discharging the effluent after degassing.
   USE - The method is useful for deep processing                municipal sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment friendly, and                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Deep processing municipal sewage comprises (i)                adding acidic coagulant to the biochemical effluent                of landfill leachate, entering the coagulation                sedimentation tank to mix and stand still, (ii)                providing the precipitated landfill leachate enters                the heterogeneous fluidized bed for heterogeneous                catalytic pretreatment, which causes the partially                refractory macromolecular long-chain organic matter                to be broken, and generates small-molecular                short-chain organic matter which is easy to                biodegrade, (iii) providing the landfill leachate                after heterogeneous catalytic pretreatment enters                the BAF treatment system to remove the small                molecule short-chain organic matter produced during                the heterogeneous catalytic pretreatment, and (iv)                acidifying the leachate, and enters the                heterogeneous fluidized bed for the heterogeneous                oxidation deep treatment, the organic matter is                deeply removed, and the organic matter is                completely mineralized to form carbon dioxide,                discharging the effluent after degassing, alkali                adjustment, flocculation and sedimentation, the                heterogeneous catalytic pretreatment differs from                the catalyst used in the deep treatment of                heterogeneous oxidation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  chemical oxygen demand value of the biochemical                  effluent of the landfill leachate is 600-2000                  mg/l. The acidic coagulant comprises an acidic                  flocculant and a coagulant, and the active                  components of the acidic flocculant comprise:                  sulfuric acid, polyferric chloride and                  polyaluminum chloride. The coagulant is                  polyacrylamide. The acid flocculant has a                  sulfuric acid concentration of 20-40%, a                  polyferric chloride concentration of 10-30%, and                  a polyaluminum chloride concentration of 1-20%.                  The dosage of acidic flocculant is 1-5 ml/l                  wastewater, 5-10 mg/l polyacrylamide, the COD                  removal rate after coagulation precipitation is                  50-75%, and the effluent COD value is controlled                  at 500 mg/l. In the step (ii), the catalyst used                  in the heterogeneous catalytic pretreatment is a                  nanometer ferric oxide solid catalyst, and the                  nanometer ferric oxide solid catalyst                  continuously rolls in a heterogeneous fluidized                  bed and collides with the wastewater, increases                  the efficiency of catalytic oxidation. In the                  step (ii), the landfill leachate after                  sedimentation enters the heterogeneous fluidized                  bed at a flow rate of 30-70 m/hour, and the                  heterogeneous catalytic pretreatment influent COD                  value is not less than 500 mg/l, and the BOD                  value is close to 0 mg/l, after heterogeneous                  catalytic pretreatment, the COD value becomes                  300-400 mg/l, and the BOD value is 60-100 mg/l,                  which greatly improves the biodegradability of                  wastewater, the amount of heterogeneous catalytic                  pretreatment hydrogen peroxide is 0.4-0.6 Kg/ton                  of water. In the step (iii), the BAF-treated                  filler is a bio-coke filler, the bio-coke filler                  accounts for 1/3 to 1/2 of the volume of the                  packed pool, and the bio-coke filler has a pore                  size of 2-50 nm, the mud load is 1-6 kgBOD/(m3d),                  residence time: 2-3 hours, water temperature                  15-35 degrees C, pH value 6-9, effluent dissolved                  oxygen 2-4 mg/l. The catalyst used in the deep                  treatment of heterogeneous oxidation is an                  iron-manganese composite oxide catalyst, and the                  iron-manganese composite oxide catalyst                  continuously rolls in a heterogeneous fluidized                  bed and collides with the wastewater, improving                  the efficiency of advanced oxidation. In the step                  (iv), the amount of hydrogen peroxide in the                  heterogeneous oxidation deep treatment is 0.6-1                  ml/l, the effluent COD value is less than or                  equal to 100 mg/l, and the influent flow rate in                  the heterogeneous oxidation deep treatment is                  30-70 m/hour.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN109452426-A
TI High concentrated tea flavoring agent, prepared by e.g. taking brown sugar, ginger powder, Chrysanthemum powder, xylooligosaccharide powder and Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder, crushing, sieving, mixing and adding entraining agent.
AU LI X
   MENG J
   WANG H
   ZHOU Y
AE QIANJIN PHARM CO LTD ZHUZHOU (QJPM-C)
GA 201925470L
AB    NOVELTY - High concentrated tea flavoring agent is prepared by e.g. (i) taking brown sugar, ginger powder, Chrysanthemum powder, xylooligosaccharide powder and Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder, crushing separately and sieving; (ii) mixing ginger powder, Chrysanthemum powder and Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder obtained in the step (i) and introducing into supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, adding entraining agent and setting extraction pressure and extraction temperature to obtain extraction liquid; (iii) heating brown sugar after step (i) and melting, adding to extract obtained in the step (ii) and adding xylo-oligosaccharide powder and stirring at high speed to obtain mixed liquid; and (iv) spray-drying mixture obtained in step (iii) and sterilizing until moisture content is qualified and sieving.
   USE - The high concentrated tea flavoring agent is useful for promoting blood circulation, nourishing qi, warming stomach, warming womb, resisting bacteria and diminishing inflammation, improving immunity.
   ADVANTAGE - The high concentrated tea flavoring agent is rich in taste, has high extraction rate, improves utilization rate of raw material and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High concentrated tea flavoring agent is prepared by (i) taking brown sugar, ginger powder, Chrysanthemum powder, xylooligosaccharide powder and Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder, crushing separately and sieving; (ii) mixing ginger powder, Chrysanthemum powder and Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder obtained in the step (i) and introducing into supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, adding entraining agent and setting extraction pressure and extraction temperature to obtain extraction liquid; (iii) heating brown sugar after step (i) and melting, adding to extract obtained in the step (ii) and adding xylo-oligosaccharide powder and stirring at high speed to obtain mixed liquid; and (iv) spray-drying mixture obtained in step (iii) and sterilizing until moisture content is qualified and sieving, where step (i) comprises 60-72 pts. wt. brown sugar, 2-10 pts. wt. ginger powder, 2-10 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum powder, 1-5 pts. wt. xylooligosaccharide powder and 0.4-8 pts. wt. Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i) sieving using 70-90 mesh sieve. In the step (ii) repeating extraction three times, where first extraction pressure is 20-30 MPa, extraction temperature is 40-50 degrees C and second extraction pressure is 12-20 MPa, extraction temperature is 35-45 degrees C, third extraction pressure is 4-12 MPa and extraction temperature is 25-35 degrees C. The extraction time is 30-60 minutes and carbon dioxide flow rate is 40-160 kg/hours. In the step (iii) heating temperature is 90-120 degrees C, high-speed stirring speed is 1500-1800 rotations/minute and stirring time is 15-30 minutes. In the step (iv) rotation speed of spray drying head is 350-380 rotations/minute, inlet air temperature of 100-120 degrees C, outlet air temperature of 60-75 degrees C, feed flow rate of 30-40 rotations/minute and fan frequency of 20-50 Hz. In the step (iv) sterilization temperature is 90-100 degrees C, sterilization time is 10-30 minutes, moisture content is less than or equal to 5% and sieve mesh is 30-50 mesh. Preferred Composition: In the step (ii) entraining agent is at least one of water, ethanol, ethanol and water and entraining agent is used in an amount of 1:7 by weight of the ginger powder and entraining agent. In the step (iii) ratio of brown sugar to extract of 4-10:1.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen; Gynecological; Antibacterial; Antiinflammatory; Immunostimulant.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC B04-A08G; B04-A10; B04-A98; B14-A01; B14-C03; B14-E10B; B14-F02; B14-G01; B14-S18; D03-D03B; D03-H01B; D03-H01T2B
IP A23F-003/34; A61K-031/7016; A61K-031/702; A61K-036/9068; A61P-031/04; A61P-037/04

PT P
PN WO2019012347-A1; EP3651878-A1; US2020199141-A1
TI New pseudo Troger's base based dianhydride comprising substituted isobenzofuran-1,3-dione compounds useful for forming the polyimide that is useful in a polyimide-based membrane for separating chemical species in a fluid composition.
AU MA X
   HAMID M A
   PINNAU I
   ABDULHAMID M
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 201907030S
AB    NOVELTY - Pseudo Troger's base based dianhydride (Q1) comprising at least one of substituted isobenzofuran-1,3-dione compounds (Q2) is new.
   USE - (Q1) is useful for forming the polyimide that is useful in a polyimide-based membrane for separating chemical species in a fluid composition, where the chemical species includes at least two of oxygen, nitrogen, hydrogen, helium, carbon dioxide, aliphatic hydrocarbons of more than one carbon, olefins, paraffins, xylene isomers, n-butane, isobutane or butenes, and the captured chemical species includes at least one hydrogen, oxygen or carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (Q1) provides polymer membranes which: exhibit excellent fluid transport properties (e.g. gas transport properties); include inefficient chain packing and high chain rigidity, with size-selective ultramicropores (less than 7 Angstrom ); exhibit strong charge-transform complex formation and high Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface areas; and exhibit high gas permeabilities and moderate to high gas-pair selectivities that exceed and/or approach the upper bounds for numerous gas pairs, and further exhibit low degradation in response to physical aging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pseudo Troger's base-based dianhydride (Q1) comprising at least one of substituted isobenzofuran-1,3-dione compounds (Q2) of formulae (I) and (II) is new.
   Y1 = O, CH2 or H2; and
   R, R1 = aromatic group or aliphatic group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a polyimide comprising at least one substituted isoindole-1,3-dione compounds of formulae (Ia)-(Id); and
   (2) separating chemical species in a fluid composition, comprising contacting a polyimide-based membrane with a fluid composition including at least two chemical species, where the polymeric membrane includes the polyimide and capturing at least one of the chemical species from the fluid composition.
   A1 = dianhydride, preferably e.g. substituted fluoro-phenyl moieties of formula (IIa)-(IIb);
   B1 = diamine, preferably substituted fluoro-phenyl moieties (IIa)-(IIc); and
   n, m = 1-10000.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is given.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The pseudo Troger's base (TB) dianhydride is a carbocyclic pseudo TB dianhydride. The pseudo TB dianhydride is a building block for the synthesis of microporous polymers. The microporous polymers include at least one of a microporous polyimide. The polyimide is derived from a carbocyclic pseudo TB-derived dianhydride, where the polyimide is soluble in organic solvents. The organic solvents include at least one N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, m-cresol, dimethylformamide or chloroform.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: A molecular weight of microporous polyimides and polymers of intrinsic microporosity polyimides (PIM-PI) or polyimide range is 20000-60000 g/mol. A polydispersity index is 1.5-1.6. The PIM-PI or polyimide is fabricated into a polymeric membrane.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R, R1 = methyl, ethyl, propyl, isopropyl, n-butyl or isobutyl.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 2 Compounds (Q1) are specifically claimed i.e. 6,17-dioxahexacyclo(10.10.1.0(2,10).0(4,8).0(13,21).0(15,19))tricosa-2,4(8),9,13(21),14,19-hexaene-5,7,11,16,18,22-hexone of formula (II) and 6,17-dioxahexacyclo(10.10.1.0(2,10).0(4,8).0(13,21).0(15,19))tricosa-2,4(8),9,13(21),14,19-hexaene-5,7,16,18-tetrone.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC J01-E03E; A05-J01B; A12-W11A

PN WO2019006496-A1; EP3648823-A1; US2020282161-A1; EP3648823-A4
TI Device for measuring characteristic associated            with flow of e.g. carbon dioxide, has acoustic            generator configured to generate acoustic signal as            function of gas characteristic of gas flow, where            acoustic signal is inaudible to human.
AU CHAMTIE H
   HOLLEY L
   BURNS P A
AE RESMED PTY LTD (RESM-C)
   RESMED PTY LTD (RESM-C)
   RESMED PTY LTD (RESM-C)
GA 2019047160
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an acoustic generator (8500)                configured to generate an acoustic signal as a                function of a gas characteristic of gas flow, where                the acoustic signal is inaudible to a human and the                acoustic signal is an ultrasonic acoustic signal.                The acoustic generator is adapted to be                incorporated into a patient interface for a                respiratory therapy. The acoustic generator is                located in fluid communication with a plenum                chamber of the patient interface for measurement of                gas pressure of respiratory therapy gas within the                plenum chamber, where the acoustic generator is a                whistle, a resonant pipe, a reed, a helmholtz                resonator, a membrane, and a tensioned                string.
   USE - Device for measuring a characteristic                associated with flow of gas e.g. carbon dioxide and                carbon monoxide (all claimed) during various                therapies e.g. continuous positive airway pressure                therapy and high flow therapy.
   ADVANTAGE - The device delivers higher quality of therapy,                improves ease of use of a therapy system, reduces                waste, and provides digital connectivity at a low                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a flow rate sensor
   (2) a system for measuring a characteristic of                flow of breathable gas
   (3) a method for determining operational                parameters associated with a respiratory                therapy
   (4) a method for facilitating automatic                configuration of a respiratory therapy device                adapted for treating a respiratory disorder in a                patient
   (5) a method for monitoring a respiratory                therapy provided by a respiratory therapy                device
   (6) a processor readable medium comprising a                set of instructions for measuring a characteristic                of gas flow
   (7) a method for identifying a patient                interface with a respiratory therapy device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                device for measuring a characteristic associated                with flow of gas.
   Acoustic generator (8500)
   Windway inlet (8511)
   Bore outlet (8541)
   Flow path (8800)
   Wall boundary (8810)

PN CN109097327-A
TI Stem cell culture medium useful of culturing stem cells, comprises e.g. multivitamin, insulin, lipoic acid, trehalose, taurine, dextran, betamethasone, inorganic salts, sodium bicarbonate and protein polypeptide.
AU LIU L
   LIU B
AE LIU L (LIUL-Individual)
GA 201902431M
AB    NOVELTY - Stem cell culture medium comprises multivitamin, insulin, lipoic acid, Camellia seed oil, trehalose, Perilla extract, taurine, dextran, betamethasone, inorganic salts, sodium bicarbonate and protein polypeptide.
   USE - The stem cell culture medium is useful of culturing stem cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate, has good separation effect, obtains 6.1/asterisk107-6.5/asterisk107 primary adipose stem cells, and primary fat stem cell viability can reach 99.1-99.6%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for separating stem cells, comprising (i) taking body tissue, the body tissue is adipose tissue, transferring the adipose tissue to a sterile collection bottle, and storing at 1-5 degrees C, washing body tissue with physiological saline for 1-3 times, mixing with the stem cell culture medium, washing the blood cells away until the adipose tissue suspension is clarified and blood-free, centrifuging the washed adipose tissue at 100-500 rotations/minute for 8-15 minutes, removing the lower layer of liquid, absorbing the upper tissue block into the culture flask, spreading the tissue block evenly in the culture flask, and incubating in an incubator at 33-37 degrees C for 40-55 minutes, introducing 7% carbon dioxide, and attaching the tissue block to the culture surface to obtain an isolated tissue, (ii) mixing the above-mentioned isolated tissue and stem cell culture medium in a culture flask, and culturing until the cells climb out of the body tissue and scraping off the excised tissue, and scraping off the body tissue of the excised tissue on the 10th-15th day of the culture of the stem cell culture medium, and (iii) continuously culturing in an incubator at 33-37 degrees C at 7% carbon dioxide , and when the cell fusion degree in the medium reaches 80-90%, digesting and subculturing, and subculturing the subculture using a stem cell culture medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The stem cell culture medium comprises 2-5 g/l, preferably 3 g/l multivitamin, 6-15 g/l, preferably 11 g/l insulin, 3-7 g/l, preferably 5 g/l lipoic acid, 5-15 g/l, preferably 10 g/l Camellia seed oil, 13-20 g/l, preferably 16 g/l trehalose, 10-20 g/l, preferably 13 g/l Perilla extract, 5-10 g/l, preferably 8 g/l taurine, 12-25 g/l, preferably 22 g/l dextran, 6-14 g/l, preferably 12 g/l betamethasone, 1-2 g/l, preferably 2 g/l inorganic salts, 2-6 g/l, preferably 4 g/l sodium bicarbonate and 11-17 g/l, preferably 16 g/l protein polypeptide Preferred Components: The physiological saline contained 2% penicillin and 0.5% folic acid. The digestion is EDTA-pancreatin digestion. Preferred Method: The body tissue is mixed with the stem cell culture medium and cultured in a culture flask until subculture, and the stem cell culture medium is changed every 2-3 days.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108946727-A; CN108946727-B
TI Preparation of activated carbon, involves passing            microalgae-alginate complex through physical activation            process, chemical activation process or physicochemical            process using activator e.g. sulfuric acid, phosphoric            acid, nitric acid.
AU WU Y
   CHEN Z
   LIU Y
   XU Y
   LIU Z
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2018A0555K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of activated carbon, involves                passing microalgae-alginate complex through                physical activation process, chemical activation                process or physicochemical process. The physical                activation process involves heating                microalgae-alginate complex under a nitrogen                atmosphere, then passing carbon dioxide, steam, air                or nitrogen, activating at 400-900 degrees C,                finally cooling, washing and drying. The chemical                activation process involves mixing the                microalgae-alginate complex with an activator,                carbonizing, activating, finally cooling, washing                and drying. The physicochemical combination                activation process involves heating                microalgae-alginate complex at 150-500 degrees C                under a nitrogen atmosphere, carbonizing, then                mixing with activator, heating at 400-900 degrees C                for activation, finally cooling, washing and                drying. The activator is selected from sulfuric                acid, phosphoric acid, nitric acid, sodium                hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate,                potassium carbonate and/or zinc chloride.
   USE - Preparation of activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of activated                carbon with large specific surface area, abundant                micropores and narrow pore size distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of activated carbon, involves                passing microalgae-alginate complex through                physical activation process, chemical activation                process or physicochemical process. The physical                activation process involves heating                microalgae-alginate complex at a temperature of                150-500 degrees C under a nitrogen atmosphere, then                passing carbon dioxide, steam, air or nitrogen,                activating at 400-900 degrees C, finally cooling,                washing and drying. The chemical activation process                involves mixing the microalgae-alginate complex                with an activator, then heating up to 150-500                degrees C for carbonization, then heating up to                400-900 degrees C for activation, finally cooling,                washing and drying. The physicochemical combination                activation process involves heating                microalgae-alginate complex at 150-500 degrees C                under a nitrogen atmosphere, carbonizing, then                mixing with activator, heating at 400-900 degrees C                for activation, finally cooling, washing and                drying. The activator is selected from sulfuric                acid, phosphoric acid, nitric acid, sodium                hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate,                potassium carbonate and/or zinc chloride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae is                  selected from Chlorella, Sargassum horneri,                  Tetraselmis, blue-green algae, green algae,                  Botryococcus, diatom algae and/or                  Dunaliella.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alginate is                  selected from potassium alginate, sodium                  alginate, calcium alginate, zinc alginate, iron                  alginate, copper alginate, cobalt alginate,                  nickel alginate, palladium alginate, magnesium                  alginate and/or barium alginate. Preferred                  Process: The microalgae-alginate complex is                  obtained by mixing an aqueous solution of                  microalgae with alginate and then drying. The                  washing process comprises washing with                  hydrochloric acid solution and then washing with                  water until neutral. During the physical                  activation process, the nitrogen flow rate is                  50-200 ml/minute. Preferred Composition: The mass                  ratio of microalgae to alginate is 1:0.5-1:5. The                  mass ratio of microalgae-alginate complex to                  activator is 1:0.5-1:5. Preferred Conditions: The                  activation process comprises heating to 400-900                  degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 1-10                  degrees C/minute. The drying is carried out at                  80-150 degrees C. The carbonizing process is                  carried out for 1-4 hours and the activation                  process is carried out for 0.5-4 hours.

TI Fishmeal collagen mask comprises fish collagen substrate and surface-active additive, and is prepared by taking large salmon fish seed, freezing, drying then extracting by using supercritical carbon dioxide and taking slag.
AU HU C
   LU Y
   LU X
AE FOSHAN SENANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201884939D
AB    NOVELTY - Fishmeal collagen mask comprises fish collagen substrate and surface-active additive, and is prepared by taking large salmon fish seed, freezing, drying then extracting by using supercritical carbon dioxide, taking slag, sieving with 300-mesh sieve, collecting sieved particles with mass ratio of 1-3:20-30, adding de-ionized water with constant stirring and mixing at 30-35 degrees C, centrifuging, taking precipitate, performing ultrafiltration to obtain concentrates, taking condensate with mass ratio of 1-4:10-20, adding de-ionized water, adjusting pH value to 8-8.5, raising temperature to 45-50 degrees C, adding 10-15 wt.% concentrate to preservation alkaline protease, inactivating enzyme, centrifuging and taking supernatant, adding sodium chloride according to mass ratio of 10-20:1-3, centrifuging, taking precipitated and dialysing by using acetic acid solution, freezing and drying to obtain freeze-dried product.
   USE - Fishmeal collagen mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The fish meal collagen mask stimulates cell activity and increases cell metabolism.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Taking fruit sugar with mass ratio of 1-3:10-20, adding isopropanol and then dripping by using i25-35% sodium hydroxide solution to isopropanol with constant stirring and mixing at 25-30 degrees C to obtain mixture, stirring mixture according to mass ratio of 20-30:1-4, adding chloroacetic acid, stirring and mixing at 55-60 degrees C, adjusting pH value to 6.5-7, dialyzing dialysis liquid, freezing and drying to obtain freeze-dried product, taking frozen dried compound with mass ratio of 1-3:8-10, adding 0.2 moles/L 2-(N-morpholino)ethanesulfonic acid (MES) buffer solution, stirring and mixing, adding lyophilized material to 50-70 wt.% 1-(3-dimethyl methoxide aminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimide hydrochloride and 15-25 wt.% N-hydroxythiosuccinimide sodium salt with constant stirring to obtain stirred mixture with mass ratio of 4-7:2-4, stirring and mixing for 10-15 minutes, adjusting pH to 6-6.3, and de-compressing and condensing to obtain fishcollagen substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The mixture is stirred at 25-30 degrees C for 15-20 hours. The mixture is performed at 60-65 degrees C for 30-40 minutes. The mixed solution is mixed at 25-30 degrees C for 24-48 hours. The surfactant additive is mixed at 25-30 degrees C for 18-24 hours. Preferred Process: The surface active additive is prepared by adding carbon tetrachloride to sulfuric acid at mass ratio of 1-3:10-15, stirring and mixing for 20-30 minutes to obtain mixture, adding glutamic acid to benzyl alcohol at mass ratio of 2-4:7-9, and then adding glutamic acid to 5-10 wt.% sulfuric acid with stirring to obtain second mixture, adding second mixture to tetrahydrofuran at mass ratio of 2-5:9-13to obtain third mixture, stirring third mixture according to mass ratio of 1-3:6-9, introducing petroleum ether, standing at 3-5 degrees C followed by suction filtration, taking filter residue and washing by de-ionized water followed by rotary evaporation, taking rotary evaporate with mass ratio 1-4:9-12, adding N,N'-dimethylformamide, and then adding 2-5 wt.% amine-terminated polyethylene glycol to obtain mixed solution, adding mixture at mass ratio of 2-5:10-20, mixing trifluoroacetic acid to diethyl ether at mass ratio of 1-4:10-15, adding 20-25 wt.% hydrogen bromide/trifluoroacetic acid at -2 degrees C to 0 degrees C, stirring liquid and mixing at 25-30 degrees C, and adding anhydrous diethyl ether for 3-6 times with trifluoroacetic acid, and allowing mixture to stand, precipitating and dialyzing and drying to obtain lyophilized product, adding de-ionized water into it to mass ratio of 1-5:200-300, adding 1-3 times N-hydroxythiosuccinimide sodium salt and standing for 30-40 minutes then adding 2-4 times of tyramine hydrochloride with constant stirring and mixing at 25-30 degrees C for 18-24 hours to obtain surface active additive. The fish collagen facial mask is prepared by taking 20-50 pts. wt. fish collagen substrate, 15-20 pts. wt. additive surfactant, 80-100 pts. wt. de-ionized water, 2-5 pts. wt. carbomer, 1-3 pts. wt. rhodiola rosea extract, 2-5 pts. wt. Ginseng extract, 20-30 pts. wt. phosphate-buffered saline buffer solution, stirring and mixing for 30-50 minutes at 35-40 degrees C to obtain matrix materialwith mass ratio of 100-200:10-20:1-3, adding 3 wt.% hydrogen peroxide and horseradish peroxidase, stirring and mixing for 2-5 hours, dialysis liquid to obtain fish collagen facial mask.

PN CN108654572-A
TI Preparing adsorbent molecular sieve useful for aquaculture wastewater, comprises e.g. ultrasonically cleaning zeolite molecular sieve, heating, insulating, sintering, naturally cooling, cleaning reinforced zeolite molecular sieve and drying.
AU LIU H
   ZHANG X
AE MAANSHAN SNAHUAN BIYUAN WATER TREATMENT (MAAN-Non-standard)
GA 201883724B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing adsorbent molecular sieve comprises (i) ultrasonically cleaning zeolite molecular sieve with deionized water; (ii) heating the zeolite molecular sieve in a vacuum furnace, circulating nitrogen, reducing temperature after insulating and stopping introducing nitrogen, circulating carbon dioxide into the vacuum furnace when the temperature is lowered, and reducing the temperature after insulating; (iii) mixing uniformly zeolite molecular sieve, activated glass fiber, hemp fiber and polylactic acid, sintering in resistance furnace, and naturally cooling to room temperature; (iv) mixing uniformly modified zeolite molecular sieve, zinc dialkyldithiophosphate, polytetrafluoroethylene and graphene, sintering in a furnace under hydrogen protection atmosphere, and naturally cooling to room temperature; and (v) cleaning the reinforced zeolite molecular sieve with deionized water to a constant weight, and placing in a vacuum oven for drying.
   USE - The sieve is useful for aquaculture wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical; and has strong adsorption property and good sewage purification effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing adsorbent molecular sieve comprises (i) ultrasonically cleaning zeolite molecular sieve with deionized water until the washing liquid is clear; (ii) heating the zeolite molecular sieve to 600-700 degrees C in a vacuum furnace, circulating nitrogen into the vacuum furnace, reducing the temperature after insulating for 30 minutes and at the same time stopping introducing nitrogen, circulating carbon dioxide into the vacuum furnace when the temperature is lowered to 400-500 degrees C, and reducing the temperature after insulating for 20 minutes; (iii) mixing uniformly zeolite molecular sieve, activated glass fiber, hemp fiber and polylactic acid, sintering in resistance furnace at 500-600 degrees C for 2-3 hours, and naturally cooling to room temperature; (iv) mixing uniformly modified zeolite molecular sieve, zinc dialkyldithiophosphate, polytetrafluoroethylene and graphene, sintering in a furnace under hydrogen protection atmosphere at 400-500 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and naturally cooling to room temperature; and (v) cleaning the reinforced zeolite molecular sieve with deionized water to a constant weight, and placing in a vacuum oven for drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of zeolite molecular sieve, activated glass fiber, hemp fiber and polylactic acid is 500-600:11-13:8-10:13-15. The mass ratio of modified zeolite molecular sieve, zinc dialkyldithiophosphate, polytetrafluoroethylene and graphene is 200-300:10-20:10-12:5-7.Preferred Method: The method comprises subjecting polylactic acid to modified treatment comprising (a) heating polylactic acid to 160-170 degrees C to a molten state, adding 3% carbon particles into the melting liquid, stirring uniformly, rapidly placing into water for cooling to the room temperature, filtering and collecting the filter residue; (b) immersing the filter residue into 3-5 wt.% hydrochloric acid solution for 3-4 hours, immersing into 1 wt.% potassium permanganate solution for 20-30 minutes, cleaning and drying; and (c) placing the dried modified polylactic acid into a vacuum drying box and drying at 70-80 degrees C for 40 minutes. The preparation of carbon particles comprises (d) cleaning wheat straw with deionized water until the solution is clarified, placing into a oven for drying and adding into a hydrofluoric acid solution and stirring for 20 minutes until the solution is turbid, filtering, cleaning the wheat straw with deionized water until the solution is clear, and placing into oven for drying; (e) placing processed wheat straw into a oxygen furnace, heating to 500-600 degrees C, sintering for 3-4 hours and naturally cooling to room temperature; (f) grinding sintered product, and sieving through 1000 mesh sieve.

PN CN108408701-A; CN108408701-B
TI Helium and neon gas production involves placing air separations contain barren liquid oxygen into liquid oxygen storage tank, and then initially concentrating in lean tower to supercritical pressure.
AU MAO L
   CHAI X
   XU Q
   LI C
   LOU Y
   CUI J
   YE Z
   GUO Z
   WANG Y
   YE C
AE QUZHOU HANGYANG SPECIAL GAS CO LTD (QUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201866834T
AB    NOVELTY - Helium and neon gas production involves placing air separations contain barren liquid oxygen into liquid oxygen storage tank, initially concentrating in lean tower to supercritical pressure, and then transferring initially concentrated barium-containing liquid oxygen to oxygen vaporization heat exchanger for vaporization. The oxygen regenerator exchanges heat with the high-temperature reflux oxygen gas and enters the electric heater to heat at 450 degrees C. The heated barium-containing liquid oxygen enters the methane reactor under action of palladium catalyst, where methane is reacted with oxygen to generate carbon dioxide and water. The methane-removed oxygen is entered into the oxygen regenerator at 10 degrees C. The cooled oxygen enters the molecular sieve adsorber for switching, where carbon dioxide, hydrocarbons and moisture are adsorbed in oxygen.
   USE - Method for producing helium and neon gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the helium and neon gas in a simple manner with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The oxygen purified by the molecular sieve adsorber is throttled and entered into pre-cooling heat exchanger having refluxing low-temperature oxygen and nitrogen. The helium and oxygen are separated in the helium concentration tower, where the oxygen is withdrawn from the top and returned to the lean tower. The concentrated helium is stored at the bottom of the tower, where the helium mixture is withdrawn and sent to the helium separation column. The crude mash at the bottom of the separation tower is sent to the pure sputum tower to obtain 99.995% pure helium gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a production line for producing helium and neon gas, which comprises a liquid oxygen storage tank, a liquid oxygen pump, a lean tower, an oxygen vaporization heat exchanger, and a high pressure throttle valve.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the methane reaction furnace and an oxygen regenerator.
   Furnace body (61)
   Feed port (611)
   Discharge port (612)
   Catalyst bed (613)
   Palladium catalyst (613)

TI Well gas-turbine-nuclear oil-and-gas producing complex contains mine shafts for penetration of an oil and gas bearing deposit.
AU ILYUSHA A V
   AMBARTSUMYAN G L
   PANKOV D A
   GROSHEV I V
   GRUSHCHENKO A V
   NECHAEV D I
AE TEKHNOPODZEMENERGO SCI-TECH & COMML-IND (TEKH-Non-standard)
GA 2018421844
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to a device for the development and operation of oil and gas fields with the help of a mine-and-well gas-turbine-nuclear complex. Mine-and-well gas-turbine-nuclear oil and gas producing complex (plant) contains mine shafts for penetration of an oil and gas bearing deposit, underground mining development workings of excavation and production well blocks, production wells with horizontal sections conducted in the productive layer, drilled from a surface or from underground development workings. System of injection-stimulating wells is drilled from underground mining development workings. Gas turbine electric power plant and integrated oil and gas treatment unit are installed on the surface of the complex. Nuclear power plant is located in a shaft bottom of the mine. High-temperature turbine exhaust gas channel the gas turbine power plant through a waste heat boiler and a compression-liquefaction device of carbon dioxide by a stem pipeline is connected to the heads of injection-stimulating wells directly or through one of the inputs of the switching and discharging device of a working substance - carbon dioxide or coolant into an oil and gas bearing layer. Another input of the installation located in the shaft bottom of the mine of the switching and discharging of the working substance by a thermally insulated pipeline is connected with a nuclear reactor of the nuclear power plant. Tubular heat exchangers installed in the injection-stimulating wells are included by pipelines in a circulation circuit of the thermal energy of the nuclear reactor of the nuclear power plant. Located in the injection-stimulating wells, electric well heaters or electromagnetic flowlines of induction heating are powered with redundancy from a surface gas turbine power station and from a turbomachinery power converter of an underground nuclear power plant.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result consists in the possibility of the oil and gas fields development stimulation, increasing the completeness of recovery of hydrocarbon crude (increase in ORF), and increasing the degree and efficiency of its use.

AE CHINA ORDNANCE SCI INST NINGBO BRANCH (CHNI-C)
GA 201811770E
AB    NOVELTY - Biomedical degradable absorbable magnesium alloy comprises 0.1-8 wt.% strontium, 0.1-5 wt.% calcium, 0.01-2 wt.% silver, magnesium and unavoidable impurities, where the unavoidable impurities is copper, iron, nickel, aluminum, manganese and/or rare earth elements, the each impurity content is not greater than 0.1%, and total amount of the unavoidable impurities is not greater than 0.5%.
   USE - The magnesium alloy is useful in orthopedic implantation therapy field as implant material under human environment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The magnesium alloy: has excellent biocompatibility, excellent mechanical properties, corrosion resistance performance, and long lasting antibacterial function to promote new bone formation function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing biomedical degradable absorbable magnesium alloy, comprising (i) taking raw materials of pure magnesium, pure strontium, pure calcium and pure silver based on molar ratio, (ii) placing the above raw materials in 100-150 degrees C oven and drying for 0.5-2.5 hours, (iii) taking nickel free-low carbon steel crucible in which inner wall coated with boron nitride paint, heating the crucible to 620-680 degrees C, adding the pure magnesium into the crucible, heating at 710-730 degrees C for 30-40 minutes until to melt completely the pure magnesium, (iv) heating the crucible to 730-750 degrees C, pressing the pure strontium and pure calcium into the pure melt magnesium using graphite bell jar, stirring, and melting for 30-50 minutes to obtain solution A, (v) heating the resistance furnace to 750-780 degrees C, adding dried high pure silver into the solution A, then stirring and melting for 30-40 minutes to obtain solution B, and (vi) pouring the solution B into the pre-dried nickel-free-low carbon steel mold in which inner wall coated with boron nitride paint, under a mixture of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride gas atmosphere, melting to obtain final product, where the carbon dioxide gas content is 99-99.5% and sulfur hexafluoride gas content is 0.5-1%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The biomedical degradable absorbable magnesium alloy comprises 0.5-5 wt.% strontium, 0.5-2 wt.% calcium, 0.05-1.5 wt.% silver and magnesium (balance). Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the stirring conditions are: for every 10 minutes stirring for one time, and the total stirring times is 2-4 times. In the step (v), the stirring conditions are: for every 10 minutes stirring for one time, and the total stirring times is 2-3 times.

TF EXAMPLE - Alkalized alumina adsorbent was prepared from                alumina powder (boehmite) and sodium carbonate. The                boehmite and sodium carbonate were mixed in                distilled water. The resulting solution was                filtered and sediment dried. This sediment mixed                with a nitric acid solution was extruded into                pellets and fired. 210 g Boehmite V-700 (RTM:                Mid-density pseudoboehmite), 132 g disodium                carbonate and 900 ml water were mixed at 90 degrees                C for 2 hours. The obtained solution was filtered                and the sediment was left to dry over night at room                temperature. 380 g Sediment material were put in                the drying oven for 3.5 hours. 309.4 g Dried                material were mixed with 163 ml nitric acid                solution. Pellets were extruded, dried at room                temperature and then calcined with heating from                room temperature up to 100 degrees C at 0.2 degrees                C/minute for 2 hours and then heating 100-650                degrees C at 0.5 degrees C/minute for 4                hours.

TI Polymerization process used to make polymer of e.g. polyether carbonate polyols and polyester polyols, comprises reacting carbon dioxide with epoxide and/or anhydride with epoxide in presence of chain transfer agent and metal complexes.
AU KEMBER M
   LEELAND J
   LANDEL M
   SMITH R
   MORTON A
   DRANE S
AE ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
GA 201924176X
AB    NOVELTY - Polymerization process, comprises reacting (a) carbon dioxide with an epoxide and/or (b) an anhydride with an epoxide, where the process is carried out in the presence of a chain transfer agent and a catalyst of substituted metal complexes (I).
   USE - The polymerization process is useful for producing a polymer comprising polycarbonates, polyether carbonate polyols or polyester polyols which are useful in e.g. polyurethane or other higher polymer (all claimed), electronic components, construction materials, data storage products, automotive and aircraft products, security components, medical applications, mobile phones, packaging (including bottles) and optical applications (e.g. safety glass, windscreens).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymerization process: utilizes (I) that retain good activity during the polymerization process; utilizes the chain transfer agent that controls the molecular weight of the polymers produced; and provides the polyether carbonate polyols that exhibit high strength, high toughness, high gloss, high transparency, low haze, high gas (e.g. oxygen and carbon dioxide) or water barrier properties, flame resistance, UV resistance, high durability, rigidity and stiffness, compatibility with plasticizers, broad dimensional stability temperature, biodegradability and biocompatibility, and modulus of elasticity and yield strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polymerization process, comprises reacting (a) carbon dioxide with an epoxide and/or (b) an anhydride with an epoxide, where the process is carried out in the presence of a chain transfer agent and a catalyst of substituted metal complexes of formula (I).
   R1, R2 = alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, haloalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, aryloxy, alkylthio, arylthio, alicyclic or heteroalicyclic group (all optionally substituted), H, halide, NO2, nitrile, imine,, -NCR13R14, NH2, ether -OR15, -R18OR17, ester -OC(O)R10 or -C(O)OR10, amido -NR9C(0)R9 or -C(O)-NR9(R9), -COOH, -C(O)R15, -OP(O)(OR18)(OR19), -P(O)R20R21, -P(O)(OR)(OR), -OP(O)R(OR), silyl, silyl ether, sulfoxide, sulfonyl, sulfinate or acetylide;
   R3 = alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene, heteroalkynylene, arylene, heteroarylene or cycloalkylene (all optionally substituted by aryl, heteroaryl, alicyclic or heteroalicyclic);
   R4 = aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl or alkylaryl (all optionally substituted) or H;
   R9, R10, R13, R14, R18, R19, R20, R21 = H or aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl or heteroaryl;
   E1 = C;
   E2 = O, S or NH or E1 is N and E2 is O;
   E3 = N, NR5, O or S, when E3 is N, broken line is double bond, and when E3 is NR5, O or S, the broken line is single bond;
   R5 = aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl, -alkyl-C(O)OR10, -alkyl nitrile or alkylaryl (all optionally substituted) or H;
   X = aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl or heteroaryl (all optionally substituted), OC(O)R , OSO2R1x, OSOR1x, OSO(R1x)2, S(O)R1x, OR1x, phosphinate, halide, nitrate, OH, carbonate, NH2, NO2 or amido;
   m, n = 0-3;
   m+n = 0-4;
   R1x = H or aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl or heteroaryl;
   G = absent or neutral or anionic donor ligand which is a Lewis base;
   Y1, Y2 = neutral or anionic donor group capable of donating a lone pair to the metal M2; and
   M1, M2 = Zn(ll), Cr(ll), Co(ll), Cu(ll), Mn(ll), Mg(ll), Ni(ll), Fe(ll), Ti(ll), V(ll), Cr(lll), Co(lll), n(lll), Ni(lll), Fe(lll), Ca(ll), Ge(ll), Al(lll), Ti(lll), V(lll), Ge(IV), Y(lll), Sc(lll) or Ti(IV).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) substituted metal complexes (I);
   (2) a polymer produced by the process; and
   (3) a polyurethane or other higher polymer produced from polycarbonates, polyether carbonate polyols or polyester polyols.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lone pair donating atom of the Y1 and Y2 may be attached directly to the remainder of (I), via a bond to the respective aryl group, or may be attached to the remainder of (I) via a linking group attached to the respective aryl group, where the linking group, when present in Y1 and/or Y2, is alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene, heteroalkynylene, arylene, heteroarylene or cycloalkylene (all optionally substituted), the linking group, when present in Y1 and/or Y2 is alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene or arylene (all optionally substituted), the linking group, when present in Y1 and/orY2, is optionally substituted methylene, for the avoidance of doubt, when the lone pair donating atom of the Y1 and/or Y2 groups is a carbene carbon and the carbene carbon is not attached directly to the remainder of (I). (I) comprises substituted metal complexes of formula (II), preferably 64 metal complexes e.g. nickel complexes of formula (III)-(VII). The DMC catalyst has metal complexes (M1ad(M2ae(CN)f)g) (VIII) or (M1ad(M2ae(CN)f)g.hM3aX2i.JR1c.kH2O.lHfX1) (IX). The chain transfer agent comprises water or substituted polymeric compounds (Z-(W1)a) (X), preferably water, mono-alcohols (i.e. alcohols with one hydroxy e.g. 4-ethylbenzenesulfonic acid, methanol,
