The epoxide is on a group which is aliphatic, including acyclic and alicyclic, or aromatic. The epoxide comprises cyclohexene oxide, styrene oxide, optionally substituted alkylene oxides e.g. ethylene oxide, propylene oxide and butylene oxide, substituted cyclohexene oxides (e.g. limonene oxide, C10H16O or 2-(3,4-epoxycyclohexyl)ethyltrimethoxysilane, C11H2O), optionally substituted oxiranes (e.g. oxirane, epichlorohydrin, 2-(2-methoxyethoxy)methyl oxirane (MEMO), 2-(2-(2-methoxyethoxy)ethoxy)methyl oxirane (ME2MO), 2-(2-(2-(2-methoxyethoxy)ethoxy)ethoxy)methyl oxirane (ME3MO), 1,2-epoxybutane, glycidyl ethers, glycidyl esters, glycidyl carbonates, vinyl-cyclohexene oxide, 3-phenyl-1,2-epoxypropane, 1,2- and 2,3-epoxybutane, isobutylene oxide, cyclopentene oxide, 2,3-epoxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydronaphthalene, indene oxide and/or functionalized 3,5-dioxaepoxides, preferably propylene oxide.d, e, f, g = integers and are chosen so that the DMC catalyst has electron neutrality;X = anion comprising halide, oxide, hydroxide, sulfate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate, preferably halide;i = 1 or more and the charge on the anion X multiplied by i satisfies the valency of M3a;r = integer that corresponds to the charge on the counterion X1;R1c = complexing agent, may be a (poly)ether, a polyether carbonate, a polycarbonate, a poly(tetramethylene ether diol), a ketone, an ester, an amide, an alcohol (e.g. 1-8C alcohol) or urea, preferably propylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, (m)ethoxy ethylene glycol, dimethoxyethane, tert-butyl alcohol, ethylene glycol monomethyl ether, diglyme, triglyme, methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, n-butyl alcohol, isobutyl alcohol, sec-butyl alcohol, 3-buten-1 -ol, 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol, 2-methyl-3-butyn-2-ol or 3-methyl-1-pentyn-3-ol;h, j, k and/or I = 0-20;Z = optionally substituted moiety comprising aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, polyolefin, polyester, polyether and/or polycarbonate;W1 = OH, NH2, thiol or carboxylate group; anda = integer which is at least 2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The process is carried out in the presence of a further catalyst being a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst. The first two of the metal centers in the double metal cyanide (DM0) catalyst are represented by M1a and M2a, where M1a comprises zinc(ll) (preferred), ruthenium(ll), ruthenium(lll), iron(ll), nickel(ll), manganese(ll), cobalt(ll), tin(ll), lead(ll), iron(lll), molybdenum(IV), molybdenum(Vl), aluminum(lll), vanadium(V), vanadium(Vl), strontium(ll), tungsten(IV), tungsten(Vl), copper(ll) or chromium(ll) and M2a comprises iron(ll), iron(lll), cobalt(ll), cobalt(lll), chromium(ll), chromium(lll), manganese(ll), manganese(lll), iridium(lll), nickel(ll), rhodium(lll), ruthenium(ll), vanadium(IV) or vanadium(V), preferably cobalt(II) or cobalt (lll) and optionally, a further metal center(s) is present, the further metal center comprises M1a or M1b. When the polymerization process comprises the reaction of an anhydride with an epoxide, the anhydride may be any compound comprising an anhydride moiety in a ring system (i.e. a cyclic anhydride) and the epoxide may be any of the epoxides, preferably the anhydrides have e.g. dihydro-furan-2,5-dione, furan-2,5-dione, isobenzofuran-1,3-dione, tetrahydro-cyclopenta(c)furan-1,3-dione and 3a,4,7,7a-tetrahydro-isobenzofuran-1,3-dione. The polymerization process is carried out at: a pressure of 1-100 (preferably 1 or 10) atmospheres; and 0-250 degrees C, preferably 50-120 degrees C. The polymerization process is carried out in the reaction of carbon dioxide with an epoxide at a catalytic mol:mol loading of 1:1000-100000 catalyst:epoxide, preferably 1:50000-100000 catalyst:epoxide and in the reaction of an anhydride with an epoxide at a catalytic loading of 1:1000-300000 catalyst:total monomer content, preferably 1:50000- 100000 catalyst:total monomer content. The mass ratio of (I) to the DMC catalyst is 300:1-0.1:1, preferably 5:1-3:1. A starter compound may be present in molar ratios of 200:1-1:1, preferably 50:1-20:1, relative to (I). Preferred Components: The polymer comprises polycarbonates, polyether carbonate polyols or polyester polyols. The number-average molecular weight of the polymer products is 1000-100000 g/mol. The polyether carbonate polyols and polyester polyols have a number-average molecular weight of 200-20000 (preferably less than 10000) g/mol. The polymer products have a polydispersity index of less than 2, preferably less than 1.2.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M1, M2 = Ni(ll), Ni(lll), Co(ll), Co(lll), Cr(ll), Cr(lll), Fe(ll), Fe(lll), Mn(lll), Al(lll), Zn(ll) or Mg(ll);
   E1 = C;
   E2 = O;
   R1 = H, methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, i-propyl n-butyl, t-butyl, t-amyl or t-octyl;
   R3 = -CH2C(CH3)2CH2-, -CH2CH2CH2, -CH2CH(CH3)CH2- or -CH2C(C2H5)2CH2-;
   R4 = H, methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, n-butyl, phenyl or trifluoromethyl; and
   R5 = H, Me, Et, Bn, i-Pr, t-Bu or Ph.

TI Hybrid system of carbon dioxide membrane used for separation of carbon dioxide in atmosphere, has controller to control flow rate and carbon dioxide content of gas supplied and discharged to blower, light culture process unit, mixing tank, membrane process unit, and mineralization reaction unit.
AU CHANG W S
   LIM J W
   OH M S

   KIM K M
   NAMGUNG H G
   YU J H
   JANG W S
   LIM J
   JUN L
   NAMGUNG H

AE KOREA DISTRICT HEATING CORP (KODI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C23529
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a separation membrane processing unit (400) that is configured for separating the concentrated gas. A mineralization reaction unit is configured for mineralizing carbon dioxide using the third concentrated gas separated in the separation membrane processing unit and discharging a third processing gas to the mixing tank (300). A sensor unit is configured for measuring the concentration of carbon dioxide emitted from each process using sensors. A controller is configured for controlling the flow rate and carbon dioxide content of the gas supplied and discharged to the blower, the light culture process unit, the mixing tank, the separation membrane process unit, and the mineralization reaction unit using the detection value of the sensor unit.
   USE - Hybrid system of carbon dioxide membrane used for separation of carbon dioxide in atmosphere at urban power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The compact system effectively captures and separates carbon dioxide in a small space at low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The urban power plant has light culture process unit that receives exhaust gas from the blower (100) for performing a light culture process using microalgae for discharging a first processing gas. A mixing tank into which the exhaust gas supplied from the blower and the first process gas are introduced. A second process gas mixed in the mixing tank is supplied, and the second process gas is used with separation membranes to form a first enriched gas containing nitrogen, a second enriched gas containing oxygen, and a third enriched gas containing carbon dioxide. A blower is configured for distributing first enriched gas. A light culture process unit is configured for receiving exhaust gas from the blower for performing a light culture process using microalgae, and discharging a first processing gas. The first concentrated gas is micro-bubbled through a fine disperser and injected into the mineralization reaction unit, and reacts with the third concentrated gas supplied to the mineralization reaction unit to form a mineralization slurry tank inside. A carbon dioxide compact separation membrane and a carbon resource recycling hybrid system uniformly mix the exhaust gas. The carbon dioxide compact separation membrane and a carbon resource conversion hybrid system supplies second concentrated gas of 45 to 55m 3A per hour. A first sensor (610) is configured for measuring the carbon dioxide of the exhaust gas introduced through the first blower (110). A second sensor (620) is configured for measuring the carbon dioxide of the first processing gas discharged from the light culture process unit. A third sensor (630) is configured for measuring the carbon dioxide of the mixed gas discharged from the mixing tank. A fourth sensor (640) is configured for measuring the carbon dioxide discharged from the separation membrane process unit. A fifth sensor is configured for measuring the carbon dioxide of the third concentrated gas discharged from the separation membrane process. A sixth sensor (660) is configured for measuring carbon dioxide emitted from the mineralization reaction unit (500). The control unit controls the carbon dioxide concentration detected by the sixth sensor by increasing the recirculation rate of the separation membrane process unit, when the carbon dioxide concentration sensed by the fourth sensor is higher than a preset value. The control unit increases the amount of calcium carbonate input to the mineralization reaction unit to increase the carbon dioxide concentration detected by the sixth sensor, when the carbon dioxide concentration detected by the fourth sensor is higher than a preset value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a hybrid system of carbon dioxide membrane. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Separation membrane processing unit (400)
   Mineralization reaction unit (500)
   First sensor (610)
   Second sensor (620)
   Third sensor (630)
   Fourth sensor (640)
   Sixth sensor (660)

GA 202297841N
AB    NOVELTY - Judging oil chromatogram hydrogen                over-standard fault of an oil-immersed transformer                involves oil chromatographic data collecting and                collecting oil chromatographic data of the                transformer to be tested the oil chromatographic                data comprises oil in each gas content, total                hydrocarbon content and water content (mg/L), where                each gas comprises hydrogen, methane, ethane,                ethylene, acetylene, carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide; calculating the gas change trend to the                gas absolute gas rate as the evaluation state                quantity, time is independent variable, calculating                the gas change trend by formula; and calculating                the absolute gas production rate of hydrogen,                ethane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and total                hydrocarbon operation by the absolute gas                production rate calculation by a formula.
   USE - Method for judging oil chromatogram hydrogen                over-standard fault of an oil-immersed transformer                i.e. power transformer, in a fault diagnosis                technical field power transformer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively solve the problem                that the hydrogen content exceeds the fault                accurately classified. It has good social and                economic benefits. The method is simple, accurate,                and can quickly identify the reason of the hydrogen                over proof fault.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Judging oil chromatogram hydrogen                over-standard fault of an oil-immersed transformer                involves oil chromatographic data collecting and                collecting oil chromatographic data of the                transformer to be tested the oil chromatographic                data comprises oil in each gas content, total                hydrocarbon content and water content (mg/L), where                each gas comprises hydrogen, methane, ethane,                ethylene, acetylene, carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide; calculating the gas change trend to the                gas absolute gas rate as the evaluation state                quantity, time is independent variable, calculating                the gas change trend by formula; calculating the                absolute gas production rate of hydrogen, ethane,                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and total                hydrocarbon operation by the absolute gas                production rate calculation by a formula; when the                absolute gas production rate of hydrogen exceeds 10                mL, if there is one of the two conditions such as a                when the transformer voltage level is &lt; 220                kVH2 L/L, ethane content is &lt; 5 L/L, and the                total hydrocarbon content is &lt; 150 L/L, when                the voltage grade of the transformer is                &#8805;330 kVH2 uL/L, the content of ethane is                &lt; 1 uL/L, and the total hydrocarbon content                is &lt; 150 uL/L, can be determined that a                single hydrogen over proof abnormality occurs, at                this time, the methane and the transformer water                content in the hydrocarbon gas are synchronously                monitored, the change trend analyzed; shortening                the sampling time interval continuously monitoring                the change of trend, ethane, water, combined with                transformer history maintenance record, judging the                hydrogen over proof abnormal failure mode A, when                the hydrogen content is continuously increased, at                the same time, with the methane content is                continuously increased and methane/hydrogen is                &lt; 0.1, the water content in the transformer                is not changed, if the starting time point of                hydrogen content is matched with the transformer                development maintenance time point, then judging                the hydrogen exceeds the over proof reason for                partial discharge in the transformer, when the                hydrogen content is increased to a certain value                keeping unchanged and the content of methane and                water in the transformer is not changed, at the                same time, replacing the metal accessory contact                with the oil with the oil in the 1-year                transformer, then judging that the hydrogen super                standard is analyzed stainless steel the material                absorbing hydrogen, when the hydrogen content is                continuously increased and the water content in the                transformer is continuously increased, at the same                time, the methane content is not changed, then it                can judge the hydrogen over proof reason is the                insulating moisture in the transformer.

TI Dewaxing and decolorization of industrial hemp mosaic leaf extract involves heat-treating hemp mosaic particles for conversion and decarboxylation, subjecting decarboxylated particles to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, extracting crude extract, and short-path molecular distillation.
AU JIANG Y
   KONG L
   WANG Z
   LIU S
   XUE H
AE YUNNAN LVXIN BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202206983U
AB    NOVELTY - Dewaxing and decolorization of industrial hemp mosaic leaf extract involves sieving the industrial hemp mosaic leaves to remove impurities, removing the hemp stems and hemp seeds, crushing into 30-40 mesh mosaic particles, heat-treating at 120-150 degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours for conversion and decarboxylation to obtain decarboxylated particles, subjecting the decarboxylated particles to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, extracting to obtain a crude extract of the extract with a cannabidiol content of 18-24 (w/w)%, filtering the crude extract under the heating condition of 100-120degrees Celsius to remove the solid impurities in the crude extract of the extract, and the filtration accuracy of the crude extract of the extract is 400-500 meshes, collecting the filtrate by filtering, and short-path molecular distillation to carry out dewaxing, decolorization.
   USE - Dewaxing and decolorization of industrial hemp mosaic leaf extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, safe and environmentally-friendly, and enables dewaxing and decolorization of industrial hemp mosaic leaf extract with excellent fluidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dewaxing and decolorization of industrial hemp mosaic leaf extract involves sieving the industrial hemp mosaic leaves to remove impurities, removing the hemp stems and hemp seeds, crushing into 30-40 mesh mosaic particles, heat-treating at 120-150degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours for conversion and decarboxylation to obtain decarboxylated particles, subjecting the decarboxylated particles to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where the process conditions for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction such as the temperature of the extraction kettle is 40-55degrees Celsius, the extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa, and the temperature of the separation kettle is 40-50degrees Celsius, the separation pressure is 7-9 MPa, the extraction time is 2-4 hours, and extracting to obtain a crude extract of the extract with a cannabidiol content of 18-24 (w/w)%, filtering the crude extract under the heating condition of 100-120degrees Celsius to remove the solid impurities in the crude extract of the extract, and the filtration accuracy of the crude extract of the extract is 400-500 meshes, collecting the filtrate by filtering, and short-path molecular distillation to carry out dewaxing, decolorization. The short-path molecular distillation involves subjecting the filtrate to level (L1) molecular distillation to a preheating temperature of 120-140degrees Celsius, heating temperature of 140-160degrees Celsius, condensation temperature of 50-70degrees Celsius, vacuum degree of (-0.07)-(-0.09) MPa to obtain light component (A) and heavy component (A) after one-stage molecular distillation, secondary molecular distilling the heavy component (A) at preheating temperature of 140-150degrees Celsius, heating temperature of 160-180degrees Celsius, condensing temperature of 50 degreesC-70degrees Celsius, vacuum degree of (-0.07)-(-0.09) MPa to obtain light component (B) and heavy component (B), subjecting heavy component B to tertiary molecular distillation at preheating temperature of 140-150degrees Celsius, heating temperature of 180-200degrees Celsius, condensing temperature of 50-70degrees Celsius, vacuum degree of (-0.07)-(-0.09) MPa to obtain light component (C) and heavy component (C), subjecting light component (B) and the light component (C) to quaternary molecular distillation at preheating temperature of 140-160degrees Celsius, condensation temperature of 50-70degrees Celsius, vacuum degree of (-0.07)-(-0.09) MPa to obtain light component (D) and heavy component (D), mixing the light component (A) and the light component (D) to obtain a wax-free and golden-yellow cannabis crude oil, where the cannabidiol content in the cannabis crude oil is 35-45 (w/w)%, and the tetrahydrocannabinol content in the heavy component (C) and the heavy component (D) obtained by quaternary molecular distillation are 0.3 (w/w)%or less.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The feeding rates of the primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary molecular distillation are 2-5 g/minute. The wiper rotation speed of the primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary molecular distillation are 200-300 rpm. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 30-50 L/minute.

TI Forming a polymer, comprises polymerizing monomer            in presence of composition comprising bulk material,            and surface comprising ions of metal bonded to one            another via linker groups same or different comprising            first and second sites.
AU RAJA R
   STEWART D
AE UNIV SOUTHAMPTON (USTH-C)
   UNIV SOUTHAMPTON (USTH-C)
GA 202170460L
AB    NOVELTY - Forming a polymer, comprises polymerizing a                monomer in the presence of a composition                comprising: a bulk material; and at least one                surface; the bulk material comprising ions of a                metal M1 bonded to one another via linker groups;                the surface comprising ions of a metal M' bonded to                one another via linker groups; the metals M and M'                being the same or different; the surface comprising                at least one first site A1 and at least one second,                different site B1; the site A1 having a hydroxyl                group bonded thereto, the site A1 capable of                interacting with a monomer molecule; and the site B                being a Lewis acid site capable of interacting with                a monomer molecule, where the distance d between                the site A1 and site B1 is monomer molecules can                polymerize on the surface to form the                polymer.
   USE - The method is useful for forming polymer to                composition as catalyst used to catalyze                polymerisation reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides composition has surface                in which hydroxyl-bound sites are present on                surface in sufficient density and in sufficient                proximity to Lewis acidic sites on surface, in use,                both sites can participate in catalytic activity,                these favourable surface properties enable it to be                useful as a hybrid platform catalyst for range of                reactions; and provides composition can be used to                catalyze polymerisation reaction in which carbon                dioxide incorporated into the structure of the                polymer, under much milder conditions, preferably                reaction can be carried out at lower temperatures,                lower pressures and for much shorter reaction times                than, it also provides higher selectivity for                producing polycarbonate-polyether copolymer over                cyclic carbonate), with comparable carbonate                linkages in the polymer backbone; and provides                composition that has greater control of crystallite                size and particle morphology than has been                demonstrated in the prior art.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) forming a polymer in which carbon dioxide                is incorporated into the structure of the polymer,                comprising reacting (a) a molecule Y1, which is a                monomer capable of polymerizing to form a polymer                in which carbon dioxide is incorporated into the                structure of the polymer and (b) carbon dioxide, in                the presence of the composition;
   (2) composition comprising a bulk material,                and at least one surface, the bulk material                comprising ions of a metal M1 bonded to one another                via linker groups, the surface comprising ions of a                metal M' bonded to one another via linker groups,                the metals M1 and M' being the same or different,                the surface comprising at least one first site A1                and at least one second, different site B1, the                site A1 having a hydroxyl group bonded thereto, the                site A1 being capable of interacting with a monomer                molecule, the site B1 being a Lewis acidic site,                the site B1 being capable of interacting with a                monomer molecule, where the distance d between the                site A1 and site B1 is monomer molecules can                polymerize on the surface to form a polymer;                and
   (3) crystallite composition comprising baulk                material and surface, the bulk material comprising                ions of a metal M1 bonded to one another via linker                groups, the surface comprising ions of a metal M'                bonded to one another via linker groups, the metals                M and M' being the same or different, the surface                comprising at least one first site A1 and at least                one second, different site B1, the site A1 having a                hydroxyl group bonded, the site A1 being capable of                interacting with a monomer molecule, the site B1                being a Lewis acidic site, the site B1 being                capable of interacting with a monomer                molecule
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The metal ions M1 and                  M' are scandium, chromium(3+), aluminum cation or                  ferric ion. The linker groups of formula                  (T1(R1)x(R')y(H)z) (I). The composition has                  hydroxy group of formula (Sc3O(OH)(BTC1)2)                  (II).T1 = cycloalkyl, heterocycloalkyl, aryl or                  heteroaryl ring;R1 = functional group capable of                  coordinating to metal ions M1 and M';R' = functional group incapable of                  coordinating to metal ions M1 and M', the                  functional group being other than H;x = 2-6;y = 0-4;z = number sufficient to occupy the                  remaining valences on T1; andBTC = benzene-1, 3, 5-tricarboxylic                  acid.The distance (d) is 0.3-1 nm. The polymer                  formed is a polycarbonate-polyether copolymer.                  The method is carried out at a pressure of 0.1-10                  MPa. The polymer formed is a polyether or                  polyester. The polymer is a                  polycarbonate-polyether copolymer having a                  carbonate linkage incorporation of greater than                  95%. The composition is a crystallite                  composition. The stoichiometric ratio of the                  modulator to the source of linker groups is                  50:1-1:5. The stoichiometric ratio of the                  modulator to the source of linker groups is                  20:1-1:2. The stoichiometric ratio of the source                  of metal ions to the modulator is 1:0.1-1:500.                  The modulator comprises trifluoroacetic acid,                  trichloroacetic acid, acetic acid or benzoic                  acid. The method includes (a) mixing the source                  of metal ions with the modulator and (b) adding                  the linker to the mixture formed in step (a). The                  method is carried out in a solvent, and the                  stoichiometric ratio of the source of metal ions                  to the solvent is 1:10-1:200. The reaction of the                  metal ion source and the linker source, the                  reaction mixture is cooled to a temperature of                  less than 10 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112919413-A
TI Purifying and separating hydrogen production from coal gas involves collecting gas, passing gas through dust collector to filter and remove dust, pumping converter gas into the cooling flue through the fan and cooling temperature of gas.
AU HUANG C
   MENG F
   LI H
AE SHANDONG HENGWEI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202165571U
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying and separating hydrogen production from coal gas involves collecting gas, passing gas through the dust collector to filter and remove dust. The converter gas is pumped into the cooling flue through the fan, the temperature of the gas is cooled, pumped into the shift reaction adsorption tower through the compressor, added iron chromium catalyst in the tower, sprayed water vapor into the shift reaction adsorption tower under the action of the iron chromium catalyst, carbon monoxide and water vapor in the coal gas undergo a shift reaction to form a mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, passed the gas into the first-stage spray tower, kept the temperature in the tower at 200-300 degrees C, and kept the pressure at 0.7-1.5 megaPascal.
   USE - Method for purifying and separating hydrogen production from coal gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes the large amount of waste gas in the coal gasat the initial stage, ensures the influence of the waste gas to the hydrogen purity in the subsequent production proces.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying and separating hydrogen production from coal gas involves collecting gas, passing gas through the dust collector to filter and remove dust. The converter gas is pumped into the cooling flue through the fan, the temperature of the gas is cooled, pumped into the shift reaction adsorption tower through the compressor, added iron chromium catalyst in the tower, sprayed water vapor into the shift reaction adsorption tower under the action of the iron chromium catalyst, carbon monoxide and water vapor in the coal gas undergo a shift reaction to form a mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, passed the gas into the first-stage spray tower, kept the temperature in the tower at 200-300 degrees C, and kept the pressure at 0.7-1.5 megaPascal, and then sprayed the carbon dioxide adsorbent in the pressure swing adsorption tower. The gas-liquid ratio is 0.2-3 m3/L, so that the carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is adsorbed and separated to obtain the mixed gas to be processed, passed the gas into the second-stage spray tower. The second-stage spray tower sprays 0.5-5 mole/L sodium hydroxide solution with a gas-liquid ratio of 0.2-3 m3/L, and the gas is desulfurized, passed the gas into the third-stage spray tower, and the third-stage spray tower sprays 1-10 mole/L sodium hydroxide solution with a gas-liquid ratio of 0.1-1 m3/L. The ammonia gas in the coal gas isa removed after the first-stage spray tower, the second-stage spray tower in contact with water, passed the gas into the debenzene tower for debenzene treatment to obtain crude gas to be treated. The crude gas to be processed still has tar remaining, and the coal gas is passed into the degreasing device for tar removal, and the pre-purified gas is passed into the bottom of the naphthalene removal tower for naphthalene removal, passed the treated crude gas to be treated into an adsorption device with two temperature swing adsorption devices connected in series to further remove residual hydrogen sulfide, naphthalene, and benzene harmful impurities. The gas processed enters from the bottom of the pressure swing adsorption tower, and product hydrogen is obtained from the top of the tower. The produced hydrogen is cooled by an exchanger to produce hydrogen. The hydrogen produced is sent to a deoxygenating dryer for further processing to obtain high-purity hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The cooling flue can be replaced with at least one cold heat exchanger, and the cold heat exchangers can be connected in series to connect the gas In the heat to collect. The oil eliminator is an electrostatic coke trap, and the liquid oil droplets entrained in the coal gas are removed by the oil eliminator and then passed through into the deoiling tower, the liquid and portion of the gaseous heavy components in the coal gas are adsorbed and removed, and then the coal gas is passed through the TSA pretreatment tower, and the TSA pretreatment tower absorbs impurities in the coal gas to obtain oil-free coal gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN112458131-A
TI Preparing trehalose, by activating beer waste yeast, inoculating seed culture solution to fermentation medium, centrifuging, passing carbon dioxide to lyse yeast cell and release trehalose, lyophilizing liquid and freeze-drying liquid.
AU YU L
   HAO Y
   LI L
AE JILIN AOGU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 202126135Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing trehalose using beer waste yeast, involves (a) diluting beer waste liquid with pure water, sieving and centrifugally separating to obtain pure beer waste yeast, (b) activating beer waste yeast, inoculating to seed culture medium and culturing, (c) inoculating heat-treated seed culture solution to fermentation tank containing fermentation culture medium, (d) adding fermentation culture solution to high-pressure reaction kettle, and uniformly stirring, (e) centrifugally filtering second fermentation culture solution for collecting solid to obtain yeast portion, and collecting the filtrate, (f) adding pure water for suspending yeast portion to obtain yeast liquid, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide to lyse yeast cells and release trehalose, (g) lyophilizing liquid and ultrasonically breaking for lysing yeast cells and releasing trehalose, and (h) dissolving substance in water, centrifuging and ultrafiltering filtrate and vacuum freeze-drying.
   USE - Method for preparing trehalose using beer waste yeast.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the fermentation preparation of beer yeast, reduces energy consumption, protects ecological balance, utilizes the waste, reduces production cost, ensures extensive and long-term social benefits, adopts specific anti-stress protective effect of trehalose, applies stress conditions to cause yeast to produce stress response, produces more stress metabolite trehalose, and ensures high yield of trehalose.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing trehalose using beer waste yeast, involves (a) diluting beer waste liquid with pure water, sieving, and centrifugally separating to obtain pure beer waste yeast, (b) activating beer waste yeast, inoculating in fermentation tank filled with seed culture medium, culturing at 180 rpm at 23-28 degrees C for 18-24 hours to obtain seed culture solution, (c) inoculating 10% heat-treated seed culture solution to fermentation tank containing fermentation culture medium at a filling volume of 30-50%, uniformly stirring at a speed of 200 rpm at 30-35 degrees C and culturing for 36 hours to obtain fermentation broth, where the fermentation culture medium comprises 2.05 wt.% peptone, 1.85 wt.% glucose, 2.05 wt.% yeast powder, 1.5 wt.% sodium chloride, and 0.85 wt.% methanol under pH of 5.5-6, and sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 20 minutes, (d) adding fermentation broth to high-pressure reaction kettle, uniformly stirring at a stirring speed of 140 rpm at 30-35 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide at step-up rate of 0.08 MPa/minute to 0.8 MPa, performing constant-pressure fermentation culture for 2-5 hours, performing pressure reduction at step-down rate of 0.08 MPa/minute, and taking out to obtain second fermentation culture solution, (e) centrifugally filtering the second fermentation culture solution, collecting solid to obtain yeast portion, and collecting the filtrate, (f) adding pure water for suspending yeast portion to obtain yeast liquid, placing the yeast liquid in a high-pressure reaction kettle, and uniformly stirring at a stirring speed of 140 rpm at 10-15 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide at a pressure increase rate of 0.1 MPa/minute to increase pressure to 1 MPa and maintaining at constant pressure for 10 minutes, and decreasing the pressure at a pressure decrease rate of 10 MPa/minute to lyse yeast cells and release trehalose, (g) lyophilizing obtained liquid and ultrasonically breaking for completely lysing yeast cells and releasing trehalose, and (h) dissolving obtained substance in water, centrifuging obtained supernatant and filtrate for ultrafiltration, ultrafiltering the filtrate using acid-base column, and performing vacuum freeze-drying process using liquid of acid-base column to obtain trehalose.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (c) involves performing heat treatment at 100 degrees C for 30 minutes. The step (f) involves after reducing the pressure at a pressure reduction rate of 10 MPa/minute, reducing the pressure at 10 MPa/minute, increasing the pressure to 2 MPa, reducing the pressure, and repeating the cycle twice. The step (h) involves performing ultrafiltration process using ultrafiltration membrane with molecular weight of 3 KDa.

PN CN111387433-A
TI Processing lightly fermented pork products, comprises e.g. selecting small pig breeds, washing with clean drinking water, thoroughly washing pigs before slaughter, blowing and breezing until the pigs, and drying.
AU WANG W
   ZHANG J
   BAI T
   JI L
   CHEN L
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV CHENGDU (UYUD-C)
GA 2020688443
AB    NOVELTY - Processing lightly fermented pork products, comprises e.g. (i) selecting and treating raw pigs by (ia) selecting small pig breeds, stop feeding 2-3 days before slaughter, washing with clean drinking water, thoroughly washing the pigs before slaughter, and blowing and breezing until the pigs are dried, (ib) driving the piglets into the carbon dioxide stunning room, using the carbon dioxide stunning method to slowly anesthetize the pigs to stun, immersing in a warm water pool, shaving, washing with clean water, making a cut in the piglet's abdomen, taking out the abdomen and chest organs as a whole, and pulling out the throat, and (ic) hanging the pig's head from the chest and abdomen organs upwards, dripping out the residual blood, wiping the abdomen and thoracic cavity with a clean wet towel, do not rinsing with water, using a deboning knife to remove the limbs, spine and ribs, retaining the bones of the head, cutting out the eyeballs, and deboning.
   USE - The method is useful for processing lightly fermented pork products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fermented pork: exhibits unique color and strong fermented flavor after being cut; and has shelf life of more than 2 years.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing lightly fermented pork products, comprises (i) selecting and treating raw pigs by (ia) selecting small pig breeds, stop feeding 2-3 days before slaughter, washing with clean drinking water, thoroughly washing the pigs before slaughter, and blowing and breezing until the pigs are dried, (ib) driving the piglets into the carbon dioxide stunning room, using the carbon dioxide stunning method to slowly anesthetize the pigs to stun, immersing in a warm water pool, shaving, washing with clean water, making a cut in the piglet's abdomen, taking out the abdomen and chest organs as a whole, and pulling out the throat, and (ic) hanging the pig's head from the chest and abdomen organs upwards, dripping out the residual blood, wiping the abdomen and thoracic cavity with a clean wet towel, do not rinsing with water, using a deboning knife to remove the limbs, spine and ribs, retaining the bones of the head, cutting out the eyeballs, deboning, and making sure that all parts of the pork are completely connected, (ii) using rock salt and cypress seeds as auxiliary materials, drying and crushing cypress seeds instantaneously treated with superheated steam, cooling, milling together with rock salt into granules, simmering crushed cypress seeds and rock salt until the cypress seeds are slightly yellow, and the aroma of cypress seeds enters the salt, (iii) taking part of the fried rock salt and cypress seeds, uniformly spreading on the abdominal cavity and thoracic cavity of the pig and rub them, placing in a tank, marinating in time, applying the remaining rock salt and cypress seeds to the mouth, nostrils and eye sockets, applying at the same time, then using thin twine to completely suture the opening of the pig's abdomen, tightening the mouth with twine, sealing the nasal cavity and eye sockets with food-grade paraffin, and wiping the skin of the pig with a small amount of soju, (iv) conveying marinated and treated piglets to the air-drying fermentation room, placing on the meat rack, using the conditions of a temperature of 10-12 degrees C, a relative humidity of 50-55% and an air velocity of 0.6-0.8 m/s, air-drying and light fermentation for 10-14 months, during which the piglets are turned over and shaped at regular intervals, so that the piglets are gradually shaped into a regular flat shape, and (v) cutting the air-dried, fermenting piglets, and vacuum-packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (ia), the small and medium-sized pig breeds refer to piglets that reach the slaughter age and weigh less than 30 kg. In the step (ii), 60-70 g cypress seeds per kilogram of rock salt are added, and stirring-frying for 25-30 minutes. In the step (iii), the total weight of rock salt and cypress seeds is 5-7% of the net carcass weight of piglets after slaughter, for the first time, 2/3 of the total weight of rock salt and cypress seeds is applied and rubbed, then placed in the tank, marinates for 20-24 hours, and applied the remaining 1/3 of rock salt and cypress seeds. In the step (iv), the piglet is turned over every 20 days during the fermentation period, after 3 months of fermentation, the piglet is pressed once every time the piglet is turned over, and pressed for 2 hours each time. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the rock salt comprises Himalayan rock salt with high iron content, uncontaminated, super quality, Tibet Changdu yellow salt or Beijing Yexian rock salt.

PT P
PN IN201821007564-A; IN389995-B
TI Producing paraxylene by reacting carbon dioxide with alkane in presence of catalyst at predetermined temperature and pressure, separating unconverted carbon dioxide from stream, and contacting stream of syngas with another catalyst.
AU BHATT U B
   KALANTRI P B
   SAPRE A V
   JASRA R V
AE RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
   RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
GA 201976682M
AB    NOVELTY - A paraxylene is produced by (a) reacting carbon dioxide (CO2) with greater than or equal to 1 alkane in presence of 1st catalyst at 1st predetermined temperature and 1st predetermined pressure to obtain 1st stream comprising syngas and unconverted CO2; (b) separating unconverted CO2 from 1st stream to obtain stream of syngas and recycling unconverted CO2 to step (a); (c) contacting stream of syngas with 2nd catalyst at 2nd predetermined temperature and 2nd predetermined pressure to obtain 2nd stream comprising dimethyl ether (DME) and unconverted syngas; (d)separating unconverted syngas from 2nd stream to obtain stream of DME and recycling the unconverted syngas to step (c); (e) adding aromatic stream comprising greater than or equal to 1 of toluene and benzene to stream of DME in the presence of 3rd catalyst at 3rd predetermined temperature and 3rd predetermined pressure to obtain a 3rd stream comprising paraxylene and other compounds; and (f)separating paraxylene from other compounds in 3rd stream.
   USE - Production of paraxylene.
   ADVANTAGE - Consumption of energy is reduced by providing integrated process that combines steps for production of paraxylene from carbon dioxide. The method separates products from by-products in each step of integrated process, and enables recycling of unused feed and by-products for improving the yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus (100) for producing paraxylene comprising (a) reformer (104) which receives CO2 and greater than or equal to 1 alkane and reacting CO2 with alkane in the presence of 1st catalyst to form a 1st stream comprising syngas and unconverted CO2; (b) 1st separating unit (108) for receiving 1st stream and separating unconverted CO2 from 1st stream to obtain stream of syngas; (c) 1st conduit (106) for leading the 1st stream from reformer to 1st separating unit; (d) 2nd conduit (130) for recycling unconverted CO2 to reformer; (e) 1st reactor (112) which receives stream of syngas and contacting stream of syngas with 2nd catalyst to obtain a 2nd stream comprising dimethyl ether (DME) and unconverted syngas; (f) 3rd conduit (110) for leading stream of syngas from 1st separating unit to 1st reactor; (g) 2nd separating unit (116) for receiving 2nd stream, and separating unconverted syngas from 2nd stream to obtain stream of DME; (h) 4th conduit (114) for leading 2nd stream from the 1st reactor to 2nd separating unit; (i) 5th conduit (128) for recycling unconverted syngas to 1st separating unit; (j) 2nd reactor (120) configured for receiving stream of DME and aromatic stream (132) comprising greater than or equal to 1 of toluene and benzene and reacting the two streams in presence of 3rd catalyst to obtain a 3rd stream comprising paraxylene and other compounds; (k) a 6th conduit (118) for leading stream of DME from 2nd separating unit to 2nd reactor; (l) 3rd separating unit (124) which receives 3rd stream and separating paraxylene from other compounds in 3rd stream to yield stream of paraxylene, where other compounds comprise metaxylene, orthoxylene, toluene, benzene, dimethyl ether and water; (m) 7th conduit (122) for leading 3rd stream from the 2nd reactor to 3rd separating unit; and (n) 8th conduit (126) for recycling toluene, benzene and dimethyl ether to 2nd reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus to carry out production of paraxylene.
   Apparatus (100)
   Reformer (104)
   1st conduit (106)
   1st separating unit (108)
   3rd conduit (110)
   1st reactor (112)
   4th conduit (114)
   2nd separating unit (116)
   6th conduit (118)
   2nd reactor (120)
   7th conduit (122)
   3rd separating unit (124)
   8th conduit (126)
   5th conduit (128)
   2nd conduit (130)
   Aromatic stream (132)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 1st catalyst contains greater than or equal to 1 element chosen from nickel, cerium, and cobalt in elemental or compound form. The 2nd catalyst comprises greater than or equal to 1 compound chosen from chromium oxide, zinc oxide and aluminum oxide. The 3rd catalyst contains greater than or equal to 1 zeolite modified with greater than or equal to 1 of zinc oxides, molybednum oxide and boron oxide, where the zeolite is Zeolite Socony Mobil-5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The syngas contains hydrogen gas (H2) and carbon monoxide in 1:1 ratio. The alkane in greater than or equal to 1 alkane having 1-20 carbon atoms. The other compounds in 3rd stream comprise metaxylene, orthoxylene, toluene, benzene, dimethyl ether and water, where toluene, benzene, dimethyl ether in other compounds are separated from metaxylene, orthoxylene, water and recycled to step (e). Preferred Parameters: The 1st predetermined temperature is 300-1000 degrees C, 2nd predetermined temperature is 100-400 degrees C and 3rd predetermined temperature is 200-600 degrees C. The 1st predetermined pressure is 1-80 kg/cm2, 2nd predetermined pressure is 1-60 kg/cm2, and 3rd predetermined pressure is 0.5-5 kg/cm2.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing recombinant platelet-derived growth factor comprises placing human-derived rhPDGF-BB gene in expression vector pCHO1.0, transforming Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cell strain to obtain and obtaining an engineered cell secreting rhPDGF-BB, planting engineered cell strain to produce recombinant platelet-derived growth factor comprising (1) primary seed culture, (2) secondary seed culture, (3) continuously feeding reactor and culturing and the secondary seed cells is diluted to 2 l cell culture reactor at a seeding density of 1.0x 106 /ml with basal medium to obtain 1 l of mixture of secondary seed cells and a basal medium, stirring speed is set to 280 rotations/minutes, the pH is set to 6.95 plus minus 0.15, the pH value is adjusted by sodium bicarbonate solution and carbon dioxide, the dissolved oxygen is set to 40 plus minus 20% and the oxygen gas flow rate is 1.5-400 ml/minutes, according to dissolved oxygen, the oxygen gas flow rate is adjusted according to the consumption condition.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing recombinant platelet-derived growth factor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing recombinant platelet-derived growth factor comprises placing human-derived rhPDGF-BB gene in expression vector pCHO1.0, transforming Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cell strain to obtain and obtaining an engineered cell secreting rhPDGF-BB, planting engineered cell strain to produce recombinant platelet-derived growth factor comprising (1) primary seed culture, (2) secondary seed culture, (3) continuously feeding reactor and culturing and the secondary seed cells is diluted to 2 l cell culture reactor at a seeding density of 1.0x 106 /ml with basal medium to obtain 1 l of mixture of secondary seed cells and a basal medium, stirring speed is set to 280 rotations/minutes, the pH is set to 6.95 plus minus 0.15, the pH value is adjusted by sodium bicarbonate solution and carbon dioxide, the dissolved oxygen is set to 40 plus minus 20% and the oxygen gas flow rate is 1.5-400 ml/minutes, according to dissolved oxygen, the oxygen gas flow rate is adjusted according to the consumption condition, the culture temperature is set to 36.5 plus minus 0.5 degrees C in 1-4 days, the temperature is lowered to 33.5 plus minus 0.5 degrees C on the 5th day, and the fermentation is finished in 14 days, in the continuous flow and culture process, the 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th the feed medium is fed for 11 days and the feed volume is 40 ml, when the glucose concentration is less than 2 g/l, the glucose concentrate is added to increase the glucose concentration to 4.0 g/l. In the step (1), culturing the seed with a seed medium preheating to 36.5 plus minus 0.5 degrees C, diluting to a density of plus minus 0.1x 106/ml to a shake flask for amplification culture, shake flask with carbon dioxide shaker at 36.5 plus minus 0.5 degrees C, 6 plus minus 1% carbon dioxide, 130 rotations/minutes for 3 plus minus 1 for each expansion culture Days, amplification 3 times, the viable cell density during the amplification process does not exceed 6.0 x 106/ml. AU CHENG S

The support                table is provided with servo motor (2) and bracket                (3). The servo motor and bracket are connected by a                coupling. PN WO2022079408-A1; GB2602695-A
TI Producing gas stream comprising carbon monoxide by            feeding gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and            hydrogen to burner, combusting, passing combusted gas            mixture through bed of reverse water-gas shift            catalyst, cooling crude product gas mixture and            removing carbon dioxide.
AU CLAXTON H A
   COOK A L S
   MCKENNA M J
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY DAVY TECHNOLOGIES LTD (JOHO-C)
GA 202253368P
AB    NOVELTY - A process for producing a gas stream                comprising carbon monoxide comprises (a) feeding a                gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen                to a burner arranged in reverse water-gas shift                vessel and combusting it with sub-stoichiometric                amount of an oxygen gas stream to form a combusted                gas mixture comprising carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, hydrogen and steam, (b) passing the                combusted gas mixture through a bed of reverse                water-gas shift catalyst arranged within the                reverse water-gas shift vessel to form a crude                product gas mixture, (c) cooling the crude product                gas mixture to below the dew point and recovering a                condensate to form a dewatered product gas, (d)                removing carbon dioxide from the dewatered product                gas in a carbon dioxide removal unit to form the                gas stream comprising carbon monoxide, and (e)                combining carbon dioxide recovered by the carbon                dioxide removal unit with the gas mixture                comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide fed to the                reverse water-gas shift vessel.
   USE - The process is used for producing gas stream                comprising carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process efficiently performs the reverse                water-gas shift reaction with satisfactory                conversion to produce a gas stream containing                carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing gas stream by the process comprising                (b) feed line for feeding a gas mixture comprising                carbon dioxide and hydrogen, (b) reverse water-gas                shift vessel operatively connected to the feed                line, and comprising (i) burner operatively                connected to a source of oxygen, (ii) combustion                zone adjacent to the burner, (iii) bed of reverse                water-gas shift catalyst arranged downstream of the                combustion zone, and (iv) outlet for crude product                gas mixture containing carbon monoxide, steam and                carbon dioxide, (c) unit operatively connected to                the reverse water-gas shift vessel for cooling the                crude product gas mixture to below the dew point                and recovering the condensate to form a dewatered                product gas, (d) carbon dioxide removal unit                coupled to the unit for cooling the crude product                gas mixture and recovering the condensate to                recover carbon dioxide from the dewatered product                gas, and (e) conduit that feeds at least a portion                of the recovered carbon dioxide from the carbon                dioxide removal unit to the feed line for feeding                the gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and                hydrogen to the reverse water-gas shift                vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide is                  obtained from ammonia plant that uses hydrocarbon                  or carbonaceous feed, or from furnace or boiler                  flue gas, where the furnace or boiler is heated                  by combustion of fossil fuel or carbonaceous                  wastes, or from air or seawater. The hydrogen and                  oxygen for the process are generated using an                  electrolysis unit to which a source of water is                  fed. The carbon dioxide removal unit comprises a                  physical wash system or reactive wash system. The                  condensate, optionally after treatment to remove                  contaminants, is fed to the electrolysis unit.                  The product gas stream comprising carbon monoxide                  is fed to a Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis                  unit. The gas mixture comprising methane and                  carbon dioxide formed by pre-reforming a                  Fischer-Tropsch tail gas and optionally                  non-condensable hydrocarbons recovered from a                  downstream Fischer-Tropsch process are fed to the                  reverse water-gas shift unit. The water                  co-produced in the Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon                  synthesis unit is treated to remove organic                  compounds and used in the process. Preferred                  Composition: The gas mixture comprising carbon                  dioxide and hydrogen has a hydrogen to carbon                  dioxide molar ratio of 2:1-10:1. The gas mixture                  comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen fed to the                  burner comprises 15-50 vol.%, preferably 25-40                  vol.%, carbon dioxide. The reverse water-gas                  shift catalyst comprises 3-20 wt.% nickel,                  expressed as nickel oxide, on a refractory metal                  oxide support.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN JP2021074672-A; JP6975757-B2
TI Carbon dioxide fixation-based neutral recycled soil production system promotes drying and neutralization of loosened granule soil by using hot compressed air and fixes carbon dioxide gas to loosened granulated soil as calcium carbonate.
AU SUZUKI T
   YAMAUCHI H
   MORIYA T
AE SUZUKI T (SUZU-Individual)
   DOMI ENVIRONMENTAL SOLUTIONS KK (DOMI-Non-standard)
   MORIYA T (MORI-Individual)
   SUZUKI T (SUZU-Individual)
   DOMI KANKYO KK (DOMI-Non-standard)
   MORIYA T (MORI-Individual)
GA 202155703P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a modified solidification treatment area where alkaline modified solidifying material is added to construction sludge and mixed to generate modified solidifying treated soil. A loosening granulation unit produces finely divided loosened granulated soil by loosening the modified solidified soil at the stage of becoming semi-solid. The drying and neutralization of the loosened granule soil are promoted by using hot compressed air and carbon dioxide gas, which are heated compressed air, and carbon dioxide gas is fixed to the loosened granulated soil as calcium carbonate. The modified solidified soil exhibits a strong alkalinity having a pH of 12 or more. The drying/neutralizing/ carbon dioxide fixation area is provided with a pit (2) for charging the loosened granulated soil and an inflow pipe (4) for supplying heat-compressed air and carbon dioxide gas from the bottom of the pit to the loosened granulated soil.
   USE - Neutral recycled soil production system based on carbon dioxide fixation technology used in construction site.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-quality neutral recycled soil is efficiently produced to reliably neutralize alkaline construction sludge. The carbon dioxide gas is prevented from flowing out to the outside by collecting and circulating the carbon dioxide gas that has reached the soil surface layer in the pit. Hence, the carbon dioxide gas is fixed to the soil more efficiently and reliably. By adjusting supply amount of carbon dioxide gas and compressed air according to a condition, thus the supply amount of carbon dioxide gas is minimized. Hence, the neutralization is achieved. The efficient and low-cost of soil production system are achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The first heat-compressed air is supplied to the loosened granulated soil, and then hot compressed air and carbon dioxide gas are supplied to the loosened granulated soil. The hot compressed air and carbon dioxide gas are supplied to the loosened granulated soil at the same time. A blower (8) recovers air containing excess carbon dioxide gas that has reached the ground surface in the pit and supplies it to the loosened granulated soil as heat compressed air again from the bottom surface of the pit. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing neutral recycled soil using carbon dioxide fixation technology.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the neutral recycled soil production system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Pit (2)
   Inflow pipe (4)
   Base layer (6)
   Blower (8)
   Ventilation layer (14)

TI Generating a gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream, comprises e.g. introducing a gaseous input stream of a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide into a first interior volume of a separation vessel, and conveying.
AU KING P E
   WILCOX J
AE ECOKAP TECHNOLOGIES LLC (ECOK-Non-standard)
   ECOKAP TECHNOLOGIES LLC (ECOK-Non-standard)
GA 2018359446
AB    NOVELTY - Generating a gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream (22), comprises: (a) introducing a gaseous input stream of a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide into a first interior volume of a separation vessel; (b) conveying, from the first interior volume of the separation vessel into a reaction vessel (400), a gaseous output stream of a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide as the gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream; and (c) removing nitrogen from the second interior volume of the separation vessel.
   USE - The method is useful for generating a gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the metals or alloys that forms the metal layer which exhibits sufficiently rapid and selective movement of nitrogen through the metal layer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generating a gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream (22), comprises: (a) introducing a gaseous input stream of a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide into a first interior volume of a separation vessel, where the input stream is having an input volume fraction of nitrogen and an input volume fraction of carbon dioxide and the separation vessel is divided into the first interior volume and a second interior volume by a separation membrane that includes a metal layer that selectively permits movement of nitrogen through the metal layer; (b) conveying, from the first interior volume of the separation vessel into a reaction vessel (400), a gaseous output stream of a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide as the gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream, where the reactant stream is having an output volume fraction of nitrogen that is less than the input volume fraction of nitrogen and an output volume fraction of carbon dioxide that is greater than the input volume fraction of carbon dioxide; and (c) removing nitrogen from the second interior volume of the separation vessel so as to maintain a gradient of nitrogen partial pressure across the separation membrane that causes net transport of nitrogen from the first interior volume through the separation membrane into the second interior volume.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of a gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream entering a reaction vessel containing a solid carbonaceous material for forming carbon monoxide using microwaves.
   Gaseous reduced-nitrogen carbon dioxide reactant stream (22)
   Reaction vessel (400)
   Chamber (410)
   Dried, solid carbonaceous material (411)
   Solid susceptor or catalytic material (412)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: heating the separation vessel so as to increase a net transport rate of nitrogen from the first interior volume through the separation membrane into the second interior volume; flowing a sweep gas through the second interior volume of the separation vessel so as to remove nitrogen from the second interior volume, where the sweep gas is non-reactive with nitrogen and the nitrogen is removed from the second interior volume by being entrained in the flowing sweep gas; flowing a sweep gas through the second interior volume of the separation vessel so as to remove nitrogen from the second interior volume, where the sweep gas includes at least one component, preferably hydrogen that is reactive with nitrogen transported through the metal layer and the nitrogen is removed from the second interior volume by reacting with the reactive component, preferably hydrogen of the sweep gas, preferably to produce ammonia; generating the hydrogen by electrolysis; generating the hydrogen by (i) a microwave-assisted reverse Boudouard reaction of a solid carbon source and carbon dioxide generating carbon monoxide, and (ii) a water-gas-shift reaction of carbon monoxide and water generating a gaseous mixture of carbon dioxide and the hydrogen as the sweep gas; dehumidifying the input stream so that an output volume fraction of water in the output stream is less than 3%, or dehumidifying the output stream so that the output volume fraction of water in the output stream is less than 3%;introducing a flow of a dehumidified gaseous source of methane into the higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel with the reactant stream; irradiating first catalytic material in the higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel with microwave energy so as to heat the first catalytic material and drive an endothermic reaction of the carbon dioxide of the reactant stream and the methane, catalyzed by the first catalytic material, that produces hydrogen and carbon monoxide, cooling a lower-temperature portion of the reaction vessel, thus establishing a temperature gradient within the reaction vessel where the irradiated, higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel exhibits a higher temperature than the cooled, lower-temperature portion of the reaction vessel, where at least a portion of heat required to maintain the temperature gradient is supplied by the microwave energy irradiating the first catalytic material in the higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel; allowing the hydrogen and the carbon monoxide produced to flow from the higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel into the lower-temperature portion of the reaction vessel, where the second catalytic material in the lower-temperature portion of the reaction vessel catalyzes exothermic reactions involving the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen to produce at least one multiple-carbon reaction product; allowing a mixture that includes the multiple-carbon reaction products to flow out of the reaction vessel from their lower-temperature portion; and separating at least a portion of the multiple-carbon reaction products from the mixture that leaves the reaction vessel; introducing a flow of a dehumidified gaseous source of methane into the reaction vessel with the reactant stream; irradiating catalytic material in the reaction vessel with microwave energy so as to heat the catalytic material and drive an endothermic reaction of the carbon dioxide of the reactant stream and the methane, catalyzed by the catalytic material, that produces hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where at least a portion of heat required to maintain a temperature within the reaction vessel is supplied by the microwave energy irradiating the catalytic material in the reaction vessel; and allowing a mixture that includes the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen to flow out of the reaction vessel; separating at least a portion of the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen from the mixture that leaves the reaction vessel; introducing at least a portion of the mixture that leaves the reaction vessel into a second reaction vessel containing a second catalytic material, where the second catalytic material in the second reaction vessel catalyzes exothermic reactions involving the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen to produce at least one multiple-carbon reaction product; irradiating dried, solid carbonaceous material (411) in the reaction vessel with microwave energy so as to heat the solid carbonaceous materials and drive an endothermic reaction of the carbon dioxide of the reactant stream and carbon that produces carbon monoxide, where at least a portion of heat required to maintain a temperature within the reaction vessel is supplied by the microwave energy irradiating the carbonaceous material in the reaction vessel; and allowing the generated carbon monoxide to flow out of the reaction vessel, where solid susceptor or catalytic material (412) is mixed with the solid carbonaceous material, and irradiating the solid susceptor or catalytic material along with the carbonaceous material; and separating at least a portion of the generated carbon monoxide from a mixture that flows out of the reaction vessel. The separation vessel is heated to greater than 300 degrees C, preferably 400-600 degrees C. The pressure in the first interior volume of the separation vessel is 5-50 bar or 50-100 bar. In the method, the input volume fraction of nitrogen is greater than 60%, preferably greater than 80%, the output volume fraction of nitrogen is less than 50%, the input volume fraction of carbon dioxide is less than 20% and the output volume fraction of carbon dioxide is greater than 30%, preferably greater than 50%. The step of removing the nitrogen comprises applying negative relative pressure to the second interior volume of the separation vessel so as to pull nitrogen out of the second interior volume. The hydrogen partial pressure in the second interior volume of the separation vessel is 50-100 bar or 5-50 bar, and the separation vessel is heated to 400-600 degrees C. The metal layer is grown on the substrate by vapor deposition or by sputtering. The reactant stream is conveyed into a higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel. Preferred Components: The gaseous input stream comprises combustion exhaust, preferably flue gas from an electrical or steam generation facility. The metal layer comprises vanadium or an alloy of vanadium and iron.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The separation membrane comprises the metal layer on a porous substrate, where the porous substrate is a ceramic substrate. The metal layer is less than 50 mu m thick, preferably less than 10 mu m thick. The metal layer comprises a foil layer attached to the substrate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Extracting microalgae oil by supercritical carbon dioxide entrainment with ethanol comprises e.g. centrifuging microalgae fermentation liquid, collecting cells, freeze-drying and grinding to obtain microalgae lyophilized powder.
AU DENG G
   LI P
   LI H
   ANDREA D
   LIN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG NORMAL (UYZN-C)
GA 201965484R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting microalgae oil by supercritical carbon dioxide entrainment with ethanol comprises e.g. centrifuging microalgae fermentation liquid, collecting microalgae cells, freeze-drying and grinding to obtain microalgae lyophilized powder, mixing microalgae lyophilized powder and glass beads, and loading into extraction vessel as a material, pressurizing carbon dioxide by plunger pump, entering the extraction kettle, setting the pressure in extraction kettle, pumping ethanol as cosolvent and carbon dioxide by plunger pump, mixing by mixer, heating by preheating coil to set temperature of extraction vessel, and entering extraction kettle, and forming a supercritical carbon dioxide entrained ethanol fluid and extracting carbon dioxide entrains the ethanol fluid to material in extraction kettle and passing obtained extract together with supercritical carbon dioxide entrained ethanol fluid through expansion valve at outlet of extraction vessel.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting microalgae oil by supercritical carbon dioxide entrainment with ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has non-toxicity, strong selectivity, green environmental protection, adjustable polarity, recyclability and is safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting microalgae oil by supercritical carbon dioxide entrainment with ethanol comprises (i) centrifuging microalgae fermentation liquid, collecting microalgae cells, freeze-drying and grinding to obtain microalgae lyophilized powder, (ii) mixing microalgae lyophilized powder and glass beads having a diameter of 2-5 mm at a mass ratio of 1:0.95-1.05, and loading into extraction vessel as a material, (iii) pressurizing carbon dioxide by plunger pump, entering the extraction kettle, setting the pressure in extraction kettle to 25-45 MPa, and the temperature at 40-60 degrees C under the action of liquid phase pump, pumping ethanol as cosolvent and carbon dioxide by plunger pump, mixing by mixer, heating by preheating coil to set temperature of extraction vessel, and entering extraction kettle, where molar fraction of ethanol is 0.04-0.09 mol/mol and forming a supercritical carbon dioxide entrained ethanol fluid and (iv) extracting carbon dioxide entrains the ethanol fluid to material in extraction kettle for 2.5-3 hours, passing obtained extract, together with supercritical carbon dioxide entrained ethanol fluid through expansion valve at outlet of extraction vessel, and entering separation vessel for gas-liquid separation under the action of peristaltic pump at bottom outlet of separation vessel, discharging algae oil containing ethanol from bottom outlet of separation vessel, collecting, and discharging the carbon dioxide gas is at top of separation vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the process of extracting microalgae oil by supercritical carbon dioxide entrainment with ethanol (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The expansion valve at outlet of plunger pump and extraction kettle controlled to control carbon dioxide flow rate to 4.9-5.1 l/minute. Preferred Method: In the step (iii) the pressure in extraction vessel is 37-39 MPa, the temperature at 50-52 degrees C, and the molar fraction of ethanol is 0.04-0.05 mol/mol. In the step (i) centrifuging the microalgae fermentation liquid at 4 plus minus 0.5 degrees C, at 8000 plus minus 800 revolutions/minute for 20 plus minus 5 minutes, freeze-drying microalgae cells to a constant weight at -53 degrees C, 0.1 mbar, and grinding to a sieve of 80 mesh to obtain microalgae lyophilized powder. In the step (iv) setting the separation vessel at a temperature of 40 plus minus 1 degrees C and a pressure of 4 plus minus 0.2 MPa for decompression separation.

PN WO2022189039-A1
TI Chilled ammonia-based carbon dioxide removal            system for removing carbon dioxide from flue gas,            comprises direct contact cooler, carbon dioxide            absorber, ammonia water wash section, regenerator, and            ammonia stripper.
AU SCHNOOR B
   BILDESHEIM J
   WEINGARTNER C
AE NUOVO PIGNONE TECNOLOGIE SRL (NUOV-C)
GA 2022B66765
AB    NOVELTY - The chilled ammonia-based carbon dioxide                removal system (1) has a direct contact cooler (3)                adapted to receive and cool a flue gas containing                gaseous carbon dioxide. An ammonia stripper (20) is                adapted to receive the ammonia-rich water stream                from the CO2 water wash section (9) and from the                aqueous ammonia wash section and to remove ammonia                from the ammonia-rich water streams and to return                ammonia to the absorber and ammonia-lean washing                solution to the CO2 water wash section and the                aqueous ammonia wash section. A direct contact                heater (13) is fluidly coupled to the aqueous                ammonia wash section and is adapted to receive the                ammonia-lean, CCh-lean flue gas from the aqueous                ammonia wash section, and heat the flue gas prior                to discharging the flue gas to atmosphere. The                regenerator, the CO2 water wash section and the                ammonia stripper are stacked in a single                column.
   USE - Chilled ammonia-based carbon dioxide removal                system for reducing carbon dioxide emissions from                flue gas or other sources of carbon dioxide in the                combustion of fuel, such as e.g. coal, oil, peat,                waste, biofuel, natural gas or the like, used for                the power generation or for the production of                materials such as cement, steel and glass, steam,                heating media and hydrogen, and the like.
   ADVANTAGE - The overall footprint of the system and the                number of components can be reduced by stacking the                direct contact cooler, ammonia water wash section                and direct contact heater in a single column. The                overall dimension and footprint of system as well                as the pieces of equipment required, the                regenerator, the CO2 water wash sections and the                ammonia stripper are stacked in single column, so                that the overall footprint and cost of system are                reduced. The ammonia-based carbon dioxide abatement                system can be used to reduce carbon dioxide                emissions from flue gas or other sources of carbon                dioxide, i.e. for removing carbon dioxide.

PN CN114515599-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide conversion material            used as e.g. adsorbent, involves dispersing Artemisia            selengensis biomass charcoal in fatty acid, adding e.g.            pyridine-4-formaldehyde, dimethyl formamide,            heat-refluxing, adding divalent metal salt,            acetonitrile and ferric bromide, and filtering.
AU SHI L
   MAO F
   WANG X
   XU X
   WANG H
   HUI W
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2022810064
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide conversion                material, involves (s1) washing Artemisia                selengensismaterials with water, removing                impurities, squeezing and removing juice, putting                residues into mixed liquid of ethanol and water,                washing, soaking for 1-10 hours, drying and                calcining in an inert gas atmosphere to obtain                A.selengensisbiomass charcoal, (s2) dispersing the                biomass charcoal in fatty acid, adding pyrrole and                pyridine-4-formaldehyde in a molar ratio of 1:1,                drying to remove fatty acid, adding                dimethylformamide for dispersion and dissolution,                adding 1-bromooctane, heating, refluxing, stirring,                adding divalent metal salt, heating at                60-100&#8451; stirring for 10-20 hours,                filtering to remove dimethylformamide, adding                acetonitrile and ferric bromide, heating and                stirring at 60-100&#8451; for 10-20 hours, and                removing acetonitrile to obtain the biomass                charcoal-loaded multi-metal ionic liquid carbon                dioxide conversion material.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide conversion                material used as adsorbent and conversion catalyst                for selectively adsorbing and diluting carbon                dioxide and cycloaddition reaction of low-pressure                carbon dioxide and epoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide conversion material can be                used as a good adsorbent and a conversion catalyst                for selectively adsorbing and diluting carbon                dioxide. The conversion material has good catalytic                activity and selectivity for cycloaddition reaction                of low-pressure carbon dioxide and epoxide, and is                easy to separate and regenerate after the reaction                is finished.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for usage of                the carbon dioxide-converting material, comprises                1.2 g of styrene oxide and 50 mg of the carbon                dioxide-converting material are charged in one                portion into a 25 mL round-bottomed flask, a reflux                tube is inserted, the mouth of the flask is sealed                and evacuated, a low-pressure carbon dioxide gas                tube is inserted into the mouth of the flask after                the air in the flask is exhausted, the reaction is                carried out at room temperature, the mixture is                centrifuged after the completion of the reaction to                remove the solid carbon dioxide-converting                material, and the carbon dioxide-converting                material is dried and used next.
