TI Coupling system for capturing carbon dioxide in            flue gas and mineralizing carbon dioxide based on            industrial solid waste, has adsorption reaction device,            where flue gas is input into adsorption reaction device            and subjected to adsorption reaction with adsorbent to            generate first carbonate product.
AU ZHOU X
   ZHAO H
   GAO M
AE GREENORE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNO (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 202298475P
AB    NOVELTY - The coupling system for capturing carbon                dioxide in flue gas (10) and mineralizing carbon                dioxide based on industrial solid waste, has an                adsorption reaction device (20), where the flue gas                is input into the adsorption reaction device and                subjected to an adsorption reaction with an                adsorbent arranged in the adsorption reaction                device to generate a first carbonate product. The                first carbonate product is subjected to a thermal                decomposition treatment by a thermal decomposition                device (30) to generate an oxide and carbon                dioxide. In an industrial solid waste                mineralization system (40), the oxides are returned                to the adsorption reaction device for cyclic                reaction. After the cyclic reaction is carried out                for multiple times, the inactivated and scrapped                oxides are conveyed to the industrial solid waste                mineralization system to participate in the                reaction. The carbon dioxide is conveyed to the                industrial solid waste mineralization system to                participate in reaction.
   USE - Coupling system for capturing carbon dioxide                in flue gas and mineralizing carbon dioxide based                on industrial solid waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can couple the carbon dioxide                capture in flue gas and the industrial solid waste                mineralization system, and the coupling of the two                processes not only realizes the capture and                utilization of carbon dioxide, but also realizes                the reuse of waste, and can also produce                high-quality chemical products. At the same time,                the flue gas carbon dioxide capture production line                is also easy to organically combine with the                production line of the industrial solid waste                mineralization system, which reduces the process                cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the                coupling system for capturing carbon dioxide in                flue gas and mineralizing carbon dioxide based on                industrial solid waste. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

The adsorbent comprises an oxide                  produced by thermal decomposition of the first                  carbonate product, an oxide produced by thermal                  decomposition of the second carbonate product, a                  natural calcium-based adsorbent, a natural                  magnesium-based adsorbent, or a basic industrial                  solid waste. The first and second carbonate                  product comprises calcium carbonate, magnesium                  carbonate or calcium magnesium carbonate.

PN US2019376940-A1; WO2019241126-A1; EP3803342-A1; EP3803342-A4
TI Package for carbon dioxide sensor for safety monitoring in e.g. rail station, has application specific integrated circuit arranged within housing, and UV light source arranged within housing and configured to generate UV light.
AU DEBNATH R
   THOMSON B
   MOTAYED A
   DIAGNE I
AE N5 SENSORS INC (NFIV-Non-standard)
   N5 SENSORS INC (NFIV-Non-standard)
   N5 SENSORS INC (NFIV-Non-standard)
GA 2019A38720
AB    NOVELTY - The package (4400) has a housing (4402) including an opening. A filter membrane (4406) is mounted in the opening of the housing. A sensor is arranged within a cavity in the housing. The sensor is configured with particles functionalizing an outer surface to adsorb target analyte in presence of UV light. An application specific integrated circuit (ASIC) is arranged within the housing and configured to process data from the sensor and output processed data associated with concentration of carbon dioxide. A UV light source (4405) is arranged within the housing and configured to generate the UV light, where the particles include zinc oxide, indium oxide, tungsten oxide or tin oxide, palladium, cuprum, platinum, silver and gold particles.
   USE - Package for a carbon dioxide sensor (claimed) for safety monitoring in public place e.g. subway/rail station, airport and public building.
   ADVANTAGE - The package improves ability to discriminate target carbon dioxide gas from other interfering gases that minimizes cross-sensitivity of the carbon dioxide sensor and avoids problem of overfitting which degrades performance of a GANN algorithm when predicting unknown carbon dioxide concentrations. The package can block sufficient amount of carbon dioxide from entering a detection zone to skew concentrations of carbon dioxide detected by sensor relative to actual concentrations of carbon dioxide found outside the sensor package.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for sensing carbon dioxide gas concentration
   (2) a carbon dioxide sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a sensor package including a carbon dioxide sensor, an application specific integrated circuit (ASIC) and a light source.
   Housing (4402)
   Bottom portion (4404)
   UV light source (4405)
   Filter membrane (4406)
   Gas sensor block (4407)

The working medium channel is selected as a supercritical carbon dioxide conveying channel. A burner comprises a control valve (1), a gas inlet channel (2), an air inlet channel (3), an air valve (4), a mixed gas channel (5), a pressure switch (6) and an ignition gas valve (7). The blower device is provided at an outlet end of an air inducing device so that the carbon dioxide pipeline blows gas to a turbine to generate electricity.

   ADVANTAGE - The device gasifies supercritical carbon dioxide into pure carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide pipeline, allows the blower device provided at an outlet of the air inducing device to make the pipeline blows the gas to the turbine to generate electricity, thus recycling industrial waste carbon dioxide in efficient manner.

PN KR2021020368-A; KR2304316-B1
TI Carbon dioxide reduction apparatus comprises first chamber including gas inlet and current collector, second chamber including reduction electrode and oxidation electrode, and third chamber including solar panel.
AU OH J H
   IL K B
   KIM J H
   LEE D I
   SUNG H
   KWAK K
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 202119880Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction apparatus comprises 1st chamber (100) including gas inlet (110) and current collector (120); 2nd chamber (200) including reduction electrode (212) and oxidation electrode (222); and 3rd chamber (300) including a solar panel (310).
   USE - A carbon dioxide reduction apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - Since carbon dioxide participates in the reduction reaction in a gaseous state, the carbon dioxide reduction reaction can proceed even at a low partial pressure. In addition, unlike the conventional carbon dioxide dissolved in the electrolyte participating in the reduction reaction, by participating in the reduction reaction in a gaseous state, carbon dioxide can be efficiently reduced regardless of the solubility of carbon dioxide in the electrolyte. Furthermore, since the solar panel is easily mounted in a pocket type and is directly connected to the reduction electrode and the oxidizing electrode, electric energy can be directly supplied without additional equipment, so it is suitable for manufacturing cost reduction and size reduction. Since the reduction electrode of the microporous structure provided in the device contains a catalyst having high efficiency and high lifespan characteristics, it can have a high carbon monoxide conversion efficiency compared to the conventional power-to-gas (P2G) carbon dioxide conversion device, and from this, a carbon dioxide reduction effect can be expected.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon dioxide reduction method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of the carbon dioxide reduction apparatus, a cross-sectional view of the 2nd chamber, and a cross-sectional view of the 3rd chamber.
   1st chamber (100)
   Gas inlet (110)
   Current collector (120)
   2nd chamber (200)
   Reduction electrode (212)
   Oxidation electrode (222)
   3rd chamber (300)
   Solar panel (310)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Reduction electrode comprises a fiber layer having a microporous structure and a catalyst formed on the carbon fiber layer. Carbon fiber layer is selected from carbon paper and/or titanium fiber. Preferred Processes: Catalyst is formed on carbon fiber layer by chemical vapor deposition method, thermal vapor deposition method, electron beam vapor deposition method, sputtering vapor deposition method, electroplating method, spray method, or spin coater method. Catalyst is coated on carbon fiber layer after being mixed and dispersed with a binder in a solvent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: Current collector is selected from stainless steel and/or carbon steel. Catalyst is selected from silver (Ag), gold (Au), zinc (Zn), tin (Sn), copper (Cu), palladium (Pd), nickel (Ni), and/or their alloy. Oxidation electrode is selected from nickel (Ni), iron (Fe), iridium oxide (IrO2), and/or their alloys.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: Reduction apparatus is made of polymer material selected from acrylic, polyether ether ketone (PEEK), and/or stainless steel.

TI Use of polymer separation membranes for selectively separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen from product gas mixture containing methane in membrane separation process during purification of methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU MAKARUK A
AE AXIOM ANGEWANDTE PROZESSTECHNIK GMBH (AXIO-Non-standard)
   AXIOM ANGEWANDTE PROZESSTECHNIK GMBH (AXIO-Non-standard)
GA 202255640L
AB    NOVELTY - Use of polymer separation membranes for selectively separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen from methane in membrane separation process during purification of methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen comprehensive, is claimed. The pre-dried, product gas mixture (102) of a methanation process contains methane. The separation is performed at an operating temperature (TB) of (-20 degrees C)-(100 degrees C). The polymer membranes are able to separate carbon dioxide and hydrogen from methane, and have a higher selectivity for the separation of carbon dioxide than that of hydrogen from methane, i.e. a ratio a1/a2 is less than 1, and a glass transition temperature (Tg) below the service temperature (TB).
   USE - Use of polymer separation membranes for selectively separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen from methane in membrane separation process for the purification of methane (claimed) used as synthetic fuel for natural gas vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer separation membrane is inexpensive, and has excellent separation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of methane, which involves forming a product gas comprising methane, water, hydrogen and carbon dioxide by reducing carbon dioxide with hydrogen, drying in which water is removed from the product gas, separating the gas mixture comprising methane, hydrogen and carbon dioxide using separation membranes which are capable of selectively separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide from methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a methane production plant.
   Reactor (01)
   Gas membrane separator (3)
   Compressor (05)
   Pretreated product gas (102)
   Low-pressure permeate stream (103)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: During membrane separation, the content of carbon dioxide in the purified methane is reduced to below 2 vol.%, preferably below 0.5 vol.%, and the content of hydrogen in the purified methane is reduced to below 10 vol.%, preferably below 2 vol.%. Preferred Components: The separating membranes are made of polyethers, polyurethane-urea elastomers, polysiloxanes or thermoplastic polyether-block-polyamide (PEBA) copolymers, preferably PEBA copolymers. The separating membranes are capable of separating residues of water and methane at the same time. Preferred Conditions: The separation is carried out at an operating temperature (TB) of 10-25 degrees C.

AU CHU X
   DUAN Z
   HU W
   CHEN H
AE QINGDAO HAIER BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAITE BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2021D7683Q
AB    NOVELTY - Distributing carbon dioxide culture box power comprises determining a power index according to the current temperature and the target temperature of the carbon dioxide culture box body, under the condition that the current temperature is less than or equal to the target temperature, according to the power index, determining the heating strategy of each inner surface of the carbon dioxide culture box, according to the heating strategy of each inner surface, adjusting the power and/or starting and stopping time of heating wire inner surface, where the multiple inner surfaces of carbon dioxide culture box is provided with heating wires.
   USE - The method is useful for distributing carbon dioxide culture box power.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables adjusting power and/or start-stop time of the heating wire on each inner surface according to this strategy to realize the separate control of the heating wire on the inner surface of the carbon dioxide culture box; prevents condensation in the carbon dioxide culture box, there is no need to calculate the heating wire through multiple sets of control algorithms, which reduces the redundancy of the algorithm, improves the control efficiency, and saves system resources.

   (1) device for power distribution of carbon dioxide culture box; and

PT P
PN CN113266761-A
TI Liquid carbon dioxide filling method, involves obtaining the car-loaded weight of tank car, and adjusting on-off state of filling pipeline according to re-compensation difference value, so that vehicle weight is matched with preset weight.
AU LI D
AE HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202199132B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining the car-loaded weight of a tank car, where the car-loaded weight comprises the weight of liquid carbon dioxide in the tank car. The weight difference-seeking operation is performed between the car-loaded weight and a preset weight to obtain a weight compensation value. The on-off state of a filling pipeline is adjusted according to the weight compensation value. The car-loaded weight is matched with the preset weight. The car-loaded weight is provided with the weight of liquid carbon dioxide in the tank car. The weight of the liquid carbon dioxide in the tank car is determined.
   USE - Method for filling liquid carbon dioxide of a tank car.
   ADVANTAGE - The current weight of the tank car is determined after comparing the car-loaded weight with the preset weight, which facilitates the knowing of the weight of the liquid carbon dioxide in the tank through the weight compensation value, thus facilitating the filling of the tank with liquid carbon dioxide, ensuring the weight of the tank car meets the standard after filling, and reducing the probability of overfilling the liquid carbon dioxide in the tank car, thus improving the filling precision of the liquid carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a liquid carbon dioxide filling system comprises a ball tank that is used for storing liquid carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI System used for partially removing carbon dioxide            from emission streams, comprises anode within anodic            chamber, cathode within cathodic chamber, solution of            complexation agent, metal ions and supporting            electrolyte, and membrane separator.

   WANG M
   VOSKIAN S
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)

GA 201930206P
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-removing system comprises an                anode at least partially contained within an anodic                chamber (104), a cathode (106) at least partially                contained within a cathodic chamber (108), a                solution comprising a complexation agent, metal                ions and a supporting electrolyte, a membrane                separator (110) at least partially arranged between                the anodic chamber and the cathodic chamber, at                least one gas input associated with the cathode                and/or the cathodic chamber, and at least one gas                outlet for removing uncaptured gas and carbon                dioxide from the system. The anodic chamber and the                cathodic chamber are in fluid connection with each                other. The cathode comprises at least one cathodic                channel (112) capable of allowing the flow of                carbon dioxide,. The complexation agent is capable                of associating and/or disassociating with carbon                dioxide and/or other metal ions.
   USE - System used for at least partially removing                carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide-containing                emission streams (claimed) e.g. exhaust gas                discharged from natural gas fired power plants,                cement production, and aluminum production.
   ADVANTAGE - The addition of electrolyte can increase the                energy efficiency of removal of carbon dioxide in                the system. The complexation agent can be easily                removed by application of electrical                potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                at least partially removing carbon dioxide from a                feed gas comprising carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide-removing system.

   Cathode (106)
   Cathodic chamber (108)
   Membrane separator (110)
   Cathodic channels (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal ions comprise                  copper. Preferred Property: The carbon dioxide                  has a partial pressure of 0.02-0.15 bar at the                  cathode, and 1-20 bars at the anode.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The complexation agent                  is an amine. The amine is ethylenediamine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cathodic channel                  comprises hollow fiber membranes which are                  permeable. The hollow fiber membranes are chosen                  from cellulose acetate, polysulfone and                  polyvinylidene fluoride.

AU STEVIC L
   IZETT E
   MISHRA A D
   CORP K L
   BEKKEDAHL T A
   HAINES C S
   HUO Z
   MA S
   CAVE E R
   KUHL K P
   FLANDERS N H
AE OPUS 12 INC (OPUS-Non-standard)
GA 202268431C
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer comprising a membrane electrode assembly, which comprises ion conductive polymer layers and a cathode catalyst for facilitating chemical reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide; and a liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor configured to produce a liquid hydrocarbon mixture from the carbon monoxide and hydrogen, where the system is configured to transport the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen from the carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer to the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor.
   USE - System for producing liquid hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide
   ADVANTAGE - The system controls focus on maximization of aspects relating to production of carbon monoxide (CO) and/or other carbon-containing products (CCPs), such as maximizing or adjusting ratios of CO to other reactor products, CO concentration, and/or total CO output or output rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing liquid hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide which involves:
   i. reducing chemicallycarbon dioxide to carbon monoxide using a carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer comprising a membrane electrode assembly, which comprises one or more ion conductive polymer layers and a cathode catalyst for facilitating chemical reduction of the carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide; and
   ii. transporting hydrogen and the carbon monoxide from the carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer to a liquid hydro- carbon synthesis reactor; and in the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor, producing a liquid hydrocarbon mixture from the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components:The carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer is configured to produce hydrogen and the carbon monoxide in a molar ratio of 1:1. The carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer and the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor are located in a single plant. The system further comprises a carbon dioxide capture subsystem configured to directly capture carbon dioxide from a gas, and where the system is further configured to provide captured carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide capture subsystem to the carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer. The system is further configured to provide heat generated by the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor to the carbon dioxide capture subsystem to facilitate release of carbon dioxide. The system and/or the carbon dioxide capture subsystem is configured to capture carbon dioxide from a combustion reaction on a vessel or vehicle. The system further comprises a compressor configured to increase pressure of the carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer before introduction to the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor. The carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer is configured to produce an outlet stream comprising carbon dioxide, the carbon monoxide, and the hydrogen, where the system further comprises a carbon dioxide absorption chamber configured to contact the outlet stream with an absorbent to remove at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the outlet stream prior to providing the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen to the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor. The absorbent is an ionic liquid. The carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer is configured to produce an outlet stream comprising carbon dioxide, the carbon monoxide, and the hydrogen, where the system further comprises a carbon monoxide absorption chamber configured to contact the outlet stream with an absorbent to remove at least a portion of the carbon monoxide upstream from the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor. The system further comprises a carbon dioxide separator comprising a membrane configured to separate a portion of the hydrogen from the carbon dioxide upstream from the liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor. The liquid hydrocarbon synthesis reactor is configured to perform a Fischer-Tropsch process. The system is further configured to deliver heat generated by the Fischer-Tropsch process to a carbon dioxide capture subsystem configured to directly capture carbon dioxide from a gas, and wherein the system is further configured to provide captured carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide capture subsystem to the carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer. The system is further configured to transport tail gas from the Fischer-Tropsch process to the steam-methane reforming reactor. The cracking reactor is configured to produce phenol, propylene, or p-xylene from the unsaturated hydrocarbons.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical compositions that do not support the claims.

The multiple photosynthetic enzymes is                  selected from the group consisting of                  ribulose-l,5-bisphosphate carboxylase oxygenase                  (Rubisco), adenylate cyclase, glyceraldehyde                  3-phosphate dehydrogenase (GAPDH), aldolase,                  fructose 1,6- bisphosphatase, fructose                  6-phosphatase, phosphoglucoisomerase, glucose                  6-phosphatase, and phosphoglycerate kinase (PGK),                  or combinations or where the at least one                  substrate includes ribulose 1,5-bisphosphate                  (RuBP), glyceraldehyde-3 phosphate,                  3-phosphoglycerate, 1,3-bisphosphogly cerate, or                  combinations. The                  monosaccharide is selected from the group                  consisting of glucose, fructose, and                  glyceraldehyde. The electrochemical cell includes                  at least one pair of graphite-based electrodes or                  at least one photochemical catalyst; or where the                  electrochemical cell contains a carbon nitride                  catalyst. The reaction mixture contains at least                  one ATP regenerating enzyme selected from                  polyphosphate kinase (PPK), adenylate kinase                  (ADK), and AMP -phosphotransferase, or                  combinations; or where the reaction mixture                  contains at least one ATP regenerating substrate                  selected from adenine monophosphate (AMP) and                  polyphosphate, or combinations. The method                  involves regenerating the NADPH by reacting an                  amount of NAD(P)+ with the hydrogen source. The                  method involves feeding the carbon dioxide source                  into the aqueous reaction solution at a flow rate                  of from about 80 ml/min to about 110 ml/min, or                  performing hydrolysis at a voltage of from about                  -1.5 V to about 5.5 V. The amount                  of monosaccharide formed has a concentration in                  the reaction mixture of from about 2 mg/ml to                  about 40 mg/ml or more or where the aqueous                  reaction solution includes multiple                  photosynthetic enzymes immobilized in a hydrogel.                  The hydrogel includes alginate or calcium                  alginate. At least one of multiple photosynthetic                  enzymes is expressed by a Cyanobacteria sp.. The                  power source includes solar power, sunlight,                  electrical power, or a combination. PN KR2019038209-A
TI Container for storing agricultural product e.g. vegetable or fruit, in home, has push unit for manipulating jet such that reservoir is formed for emitting carbon dioxide, and injection pipe formed for ejecting carbon dioxide to outer side.
AU BONGEUN K
AE BONGEUN K (BONG-Individual)
GA 201934428C
AB    NOVELTY - The container has a body portion (10) for forming a given accommodation space. A cover unit (20) performs opening and closing of the body portion according to attaching/detaching. A carbon dioxide injection system is installed by a partitioning plate (11) classifying the accommodation space into an either side of the body portion. A jet push unit (32) manipulates jet such that a reservoir (31) is formed for emitting carbon dioxide to an outer side. An injection pipe (33) is formed for ejecting the carbon dioxide to the outer side by a jet push unit. The carbon dioxide injection system stores the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Container for storing an agricultural product e.g. vegetable or fruit, in a home.
   ADVANTAGE - The container launches aging by increasing speed and concentration of a storing container, so that carbon dioxide in a storage part of the agricultural product is decelerated with generated ethylene gas, thus minimizing degradation of the fresh agricultural product. The container installs the carbon dioxide injection system in a storage space part such that the agricultural product is kept and marked off for emitting the carbon dioxide within the storage space part, thus improving carbon dioxide spray efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a container.
   Body portion (10)
   Partitioning plate (11)
   Cover unit (20)
   Reservoir (31)
   Jet push unit (32)
   Injection pipe (33)

TI Membrane for e.g. carbon dioxide capture from flue gas exhaust in coal-fired power generation plant, comprises coating comprising graphene oxide and amine, arranged on surface of polymeric porous hollow fiber substrate.
AU YU M
   ZHOU F
   HUYNH N T
   LI S
AE YU M (YUMM-Individual)
   ZHOU F (ZHOU-Individual)
   HUYNH N T (HUYN-Individual)
   LI S (LISS-Individual)
   YU M (YUMM-Individual)
   ZHOU F (ZHOU-Individual)
   HUYNH N T (HUYN-Individual)
   LI S (LISS-Individual)
GA 2018775577
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane comprises a polymeric porous hollow fiber substrate and a coating comprising graphene oxide and amine, arranged on the surface of the polymeric porous hollow fiber substrate (114).
   USE - Membrane for carbon dioxide capture from flue gas exhaust in coal-fired power generation plant and for carbon dioxide separation from natural gas or biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane is feasible and economically viable for carbon dioxide capture. The membrane serves as an effective carbon dioxide carrier and greatly enhances carbon dioxide solubility and transport rate, especially under wet conditions and at elevated temperatures. The presence of water in the membrane can result in higher carbon dioxide permeance than that of other gases and increases the selectivity of carbon dioxide over other gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) removing carbon dioxide from a mixture of gas; and
   (2) forming a coated polymeric hollow fiber support which involves dispersing graphene oxide and amine in a coating solution comprising a solvent and exposing a polymeric hollow fiber support having carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity of 200-2000 and carbon dioxide permeance of 100-1000 gas permeation units to the coating solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for coating a graphene oxide membrane onto a hollow fiber substrate.
   Coating system (100)
   Coating solution (102)
   Syringe pump (106)
   Tubings (110,118)
   Valves (112,122,124)
   Porous hollow fiber substrate (114)
   Vacuum pump (116)
   Module (120)

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amine comprises ethylenediamine, piperazine and/or monoethanolamine. Preferred Property: The coating solution comprises 0.001-1 wt.% graphene oxide and 0.1-10 wt.% amine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The substrate comprises polyethersulfone, polyetheretherketone and/or polyetherimide. Preferred Property: The pore size of substrate is 1-100 nm. The thickness of the coating is 1-100 nm.

PT P
PN GB2552010-A
TI Synthesizing carbon compounds comprises generating electricity from renewable source, recovering carbon dioxide from atmosphere, generating supply of hydrogen, and reacting recovered carbon dioxide with hydrogen in exothermic reaction.
AU MCNEIGHT D L
AE MCNEIGHT D L (MCNE-Individual)
GA 2018027428
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing carbon compounds (10) comprises: generating electricity from a renewable source (11); recovering carbon dioxide from the atmosphere (12); generating a supply of hydrogen (13); and reacting the recovered carbon dioxide with the hydrogen in an exothermic reaction (14). The step of recovering the carbon dioxide is carried out by a method powered by electricity and by thermal energy, and the thermal energy is recovered from the exothermic reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing carbon compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows generation of sufficient hydrogen and carbon dioxide during the hours of daylight to carry on the exothermic reaction overnight, avoiding the need to start and stop the reaction, where the overnight thermal energy from the reaction is stored for use in the carbon dioxide recovery during the day, or the overnight thermal energy is stored in a phase-change material, a high thermal capacity material e.g. concrete or in insulated tanks containing water or a water/glycol mixture (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for synthesizing carbon compounds comprising a renewable energy source electricity generator, an atmospheric carbon dioxide recovery apparatus, a hydrogen source, and an exothermic reaction apparatus adapted to synthesize carbon compounds from carbon dioxide from the recovery apparatus, in which the carbon dioxide recovery apparatus is powered by electricity from the electricity generator and by thermal energy from the exothermic reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a block diagram of an apparatus for synthesizing carbon compound including a carbon dioxide recovery apparatus.
   Carbon compounds (10)
   Renewable source (11)
   Atmosphere (12)
   Supply of hydrogen (13)
   Exothermic reaction (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The exothermic reaction is a reaction under heat and pressure in the presence of a catalyst. The reaction is the Fischer-Tropsch reaction, and generates hydrocarbons from hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The carbon dioxide is reformed to carbon monoxide by a similar reaction. The carbon dioxide recovery step comprises an adsorption/desorption cycle process in which atmospheric air is directed to a sorbent material that absorbs carbon dioxide and the material is heated to desorb the carbon dioxide. The sorbent material also adsorbs water from the atmosphere. The water desorbed from the material is used to generate hydrogen. In an adsorption stage, air is driven over the sorbent material by electrically driven pumps or fans. In a desorption stage, the sorbent material is heated under reduced pressure so that it gives up the adsorbed carbon dioxide, which is then drawn off. The adsorbed water is also drawn off at this stage. The adsorption takes place at ambient temperature. The desorption takes place: at a temperature of 80-130 degrees C at vacuum pressure of 20-400 mbar; and into steam. The thermal energy from the exothermic process is supplemented by energy derived from the renewable energy source. The hydrogen is generated: using energy from the renewable energy source; and by electrolysis of water. The water recovered from the atmosphere is used in the electrolysis. The water is extracted from the atmosphere by cooling to below the dew point. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises: at least one of iron, cobalt and ruthenium, where the product comprises alkanes; or copper, where the reaction is arranged to yield compounds comprising carbon, hydrogen and oxygen. The renewable source comprises solar energy and a nuclear reactor. The sorbent material comprises: a weakly basic ion exchange resin; and an amine functionalized cellulose. The power for moving, air generating any vacuum and drawing off carbon dioxide as well as pumping heat exchange fluid for the desorption stage comprises electrical power from the renewable source. The thermal energy heating the sorbent material comprises thermal energy recovered from the exothermic process. The hydrogen source comprises a hydrogen production facility.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The hydrogen production facility comprises an electrolysis plant powered from the electricity generator. The exothermic reaction apparatus comprises a Fischer-Tropsch plant in which hydrogen and carbon monoxide are reacted under heat and pressure in the presence of a catalyst to produce hydrocarbons. The Fischer-Tropsch plant is adapted also to reform carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. The carbon dioxide recovery apparatus comprises a sorbent material, flow means adapted to flow atmospheric air to the sorbent material, vacuum means adapted to reduce pressure around the sorbent material and heat exchange means adapted to heat the sorbent material under the reduced pressure to desorb adsorbed carbon dioxide using heat produced by the exothermic reaction apparatus. The electricity generator comprises: a solar power generator e.g. a photovoltaic array, a solar tower or a solar chimney; or a nuclear generator. The components of the apparatus are grouped together in a single location. The production capacities of the components are matched, so that the electricity generator generates enough electricity to provide enough carbon dioxide to feed the exothermic reaction apparatus and enough hydrogen to match the carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The resin has adsorbing amine groups. The sorbent material comprises a weakly basic ion exchange resin. The resin has adsorbing amine groups.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108912605-A
TI Preparing ramie fiber/epoxy composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. dispersing ramie fiber in ethanol, adding into supercritical carbon dioxide treatment device, air-drying, pressure-molding and solidifying.

   YAO D
   WU C
   SUN W
AE GONGYI FANRUIYIHUI COMPOSITE MATERIALS (GONG-Non-standard)
GA 201898399W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ramie fiber/epoxy composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (i) dispersing ramie fiber in ethanol, adding into supercritical carbon dioxide treatment device, heat treating at 60-80 degrees C and 20-30 MPa for 30-60 minutes, releasing normal pressure, opening supercritical carbon dioxide treatment device to remove ramie fiber, heat treating at 200-300 degrees C for 40-60 minutes under nitrogen atmosphere, and (ii) taking ramie fiber after the heat treatment in supercritical carbon dioxide treatment device, adding silicon carbide particles, nano silica and curing agent into epoxy resin, mixing, pouring the mixed material into supercritical carbon dioxide treatment device to soak ramie fiber, heat treating at 40-60 degrees C, 20-30 MPa for 30-60 minutes, releasing normal pressure, opening supercritical carbon dioxide treatment device to remove ramie fiber, air-drying at room temperature, pressure-molding and solidifying to obtain final product.

   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has excellent mechanical properties.

In step (ii), weight ratio between silicon carbide, nano silica, curing agent and epoxy resin is 60-100:5-10:1.5-5:0.8-1:100. Preferred Method: In step (ii), pressure of press molding is 100-150 MPa, curing temperature is 60-80 degrees C and curing time is 24-36 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing isopropanol from electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide involves providing an                electrochemical cell comprising a cathode electrode                and an anode electrode, where the cathode electrode                comprises a copper-based electrocatalyst; supplying                carbon dioxide to the cathode electrode; and                applying a potential between the anode and the                cathode. The potential is in the range of about - 2                Volts to about - 3 Volts versus a silver chloride                electrode and the potential is sufficient to                electrochemically reduce carbon dioxide and produce                isopropanol.
   USE - Method of producing isopropanol from                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide reduction reaction                electrocatalyst, which comprises a pyrolyzed                copper-based metal-organic framework (MOF), where                the electrocatalyst produces isopropanol from                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide;
   (2) an ink composition, which comprises the                electrocatalyst and a polymer;
   (3) a cathode electrode for an electrochemical                cell, which comprises a metal catalyst coated with                the ink composition; and
   (4) a method for the manufacture of a carbon                dioxide reduction reaction catalyst, which involves                pyrolyzing a copper-based metal-organic framework                (MOF).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cathode electrode                  comprises a metallic support comprising copper or                  nickel. The copper-based electrocatalyst is a                  copper foil electrocatalyst, a copper mesh                  electrocatalyst, an electrocatalyst comprising                  pyrolyzed polymeric carbon nitride (PCN)-62, or                  an electrocatalyst comprising pyrolyzed HKUST-1.                  The copper-based metal-organic framework (MOF) is                  a PCN MOF or a HKUST-1 MOF. Preferred Conditions:                  The potential is in the range of about - 2V to                  about - 2.5V versus a silver chloride electrode.                  The current delivered to the cathode electrode is                  in the range of about 100 mA to about 280 mA. The                  unreacted carbon dioxide is continually recycled                  in the electrochemical cell. The electrocatalyst                  produces isopropanol with a faradaic efficiency                  in the range of about 3% to about 30%. The                  electrocatalyst produces isopropanol with a                  faradaic efficiency in the range of about 5% to                  about 20%. The pyrolysis of the copper-based MOF                  is conducted at a temperature in the range of                  about 400-1100 degrees C and under an atmosphere                  comprising argon, nitrogen, ammonia, or hydrogen,                  or mixtures. Preferred Process: The method                  involves applying a potential by step-wise                  decrease in voltage in hourly increments of 0.1V                  and supplying carbon dioxide by using flue gas                  streams or concentrated carbon dioxide                  streams.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer comprises                  a sulfonated polymer with high proton                  conductivity.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - The gas hydrate formation accelerator is useful for forming gas hydrate for carbon dioxide separation for reducing carbon dioxide emitted from chemical power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas hydrate: can captures up to 95% of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas; and maintains the sH structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide separation comprising forming an aqueous solution by adding 3,3-dimethyl-1-butanol, which is a gas hydrate formation accelerator, to water, injecting first mixed gas containing nitrogen and carbon dioxide into the aqueous solution, forming sH gas hydrate by reacting the aqueous solution and the first gas mixture, and dissociating the sH gas hydrate to obtain a second gas mixture including nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 3,3-Dimethyl-1-butanol acts as a thermodynamic stabilizer so that carbon dioxide is captured in the empty pores of the sH gas hydrate. The adding amount of 3,3-dimethyl-1-butanol is 1-5 mol.%. The sH gas hydrate is formed at a temperature of less than or equal to 278 K and a pressure of less than or equal to 10 MPa. The ratio of carbon dioxide in the second mixed gas is higher than the ratio of carbon dioxide in the first mixed gas.

PN CN111172203-A; CN111172203-B
TI Accelerating conversion of enzyme-catalyzed carbon dioxide to formic acid, comprises performing enzyme-catalyzed conversion of carbon dioxide to formic acid in buffer solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and maintaining pressure.
AU LIU W
   WANG C
   DI T
AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
GA 2020453634
AB    NOVELTY - Accelerating conversion of enzyme-catalyzed carbon dioxide to formic acid, comprises performing enzyme-catalyzed conversion of carbon dioxide to formic acid in a buffer solution, adding 0.01-1 g/ml polydopamine/polyethyleneimine-silicon dioxide-PDA/PEI-SiO2-CA, introducing carbon dioxide gas, maintaining the pressure, reacting in constant temperature oscillator, centrifuging, removing supernatant, repeatedly washing the precipitation buffer solution, centrifuging for 2-3 times, reusing the PDA/PEI-SiO2-CA as a carrier, and fixing CA to the surface of the PDA/PEI-SiO2 by using a covalent bond through GA.
   USE - The method is useful for accelerating conversion of enzyme-catalyzed carbon dioxide to formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: accelerates enzyme-catalyzed conversion of carbon dioxide to formic acid; overcomes the shortcomings of poor reuse of PDA/PEI-SiO2; and has unprecedented acceleration synergetic effect and excellent reusability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The PDA/PEI-SiO2 is prepared by using SiO2 microspheres as a carrier, and adopting DA and PEI co-deposition method to modify the surface of SiO2, preferably taking the SiO2 powder, adding to the buffer solution, then sequentially adding DA and PEI, sonicating, stirring and reacting for a period of time, centrifuging, washing with deionized water until the supernatant is colorless and transparent, and drying in a vacuum oven. The reaction system is catalyzed by converting the enzyme to carbon dioxide and formic acid, preferably FDH and NADH. The temperature of the reaction system is 20-45 degrees C, the pressure is 0.1-10 MPa, and reaction time is 2-120 minutes. The rotational speed of the oscillator is 50-250 revolutions/minute (rpm), preferably 100-150 rpm. The reaction is carried out at 20-30 degrees C for 6-36 hours, preferably 10-24 hours. The centrifugation is carried out at 5000-14000 rpm, preferably 8000-12000 rpm for 5-20 minutes, preferably 8-15 minutes. The ultrasound time is 5-60 minutes. The stirring speed is 100-400 rpm and the reaction is carried out at 10-40 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours. The centrifugation is carried out at 5000-16000 rpm for 5-30 minutes. The drying is carried out at 40-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours. Preferred Components: The amount of SiO2 is 0.01-1 g/ml. The amount of DA is 0.1-10 mg/ml. The amount of the PEI is 0.1-10 mg/ml. The FDH amount is 0.01-10 mg/ml. The NADH concentration is 0.1-200 mM. The buffer solution is tris-hydrochloride buffer solution or phosphate buffer solution. The pH value of the buffer solution is 5-8. The concentration of the buffer solution is 0.01-0.5 M, preferably 0.01-0.5 M. The pH value of the buffer solution is 6-7. The amount of the PDA/PEI-SiO2-CA is 0.05-0.5 g/ml. The amount of CA is 0.1-10 mg/ml, preferably 0.5-5 mg/ml. The amount of GA not greater than 1 wt.%, preferably 0.01-0.5 wt.%. The amount of the PDA/PEI-SiO2 is 0.01-1 g/ml, preferably 0.05-0.5 g/ml. The size range of the SiO2 microspheres is 10 nm to 10 mu m. The molecular weight of the PEI is 100-10000 Da.

PN CN110841606-A
TI Composite material used for capturing carbon dioxide to adsorb pure carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide in flue gas, air, biogas and exhaust gas, comprises polyamine-based ionic liquid and porous support.
AU SHI Y
   ZHANG W
   HE Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202019505J
AB    NOVELTY - Composite material comprises a polyamine-based ionic liquid and a porous support, where a mass ratio of the polyamine-based ionic liquid and the porous support is (0.1-8):1.
   USE - The composite material is used for capturing carbon dioxide, which is used to adsorb pure carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide in flue gas, air, biogas and exhaust gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has a large amount of carbon dioxide adsorption, an adsorption rate block, and a strong tolerance to impurity gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the carbon dioxide-capturing composite material, comprising (a) fully mixing the aqueous solution of organic amine with the porous support under the action of stirring or ultrasonic treatment, and (d) dripping aqueous nitric acid solution in an ice bath under the protection of an inert gas, mixing the reaction at room temperature, drying and grinding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous support is any one of mobil composition (MCM)-41, Pore(PE)-expanded-MCM-41, MCM-48, santa barbara amorphous (SBA)-15 (preferred), and SBA-16. Preferred Properties: The pore size of the porous support is 0.3-10 nm. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature in step (a) is 50-80 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-8 hours, and the reaction time in step (b) is 6-24 hours. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide adsorption pressure is 0.01-0.1 MPa, and the adsorption temperature is 20-75 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cation of the polyamine-based ionic liquid is an organic amine, and the anion is nitrate. The organic amine includes any one of triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine (all preferred), ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, and polyethyleneimine. TI Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide involves reacting feed gas comprising carbon dioxide with hydrogen in presence of catalyst comprising carrier material containing cerium oxide, and active composition comprising iridium and/or rhodium.
AU SCHUNK S A
   LIZANDARA C
   WASSERSCHAFF G
   MUELLER R
   MILANOV A
   KAUFMAN RECHULSKI M D
   KAUFMAN R M D
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 201896870H
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide involves reacting a feed gas comprising carbon dioxide and having a temperature of 600-1300 degrees C with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst comprising a carrier material comprising cerium oxide, lanthanum oxide, zirconium oxide, aluminum oxide, titanium oxide, calcium oxide, silicon oxide, magnesium oxide, silicon carbide, magnesium aluminum oxide spinel, strontium aluminate, lanthanum hexaaluminate, barium hexaaluminate and/or strontium hexaaluminate, and an active composition comprising 0.005-2 wt.% iridium and/or 0.005-1 wt.% rhodium.
   USE - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, energy-efficient and environmentally-friendly hydrogenation of carbon dioxide with reduced carbon dioxide emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst preferably comprises 0.005-0.75 wt.% rhodium and 0.005-1.5 wt.% iridium. The carrier material preferably comprises zirconium oxide, titanium oxide and/or aluminate. The active composition is finely dispersed on the support. The carrier material further comprises doping element(s) chosen from silica, yttrium, lanthanum and cerium. Preferred Property: The active composition is in the form of particles having a particle size of 1-100 nm, preferably 1-10 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The feed gas comprises 10-60 mol% carbon dioxide, 10-90 mol% hydrogen, and 0-20 mol% methane. The ratio of hydrogen with respect to carbon monoxide is 0.1-10, preferably 1.5-3. Preferred Property: The feed gas has a pressure of 1-100 bar, preferably 10-30 bar, temperature of 850-1200 degrees C, and a gas hourly space velocity of 1000-1000000 hr-1, preferably 10000-300000 hr-1.

TI Optically pumped carbon dioxide laser for industrial application has carbon dioxide laser provided in atmospherically transmissive window of gas cell, within predetermined spectral region.
AU CAMPBELL R N
AE CAMPBELL R N (CAMP-Individual)
GA 201805235Q
AB    NOVELTY - The optically pumped carbon dioxide laser has a laser diode excited solid-state laser tuned to predetermined combination band transition of the carbon dioxide isotopologue, to be optically pumped for lasing purposes. A gas cell has a desired carbon dioxide gas mix and pressure internal to a resonant cavity. A carbon dioxide laser is provided in atmospherically transmissive window of gas cell, within spectral region of - 9 to - 11.5 micrometers.
   USE - Optically pumped carbon dioxide laser for industrial application.
   ADVANTAGE - Appropriate optical pumping admits catalyzer free near indefinite gas lifetime by the absence of significant discharge driven dissociation and contamination. Improves scalability, pulse energy and power over alternative non-linear conversion techniques in access to the spectral region.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating pulsed manifestation.

AB    NOVELTY - Mineral microbial cementation filling material comprises carbon dioxide, microorganisms, calcium sources and aggregates. The microorganism is carbonic anhydrase bacteria that produce carbonic anhydrase, which can convert carbon dioxide into carbonate, combine with calcium source to form calcium carbonate gel. The calcium carbonate gel is used for cementing aggregates to form cementation filling material.
   USE - The filling material is useful for mineralizing and sealing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The filling material: achieves mineralizing and sealing carbon dioxide; achieves greenhouse effect; reduces carbon discharge while carbon dioxide is sealed; meets the technical requirement of mine filling and mining; is an environmentally friendly; and has low energy consumption and low emission.

The calcium source is aqueous solution of calcium chloride, calcium nitrate or calcium acetate. The aggregate is composed of fine aggregate and/or coarse aggregate. The fine aggregate is fly ash. The coarse aggregate is gangue, tail sand and/or aeolian sand.

PT P
PN CN109652120-A
TI Producing low carbon olefins from heavy oil comprises e.g. contacting and separating heavy oil from supercritical carbon dioxide, separating the light fluid phase containing light oil and subjecting the light oil to steam pyrolysis.
AU LIU T
   WANG G
   DU Z
   LI W
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG L
   NAN X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 201939360V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing low carbon olefins from heavy oil comprises (i) contacting and separating heavy oil from supercritical carbon dioxide, to obtain light fluid phase containing light oil and carbon dioxide and a heavy fluid phase containing heavy oil; (ii) separating the light fluid phase containing light oil and carbon dioxide into components, to obtain light oil as well as carbon dioxide; and (iii) subjecting the light oil to steam pyrolysis.
   USE - The method is useful for producing low carbon olefins from heavy oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the greenhouse effect caused by carbon dioxide emissions into the atmosphere and carbon dioxide can be recycled and can achieves effect of synergistic emission reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii),the pressure of the steam cracking of the reduced pressure steam is an absolute pressure of 0.5-101 kPa, preferably 1-10 kPa. The pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide is 7-50 MPa, preferably 7-20MPa and the temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 30-70 degrees C. The method further comprises pressurizing the heavy oil at 7-50 MPa, preferably 7-20MPa and heating heavy oil at 30-70 degrees C. In the step (iii), the conditions for the reduced pressure steam cracking comprises the weight ratio of water vapor and light oil is (0.1-0.9): 1,preferably (0.4-0.8): 1, the temperature of the cracking furnace is 500-750 degrees C, preferably540-700 degrees C , the outlet temperature of the furnace section of the cracking furnace is 780-950 degrees degrees C, 800-900 degrees C and the residence time is 1-500, preferably 10-400 milliseconds. Preferred Components: The heavy oil is crude oil, coal oil, the primary processing is atmospheric distillation and/or vacuum distillation, the secondary process is catalytic cracking and/or hydrocracking. In the step (i),the mixing ratio of the heavy oil and supercritical carbon dioxide is 1: (0.5-20) , preferably 1: (2-8). In the step (ii), the separating the components is a temperature rising method and/or a pressure reducing method. The supercritical carbon dioxide further comprises modifier, the modifier is a lower alcohol and the weight ratio of the supercritical carbon dioxide and modifier is 1: (0.001-0.15).
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PN CN108993098-A; CN108993098-B
TI Eutectic solvent system useful in preparation of absorber for absorbing carbon dioxide, comprises quaternary ammonium salt and/or quaternary phosphonium salt, and ethylene glycol.
AU YANG D
   CUI G
   LV M
   QI H

AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES BEIJING (UYGJ-C)
GA 2018A36782
AB    NOVELTY - An eutectic solvent system comprises a quaternary ammonium salt and/or a quaternary phosphonium salt, and ethylene glycol.
   USE - Eutectic solvent system useful in preparation of absorber for absorbing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The eutectic solvent system has high absorption capacity with respect to carbon dioxide, and excellent recycling effect, and enables efficient and reversible absorption of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of eutectic solvent system, which involves mixing quaternary ammonium salt and/or quaternary phosphonium salt with ethylene glycol, heating, and cooling to room temperature; and
   (2) absorption of carbon dioxide, which involves contacting the eutectic solvent system with a carbon dioxide-containing gas to absorb carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the quaternary ammonium salt or quaternary phosphonium salt to ethylene glycol is 1:2-8. Preferred Components: The quaternary ammonium salt is tetraethylammonium imidazolium salt of formula (I) or tetraethylammonium triazole salt of formula (II). The quaternary phosphonium salt is tetraethylphosphonium imidazole salt of formula (III) and/or tetraethylphosphonium triazole salt of formula (IV). Preferred Conditions: The absorption is carried out at 0.01-0.1 MPa and 20-60 degrees C. The solvent system absorbed with carbon dioxide is heated, and carbon dioxide gas is desorbed to recover the solvent system.

PN US10131601-B1
TI Forming methanol by irradiating a mixture containing water, carbon dioxide, and photocatalyst with UV light to reduce the carbon dioxide to form methanol in a reaction cell pressurized with carbon dioxide and with UV light.
AU GONDAL M A
   DASTAGEER M A
   BAIG U
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2018906987
AB    NOVELTY - A methanol is formed by irradiating a mixture containing water, carbon dioxide, and photocatalyst with UV light to reduce the carbon dioxide to form methanol where irradiating is carried out in a reaction cell pressurized with carbon dioxide and with UV light from a pulsed laser beam, photocatalyst contains templated indium-oxide nanoparticles with specific pore volume of 0.08-0.15 cm3/g, templated indium-oxide nanoparticles have cubic nanocrystals having crystallite size of 50-80 nm, and cubic nanocrystals are arranged in an ordered structure.
   USE - Formation of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - Methanol is formed by irradiating mixture containing carbon dioxide without added hydrogen gas, thus, providing a maximum yield of methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Irradiating is carried out without added hydrogen gas. UV light has a wavelength of 150-300 nm. UV light is in a form of single-frequency laser beam with wavelength of 150-300 nm. Mixture is irradiated with UV light for 1-3 hours. Preferred Properties: Templated indium-oxide nanoparticles have specific surface area of 30-80 m2/g and average pore size of 1-10 nm. Templated indium-oxide nanoparticles have a band gap energy of 2.9-3.3 eV. Preferred Components: Photocatalyst further contains non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles in which weight ratio of non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles to templated indium-oxide nanoparticles is 10:1-1:10. Photocatalyst consists of templated indium-oxide nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Methanol yield is 400-600 mu mol/hour/g photocatalyst. Conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide to methanol is 30-60 mol.% and quantum efficiency of forming methanol is 1-10 mol.% relative to amount of photons absorbed.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composition comprises an amine compound of                formula (I) and solvents other than the amine                compound.
   R =1-4C alkyl containing hydroxy;and
   R -R =H or 1-4C alkyl containing                hydroxy.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separating carbon dioxide, which involves                contacting a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide                with the composition to absorb carbon dioxide in                the mixed gas.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound (I)                  is chosen from at least one of                  2-hydroxymethylpiperazine, 2-hydroxyethyl                  piperazine, 2-hydroxypropyl piperazine,                  2-hydroxybutylpiperazine,                  2,3-dihydroxymethylpiperazine,                  2,3-dihydroxyethylpiperazine, 2,3-dihydroxypropyl                  piperazine, 2,3-dihydroxybutylpiperazine,                  2,5-dihydroxymethylpiperazine,                  2,5-dihydroxyethylpiperazine, 2,5-dihydroxypropyl                  piperazine, 2,5-dihydroxybutylpiperazine,                  2,6-dihydroxymethylpiperazine,                  2,6-dihydroxyethylpiperazine,                  2,6-dihydroxypropylpiperazine,                  2,6-dihydroxybutylpiperazine,                  1-hydroxyethyl-3-hydroxymethylpiperazine,                  1-hydroxyethyl-2-hydroxymethylpiperazine, and                  1,4-dihydroxyethyl-2-hydroxymethylpiperazine. The                  composition further comprises at least one amine                  compound (A) chosen from alkanolamines,                  propylenediamines, and polyethylene polyamines                  (excluding the amine compound (I)). Preferred                  Composition: The composition comprises 10-1000                  pts. wt. solvent with respect to 100 pts. wt. of                  amine compound (I).
   Preferred Definitions:
   R -R =hydroxymethyl, hydroxyethyl,                hydroxypropyl or hydroxybutyl.
   EXAMPLE - 70 g Pure water was added to 30 g                hydroxymethylpiperazine to prepare composition for                carbon dioxide separation. The composition was                placed in 200 mL gas absorption bottle and heated                to 40&#176; C in water bath. Mixed gas of 100                mL/minute carbon dioxide gas and 400 mL/minute                nitrogen gas was blown into the carbon dioxide                separation composition for 1 hour. When the amount                of carbon dioxide gas absorbed was measured using                gas flow meter and carbon dioxide densitometer,                27.8 L carbon dioxide was absorbed in standard                state per 1 kg of carbon dioxide separation                composition. The amount of carbon dioxide gas                absorbed per unit time was 463 mL/min (standard                temperature equivalent) per 1 kg of the carbon                dioxide separation composition.

GA 2022C1596U
AB    NOVELTY - The compressed gas gun includes a removable                cylinder (31) including a portion of a firing                mechanism removably received within a channel of                the gun. A user-operable non-threaded mechanical                linkage moves from a position for retaining the                cylinder within the channel, to another position                for removal of the cylinder from the channel. The                mecanical linkage is held in place by a                spring.
   USE - Compressed gas gun e.g. carbon dioxide tank                and nitrous tank, for propelling a liquid                containing frangible projectile i.e.                paintball.
   ADVANTAGE - The compressed gas gun ensures that the gas                governor reduces gas flow from a compressed gas                source to a valve area when the bolt is in a firing                position, and increases efficiency in the marker                because only the required air is used to fire the                paintball. This particular design operates                independent of the valve pin, which increases cycle                speed and enables the governor to open and close at                the optimum time in the firing cycle.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for                concentrating and recycling carbon dioxide by                low-pressure method. The                utility model has simple structure, safe and                environment-friendly and energy-saving, the mixed                gas containing CO25 to 40 % is processed at low                pressure to obtain the product with CO2 content of                95 to 99.5 %, it can be widely used for tail gas                treatment of electric power, metallurgy, building                material and other industries.

PN US2022282651-A1; WO2022187221-A1
TI Method for capturing mobile carbon in carbon            capture field on high pressures, heavy equipment and            cycles over large temperature and pressure ranges,            involves passing second volume of exhaust gas through            second housing, such that second solid capture medium            adsorbs second portion of carbon dioxide.
AU GROSS P
   HARDING E
   REYNOLDS C
AE ECHENEIDAE INC (ECHE-Non-standard)
   ECHENEIDAE INC (ECHE-Non-standard)
GA 2022B5328C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving a first volume                of exhaust gas with a first portion of carbon                dioxide (CO2) from an internal combustion engine of                a vehicle. The solid capture medium contained in a                first housing below the threshold temperature is                maintained. The first volume of exhaust gas is                passed through the first housing, such that the                first solid capture medium adsorbs the first                portion of CO2 from the exhaust gas while                maintaining the first solid capture medium below                the threshold temperature. The second volume of                exhaust gas is passed through a second housing,                such that the second solid capture medium adsorbs                the second portion of CO2 from the exhaust                gas.
   USE - Method for capturing the mobile carbon in a                carbon capture field on high pressures, heavy                equipment and the cycles over large temperature and                pressure ranges.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon capture system comprises a carbon                capture device that is provided with a pressure                sensor, and hence enhances the efficiency of the                carbon capture process, and reduces the energy                consumption of the mobile carbon capture                device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for capturing the mobile carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                operation of a system for capturing mobile carbon                .
   100Mobile carbon capturing system

   120Regeneration modules
   130Storage module
   140Thermal control module

PN CN114823149-A
TI Preparing gold nanoparticle composite            environment-friendly quantum dot sensitized solar            electrode useful in e.g. preparing solar cells,            comprises coating titanium dioxide transparent layer on            conductive glass, and coating carbon dioxide mesoporous            layer on dried titanium dioxide transparent layer.
AU WANG R
   WANG Z
   XIA L
   TONG X
AE UNIV CHINA YANGTZE DELTA REGION ELECTRON (UEST-C)
GA 2022A78849
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gold nanoparticle composite                environment-friendly quantum dot sensitized solar                electrode comprises (i) coating a titanium dioxide                transparent layer on the conductive glass, then                drying, and then coating a carbon dioxide                mesoporous layer on the dried titanium dioxide                transparent layer, sintering to obtain a mesoporous                titanium dioxide photoelectrode, (ii) soaking the                mesoporous titanium dioxide photoelectrode in an                electrolyte comprising chloroauric acid and a                phosphate buffer solution for electrodeposition,                and degassing with nitrogen to obtain                gold-deposited titanium dioxide electrode, and                (iii) soaking the gold-deposited titanium dioxide                electrode in the organic solution of quantum dots                for 25-35 minutes to obtain the finished                product.
   USE - The electrode is useful in preparing solar                cells i.e. anode which is gold nanoparticle                composite environment-friendly quantum dot                sensitized solar electrode, and cathode which is                copper mesh and electrolyte which is polysulfide                electrolyte aqueous solution mixed with sodium                sulfide and sulfur (all claimed), and in light                emitting solar concentrator (LSC) and PEC                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite electrode: has excellent solar                cell photovoltaic performance; provides opportunity                for developing green quantum dot sensitized solar                cells with excellent photo-voltage performance; and                gives feasibility of designing future efficient                durable solar cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                gold nanoparticle composite environment-friendly                quantum dot sensitized solar electrode prepared by                the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quantum dot is                  manganese-copper indium sulfide/zinc sulfide                  core/shell quantum dot.Preferred Method: The manganese-copper                  indium sulfide/zinc sulfide core/shell quantum                  dots are prepared by manganese(II) acetate,                  copper(II) acetate, indium(III) acetate, ammonium                  oxide and octadecene, degassing at room                  temperature, then heating to 110-130&#8451;,                  adding bis-p-chlorophenyltrichloroethane, then                  adding sulfur oxaquinol solution at this                  temperature, keeping for 15-25 minutes, then,                  adding zinc acetate dissolved in mixed solution                  of octadecylene and ammonium oxide, and heating                  to 220-240&#8451; for 25-35 minutes.

PN CN114632536-A
TI Preparing nickel cobalt oxide-nickel            oxide-graphitic carbon nitride nanotube photocatalyst            for carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide, involves            stirring polymethylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide,            cobalt acetate, nickel acetate and            polyvinylpyrrolidone, and performing coaxial            electrospinning.
AU LIU F
   ZHANG H
   CHEN F
   YANG L
   CHEN Z
AE JILIN INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (JLCC-C)
GA 202284460K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel cobalt oxide/nickel                oxide/graphitic carbon nitride (NiCo2O4/NiO/g-C3N4)                nanotube photocatalyst involves stirring                polymethylpyrrolidone K-90 (PVP K-90),                N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF), cobalt acetate, nickel                acetate and polyvinylpyrrolidone K-90 (PVP K-90),                and then performing coaxial electrospinning to                obtain polyvinylpyrrolidone/nickel cobalt                oxide/nickel oxide (PVP/NiCo2O4/NiO) composite                nanofibers. The PVP/NiCo2O4/NiO composite                nanofibers are calcined in a muffle furnace to                obtain NiCo2O4/NiO nanotubes. The NiCo2O4/NiO                nanotubes are placed in a methanol solution                dissolved with melamine for precipitation, and                obtained melamine/nickel cobalt oxide/nickel oxide                (C3H6N6/NiCo2O4/NiO) nanotubes after precipitation.                The C3H6N6/NiCo2O4/NiO nanotubes are calcined in a                muffle furnace to obtain NiCo2O4/NiO/g-C3N4nanotube                photocatalysts with photocatalytic                properties.
   USE - Method for preparing NiCo2O4/NiO/g-C3N4                nanotube photocatalyst used in reducing carbon                dioxide (CO2) to produce carbon monoxide (CO) and                methane (CH4) or/and the application in degrading                antibiotics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exhibits excellent photocatalytic                performance for CO2reduction to CO and CH4under                visible light irradiation, and the yield of CO2and                CH4is as high as 796.1 &#956;mol/g within two                hours of illumination. The yield is as high as 51.8                &#956;mol/g, and can also effectively degrade                antibiotics such as oxytetracycline (OTC),                levofloxacin (LEV), tetracycline hydrochloride                (TCH), norfloxacin (NFX), and dye reactive red 2                (RR2).

PN CN114545746-A
TI Removing photoresist comprises e.g. bombarding            ionized silicon wafer with solid carbon dioxide            particles, injecting hydrogen peroxide, placing silicon            wafer with damaged outer hard shell with cleaning            solution, reacting cleaning solution with photoresist            on silicon wafer to remove the photoresist.
AU GUO G
AE JINAN JINGSHUO ELECTRONICS CO LTD (JINA-Non-standard)
GA 202275321H
AB    NOVELTY - Removing photoresist comprises (i) bombarding                ionized silicon wafer with solid carbon dioxide                particles to obtain silicon wafer with external                hard shell damage, (ii) injecting hydrogen peroxide                into reaction chamber of sulfuric acid solution,                hydrogen peroxide and sulfuric acid undergo an                exothermic reaction to form high-temperature                cleaning solution, (iii) placing silicon wafer with                damaged outer hard shell obtained in step (i) in                reaction chamber with a cleaning solution, (iv)                reacting cleaning solution obtained in step (ii)                with photoresist on the silicon wafer to remove the                photoresist, (v) rinsing with plasma water and                spraying repeatedly to the surface of the silicon                wafer after removing the photoresist, and (vi) in                the removal, the catalyst is injected into the                reaction chamber alone for the reaction                activity.
   USE - The method is useful for removing photoresist                in display panel, integrated circuit and                semiconductor discrete device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is convenient; solves problem that                the hard shell formed by photo resist is surface to                be removed, causing photo resist removing is slow;                can cause high enough damage to the hard-shell of                the photoreceptor surface so that the cleaning                liquid can enter into the lower part of the hard                shell; time of the remove-resist can be shortened                by the batch spraying system, and the time for                removing the photo-resist is greatly                shortened.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite positive electrode active                material involves performing surface-treating a                nickel-based active material utilizing carbon                dioxide to obtain a lithium carbonate layer on the                surface of the nickel-based active material, mixing                the nickel-based active material having the lithium                carbonate layer on the surface on it with a metal                precursor comprising cobalt (Co), aluminum (Al),                magnesium (Mg), and gallium (Ga) to obtain a                mixture, and performing heat-treating the                mixture.

   3. a lithium secondary battery, which                comprises: a positive electrode (83), a negative                electrode (82), and an electrolyte positioned                between the positive electrode and the negative                electrode.
