PN CN114214249-A
TI Double microbial inoculum useful in            desertification soil remediation , comprises carbonate            mineralizing bacteria and carbonic anhydrase-producing            bacteria.
AU LI J
   HAO D
   XIONG J
   WANG Q
   LI S
   GAO Y
   LIU Y
   GUO L
AE BCEG ENVIRONMENTAL REMEDIATION CO LTD (BCEG-C)
GA 202246417H
AB    NOVELTY - Double microbial inoculum, comprises carbonate                mineralizing bacteria and carbonic                anhydrase-producing bacteria, and the viable                bacteria ratio of carbonate mineralizing bacteria                and carbonic anhydrase-producing bacteria in                described double-bacteria inoculum is 1- 5:1.
   USE - The inoculum is useful in desertification soil                remediation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The inoculum improves the curing rate of the                system, curing time and independently using                carbonate mineralizing bacteria is greatly reduced,                carbon dioxide as part of the carbon source, the                discharge of carbon dioxide is reduced and the                dosage of urea is reduced, at the same time, the                method is non-toxic, harmless, no secondary                pollution, low cost, double bacteria microbial                inoculum preparation is simple, and the curing                process in the soil in the urea also makes the soil                more fertile.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preapring the microbial inoculum,                comprising respectively fermenting the carbonate                mineralization bacterium and the carbonic anhydrase                to obtain the bacterium solution, mixing the                bacterium liquid uniformly, and obtaining the final                product; and
   (2) desertification soil remediation,                comprises (1) mixing the double microbial inoculum                and the soil to be repaired, and allowing to stand                to obtain the first mixed soil, (2) adding                immobilization solution to the first mixed soil,                and mixing to obtain a second mixed soil, (3)                adding cementitious liquid to the second mixed                soil, and mixing, and obtaining the third mixed                soil, and (4) repeating step (1) to step (3) for                1-5 times to obtain the soil after                restoration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method for fermenting                  the carbonate mineralizing bacteria is as                  follows: picking a single colony of carbonate                  mineralizing bacteria to the first culture                  medium, placing at 25-35degrees Celsius and                  200-250 revolutions/minute to ferment the                  activated bacteria overnight to obtain overnight                  cultures, pipeting the overnight culture into the                  first medium of 100-200 times its volume, and                  fermenting at 25-35degrees Celsius and 200-250                  revolutions/minute for 24 hours to obtain a                  carbonate mineralization bacteria liquid. The                  method for culturing the carbonic                  anhydrase-producing bacteria is as follows:                  picking a single colony of carbonic                  anhydrase-producing bacteria to the second                  culture medium, and placing at 25-35degrees                  Celsius and 200-250 revolutions/minute to                  cultivate the activated bacteria overnight to                  obtain overnight cultures, aspirating the                  overnight culture and adding into the second                  medium of 100-200 times its volume, and                  cultivating at 25-35degrees Celsius and 200-250                  revolutions/minute for 24 hours to obtain                  carbonic anhydrase-producing bacteria liquid. The                  method comprises using the first medium to dilute                  the carbonate mineralizing bacteria liquid to                  OD600=1, using the second medium to dilute the                  carbonic anhydrase bacteria liquid to OD600=1,                  and mixing the carbonate mineralizing bacteria                  liquid and the carbonic anhydrase-producing                  bacteria liquid uniformly in a volume ratio of                  1-5:1.Preferred Components: Each liter of the                  first culture medium contains 20 g yeast extract,                  10 g ammonium sulfate, and 15.748 g Tris, and the                  adjusting pH to 9 with dilute hydrochloric acid;                  and the second culture medium comprises 10 g                  peptone, 5 g yeast powder and 10 g sodium                  chloride per liter. In step (2), the fixative                  solution is calcium chloride solution, and the                  concentration is 0.01-0.05M. In step (3), the                  cementing solution is a mixed solution of calcium                  chloride and urea, and the molar concentrations                  of the calcium chloride and urea are the same,                  and are 0.5-1.5M. In step (1), the volume ratio                  of the double bacteria inoculum and the soil to                  be repaired is 1-1.5:1, and allowing to stand for                  2-5 hours. In step (2), the volume ratio of the                  fixative solution and the soil to be repaired is                  0.1-0.3:1. In step (3), the volume ratio of the                  cementing solution and the soil to be repaired is                  5-10:1.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon (Si)/iron (Fe)/iron oxide (Fe3O4)/carbon (C) (Si/Fe/Fe3O4/C) composite material by using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid medium involves adding silicon powder, carbon material and iron source into the ball mill jar, then wet grinding agent and ball mill beads is added, sealed and the ball mill jar is vacuumized, the CO2 gas is pumped into the sealed ball milling tank, and the ball milling reaction is carried out under supercritical conditions to obtain the Si/ iron carbonate (FeCO3)/C precursor product, after the ball milling reaction is finished, the solid product in the ball milling tank is collected and dried to obtain powder, sintering the collected powder under the inert atmosphere to obtain the Si/Fe/Fe3O4/C composite material.

PN CN113522367-A
TI Manufacture of nano-silver chromate modified metal organic framework photocatalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves preparing metal organic framework material using inorganic ligand solution and organic ligand solution, and loading nanosilver chromate catalyst.
AU ZHAO D
   WANG J
AE HARBIN LINGSHENG BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY (HARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021C5044T
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of nano-silver chromate modified metal organic framework (MOF) photocatalyst, involves (i) adding iron, lanthanum and cobalt salts as inorganic ligand to 75 vol.% ethanol solution, stirring and dissolving at 50degrees Celsius to obtain an inorganic ligand solution (a), (ii) dissolving trimesic acid and 1,3,5-tris(4-carboxyphenyl)benzene in dimethylformamide to obtain organic ligand solution (b), (iii) mixing the solutions (a) and (b) evenly in equal volume ratio, transferring to a reaction kettle, stirring and reacting for 12-36 hours under solvothermal condition at 100-160degrees Celsius, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain MOF material which is Lavoisier framework material, and (iv) loading nanosilver chromate catalyst by adding the material to 0.05 mol/l potassium chromate solution, immersing and absorbing for 0.5-3 hours, dripping 0.1 mol/L silver nitrate solution, stirring and reacting for 1-6 hours at 25degrees Celsius, filtering, washing and drying.
   USE - Manufacture of nano-silver chromate modified metal organic framework photocatalyst used in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of photocatalyst which efficiently separates photogenerated electrons and holes and significantly improves the photocatalytic reduction ability. The photocatalyst has wide spectrum response range to visible light, and effectively improves the absorption rate and effective utilization rate of sunlight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron salt is one or more of iron nitrate, iron acetate, iron chloride, and iron sulfate. The lanthanum salt is one or more of lanthanum nitrate, lanthanum acetate, lanthanum chloride, and lanthanum sulfate. The cobalt salt is one or more of cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate, cobalt chloride, and cobalt sulfate. The ratio of the iron salt, lanthanum salt, cobalt salt, and ethanol solution is (1-3) mmol:(0.5-1) mmol:(1.5-3.5) mmol:10 ml. The ratio of the trimesic acid, 1,3,5-tris(4-carboxyphenyl)benzene, and dimethylformamide is 1 mmol:(1-3) mmol:10 ml. The ratio of MOF material, potassium chromate solution, and silver nitrate solution is 1 g:(6-10) ml:(6-10) ml.

PN WO2021172433-A1; TW202136196-A; JP2022503693-X; CN115151523-A
TI New metal organic framework for storage of gas,            such as hydrogen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, is            obtained by coupling carboxylate ion and polyvalent            metal ion.
AU MINOURA M
   SUGAMATA K
   IIHAMA T
AE RIKKYO EDUCATIONAL CORP (RIKK-Non-standard)
   NIPPON SODA CO (NIPS-C)
   NIPPON SODA CO (NIPS-C)
   RIKKYO GAKUIN (RIKK-Non-standard)
GA 2021A1937P
AB    NOVELTY - A metal organic framework obtained by coupling                a carboxylate ion (I) and a polyvalent metal ion,                is new.
   USE - Metal organic framework for storage of gas                (claimed), such as hydrogen, carbon dioxide and                nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal organic framework efficiently                enables storage of gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal organic framework obtained by coupling                a carboxylate ion of formula (I) and a polyvalent                metal ion, is new.
   R1,R2 = hydroxy, halo, cyano, nitro, group of                formula (i) or 1-6C alkyl, 3-8C cycloalkyl, 6-10C                aryl, 3-6-membered heterocyclyl, 1-6C alkoxy, 6-10C                aryloxy, heteroaryloxy, 1-6C alkylthio, 6-10C                arylthio, hetero arylthio, 1-6C alkylsulfinyl,                6-10C arylsulfinyl, heteroaryl sulfinyl, 1-6C                alkylsulfonyl, 6-10C arylsulfonyl, or heteroaryl                sulfonyl (all optionally substituted);
   R3,R4 = H or 1-6C alkyl, 6-10C aryl, 1-6C                alkylcarbonyl or 6-10C arylcarbonyl (all optionally                substituted);
   n,m = 0-4;
   R10,R11 = R1 excluding hydroxy, halo and group                (i), or group of formula (ii);
   R5,R6 = R3;
   R20 = R1 excluding hydroxy, halo and group                (i), or group of formula (iii);
   R7,R8 = R3;and
   q = 0-2.
   (i) Substituent of R1-R11,R20 excludes carboxy                and carboxylate ion group; and (ii) R10 and R11 are                not methyl simultaneously. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for gas storage method, which involves                contacting the gas and metal-organic frameworks,                and adsorbing or occluding the gas inside the                metal-organic framework.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The polyvalent metal                  ion is at least 1 metal ion chosen from group 2                  to group 13 metal of periodic table, preferably                  zinc, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, aluminum,                  zirconium, titanium, chromium and                  magnesium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (disclosed): A metallic compound                  is reacted with carboxylic acid in the presence                  of solvent to produce metal organic framework.                  Preferred Components: The carboxylate ion (I) is                  a carboxylate ion of formula (V). The metal                  organic framework further contains an organic                  ligand other than the carboxylate ion (I) as a                  structural component.
   EXAMPLE - 6 ml Dimethylformamide was added to 0.3 mmol                2',5'-diethyl(1,1':4',1"-terphenyl)-4,4"-dicarboxylic                acid and 0.6 mmol zinc nitrate hexahydrate, and                mixture was heated at 120 degrees C for 24 hours.                The reaction liquid was returned to room                temperature and the supernatant liquid was removed.                The solvent was removed, and 10 ml chloroform was                added. Vacuum-drying was performed, and                metal-organic framework was obtained.

PN CN113198503-A
TI Preparing non-metal-doped carbon aerogel            electrocatalyst by mixing water and non-metal            compounds, adding aldehyde, stirring, adding sodium            carbonate, stirring, adding aging liquid to wet gel            obtained, replacing in oven, subjecting to carbon            dioxide supercritical drying treatment and            heat-treating.
AU WU X
   LU J
   SHEN X
   CUI S
   DING J
   HAO W
   AN C
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   JIANGSU RUIYING NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202191992Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing non-metal-doped carbon                aerogel electrocatalyst involves (i) uniformly                mixing deionized water, phenol and non-metal                compounds, adding aldehyde and continuously                stirring to obtain a sol system, where the molar                ratio of deionized water, phenol, non-metal                compound and aldehyde is                (30-60):1:(0.1-6.0):(5-20), (ii) adding sodium                carbonate to the sol system obtained in the step                (i) and stirring to obtain a wet gel doped with                non-metallic elements, where the molar ratio of                sodium carbonate to phenol in the step (i) is                (0.04-0.1):1, (iii) adding aging liquid to the wet                gel obtained in the step (ii), and replacing in an                oven, (iv) subjecting the wet gel in the step (iii)                to carbon dioxide supercritical drying treatment to                obtain a non-metal-doped organic aerogel and (v)                heat-treating the non-metal-doped organic aerogel                obtained in the step (iv) in a tube furnace                containing an inert gas atmosphere to finally                obtain a non-metal-doped carbon aerogel                electrocatalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing non-metal-doped carbon                aerogel electrocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has excellent electro-catalysis                performance, large specific surface area, simple                technique and high efficiency, and is                economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  non-metal element comprises nitrogen, phosphorus,                  sulfur and/or boron. The nitrogen-containing                  compound comprises ethylenediamine, urea,                  melamine, hydrazine or nitrogen. The                  phosphorus-containing compound comprises phytic                  acid, phosphoric acid, diammonium hydrogen                  phosphate, sodium hypophosphite and/or                  triphenylphosphine. The sulfur-containing                  compound comprises thiophene, thiourea, sulfuric                  acid, thioacetamide and/or mercaptan. The                  boron-containing compound comprises boric acid,                  diboron trioxide, trimethyl borate, sodium                  tetraphenylborate and/or boron trichloride. In                  step (v), the inert gas comprises argon, helium                  and/or nitrogen, the heat treatment temperature                  is 300-800 degrees C, the heating rate is 1-10                  degrees C/minute, and the heat preservation time                  is 2-8 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  phenol comprises resorcinol, hydroquinone,                  catechol and/or phloroglucinol, and the aldehyde                  comprises formaldehyde, acetaldehyde,                  propionaldehyde and/or benzaldehyde. In step                  (iii), the aging liquid comprises methanol,                  n-pentanol, ethanol, ether and/or isopropanol.                  Preferred Condition: In step (i), the stirring                  time is 5-30 minutes. In step (ii), the stirring                  time is 0.5-2 hours. In step (iii), the                  temperature of the oven is 20-70 degrees C, the                  number of replacements is 3-12 times, and the                  time for each replacement is 8-24 hours.                  Preferred Method: In step (iv), the carbon                  dioxide supercritical drying method involves                  using carbon dioxide as the drying medium, where                  the reaction temperature is 40-90 degrees C, the                  pressure inside the autoclave is 8-12 MPa, the                  drying rate is 5-10 l/minute, and the drying time                  is 8-16 hours.

The                  self-rotating speed is 40 revolutions/minute, the                  public rotating speed is 2500 revolutions/minute,                  the stirring time is 180 minutes, and the                  temperature is controlled at 25-35 degrees C.                  Preferred Conditions: The high nickel positive                  electrode slurry glue is reversed after                  revolution, the vacuum degree is controlled, and                  the vacuum anti-degassing is carried out, and the                  material is discharged after sieving to obtain                  high nickel positive electrode slurry. The                  preparation method of the positive pole piece                  involves coating the high nickel positive                  electrode slurry on an aluminum foil, and baking                  to remove the N-methylpyrrolidone solvent.

AB    NOVELTY - An impregnated catalyst composition comprises                30-50 wt.% metal oxide and 50-70 wt.% support                material.
   USE - Impregnated catalyst composition used in                carbon monoxide production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst composition has high selectivity                and produces carbon monoxide with excellent purity.                The catalyst reduces usage of energy, maintains                excellent production quality and reduces the                reaction temperature by 1 fold. The catalyst has                excellent performance stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of impregnated catalyst (10)                which involves (11) providing precursor and support                material, (12) adding the precursor into water to                form a solution and adding the solution with                corresponding metal cation into the support                material to form a mixture, (13) stirring the                mixture to form the impregnated catalyst, (14)                drying and calcining the catalyst;
   (2) production of carbon monoxide which                involves loading the impregnated catalyst, heating                the catalyst with flowing nitrogen gas with preset                flow rate to reach preset temperature, reacting the                heated impregnated catalyst with flowing carbon                dioxide to produce carbon dioxide, regaining the                catalyst for reuse and collecting the carbon                monoxide at a temperature of 700-850 degrees C;                and
   (3) use of the catalyst for carbon monoxide                production. The reaction temperature is reduced by                1 fold.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of preparation                of impregnated catalyst.
   Impregnated catalyst preparation method                (10)
   Providing precursor and support material                (11)
   Adding the precursor into water to form                solution and adding the solution into support                material to form a mixture (12)
   Stirring the mixture to form impregnated                catalyst (13)
   Drying and calcining the catalyst (14)

The                  source of the metal oxide is chosen from calcined                  dolomite, calcined carbonate, calcined nitrate                  and calcined hydroxide. The support material                  chosen is from activated carbon. The precursor in                  step (11) is chosen from hydroxide or nitrate                  salt. Preferred Properties: The impregnated                  catalyst yields 33.0-65.5% carbon monoxide. The                  impregnated catalyst has high performance                  stability of 4-20 hours. The flow rate of carbon                  dioxide gas and nitrogen gas is 50-100 mL/minute.                  Preferred Conditions: In the stirring step, the                  mixture is stirred at 40-80 degrees C for 3-5                  hours. The catalyst is dried at 110-115 degrees C                  overnight and calcined at 400-850 degrees C. The                  carbon monoxide production is carried out at                  700-850 degrees C.

PN JP2021024832-A
TI New diphenylurea compound used as functional control agent of ion transporter of plant.
AU UOZUMI N
   ARISAWA M
   SUZUKI K
AE UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
GA 2021179849
AB    NOVELTY - A diphenylurea compound (I), is new.
   USE - New diphenylurea compound used as functional control agent of ion transporter of plant or plant growth regulator for growing plant (claimed) by inhibiting channels of potassium channel protein (KAT)-1 that function in guard cells and vascular systems, and inhibiting stomatal opening.
   ADVANTAGE - The diphenylurea compound has excellent effect in ion transport function of the plant, suppresses transpiration during drying and promotes carbon dioxide absorption from stomata in a favorable environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A diphenylurea compound of formula (I), is new.
   R1-R7 = H, linear or monovalent group containing branched saturated or unsaturated 1-10C alkyl, alkoxy, alkyl ester, halo, nitro, cyano, hydroxy, primary, secondary or tertiary amino, trihalomethyl, phenyl or substituted phenyl (all optionally substituted with carbonyl, ether, ester, amide, sulfo, sulfinyl, sulfonyl or imino), in which at least two hydrocarbon chains are bonded to each other at arbitrary positions;
   or R1-R7+C = cyclic structure of at least one 3-7 membered ring of saturated or unsaturated hydrocarbyl;
   R8 = R1, or tetrazolyl;
   X1,X2 = halo, or tetrazolyl;and
   X3,X4 = H, halo, trifluoromethyl, 1-4C alkyl, or alkoxy.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cultivation of plant, which involves using the compound (I), conferring drought tolerance, dwarfing of plants due to inhibition of carbon dioxide uptake, growth regulation, activation of nutrient absorption, adjustment of suppression and nutrient circulation in plants, controlling crop size, changing harvest time, enhancing freshness of plant, regulating flower bud and fruit formation, sugar content of plant and fruit and stem and root growth rate, and changing leaf wall thickness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is preferably of formula (IA).R1a = H, chloro, trifluoromethyl, 1-4C alkyl, or 1-4C alkoxy;R8a = bromo, or tetrazolyl;andX3a,X4a = H, halo, trifluoromethyl, methyl, or methoxy.
   ACTIVITY - Plant Growth Regulant. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 17 Compounds are specifically claimed e.g. N-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)-N'-(2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)phenyl)urea of formula (I-1), N-(3-methylphenyl)-N'-(2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)phenyl)urea, N-(4-methylphenyl)-N'-(2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)phenyl)urea, N-phenyl-N'-(2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)phenyl)urea, N-(3-methoxyphenyl)-N'-(2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)phenyl)urea, N-(4-methoxyphenyl)-N'-(2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)phenyl)urea.
   EXAMPLE - 110 mmol Bromine was added and dripped to 150 mL acetic acid solution of 50 mmol 2-aminobenzonitrile under an argon atmosphere. The mixture was stirred at room temperature for 5 hours. The reaction solution was poured into 200 mL ice water, and collected resulting solid by filtration and washed with water. The residue was recrystallized from ethyl acetate to obtain 66.9 g 2-amino-3,5-dibromobenzonitrile. The obtained 2.75 g 2-amino-3,5-dibromobenzonitrile, 1.58 g tetrabutylammonium fluoride trihydrate, 2.0 mL trimethylsilyl azide was stirred at 85 degrees C for 2 hours under an argon atmosphere. In the obtained reaction solution, the ethyl acetate was added, and the mixture was extracted 3 times with 1 M hydrochloric acid. The organic layer was dried over magnesium sulfate, and ethyl acetate was distilled off under reduced pressure. The residue was subjected to silica gel column chromatography in a ratio of ethyl acetate and ethanol was 10:1 to obtain 1.40 g 2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)aniline as a white solid. In the obtained solution, 90 mL 3,5-dimethylphenyl isocyanate and 100 g 2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)aniline was added to 4.0 mL tetrahydrofuran solution. The mixture was stirred under heating and reflux for 20 hours. The solvent was distilled off under reduced pressure, and the residue was subjected to silica gel column chromatography in a ratio of toluene and acetone was 15:1 to obtain white solid of N-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)-N'-(2,4-dibromo-6-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)phenyl)urea had a yield of 30%.

PT P
PN CN112275317-A
TI Preparing metal-supported molecular sieve catalyst for carbon dioxide conversion, by stirring Pluronic P123 or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, tetraethyl orthosilicate and Irgacure 907, adding ferric chloride, irradiating and calcining.
AU LI X
   WANG Y
   JIANG W
   WU M
   XU C
   JIANG J
AE HUAIYIN TECHNOLOGY INST (HUIA-C)
GA 202113360X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-supported molecular sieve catalyst, involves (i) adding Pluronic P123 (RTM: Poly(ethylene oxide)-poly(propylene oxide)-poly(ethylene oxide) triblock copolymer) or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, tetraethyl orthosilicate, photo acid generator (PAG) or photobase generator (PBG) Irgacure 907 (RTM: 2-Methyl-4'-(methylthio)-2-morpholinopropiophenone) to a brown bottle at a molar ratio of 20-40:100:20 under normal temperature and pressure, stirring until completely dissolved, adding 1-3% ferric chloride or copper chloride and ultrasonically dissolving to obtain a uniform mixed solution, (ii) irradiating the mixed solution with UV light, and ultrasonically treating until mixed solution is solidified, and washing the solid powder multiple times, and (iii) placing the solid powder in a muffle furnace, raising the temperature from room temperature to 500-600 degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute, calcining for 5-8 hours, and removing template agent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing metal-supported molecular sieve catalyst for carbon dioxide conversion.
   ADVANTAGE - The sieve catalyst has high dispersion degree, high stability and long life. The method highly disperses the metal in the pore wall of the molecular sieve during the synthesis of the molecular sieve, which is firmly combined with the active site of the molecular sieve and not easy to fall off during the reaction, and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The UV light irradiation is carried out under light intensity of 65-70%, preferably 20-60% for 15-20 minutes. The environmental humidity during the UV irradiation is maintained at 60-100%.

   WANG J

   HE J
   GONG H

   YU Q
AE JIANGNAN SHIPYARD GROUP CO LTD (CHSB-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Method involves selecting a submerged arc welding trolley with compact structure and light weight. A corner gun rod is provided with a bent angle conductive rod and a conical conductive nozzle. Welding material is matched with the submerged arc welding material. A sample processing groove is processed by flame cutting process. Welding flux is heated at 300-350 degrees C for 2 hours. Welding wire dry elongation is 20-30 mm. Front and back surfaces of multi-layer multi-pass welding are alternately welded.
   USE - The method is useful for deep-melting and submerged arc automatic fillet welding of large thick plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate, has low welding operation skill level requirement, reduces welding quality caused by welding and environmental factors is not stable, improves the construction environment, solves the carbon dioxide semi-automatic welding and carbon dioxide automatic fillet welding bead filling, stress concentration, and welding quality is not stable, avoids the welding gun with the thickness of the welding gun is not fused, slag welding defect caused by slag, reduces the welding number, reduces the welding deformation, reduces the manual repair, polishing, pyrotechnic correction time, ensures the large box ship anti-torsion box area welding quality, improves the welding construction environment, shortens the construction period, reduces the production cost, with good economic and social benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the diameter of the welding wire is 1.6-2.4mm.In the step (iii), root gap C is 0-3 mm, the root thickness is 3 to 1/3t, in order to ensure the slag-removing, the welding seam is beautiful and angle of the groove is 45-60 degrees C.In the step (iv), the welding process parameters are as follows: the current polarity is direct current reverse connection.In the step (iv), the welding bead is 1-14; the welding sequence is as follows: A, bottom: front welding channel 1; the reverse welding channel 2 is bent angle conductive rod, adjusting the wire feeding wheel locking bolt locking, avoiding the wire feeding wheel sliding phenomenon, affecting the normal welding through wire feeding wheel locking nut adjusting the angle beta between the wire feeding wheel and the corner conductive rod, the angle range is 30-40 degrees C, the bottom welding current is 310-330A; voltage 31-34V, welding speed 30-36 cm/minutes; B Filling the front welding channel is 6-8; the reverse welding channel is 3-5 uses the bent angle conductive rod. The angle beta between the wire feeding wheel and the bent angle conductive rod is adjusted by the wire feeding wheel locking nut, the angle range is 35-45 degrees C; the welding current is 300-330 A, the voltage is 30-33V, the welding speed is 33 40 cm/minutes; C, cover surface: 9-10 of front weld pass; back welding channel 12-13 by corner conductive rod, adjusting angle beta between the wire feeding wheel and the corner conductive rod through the wire feeding wheel locking nut, the angle range is 20-30 degrees , the welding current is 300-320 A, voltage is 30-33 V, the welding speed is 38 42cm/minutes; D the last channel, front welding channel 11; the reverse welding channel 14adopts corner conductive rod cover surface, adjusting angle beta between the wire feeding wheel and the bent angle conductive rod through the wire feeding wheel locking nut, the angle range is 45-55 degrees C, current 265-280 A, voltage 30-33 V, welding speed is 38-42cm/min. The welding process, the interlayer temperature T is controlled at 100-200 degrees C and cleaning welding slag of each layer. In the step (iv), the performing non-destructive detection after finishing welding for 24 hours.

TI Fruit wine comprises purified water, fructose            syrup, concentrated pear juice, concentrated apple            juice, vodka, erythritol, trehalose, vitamin C, carbon            dioxide, citric acid, sodium citrate, potassium            sorbate, additive and guarana extract.
AU WANG H
AE WANG H (WANG-Individual)
GA 202078674A
AB    NOVELTY - Fruit wine comprises 2000 parts mass purified                water, 70 parts mass fructose syrup, 50 parts mass                concentrated pear juice, 30 parts mass concentrated                apple juice, 153 parts mass vodka, 0.8 part mass                gamma -aminobutyric acid, 40 parts mass erythritol,                40 parts mass trehalose, 0.04 part mass vitamin C,                10 parts mass carbon dioxide, 1 part mass citric                acid, 0.04 part mass sodium citrate, 0.1 part mass                potassium sorbate, 0.06 part mass additive and 0.4                part mass guarana extract.
   USE - As fruit wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The fruit wine has high quality, unique                flavor, rich nutrition and low sugar and alcohol,                is a healthy and pressure-reduced fruit wine,                adopts simple preparation method and can be                manufactured quickly without pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the fruit wine involving weighing                each component, heating purified water at 100                degrees C, pouring into a blending tank, adding                fructose syrup and concentrated pear juice and                concentrated apple juice according to a ratio of                3:1 to obtain a mixed solution, performing a                constant-temperature pasteurization on the mixed                solution, adding the pasteurized mixed solution and                other raw materials to the blending tank, fully                mixing, performing plate and frame filtration and                storing to obtain a storage material liquid and                sequentially filling and sterilizing the storage                material liquid to obtain the fruit wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves                  subjecting the storage material liquid to                  zero-degree cooling through an instant                  refrigeration equipment, performing carbon                  dioxide mixing after the storage material liquid                  is reduced to zero-degree, filling, canning and                  sterilizing the obtained product to obtain the                  fruit wine, where the sterilization is                  pasteurization and 0.45 micron plate and frame                  filtration and sterilization. Preferred                  Composition: The additive comprises a combination                  of amaranth and brilliant blue in a mass ratio of                  2:1. The additive is lemon yellow.

PT P
PN CN111494256-A; CN111494256-B
TI Preparing natural liquid facial mask having            antibacterial activity, by using Sphagnum moss as main            raw material, preparing facial mask liquid from            Sphagnum moss by supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction, and preparing liquid facial mask.
AU HUANG H
   WU L
   LIU Y
AE HUANG H (HUAN-Individual)
   SHANGHAI LANWEISAIER BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202077336U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing natural liquid facial                mask, involves using Sphagnum moss as the main raw                material, preparing facial mask liquid and                preparing liquid facial mask. The step of preparing                facial mask liquid involves extracting the                effective ingredients of Sphagnum moss, preparing                the components (A) and (B) of the facial mask                liquid, and finally mixing. The step of preparing                liquid facial mask involves pretreating Sphagnum                moss, preparing film materials, and preparing the                liquid Sphagnum moss mask. The step of extracting                the effective ingredients of the Sphagnum moss                involve using a high-speed pulverizer to pulverize,                using a plate and frame filter press to squeeze to                remove most of the moisture in the Sphagnum moss,                using the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                method to extract the effective components, and                finally, using a filter press to separate the                effective components and the residue.
   USE - The method is used for preparing natural                liquid facial mask (claimed) for providing                antibacterial and wound healing activities.
   ADVANTAGE - The facial mask has excellent water absorption                and antibacterial property, promotes wound healing,                and can be used when the skin is broken. The facial                mask can be directly applied to the skin, will form                a layer of film on the surface of the skin, which                has the same effect as a patch mask, and does not                need to use a mask base cloth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The preparation step of                  component (A) involves firstly weighing                  hydroxyethyl cellulose and carbomer, mixing                  uniformly, heating up 1/3-1/2 of the required                  amount of water to 70-90 degrees C, slowly adding                  the mixture of hydroxyethyl cellulose and                  carbomer while stirring, waiting until the                  mixture is completely added to the water,                  supplementing the remaining water in the required                  amount of water, maintaining at a constant                  temperature, stirring for 20-30 minutes, where                  the stirring speed is 200-400 rpm, cooling to                  30-40 degrees C, stirring for 10-15 minutes,                  where the stirring speed is 500-700 rpm. The                  preparation step of component (B) involves taking                  10-20 pts. wt. purified water, adding the raw                  materials of the component (B) to the purified                  water at the same time and stirring to fully                  dissolve it. The step of mixing to obtain a                  facial mask liquid is specifically involves                  firstly dissolving the extracted active                  ingredients of Sphagnum moss in 10-20 pts. wt.                  purified water, increasing the temperature to                  35-45 degrees C, stirring at a speed of 200-400                  rpm for 60-90 minutes, slowly adding component                  (A) to component (B), stirring and mixing                  uniformly, where the stirring temperature is                  30-40 degrees C, the stirring speed is 500-700                  rpm, and the stirring time is 20-40 minutes,                  adding the purified aqueous solution of Sphagnum                  moss to the mixed solution of component (A) and                  component (B), mixing and stirring evenly, where                  the stirring speed is 500-700 rpm and the                  stirring time is 50-90 minutes, and finally,                  under the condition that the stirring speed is                  unchanged, adding 100 pts. wt. purified water and                  stirring for 40-50 minutes to obtain a mask                  liquid. The pretreatment step of the Sphagnum                  moss involves firstly pulverizing the Sphagnum                  moss into coarse particles with a high-speed                  grinder, using a plate and frame filter press to                  squeeze to remove most of the water, and using a                  vacuum dryer to dry immediately after squeezing,                  crushing the dried coarse Sphagnum moss by a                  low-temperature pulverizer to obtain fine                  Sphagnum moss particles. The step of preparing                  the film raw materials involves weighing                  hydroxyethyl cellulose, chitosan and Sphagnum                  moss granules and mixing evenly, adding cysteine                  and 0.1-0.2 mol/l acetic acid solution into the                  above mixture, adding aqueous ammonia to adjust                  the pH to 4-6, and placing in a constant                  temperature water bath and stirring for 6-9 hours                  until a milky white viscous paste is obtained,                  washing with aqueous ammonia and distilled water                  until the filtrate is neutral, and vacuum drying                  at 40-50 degrees C to obtain a creamy yellow                  cotton-like solid, which is the membrane raw                  material. The specific step of preparing the                  Sphagnum moss liquid facial mask involves adding                  5-10 pts. wt. film material to 40-70 pts. wt.                  liquid mask, stirring and mixing well to obtain a                  liquid mask. Preferred Conditions: The                  temperature of the constant temperature water                  bath is 35-45 degrees C. The particle size after                  crushing by the high-speed crusher is 0.1-0.5 mm,                  and the filter hole diameter of the filter plate                  extruder is 0.2 mu m or less. Preferred                  Components: The component (A) includes 1-3 pts.                  wt. hydroxyethyl cellulose and 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  carbomer. The component (B) includes 2-5 pts. wt.                  propylene glycol, 2-8 pts. wt. glycerin, 4-5 pts.                  wt. bamboo vinegar, 1-4 pts. wt. hyaluronic acid,                  3-6 pts. wt. alkyl glycosides, 2-5 pts. wt.                  capric triglyceride, 0.01-0.05 pt. wt. jasmine                  essential oil, and 0.01-0.1 pt. wt. sandalwood                  essential oil.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Antibacterial;                Vulnerary.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.    PARK Y M
   WON J E
AE UNIV KONKUK GLOCAL IND ACADEMIC COLLABOR (UKUK-C)
GA 202070585M
AB    NOVELTY - Pharmaceutical composition comprises a tumor cell lysate encapsulated carbon dioxide (CO2) generating liposome as an active ingredient.
   USE - The pharmaceutical composition is useful for preventing and treating cancer. The cancer is chosen from brain tumor, melanoma, myeloma, non-small cell lung cancer, oral cancer, liver cancer, stomach cancer, colon cancer, breast cancer, lung cancer, bone cancer, pancreatic cancer, skin cancer, head or neck cancer, cervical cancer, ovarian cancer, colon cancer, small intestine cancer, rectal cancer, fallopian tube carcinoma, anal anus cancer, endometrial carcinoma, vaginal carcinoma, vulva carcinoma, Hodgkin's disease, esophageal cancer, lymph node cancer, bladder cancer, gallbladder cancer, endocrine adenocarcinoma, thyroid cancer, parathyroid cancer, adrenal cancer, soft tissue sarcoma, urethral cancer, penile cancer, prostate cancer, chronic or acute leukemia, lymphocyte lymphoma, renal or urinary tract cancer, renal cell carcinoma, renal pelvic carcinoma, central nervous system tumor, primary CNS lymphoma, spinal cord tumor, brainstem glioma, and pituitary adenoma. The method is useful for producing tumor cell lysate encapsulated CO2 generating liposome (all claimed). The composition is also useful for providing immune function.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides synergistic effect of immune function and anti-cancer effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing tumor cell lysate encapsulated CO2 generating liposome, which involves (a) preparing a phospholipid film, (b) hydrating the phospholipid film with ammonium bicarbonate and tumor cell lysate, and (c) preparing liposome in form of unilamellar by sonication.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The tumor cell lysate is one or more cell lysate derived from cancer chosen from brain tumor, melanoma, myeloma, non-small cell lung cancer, oral cancer, liver cancer, stomach cancer, colon cancer, breast cancer, lung cancer, bone cancer, pancreatic cancer, skin cancer, head or neck cancer, cervical cancer, ovarian cancer, colon cancer, small intestine cancer, rectal cancer, fallopian tube carcinoma, anal anus cancer, endometrial carcinoma, vaginal carcinoma, vulva carcinoma, Hodgkin's disease, esophageal cancer, lymph node cancer, bladder cancer, gallbladder cancer, endocrine adenocarcinoma, thyroid cancer, parathyroid cancer, adrenal cancer, soft tissue sarcoma, urethral cancer, penile cancer, prostate cancer, chronic or acute leukemia, lymphocyte lymphoma, renal or urinary tract cancer, renal cell carcinoma, renal pelvic carcinoma, central nervous system tumor, primary CNS lymphoma, spinal cord tumor, brainstem glioma, and pituitary adenoma. The liposome is produced by a self-assembly process between phospholipid. The phospholipid is at least one lipid chosen from L- alpha -phosphatidylcholine (soy hydrogenated) (HSPC), 1,2-dipalmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphochline (DPPC), 1-myristoyl-2-stearoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine (MSPC), 1-myristoyl-2-palmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine (MPPC), 1,2-distearoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine (DSPC), 1,2-distearoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine-N-(methoxy(polyethylene glycol)-2000) (DSPE-mPEG2000), and cholesterol. The liposome has a particle size of 100-200 nm. In liposome, CO2 is generated by near infrared or external heat source to release tumor cell lysate, where tumor cell lysate is released from liposome matures dendritic cells. The liposome further comprises an anti-cancer agent chosen from doxorubicin, cisplatin, paclitaxel, vincristine, topotecan, docetaxel, 5-fluorouracil, Gleevec (RTM: Imatinib mesylate), carboplatin, daunorubicin, valrubicin, flutamide and gemcitabine. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves preparing phospholipid film by dissolving phospholipid in an organic solvent, and evaporating solution under reduced pressure to prepare phospholipid film.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Immunostimulant. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

AB    NOVELTY - Healthcare food comprises kudzu vine root,                mulberry and black sesame in weight ratio of                8-15:3-10:1-5.
   USE - The healthcare food is used as a drink for                beautifying.
   ADVANTAGE - The healthcare food completely dissolves in                water, is safe, more reliable and convenient to                take, has sweet taste and higher added value,                provides better experience, does not produce                satiety feel, and improves quality of life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process                for preparing the food, which involves (a)                extracting kudzu vine root with 40-70% ethanol                under reflux, concentrating the extract until free                of ethanol smell, passing through macroporous resin                column, eluting with water and 40-70% ethanol                successively, collecting ethanol eluate,                concentrating the ethanol eluate, drying and                crushing into fine powder, (b) taking fresh                mulberries, beating, adding 0.2-0.9 wt.% pectinase                and 0.05-0.3 wt.% Saccharomyces cerevisiae,                sealing, heating at 25-35 degrees C for 24-48                hours, removing pomace by centrifugation, drying                and pulverizing into fine powder, (c) crushing the                black sesame into powder, adding into supercritical                extraction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide for                supercritical extraction, controlling extraction                pressure to 20-40 MPa and temperature to 30-50                degrees C, and obtaining black sesame oil after                carbon dioxide is separated and (d) mixing the fine                powder obtained in step (a) and step (b) with                filler evenly, dissolving black sesame oil in                binder solution, adding to above mixture, stirring                to make a soft material, granulating the soft                material and drying the granules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The food comprises                  kudzu vine root, mulberry and black sesame in                  weight ratio of 10:6:3. Preferred Method: The                  ethanol is 50% ethanol. The step (b) involves                  adding 0.55% pectinase and 0.15% S.cerevisiae.                  The extraction is performed at 30 MPa and 40                  degrees C. Preferred Components: The filler is                  one or more chosen from sucrose, maltose,                  lactose, mannitol, dextrin, and starch. The                  binder is povidone and the binder solution is                  0.1-2% povidone ethanol solution.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method by which the                  inflammatory cytokine processes the mesenchymal                  stem cells comprises taking mesenchymal stem                  cells that have been passaged to the P4                  generation, inoculating in alpha -MEM medium                  containing 10% fetal bovine serum and                  inflammatory cytokines and culturing in a 5%                  carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for                  24-48 hours to obtain mesenchymal stem cells                  treated with inflammatory cytokines. Preferred                  Components: The inflammatory cytokine is TNF-                  alpha and the final concentration of the TNF-                  alpha in the culture solution is 20-40 ng/ml. The                  concentration of the inflammatory                  cytokine-treated mesenchymal stem cells is                  105-108 cells/ml. The mass-to-volume ratio of                  human serum albumin in the mesenchymal stem cell                  preparation is 5%, low molecular weight heparin                  is 0.5%, compound amino acid is 10%, vitamin C is                  0.5% and compound electrolyte (balance). The                  compound electrolyte comprises 5.26 wt.% sodium                  chloride, 5.02 wt.% sodium gluconate, 3.68 wt.%                  sodium acetate, 0.37 wt.% potassium chloride,                  0.30 wt.% magnesium chloride and water (balance).                  The mesenchymal stem cells are bone marrow                  mesenchymal stem cells, adipose-derived                  mesenchymal stem cells, synovial mesenchymal stem                  cells and/or umbilical cord mesenchymal stem                  cells. The concentration of mesenchymal stem                  cells in mesenchymal stem cell composition is                  105-108 cells/ml.
   ACTIVITY - Antiarthritic; Antirheumatic.

The mesenchymal stem cells are bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, synovial mesenchymal stem cells and/or umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells. PN CN110016598-A
TI High strength rapid corrosion magnesium alloy used as structural component, comprises aluminum, germanium and balanced magnesium.
AU LIU X
   HOU L
   WEI Y
   YIN M
   DU H
   LIU B
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201963757L
AB    NOVELTY - High strength rapid corrosion magnesium alloy comprises 2-8 wt.% aluminum, 0.5-7 wt.% germanium and balanced magnesium.
   USE - High strength rapid corrosion magnesium alloy used as structural component.
   ADVANTAGE - The high strength rapid corrosion magnesium alloy meets performance requirements of the fracturing ball, and can be dissolved quickly under specific conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing high strength rapid corrosion magnesium alloy, which involves:
   (A) removing oxides and oil stains on surface of magnesium ingots and aluminum ingots;
   (B) cleaning with absolute alcohol;
   (C) drying to constant temperature at 90 degrees C to give pretreated magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot respectively;
   (D) removing the oxides and oil stains on the surface of the granules;
   (E) washing them with absolute alcohol;
   (F) placing the pretreated magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot in a furnace protected by carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas to be heated at 700-760 degrees C to melt;
   (G) heating at 700-760 degrees C for 25-35 minutes under protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride to obtain melt;
   (H) cooling the melt at 650-700 degrees C for 10-20 minutes;
   (I) placing the ingot in a high temperature box at 420 degrees C for 24 hours; and
   (J) taking out of water for quenching at room temperature to obtain the high strength rapid corrosion magnesium alloy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic microstructure diagram of the prepared high strength rapid corrosion magnesium alloy.

PN CN109776260-A
TI Methanol preparation involves sequentially processing low rank coal by drying process and gasification reduction process to obtain rich gas, where rich gas is purified by purification process.
AU XUE A
   WANG J
   WANG Z
   YU T
   LV B
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201948278E
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol preparation involves sequentially processing low rank coal by a drying process and a gasification reduction process to obtain a rich gas, where the rich gas is purified by a purification process. The purified rich gas comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and hydrocarbons. The gasification reduction process is a chemical reaction process for heating low-rank coal after drying under oxygen-free or micro-oxygen conditions. The purified rich gas is sequentially subjected to a desulfurization process and a compression process to obtain a reforming conversion feed gas.
   USE - Method for preparing methanol by using volatiles in low rank coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the methanol in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with effective utilization of the volatile matter in the low rank coal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The reforming conversion feed gas is processed by a reforming conversion process to obtain a reformed gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen. The reformed gas is obtained by using decarburization and carbon supplementation process, a shift conversion process, and a hydrogen supplementation process. The methanol synthesis gas is sequentially subjected to another compression process and a methanol synthesis process to obtain crude methanol and methanol purge gas, where the crude methanol is also subjected to a methanol rectification process to obtain the product methanol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of the method for preparing methanol by using volatiles in low rank coal. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The gas is passed through decarburization and carbon supplementation process to obtain a methanol synthesis gas. The purification process comprises a dust removal process and a de-tarring process. The gasification reduction process is performed at 350-800 degrees C.

PN CN109590001-A; CN109590001-B
TI Anti-carbon catalyst useful in preparing syngas for reforming of methane and for promoting carbon dioxide carbon elimination reaction, comprises alumina, nickel, phosphorus and alkali metal.
AU ZHANG J
   FENG N
   WANG X
   WEI J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   SHAANXI SHOUXIN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019362596
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-carbon catalyst comprises alumina, nickel, phosphorus and alkali metal, where mass ratio between alumina and nickel is 5-25:1, molar ratio between nickel and phosphorus is 0.5-10:1 and molar ratio between nickel and alkali metal is 0.01-0.5:1.
   USE - The anti-carbon catalyst useful in preparing syngas for reforming of methane (claimed); and for promoting carbon dioxide carbon elimination reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-carbon catalyst: has simple and easy operation, high repeatability of the preparation process, distributed load component on the surface of alumina carrier and has excellent strength, thermal stability, carbon deposition resistance and activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing anti-carbon catalyst comprises (i) dissolving soluble nickel salt in deionized water to obtain immersion liquid, adding impregnated droplets into alumina carrier, stirring uniformly, absorbing, drying and calcining to obtain catalyst precursor, (ii) dissolving phosphoric acid or soluble phosphorus salt in deionized water to obtain immersion liquid, adding immersion droplets into catalyst precursor, stirring constantly to obtain uniformly absorbed solution, drying and calcining to obtain catalyst precursor, (iii) dissolving alkali metal salt in deionized water to obtain immersion liquid, adding impregnated droplets into catalyst precursor, stirring to obtain uniformly absorbed solution, drying and calcining to obtain catalyst precursor, (iv) placing obtained catalyst precursor in a fixed bed reactor and passing reducing gas to obtain final product
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of alumina is 100-800 mu m. In step (i), particle diameter of alumina is 100-800 mu m. In steps (i), (ii) and (iii), volume of deionized water is equal to the volume of the micropores of alumina. In step (i), soluble nickel salt is nickel nitrate, nickel acetate, nickel oxalate, nickel sulfate, nickel chloride and/or nickel sulfamate. In step (ii), soluble phosphorus salt is ammonium phosphate, diammonium hydrogen phosphate and/or ammonium biphosphate. In step (iii), alkali metal salt is sodium phosphate, disodium hydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, sodium nitrate, potassium phosphate, dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and/or potassium nitrate. Preferred Method: The steps (i), (ii) and (iii) comprises calcining at 700-1000 degrees C for 6-48 hours under air atmosphere. The steps (iv) comprises reducing at 700-1000 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, where reducing gas is hydrogen under argon gas protection and volumetric content of hydrogen in the mixed gas of hydrogen and argon is 10%.

PN CN109012731-A; CN109012731-B
TI Biliary cobalt zinc aluminum layered double hydroxides/reduced graphene oxide/graphitic carbon nitride urchin-like Z-type heterojunction structure used e.g. for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction includes reduced graphene oxide.
AU YANG Y
   WU J
   XIAO T
   TANG Z
   HUANG J
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2018A4670R
AB    NOVELTY - A biliary cobalt zinc aluminum layered double hydroxides/reduced graphene oxide/graphitic carbon nitride urchin-like Z-type heterojunction structure comprises cobalt zinc aluminum layered double hydroxide nanosheets, reduced graphene oxide and graphitic carbon nitride. The reduced graphene oxide is used as an electron conduction medium between semiconductor interfaces. The cobalt zinc aluminum layered double hydroxide nanosheets are self-assembled to form urchin-like hollow structure. The content of graphitic carbon nitride is 3-7 wt.%. The mass ratio of graphitic carbon nitride to reduced graphene oxide is 4:1-6:1.
   USE - Biliary cobalt zinc aluminum layered double hydroxides/reduced graphene oxide/graphitic carbon nitride urchin-like Z-type heterojunction structure used for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction and photocatalytic degrading organic pollutant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterojunction structure has high specific surface area, photon utilization rate, and photogenerated electron hole separation efficiency and can be applied to catalysis and energy transformation fields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the heterojunction structure comprising dispersing graphene oxide and graphitic carbon nitride in water and sonicating for 0.5-1 hour; dissolving aluminum nitrate, cobalt nitrate, and zinc nitrate in water, adding urea, stirring, and dissolving; and adding suspension, vigorous stirring at room temperature for 0.5-1 hour, hydrothermal reacting at 180 plus minus 10 degrees C for 6-8 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, centrifugally separating, washing, and vacuum drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor of graphitic carbon nitride is melamine, dicyandiamide, cyanamide, or urea. The ratio of cobalt nitrate and urea is 1:6. Preferred Method: The vacuum drying is done at 60 degrees C for 6-12 hours.

PN CN108714120-A
TI Bacteriostatic denture cleaner used e.g. for killing bacteria comprises attapulgite, dilute sulfuric acid solution, traditional Chinese medicine extract liquid, sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxymethyl starch, vitamin C, and malic acid.
AU LENG P
AE BENGBU JIAMEISHI DENTURE CO LTD (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201888153G
AB    NOVELTY - A bacteriostatic denture cleaner comprises 40-60 pts. wt. attapulgite, 100-200 pts. wt. dilute sulfuric acid solution, 100-150 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicine extract liquid, 10-15 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, 5-10 pts. wt. sodium hydroxymethyl starch, 3-5 pts. wt. vitamin C, and 5-10 pts. wt. malic acid, where attapulgite clay granularity is 200-300 meshes and concentration of the dilute sulfuric acid solution is 30-35%.
   USE - Bacteriostatic denture cleaner used for killing bacteria and preventing periodontitis, dental caries, and gingivitis.
   ADVANTAGE - The denture cleaner quickly removes residue and dental plaque and whitens teeth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the denture cleaner comprising vacuum drying attapulgite at 0.07-0.08 MPa and 30-40 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, filling carbon dioxide gas to standard atmospheric pressure, drying at 40-50 degrees C for 3-5 hours, taking out, putting into dilute sulfuric acid solution, acidifying for 2-3 hours at normal temperature, ultrasonic oscillating, vacuum drying under carbon dioxide at 40-50 degrees C, and adding into traditional Chinese medicine extract liquid for 4-6 hours; boiling, continuously stirring until water is evaporated completely, pelletizing, roasting for 2-2.5 hours at 350-400 degrees C, and cooling to room temperature; and mixing with remaining raw materials, grinding to 50-500 mu m, weighing, and packing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The traditional Chinese medicine extract solution is prepared by taking 2-5 pts. wt. radix paeoniae alba, 10-15 pts. wt. notoginseng, 5-10 pts. wt. dried old orange peel, and 10-20 pts. wt. Aloe, freeze-drying raw materials at - 10 degrees C to - 20 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, ball milling for 20-30 minutes, adding 10-20 times ethanol and 5-10 pts. wt. malic acid, stirring, high pressure extracting at 200-250 MPa and 20-30 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, centrifuging, filtering, and collecting filtrate. The centrifuging is done at 2000-2500 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5-10 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Antiinflammatory.

PN CN108672933-A
TI Welding ultra-narrow heat affected zone of ferritic stainless steel weld in continuous rolling production line by using e.g. carbon dioxide laser welding method to weld tail portion of previous strip steel with head of next strip steel.
AU SHI Y
   XU S
   WU Z
   FU J
   DUAN W
AE SHANXI TAIGANG STAINLESS STEEL CO LTD (TYGT-C)
GA 2018833041
AB    NOVELTY - Welding ultra-narrow heat affected zone of a ferritic stainless steel weld in a continuous rolling production line involves using carbon dioxide laser welding method and nickel based welding wire as a filler metal to weld the tail portion of the previous strip steel with the head of the next strip steel, where the thickness of the welded strip is 0.6-6 mm, the maximum parallelism tolerance of welded steel plate joints is less than or equal to 0.1 mm, and the unfitness of butt joint is less than or equal to 0.05 mm. The welding parameters from thin to thick according to the thickness of the welded strip are such that the laser power is 8-12 kW, the welding speed is 12-3.4 m/minute, the weld gap is 0.10-0.33 mm, the wire feeding speed is 1.13-10.58 m/minute and the annealing power is 22.1-40 kW.
   USE - The method is useful for welding ultra-narrow heat affected zone of a ferritic stainless steel weld in a continuous rolling production line.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs welding at the fastest welding speed while ensuring the quality of the weld seam, and reduces the welding time and the heat input of the entire weld seam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The ferritic stainless steel is 430 stainless steel. The material of the welded strip steel is 430 stainless steel. Preferred Properties: When the thickness of the strip is less than or equal to 1.5 mm, the weld gap is 0.10 mm. When the thickness of the strip is 1.5-2.5 mm, the weld gap is 0.15 mm. When the thickness of the strip is 2.5-3 mm, the weld gap is 0.20 mm. When the thickness of the strip is 3-3.5 mm, the weld gap is 0.25 mm. When the thickness of 3.5-5 mm, the weld gap is 0.30 mm. When the thickness of the strip is 5-6 mm, the weld gap is 0.33 mm.

AE WUHU JINYAOSHI INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201879403Y
AB    NOVELTY - The purifier has a dust absorbing device (4) whose lower end is detachably connected a base (11). The dust absorbing device is provided with a first fan (12). The first fan is located on a shell (1). An upper end of an air storage tank (5) is symmetrically provided with a first connection pipe (51). The shell is provided with a reaction tank (6). An end of the first connection pipe moves along with an inner wall of the reaction tank. A bottom part of the reaction tank is provided with a filtering device (7). The filtering device is glidingly connected to the reaction tank that is fixed with a second connection pipe. The second connection pipe is detachable connected with the reaction tank (13) and a water tank (14). A second fan (15) is located on the shell.

PN CN108187713-A
TI Preparing copper-nitrogen co-doped carbon nanotube            catalyst by carrying out reflux reaction of carbon            nanotube in acid solution, washing, drying, adding            copper salt solution and dispersant, stirring and            ultrasonically dispersing.
AU ZHANG J
   CHEN W
   WEI W
   SUN Y
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 201850866G
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing copper-nitrogen                co-doped carbon nanotube catalyst involves (a)                taking carbon nanotube as a substrate, carrying out                reflux reaction in an acid solution, washing with                water until the carbon nanotube is neutral and                drying, (b) adding a copper salt solution and a                dispersant to the carbon nanotube powder, stirring                and adding pH modifier until to be neutral to                obtain a suspension, (c) carrying out ultrasonic                dispersion on the suspension, stirring and drying,                (d) calcining the powder obtained in the step (c)                under an inert gas atmosphere and a reducing gas                atmosphere to obtain copper-doped carbon nanotube,                (e) transferring the copper-doped carbon nanotube                to a hydrothermal reaction kettle, adding a                nitrogen source and reacting, and (f) cleaning the                suspension obtained in the step (e) and drying to                obtain the catalyst.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing copper-nitrogen                co-doped carbon nanotube catalyst, which is useful                for electrocatalytic conversion of carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, simple and easy                preparation copper-nitrogen co-doped carbon                nanotube catalyst with high reducing                activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the copper-nitrogen co-doped carbon                nanotube catalyst prepared by the above method;                and
   (2) the application of the copper-nitrogen                co-doped carbon nanotube catalyst for                electrocatalytic conversion of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The carbon nanotube                  has a diameter of 10-50 nm. Preferred Components:                  The acid solution is nitric acid and hydrochloric                  acid. The copper salt solution is aqueous copper                  salt solution. The copper salt in the copper salt                  solution is copper nitrate, copper chloride,                  copper acetate or copper sulfate. The pH modifier                  is sodium hydroxide or aqueous ammonia. The inert                  gas atmosphere in the step (d) is nitrogen, argon                  or helium. The reducing gas atmosphere in the                  step (d) is hydrogen gas or carbon monoxide gas.                  The nitrogen source in the step (e) is aqueous                  ammonia, hydrazine hydrate and urea. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of the acid                  solution is 30-60 wt. The concentration of the                  copper salt solution is 0.01-0.1 mol/l. The mass                  ratio of copper to carbon nanotube in the copper                  salt is 1:20-100. The molar ratio of nitrogen in                  the nitrogen source to carbon in the carbon                  nanotube is 1:200-400. Preferred Conditions: In                  the step (a), the reflux reaction is carried out                  at 80-150 degrees C. In the step (c), the                  ultrasonic dispersion step is carried out a 5-20                  degrees C. The calcining step (d) is carried out                  under inert gas atmosphere at a inert gas flow                  rate of 10-300 ml/minute, a temperature of                  100-400 degrees C, an initial temperature of                  20-30 degrees C and a heating rate of 1-20                  degrees C/minute for 1-10 hours and under                  reducing gas flow rate of 10-300 ml/minute, a                  temperature of 100-300 degrees C, an initial                  temperature of 20-30 degrees C and a heating rate                  of 1-20 degrees C/minute for 1-10 hours. The                  hydrothermal reaction step (e) is carried out at                  100-300 degrees C for 10-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersant is                  citric acid, sodium citrate, ammonium citrate and                  polyethylene glycol. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of the dispersant is 0.01-1 mol/l.                  Preferred Conditions: The application method                  involves carrying out reaction at carbon dioxide                  flow rate of 10-100 ml/minute and a temperature                  of 10-60 degrees C.

TI Preparing manure liquid using lithium vermiculite and removing magnesium, calcium and aluminum ions in human feces comprises e.g. adding sterile sodium chloride solution to the fecal sample, adding lithium-based vermiculite, and stirring.
AU CHEN T
   CAI X
   ZHANG Z
   MA D
AE GUANGDONG LONGSEE MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201836412C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing manure liquid using lithium vermiculite and removing magnesium, calcium and aluminum ions in human feces comprises e.g. adding sterile sodium chloride solution to the fecal sample, adding lithium-based vermiculite, and stirring uniformly to obtain slurry, filtering the feces slurry initially, taking the filtrate, under the protection of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, helium, helium, or argon, stirring, and obtaining fecal bacteria slurry, filtering, centrifuging, and resuspending.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing manure liquid using lithium vermiculite and removing magnesium, calcium and aluminum ions in human feces.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing manure liquid using lithium vermiculite and removing magnesium, calcium and aluminum ions in human feces comprises (1) adding sterile sodium chloride solution to the fecal sample, adding lithium-based vermiculite, and stirring uniformly to obtain slurry, (2) filtering the feces slurry initially, taking the filtrate, under the protection of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, helium, helium, or argon, stirring at 1800-2200 revolutions/minute for 3-6 minutes at interval of every 0.8-1.2 hours, and obtaining fecal bacteria slurry after 4-7 hours, and (3) filtering fecal bacteria slurry to obtain crude fecal bacteria solution, centrifuging the crude fecal bacteria solution, taking out precipitate, resuspending to obtain colorless and odorless fecal bacteria solution free of magnesium, calcium and aluminum ions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cation exchange capacity of the lithium-based vermiculite is 120-130 mmol/100 g. Preferred Method: The lithium-based vermiculite is prepared by heating gangue mineral water and lithium metaphosphate to 70-80 degrees C, adding sodium carbonate, stirring for 2.5-3.5 hours, allowing to stand for 20-30 hours, washing with deionized water, centrifuging to recover solids, and drying to obtain lithium-based rock stone, where the ratio of the gangue mineral, lithium metaphosphate, sodium carbonate and water is 80-120 g: 8-12 g: 18-22 g: 1 l. In the step (1), the dosage ratio of fecal sample, sodium chloride solution and lithium-based vermiculite is 1 g:3-7 ml:0.5-1.5 g, where the concentration of the sodium chloride solution is 0.85-0.95 wt./vol. In the step (1), the stirring speed is 1800-2200 revolutions/minute and the time is 2-4 minutes. In the step (2), the specific operation of primary filter is filtering fecal slurry through 4.5-5.5 mm aperture filter screen to remove large particle matter. In the step (3), the specific operation is filtering the fecal slurry through 1.5-2.5 mm, 0.8-1.2 mm, 0.4-0.6 mm, and 0.2-0.3 mm aperture filter. In the step (3), the rotating speed of the centrifuging is 5500-6500 revolutions/minute and the time is 12-18 minutes.

GA 201821456P

   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer: meets nutritional requirements of tomato plant growth; is environmentally friendly and biologically degradable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing medicinal fertilizer comprises (i) providing wolfsbane, Japanese ginseng, peppermint, garlic, hot pepper leaf, and elm, sterilizing, drying, grinding to obtain powder, placing into supercritical fluid extraction device, carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction, and separating to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine liquid and medicine dreg, (ii) mixing medicine dregs with ash, straw, and apple pulp, and fermenting to obtain fermented fertilizer, (iii) mixing medicine liquid with manganese sulfate, boric acid, zinc sulfate, calcium superphosphate, EDTA iron sodium salt, and ammonium molybdate to obtain liquid fertilizer, mixing liquid fertilizer and fermented fertilizer drying and granulating to obtain medicinal fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (i), powder supercritical fluid extraction conditions: the extraction pressure is 35 MPa, extraction temperature is 38 degrees C, separator pressure is 7 MPa, separator temperature is 50 degrees C, extracting time is 4 hours, the extracting medium is mixture of absolute ethanol and carbon dioxide, where absolute ethanol is 5-10 wt.%. In the step (ii), the fermentation temperature is 35-45 degrees C. In the process drying and granulating at 50-65 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer; Insecticide.

PN KR2018004518-A; KR1854775-B1
TI Mesoporous carbon nanosphere manufacturing involves preparing hollow mesoporous silica particle having large pore size by using alkyl group-long surfactant, and then modifying hollow mesoporous silica particles into weakly acidic state.
AU KWON Y K
   LEE K S
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 2018073802
AB    NOVELTY - Mesoporous carbon nanosphere manufacturing involves preparing hollow mesoporous silica particle having a large pore size by using an alkyl group-long surfactant, and then modifying the hollow mesoporous silica particles into a weakly acidic state by using a mineral acid in presence of a solvent to prepare mesoporous hollow silica particles containing mineral acid. A particle-polymer hybrid precursor is prepared by immersing the carbon material precursor into mesoporous hollow silica particles including the mineral acid into the particles.
   USE - Method for manufacturing mesoporous carbon nanosphere for organic compound adsorption and for use in carbon dioxide capture template.
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures the mesoporous carbon nanosphere in a simple manner having an ability to adsorb organic materials and capture carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mesoporous carbon nanosphere manufacturing involves preparing hollow mesoporous silica particle having a large pore size by using an alkyl group-long surfactant, and then modifying the hollow mesoporous silica particles into a weakly acidic state by using a mineral acid in presence of a solvent to prepare mesoporous hollow silica particles containing mineral acid. A particle-polymer hybrid precursor is prepared by immersing the carbon material precursor into mesoporous hollow silica particles including the mineral acid into the particles. The silica particle-polymer hybrid precursor is polymerized under a carbonization condition, where the silica template is removed by using a solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is cetyltrimethylammonium bromide or trimethyloctadecylammonium bromide. The mineral acid is a dilute sulfuric acid solution or a dilute phosphoric acid solution. The carbon material precursor is a perfuryl alcohol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
